Name: Mathlib
Description: The root of the Mathlib4 library: the top-level organizational directory from which all formalized mathematics descends. Houses the lakefile configuration, the primary import entry point (Mathlib.lean), and the directory structure organizing Mathlib's tens of thousands of declarations across algebra, analysis, topology, geometry, category theory, number theory, logic, probability, and computability. The single largest library of machine-checked mathematics in existence.

Name: ModelTheory
Description: Studies mathematical structures, their definable sets, and the relation between syntactic formulas and semantic models. Used to prove transfer principles, categoricity, and the existence of saturated models. Relied on by algebra, geometry (through stability theory), number theory, and theoretical computer science.

Name: Topology
Description: Topological properties of spaces of types and models in first-order logic. Formalizes the Stone space of a complete theory and the topology on type spaces, which is central to stability theory and the study of definable sets.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Topological types in model theory: the type-space topology on the Stone space of complete types `S_n(T)` — the space of complete `n`-types of a theory `T` with the Stone topology. Compactness of the type space follows from the compactness theorem. Distinct from ModelTheory/Basic and Topology/Separation/Profinite.

Name: Arithmetic
Description: Model-theoretic treatment of arithmetic theories, including Presburger arithmetic (the first-order theory of addition over the integers). Contains decidability results and semi-linear set characterizations of definable sets.

Name: Presburger
Description: Presburger arithmetic: the first-order theory of (ℤ, +, 0, 1, <) (or (ℕ, +)). Decidability via quantifier elimination (every formula is equivalent to a quantifier-free formula). Semi-linear sets as the definable sets. Distinct from Peano arithmetic (which adds multiplication and is undecidable).

Name: Semilinear
Description: Semi-linear sets as the definable sets of Presburger arithmetic: a set S ⊆ ℤⁿ is semi-linear if it is a finite union of linear sets {a + Σ cᵢbᵢ | cᵢ ∈ ℕ}. The Ginsburg-Spanier theorem: definable sets in Presburger arithmetic are exactly the semi-linear sets. Used in automata theory (Parikh's theorem) and formal verification.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Semilinear set definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Semilinear sets: finite unions of linear sets.

Name: Definability.lean
Description: Definable sets in Presburger arithmetic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Presburger arithmetic: model theory of `(ℤ, +, <)`.

Name: Algebra
Description: Algebraic structures (rings, fields) studied from a model-theoretic perspective. Includes definability results, quantifier elimination in algebraically closed fields, and connections between algebraic and logical properties.

Name: Ring
Description: Rings as first-order structures: the language {+, ×, -, 0, 1} of rings, the theory of integral domains, and model-theoretic properties of rings (e.g., the theory of ℤ is not complete). Definable sets in rings. Distinct from ModelTheory/Algebra/Field (adds division) and RingTheory (algebraic ring theory without the model-theoretic perspective).

Name: FreeCommRing.lean
Description: Free commutative ring in model theory.

Name: Definability.lean
Description: Definable sets in ring models.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Model theory of rings.

Name: Field
Description: Fields as first-order structures: the language {+, ×, -, 0, 1, /} of fields, the theory of algebraically closed fields (ACF_p), quantifier elimination for ACF (Chevalley-Tarski theorem: every definable set is constructible). Model-theoretic proofs of algebraic results via transfer principles. Distinct from ModelTheory/Algebra/Ring (rings, a weaker language).

Name: IsAlgClosed.lean
Description: Model theory of algebraically closed fields.

Name: CharP.lean
Description: Model theory of fields with characteristic p.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Model theory of fields: the language and axioms.

Name: Quotients.lean
Description: Quotient structures: forming a new L-structure by quotienting by a congruence, with the quotient map being a homomorphism.

Name: Satisfiability.lean
Description: Satisfiability and the compactness theorem: a theory T is satisfiable iff every finite subset is satisfiable.

Name: PartialEquiv.lean
Description: Partial isomorphisms between structures: finite bijections preserving the language, used in back-and-forth arguments.

Name: LanguageMap.lean
Description: Language maps: morphisms between first-order languages, and reduct/expansion of structures along language maps.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Ordered structures in model theory: the theory of linear orders, dense linear orders, and their completions.

Name: Semantics.lean
Description: Semantics of first-order logic: the satisfaction relation M satisfies phi[v], term interpretation, and key semantic facts.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Types in model theory: the Stone space of complete n-types over a theory, type omission, and realization.

Name: Ultraproducts.lean
Description: Ultraproducts: the construction of a product of structures modulo an ultrafilter, with Los's theorem.

Name: Syntax.lean
Description: Syntax of first-order logic: terms, formulas, free and bound variables, and substitution.

Name: Skolem.lean
Description: Skolem functions and Skolemization: adding function symbols to eliminate existential quantifiers.

Name: Substructures.lean
Description: Substructures: subsets closed under all function symbols, with the generated substructure and lattice.

Name: Graph.lean
Description: Graphs as first-order structures: the language of graphs and the theory of the random (Rado) graph.

Name: Definability.lean
Description: Definable sets: subsets of M^n defined by a first-order formula, and definable functions and relations.

Name: DirectLimit.lean
Description: Direct limits of structures: colimits in the category of L-structures along a directed diagram.

Name: Complexity.lean
Description: Complexity of formulas: quantifier rank, the Sigma_n and Pi_n hierarchies, and quantifier-free equivalence.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic model theory: first-order structures (interpretations), the satisfaction relation, and homomorphisms.

Name: Bundled.lean
Description: Bundled structures: packaging a type with its L-structure into a single object for categorical constructions.

Name: ElementaryMaps.lean
Description: Elementary maps (elementary embeddings): maps preserving all first-order formulas, stronger than homomorphisms.

Name: FinitelyGenerated.lean
Description: Finitely generated structures: structures generated by a finite set, with chain conditions and applications.

Name: Fraisse.lean
Description: Fraisse limits: the unique countable homogeneous structure with a given age (isomorphism class of finitely generated substructures).

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Elementarily equivalent structures: two structures agreeing on all first-order sentences, with characterizations.

Name: ElementarySubstructures.lean
Description: Elementary substructures: substructures M elementary in N where every formula holds in M iff in N, with Tarski-Vaught test.

Name: Encoding.lean
Description: Encoding first-order structures and formulas: representing syntax as terms of an inductive type.

Name: MeasureTheory
Description: Develops measures, measurable spaces, Lebesgue integration, and probability measures. Used to assign sizes to sets, integrate functions in great generality, and provide a rigorous foundation for probability. Relied on by probability, analysis, ergodic theory, mathematical physics, and statistics.

Name: Order
Description: Measure-theoretic results on ordered structures, including lattice-valued measures and monotone convergence in ordered Banach spaces. Bridges order theory and integration theory.

Name: Group
Description: Measures on ordered groups: the interaction between a measure and the group order, including translation-invariant measures on ordered groups. The relationship between Haar measure and the ordering on ℝ (Lebesgue measure is both Haar measure and a measure respecting the order). Distinct from Algebra/Order/Group (algebraic ordered groups without measures).

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice-ordered group measures.

Name: UpperLower.lean
Description: Measurability of upper and lower sets: upper/lower sets in an ordered measurable space are measurable, and measurability of `{x | f(x) ≤ g(x)}`. Distinct from Order/UpperLower/Basic and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on measures: `μ ⊔ ν` and `μ ⊓ ν` as pointwise max/min, and the complete lattice of measures on a measurable space. Distinct from Measure/MeasureSpace and Order/CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: Measure
Description: Core measure space constructions: Lebesgue measure, Haar measure on locally compact groups, product measures, and pushforward measures. The central hub for all concrete measures used throughout Mathlib.

Name: Lebesgue
Description: Lebesgue measure on ℝⁿ: the unique translation-invariant Borel measure with λ([0,1]ⁿ) = 1. The relationship to Hausdorff measure, the Cantor set (Lebesgue measure 0, uncountable), and the Lusin N-property. The standard measure for real analysis and integration. Distinct from Haar (which is more general) and from the abstract Measure type.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integration with Lebesgue measure: specific results for `∫ f dλ`.

Name: VolumeOfBalls.lean
Description: Volume of balls in ℝⁿ: `vol(B(x,r)) = πⁿ/² rⁿ / Γ(n/2+1)`.

Name: EqHaar.lean
Description: Lebesgue measure equals Haar measure on ℝⁿ.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Lebesgue measure: `MeasureTheory.volume` on ℝⁿ, the Borel-regularity and outer measure.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Lebesgue measure on ℂ: identification `ℂ ≃ ℝ²`.

Name: Typeclasses
Description: Measure typeclasses: IsFiniteMeasure (μ(univ) < ∞), IsProbabilityMeasure (μ(univ) = 1), SigmaFinite (countable cover by finite-measure sets), IsLocallyFiniteMeasure (every point has a neighborhood of finite measure). Used as hypotheses in theorems requiring finiteness or sigma-finiteness conditions.

Name: SFinite.lean
Description: `SFinite μ` — σ-finite measure.

Name: ZeroOne.lean
Description: Zero-one measures.

Name: Probability.lean
Description: `IsProbabilityMeasure μ` — `μ univ = 1`.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: `IsFiniteMeasure` typeclass.

Name: NoAtoms.lean
Description: `NoAtoms μ` — measure has no atoms.

Name: Haar
Description: Haar measure on locally compact topological groups: the unique (up to scalar) left-invariant Radon measure on a locally compact group. Existence and uniqueness. The modular function (relating left and right Haar measures). Used in harmonic analysis on groups and in number theory (Haar measure on p-adic groups).

Name: NormedSpace.lean
Description: Haar measure on normed spaces: the Lebesgue measure as the Haar measure.

Name: MulEquivHaarChar.lean
Description: Multiplicative equivalence and Haar character.

Name: OfBasis.lean
Description: Haar measure from a basis.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: Uniqueness of Haar measure.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Haar measure on quotient groups.

Name: Disintegration.lean
Description: Disintegration of Haar measure.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Haar measure on locally compact groups: existence, uniqueness, and properties.

Name: DistribChar.lean
Description: Distributional characteristic function of Haar measure.

Name: InnerProductSpace.lean
Description: Haar measure on inner product spaces.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extension of Haar measure.

Name: CharacteristicFunction
Description: Characteristic functions (Fourier transforms) of probability measures: φ_μ(t) = ∫ e^(itx) dμ(x), the Bochner-Minlos theorem, and Lévy's continuity theorem (pointwise convergence of characteristic functions implies weak convergence of measures). Used in probability theory to prove the central limit theorem.

Name: TaylorExpansion.lean
Description: Taylor expansion of the characteristic function.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Characteristic function `E[e^{itX}]`.

Name: Decomposition
Description: Decompositions of measures: the Lebesgue decomposition μ = μ_ac + μ_s (absolutely continuous plus singular parts), the Radon-Nikodym theorem (μ_ac = f · ν for a density f), and the Jordan decomposition of signed measures. Used to define conditional expectation and to compare measures.

Name: Lebesgue.lean
Description: Lebesgue decomposition: `μ = ν_ac + ν_s`.

Name: RadonNikodym.lean
Description: Radon–Nikodym theorem.

Name: IntegralRNDeriv.lean
Description: Radon–Nikodym derivative in terms of integrals.

Name: Exhaustion.lean
Description: Exhaustion in measure decomposition.

Name: Hahn.lean
Description: Hahn decomposition of signed measures.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product measure: `μ × ν` — the unique measure on `α × β` satisfying `(μ × ν)(s × t) = μ(s) * ν(t)`. The Fubini theorem setup and σ-finiteness of products. Distinct from Integral/Prod and Constructions/Pi.

Name: ProbabilityMeasure.lean
Description: Probability measures: `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure α` — measures with total mass 1, the weak topology, and `ProbabilityMeasure.toMeasure`. Distinct from Measure/FiniteMeasure and Measure/GiryMonad.

Name: PreVariation.lean
Description: Pre-variation of a signed/vector measure on an interval: the preparatory lemmas for the Jordan decomposition (total variation). Distinct from VectorMeasure/BoundedVariation and Measure/MutuallySingular.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Real-valued measures: `MeasureTheory.Measure.toReal` and the signed measure structure on ℝ-valued measures. Distinct from Measure/MeasureSpace and VectorMeasure/Basic.

Name: QuasiMeasurePreserving.lean
Description: Quasi-measure-preserving maps: `MeasureTheory.QuasiMeasurePreserving f μ ν` — `ν ≪ μ.map f`. Used in ergodic theory and the Poincaré recurrence theorem. Distinct from Measure/Map and Group/Measure.

Name: Prokhorov.lean
Description: Prokhorov's theorem: a set of probability measures is precompact in the weak topology iff it is tight. Key: `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.isCompact_of_tight`. Distinct from Measure/FiniteMeasure and Measure/Tight.

Name: Portmanteau.lean
Description: Portmanteau theorem: characterisations of weak convergence of probability measures: via closed sets, open sets, boundary-null sets, and bounded continuous functions. Distinct from Measure/FiniteMeasure and Measure/LevyConvergence.

Name: MeasureSpaceDef.lean
Description: Definition of `MeasureTheory.Measure α`: the structure of a complete σ-additive outer measure restricted to measurable sets. Contains the type definition and `Measure.ofMeasurable`. Distinct from Measure/MeasureSpace (API).

Name: MeasureSpace.lean
Description: Main API for measure spaces: `MeasureTheory.Measure α`, the measure of sets, `σ`-additivity, `MeasureTheory.ae` (the a.e. filter), and common instances for ℝ, ℝⁿ. Distinct from MeasureSpace/Def (type definition) and Measure/MeasureSpaceDef.

Name: MeasuredSets.lean
Description: Measured sets: the σ-algebra of sets measurable with respect to a given outer measure (Carathéodory measurability). Distinct from OuterMeasure/Caratheodory and Measure/MeasureSpaceDef.

Name: OpenPos.lean
Description: Measures with all open sets having positive measure: `MeasureTheory.Measure.IsOpenPos μ` — `μ(U) > 0` for every nonempty open `U`. Implies the support equals the whole space. Distinct from Measure/EverywherePos and Measure/Support.

Name: NullMeasurable.lean
Description: Null-measurable sets: sets differing from a measurable set by a null set. The completion of a measure space. Distinct from Measure/AEDisjoint and Measure/MeasureSpaceDef.

Name: MutuallySingular.lean
Description: Mutually singular measures: `μ ⊥ ν` — there exists a partition of the space into a `μ`-null and a `ν`-null set. The Lebesgue decomposition: `ν = ν_ac + ν_s` with `ν_ac ≪ μ` and `ν_s ⊥ μ`. Distinct from Measure/AbsolutelyContinuous and Measure/Sub.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular Borel measures: `MeasureTheory.Measure.Regular μ` — inner regular (from compact sets) and outer regular (from open sets). Proves the Riesz representation theorem and regularity for Haar measure. Distinct from Measure/RegularityCompacts and Integral/Regular.

Name: Tilted.lean
Description: Tilted (exponentially tilted) measures: `MeasureTheory.Measure.tilted μ f` — the measure `dμ_f = e^f / ∫ e^f dμ * dμ`. Used in large deviation theory. Distinct from Measure/LogLikelihoodRatio and Measure/WithDensity.

Name: TightNormed.lean
Description: Tightness in normed spaces: criteria for tightness of measures on normed spaces via moment bounds. Distinct from Measure/Tight and Measure/Prokhorov.

Name: Tight.lean
Description: Tightness of a set of measures: `MeasureTheory.Tight S` — for every `ε > 0` there is a compact `K` with `μ(Kᶜ) < ε` for all `μ ∈ S`. Distinct from Measure/Prokhorov (precompactness characterisation) and Function/UnifTight.

Name: WithDensityFinite.lean
Description: Finiteness of measures with density: `(μ.withDensity f)` is finite iff `f ∈ L¹(μ)`. Distinct from Measure/WithDensity and Measure/FiniteMeasure.

Name: WithDensity.lean
Description: Measures with density: `μ.withDensity f` — the measure `A ↦ ∫_A f dμ`. Radon–Nikodym theorem: `ν ≪ μ → ν = μ.withDensity (ν.rnDeriv μ)`. Distinct from Measure/AbsolutelyContinuous and VectorMeasure/WithDensity.

Name: Trim.lean
Description: Trimming a measure to a sub-σ-algebra: `μ.trim hm` — the restriction of `μ` to a coarser σ-algebra `m`. Used in conditional expectation theory. Distinct from Measure/Restrict and Function/ConditionalLExpectation.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of a measure: `MeasureTheory.Measure.support μ` — the closed set `{x | μ(U) > 0 for all open U ∋ x}`. Proves `support μ = ∅ ↔ μ = 0`. Distinct from Measure/OpenPos and Measure/EverywherePos.

Name: SeparableMeasure.lean
Description: Separable measures: `MeasureTheory.Measure.IsSeparable μ` — the Lᵖ space is separable, equivalently the measure class has a countable dense subset. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/CountablyGenerated and Function/L2Space.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restriction of a measure: `μ.restrict s` — the measure `A ↦ μ(A ∩ s)`. Properties: `restrict_univ`, `restrict_restrict`, Fubini for restricted measures. Distinct from Measure/MeasureSpace and Integral/IntegrableOn.

Name: RegularityCompacts.lean
Description: Regularity via compact sets: inner regularity `μ(s) = sup{μ(K) | K compact, K ⊆ s}` for locally finite measures on locally compact spaces. Distinct from Measure/Regular and Measure/HasOuterApproxClosed.

Name: SubFinite.lean
Description: Sub-finite measures: measures that are locally finite or sigma-finite, and their regularity properties. Distinct from Measure/FiniteMeasure and Measure/Regular.

Name: Sub.lean
Description: Subtraction of measures: `μ - ν` — defined as the smallest measure `λ` with `ν + λ = μ`, or 0 if `μ ≤ ν`. Properties and the Hahn decomposition connection. Distinct from Measure/MutuallySingular and Measure/MeasureSpace.

Name: Stieltjes.lean
Description: Stieltjes measure: `StieltjesMeasure f` — the Borel measure on ℝ corresponding to a monotone right-continuous function `f`, satisfying `μ((a, b]) = f(b) - f(a)`. Distinct from Measure/MeasureSpace and Function/AbsolutelyContinuous.

Name: Count.lean
Description: Counting measure: `MeasureTheory.Measure.count` on a discrete measurable space — `count s = #s` for finite `s`, `∞` otherwise. Distinct from Measure/Dirac (single-point measure) and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: ContinuousPreimage.lean
Description: Preimages under continuous maps are measurable: a continuous map between topological spaces with Borel σ-algebras is measurable. Distinct from Measure/Map and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: Content.lean
Description: Content on locally compact spaces: `MeasureTheory.Content K` — a monotone subadditive map on compact sets used to construct Haar measure via the Riesz representation theorem. Distinct from Measure/AddContent and Group/Measure.

Name: Doubling.lean
Description: Doubling measures: `MeasureTheory.Measure.IsDoubling μ` — `μ(B(x, 2r)) ≤ C * μ(B(x, r))` for some constant `C`. Used in harmonic analysis and differentiation theory. Distinct from Covering/DensityTheorem and Covering/Vitali.

Name: DiracProba.lean
Description: Dirac delta as a probability measure: `MeasureTheory.Measure.diracProba x` with `diracProba x univ = 1`. Properties in the space of probability measures. Distinct from Measure/Dirac (bare measure) and Measure/ProbabilityMeasure.

Name: Dirac.lean
Description: Dirac measure: `MeasureTheory.Measure.dirac x` — the unit mass at a point `x`. `∫ f dδₓ = f(x)`. Distinct from Measure/Count and Measure/DiracProba.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Complex measures: `MeasureTheory.ComplexMeasure α` — finitely additive measures taking values in `ℂ`, and the total variation. Distinct from VectorMeasure/Basic (real vector measures) and Measure/MeasureSpace.

Name: AEDisjoint.lean
Description: A.e. disjoint sets: `MeasureTheory.AEDisjoint μ s t` — `μ(s ∩ t) = 0`. Proves the Hahn decomposition theorem (decomposing into positive and negative parts). Distinct from Measure/MutuallySingular and Measure/NullMeasurable.

Name: AddContent.lean
Description: Additive content (finitely additive measures) on a semiring of sets: `AddContent S` — a nonneg function on a semiring `S` that is finitely additive. Distinct from OuterMeasure/OfAddContent and VectorMeasure/AddContent.

Name: AbsolutelyContinuous.lean
Description: Absolutely continuous measures: `μ ≪ ν` (`μ` is absolutely continuous with respect to `ν`) — `ν(s) = 0 → μ(s) = 0`. The Radon–Nikodym theorem: `μ ≪ ν` iff `μ = f • ν` for some nonneg `f ∈ L¹(ν)`. Distinct from Measure/MutuallySingular and Measure/WithDensity.

Name: Comap.lean
Description: Comap of a measure: `μ.comap f` — the pullback measure satisfying `(μ.comap f)(s) = μ(f '' s)` when `f` is an embedding. Distinct from Measure/Map (pushforward) and MeasurableSpace/Embedding.

Name: CharacteristicFunction.lean
Description: Characteristic function of a measure: `MeasureTheory.charFun μ t = ∫ e^{i⟨t,x⟩} dμ(x)` — the Fourier transform of `μ`. Injectivity (characteristic function determines the measure), Lévy's continuity theorem. Distinct from Measure/IntegralCharFun and Fourier/FourierTransform.

Name: AEMeasurable.lean
Description: A.e. measurable functions: `AEMeasurable f μ` — there exists a measurable `g` with `f = g` a.e. Properties and instances. Distinct from Function/AEMeasurableSequence and MeasurableSpace/Embedding.

Name: EverywherePos.lean
Description: Everywhere positive measures: `MeasureTheory.Measure.IsEverywherePos μ` — `μ(U) > 0` for every nonempty open set `U`. Properties and examples. Distinct from Measure/OpenPos and Measure/Support.

Name: LevyConvergence.lean
Description: Lévy's continuity theorem: a sequence of probability measures converges weakly iff their characteristic functions converge pointwise. Distinct from Measure/CharacteristicFunction and Measure/Portmanteau.

Name: IntegralCharFun.lean
Description: Integral expressions for characteristic functions of measures: `∫ e^{i⟨t,x⟩} 1_s(x) dμ` and estimates. Distinct from Measure/CharacteristicFunction and Integral/Indicator.

Name: Hausdorff.lean
Description: Hausdorff measure `MeasureTheory.Measure.hausdorff d`: the `d`-dimensional Hausdorff measure on a metric space. Proves `hausdorff 0 = count`, `hausdorff n` on `ℝⁿ` is a multiple of Lebesgue measure, and the Hausdorff dimension. Distinct from Topology/MetricSpace/HausdorffDimension.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Pushforward of a measure: `μ.map f` — the pushforward measure satisfying `(μ.map f)(s) = μ(f⁻¹ s)`. Properties: composition, absolute continuity preservation, and the change of variables formula. Distinct from Measure/Comap and Function/Jacobian.

Name: LogLikelihoodRatio.lean
Description: Log-likelihood ratio (relative entropy / KL divergence): `MeasureTheory.Measure.kl μ ν = ∫ log(dμ/dν) dμ` — the Kullback–Leibler divergence. Proves nonnegativity, convexity, and the chain rule. Distinct from Measure/AbsolutelyContinuous and Measure/Tilted.

Name: LevyProkhorovMetric.lean
Description: Lévy–Prokhorov metric: `levyProkhorovDist μ ν = inf{ε > 0 | μ(A) ≤ ν(Aᵉ) + ε for all closed A}` — a metric on probability measures generating the weak topology. Distinct from Measure/ProbabilityMeasure and Measure/Prokhorov.

Name: HasOuterApproxClosedProd.lean
Description: Outer approximation by closed sets for product measures: the product of two measures with outer approximation by closed sets also has this property. Distinct from Measure/HasOuterApproxClosed and Measure/Prod.

Name: FiniteMeasurePi.lean
Description: Finite product measures: `FiniteMeasure.pi` — the finite product measure on `Πᵢ αᵢ` and its properties as a finite measure. Distinct from Constructions/Pi and Measure/FiniteMeasure.

Name: FiniteMeasureExt.lean
Description: Extension results for finite measures: two finite Borel measures agreeing on a π-system (closed under finite intersections) generating the σ-algebra are equal. Distinct from Measure/FiniteMeasure and MeasurableSpace/Basic.

Name: FiniteMeasure.lean
Description: Finite measures and the topology of weak convergence: `MeasureTheory.FiniteMeasure α` — measures with `μ univ < ∞`, the weak topology, and the Prokhorov theorem. Distinct from Measure/ProbabilityMeasure and Measure/Tight.

Name: HasOuterApproxClosed.lean
Description: Measures with outer approximation by closed sets: `μ.HasOuterApproxClosed` — `μ(s) = inf{μ(C) | C closed, s ⊆ C}`. Used in regularity theory. Distinct from Measure/Regular and Measure/HasOuterApproxClosedProd.

Name: GiryMonad.lean
Description: Giry monad: the functor `M : MeasurableSpace → MeasurableSpace` sending `α` to the space of probability measures on `α`, with the monad structure encoding conditional expectation. Distinct from Measure/ProbabilityMeasure and Category/MeasCat.

Name: FiniteMeasureProd.lean
Description: Product of finite measures: `FiniteMeasure.prod μ ν` — the product of two finite measures is finite. Distinct from Measure/Prod and Measure/FiniteMeasurePi.

Name: MeasurableSpace
Description: Sigma-algebras and their generators, measurable spaces, and the Borel sigma-algebra. Defines the foundational structures on which all measure-theoretic work in Mathlib is built.

Name: MeasurablyGenerated.lean
Description: Measurably generated σ-algebras: a σ-algebra is measurably generated by a family `C` if every measurable set is a countable intersection of finite unions from `C`. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/CountablyGenerated.

Name: Invariants.lean
Description: Invariant σ-algebras: the σ-algebra of sets invariant under a measurable transformation, used in ergodic theory. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Basic and Group/Action.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard measurable space instances: `MeasurableSpace ℝ`, `ℤ`, `ℕ`, `Bool`, and the Borel σ-algebra for metrizable spaces via `borel`. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Defs and MeasurableSpace/Constructions.

Name: NCard.lean
Description: Cardinality of measurable spaces: the number of measurable sets in a finite measurable space, and cardinality bounds on generated σ-algebras. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Card and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product measurable space `α × β`: `MeasurableSpace.prod` — the σ-algebra generated by products of measurable sets. Proves `MeasurableSet.prod` and the universal property. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Pi (infinite products) and MeasurableSpace/Constructions.

Name: PreorderRestrict.lean
Description: Measurable spaces on preordered types: the σ-algebra generated by the order (intervals), and measurability of order operations. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Instances and Order/MeasureTheory files.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Product σ-algebra on `Π i : ι, α i`: `MeasurableSpace.pi` — the coarsest σ-algebra making all projections measurable. Proves measurability of cylinder sets and the Ionescu-Tulcea theorem setup. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Constructions and Constructions/Pi.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard constructions of measurable spaces: product σ-algebras (`MeasurableSpace.prod`), subspace σ-algebras, quotient σ-algebras, and the coarsest/finest σ-algebras making maps measurable. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Basic and MeasurableSpace/Pi.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality bounds on measurable spaces: the measurable space on a countable type, and the fact that the Borel σ-algebra of a separable metrizable space has cardinality `𝔠`. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Basic and MeasurableSpace/CountablyGenerated.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for measurable spaces: `MeasurableSet`, `measurable_id`, `Measurable.comp`, and the Borel σ-algebra. Main workhorse file. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Defs (type definition) and MeasurableSpace/Constructions.

Name: CountablyGenerated.lean
Description: Countably generated σ-algebras: `MeasurableSpace.IsCountablyGenerated m` — a σ-algebra generated by countably many sets. Proved for Borel σ-algebras of separable metrizable spaces. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Basic and MeasurableSpace/MeasurablyGenerated.

Name: EventuallyMeasurable.lean
Description: Eventually measurable functions: functions that are measurable with respect to the tail σ-algebra (eventually), and their relationship to a.e. measurability. Distinct from Function/AEMeasurableSequence and Filter/Cofinite.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Measurable embeddings: `MeasurableEmbedding f` — an injective measurable map with measurable range. The sub-σ-algebra from an embedding, and the pullback of measures. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Constructions and Measure/Comap.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `MeasurableSpace α`: a collection of measurable sets closed under countable unions and complements. The typeclass with `MeasurableSet`, `measurableSpace_iSup`. The foundational file for MeasurableSpace. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Basic (API).

Name: VectorMeasure
Description: Signed measures and vector-valued measures taking values in Banach spaces. Includes the Hahn and Jordan decomposition theorems for signed measures.

Name: Variation
Description: Total variation of vector measures: the total variation ‖μ‖ = sup{∑|μ(Aᵢ)| | {Aᵢ} partition} making the space of vector measures into a Banach space. The relationship between the total variation norm and the Jordan decomposition. Used to define the dual of C(X) in the Riesz-Markov theorem for signed measures.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Total variation definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Total variation of a vector measure.

Name: Decomposition
Description: Decompositions of vector measures: the Hahn decomposition (for signed measures: a partition of the space into a positive and a negative set), the Jordan decomposition (signed measure = positive measure - negative measure), and Lebesgue decomposition for vector measures. Distinct from VectorMeasure/Variation (total variation norm).

Name: Lebesgue.lean
Description: Lebesgue decomposition for vector measures.

Name: RadonNikodym.lean
Description: Radon–Nikodym for vector measures.

Name: JordanSub.lean
Description: Jordan decomposition: subtraction part.

Name: Hahn.lean
Description: Hahn decomposition of vector measures.

Name: Jordan.lean
Description: Jordan decomposition of a signed measure.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integration with respect to vector measures: `∫ f dμ` for a vector measure `μ : VectorMeasure α F` and `f : α → E`, taking values in a bilinear target. Distinct from VectorMeasure/Basic and Integral/SetToL1.

Name: WithDensity.lean
Description: Vector measures with density: `μ.withDensity f` for a vector measure `μ` and measurable `f`. Radon–Nikodym for vector measures. Distinct from Measure/WithDensity (scalar) and VectorMeasure/Basic.

Name: BoundedVariation.lean
Description: Bounded variation of vector measures: `MeasureTheory.VectorMeasure.variation μ` — the total variation measure of a vector measure. Distinct from VectorMeasure/Basic and Topology/EMetricSpace/BoundedVariation.

Name: AddContent.lean
Description: Additive content for vector measures: finitely additive vector-valued set functions and their extension to vector measures. Distinct from Measure/AddContent (scalar) and VectorMeasure/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Vector measures: `MeasureTheory.VectorMeasure α E` — σ-additive maps from measurable sets to a Banach space `E`. Jordan decomposition for signed measures `E = ℝ`. Distinct from Measure/Complex and VectorMeasure/BoundedVariation.

Name: SpecificCodomains
Description: Measures taking values in specific codomains beyond the reals, including complex-valued measures and measures valued in ordered groups.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Integration of functions into pi types: `∫ f dμ : Π i, Eᵢ` is computed componentwise `(∫ f dμ) i = ∫ (f · i) dμ`. Distinct from SpecificCodomains/ContinuousMap and Integral/Pi.

Name: WithLp.lean
Description: Integration of functions into `WithLp p E`: the Bochner integral into renormed product spaces. Distinct from SpecificCodomains/Pi and LinearAlgebra/WithLp.

Name: ContinuousMap.lean
Description: Integration of functions into `C(X, E)`: the Bochner integral `∫ f dμ : C(X, E)` when `f : α → C(X, E)`, and measurability of continuous map-valued functions. Distinct from SpecificCodomains/Pi and Function/ContinuousMapDense.

Name: ContinuousMapZero.lean
Description: Integration of functions into `ContinuousMapZero X E`: integrals taking values in the space of continuous maps vanishing at a basepoint. Distinct from SpecificCodomains/ContinuousMap and Topology/ContinuousMap/ZeroAtInfty.

Name: OuterMeasure
Description: Outer measures and the Carathéodory extension theorem, which constructs sigma-additive measures from pre-measures defined on simpler collections of sets.

Name: OfAddContent.lean
Description: Outer measure from additive content: extending an additive content to an outer measure via the Carathéodory extension theorem. Distinct from Measure/AddContent and OuterMeasure/OfFunction.

Name: Induced.lean
Description: Induced outer measure: from a function on a semi-ring of sets, construct the outer measure `OuterMeasure.ofFunction`. Distinct from OuterMeasure/OfFunction and OuterMeasure/OfAddContent.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on outer measures: restriction, pushforward, and the product of outer measures. Distinct from OuterMeasure/Basic and Measure/Map.

Name: OfFunction.lean
Description: `OuterMeasure.ofFunction h`: the outer measure defined as `μ(s) = inf{∑ h(Aᵢ) | s ⊆ ∪ Aᵢ}`. Used to construct Hausdorff and Lebesgue measures. Distinct from OuterMeasure/Induced and OuterMeasure/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `OuterMeasure α` and basic typeclass instances. Distinct from OuterMeasure/Basic (API) and Measure/MeasureSpaceDef.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Outer measures: `MeasureTheory.OuterMeasure α` — monotone countably subadditive functions from `Set α` to `ℝ≥0∞` with `∅ ↦ 0`. Operations and the restriction of outer measures. Distinct from OuterMeasure/Defs (type definition) and OuterMeasure/Caratheodory.

Name: AE.lean
Description: A.e. filter for outer measures: the a.e. filter `OuterMeasure.ae μ` of sets whose complement has outer measure 0. Distinct from OuterMeasure/Basic and Measure/AEDisjoint.

Name: Caratheodory.lean
Description: Carathéodory's measurability criterion: `s` is Carathéodory-measurable for outer measure `μ` iff `μ(t) = μ(t ∩ s) + μ(t ∩ sᶜ)` for all `t`. The extension of an outer measure to a measure. Distinct from OuterMeasure/Basic and Measure/MeasuredSets.

Name: BorelCantelli.lean
Description: Borel–Cantelli lemma: if `∑ μ(Aₙ) < ∞` then `μ(limsup Aₙ) = 0` (first Borel–Cantelli). For independent events, the converse holds (second Borel–Cantelli). Distinct from OuterMeasure/Basic and Probability independence files.

Name: Covering
Description: Covering theorems including the Vitali and Besicovitch covering lemmas, foundational tools for differentiating measures and proving the Lebesgue differentiation theorem.

Name: OneDim.lean
Description: Covering and differentiation in dimension 1: the one-dimensional Besicovitch and Vitali covering lemmas are simpler; explicit constants and sharper results for intervals in ℝ. Distinct from Covering/Besicovitch (n-dimensional) and Covering/Vitali.

Name: LiminfLimsup.lean
Description: Liminf and limsup of measures of balls: `liminf_{r→0} μ(B(x,r))/ν(B(x,r))` and its a.e. properties. Used in the proofs of density theorems. Distinct from Covering/Differentiation and Order/Filter/IsBounded.

Name: VitaliFamily.lean
Description: Vitali families: an abstract class of covering families satisfying the Vitali property (differentiation works for any locally finite measure). Distinct from Covering/Vitali (the specific ball version) and Covering/Differentiation.

Name: Vitali.lean
Description: Vitali covering lemma: any collection of balls in a metric space has a disjoint sub-collection whose 5-fold dilates cover the original set. Distinct from Covering/VitaliFamily (abstract Vitali families) and Covering/Besicovitch.

Name: BesicovitchVectorSpace.lean
Description: Besicovitch covering in normed vector spaces: the Besicovitch covering constant for `ℝⁿ` is bounded by a function of `n`, and the full Besicovitch covering theorem in finite-dimensional normed spaces. Distinct from Covering/Besicovitch (abstract version) and Covering/DensityTheorem.

Name: Besicovitch.lean
Description: Besicovitch covering theorem: in `ℝⁿ`, any family of balls `{B(x, r(x))}` has a countable sub-family with bounded overlap that covers the original set. Key: `Besicovitch.exists_fin_subcovers`. Distinct from Covering/BesicovitchVectorSpace and Covering/Vitali.

Name: Differentiation.lean
Description: Differentiation of measures: the abstract Vitali covering theorem and differentiation of a locally finite measure with respect to a doubling measure. The Lebesgue differentiation theorem in the abstract setting. Distinct from Covering/DensityTheorem (ℝⁿ) and Covering/VitaliFamily.

Name: DensityTheorem.lean
Description: Lebesgue differentiation/density theorem: for a locally finite Borel measure μ in ℝⁿ, the density `lim_{r→0} μ(B(x,r))/vol(B(x,r))` exists a.e. and equals the Radon–Nikodym derivative. Distinct from Covering/Differentiation and Covering/Besicovitch.

Name: Constructions
Description: Standard constructions of measures: restriction, completion, inner regularity, the counting measure, and the Dirac delta measure.

Name: Polish
Description: Polish spaces: separable completely metrizable topological spaces (like ℝ, ℝⁿ, and Cantor space 2^ℕ). The Kuratowski isomorphism theorem (every uncountable Polish space is Borel isomorphic to ℝ). Analytic sets (continuous images of Polish spaces). Used in descriptive set theory and in the existence theorems for regular conditional distributions.

Name: StronglyMeasurable.lean
Description: Strongly measurable functions on Polish spaces.

Name: EmbeddingReal.lean
Description: Embedding Polish spaces in ℝ.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Polish spaces: separable completely metrizable spaces.

Name: BorelSpace
Description: Borel measurable spaces: the Borel σ-algebra on a topological space, generated by the open sets. The BorelSpace typeclass (the measurable structure equals the Borel structure). Borel functions (measurable with respect to Borel σ-algebras), and the Lusin-Souslin theorem. Used whenever analysis and measure theory interact.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra and order topology.

Name: Metrizable.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra of metrizable spaces.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra of `WithTop` spaces.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra of ℝ.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra on ℂ.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra basics.

Name: Metric.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra of metric spaces.

Name: ContinuousLinearMap.lean
Description: Borel σ-algebra on continuous linear maps.

Name: ProjectiveFamilyContent.lean
Description: Projective family content: the content (finitely additive measure) on cylinder sets defined by a projective family of measures, serving as the input to the Kolmogorov extension theorem. Distinct from Constructions/Projective and OuterMeasure/OfAddContent.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projective limit of measures (Kolmogorov extension theorem): given a consistent family of measures on finite-dimensional projections, there exists a unique measure on the infinite product. Distinct from Constructions/Pi and Constructions/ProjectiveFamilyContent.

Name: SimpleGraph.lean
Description: Measures on simple graphs: the counting measure and uniform measure on the vertex set of a simple graph, and measurability of graph properties. Distinct from Combinatorics/SimpleGraph and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: UnitInterval.lean
Description: Lebesgue measure on the unit interval `[0, 1]`: the restriction of `volume` to `[0, 1]`, its total mass `1`, and special properties of measurable sets in `[0, 1]`. Distinct from Measure/Stieltjes and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: SubmoduleQuotient.lean
Description: Measure on a quotient of a topological abelian group by a closed subgroup: the pushforward of Haar measure to the quotient `G / H`, and its properties. Distinct from Group/Measure (Haar measure) and MeasurableSpace/Constructions.

Name: ClosedCompactCylinders.lean
Description: Closed compact cylinders in product spaces: the σ-algebra generated by closed compact cylinders in `Π i, α i`. Used in the construction of the projective limit of measures. Distinct from Constructions/Cylinders (open cylinders) and Constructions/Projective.

Name: AddChar.lean
Description: Additive characters as measurable functions: `AddChar.measurable` — additive characters of locally compact abelian groups are measurable. Used in Fourier analysis on LCA groups. Distinct from NumberTheory/LegendreSymbol/AddCharacter and Fourier/FourierTransform.

Name: Cylinders.lean
Description: Cylinder sets in product spaces: `cylinder I S = {f | ∀ i ∈ I, f i ∈ S i}` for finite `I`. Proves the cylinder sets generate the product σ-algebra. Distinct from Constructions/ClosedCompactCylinders and MeasurableSpace/Pi.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Product measure on `Π i : ι, α i` for a finite index type `ι`: `MeasureTheory.Measure.pi μ = ⨂ᵢ μᵢ`, the Fubini theorem for product measures, and the projection maps. Distinct from Constructions/Cylinders and Measure/Prod.

Name: HaarToSphere.lean
Description: Relationship between Haar measure on `O(n)` and the uniform measure on the sphere `Sⁿ⁻¹`: the pushforward of Haar measure under the orbit map `g ↦ g e₁` is the surface measure on `Sⁿ⁻¹`. Distinct from Group/Measure (Haar measure) and Measure/Hausdorff.

Name: Category
Description: Categorical aspects of measure theory: the category of measurable spaces, the Giry monad, and functorial constructions relating measure theory to category theory.

Name: MeasCat.lean
Description: The category `MeasCat` of measurable spaces and measurable maps: objects, morphisms, limits (products as product σ-algebras), colimits, and the forgetful functor to `Type`. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Defs (the typeclass) and Topology/Category/Born.

Name: Integral
Description: Lebesgue and Bochner integration: the integral of measurable functions, dominated convergence, Fubini's theorem, and the Radon-Nikodym derivative.

Name: Lebesgue
Description: The Lebesgue integral of nonneg measurable functions: ∫ f dμ = sup{∫ φ | φ simple, φ ≤ f}. Monotone convergence theorem (Beppo Levi), Fatou's lemma, and the definition of integrable functions (signed integral via positive and negative parts). The foundational integral. Distinct from Bochner (vector-valued) and IntervalIntegral (signed interval integral).

Name: Markov.lean
Description: Markov inequality: `μ{f ≥ t} ≤ (1/t) ∫⁻ f dμ`.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Lebesgue integral under measure maps.

Name: Sub.lean
Description: Lebesgue integral and subtraction.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: Norm and the Lebesgue integral.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The Lebesgue integral for nonneg measurable functions: lintegral f = sup over simple functions φ ≤ f of ∫ φ dμ. The monotone convergence theorem (Beppo Levi): if fₙ ↗ f pointwise then ∫ fₙ → ∫ f. The foundational integral of modern analysis — handles wilder functions than the Riemann integral, provides the rigorous foundation for probability theory, and is the basis for Lp spaces and functional analysis.

Name: Add.lean
Description: Lebesgue integral of sums.

Name: DominatedConvergence.lean
Description: Dominated convergence for the lower Lebesgue integral.

Name: Countable.lean
Description: Lebesgue integral on countable spaces.

Name: RieszMarkovKakutani
Description: The Riesz-Markov-Kakutani representation theorem: every positive linear functional on C_c(X) (continuous compactly supported functions) is integration against a unique Radon measure. Identifies the dual of C_0(X) with the space of Radon measures. Foundational for the relationship between functional analysis and measure theory.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Riesz–Markov–Kakutani for real-valued functionals.

Name: NNReal.lean
Description: Riesz–Markov–Kakutani for NNReal-valued functionals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Riesz–Markov–Kakutani theorem: positive linear functionals on C₀(X) are integrals.

Name: IntervalIntegral
Description: The interval integral ∫_a^b f(x) dx as a signed integral: defined as ∫_{[a,b]} f dλ when a ≤ b and -∫_{[b,a]} f dλ when a > b. The fundamental theorem of calculus (FTC1: derivative of the integral, FTC2: integral of the derivative), integration by parts, and substitution. Distinct from Lebesgue (unsigned measure integral) and Bochner (vector-valued).

Name: MeanValue.lean
Description: Mean value theorem for interval integrals.

Name: LebesgueDifferentiationThm.lean
Description: Lebesgue differentiation theorem for interval integrals.

Name: IntegrationByParts.lean
Description: Integration by parts.

Name: TrapezoidalRule.lean
Description: Trapezoidal rule for numerical integration.

Name: Slope.lean
Description: Slope and interval integrals.

Name: Periodic.lean
Description: Integrals of periodic functions.

Name: ContDiff.lean
Description: C^n functions and interval integrals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Interval integrals: `∫ x in a..b, f x`.

Name: AbsolutelyContinuousFun.lean
Description: Absolutely continuous functions and interval integrals.

Name: FundThmCalculus.lean
Description: FTC for interval integrals.

Name: DistLEIntegral.lean
Description: Distance bounded by integral.

Name: DerivIntegrable.lean
Description: Integrability of derivatives.

Name: Bochner
Description: The Bochner integral of Banach-space-valued functions: ∫ f dμ for f : α → E a strongly measurable integrable function. Linearity, the dominated convergence theorem in Banach spaces, and the relationship ‖∫ f‖ ≤ ∫ ‖f‖. Used in functional analysis and PDE theory. Distinct from Lebesgue (scalar-valued) — Bochner extends to vector-valued functions.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Set Bochner integrals.

Name: SumMeasure.lean
Description: Bochner integral for sum measures.

Name: VitaliCaratheodory.lean
Description: Vitali–Carathéodory for the Bochner integral.

Name: L1.lean
Description: Bochner integral on L¹.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bochner integral: `∫ f dμ` for Banach-space-valued functions.

Name: ContinuousLinearMap.lean
Description: Bochner integral and continuous linear maps.

Name: FundThmCalculus.lean
Description: FTC for the Bochner integral.

Name: CurveIntegral
Description: Line integrals along curves: ∫_γ f dz for a continuous function f along a smooth curve γ. The fundamental theorem of calculus for line integrals and the independence of path for conservative fields. Used in complex analysis (Cauchy's theorem). Distinct from IntervalIntegral (which integrates over an interval in ℝ, not a curve in ℂ).

Name: Poincare.lean
Description: Poincaré lemma for curve integrals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Curve integrals: `∫_{γ} f ds`.

Name: LebesgueNormedSpace.lean
Description: Lebesgue integration in normed spaces: the Bochner integral `∫ f dμ` for `f : α → E`, its norm bound `‖∫ f dμ‖ ≤ ∫ ‖f‖ dμ`, and the completeness of L¹(μ, E). Distinct from Function/L2Space and Integral/MeanInequalities.

Name: Marginal.lean
Description: Marginal integrals: `∫ f(x, y) dμx` as a function of `y`, measurability of the marginal, and the Fubini theorem in marginal form. Distinct from Integral/Prod and Integral/Pi.

Name: MeanInequalities.lean
Description: Integral mean inequalities: Minkowski's integral inequality `‖∫ f(·, y) dy‖_p ≤ ∫ ‖f(·, y)‖_p dy`, Hölder for integrals, and related Lᵖ norm estimates. Distinct from Function/Holder and Integral/Average.

Name: IntegralEqImproper.lean
Description: Bochner integral equals improper Riemann integral for nonnegative functions: `∫_[a,∞) f dλ = lim_{b→∞} ∫_[a,b] f dλ` when `f ≥ 0`. Distinct from Integral/IntegrableOn and BoxIntegral/Integrability.

Name: IntervalAverage.lean
Description: Average over an interval: `⨍ x in a..b, f x` — the average value over `[a, b]`. Distinct from Integral/Average (general average) and Integral/CircleAverage.

Name: Layercake.lean
Description: Layercake (Cavalieri) formula: `∫ f dμ = ∫_0^∞ μ{f > t} dt` for nonneg `f`. Used to relate Lᵖ norms to distribution functions. Distinct from Integral/MeanInequalities and Function/EssSup.

Name: MeanValue.lean
Description: Mean value theorem for integrals: `‖∫ f dμ‖ ≤ μ(s) * sup ‖f‖` on a set `s`, and its application to averaging. Distinct from Analysis/Calculus/MeanValue and Integral/Average.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regularity of integrals: inner/outer approximation of `∫ f dμ` by integrals over compact/open sets when `μ` is regular. Distinct from Measure/Regular and Integral/IntegrableOn.

Name: SetToL1.lean
Description: Extension of set-to-vector maps to L¹: `DominatedFinMeasAdditive.toL1` — extending a finitely additive dominated set function to a continuous linear map from L¹. Used to construct the Bochner integral. Distinct from Integral/FinMeasAdditive and VectorMeasure/Integral.

Name: TorusIntegral.lean
Description: Torus integrals: `∮ f dz` over an `n`-dimensional torus `{z | ∀ i, |zᵢ - cᵢ| = rᵢ}`, iterated circle integrals, and the torus version of Cauchy's formula. Distinct from Integral/CircleIntegral and Analysis/Fourier/AddCircleMulti.

Name: PeakFunction.lean
Description: Peak function approximations to the identity: a sequence of nonneg functions `φ_ε` concentrating near 0 with `∫ φ_ε = 1` serves as an approximate identity, and `φ_ε ∗ f → f` in Lᵖ. Distinct from Integral/DominatedConvergence and Group/IntegralConvolution.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Iterated integrals on pi spaces: the Fubini–Tonelli theorem for products `Πᵢ αᵢ` of finitely many measure spaces, and `∫ f d(μ₁ × μ₂) = ∫∫ f dμ₁ dμ₂`. Distinct from Constructions/Pi and Integral/Prod.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Fubini–Tonelli theorem: `∫ f d(μ × ν) = ∫ (∫ f(x, y) dμx) dνy` for nonneg or integrable `f`. Key: `MeasureTheory.integral_prod`. Distinct from Measure/Prod (product measure) and Integral/Pi.

Name: IntegrableOn.lean
Description: Integrability on a set: `MeasureTheory.IntegrableOn f s μ` — `f` is integrable with respect to `μ.restrict s`. Operations preserving integrability on sets and the dominated convergence theorem for restricted integrals. Distinct from Integral/DominatedConvergence.

Name: CircleAverage.lean
Description: Circle average of a measurable function: `circleAverage f c r = (2π)⁻¹ ∫_0^{2π} f(c + re^{iθ}) dθ`. Distinct from Integral/CircleIntegral and Complex/MeanValue.

Name: CircleIntegral.lean
Description: Circle integrals `∮ f dz = i ∫_0^{2π} f(c + re^{iθ}) re^{iθ} dθ`: definition, the Cauchy integral formula via circle integrals, and winding numbers. Distinct from Complex/CauchyIntegral (complex analysis) and Integral/CircleAverage.

Name: CircleTransform.lean
Description: Circle transform: the operator `f ↦ ∮ f(z)/(z-w) dz` as a function of `w`. Distinct from Integral/CircleIntegral and Complex/CauchyIntegral.

Name: Asymptotics.lean
Description: Asymptotic estimates for integrals: `O`/`o` bounds on `∫_a^b f(x) g(x) dx` when `g` has a known integral asymptotics. Distinct from Analysis/Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics and Integral/ExpDecay.

Name: Average.lean
Description: Average value of a function: `⨍ x, f x ∂μ = (μ univ)⁻¹ * ∫ f dμ` and the Jensen inequality for averages. Distinct from Integral/IntervalAverage (over an interval) and Integral/MeanValue.

Name: BoundedContinuousFunction.lean
Description: Integration of bounded continuous functions: `BoundedContinuousFunction.toLp` and `BoundedContinuousFunction.integral`. The duality between `C_b(X)` and finite measures. Distinct from SpecificCodomains/ContinuousMap and Measure/ProbabilityMeasure.

Name: CompactlySupported.lean
Description: Integration of compactly supported functions: integrals of functions with compact support are well-behaved, and compactly supported continuous functions are integrable. Distinct from Integral/IntegrableOn and Topology/Algebra/Support.

Name: FinMeasAdditive.lean
Description: Finitely additive measures and their integrals: `FinMeasAdditive f μ` — functions that are finitely additive on the algebra of measurable sets with finite measure. Used in the Dunford–Pettis theorem. Distinct from OuterMeasure/OfAddContent and VectorMeasure/Basic.

Name: Gamma.lean
Description: Gamma function as a Lebesgue integral: `Real.Gamma s = ∫_0^∞ x^{s-1} e^{-x} dx` for `Re s > 0`, measurability, and the functional equation `Γ(s+1) = s Γ(s)`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals and LSeries/HurwitzZeta.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Integrals of indicator functions: `∫ Set.indicator s f dμ = ∫_s f dμ`, and properties of integrating against characteristic functions. Distinct from Integral/IntegrableOn and MeasurableSpace/Basic.

Name: DivergenceTheorem.lean
Description: The divergence theorem (Gauss's theorem) in Euclidean space: the integral of div F over a region equals the flux of F through its boundary. In one dimension this reduces to the fundamental theorem of calculus — integration and differentiation are inverse operations. Contains the formal measure-theoretic statement covering Lipschitz domains in ℝⁿ, the backbone of vector calculus in physics and engineering.

Name: DominatedConvergence.lean
Description: Dominated convergence theorem: if `f n → f` a.e. and `‖f n‖ ≤ g` with `g ∈ L¹`, then `∫ f n → ∫ f`. Key: `MeasureTheory.integral_tendsto_of_tendsto_of_bounded`. Distinct from Integral/MeanInequalities and Function/UniformIntegrable.

Name: ExpDecay.lean
Description: Exponential decay integrals: `∫_a^∞ f(x) e^{-cx} dx` converges and estimates for exponentially decaying integrands. Distinct from Integral/Asymptotics and SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals.

Name: Group
Description: Integration on topological groups: Haar measure existence and uniqueness, translation invariance, and convolution of functions on locally compact groups.

Name: LIntegral.lean
Description: Lower Lebesgue integral on groups: invariance of `∫⁻ f dμ` under translation for Haar measure, and the modular function. Distinct from Group/Integral (Bochner integral) and Group/Measure.

Name: MeasurableEquiv.lean
Description: Measurable equivalences in groups: measurable group isomorphisms and their action on measures. Distinct from Group/Defs and MeasurableSpace/Embedding.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integration on groups: invariance of integrals under left/right translation `∫ f(gx) dμ = ∫ f(x) dμ` for Haar measure, and the unimodular condition. Distinct from Group/Measure (measure properties) and Group/IntegralConvolution.

Name: IntegralConvolution.lean
Description: Integral convolution: `(f ∗ g)(x) = ∫ f(xy⁻¹) g(y) dμy` — Young's inequality `‖f ∗ g‖_r ≤ ‖f‖_p ‖g‖_q` for `1/p + 1/q = 1 + 1/r`. Distinct from Group/Convolution (measure convolution) and Analysis/Fourier/Convolution.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on measures: `μ.map (· * c)` and dilations, and how Haar measure transforms under pointwise multiplication. Distinct from Group/Measure and Measure/Map.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Haar measure on product groups: `μ₁ × μ₂` is the Haar measure on `G₁ × G₂` when `μᵢ` are Haar. Also covers the product formula for the modular character. Distinct from Group/Measure and Measure/Prod.

Name: Measure.lean
Description: Haar measure: existence and uniqueness of a left-invariant Borel measure on a locally compact group. Key: `MeasureTheory.Measure.haarMeasure`. Proves left-invariance, regularity, and the modular function. Distinct from Group/Integral and Group/Defs.

Name: ModularCharacter.lean
Description: Modular character of a locally compact group: `MeasureTheory.Measure.modularChar G` — the continuous group homomorphism `G → ℝ₊` encoding the ratio of left to right Haar measure. Distinct from Group/Measure and Group/Integral.

Name: GeometryOfNumbers.lean
Description: Geometry of numbers: Minkowski's theorem — a convex symmetric set of volume > `2ⁿ * covolume(Λ)` contains a nonzero lattice point. Proved using fundamental domains. Distinct from Group/FundamentalDomain and Analysis/Convex/Basic.

Name: AEStabilizer.lean
Description: A.e. stabilizer of a measure under a group action: `MeasureTheory.Measure.aeStabilizer μ G` — the set of group elements that preserve the measure a.e. Distinct from Group/Action and Group/Measure.

Name: Arithmetic.lean
Description: Measurability of arithmetic operations: `Measurable.add`, `Measurable.mul`, `Measurable.inv` in a measurable group, and their a.e. versions. Distinct from Group/Defs and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Measurability of group actions: a continuous group action on a measurable space is measurable, and the orbit map is measurable. Distinct from Group/MeasurableEquiv and Topology/Algebra/MulAction.

Name: AddCircle.lean
Description: Haar measure on the additive circle `ℝ/ℤ`: the unique translation-invariant probability measure, and its identification with Lebesgue measure on `[0,1)`. Distinct from Group/Measure (abstract Haar) and Constructions/UnitInterval.

Name: FoelnerFilter.lean
Description: Følner sequences and filters for amenable groups: `FolnerFilter G` — the filter of Følner sets, characterising amenability. Distinct from Group/FundamentalDomain and Group/Measure.

Name: FundamentalDomain.lean
Description: Fundamental domains for group actions: `MeasureTheory.IsFundamentalDomain G s μ` — a measurable set `s` that tiles the space under the group action (a.e. disjoint translates). Proves the Siegel mean value theorem. Distinct from Group/Measure and Group/GeometryOfNumbers.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Convolution of measures and functions: `μ ∗ f`, `f ∗ g` for measures/functions on a locally compact group. Proves measurability, commutativity for abelian groups, and the convolution theorem. Distinct from Group/IntegralConvolution and Analysis/Fourier/Convolution.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for measure-theoretic groups: `MeasurableMul`, `MeasurableInv`, the invariance predicate `Measure.IsMulInvariant μ`, and basic instances. Distinct from Group/Measure (Haar measure) and Group/Arithmetic.

Name: Function
Description: Measurable functions, their basic properties, and convergence theorems (Egorov, Lusin). Defines the space of essentially bounded and Lp functions.

Name: LpSpace
Description: The Lp Banach spaces: the completion of the space of measurable functions with ‖f‖_p < ∞ modulo a.e. equality. Completeness (Riesz-Fischer), density of simple and continuous functions (Lusin's theorem), and the duality Lp* ≅ Lq for 1/p + 1/q = 1 (p ∈ (1,∞)). The cornerstone of functional analysis and harmonic analysis.

Name: DomAct
Description: Domain actions on Lp spaces: if a group G acts measure-preservingly on (X, μ), then G acts on Lp(X, μ) by (g • f)(x) = f(g⁻¹ · x), an isometric action. The Koopman representation: the group homomorphism G → Isom(Lp) given by the domain action. Used in ergodic theory and in the proof of the mean ergodic theorem.

Name: Continuous.lean
Description: Continuous domain action on Lp.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Domain action on Lp spaces.

Name: ContinuousFunctions.lean
Description: Continuous functions in Lp spaces.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator functions in Lp.

Name: ContinuousCompMeasurePreserving.lean
Description: Continuous composition with measure-preserving maps in Lp.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Lᵖ space basics: `MeasureTheory.Lp E p μ` as a normed additive commutative group.

Name: Complete.lean
Description: Completeness of Lp spaces.

Name: SpecialFunctions
Description: Measurability of special functions: proofs that exp, log, sin, cos, |·|, and other standard functions are Borel measurable, and that compositions with measurable functions remain measurable. Used as a prerequisite whenever special functions appear in integrals. Distinct from Analysis/SpecialFunctions (which studies analytic properties, not measurability).

Name: RCLike.lean
Description: Measurability for RCLike functions.

Name: Sinc.lean
Description: Measurability of the sinc function.

Name: Inner.lean
Description: Measurability of inner products.

Name: Arctan.lean
Description: Measurability of arctan.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Measurability of standard special functions.

Name: StronglyMeasurable
Description: Strongly measurable functions: functions f : α → E (into a Banach space) that are pointwise limits of simple functions. For second-countable E, strongly measurable = Borel measurable. Used to define the Bochner integral. Distinct from AEEqFun (a.e. equality) — this folder is about the type of measurability, not the equivalence relation.

Name: Inner.lean
Description: Strong measurability of inner products.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Strongly measurable function lemmas.

Name: Lp.lean
Description: Lp functions are strongly measurable.

Name: AEStronglyMeasurable.lean
Description: A.e. strongly measurable functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Strongly measurable functions: pointwise limits of simple functions.

Name: ENNReal.lean
Description: Strongly measurable ENNReal functions.

Name: LpSeminorm
Description: The Lp seminorm ‖f‖_p = (∫|f|^p dμ)^(1/p): its definition, the Hölder and Minkowski inequalities, and the relationship between Lp norms for different p (norm comparisons on finite measure spaces). The separating property of the seminorm. Distinct from LpSpace (which quotients by the null-set relation and gives a Banach space).

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Lp seminorm on product spaces.

Name: Monotonicity.lean
Description: Monotonicity of Lp seminorm in p.

Name: LpNorm.lean
Description: Lp norm from Lp seminorm.

Name: Trim.lean
Description: Lp seminorm under trimming.

Name: TriangleInequality.lean
Description: Triangle inequality for Lp seminorm.

Name: SMul.lean
Description: Lp seminorm and scalar multiplication.

Name: CompareExp.lean
Description: Comparing Lp seminorms for different p.

Name: ChebyshevMarkov.lean
Description: Chebyshev–Markov inequality via Lp seminorm.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Lp seminorm: `eLpNorm f p μ`.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Lp seminorm of indicator functions.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Lp seminorm definitions.

Name: Count.lean
Description: Lp seminorm for counting measure.

Name: AEEqFun
Description: Functions equal almost everywhere: the quotient of measurable functions by the a.e. equality relation. The AEEqFun type used to define Lp spaces. Contains the a.e. class of a function, the Lp norm on these classes, and the identification with Lp. Distinct from LpSpace (which uses AEEqFun internally but adds the norm and Banach space structure).

Name: DomAct.lean
Description: Domain action for a.e.-equality functions.

Name: ConditionalExpectation
Description: Conditional expectation in measure theory: the conditional expectation E[f | m₀] of an integrable function f with respect to a sub-σ-algebra m₀, characterized as the a.e.-unique m₀-measurable function with ∫_A E[f|m₀] dμ = ∫_A f dμ for all A ∈ m₀. Distinct from Probability/ConditionalExpectation (same concept, stated probabilistically).

Name: PullOut.lean
Description: Pull-out property: `E[fg | m] = f E[g | m]` for `m`-measurable `f`.

Name: LebesgueBochner.lean
Description: Lebesgue–Bochner conditional expectation.

Name: RadonNikodym.lean
Description: Conditional expectation via Radon–Nikodym.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: Uniqueness of conditional expectation.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Conditional expectation for real-valued functions.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Conditional expectation of indicator functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Conditional expectation: `condExp m f μ = E[f | m]`.

Name: AEMeasurable.lean
Description: A.e. measurability of conditional expectation.

Name: CondexpL1.lean
Description: L¹ conditional expectation.

Name: CondJensen.lean
Description: Jensen's inequality for conditional expectation.

Name: CondexpL2.lean
Description: L² conditional expectation.

Name: L1Space
Description: The L¹ space of integrable functions: functions with finite integral ∫|f| dμ < ∞, forming a Banach space. The dominated convergence theorem for L¹, the density of simple functions, and the duality L¹* ≅ L^∞. Distinct from LpSpace (general p) and Bochner integral (which extends the Lebesgue integral to Banach-space-valued functions).

Name: Integrable.lean
Description: Integrability and L¹ membership.

Name: HasFiniteIntegral.lean
Description: `HasFiniteIntegral f μ` predicate.

Name: AEEqFun.lean
Description: L¹ space as a.e.-equality functions.

Name: JacobianOneDim.lean
Description: One-dimensional change of variables: the special case of the Jacobian formula for functions `ℝ → ℝ`, proved directly without the full generality of the n-dimensional version. Distinct from Function/Jacobian and Function/AbsolutelyContinuous.

Name: L2Space.lean
Description: L² spaces as Hilbert spaces: `MeasureTheory.Lp E 2 μ` is a Hilbert space with inner product `⟪f, g⟫ = ∫ ⟪f x, g x⟫ dμ`. Distinct from Function/Holder (for general p) and InnerProductSpace/l2Space.

Name: LocallyIntegrable.lean
Description: Locally integrable functions: `MeasureTheory.LocallyIntegrable f μ` — `f` is integrable on every compact set. Proves continuous functions on locally compact spaces are locally integrable. Distinct from Integral/IntegrableOn and Covering/Differentiation.

Name: Holder.lean
Description: Hölder's inequality in Lᵖ spaces: `‖∫ f * g dμ‖ ≤ ‖f‖_p * ‖g‖_q` for conjugate exponents `1/p + 1/q = 1`. Distinct from Integral/MeanInequalities (which also covers Minkowski) and Function/L2Space.

Name: Intersectivity.lean
Description: Intersectivity of measurable sets: conditions under which two families of measurable sets must intersect (measurable analogues of combinatorial intersection theorems). Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Basic and Covering/Vitali.

Name: Jacobian.lean
Description: Change of variables (Jacobian): `MeasureTheory.integral_comp_mul_deriv` — the general change of variables formula `∫ f(g(x)) |g'(x)| dx = ∫ f(y) dy` for `C¹` injective `g`. Distinct from Function/JacobianOneDim and Analysis/Calculus/Rademacher.

Name: LpOrder.lean
Description: Order structure on Lᵖ spaces: `Lp.instLattice` — the lattice structure `f ⊔ g` and `f ⊓ g` on Lᵖ via pointwise operations. Distinct from Function/AEEqFun and Function/Holder.

Name: SimpleFuncDenseLp.lean
Description: Density of simple functions in Lᵖ: bounded simple functions are dense in `Lp E p μ` for `1 ≤ p < ∞`. Used to extend the integral from simple functions to all Lᵖ. Distinct from Function/SimpleFuncDense and Function/ContinuousMapDense.

Name: UniformIntegrable.lean
Description: Uniform integrability: `UniformIntegrable f p μ` — a family is uniformly integrable in Lᵖ when `‖f n‖_p` are uniformly small on small measure sets. Vitali convergence theorem: UI + a.e. convergence implies Lᵖ convergence. Distinct from Function/UnifTight and Function/ConvergenceInMeasure.

Name: UnifTight.lean
Description: Uniform tightness of sequences of functions: `UnifTight f μ` — a sequence `f n` is uniformly tight if for each `ε > 0` there exists a compact set `K` with `∫_{Kᶜ} ‖f n‖ dμ < ε` uniformly. Distinct from Function/UniformIntegrable and Measure/Tight.

Name: Piecewise.lean
Description: Piecewise functions and measurability: `Set.piecewise f g s` is measurable when `f`, `g` are measurable and `s` is measurable. Distinct from Function/SimpleFunc and MeasurableSpace/Basic.

Name: SimpleFunc.lean
Description: Simple functions `MeasureTheory.SimpleFunc`: measurable functions with finite range, operations (sum, product), measurability of range, and the integral of a simple function as a finite sum. Foundation for Bochner integration. Distinct from Function/SimpleFuncDense.

Name: SimpleFuncDense.lean
Description: Density of simple functions in measurable functions: every bounded measurable function is a pointwise limit of simple functions. Used in the construction of the Bochner integral. Distinct from Function/SimpleFunc and Function/SimpleFuncDenseLp.

Name: Floor.lean
Description: Measurability of floor and ceiling functions: `Int.floor` and `Nat.ceil` of measurable functions are measurable, and their interaction with Lebesgue measure. Distinct from Function/SimpleFunc and MeasurableSpace/Instances.

Name: AEEqOfLIntegral.lean
Description: A.e. equality from lower integral equality: if `∫⁻ f dμ = ∫⁻ g dμ` on every measurable set, then `f = g` a.e. (for nonneg measurable `f, g`). Distinct from Function/AEEqOfIntegral (Bochner integral version).

Name: AEMeasurableOrder.lean
Description: A.e. measurability for ordered-valued functions: if `f` is a.e. equal to a measurable function and takes values in a second-countable ordered space, then `f` is a.e. measurable. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Instances and Function/AEMeasurableSequence.

Name: AEMeasurableSequence.lean
Description: A.e. measurable sequences: a pointwise a.e. limit of a.e. measurable functions is a.e. measurable. Distinct from Function/AEMeasurableOrder and Measure/AEMeasurable.

Name: AbsolutelyContinuous.lean
Description: Absolutely continuous functions and the fundamental theorem of calculus: an absolutely continuous function `f : [a, b] → ℝ` has a derivative a.e. and `f(b) - f(a) = ∫_a^b f' dx`. Distinct from Measure/AbsolutelyContinuous (absolute continuity of measures) and Function/Jacobian.

Name: AEEqFun.lean
Description: A.e.-equality classes of measurable functions: `AEEqFun μ` (functions identified when equal a.e.), their `L⁰` topology, and operations. The quotient by the a.e. equality relation used to define Lᵖ spaces. Distinct from Function/AEEqOfIntegral and Function/SimpleFunc.

Name: AEEqOfIntegral.lean
Description: A.e. equality from integral equality: if `∫ φ f dμ = ∫ φ g dμ` for all bounded continuous `φ`, then `f = g` a.e. (for integrable `f, g`). Distinct from Function/AEEqOfLIntegral and Distribution/AEEqOfIntegralContDiff.

Name: ConditionalLExpectation.lean
Description: Conditional lower expectation: `condLexp f m μ` — the conditional expectation with respect to a sub-σ-algebra `m`, defined via the Radon–Nikodym theorem. Distinct from Probability/Conditional files and Integral/Average.

Name: Egorov.lean
Description: Egorov's theorem: on a finite measure space, a.e. convergence implies almost uniform convergence (uniform convergence outside a set of arbitrarily small measure). Key: `MeasureTheory.Egorov`. Distinct from Function/ConvergenceInMeasure and Function/AEEqFun.

Name: EssSup.lean
Description: Essential supremum and infimum: `MeasureTheory.essSup f μ = μ.ae.limsup f` and `essInf`. Proves `essSup f = sInf {c | f ≤ c a.e.}` and the relationship to the L∞ norm. Distinct from Function/LpOrder and Measure/Support.

Name: FactorsThrough.lean
Description: Measurability of factor maps: if `f = g ∘ h` with `h` measurable and surjective, and `f` measurable, then `g` is measurable with respect to the pushforward σ-algebra. Distinct from MeasurableSpace/Embedding and Measure/Map.

Name: ContinuousMapDense.lean
Description: Density of continuous maps in Lᵖ: `BoundedContinuousFunction` and `C(X, E)` are dense in `Lᵖ(μ)` for a regular Borel measure μ on a locally compact space. Distinct from Function/SimpleFuncDenseLp and SpecificCodomains/ContinuousMap.

Name: ConvergenceInDistribution.lean
Description: Convergence in distribution (weak convergence of probability measures): `TendstoInDistribution f g μ` — `f_n → f` in distribution iff `∫ φ dμ_{f_n} → ∫ φ dμ_f` for all bounded continuous `φ`. Distinct from Measure/Portmanteau and Function/ConvergenceInMeasure.

Name: ConvergenceInMeasure.lean
Description: Convergence in measure: `TendstoInMeasure μ f l` — `μ{x | ‖f n x - f x‖ > ε} → 0` for each `ε > 0`. Proves Vitali's theorem: uniform integrability + convergence in measure implies Lᵖ convergence. Distinct from Function/ConvergenceInDistribution and Function/AEEqFun.

Name: SetSemiring.lean
Description: Set semirings: families of sets closed under intersection and relative complement, the basis for constructing outer measures.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Interaction between measure theory and topology: Borel measures, inner regularity, and the Portmanteau theorem.

Name: PiSystem.lean
Description: Pi-systems and Dynkin systems: families of sets used to prove uniqueness of measures via the pi-lambda theorem.

Name: SetAlgebra.lean
Description: Set algebras (fields of sets): families closed under complement and finite union, the domain for finitely additive measures.

Name: Order
Description: Handles partially ordered sets, lattices, Boolean algebras, and well-founded orders. Used to compare elements, prove fixed-point theorems, and define convergence and completeness. Relied on by analysis, topology, logic, computer science (domain theory), and combinatorics.

Name: Monotone
Description: Monotone and antitone functions between ordered sets, including strictness conditions, composition lemmas, and monotonicity with respect to product orders.

Name: MonovaryOrder.lean
Description: Order properties of monovariance: the interaction of `Monovary` with composition, pointwise operations, and permutations. Distinct from Monotone/Monovary and Order/Monotone/Basic.

Name: Odd.lean
Description: Monotone odd functions: an odd function `f(-x) = -f(x)` that is monotone on `[0,∞)` is globally monotone. Distinct from Monotone/Basic and Algebra/Order.

Name: Union.lean
Description: Monotone functions on unions: gluing monotone functions on overlapping intervals — `Monotone f` on `[a,b]` and `Monotone g` on `[b,c]` with `f b = g b` gives monotone on `[a,c]`. Distinct from Monotone/Basic and Topology/Order/Monotone.

Name: Monovary.lean
Description: Monovary: `Monovary f g` — `g i ≤ g j → f i ≤ f j` (same variation). Used in rearrangement inequalities. Distinct from Monotone/Basic and Combinatorics/Rearrangement.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monotone and antitone functions: `Monotone f` (`a ≤ b → f a ≤ f b`), `Antitone`, `StrictMono`, closure under composition and pointwise operations. Distinct from Monotone/Defs (definition) and Monotone/Monovary.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions of monotonicity: `Monotone`, `Antitone`, `StrictMono`, `StrictAnti`, `MonotoneOn`, and their relationship. Distinct from Monotone/Basic (API) and Hom/Basic.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extending monotone functions: a monotone function on a subset of a linear order extends to a monotone function on the whole order. Distinct from Monotone/Basic and Topology/Order/ExtendFrom.

Name: Partition
Description: Partitions of sets into disjoint parts viewed as equivalence relations on ordered sets, with lattice structure on the set of all partitions.

Name: Finpartition.lean
Description: Finite partitions: `Finpartition s` — a `Finset` of pairwise disjoint subsets of `s` covering `s`. Operations: coarsenings, refinements, and the lattice of finpartitions. Distinct from Partition/Basic and Combinatorics/Finset.

Name: Equipartition.lean
Description: Equipartitions: partitions into sets of equal size. Used in the Szemerédi regularity lemma. Distinct from Partition/Finpartition and Combinatorics/Regularity.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Partitions of a set: `Setoid.IsPartition c` — a collection of pairwise disjoint nonempty sets covering the whole. The equivalence between partitions and equivalence relations. Distinct from Partition/Finpartition and Partition/Equipartition.

Name: Preorder
Description: Preorders (reflexive and transitive binary relations) and the quotient construction that produces a partial order. Includes dual preorders and the induced equivalence relation.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Preorder on `Finsupp`: the pointwise preorder on finitely-supported functions and the `Finsupp.lex` ordering. Distinct from Preorder/Chain and LinearAlgebra/Finsupp/Defs.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite preorders: a preorder on a finite type satisfies DCC (descending chain condition), and every element below a minimal element is minimal. Distinct from Preorder/Chain and Order/Category/FinPartOrd.

Name: Chain.lean
Description: Chains in preorders: `IsChain s` — a totally ordered subset. Zorn's lemma in chain form: every nonempty chain-complete poset has a maximal element. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Chain and Order/Preorder/Finite.

Name: Lattice
Description: Lattices with meet (inf) and join (sup) operations, distributive lattices, and the basic lattice algebra used throughout Mathlib.

Name: Congruence.lean
Description: Congruences on lattices: `LatticeCongruence α` — an equivalence relation compatible with `⊔` and `⊓`. The quotient lattice and the congruence lattice. Distinct from Order/CompleteLattice/Basic and Algebra/Congruence.

Name: Heyting
Description: Heyting algebras, which generalize Boolean algebras and provide semantics for intuitionistic logic. Includes co-Heyting algebras and bi-Heyting algebras.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Heyting algebra homomorphisms: `HeytingHom α β` — lattice homomorphisms preserving `⇨` and `⊥`. Distinct from Heyting/Basic and Order/Category/HeytAlg.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular elements of a Heyting algebra: `IsRegular a ↔ a = ¬¬a`, the regular elements form a Boolean algebra, and this gives a functor `Heyt → Bool`. Distinct from Heyting/Boundary and Heyting/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Heyting algebras: `HeytingAlgebra α` — a lattice with relative pseudo-complement `a ⇨ b` satisfying `c ≤ a ⇨ b ↔ c ⊓ a ≤ b`. Proves `compl a = a ⇨ ⊥` and basic intuitionistic logic identities. Distinct from Heyting/Boundary and BooleanAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Boundary.lean
Description: Boundary elements in Heyting algebras: `∂ a = a ⊓ aᶜ` (the boundary, always 0 in Boolean algebras), and regular elements `a = ¬¬a`. Distinct from Heyting/Basic and Heyting/Regular.

Name: Hom
Description: Order-preserving maps (monotone maps, lattice homomorphisms, order isomorphisms) and their bundled typeclass hierarchy.

Name: Order.lean
