Description: Order isomorphisms and order embeddings: `OrderIso.ofStrictMono`, `OrderEmbedding.ofStrictMono`, and their basic properties. Distinct from Hom/Basic and RelIso/Basic.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: Order homomorphisms for lexicographic orders: monotone maps between `Lex`-ordered types. Distinct from Hom/Basic and Order/Fin/Tuple.

Name: PowersetCard.lean
Description: Order homomorphisms on powersets of fixed cardinality: `Finset.powerset_len_mono` and the order-embedding `Finset.powersetCard n ↪o Finset.powersetCard (n+1)`. Distinct from Hom/Basic and Combinatorics/Finset.

Name: WithTopBot.lean
Description: Order homomorphisms for `WithTop` and `WithBot`: extending an `OrderHom α β` to `WithTop α →o WithTop β` by `some ↦ some` and `⊤ ↦ ⊤`. Distinct from Hom/Basic and BoundedOrder/Basic.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Order homomorphisms on set lattices: `OrderIso.setCongr`, `Set.orderIsoImageOfInjOn`, and maps between `Set α` and `Set β`. Distinct from Hom/Basic and BooleanAlgebra/Set.

Name: Bounded.lean
Description: Bounded order homomorphisms: `BoundedOrderHom α β` — order homomorphisms also preserving `⊥` and `⊤`. Distinct from Hom/Basic and BoundedOrder/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order homomorphisms: `OrderHom α β` (monotone maps `α →o β`), `OrderIso α β` (order isomorphisms), `OrderEmbedding α β` (order embeddings). Core API and composition. Distinct from Hom/Bounded and Hom/Lattice.

Name: BoundedLattice.lean
Description: Bounded lattice homomorphisms: `BoundedLatticeHom α β` — maps preserving `⊔`, `⊓`, `⊥`, `⊤`. The composition and kernel. Distinct from Hom/Lattice and Hom/Bounded.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice homomorphisms: `LatticeHom α β` — maps preserving `⊔` and `⊓`. The kernel and image, and the lattice of all `LatticeHom`. Distinct from Hom/BoundedLattice and Hom/Basic.

Name: CompleteLattice.lean
Description: Complete lattice homomorphisms: `CompleteLatticeHom α β` — maps preserving all `⨆` and all `⨅`. Distinct from Hom/BoundedLattice and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: Interval
Description: Intervals in ordered sets — open, closed, half-open — and their algebraic and topological properties.

Name: Set
Description: Set-theoretic intervals: Set.Icc a b = {x | a ≤ x ≤ b}, Set.Ioo, Set.Ico, Set.Ioc, Set.Ici = {x | a ≤ x}, Set.Ioi, Set.Iic, Set.Iio (the eight interval types). Operations and membership lemmas. Used pervasively in analysis (integration limits, function domains). Distinct from Order/Interval/Finset (enumerable intervals as Finset).

Name: OrderEmbedding.lean
Description: Order embeddings and intervals.

Name: OrderIso.lean
Description: Order isomorphisms and intervals.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Intervals in pi types.

Name: OrdConnected.lean
Description: Order-connected intervals.

Name: OrdConnectedComponent.lean
Description: Order-connected components.

Name: OrdConnectedLinear.lean
Description: Order-connected sets in linear orders.

Name: ProjIcc.lean
Description: Projection onto Icc.

Name: Union.lean
Description: Unions of intervals.

Name: UnorderedInterval.lean
Description: Unordered intervals `[[a, b]]`.

Name: WithBotTop.lean
Description: Intervals with WithBot/WithTop.

Name: SuccOrder.lean
Description: Intervals in successor order types.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Intervals and successor-predecessor order.

Name: SurjOn.lean
Description: Surjections onto intervals.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Intervals in Fin n.

Name: Final.lean
Description: Final segments and intervals.

Name: Image.lean
Description: Image of an interval under a monotone map.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Set interval API: `Icc`, `Ico`, `Ioc`, `Ioo`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Set interval definitions.

Name: Disjoint.lean
Description: Disjoint intervals.

Name: Infinite.lean
Description: Infinite intervals.

Name: LinearOrder.lean
Description: Intervals in linear orders.

Name: Monotone.lean
Description: Monotone maps on intervals.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Intervals of natural numbers.

Name: InitialSeg.lean
Description: Initial segments as intervals.

Name: IsoIoo.lean
Description: Order isomorphism `ℝ ≃ (-∞,∞)` = `ℝ`.

Name: Limit.lean
Description: Limit points of intervals.

Name: Finset
Description: Finite intervals as Finsets: Finset.Icc a b = {x | a ≤ x ≤ b} as a decidably enumerable finite set when the order is locally finite. The LocallyFiniteOrder typeclass. Used in combinatorics (∑_{k=a}^{b} f(k)) and in discrete mathematics. Distinct from Order/Interval/Set (set-theoretic intervals without finiteness).

Name: Gaps.lean
Description: Gaps in finite intervals.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Finite intervals in Fin n.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Finite intervals and successor-predecessor.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Finite intervals of ℕ.

Name: Box.lean
Description: Box products of finite intervals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Finite intervals as Finsets.

Name: DenselyOrdered.lean
Description: Finite intervals in densely ordered types.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Finite interval Finset definitions.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Intervals and multisets: the multiset of elements in a finite interval `{a, …, b}`. Distinct from Interval/Basic and Combinatorics/Finset.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: Lexicographic order on intervals: the lex order on `NonemptyInterval α` extending the order on `α`. Distinct from Interval/Basic and Order/Fin/Tuple.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Intervals as order-theoretic objects: `Set.Icc a b`, `Set.Ioo a b`, etc., as `NonemptyInterval α` structures. The lattice of intervals. Distinct from Interval/Lex and Topology/Order/Basic.

Name: SuccPred
Description: Successor and predecessor functions on well-ordered or locally finite ordered types, used in induction arguments and in defining discreteness.

Name: LinearLocallyFinite.lean
Description: Locally finite linear orders with successors: a linear order that is locally finite and has a successor function is isomorphic to ℤ, ℕ, or a finite segment. Distinct from SuccPred/Archimedean and Order/Preorder/Finite.

Name: Limit.lean
Description: Limit elements in successor-predecessor orders: `Order.IsSuccLimit a` — `a` is not the successor of any element (a limit ordinal in the order-theoretic sense). Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and SuccPred/Archimedean.

Name: Relation.lean
Description: Successor relation: `Covby a b` (`a ⋖ b` — `a` is covered by `b`, i.e., `a < b` with no element between). In a `SuccOrder`, `a ⋖ succ a` when `a` is not a maximal element. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and Order/Defs.

Name: WithBot.lean
Description: Successor-predecessor orders on `WithBot α` and `WithTop α`: extending `succ`/`pred` to the adjoined bottom/top element. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and BoundedOrder/Basic.

Name: Tree.lean
Description: Trees as successor orders: rooted trees where each node has a unique predecessor. The `Tree` structure defined via `PredOrder` axioms. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and Combinatorics/Tree.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Successor and predecessor in orders: `SuccOrder α` (`succ x` is the smallest element > `x`), `PredOrder α`, and the interaction with `Ioi`/`Iio` intervals. Distinct from SuccPred/Archimedean and SuccPred/Limit.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: Archimedean successor-predecessor orders: `IsSuccArchimedean α` — every element is reachable from any other by finitely many applications of `succ`. Characterisation: equivalent to `ℤ` or `ℕ` order type. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and SuccPred/LinearLocallyFinite.

Name: CompleteLinearOrder.lean
Description: Successor in complete linear orders: in a complete linear order with no top element, `succ a = sInf (Ioi a)`. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: IntervalSucc.lean
Description: Intervals and successors: `Ico a (succ a) = {a}` and related interval-successor identities. Used for computing `Finset.Ico` in discrete orders. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and Order/Interval/Basic.

Name: InitialSeg.lean
Description: Initial segments and successor orders: ordinal-type results about order embeddings that preserve the successor structure. Distinct from SuccPred/Basic and Order/RelIso/Basic.

Name: Types
Description: Specific order types: the ordinal sum, the lexicographic order, and type-level constructions used to build ordered structures.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Type-theoretic order definitions: `Lex` (lexicographic order on products/sums), `OrderDual` (opposite order), and `Finset.orderIsoOfFin`. Distinct from Types/Arithmetic and Order/Defs.

Name: Arithmetic.lean
Description: Order on arithmetic types: the canonical orders on `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `ℚ` arising from ring operations, and their interaction with `succ`/`pred`. Distinct from Types/Defs and Order/Instances.

Name: UpperLower
Description: Upper sets (upward-closed) and lower sets (downward-closed) in preorders, and the Alexandrov topology they generate.

Name: Principal.lean
Description: Principal upper/lower sets: `Set.Ici a = {x | a ≤ x}` (principal upper set) and `Set.Iic a = {x | x ≤ a}`. Their role as generators of the upper-set topology. Distinct from UpperLower/Closure and Order/Interval/Basic.

Name: LocallyFinite.lean
Description: Upper and lower sets in locally finite orders: `LocallyFiniteOrder` instances for upper and lower sets, and `Finset` representations of intervals. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and Order/Preorder/Finite.

Name: Relative.lean
Description: Relative upper/lower sets: `IsUpperSet s` relative to a suborder, and the restriction of upper sets to subsets. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and Order/Hom/Set.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Upper/lower sets on products: `IsUpperSet s × t ↔ IsUpperSet s ∧ IsUpperSet t`, and the product lattice of upper sets. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and Order/Hom/Basic.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of upper/lower sets: `UpperSetHom α β` — maps between `UpperSet α` and `UpperSet β` preserving the lattice structure. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and Order/Hom/Lattice.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Upper and lower closures: `upperClosure s = {x | ∃ a ∈ s, a ≤ x}` (smallest upper set containing `s`) and `lowerClosure s`. Galois connection with `IsUpperSet`. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and GaloisConnection/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Upper and lower sets: `IsUpperSet s` (closed upward: `a ∈ s, a ≤ b → b ∈ s`) and `IsLowerSet s`. The Boolean algebra `UpperSet α` and `LowerSet α`. Core API file. Distinct from UpperLower/Closure and UpperLower/Hom.

Name: Fibration.lean
Description: Fibrations of upper/lower sets: an order map `f : α → β` is a fibration if upper sets of `β` pull back to upper sets of `α`. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and Order/Hom/Basic.

Name: CompleteLattice.lean
Description: Upper and lower sets form complete lattices: `UpperSet α` ordered by reverse inclusion is a complete lattice. Distinct from UpperLower/Basic and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: ScottContinuity
Description: Scott-continuous functions between dcpos (directed-complete partial orders), foundational for domain theory and denotational semantics.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Scott-continuous maps on products: `ScottContinuous (f × g)` from `ScottContinuous f` and `ScottContinuous g`. Distinct from ScottContinuity/Complete and Order/Category/OmegaCompletePartialOrder.

Name: Complete.lean
Description: Scott-continuous maps into complete lattices: `ScottContinuous f` — `f` preserves directed sups. The fixed-point theorem (Kleene): the least fixed point of `f` is `⨆ n, fⁿ(⊥)`. Distinct from ScottContinuity/Prod and Topology/Order/ScottTopology.

Name: Prod
Description: Products of ordered sets and their projections; the product order and lexicographic order on Cartesian products.

Name: Lex
Description: The lexicographic order on products: (a₁, b₁) <_lex (a₂, b₂) iff a₁ < a₂, or a₁ = a₂ and b₁ < b₂. The Lex α β type wrapper that carries the lexicographic order instead of the pointwise order. Used in well-ordering constructions and in ordinal arithmetic.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Lexicographic product order homomorphisms.

Name: Rel
Description: Relations viewed order-theoretically: reflexive, transitive, and symmetric closures, and relational composition.

Name: GaloisConnection.lean
Description: Galois connections from relations: given a relation `r : α → β → Prop`, the maps `A ↦ {b | ∀ a ∈ A, r a b}` and `B ↦ {a | ∀ b ∈ B, r a b}` form a Galois connection between `Set α` and `Set β`. Distinct from GaloisConnection/Basic and Order/Bounds/Basic.

Name: RelIso
Description: Order isomorphisms and order embeddings: bundled structures encoding strict monotone bijections and their inverses.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Relation isomorphisms on sets: `RelIso.image`, `OrderIso.image`, and the Cantor–Bernstein theorem for ordered sets. Distinct from RelIso/Basic and Order/Hom/Set.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Relation isomorphisms and embeddings: `RelIso r s` — a bijection mapping `r`-related pairs to `s`-related pairs. The `RelEmbedding` (injective version) and composition. Used to compare well-orders. Distinct from RelIso/Set and Hom/Basic.

Name: GaloisConnection
Description: Galois connections (adjoint pairs) between ordered sets, with applications to closure operators, fixpoints, and the adjoint functor theorem.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions: `GaloisConnection`, `GaloisInsertion`, `GaloisCoinsertion`. The bare type definitions and the monotonicity consequences. Distinct from GaloisConnection/Basic (API).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Galois connections: `GaloisConnection l u` — `l a ≤ b ↔ a ≤ u b`. Proves `l` preserves all sups and `u` preserves all infs. Core API including `GaloisInsertion` and `GaloisCoinsertion`. Distinct from GaloisConnection/Defs.

Name: Category
Description: Categorical treatment of ordered structures: the category of preorders, lattices, and Heyting algebras, with functors to and from Set.

Name: LinOrd.lean
Description: Category `LinOrd` of linear orders and order-preserving maps. Distinct from Order/Category/PartOrd and Order/Category/NonemptyFinLinOrd.

Name: NonemptyFinLinOrd.lean
Description: Category `NonemptyFinLinOrd` of nonempty finite linear orders: the simplex category Δ used in simplicial sets and homological algebra. Distinct from Order/Category/LinOrd and AlgebraicTopology files.

Name: HeytAlg.lean
Description: Category `HeytAlg` of Heyting algebras and Heyting homomorphisms. The categorical treatment of intuitionistic propositional logic. Distinct from Order/Heyting/Basic and Order/Category/Frm.

Name: Lat.lean
Description: Category `Lat` of lattices and lattice homomorphisms (preserving `⊔` and `⊓`). Distinct from Order/Category/BddLat (with bounds) and Order/Category/DistLat.

Name: OmegaCompletePartialOrder.lean
Description: Category `ωCPO` of ω-complete partial orders and Scott-continuous maps: objects have suprema for all ω-chains, morphisms preserve these. Used in domain theory and denotational semantics. Distinct from Order/Category/CompleteLat and Order/ScottContinuity/Complete.

Name: Preord.lean
Description: Category `Preord` of preorders and monotone maps. The free cocompletion and the Yoneda lemma for preorders. Distinct from Order/Category/PartOrd (antisymmetric) and Order/Category/Lat.

Name: Semilat.lean
Description: Category `Semilat` of semilattices and semilattice homomorphisms. Distinct from Order/Category/Lat (full lattice) and Order/Category/PartOrd.

Name: PartOrd.lean
Description: Category `PartOrd` of partial orders and order-preserving maps. Limits (products as products of posets), colimits, and the forgetful functor to `Type`. Distinct from Order/Category/Preord and Order/Category/LinOrd.

Name: PartOrdEmb.lean
Description: Category `PartOrdEmb` of partial orders and order embeddings: morphisms are injective order-preserving maps with order-reflecting property. Distinct from Order/Category/PartOrd and Order/RelIso/Basic.

Name: Frm.lean
Description: Category `Frm` of frames (complete Heyting algebras) and frame homomorphisms (preserving finite meets and arbitrary joins). The localic topos. Distinct from Order/Heyting/Basic and Topology/Category/Locale.

Name: BddOrd.lean
Description: Category `BddOrd` of bounded orders: objects are `BoundedOrder`, morphisms are order-preserving maps preserving `⊥` and `⊤`. Distinct from Order/Category/PartOrd and Order/Category/BddLat.

Name: BoolAlg.lean
Description: Category `BoolAlg` of Boolean algebras and Boolean homomorphisms. Stone duality: `BoolAlg` is dually equivalent to the category of Stone spaces (compact totally disconnected Hausdorff spaces). Distinct from Order/Category/BddDistLat and Topology/Separation/Profinite.

Name: BddDistLat.lean
Description: Category `BddDistLat` of bounded distributive lattices: objects, morphisms (bounded lattice homomorphisms), and the duality with Stone spaces. Distinct from Order/Category/DistLat and Order/Category/BddLat.

Name: BddLat.lean
Description: Category `BddLat` of bounded lattices and bounded lattice homomorphisms. Distinct from Order/Category/Lat (unbounded) and Order/Category/BddDistLat.

Name: CompleteLat.lean
Description: Category `CompleteLat` of complete lattices and complete lattice homomorphisms (preserving all sups and infs). Distinct from Order/Category/Lat and Order/CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: FinBoolAlg.lean
Description: Category `FinBoolAlg` of finite Boolean algebras: equivalent to `FinPartOrdᵒᵖ` and to finite sets. Distinct from Order/Category/BoolAlg and Order/Category/FinBddDistLat.

Name: FinPartOrd.lean
Description: Category `FinPartOrd` of finite partial orders and order-preserving maps. Birkhoff duality setup. Distinct from Order/Category/PartOrd and Order/Category/FinBddDistLat.

Name: DistLat.lean
Description: Category `DistLat` of distributive lattices and lattice homomorphisms. Birkhoff duality: distributive lattices correspond to finite posets. Distinct from Order/Category/BddDistLat and Order/Category/Lat.

Name: FinBddDistLat.lean
Description: Category `FinBddDistLat` of finite bounded distributive lattices. Birkhoff duality: equivalent to the opposite of `FinPartOrd`. Distinct from Order/Category/BddDistLat and Order/Category/FinBoolAlg.

Name: Circular
Description: Circular orders, which generalize linear orders to cyclic structures such as the unit circle.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: Circular order on `ZMod n`: the cyclic order `c(a, b, c)` on `ℤ/nℤ` — going clockwise, `b` is between `a` and `c`. Used in combinatorics of cyclic sequences. Distinct from Order/Defs and Instances/ZMod.

Name: CompactlyGenerated
Description: Compactly generated lattices and algebraic lattices, where every element is the join of compact elements.

Name: Intervals.lean
Description: Compactly generated intervals: intervals `[a, b]` in a compactly generated lattice are compactly generated. Distinct from CompactlyGenerated/Basic and Order/Interval/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Compactly generated lattices: `IsCompactlyGenerated α` — every element is a join of compact elements. Proves a lattice is compactly generated iff it satisfies the ascending chain condition. Distinct from CompactlyGenerated/Intervals and Topology/Compactness.

Name: Bounds
Description: Upper and lower bounds of sets in ordered structures, including supremum, infimum, and conditions for their existence.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Bounds in lattices: `sup s` is the least upper bound in a lattice, and `IsLUB s (⨆ s)` in a complete lattice. Interaction with lattice operations. Distinct from Bounds/Basic and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: OrderIso.lean
Description: Bounds preserved by order isomorphisms: `OrderIso` sends `IsLUB s a` to `IsLUB (f '' s) (f a)`. Distinct from Bounds/Basic and Order/RelIso/Basic.

Name: Image.lean
Description: Bounds of images: `upperBounds (f '' s) = f '' (upperBounds s)` for monotone `f`, and image-bounds lemmas for `Finset.sup`. Distinct from Bounds/Basic and Order/Monotone/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Upper and lower bounds: `upperBounds s = {x | ∀ y ∈ s, y ≤ x}`, `lowerBounds s`, `IsLUB s a`, `IsGLB s a`. Core API for supremum/infimum theory. Distinct from Bounds/Defs and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions: `BddAbove s`, `BddBelow s`, `upperBounds`, `lowerBounds`. The basic predicates for bounded sets in a preorder. Distinct from Bounds/Basic (API) and ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Defs.

Name: Atoms
Description: Atoms (minimal nonzero elements) and coatoms (maximal non-top elements) in lattices, and atomistic lattices where every element is a join of atoms.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Atoms in finite lattices: in a finite lattice, every nonzero element is above an atom, and a finite atomistic lattice is isomorphic to a power set. Distinct from Order/CompactlyGenerated and Order/BooleanAlgebra.

Name: BooleanAlgebra
Description: Boolean algebras as complemented distributive lattices, with De Morgan's laws and the connection to classical propositional logic.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Boolean algebra structure of `Set α`: `Set.instBooleanAlgebra` — set-theoretic union, intersection, complement, and difference form a Boolean algebra. The canonical example of a Boolean algebra. Distinct from BooleanAlgebra/Basic and Order/UpperLower/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for Boolean algebras and related structures: `GeneralizedBooleanAlgebra` (without top), `BooleanAlgebra` (with top), and the `sdiff` (set difference) operation. Distinct from BooleanAlgebra/Basic (API) and Order/Heyting/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Boolean algebras: `BooleanAlgebra α` — a complemented distributive lattice. Core API: `compl_sup_eq_top`, `compl_inf_eq_bot`, `inf_compl_eq_bot`, De Morgan laws, and the Boolean ring structure. Distinct from BooleanAlgebra/Defs and BooleanAlgebra/Set.

Name: BoundedOrder
Description: Orders with a global top and bottom element, and the typeclasses formalizing them.

Name: Monotone.lean
Description: Monotone maps preserving bounds: `OrderHomClass` maps that also send `⊥` to `⊥` and `⊤` to `⊤`. Distinct from BoundedOrder/Basic and Order/Monotone/Defs.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Bounded lattices: `BoundedLattice α = Lattice α ∧ BoundedOrder α`. Properties of `⊤` and `⊥` as the greatest and least elements in lattice context. Distinct from BoundedOrder/Basic and Order/CompleteLattice/Defs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bounded orders: `BoundedOrder α` — an order with `⊤` (top) and `⊥` (bottom). Core API: `le_top`, `bot_le`, `top_unique`, `OrderTop` and `OrderBot` instances. Distinct from BoundedOrder/Lattice and Order/Defs.

Name: Extension
Description: Order extension theorems: every partial order extends to a total order (Szpilrajn's theorem) and related results.

Name: Well.lean
Description: Well-order extension: every partial order extends to a well-order. Distinct from Extension/Linear and Order/WellFounded files.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Linear extension of a partial order: Szpilrajn's theorem — every partial order extends to a total order. Distinct from Extension/Well and Order/Category/LinOrd.

Name: Filter
Description: Filters on sets and ordered structures: ultrafilters, the filter of neighborhoods in topology, and limit theory via filters.

Name: Germ
Description: Germs of functions at a filter: the quotient of functions by the filter's a.e. equivalence relation (f ~ g iff {x | f(x) = g(x)} ∈ F). Filter germs extend from ultrafilters (where germs form a field) and are used in nonstandard analysis. Distinct from Topology/Germ (germs at a topological point).

Name: OrderedMonoid.lean
Description: Ordered monoid structure on germs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Filter germs: `Filter.Germ l α`.

Name: Ultrafilter
Description: Ultrafilters: maximal filters (equivalently, filters where every set or its complement is in the filter). The ultrafilter lemma (every filter extends to an ultrafilter, provable from AC). Stone-Čech compactification as the space of ultrafilters on ℕ. The ultrafilter monad. Distinct from Filter/Bases (general filter bases) — ultrafilters are the extreme case.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Ultrafilter definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ultrafilter API.

Name: AtTopBot
Description: The filters atTop and atBot on an ordered type: atTop = {S | ∃ a, ∀ x ≥ a, x ∈ S} (the cofinite filter when the order has no top), and atBot dually. Used for limits at +∞ and -∞ in analysis. The filter atTop is the neighborhood filter of +∞ in the extended reals. Distinct from Filter/Bases (general filter bases).

Name: Interval.lean
Description: AtTop and intervals.

Name: Map.lean
Description: AtTop under maps.

Name: Floor.lean
Description: AtTop and floor functions.

Name: Group.lean
Description: AtTop in ordered groups.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: AtTop and modular arithmetic.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: AtTop in ordered rings.

Name: Tendsto.lean
Description: Tendsto to AtTop.

Name: Monoid.lean
Description: AtTop in ordered monoids.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: AtTop in product orders.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: AtTop for Finsets.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators and AtTop filter.

Name: CompleteLattice.lean
Description: AtTop in complete lattices.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: AtTop filter in Archimedean ordered groups.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: AtTop and AtBot filter API.

Name: CountablyGenerated.lean
Description: AtTop filter is countably generated.

Name: Field.lean
Description: AtTop in ordered fields.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: AtTop in finite ordered sets.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: AtTop/AtBot definitions.

Name: Disjoint.lean
Description: AtTop and AtBot are disjoint.

Name: Bases
Description: Filter bases and the filter generated by a basis: a collection B is a filter basis if it is nonempty and directed downward (∀ A B ∈ B, ∃ C ∈ B, C ⊆ A ∩ B). The generated filter is {S | ∃ B ∈ ℬ, B ⊆ S}. Used to describe neighborhood filters in topology and to specify filters efficiently. Distinct from Filter/Ultrafilter (maximal filters).

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite filter bases.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Filter bases.

Name: NAry.lean
Description: n-ary filter operations: `Filter.map₂ f l₁ l₂` for binary operations, and generalisation to `n`-ary. Used in continuity of binary operations on filtered spaces. Distinct from Filter/Prod and Filter/Map.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Pushforward and pullback of filters: `Filter.map f l = l.map f` (pushforward) and `Filter.comap f l` (pullback). Proves the adjunction `map f l ≤ m ↔ l ≤ comap f m`. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Filter/Lift.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Filters on pi types: `Filter.pi l` — the product filter on `Π i, αᵢ`, generated by sets of the form `{x | x i ∈ sᵢ}` for finitely many `i`. Distinct from Filter/Prod and Filter/Basic.

Name: Partial.lean
Description: Partial functions and filters: filters on the domain of definition of a partial function, and `Filter.restrict` to a set. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Order/Filter/Extr.

Name: Ker.lean
Description: Filter kernel: `Filter.ker l = ⋂ U ∈ l, U` — the intersection of all sets in a filter. In a T1 topology, `ker (nhds x) = {x}`. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Compactness/NhdsKer.

Name: IsBounded.lean
Description: Bounded filters: `Filter.IsBounded (≤) f` — the filter `f` has a bounded set, i.e., `∃ b, {x | x ≤ b} ∈ f`. Used in `limsup`/`liminf` theory. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Order/LiminfLimsup.

Name: ListTraverse.lean
Description: Traversal of lists with filter: `Filter.Tendsto` for list operations, and `Filter.map` applied to list traversal. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Combinatorics/List files.

Name: Lift.lean
Description: Filter lifts: `Filter.lift l f` — applying a set-to-filter function `f` to each element of `l` and taking the sup. Used in the definition of `Filter.comap`. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Filter/Map.

Name: Tendsto.lean
Description: `Filter.Tendsto f l m` (convergence of `f` along `l` to `m`): the key convergence concept. Proves `Tendsto.comp`, monotonicity, and the equivalence with ε-δ definitions. Distinct from Filter/Basic (definitions) and Topology/Defs/Filter.

Name: Subsingleton.lean
Description: Filters and subsingleton types: `Filter.unique` on a `Subsingleton` type, and degenerate filter results. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Order/Defs.

Name: ZeroAndBoundedAtFilter.lean
Description: Functions vanishing or bounded along a filter: `ZeroAtFilter l f` (`f x → 0` along `l`) and `BoundedAtFilter l f` (eventually bounded). The Banach space of bounded-at-filter functions. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Analysis/ContinuousMap/ZeroAtInfty.

Name: TendstoCofinite.lean
Description: `Filter.Tendsto f Filter.cofinite l` — `f x → l` along the cofinite filter, meaning the preimage of any set outside `l` is finite. Used for sequences converging to infinity. Distinct from Filter/Cofinite and Filter/Tendsto.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product filter: `Filter.prod l m = Filter.map Prod.mk (l ×ˢ m)` (filter on `α × β`). Proves `Filter.Tendsto (Prod.mk · b) l m ↔ Tendsto (·) l (Filter.map (· b) m)`. Distinct from Filter/Pi and Filter/NAry.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on filters: `Filter.map₂` for arithmetic operations `(l₁ * l₂)(f) ↔ ∃ A ∈ l₁, B ∈ l₂, A * B ⊆ f`. The filter as a topological monoid. Distinct from Filter/NAry and Topology/Algebra/Monoid.

Name: SmallSets.lean
Description: Small sets filter: `Filter.smallSets l` — the filter of sets that are eventually small (covered by arbitrarily small open sets). Used in bornologies and uniform spaces. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/UniformSpace/Basic.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Filters and ring structure: the filter of a ring as a subring of `Filter(Ring)`, and `Filter.Tendsto` for ring operations. Distinct from Filter/Pointwise and Topology/Algebra/UniformRing.

Name: CountableSeparatingOn.lean
Description: Countably separating filters: a filter that separates points of a set via countably many filter elements. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Separation/CountableSeparatingOn.

Name: CountableInter.lean
Description: Countably-closed filters: `CountableInterFilter l` — filters closed under countable intersections. Proves the cofinite filter on `ℕ` is countably closed, and connects to σ-algebras. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Filter/CardinalInter.

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Curried filters: `Filter.curry l₁ l₂` — the filter of sets `s` such that `{x | {y | (x,y) ∈ s} ∈ l₂} ∈ l₁`. Used in iterated limits. Distinct from Filter/Prod and Filter/Basic.

Name: CountablyGenerated.lean
Description: Countably generated filters: `Filter.IsCountablyGenerated l` — the filter has a countable filter base. Proves that first-countable topologies have countably generated neighbourhood filters. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Defs/Filter.

Name: CardinalInter.lean
Description: Filters closed under intersections of small cardinality: `Filter.IsCardinallyNormalFilter` — filters closed under κ-many intersections for a cardinal κ. Used for ω-closed and κ-complete filters. Distinct from Filter/CountableInter and Filter/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core filter API: `Filter.map`, `Filter.comap`, `Filter.prod`, filter bases, `Filter.tendsto`, `Filter.Eventually`, `Filter.Frequently`. The main workhorse file for the Filter folder; most topology files import this.

Name: Cofinite.lean
Description: Cofinite filter: `Filter.cofinite` — the filter of sets with finite complement. Proves `tendsto_atTop` characterisations and that the cofinite filter is the smallest nonprincipal filter on an infinite type. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Filter/Cocardinal.

Name: Cocardinal.lean
Description: Cocardinality filters: `Filter.cocardinal κ` — the filter of sets whose complement has cardinality < κ. Includes the cofinite filter as the case κ = ω. Distinct from Filter/Cofinite and Filter/CardinalInter.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite filters and filters on finite types: on a finite type every filter is principal, and finiteness results for filter bases. Distinct from Filter/Cofinite and Filter/Basic.

Name: FilterProduct.lean
Description: Ultrafilter products and hyperreals: `Filter.Product l f` — the ultrafilter product of structures, used to construct nonstandard models. Distinct from Filter/Prod (cartesian product of filters) and Analysis/Real/Hyperreal.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Interval-based filters: the filter `Filter.Ioi a` (filter of sets containing `(a, ∞)`) and neighbourhoods in interval terms. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Order/LeftRight.

Name: IndicatorFunction.lean
Description: Indicator functions and filters: convergence of indicator functions `1_A` along a filter. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Algebra/Indicator.

Name: ENNReal.lean
Description: Filters and `ℝ≥0∞`-valued functions: `Filter.limsup` and `Filter.liminf` for `ℝ≥0∞`-valued functions, and the monotone convergence theorem for filter limits. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Analysis/SpecificLimits/Normed.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of filters: `Filter α` — an upward-closed collection of sets closed under finite intersection. Contains `Filter.principal`, `Filter.atTop`, `Filter.atBot`, and the `CompleteLattice` on filters. Distinct from Filter/Basic (API).

Name: Extr.lean
Description: Filter-based extrema: `Filter.IsExtrFilter f l x` — `x` is a local extremum of `f` relative to a filter. Used in the Lagrange multiplier and derivative tests. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/Order/LocalExtr.

Name: EventuallyConst.lean
Description: Eventually constant functions: `Filter.EventuallyEq` and `Filter.EventuallyConst f l` — `f` is eventually equal to a constant along `l`. Distinct from Filter/Basic and Topology/LocallyConstant/Basic.

Name: Fin
Description: Order-theoretic results specific to Fin n, the canonical finite linearly ordered type with n elements.

Name: SuccAboveOrderIso.lean
Description: Order isomorphism `Fin n ≃o {i : Fin (n+1) | i ≠ j}` given by `Fin.succAbove j`: inserting `j` into the ordering. Distinct from Fin/Basic and Order/RelIso/Basic.

Name: Tuple.lean
Description: Order on `Fin`-indexed tuples: the lexicographic and product orders on `Fin n → α`, and `Fin.insertNth`, `Fin.removeNth` as order-preserving operations. Distinct from Fin/Basic and CompleteLattice/PiLex.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Finsets indexed by `Fin n`: `Finset.fin n` and related operations for `Fin`-indexed collections. Distinct from Fin/Basic and Combinatorics/Finset.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order properties of `Fin n`: `Fin n` as a `LinearOrder`, the unique order-preserving bijection `Fin n ≃o Finset.range n`, and `Fin.strictMono`. Distinct from Fin/Tuple and Fin/Finset.

Name: Clamp.lean
Description: Clamping in `Fin n`: `Fin.clamp m i = min (n-1) (max 0 i)` — projection onto `{0, …, n-1}`. Distinct from Fin/Basic and Topology/Order/ProjIcc.

Name: Defs
Description: Core definitions of order theory: the Preorder, PartialOrder, and LinearOrder typeclasses and their basic properties.

Name: Unbundled.lean
Description: Unbundled order relations: `Preorder`, `LE`, `LT`, and the bare axioms as separate typeclasses without the `Eq` axiom. Used for the lowest-level order hierarchy. Distinct from Defs/PartialOrder.

Name: PartialOrder.lean
Description: Partial order definitions: `PartialOrder α` — a reflexive antisymmetric transitive relation. Distinct from Defs/LinearOrder and Defs/Unbundled.

Name: LinearOrder.lean
Description: Linear order definitions: `LinearOrder α` — a total order, `min`/`max`, decidability instances. Distinct from Defs/PartialOrder and ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Defs.

Name: CompleteLattice
Description: Complete lattices where every subset has a supremum and infimum. Includes the fixed-point theorems of Knaster-Tarski.

Name: MulticoequalizerDiagram.lean
Description: Multicoequalizer diagrams in complete lattices: the colimit of a diagram in a complete lattice computed as a join. Used in sheaf theory and topos theory. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic and CategoryTheory colimit files.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Miscellaneous lemmas for complete lattices: `iSup_iSup_eq_left`, monotonicity lemmas for `iSup`/`iInf`, and order-theoretic identities not in Basic. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: SetLike.lean
Description: Complete lattice structure on `SetLike` types: submodules, subalgebras, and ideals form complete lattices with intersection as meet and span-of-union as join. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic and Submodule lattice files.

Name: PiLex.lean
Description: Lex order on pi types with complete lattice: the lexicographic complete lattice on `Π i, α i`. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic and Order/Fin/Tuple.

Name: Group.lean
Description: Complete lattice and group interactions: distributivity of group operations over `⨆`/`⨅`, and order-group instances (e.g., `OrdAddCommGroup`). Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic and Order/BoundedOrder/Lattice.

Name: Chain.lean
Description: Chains in complete lattices: `Chain c` (a totally ordered subset), `Set.IsChain`, and Zorn's lemma formulation using chains. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic and Order/Preorder/Chain.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Complete lattices: `CompleteLattice α` with `⨆ s` (arbitrary sup) and `⨅ s` (arbitrary inf). Core API: `le_iSup`, `iInf_le`, `iSup_eq_iInf_compl`, and the frame law `a ⊓ ⨆ s = ⨆ (a ⊓ ·)`. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Defs (definition) and ConditionallyCompleteLattice.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Complete lattice operations on finite sets: `Finset.sup s f = ⨆ i ∈ s, f i` and its interaction with `iSup`/`iInf`. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic and Order/Bounds/Lattice.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions of complete lattices: `SupSet`, `InfSet`, `iSup`, `iInf`, and the `CompleteLattice` typeclass. Split from Basic for import hierarchy reasons. Distinct from CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: ConditionallyCompleteLattice
Description: Conditionally complete lattices where every nonempty bounded subset has a supremum, modelling ℝ and ℝ-like structures.

Name: Group.lean
Description: Conditionally complete lattice ordered groups: `ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrderedAddCommGroup`, and the `ℝ` instance. Distinct from ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Basic and CompleteLattice/Group.

Name: Indexed.lean
Description: Indexed sup/inf in conditionally complete lattices: `ciSup` and `ciInf` for index types, and their interaction with boundedness. Distinct from ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Basic and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Finite operations in conditionally complete lattices: `Finset.sup` and `Finset.inf` in a `ConditionallyCompleteLattice`. Distinct from ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Basic and CompleteLattice/Finset.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Conditionally complete lattices: `ConditionallyCompleteLattice α` has `sSup` and `sInf` for nonempty bounded sets. Proves `sSup_le`, `le_csSup`. The main API file. Distinct from ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Defs and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions: `ConditionallyCompleteLattice`, `ConditionallyCompleteLinearOrder` (with `min`/`max` and conditionally complete order), and `sInf`/`sSup`. Distinct from ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: ConditionallyCompletePartialOrder
Description: Conditionally complete partial orders, a weaker variant of conditionally complete lattices where joins may only exist for directed sets.

Name: Indexed.lean
Description: Indexed suprema in conditionally complete partial orders: `iSup_of_directed` and its properties. Distinct from ConditionallyCompletePartialOrder/Basic and CompleteLattice/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions: `ConditionallyCompletePartialOrder` — a partial order in which every directed subset has a supremum. Distinct from ConditionallyCompletePartialOrder/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Conditionally complete partial orders: `ConditionallyCompletePartialOrder α` — directed sets have suprema. Includes the Scott topology as a natural structure. Distinct from ConditionallyCompleteLattice/Basic and Order/ScottContinuity.

Name: PrimeSeparator.lean
Description: Prime separators in lattices: a prime filter separating two elements, related to prime ideals in distributive lattices.

Name: PropInstances.lean
Description: Order instances for Prop: the lattice structure on propositions with And as meet, Or as join, and False/True as bottom/top.

Name: PrimeIdeal.lean
Description: Prime ideals in lattices: ideals I where a meet b in I implies a in I or b in I.

Name: PFilter.lean
Description: Proper filters (PFilter): filters not containing the bottom element, ordered by the reverse inclusion order.

Name: PiLex.lean
Description: The lexicographic order on Pi types: f <= g iff f = g everywhere before the first discrepancy.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient orders: the order induced on a quotient type by the relation on the original type.

Name: RelSeries.lean
Description: Relation series: chains a_0 < a_1 < ... < a_n in a preorder, used to define Krull dimension.

Name: Restriction.lean
Description: Order restriction to a subtype: the induced order on a subset and its properties.

Name: RelClasses.lean
Description: Relational class typeclasses: IsRefl, IsTrans, IsAntisymm, IsTrichotomous, and their combinations.

Name: Radical.lean
Description: Radicals in lattices: the meet of all primes above an element, generalizing the radical of an ideal.

Name: README.md
Description: Overview of the Order directory: describes the organization of order theory in Mathlib.

Name: PartialSups.lean
Description: Partial suprema: the monotone sequence of suprema partialSups f n = join of f(k) for k <= n.

Name: NonemptyFiniteChains.lean
Description: Nonempty finite chains: orders where every nonempty finite set has a maximum and minimum.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Order notation: the le, lt, ge, gt, join, meet, bottom, top symbols and their precedences.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Natural number order: specific order-theoretic lemmas for N not covered by the general order theory.

Name: MinMax.lean
Description: Min and max functions: min a b = if a <= b then a else b, with their algebraic properties.

Name: ModularLattice.lean
Description: Modular lattices: lattices satisfying the modular law, with the Jordan-Holder theorem.

Name: Nucleus.lean
Description: Nuclei on frames: idempotent inflationary meet-preserving maps, used to construct subframes and quotient frames.

Name: OrderIsoNat.lean
Description: Order isomorphisms with N: characterizations of when a well-ordered type is isomorphic to N.

Name: Part.lean
Description: The order on Part alpha (partial values): the information order where defined values are greater than undefined.

Name: OrderDual.lean
Description: The order dual: reversing <= to >=, with the dual of a lattice/filter/ideal and the DualOrder typeclass.

Name: OmegaCompletePartialOrder.lean
Description: Omega-complete partial orders (omegacpo): dcpos with a least element and completeness for omega-chains.

Name: OrdContinuous.lean
Description: Order-continuous maps: maps preserving directed suprema, and their role in the Scott topology.

Name: SaddlePoint.lean
Description: Saddle points of functions on products of ordered sets: minimax theorems and their order-theoretic formulation.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift instances for ordered types: lifting order structures to higher universes.

Name: WellFounded.lean
Description: Well-founded orders: the WellFounded typeclass, well-founded recursion and induction.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tags for order theory: AsLinearOrder and similar wrappers adding order structure to a type.

Name: TeichmullerTukey.lean
Description: The Teichmuller-Tukey lemma: a maximal element principle equivalent to the axiom of choice.

Name: TransfiniteIteration.lean
Description: Transfinite iteration of a function: ordinal-indexed iterates and their fixed points.

Name: WellFoundedSet.lean
Description: Well-founded sets and partial well-orders: sets with no infinite strictly descending sequences.

Name: Zorn.lean
Description: Zorn's lemma: every chain-bounded nonempty poset has a maximal element, with applications to existence proofs.

Name: ZornAtoms.lean
Description: Zorn's lemma applied to atoms: every element of an atomic complete lattice is above some atom.

Name: WithBotTop.lean
Description: Adding a bottom or top element: the WithBot and WithTop constructions extending a poset with a new extremal element.

Name: WellQuasiOrder.lean
Description: Well-quasi-orders (WQO): quasi-orders with no infinite antichains or descending chains, with Dickson's lemma.

Name: WithBot.lean
Description: WithBot: adding a bottom element to an ordered type, with the resulting typeclass instances.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Order synonyms: using one type in place of another for different typeclass instances without creating a new type.

Name: SetIsMax.lean
Description: Maximal elements of sets: the condition for an element to be maximal in a set under a given order.

Name: SetNotation.lean
Description: Set-valued supremum and infimum: sSup and sInf notation for suprema over sets in complete lattices.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Order theory for sets: monotone sets, antitone sets, and the lattice structure on the powerset.

Name: ScottContinuity.lean
Description: Scott-continuous functions: maps preserving directed suprema, the morphisms of omega-cpos.

Name: SemiconjSup.lean
Description: Semiconjugate order-preserving maps: two maps f and g semiconjugated by h, used in dynamics.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Shrinking orders: orderings on which every element has finitely many predecessors.

Name: SupIndep.lean
Description: Supremum-independent families: families where each member is disjoint (in the lattice sense) from the join of the rest.

Name: SymmDiff.lean
Description: Symmetric difference in lattices: a triangle b = (a join b) meet (a join b)^c, axiomatized in Boolean algebras.

Name: SupClosed.lean
Description: Supremum-closed sets: subsets of a lattice closed under finite joins, and their generated closure.

Name: Sublattice.lean
Description: Sublattices: subsets closed under both meet and join, with the induced lattice structure.

Name: Sublocale.lean
Description: Sublocales: quotients of a frame (the pointfree analogue of a subspace of a topological space).

Name: CompleteBooleanAlgebra.lean
Description: Complete Boolean algebras: complete lattices that are also Boolean algebras, with the infinite distributive laws.

Name: CompleteLatticeIntervals.lean
Description: Complete lattices of intervals: the lattice of intervals in a complete lattice with set inclusion.

Name: Compare.lean
Description: Comparison of elements: the Decidable version of le/lt comparisons and trichotomy.

Name: Cofinal.lean
Description: Cofinal sets in an order: sets such that every element is eventually below some member, used in cofinality theory.

Name: Comparable.lean
Description: Comparability of elements: the property of two elements being related by the order, and total orders.

Name: CompletePartialOrder.lean
Description: Complete partial orders (CPO): posets with a least element and joins of directed sets, used in domain theory.

Name: Copy.lean
Description: Order copy: transferring an order structure along a bijection, used for definitional equality tricks.

Name: CountableDenseLinearOrder.lean
Description: Countable dense linear orders: the theory of (Q, <=), the unique countable dense linear order without endpoints.

Name: Concept.lean
Description: Formal concepts: pairs (extent, intent) arising from a binary relation, forming a complete lattice via a Galois connection.

Name: CompleteSublattice.lean
Description: Complete sublattices: subsets of a complete lattice closed under all joins and meets.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: The Dedekind-MacNeille completion: the smallest complete lattice containing a given poset.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Closure operators: inflationary idempotent monotone maps, generating closed sets and closure systems.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic order theory definitions: le, lt, the monotone/antitone trichotomy, and utility lemmas for all ordered types.

Name: Birkhoff.lean
Description: Birkhoff's representation theorem: every finite distributive lattice is isomorphic to the lattice of downsets of a finite poset.

Name: Atoms.lean
Description: Atoms and coatoms in lattices: minimal nonzero and maximal non-top elements, atomistic and coatomistic lattices.

Name: Antichain.lean
Description: Antichains: sets of pairwise incomparable elements, with Dilworth's theorem.

Name: Antisymmetrization.lean
Description: Antisymmetrization of a preorder: the quotient poset obtained by identifying elements with a <= b and b <= a.

Name: BooleanGenerators.lean
Description: Generators of Boolean algebras: sets that freely generate a Boolean subalgebra.

Name: BourbakiWitt.lean
Description: The Bourbaki-Witt fixed point theorem: every inflationary map on a chain-complete poset has a fixed point.

Name: Circular.lean
Description: Circular orders: ternary relations axiomatizing cyclic betweenness on the circle.

Name: Bounded.lean
Description: Bounded orders and their instances: types with both top and bottom, and the BoundedOrder typeclass.

Name: Booleanisation.lean
Description: Booleanisation of a distributive lattice: freely adjoining complements to make it Boolean.

Name: BooleanSubalgebra.lean
Description: Boolean subalgebras: sublattices that are also closed under complementation.

Name: Cover.lean
Description: Cover relations: a covers b when a < b with nothing strictly between them, generating the Hasse diagram.

Name: KonigLemma.lean
Description: Konig's lemma: an infinite finitely-branching tree has an infinite path, used in compactness arguments.

Name: KrullDimension.lean
Description: Krull dimension of an ordered set: the supremum of lengths of strict chains, used for rings and topological spaces.

Name: JordanHolder.lean
Description: The Jordan-Holder theorem for ordered sets: all maximal chains between two elements have the same length in semimodular lattices.

Name: IsNormal.lean
Description: Normal elements in lattices: elements satisfying self-adjointness conditions in frames and Heyting algebras.

Name: Iterate.lean
Description: Iteration of order-preserving maps: monotone sequences obtained by iterating a map from below or above.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Core lattice definitions: the SemilatticeSup and SemilatticeInf typeclasses and their interaction.

Name: Max.lean
Description: Maximal and minimal elements: the IsMax, IsMin predicates and their characterizations.

Name: Minimal.lean
Description: Minimal and maximal elements in a set: the definition and existence results via Zorn's lemma.

Name: LiminfLimsup.lean
Description: Limit inferior and limit superior in ordered types: liminf and limsup of sequences and filters.

Name: LatticeIntervals.lean
Description: The interval lattice [a, b]: the set of elements between a and b with the induced lattice structure.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: Lexicographic orders on products and sums: the Lex wrapper type and its order instances.

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Irreducible elements in lattices: elements that cannot be written as a meet (or join) of strictly larger elements.

Name: Disjoint.lean
Description: Disjointness in lattices: a and b are disjoint if their meet is bottom, with complemented elements.

Name: Disjointed.lean
Description: Disjointification: converting a sequence of sets into a disjoint sequence with the same union.

Name: DirSupClosed.lean
Description: Directed-sup-closed sets: sets closed under directed suprema, the Scott-open complements.

Name: Directed.lean
Description: Directed sets and nets: sets where every finite subset has an upper bound, used for general limits.

Name: DirectedInverseSystem.lean
Description: Directed inverse systems: inverse systems indexed by a directed poset, used for p-adic and profinite constructions.

Name: FixedPoints.lean
Description: Fixed points of monotone maps on complete lattices: Knaster-Tarski theorem and least/greatest fixed points.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in ordered sets: directed downward-closed subsets, the ideal completion, and prime ideals.

Name: InitialSeg.lean
Description: Initial segments and order embeddings: order embeddings whose image is a downward-closed initial segment.

Name: Height.lean
Description: Height of an element in a poset: the supremum of lengths of chains below it.

Name: GameAdd.lean
Description: Game addition for well-founded orders: combining two well-founded games for use in ordinal arithmetic.

Name: Grade.lean
Description: Graded orders: posets with a rank function compatible with the cover relation.

Name: NumberTheory
Description: Formalizes integers, primes, Diophantine equations, modular arithmetic, quadratic reciprocity, and zeta-functions. Used to investigate properties of whole numbers, solve congruences, and understand distribution of primes. Relied on by cryptology, coding theory, algebraic geometry, and automorphic forms.

Name: MulChar
Description: Multiplicative characters on finite rings and monoids, the building blocks for Dirichlet characters, Gauss sums, and Jacobi sums.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for multiplicative characters: properties of characters on specific rings, interaction with GCD, and the conductor. Distinct from MulChar/Basic and DirichletCharacter/Bounds.

Name: Duality.lean
Description: Duality of multiplicative characters: the character group `Hom(Rˣ, ℂˣ)` and Pontryagin duality for finite groups applied to `(ℤ/nℤ)ˣ`. Distinct from MulChar/Basic and DirichletCharacter/Orthogonality.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multiplicative characters: `MulChar R S` — ring homomorphisms `Rˣ → Sˣ` extended by 0. Definition, the group structure, and the trivial character. Distinct from DirichletCharacter/Basic (special case mod n) and LegendreSymbol/ZModChar.

Name: NumberField
Description: Number fields (finite extensions of ℚ) and their rings of integers: integral bases, discriminants, and the geometry of numbers approach to finiteness results.

Name: Ideal
Description: Ideals of the ring of integers of a number field: unique factorization into prime ideals (Dedekind domain structure), the norm N(p) = |O_K/p| of a prime ideal, and the factorization of rational primes in O_K. The ideal class group and the class number formula. Distinct from RingTheory/DedekindDomain (the abstract theory) — this is the number field specialization.

Name: KummerDedekind.lean
Description: Kummer–Dedekind theorem for factoring primes.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ideal theory in number fields.

Name: Asymptotics.lean
Description: Asymptotics of ideal counting.

Name: InfinitePlace
Description: Infinite places (archimedean embeddings) of a number field: the real embeddings σ : K → ℝ and complex embeddings σ : K → ℂ, with complex conjugate pairs identified. The number of real (r₁) and complex (r₂) places. The product formula ∏_v |x|_v = 1 for x ≠ 0. Used in the canonical embedding and in the unit theorem.

Name: Ramification.lean
Description: Ramification at infinite places.

Name: TotallyRealComplex.lean
Description: Totally real and totally complex number fields.

Name: Embeddings.lean
Description: Embeddings at infinite places.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Infinite places of a number field.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion at infinite places.

Name: Units
Description: Units of the ring of integers O_K×: Dirichlet's unit theorem (O_K× ≅ μ(K) × ℤ^{r₁+r₂-1} where μ(K) is the group of roots of unity). The regulator (covolume of the unit lattice). The structure of the unit group as a finitely generated abelian group. Used in the class number formula.

Name: Regulator.lean
Description: Regulator of a number field.

Name: DirichletTheorem.lean
Description: Dirichlet's unit theorem: `𝒪_K^× ≅ μ_K × ℤʳ⁺ˢ⁻¹`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Units of the ring of integers: `𝒪_K^×` and Dirichlet's unit theorem.

Name: Discriminant
Description: The discriminant of a number field: disc(K) = disc(O_K/ℤ) = det(Tr(bᵢbⱼ)). The Minkowski bound: every ideal class contains an ideal of norm ≤ Mₙ √|disc|. The Minkowski lower bound |disc(K)| ≥ (π/4)^s (n^n/n!) (which shows only ℚ has discriminant 1). Used in bounding the class number.

Name: Different.lean
Description: Different ideal and discriminant.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Discriminant definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Discriminant of a number field.

Name: CanonicalEmbedding
Description: The canonical embedding of a number field into ℝʳ × ℂˢ: the map sending α to all its archimedean embeddings (r real embeddings and s pairs of complex conjugate embeddings). The image is a lattice. Used in the geometry of numbers approach to the class number formula and Minkowski's bound.

Name: NormLeOne.lean
Description: Norm ≤ 1 condition in canonical embedding.

Name: PolarCoord.lean
Description: Polar coordinates in the canonical embedding.

Name: FundamentalCone.lean
Description: Fundamental cone in the canonical embedding.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Canonical embedding of a number field: `K ↪ ℝʳ × ℂˢ`.

Name: ConvexBody.lean
Description: Minkowski's theorem on convex bodies: a symmetric convex body in ℝⁿ of volume > 2ⁿ contains a nonzero point of any full-rank lattice. Applied here to the canonical embedding of a number field to prove that every ideal class contains an ideal of bounded norm (the Minkowski bound), giving finiteness of the ideal class group. The geometric bridge between lattice theory and algebraic number theory.

Name: Completion
Description: Completions of number fields at finite places: the p-adic completion K_p of a number field K at a prime p of its ring of integers. The local ring of integers O_{K_p} and the residue field. Distinct from NumberTheory/Padics (the specific case K = ℚ) — this handles general number fields.

Name: LiesOverInstances.lean
Description: LiesOver instances for completions.

Name: Ramification.lean
Description: Ramification in completions.

Name: FinitePlace.lean
Description: Completion at a finite place.

Name: InfinitePlace.lean
Description: Completion at an infinite place.

Name: Cyclotomic
Description: Cyclotomic number fields ℚ(ζₙ): their ring of integers ℤ[ζₙ], discriminant, and ramification (only primes p | n ramify). The Galois group Gal(ℚ(ζₙ)/ℚ) ≅ (ℤ/nℤ)×. Used in Kummer theory, class field theory, and the proof of quadratic reciprocity. Distinct from the FieldTheory/Cyclotomic folder (algebraic properties without number-field specifics).

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideal theory in cyclotomic fields.

Name: PID.lean
Description: When cyclotomic rings are PIDs.

Name: Three.lean
Description: ℤ[ζ₃] specific results.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cyclotomic number fields.

Name: Embeddings.lean
Description: Embeddings of cyclotomic fields.

Name: Galois.lean
Description: Galois group of cyclotomic fields.

Name: House.lean
Description: House of an algebraic number: `house α` — the maximum of the absolute values of the conjugates of `α`. Related to the Mahler measure and Weil height. Distinct from Height/NumberField and NumberField/Norm.

Name: FractionalIdeal.lean
Description: Fractional ideals of a number field: `FractionalIdeal 𝒪_K K` — the group of nonzero fractional ideals, the class group, and the unique factorisation into prime ideals. Distinct from NumberField/ClassNumber and RingTheory/FractionalIdeal.

Name: FinitePlaces.lean
Description: Finite places of a number field: the finite primes `v` of `K`, their completions `K_v`, and the non-archimedean absolute values. Distinct from NumberField/AdeleRing and Padics/HeightOneSpectrum.

Name: ProductFormula.lean
Description: Product formula for number fields: `∏_v |x|_v = 1` for `x ∈ Kˣ`, over all places. Generalises `|n| * ∏_p |n|_p = 1` for `n ∈ ℚˣ`. Distinct from NumberField/AdeleRing and Height/NumberField.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: Norm and trace of number fields: the field norm `N_{K/ℚ}(x) = ∏ σ(x)` and trace `Tr(x) = ∑ σ(x)`, their integrality, and the discriminant. Distinct from NumberField/Basic and RamificationInertia/Basic.

Name: InfiniteAdeleRing.lean
Description: Infinite adele ring (archimedean part): `NumberField.infiniteAdeleRing K = ∏_{v | ∞} K_v` — the product of all archimedean completions. Distinct from NumberField/AdeleRing and NumberField/CMField.

Name: ClassNumber.lean
Description: Class number of a number field: `ClassGroup.card 𝒪_K` is finite (Minkowski bound). The Picard group / ideal class group. Distinct from ClassNumber/Finite (abstract) and NumberField/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic number field theory: `NumberField K` — a finite extension of ℚ, the ring of integers `𝒪_K`, ramification, and the norm. Distinct from NumberField/ClassNumber and NumberField/Norm.

Name: AdeleRing.lean
Description: Adele ring of a number field: `NumberField.adeleRing K` — the restricted product `∏'_v K_v` over all completions. Distinct from NumberField/InfiniteAdeleRing and NumberField/ProductFormula.

Name: EquivReindex.lean
Description: Reindexing equivalences for number fields: technical equivalences between different indexing of embeddings and places. Distinct from NumberField/Basic and NumberField/FractionalIdeal.

Name: DedekindZeta.lean
Description: Dedekind zeta function: `NumberField.dedekindZeta K s = ∑_{I ⊆ 𝒪_K} (N I)^{-s}` — the zeta function of a number field. Analytic continuation and the class number formula. Distinct from LSeries/RiemannZeta and NumberField/ClassNumber.

Name: CMField.lean
Description: CM fields: a number field `K` is a CM field if it is a totally imaginary quadratic extension of a totally real field. CM type and reflex field. Distinct from NumberField/Basic and Cyclotomic/Basic.

Name: ModularForms
Description: Modular forms: holomorphic functions on the upper half-plane satisfying a weight-k transformation law under SL₂(ℤ) and congruence subgroups.

Name: JacobiTheta
Description: Jacobi theta functions θ(τ) = ∑_{n∈ℤ} e^{iπn²τ}: their modular transformation properties (functional equations under τ ↦ τ+1 and τ ↦ -1/τ), the Jacobi triple product identity, and the connection to lattice sums and quadratic forms. Used in the proof that sums of four squares can be counted via theta series.

Name: OneVariable.lean
Description: One-variable Jacobi theta: `θ(τ) = ∑ e^{πin²τ}`.

Name: TwoVariable.lean
Description: Two-variable Jacobi theta function.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds on the Jacobi theta function.

Name: Manifold.lean
Description: Jacobi theta as a modular form on a manifold.

Name: EisensteinSeries
Description: Eisenstein series G_k(τ) = ∑_{(m,n)≠(0,0)} (mτ+n)^{-k} for even k ≥ 4: the absolute convergence, the Fourier expansion (involving divisor sums σ_{k-1}(n)), and the normalization E_k = G_k/2ζ(k). The algebra of modular forms is generated by E_4 and E_6. Distinct from JacobiTheta (a different family of modular forms).

Name: E2
Description: The quasi-modular form E₂(τ): the weight-2 Eisenstein series G₂(τ) = ∑'_{(m,n)≠(0,0)} (mτ+n)^{-2}, which is not modular but satisfies the transformation law E₂ ∣₂ γ = E₂ − C · D₂(γ) for a computable correction term D₂. Proves the quasi-modularity transformation law completely. Used in the Ramanujan differential equations expressing dEₖ/dτ in terms of E₂ and lower Eisenstein series. Distinct from EisensteinSeries (the general Eisenstein series formalization) and ModularForms (which handles genuine modular forms).

Name: Summable.lean
Description: Summability of E₂.

Name: Transform.lean
Description: Transformation formula for E₂.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: E₂ definitions: the weight-2 Eisenstein series.

Name: MDifferentiable.lean
Description: Differentiability of E₂.

Name: QExpansion.lean
Description: Q-expansion of Eisenstein series.

Name: Summable.lean
Description: Summability of Eisenstein series.

Name: UniformConvergence.lean
Description: Uniform convergence of Eisenstein series.

Name: MDifferentiable.lean
Description: Differentiability of Eisenstein series.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Eisenstein series `Eₖ(τ) = ∑_{(c,d)=1} (cτ+d)^{-k}`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Eisenstein series definitions.

Name: IsBoundedAtImInfty.lean
Description: Eisenstein series are bounded at imaginary infinity.

Name: DimensionFormulas
Description: Dimension formulas for spaces of level-1 modular forms: proves dim M_k(SL₂ℤ) and dim S_k(SL₂ℤ) = 0 for odd k. The proof proceeds algebraically via the ring of modular forms, Eisenstein series, and the discriminant function Δ, without appealing to Riemann-Roch or algebraic geometry of modular curves. Contains dimension_level_one and ModularForm.levelOne_odd_weight_eq_zero. Distinct from EisensteinSeries (which constructs the forms used here) and CuspForms.

Name: LevelOne.lean
Description: Dimension formulas for level-1 modular forms.

Name: LevelOne.lean
Description: Modular forms of level 1: the graded ring `⨁_k M_k(SL(2,ℤ)) = ℂ[E₄, E₆]`, Eisenstein series `E₄, E₆`, and dimension formulas. Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and ModularForms/Identities.

Name: NormTrace.lean
Description: Norm and trace of modular forms in field extensions: how modular forms transform under base change of the coefficient field. Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and NumberField/Norm.

Name: Discriminant.lean
Description: Discriminant modular form: `Δ = (2π)^{12} η^{24}` as a weight-12 modular form, and its relation to the j-invariant. Distinct from ModularForms/Delta and ModularForms/Identities.

Name: Identities.lean
Description: Modular form identities: algebraic relations among Eisenstein series `E₄`, `E₆`, and `Δ` (e.g., `E₄³ - E₆² = 1728 Δ`). Distinct from ModularForms/LevelOne and ModularForms/Discriminant.

Name: Petersson.lean
Description: Petersson inner product: `⟪f, g⟫ = ∫_{Γ\ℍ} f(τ) g̅(τ) Im(τ)^k dμ(τ)` on cusp forms. Hecke operators are self-adjoint for this inner product. Distinct from ModularForms/CuspFormSubmodule.

Name: SlashActions.lean
Description: Slash action on functions: `f |[k] γ` — the weight-`k` slash action of `γ ∈ GL₂` on functions `ℍ → ℂ`. The basic properties and interplay with composition. Distinct from ModularForms/SlashInvariantForms.

Name: SlashInvariantForms.lean
Description: Slash-invariant forms: `SlashInvariantForm Γ k` — functions invariant under the weight-`k` slash action of `Γ`. The intermediate structure before imposing holomorphicity (for modular forms). Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and ModularForms/SlashActions.

Name: ProperlyDiscontinuous.lean
Description: Proper discontinuity of `SL(2, ℤ)` action: the action of `SL(2, ℤ)` on the upper half-plane is properly discontinuous. Distinct from ModularForms/ArithmeticSubgroups and Topology/Algebra/MulAction.

Name: QExpansion.lean
Description: q-expansion of modular forms: `f(τ) = ∑ aₙ qⁿ` where `q = e^{2πiτ}`, the q-expansion principle (two modular forms are equal iff their q-expansions agree). Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and ModularForms/Identities.

Name: Derivative.lean
Description: Derivatives of modular forms: the Serre derivative `∂f = f' - (k/12) E₂ f` and its effect on weights. Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and ModularForms/Identities.

Name: BoundedAtCusp.lean
Description: Modular forms bounded at cusps: `ModularForm.BoundedAtFilter` — the condition that a modular form grows at most polynomially near each cusp. Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and ModularForms/Cusps.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Growth bounds for modular forms: the Hecke bound `|f(τ)| ≤ C * Im(τ)^{-k/2}` and its consequences for L-function convergence. Distinct from ModularForms/BoundedAtCusp.

Name: ArithmeticSubgroups.lean
Description: Arithmetic subgroups of `SL(2, ℝ)`: `ArithmeticSubgroup Γ` — subgroups of finite index in `SL(2, ℤ)`. Distinct from ModularForms/CongruenceSubgroups and ModularForms/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Modular forms: `ModularForm Γ k` — holomorphic functions on the upper half-plane transforming as `f(γτ) = (cτ+d)^k f(τ)` under `γ ∈ Γ`, bounded at cusps. The `SlashAction` and `ModularForm` typeclass. Distinct from ModularForms/CuspFormSubmodule.

Name: CongruenceSubgroups.lean
Description: Congruence subgroups: `Gamma0 N = {[[a,b],[c,d]] | c ≡ 0 mod N}`, `Gamma1 N`, and `Gamma N`. Their indices in `SL(2, ℤ)`. Distinct from ModularForms/ArithmeticSubgroups and ModularForms/Basic.

Name: DedekindEta.lean
Description: Dedekind eta function: `η(τ) = e^{πiτ/12} ∏_{n=1}^∞ (1 - e^{2πinτ})`. Transformation formula under `SL(2, ℤ)` and the eta multiplier system. Distinct from ModularForms/Delta and ModularForms/QExpansion.

Name: Delta.lean
Description: Ramanujan's Delta function: `Δ(τ) = η(τ)^24 = q ∏(1-qⁿ)^24` — the unique normalised weight-12 cusp form. Tau function `τ(n)` as its Fourier coefficients. Distinct from ModularForms/DedekindEta and ModularForms/QExpansion.

Name: CuspFormSubmodule.lean
Description: Cusp forms as a submodule: `CuspForm Γ k` — modular forms vanishing at all cusps. The orthogonal complement of Eisenstein series. Distinct from ModularForms/Basic and ModularForms/Petersson.

Name: Cusps.lean
Description: Cusps of a congruence subgroup: the set `Γ\(ℙ¹(ℚ))` of inequivalent cusps, their stabilisers, and the local parameter at each cusp. Distinct from ModularForms/BoundedAtCusp.

Name: LocalField
Description: Local fields — completions of number fields at finite and infinite places — including p-adic fields and their extensions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Local fields: `LocalField K` — a non-archimedean complete discretely valued field with finite residue field, or an archimedean complete field (`ℝ` or `ℂ`). Basic instances and the structure theorem. Distinct from Padics/PadicNumbers and RamificationInertia/Basic.

Name: LSeries
Description: Dirichlet L-series and more general L-series attached to arithmetic functions; convergence, analytic continuation, and special values.

Name: Nonvanishing.lean
Description: Nonvanishing of L-functions: `L(χ, 1) ≠ 0` for non-principal Dirichlet characters χ (key step in Dirichlet's theorem on primes in progressions). Distinct from LSeries/Dirichlet and EulerProduct/DirichletLSeries.

Name: Positivity.lean
Description: Positivity of L-functions on the real axis: `L(χ₀, s) > 0` for the principal character and `s > 1`, used via a product trick in the nonvanishing proof. Distinct from LSeries/Nonvanishing.

Name: MellinEqDirichlet.lean
Description: Mellin transform equals Dirichlet series: `L(f, s) = s * ∫_1^∞ (∑_{n≤x} f(n)) x^{-s-1} dx` (partial summation). Distinct from LSeries/Basic and MeasureTheory/Integral/Asymptotics.

Name: Injectivity.lean
Description: Injectivity of the L-series map: if `L(f, s) = L(g, s)` for all `s` in a half-plane, then `f = g`. A Dirichlet series is determined by its values. Distinct from LSeries/Basic and LSeries/Convergence.

Name: Linearity.lean
Description: Linearity of L-series: `L(f + g, s) = L(f, s) + L(g, s)` and `L(c * f, s) = c * L(f, s)`. The vector space structure of L-series. Distinct from LSeries/Basic and LSeries/Convolution.

Name: ZetaZeros.lean
Description: Zeros of the Riemann zeta function: the Riemann hypothesis context, known results (no zeros on `Re s = 1`, the completed zeta function), and the zero-free region for ζ used in PNT. Distinct from LSeries/RiemannZeta and LSeries/Nonvanishing.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: L-series of functions on `ℤ/nℤ`: `L(f, s)` for `f : ZMod n → ℂ` and its decomposition into Dirichlet L-functions. Distinct from LSeries/Dirichlet and DirichletCharacter/Orthogonality.

Name: SumCoeff.lean
Description: Summatory functions of L-series coefficients: partial sums `∑_{n≤x} f(n)` and their asymptotic, used via the Perron/Mellin connection. Distinct from LSeries/Convergence and ArithmeticFunction/VonMangoldt.

Name: PrimesInAP.lean
Description: Dirichlet's theorem: for any a, q with gcd(a, q) = 1, the arithmetic progression a, a+q, a+2q, … contains infinitely many primes. The proof uses Dirichlet L-functions L(s, χ) and their non-vanishing at s = 1 for non-trivial Dirichlet characters χ. Contains the analytic infrastructure — Dirichlet series, characters, the product formula over primes — needed for the complete proof.

Name: RiemannZeta.lean
Description: Riemann zeta function: `riemannZeta s = ∑ 1/nˢ`, its analytic continuation to `ℂ \ {1}`, the functional equation `ξ(s) = ξ(1-s)`, and the trivial zeros. Distinct from LSeries/HurwitzZeta and ArithmeticFunction/Zeta.

Name: HurwitzZetaValues.lean
Description: Special values of the Hurwitz zeta: `ζ(0, a) = 1/2 - a`, `ζ(-n, a)` in terms of Bernoulli polynomials, and the values `ζ(2n)` via Bernoulli numbers. Distinct from LSeries/HurwitzZeta and LSeries/RiemannZeta.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Dirichlet convolution and L-series: `L(f * g, s) = L(f, s) * L(g, s)`. The multiplicativity of L-series under Dirichlet convolution. Distinct from LSeries/Basic and ArithmeticFunction/Moebius.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of L-series: `d/ds L(f, s) = -L(n ↦ log n * f n, s)`, and the derivative of `ζ(s)` at `s = 1`. Distinct from LSeries/Convergence and Harmonic/ZetaAsymp.

Name: Convergence.lean
Description: Convergence of L-series: the abscissa of absolute convergence, uniform convergence on half-planes `Re s ≥ σ + ε`, and the Perron formula. Distinct from LSeries/Basic and LSeries/Deriv.

Name: AbstractFuncEq.lean
Description: Abstract functional equation for L-series: the general framework for functional equations `Λ(s) = ε * Λ(1-s)` relating an L-series to its dual. Distinct from LSeries/HurwitzZeta and LSeries/RiemannZeta.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic L-series theory: `LSeries f s = ∑ n, f n / n ^ s` for a function `f : ℕ → ℂ`, absolute convergence, and the half-plane of convergence. Distinct from LSeries/Convergence (detailed convergence) and ArithmeticFunction/LFunction.

Name: HurwitzZetaEven.lean
Description: Even part of the Hurwitz zeta: the even (cosine) part `ζ_E(s, a) = (ζ(s,a) + ζ(s,1-a))/2` and its functional equation. Distinct from LSeries/HurwitzZeta and LSeries/HurwitzZetaOdd.

Name: HurwitzZetaOdd.lean
Description: Odd part of the Hurwitz zeta: the odd (sine) part `ζ_O(s, a) = (ζ(s,a) - ζ(s,1-a))/2` and its functional equation. Distinct from LSeries/HurwitzZetaEven and LSeries/HurwitzZetaValues.

Name: HurwitzZeta.lean
Description: Hurwitz zeta function: `ζ(s, a) = ∑_{n=0}^∞ 1/(n+a)^s` for `0 < a ≤ 1`. Analytic continuation, functional equation, and the relation `ζ(s, 1) = ζ(s)`. Distinct from LSeries/HurwitzZetaEven and LSeries/RiemannZeta.

Name: Dirichlet.lean
Description: Dirichlet L-functions `L(χ, s)` for Dirichlet characters: convergence for `Re s > 0`, the functional equation, and `L(χ, 1) ≠ 0` for non-principal χ. Distinct from LSeries/Basic and EulerProduct/DirichletLSeries.

Name: DirichletContinuation.lean
Description: Analytic continuation of Dirichlet L-functions: `L(χ, s)` continues to an entire function for non-principal χ, and to `ℂ \ {1}` for the principal character. Distinct from LSeries/Dirichlet and LSeries/AbstractFuncEq.

Name: Padics
Description: The p-adic integers ℤ_p and p-adic rationals ℚ_p: ultrametric structure, Hensel's lemma, and power series over p-adic fields.

Name: PadicVal
Description: The p-adic valuation v_p : ℚ× → ℤ: the exponent of p in the factorization, with v_p(0) = ∞. The p-adic absolute value |x|_p = p^{-v_p(x)}, the p-adic metric, and the non-archimedean triangle inequality |x+y|_p ≤ max(|x|_p, |y|_p). The foundation for the p-adic numbers ℚ_p via completion.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: P-adic valuation definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: P-adic valuation API: `padicValNat`, `padicValInt`.

Name: ProperSpace.lean
Description: Properness of `ℤ_p` and `ℚ_p`: closed bounded sets are compact (the Heine–Borel property for p-adic integers). Distinct from Padics/PadicIntegers and Topology/MetricSpace/ProperSpace.

Name: PadicNumbers.lean
Description: p-adic numbers `ℚ_p`: the completion of ℚ with respect to the p-adic norm, as a complete non-archimedean field. Distinct from Padics/PadicIntegers (integers) and Padics/PadicNorm (the norm).

Name: PadicNorm.lean
Description: p-adic norm: `padicNorm p q = p^(-v_p(q))` for `q : ℚ`, the non-archimedean norm, and the ultrametric inequality. Distinct from Padics/PadicNumbers (the completion) and Padics/PadicIntegers.

Name: WithVal.lean
Description: Type alias `WithVal p` carrying the p-adic valuation: used to disambiguate instances when working with `ℚ` equipped with the p-adic valuation. Distinct from Padics/PadicNorm.

Name: ValuativeRel.lean
Description: Valuative relations in p-adic fields: the valuation-based equivalence relation and the residue field `𝔽_p = ℤ_p / pℤ_p`. Distinct from Padics/PadicIntegers and RamificationInertia/Basic.

Name: RingHoms.lean
Description: Ring homomorphisms from p-adic integers: `ℤ_p → R` for rings `R` with compatible p-adic structures, and the universal property of `ℤ_p = lim ℤ/pⁿℤ`. Distinct from Padics/PadicIntegers.

Name: HeightOneSpectrum.lean
Description: Height-one primes of a Dedekind domain: the prime ideals of height 1, their completions, and the correspondence with non-archimedean places. Distinct from NumberField/FinitePlaces and Padics/PadicNumbers.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Complex p-adic numbers `ℂ_p = Ĉ_p`: the completion of the algebraic closure of `ℚ_p`. Properties and comparison with `ℂ`. Distinct from Padics/PadicNumbers and Analysis/Complex/Basic.

Name: AddChar.lean
Description: Additive characters of p-adic fields: `PadicAddChar p : AddChar ℚ_p ℂ` — the standard additive character `x ↦ e^{2πi{x}}` where `{x}` is the fractional part. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/AddCharacter and Padics/PadicNumbers.

Name: PadicIntegers.lean
Description: p-adic integers `ℤ_p`: the ring of integers of `ℚ_p`, defined as the closed unit disk `{x : ℚ_p | ‖x‖ ≤ 1}`. Completeness, the maximal ideal, and the inverse limit `ℤ_p = lim ℤ/pⁿℤ`. Distinct from Padics/PadicNumbers and Padics/Hensel.

Name: MahlerBasis.lean
Description: Mahler basis of p-adic continuous functions: the binomial coefficient functions `{x choose n}` form a Schauder basis for `C(ℤ_p, ℚ_p)`. Distinct from Padics/PadicIntegers and Analysis/Polynomial/MahlerMeasure.

Name: Hensel.lean
Description: Hensel's lemma: if `f(a) ≡ 0 (mod p)` and `f'(a) ≢ 0 (mod p)`, then `f` has a root in `ℤ_p`. The general form for Newton's method in complete valued rings. Distinct from Padics/PadicIntegers.

Name: Transcendental
Description: Transcendence theory: algebraic independence results and proofs that constants such as e and π are transcendental.

Name: Liouville
Description: Liouville numbers and Liouville's theorem: an algebraic number α of degree d satisfies |α - p/q| > c/q^d for all p/q ∈ ℚ. Liouville numbers (approximated too well by rationals) are transcendental. The example ∑ 10^{-k!} is Liouville's explicit transcendental number. Distinct from Lindemann (which proves transcendence of e and π by different methods).

Name: Measure.lean
Description: Liouville numbers form a measure-zero set.

Name: Residual.lean
Description: Liouville numbers form a residual set.

Name: LiouvilleWith.lean
Description: LiouvilleWith constant c: Diophantine approximation condition.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Liouville's theorem on Diophantine approximation.

Name: LiouvilleNumber.lean
Description: Liouville numbers: `∑ 10^{-n!}` is transcendental.

Name: Lindemann
