Description: The Lindemann-Weierstrass theorem: if α₁, …, αₙ are distinct algebraic numbers then e^{α₁}, …, e^{αₙ} are linearly independent over ℚ. Corollaries: e and π are transcendental (π is transcendental because e^{iπ} = -1 and -1 is algebraic). The main result of this folder.

Name: AnalyticalPart.lean
Description: Analytical part of the Lindemann–Weierstrass theorem.

Name: Zsqrtd
Description: The ring ℤ[√d] for square-free d, a prototype for rings of integers in quadratic number fields, with norm and unit structure.

Name: QuadraticReciprocity.lean
Description: Application of `ℤ[√d]` to quadratic reciprocity: Eisenstein's proof of QR using the arithmetic of `ℤ[ζ_p]` or `ℤ[√p*]`. Distinct from Zsqrtd/GaussianInt and LegendreSymbol/QuadraticReciprocity.

Name: ToReal.lean
Description: Embedding `ℤ[√d] → ℝ`: the ring homomorphism `Zsqrtd.toReal d : ℤ[√d] → ℝ` sending `√d ↦ √d`. Distinct from Zsqrtd/Basic and Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Sqrt.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The ring `ℤ[√d]` for a squarefree integer `d`: `Zsqrtd d` — elements `a + b√d` with `a b : ℤ`, the ring operations, norm `N(a+b√d) = a² - db²`, and units. Distinct from Zsqrtd/GaussianInt and Zsqrtd/ToReal.

Name: GaussianInt.lean
Description: Gaussian integers `ℤ[i] = ℤ[√(-1)]`: Euclidean domain structure, `gcd` in `ℤ[i]`, primes in `ℤ[i]` (Fermat's two-squares theorem: `p = a² + b²` iff `p = 2` or `p ≡ 1 mod 4`). Distinct from Zsqrtd/Basic.

Name: Real
Description: Real number-theoretic results: equidistribution, Weyl's theorem, and related analytic number theory over the reals.

Name: Irrational.lean
Description: Irrationality of specific real numbers: the proof that √2 is irrational — no integers p, q satisfy (p/q)² = 2 — and more generally that √n is irrational for any non-perfect-square n. The classical parity argument on numerator and denominator, formalized as Nat.Prime.irrational_sqrt. One of the earliest proofs that not all real numbers are rational, and a foundational example in the Mathlib irrationality API.

Name: GoldenRatio.lean
Description: Golden ratio: `φ = (1 + √5) / 2` and its algebraic properties (`φ² = φ + 1`), relationship to Fibonacci numbers (`fib n / fib(n+1) → φ⁻¹`), and continued fraction expansion `φ = [1; 1, 1, 1, …]`. Distinct from SpecificLimits/Fibonacci.

Name: RamificationInertia
Description: Ramification and inertia of primes in extensions of Dedekind domains, including the fundamental identity ef = n relating ramification and inertia indices.

Name: Inertia.lean
Description: Inertia group and inertia field: the inertia group `I(P/p) ≤ Gal(L/K)` fixes the residue field, and the inertia field `L^I` is the maximal unramified subextension. Distinct from RamificationInertia/Galois and RamificationInertia/Unramified.

Name: Ramification.lean
Description: Wild and tame ramification: the wild inertia group (p-Sylow of inertia), tame ramification (`e(P/p)` coprime to `p`), and the different ideal. Distinct from RamificationInertia/Inertia and RamificationInertia/HilbertTheory.

Name: Unramified.lean
Description: Unramified extensions: `e(P/p) = 1` and `f(P/p) = [L:K]`, the unramified extension of a local field is unique and has cyclic Galois group generated by Frobenius. Distinct from RamificationInertia/Inertia and Padics/HeightOneSpectrum.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ramification and inertia: `ramificationIdx e p P` (ramification index) and `inertiaDeg f p P` (residue degree) for a prime `p` in `R` and `P | p` in `S`. The fundamental identity `∑ eᵢ fᵢ = [L:K]`. Distinct from RamificationInertia/Galois and RamificationInertia/Inertia.

Name: Galois.lean
Description: Ramification in Galois extensions: the decomposition group `D(P/p)`, inertia group `I(P/p)`, and their indices. Galois action on primes above `p`. Distinct from RamificationInertia/Basic and RamificationInertia/HilbertTheory.

Name: HilbertTheory.lean
Description: Hilbert ramification theory: higher ramification groups `G_i(P/p)` (ramification filtration), Herbrand function, and the conductor-discriminant formula. Distinct from RamificationInertia/Galois.

Name: RatFunc
Description: Rational functions over a field as a field of fractions of a polynomial ring, with valuations and partial fraction decompositions.

Name: Ostrowski.lean
Description: Ostrowski's theorem for rational function fields: every non-trivial absolute value on `k(t)` (for a finite field `k`) is either the trivial absolute value, the degree absolute value, or a `p`-adic absolute value for an irreducible `p ∈ k[t]`. Distinct from Analysis/AbsoluteValue/Equivalence.

Name: DiophantineApproximation
Description: Approximation of real numbers by rationals: Dirichlet's theorem, the three-distance theorem, and Liouville-type estimates.

Name: ContinuedFractions.lean
Description: Diophantine approximation via continued fractions: the continued fraction convergents are the best rational approximations, and Lagrange's theorem on quadratic irrationals. Distinct from DiophantineApproximation/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Dirichlet's approximation theorem and Hurwitz's theorem: for any `α ∈ ℝ` and `N`, there exist `p, q` with `1 ≤ q ≤ N` and `|qα - p| ≤ 1/(N+1)`. Distinct from DiophantineApproximation/ContinuedFractions.

Name: DirichletCharacter
Description: Dirichlet characters: completely multiplicative functions on (ℤ/nℤ)× extended to ℤ, used to separate congruence classes in analytic number theory.

Name: GaussSum.lean
Description: Gauss sums: `gaussSum χ ψ = ∑_{a mod n} χ(a) ψ(a)` for a Dirichlet character χ and additive character ψ. Absolute value `|τ(χ)| = √n` for primitive χ. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/GaussEisensteinLemmas and DirichletCharacter/Basic.

Name: Orthogonality.lean
Description: Orthogonality of Dirichlet characters: `∑_{χ mod n} χ(a) χ̄(b) = φ(n) * [a ≡ b mod n]` and `∑_{a mod n} χ(a) = 0` for non-principal χ. Distinct from DirichletCharacter/Basic and EulerProduct/DirichletLSeries.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Dirichlet characters: `DirichletCharacter R n` — a multiplicative character `(ℤ/nℤ)ˣ → R` extended to `ℤ/nℤ → R` by zero. Conductors, primitive characters, and the group structure. Distinct from MulChar/Basic and DirichletCharacter/GaussSum.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds on Dirichlet character sums: `|∑_{n≤N} χ(n)| ≤ φ(q)` and the Pólya–Vinogradov inequality `|∑_{n=M}^{M+N} χ(n)| ≤ √q log q`. Distinct from DirichletCharacter/Basic and LSeries/Dirichlet.

Name: Cyclotomic
Description: Cyclotomic fields ℚ(ζₙ), their Galois groups, rings of integers, discriminants, and ramification properties.

Name: Gal.lean
Description: Galois group of cyclotomic extensions: `Gal(ℚ(ζₙ)/ℚ) ≅ (ℤ/nℤ)ˣ`. Distinct from Cyclotomic/CyclotomicCharacter and Cyclotomic/PrimitiveRoots.

Name: PrimitiveRoots.lean
Description: Primitive roots of unity in cyclotomic fields: existence, primitivity characterisation, and the fact that `ℤ[ζₙ]` is the ring of integers of `ℚ(ζₙ)` for prime `n`. Distinct from Cyclotomic/Basic and Cyclotomic/Gal.

Name: Discriminant.lean
Description: Discriminant of cyclotomic fields: `disc(ℚ(ζₙ)/ℚ) = ±nᵠ⁽ⁿ⁾ / ∏ p|n p^{φ(n)/(p-1)}`. Distinct from Cyclotomic/Basic and NumberField/ClassNumber.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cyclotomic extensions: `IsCyclotomicExtension n K L` — `L` is a `K`-algebra generated by a primitive `n`-th root of unity. The cyclotomic polynomial divides `Xⁿ - 1`, and properties of the extension. Distinct from Cyclotomic/PrimitiveRoots and Cyclotomic/Gal.

Name: CyclotomicCharacter.lean
Description: Cyclotomic character: the group homomorphism `Gal(K(ζₙ)/K) → (ℤ/nℤ)ˣ` mapping `σ ↦ [a]` where `σ(ζₙ) = ζₙᵃ`. Distinct from Cyclotomic/Gal and DirichletCharacter/Basic.

Name: ArithmeticFunction
Description: Arithmetic functions on the natural numbers (Euler's totient φ, Möbius function μ, divisor sums σ) and Dirichlet convolution.

Name: Moebius.lean
Description: Möbius function and Möbius inversion: `ArithmeticFunction.moebius` (`μ(n)`), `μ * 1 = δ₁` (Dirichlet inverse of the constant 1), and Möbius inversion `f = ∑_{d|n} g(d) ↔ g = ∑_{d|n} μ(n/d) f(d)`. Distinct from ArithmeticFunction/Zeta.

Name: VonMangoldt.lean
Description: Von Mangoldt function: `ArithmeticFunction.vonMangoldt` (`Λ(n) = log p` if `n = pᵏ`, else 0). The relation `log = Λ * 1` (Dirichlet convolution) and its use in the prime number theorem. Distinct from ArithmeticFunction/Moebius.

Name: Zeta.lean
Description: Zeta function and the identity arithmetic function: `ArithmeticFunction.zeta` (the constant 1 function), its Dirichlet series `∑ 1/nˢ = ζ(s)`, and invertibility via Möbius. Distinct from LSeries/RiemannZeta and ArithmeticFunction/Moebius.

Name: Misc.lean
Description: Miscellaneous arithmetic functions: Liouville's function `λ(n) = (-1)^Ω(n)`, the squarefree kernel, and various combinatorial arithmetic functions. Distinct from ArithmeticFunction/Defs and ArithmeticFunction/Moebius.

Name: Carmichael.lean
Description: Carmichael's lambda function: `Nat.ArithmeticFunction.carmichael n` — the exponent of `(ℤ/nℤ)ˣ`, the smallest `k` with `aᵏ ≡ 1 (mod n)` for all `gcd(a,n)=1`. Distinct from ArithmeticFunction/Moebius and NumberTheory primes.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for arithmetic functions: `ArithmeticFunction R` — functions `ℕ → R` (often with `f 0 = 0`), the Dirichlet convolution `f * g`, and the typeclass instances. Foundational file for the ArithmeticFunction folder.

Name: LFunction.lean
Description: L-functions of arithmetic functions: the Dirichlet series `L(f, s) = ∑ f(n)/nˢ` and basic convergence. Distinct from LSeries/Basic (the general L-series framework) and ArithmeticFunction/Defs.

Name: ClassNumber
Description: Class numbers and ideal class groups of number fields and function fields, measuring the failure of unique factorization.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finiteness of the class group: the class number of a number field or function field is finite (Minkowski's theorem). Key: `ClassGroup.finite`. Distinct from ClassNumber/FunctionField and NumberField/ClassNumber.

Name: FunctionField.lean
Description: Class number of function fields: application of the admissible absolute value machinery to compute that function fields over finite fields have finite class number. Distinct from ClassNumber/Finite and NumberField/ClassNumber.

Name: AdmissibleCardPowDegree.lean
Description: Admissibility via card and degree: absolute values on function fields (of the form `q^deg`) are admissible in the sense needed for class number finiteness. Distinct from ClassNumber/AdmissibleAbsoluteValue and ClassNumber/FunctionField.

Name: AdmissibleAbs.lean
Description: Admissible absolute values for class number computations: properties of absolute values making the Minkowski bound work. Distinct from ClassNumber/AdmissibleAbsoluteValue and ClassNumber/Finite.

Name: AdmissibleAbsoluteValue.lean
Description: Admissible absolute values: `ClassNumber.AdmissibleAbsoluteValue R abv` — an absolute value admitting a `Finset.card`-based Minkowski argument for class number finiteness. Distinct from ClassNumber/AdmissibleAbs.

Name: EulerProduct
Description: The Euler product formula expressing L-functions as products over primes, and its convergence properties.

Name: ExpLog.lean
Description: Exponential and logarithm of Euler products: `log L(χ,s) = ∑_p ∑_{k≥1} χ(pᵏ)/(k pᵏˢ)`, used in the proof of Dirichlet's theorem. Distinct from EulerProduct/Basic and SpecialFunctions/Exp.

Name: DirichletLSeries.lean
Description: Euler product for Dirichlet L-series: `L(χ, s) = ∏_p (1 - χ(p)/pˢ)⁻¹` for Dirichlet characters χ. Nonvanishing at `s = 1` for non-principal χ via the product formula. Distinct from EulerProduct/Basic and LSeries/Dirichlet.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Euler product for Dirichlet series: `∏_p (1 - χ(p)/pˢ)⁻¹ = L(χ, s)` for `Re s > 1`. The abstract Euler product theorem for completely multiplicative functions. Distinct from EulerProduct/DirichletLSeries and LSeries/Basic.

Name: JacobiSum
Description: Jacobi sums defined from pairs of multiplicative characters, with applications to the number of solutions of equations over finite fields.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Jacobi sums: `jacobiSum χ₁ χ₂ = ∑_{a+b=1} χ₁(a) χ₂(b)` for multiplicative characters. The relation `|J(χ₁, χ₂)|² = p` for non-trivial characters and the connection to Gauss sums `τ(χ₁) τ(χ₂) = J(χ₁,χ₂) τ(χ₁χ₂)`. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/GaussSum and DirichletCharacter/GaussSum.

Name: LegendreSymbol
Description: The Legendre symbol (a/p) measuring quadratic residuosity, quadratic reciprocity, and its generalizations (Jacobi and Kronecker symbols).

Name: QuadraticChar
Description: The quadratic character χ₂ of a finite field: the unique nontrivial character of order 2 on (𝔽_q)×, sending x to +1 if x is a square, -1 if not. Its relationship to the Legendre symbol (a/p) for prime p. Used in Gauss sums and in the proof of quadratic reciprocity. Distinct from DirichletCharacter (characters on ℤ/nℤ for general n).

Name: GaussSum.lean
Description: Gauss sum for the quadratic character.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quadratic character of a finite field.

Name: JacobiSymbol.lean
Description: The Jacobi symbol (a/n): a generalization of the Legendre symbol to composite odd moduli, satisfying multiplicativity in both arguments. Contains the Jacobi quadratic reciprocity law (a/b)(b/a) = (-1)^{(a-1)(b-1)/4} for odd coprime a, b, and its use as a fast primality pretest. The Legendre-symbol quadratic reciprocity law — linking whether p is a square mod q to whether q is a square mod p — is derived as a special case.

Name: QuadraticReciprocity.lean
Description: Law of quadratic reciprocity: for distinct odd primes `p, q`, `(p/q)(q/p) = (-1)^{(p-1)(q-1)/4}`. Proved via Gauss's lemma or Eisenstein's argument. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/Basic and DirichletCharacter/GaussSum.

Name: ZModChar.lean
Description: The Legendre symbol as a character of `ZMod p`: `legendreCharacter p : MulChar (ZMod p) ℤ`, its relationship to `MulChar` and the Jacobi sum. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/Basic and MulChar/Basic.

Name: AddCharacter.lean
Description: Additive characters of `ZMod n`: `AddChar (ZMod n) ℂ` and their orthogonality, the standard character `e(a/n) = exp(2πi a/n)`. Distinct from DirichletCharacter/GaussSum and MeasureTheory/Constructions/AddChar.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Legendre symbol `(a/p)` for odd primes `p`: definition as `a^((p-1)/2) mod p`, quadratic residue characterisation, multiplicativity, and `(a/p) = 0 ↔ p | a`. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/QuadraticReciprocity.

Name: GaussEisensteinLemmas.lean
Description: Gauss's and Eisenstein's lemmas: `(a/p) = (-1)^(number of residues in (p/2, p))` and the Eisenstein version using a lattice count. Distinct from LegendreSymbol/Basic and LegendreSymbol/QuadraticReciprocity.

Name: Height
Description: Height functions on projective varieties and algebraic points, used in Diophantine geometry to bound the complexity of rational solutions.

Name: NumberField.lean
Description: Height theory for number fields: the logarithmic Weil height on a number field and the product formula `∑_v log|x|_v = 0`. Distinct from Height/Basic and NumberField/ProductFormula.

Name: Projectivization.lean
Description: Height on projective space: the projective height `H([x₀:…:xₙ]) = ∏_v max|xᵢ|_v`. Distinct from Height/Basic and LinearAlgebra/Projectivization/Basic.

Name: Northcott.lean
Description: Northcott's theorem: for any bound `B` and degree `d`, there are only finitely many algebraic numbers of degree `≤ d` and height `≤ B`. Distinct from Height/Basic and Height/NumberField.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Weil height on number fields: the absolute Weil height `H(x) = ∏_v max(1, |x|_v)` for `x ∈ K̄`. Distinct from Height/NumberField (field-specific) and Height/Northcott.

Name: MvPolynomial.lean
Description: Height of multivariate polynomials: `heightMvPolynomial p` and bounds on the height of polynomial values. Distinct from Height/Basic and LinearAlgebra/Matrix/MvPolynomial.

Name: FLT
Description: Fermat's Last Theorem formalization: the statement that xⁿ + yⁿ = zⁿ has no positive integer solutions for n ≥ 3, with partial results and supporting theory.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomial Fermat's Last Theorem: `xⁿ + yⁿ ≠ zⁿ` for nonconstant coprime polynomials (immediate from Mason–Stothers). Distinct from FLT/MasonStothers and FLT/Basic.

Name: Three.lean
Description: FLT for n = 3: Euler's proof that `x³ + y³ = z³` has no primitive integer solution, using arithmetic in `ℤ[ζ₃]`. Distinct from FLT/Four and FLT/Basic.

Name: MasonStothers.lean
Description: Mason–Stothers theorem (polynomial FLT): for nonconstant coprime polynomials `a + b = c` over a field, `deg(c) ≤ deg(rad(abc)) - 1`. Distinct from FLT/Polynomial and FLT/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fermat's Last Theorem: the statement `¬ ∃ x y z : ℤ, n ≥ 3 ∧ x^n + y^n = z^n ∧ xyz ≠ 0` and reduction lemmas. The `FermatLastTheorem` predicate. Distinct from FLT/Three and FLT/Four.

Name: Four.lean
Description: FLT for n = 4: Fermat's proof that `x⁴ + y⁴ = z²` has no primitive solution, via infinite descent. Distinct from FLT/Basic and FLT/Three.

Name: Harmonic
Description: The harmonic numbers Hₙ = 1 + 1/2 + … + 1/n and their asymptotics, divergence of the harmonic series, and connections to the Euler–Mascheroni constant.

Name: GammaDeriv.lean
Description: Derivative of the Gamma function: `Γ'(1) = -γ` and the digamma function `ψ(x) = Γ'(x)/Γ(x)`. Distinct from Harmonic/EulerMascheroni and MeasureTheory/Integral/Gamma.

Name: Int.lean
Description: Integer harmonic numbers are not integers: for `n ≥ 2`, `∑_{k=1}^{n} 1/k ∉ ℤ`. Proved using 2-adic valuation. Distinct from Harmonic/Defs and Harmonic/Bounds.

Name: ZetaAsymp.lean
Description: Asymptotic of `ζ(s)` near `s = 1`: `ζ(s) - 1/(s-1) → γ` as `s → 1⁺`, and the Laurent expansion. Distinct from LSeries/RiemannZeta and Harmonic/EulerMascheroni.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds on the harmonic series: `∑_{k=1}^{n} 1/k = log n + γ + O(1/n)` with explicit error bounds. Distinct from Harmonic/EulerMascheroni and Harmonic/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Harmonic numbers: `harmonicNumber n = ∑_{k=1}^{n} 1/k` as a rational number and as a real number. Distinct from Harmonic/Bounds and Harmonic/Int.

Name: EulerMascheroni.lean
Description: Euler–Mascheroni constant: `γ = lim_{n→∞} (∑_{k=1}^{n} 1/k - log n)`. Existence of the limit and basic properties. Distinct from Harmonic/Bounds and Harmonic/GammaDeriv.

Name: PrimesCongruentOne.lean
Description: Infinitely many primes congruent to 1 mod n: the proof using cyclotomic polynomials.

Name: PrimeCounting.lean
Description: The prime counting function pi(x): its basic properties and elementary upper bounds.

Name: Primorial.lean
Description: The primorial n# = product of all primes up to n: basic estimates and its role in proving Bertrand's postulate.

Name: Rayleigh.lean
Description: The Rayleigh-Beatty theorem: the sequences floor(n*alpha) and floor(n*beta) partition N iff 1/alpha + 1/beta = 1.

Name: PythagoreanTriples.lean
Description: Pythagorean triples: integer solutions to a^2 + b^2 = c^2, the parametric family, and primitive triples.

Name: PowModTotient.lean
Description: Power modulo totient: Euler's theorem and Fermat's little theorem for modular exponentiation.

Name: Niven.lean
Description: Niven's theorem: the only rational values of theta/pi in [0,1] for which sin(pi*theta) is rational are 0, 1/6, 1/2, 5/6, 1.

Name: Multiplicity.lean
Description: The p-adic valuation v_p(n): the largest k with p^k dividing n, with Legendre's formula for v_p(n factorial).

Name: Ostrowski.lean
Description: Ostrowski's theorem: every nontrivial absolute value on Q is either the usual absolute value or a p-adic one.

Name: PellMatiyasevic.lean
Description: The Pell equation and Matiyasevic's theorem: x^2 - Dy^2 = 1 and its role in proving Hilbert's 10th problem undecidable.

Name: Pell.lean
Description: The Pell equation x² - d·y² = 1 for non-square d: the proof that it has infinitely many positive integer solutions, the fundamental solution (x₁, y₁) as the smallest positive solution, and the recursive structure xₙ + yₙ√d = (x₁ + y₁√d)ⁿ. The solutions form a group under the norm-1 multiplication in ℤ[√d]. A classical problem that drove early development of algebraic number theory.

Name: SelbergSieve.lean
Description: Selberg's sieve: an upper-bound sieve method giving sharp estimates for primes in arithmetic progressions.

Name: VonMangoldt.lean
Description: The von Mangoldt function Lambda(n): log p if n = p^k, else 0, with its Dirichlet series being -zeta'/zeta.

Name: TsumDivsorsAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Tsum over divisor antidiagonals: computing the sum over pairs (d, n/d) as a Dirichlet convolution.

Name: WellApproximable.lean
Description: Well-approximable numbers: real numbers approximable to within 1/q^(1+eps) by infinitely many rationals p/q.

Name: ZetaValues.lean
Description: Special values of the Riemann zeta function: the formula ζ(2k) = (-1)^(k+1)(2π)^(2k) B_{2k} / (2·(2k)!) expressing ζ(2k) in terms of Bernoulli numbers, including the celebrated ζ(2) = π²/6 (the Basel problem, solved by Euler). Also contains ζ(-n) via Bernoulli numbers. A stunning link between the discrete world of integers and the geometry of circles, formalized in full.

Name: Wilson.lean
Description: Wilson's theorem: (p-1)! ≡ -1 (mod p) if and only if p is prime. Formalized as Nat.Prime.factorial_mulInv_atPrime and ZMod.wilsons_lemma. The 'if' direction provides a primality criterion (though an impractical one), and the 'only if' direction shows that composite n cannot satisfy the congruence. A curious characterization of primes with a clean algebraic proof via the pairing of inverses in (ℤ/pℤ)×.

Name: TsumDivisorsAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Summation over divisor antidiagonals: the analytic version of Dirichlet convolution for infinite series.

Name: SmoothNumbers.lean
Description: Smooth numbers (y-smooth): numbers with all prime factors at most y, with the counting function psi(x, y).

Name: SiegelsLemma.lean
Description: Siegel's lemma: a system of m linear equations in n > m unknowns over Z has a nontrivial small integer solution.

Name: SumFourSquares.lean
Description: Lagrange's four-square theorem (Nat.sum_four_squares): every natural number can be expressed as the sum of four integer squares n = a² + b² + c² + d². The Mathlib proof uses Euler's four-square identity and Minkowski's theorem on lattice points to reduce to the prime case. A celebrated surprise from number theory: four squares always suffice, and three do not (7 requires four).

Name: SumTwoSquares.lean
Description: Sum of two squares theorem: a prime p is a sum of two squares iff p = 2 or p is congruent to 1 mod 4.

Name: SumPrimeReciprocals.lean
Description: The divergence of the sum of reciprocals of primes: ∑ 1/p = +∞ over all primes p, proved via the Euler product formula. The primes are dense enough in the natural numbers that even their reciprocals — a thinning subsequence of the harmonic series — still diverge. A quantitative strengthening of Euclid's infinitude of primes, foundational in analytic number theory.

Name: Chebyshev.lean
Description: Chebyshev's estimates: the asymptotic bounds showing pi(x) is of order x/ln(x) via theta and psi functions.

Name: Bertrand.lean
Description: Bertrand's postulate: for every n >= 1 there is a prime p with n < p <= 2n.

Name: Dioph.lean
Description: Diophantine sets and Hilbert's 10th problem: the class of sets defined by existence of integer solutions to polynomial equations.

Name: EllipticDivisibilitySequence.lean
Description: Elliptic divisibility sequences: integer sequences satisfying a recurrence related to division polynomials of an elliptic curve.

Name: Divisors.lean
Description: Divisors of a natural number: the set of divisors, divisor sums, and perfect numbers.

Name: BernoulliPolynomials.lean
Description: Bernoulli polynomials B_n(x): the unique polynomials with derivative n times B_{n-1}(x), with the generating function.

Name: ADEInequality.lean
Description: The ADE inequality: 1/p + 1/q + 1/r > 1 characterizes the finite Dynkin diagrams A, D, E.

Name: AbelSummation.lean
Description: Abel summation (summation by parts): the discrete analog of integration by parts, used to convert sums to integrals.

Name: ArithmeticFunction.lean
Description: Arithmetic functions and Dirichlet convolution: multiplicative functions, the Mobius inversion formula, and the Dirichlet product.

Name: Bernoulli.lean
Description: Bernoulli numbers B_n and their role in power sum formulas: ∑_{k=1}^{n} kᵐ is a polynomial in n whose coefficients involve Bernoulli numbers. Contains the generating function t/(eᵗ - 1) = ∑ Bₙ tⁿ/n!, the recursion for Bₙ, and Faulhaber's formula expressing ∑kᵐ as a polynomial in n. A deep connection between discrete summation and analytic number theory, used in the zeta values formula.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic number-theoretic lemmas: gcd properties, Bezout's identity, and fundamental divisibility results.

Name: FactorisationProperties.lean
Description: Factorization properties of integers and ideals: unique factorization, squarefree factorization, and radical.

Name: LucasPrimality.lean
Description: Lucas primality test: n is prime iff there exists a with a^(n-1) = 1 and a^((n-1)/p) not = 1 for all prime p dividing n-1.

Name: LucasLehmer.lean
Description: The Lucas-Lehmer primality test for Mersenne numbers: 2^p - 1 is prime iff the Lucas-Lehmer sequence reaches 0.

Name: MahlerMeasure.lean
Description: Mahler measure of a polynomial: the product of the leading coefficient and the absolute values of roots greater than 1.

Name: Modular.lean
Description: Modular arithmetic results not covered by ZMod, including Chinese remainder theorem instances.

Name: MaricaSchoenheim.lean
Description: The Marica-Schonheim conjecture: a combinatorial result about families of sets and their translates.

Name: KummerDedekind.lean
Description: The Kummer-Dedekind theorem: factoring a prime p in an extension by factoring the minimal polynomial mod p.

Name: FermatPsp.lean
Description: Fermat pseudoprimes: composite numbers satisfying Fermat's little theorem for some base, and Carmichael numbers.

Name: Fermat.lean
Description: Fermat's last theorem for small cases: proofs for n = 3, 4 and infrastructure for the general statement.

Name: FrobeniusNumber.lean
Description: The Frobenius number (coin problem): the largest integer not representable as a nonneg integer combination of given coprime generators.

Name: GaussSum.lean
Description: Gauss sums: the character sum tau(chi) = sum chi(a) e^(2*pi*i*a/n), with the quadratic Gauss sum and its absolute value.

Name: FunctionField.lean
Description: Function fields over finite fields: analogues of number fields with the polynomial ring playing the role of Z.

Name: LinearAlgebra
Description: Covers vector spaces, linear maps, matrices, determinants, eigenvalues, and inner product spaces. Used to solve systems of linear equations, diagonalize operators, and perform computations in high-dimensional spaces. Relied on by nearly all fields, including analysis, probability, physics, engineering, and data science.

Name: Projectivization
Description: Projective spaces over division rings: points as lines through the origin, homogeneous coordinates, and the projective linear group.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of projective spaces: the direct sum `ℙ(V₁ ⊕ V₂)`, the tautological line bundle, and the Veronese embedding. Distinct from Projectivization/Basic and Projectivization/Subspace.

Name: Independence.lean
Description: Independence in projective space: `Projectivization.Independent` — a family of points whose representatives are linearly independent. The projective version of linear independence. Distinct from Projectivization/Basic and LinearIndependent/Defs.

Name: Subspace.lean
Description: Projective subspaces: `Projectivization.Subspace V` — the lattice of projective subspaces of `ℙ(V)`, corresponding to linear subspaces of `V` under the span map. Distinct from Projectivization/Basic and Projectivization/Independence.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Group actions on projective spaces: `GL(V)` acts on `ℙ(V)` by `g • [v] = [g v]`, and the kernel of this action is the scalar matrices. Distinct from Projectivization/Basic and GeneralLinearGroup/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Projective space `ℙ(k, V)`: the set of one-dimensional subspaces of `V`, defined as nonzero vectors modulo scalar equivalence. The `Projectivization.mk` constructor and the representative `Projectivization.rep`. Distinct from Projectivization/Subspace and Matrix/ProjectiveSpecialLinearGroup.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of projective spaces: `#(ℙ(𝔽_q, V)) = (q^n - 1) / (q - 1)` for an `n`-dimensional space over `𝔽_q`. Distinct from Projectivization/Basic and Combinatorics counting files.

Name: PiTensorProduct
Description: Tensor products over an arbitrary index set: the multilinear universal property, canonical isomorphisms, and functoriality.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual of a pi tensor product: `(⨂ᵢ Mᵢ)* ≃ ⨂ᵢ Mᵢ*` (when each `Mᵢ` is finite-dimensional). Distinct from PiTensorProduct/Basis and Dual/Defs.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Pi tensor product via Finsupp: when `M = ι →₀ R`, the tensor power `M^⊗n` in terms of finitely-supported functions. Distinct from PiTensorProduct/DFinsupp and TensorPower/Basic.

Name: DirectSum.lean
Description: Pi tensor product distributes over direct sums: `⨂ᵢ (⨁ⱼ Mᵢⱼ) ≃ₗ ⨁_f ⨂ᵢ M_{i, f(i)}`. Distinct from PiTensorProduct/DFinsupp and DirectSum/TensorProduct.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis of the tensor product `⨂ᵢ Mᵢ` of finitely many modules: when each `Mᵢ` has a basis `eᵢ`, the pi-tensor product has basis `{⨂ eᵢ(fᵢ)}`. Distinct from PiTensorProduct/DFinsupp and TensorProduct/Basis.

Name: DFinsupp.lean
Description: Pi tensor product and DFinsupp: `⨂ᵢ M = ⊕ directSum` when `M` is a DFinsupp type, and the interaction with direct sums. Distinct from PiTensorProduct/Basis and DirectSum/TensorProduct.

Name: Quotient
Description: Quotient modules and vector spaces: the universal property, the first isomorphism theorem, and codimension.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of the quotient module: `M ⧸ N = Quotient (N.quotientRel)` with the induced module structure `Submodule.Quotient.instModule`. Distinct from Quotient/Basic (API) and Quotient/Pi.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Quotient modules and products: `(M × N) ⧸ (P × Q) ≃ₗ (M ⧸ P) × (N ⧸ Q)` and the Chinese remainder theorem for modules. Distinct from Quotient/Basic and DirectSum/Finite.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of quotient modules: `#(M ⧸ N) = #M / #N` for finite modules, and `Submodule.card_quotient`. Distinct from Quotient/Basic and Dimension/Finrank.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quotient module API: `M ⧸ N` for `N : Submodule R M`, the universal property `Submodule.liftQ`, and exactness of `0 → N → M → M/N → 0`. Distinct from Quotient/Defs (definitions) and Quotient/Card.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Bilinear forms on quotient modules: when a bilinear form `B` on `M` vanishes on a submodule `N`, it descends to `M/N`. Distinct from Quotient/Basic and BilinearForm/Basic.

Name: QuadraticForm
Description: Quadratic forms over rings: the associated bilinear form, discriminant, Witt group, and connections to Clifford algebras.

Name: TensorProduct
Description: Tensor products of quadratic forms: given quadratic forms Q on M and Q' on N, the tensor product form Q ⊗ Q' on M ⊗ N. The Witt group as a quotient. Used in the algebraic theory of quadratic forms over fields and in the study of Clifford algebras. Distinct from QuadraticModuleCat (categorical structure).

Name: Isometries.lean
Description: Isometries of tensor products of quadratic forms.

Name: QuadraticModuleCat
Description: The category of quadratic modules: objects are pairs (M, Q) of a module with a quadratic form, morphisms are isometries (linear maps preserving Q). Contains the categorical structure and functors to ModuleCat (forgetting Q). Used in the classification of quadratic forms. Distinct from QuadraticForm/TensorProduct (operations on quadratic forms).

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric quadratic modules.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on quadratic modules.

Name: Radical.lean
Description: Radical of a quadratic form: `QuadraticForm.radical Q = ker(associated Q)` — the submodule on which the associated bilinear form vanishes. Non-degenerate forms have trivial radical. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Basic and BilinearForm/Orthogonal.

Name: QuadraticModuleCat.lean
Description: Category of quadratic modules: objects are pairs `(M, Q)` of a module with a quadratic form, morphisms are isometries. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Isometry and CategoryTheory files.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Orthogonal sum of quadratic forms: `Q₁.prod Q₂` on `M₁ × M₂` defined by `(Q₁.prod Q₂)(x,y) = Q₁ x + Q₂ y`. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Basic and CliffordAlgebra/Prod.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor product of quadratic forms: `Q₁.tmul Q₂` on `M₁ ⊗ M₂` defined by `(Q₁.tmul Q₂)(x⊗y) = Q₁(x) * Q₂(y)`. Used in the Witt ring. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Basic and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: Signature.lean
Description: Signature of a quadratic form: the pair `(n₊, n₋)` counting positive and negative entries in the diagonal form. Proves signature is well-defined (Sylvester). Distinct from QuadraticForm/Real (full Sylvester theorem) and QuadraticForm/Isometry.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Quadratic forms over ℝ (Sylvester's law of inertia): every real quadratic form is equivalent to `x₁² + ⋯ + xₚ² - xₚ₊₁² - ⋯ - xₙ²`, and the signature `(p, n-p)` is an invariant. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Complex and QuadraticForm/Signature.

Name: IsometryEquiv.lean
Description: Isometry equivalences of quadratic forms: `QuadraticForm.IsometryEquiv Q₁ Q₂` — equivalence of quadratic forms (Witt group elements). Distinct from QuadraticForm/Isometry (maps) and CliffordAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Quadratic forms in a basis: the matrix of a quadratic form `Q` in basis `e` is `Aᵢⱼ = B(eᵢ, eⱼ)` where `B` is the associated bilinear form. Orthogonalisation (Gram–Schmidt for quadratic forms). Distinct from QuadraticForm/Basic and Matrix/BilinearForm.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quadratic forms: `QuadraticForm R M` — a map `Q : M → R` satisfying `Q(r • x) = r² • Q(x)` with associated bilinear form `B(x,y) = Q(x+y) - Q(x) - Q(y)`. Core API: `QuadraticForm.associated`, `QuadraticForm.comp`. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Basis and BilinearForm/Basic.

Name: AlgClosed.lean
Description: Quadratic forms over algebraically closed fields: every non-degenerate quadratic form over an algebraically closed field is equivalent to the sum of squares. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Real (Sylvester) and QuadraticForm/Complex.

Name: Isometry.lean
Description: Isometries of quadratic forms: `QuadraticForm.Isometry Q₁ Q₂` — linear maps preserving the form, `f` such that `Q₂ ∘ f = Q₁`. Distinct from QuadraticForm/IsometryEquiv (invertible isometries) and BilinearForm/Isometry.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual quadratic form: the quadratic form on the dual module `Q* : M* → R` induced by a non-degenerate `Q`. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Basic and Dual/Defs.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Quadratic forms over ℂ: every non-degenerate quadratic form over ℂ is equivalent to `x₁² + ⋯ + xₙ²`. Distinct from QuadraticForm/Real (Sylvester's law) and QuadraticForm/AlgClosed.

Name: PerfectPairing
Description: Perfect (non-degenerate) pairings between modules and the induced duality isomorphisms.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restriction of perfect pairings to submodules: given `PerfectPairing R M N` and compatible submodules, the restricted pairing on the submodules and their orthogonal complements. Distinct from PerfectPairing/Basic and BilinearForm/Orthogonal.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Perfect pairings: `PerfectPairing R M N` — a non-degenerate bilinear pairing `M × N → R` that identifies each module as the dual of the other. Proves the induced maps `M → N*` and `N → M*` are isomorphisms. Distinct from Dual/Defs and BilinearForm/Properties.

Name: LinearIndependent
Description: Linear independence: equivalent characterizations, the Steinitz exchange lemma, and relations to bases and dimension.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: `LinearIndependent R v`: the family `v : ι → M` is linearly independent iff `Finsupp.linearCombination R v` is injective. The definitional core, also containing `LinearIndependent.ne_zero`. Distinct from LinearIndependent/Basic (API) and Finsupp/Span.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Miscellaneous lemmas for linear independence: `LinearIndependent.restrict_of_comp`, the augmentation lemma (adding a vector not in the span), and interactions with quotients. Distinct from LinearIndependent/Basic and LinearIndependent/Defs.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Linear independence under base change: `LinearIndependent R v → LinearIndependent S (scalar_extension v)` when `R → S` is a flat ring map or a field extension. Distinct from LinearIndependent/Basic and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for linear independence: `LinearIndependent.comp`, `LinearIndependent.image`, extending a linearly independent set to a basis, and independence over a submodule. Distinct from LinearIndependent/Defs (definition) and LinearIndependent/Lemmas.

Name: GeneralLinearGroup
Description: The general linear group GL(n, R) of invertible n×n matrices over a ring, and the special linear group SL(n, R).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: General linear group `GL n R = GLₙ(R)`: the group of invertible `n×n` matrices over `R`, as the units of `Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R`. Defines `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup`, its topology in the normed setting, and the determinant homomorphism. Distinct from Matrix/SpecialLinearGroup and GeneralLinearGroup/AlgEquiv.

Name: AlgEquiv.lean
Description: General linear group from algebra automorphisms: the group `AlgEquiv R A A` of algebra automorphisms embeds into `GL R A`, and automorphisms preserve the ring structure. Distinct from GeneralLinearGroup/Basic and LinearAlgebra/Matrix/SpecialLinearGroup.

Name: Multilinear
Description: Multilinear maps between modules: the universal property, symmetrization, and alternation operators.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Multilinear maps on Finsupp: multilinear maps `(ι →₀ R)^n → N` and their relationship to `n`-linear forms. Distinct from Multilinear/DFinsupp and Finsupp/Defs.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Multilinear maps on finite-dimensional spaces: the module of multilinear maps `M^n → N` is finite-dimensional when `M` and `N` are. Distinct from Multilinear/Basic and FiniteDimensional/Basic.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Multilinear maps and the tensor product: the universal property states that multilinear maps `M₁ × ⋯ × Mₙ → N` correspond to linear maps `M₁ ⊗ ⋯ ⊗ Mₙ → N`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Multilinear/Curry.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Multilinear maps on pi types: `MultilinearMap.piLinearEquiv` — multilinear maps on `Π i, M i` and their identification with tensors. Distinct from Multilinear/Basic and TensorProduct/Pi.

Name: DirectSum.lean
Description: Multilinear maps on direct sums: `MultilinearMap.DirectSum.summand` — multilinear maps on `⨁ᵢ Mᵢ` expressed in terms of summands. Distinct from Multilinear/DFinsupp and DirectSum/TensorProduct.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Multilinear maps and bases: a multilinear map on `ι`-fold products is determined by its values on basis elements, giving `MultilinearMap.ofBasis`. Distinct from Multilinear/Basic and Basis/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multilinear maps: `MultilinearMap R (fun _ : ι => M) N` — maps that are linear in each argument separately. Core API: `MultilinearMap.map_smul_univ`, `MultilinearMap.comp_linearMap`. Distinct from Multilinear/Curry and Alternating/Basic.

Name: DFinsupp.lean
Description: Multilinear maps on DFinsupp (dependent finitely supported functions): `MultilinearMap.DFinsupp.summand`. Distinct from Multilinear/Finsupp and DirectSum/Basis.

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying multilinear maps: `MultilinearMap.curryLeft` reduces a multilinear map on `Fin (n+1)` arguments to a linear map into multilinear maps on `Fin n`. Used in the universal property of tensor algebras. Distinct from Multilinear/Basic and Alternating/Curry.

Name: Matrix
Description: Matrices over rings: multiplication, transpose, trace, determinant, rank, and special matrix classes (diagonal, triangular, symmetric).

Name: GeneralLinearGroup
Description: The general linear group GL(n, R) as a group of matrices: matrix invertibility criterion (det ≠ 0 over a field), the group law under matrix multiplication, and the natural action on Rⁿ. Distinct from LinearAlgebra/GeneralLinearGroup (which is GL(M) = Units(End(M))) — this folder is the matrix-specific realization.

Name: FinTwo.lean
Description: `GL(2, R)` specific results.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projective linear group `PGL(n, R)`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: `GL(n, R)` definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: General linear group `GL(n, R)`.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of `GL(n, 𝔽_q)`.

Name: Irreducible
Description: Irreducible and primitive nonneg matrices: a square nonneg matrix is irreducible when its directed graph is strongly connected (every vertex reachable from every other), and primitive when some power has all strictly positive entries. Defines IsIrreducible, IsPrimitive, and proves the characterization isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos. Provides the graph-theoretic foundation for Perron-Frobenius theory. Distinct from RingTheory/Irreducible (irreducibility in the ring-theoretic sense).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Irreducible matrices.

Name: Charpoly
Description: The characteristic polynomial of a matrix A: charpoly A = det(XI - A), a degree-n polynomial whose roots are the eigenvalues of A. The Cayley-Hamilton theorem: A satisfies its own characteristic polynomial (charpoly A evaluated at A = 0). Distinct from Matrix/Determinant (det as a scalar, not as a polynomial in X).

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Characteristic polynomial of a linear map.

Name: FiniteField.lean
Description: Characteristic polynomial over finite fields.

Name: Univ.lean
Description: Universal characteristic polynomial.

Name: Minpoly.lean
Description: Minimal polynomial divides characteristic polynomial.

Name: Coeff.lean
Description: Coefficients of the characteristic polynomial.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Characteristic polynomial of a matrix.

Name: Eigs.lean
Description: Eigenvalues from the characteristic polynomial.

Name: Disc.lean
Description: Discriminant of the characteristic polynomial.

Name: Determinant
Description: The determinant of a square matrix: the Leibniz formula det(A) = ∑_σ sgn(σ) ∏ A_{i,σ(i)}, multilinearity and alternation, det(AB) = det(A)det(B), Cramer's rule, and cofactor expansion. Distinct from Charpoly (det(XI-A) as a polynomial) and GeneralLinearGroup (invertible matrices, which use det ≠ 0).

Name: TotallyUnimodular.lean
Description: Totally unimodular matrices.

Name: Misc.lean
Description: Miscellaneous determinant lemmas.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core matrix determinant API.

Name: Reindex.lean
Description: Reindexing matrices: `Matrix.reindex eₘ eₙ A` — relabelling rows and columns via equivalences. Proves `det (reindex e e A) = det A`. Distinct from Matrix/Permutation and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank of a matrix: `Matrix.rank A = finrank R (range (Matrix.toLin A))` — the column rank. Proves rank equals row rank, sub-multiplicativity, and `rank A = rank Aᵀ`. Distinct from Matrix/ToLin and Dimension/RankNullity.

Name: ProjectiveSpecialLinearGroup.lean
Description: Projective special linear group `PSL(n, R) = SL(n, R) / {±1}`: the quotient of `SL(n, R)` by its centre. Distinct from Matrix/SpecialLinearGroup and Projectivization/Basic.

Name: SemiringInverse.lean
Description: Semiring inverse for matrices: the unique matrix satisfying `A * A⁻¹ = 1` when `det A` is invertible (over a semiring). Distinct from Matrix/Adjugate (explicit formula) and Matrix/Defs.

Name: SchurComplement.lean
Description: Schur complement: for a block matrix `[[A, B], [C, D]]` with `A` invertible, the Schur complement `D - C * A⁻¹ * B`. Proves the block determinant formula `det M = det A * det (D - C A⁻¹ B)`. Distinct from Matrix/Block and Matrix/PosDef.

Name: RowCol.lean
Description: Row and column operations: elementary row/column operations as left/right multiplication by elementary matrices, and reduction to row echelon form. Distinct from Matrix/Transvection and Matrix/Defs.

Name: PosDef.lean
Description: Positive definite matrices in the algebraic setting: `Matrix.PosDef A` (Hermitian with `xᴴ * A * x > 0` for all `x ≠ 0`). Proves closure under products and the Cholesky decomposition. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/PosDef (with norm) and Matrix/LDL.

Name: Orthogonal.lean
Description: Orthogonal matrices: `Matrix.IsOrtho A` (equivalently `Aᵀ * A = 1`), the orthogonal group `O(n, R)`, and the relationship with isometries. Distinct from Matrix/Hermitian (Hermitian matrices) and InnerProductSpace/Adjoint.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for small matrices: `!![a, b; c, d]` syntax for 2×2 matrices, `![a, b]` for vectors, and `Matrix.of` for function-defined matrices. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Matrix/Vec.

Name: NonsingularInverse.lean
Description: The inverse of a nonsingular matrix: the formula A⁻¹ = adj(A) / det(A) where adj(A) is the classical adjugate (matrix of signed cofactors), proved as Matrix.inv_def. Cramer's rule: the unique solution to Ax = b has components xᵢ = det(Aᵢ) / det(A) where Aᵢ replaces the i-th column of A by b. Contains the characterization of invertibility by det(A) being a unit, and the group GL(n, R).

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomials in a matrix: `Polynomial.aeval A p` — evaluating a polynomial at a matrix. The Cayley–Hamilton theorem: `Polynomial.aeval A (charpoly A) = 0`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Eigenspace/Charpoly.

Name: Permutation.lean
Description: Permutation matrices: `Matrix.permMatrix σ` — the matrix of a permutation `σ`, satisfying `det (permMatrix σ) = sign σ`. Proves `permMatrix` is a ring homomorphism `Perm n → Matrix n n R`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Combinatorics/Perm.

Name: Permanent.lean
Description: Matrix permanent: `Matrix.perm A = ∑_{σ : Perm n} ∏ i, A i (σ i)` — the sum (rather than signed sum) over all permutations. Distinct from Matrix/Defs (determinant uses signs) and Combinatorics/Permanent.

Name: SesquilinearForm.lean
Description: Sesquilinear forms on matrix spaces: the matrix of a sesquilinear form and the standard sesquilinear form `(A, B) ↦ trace(Aᴴ * B)`. Distinct from Matrix/BilinearForm (bilinear, not sesquilinear) and SesquilinearForm/Basic.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: Matrices over a unique factorisation domain: GCD computations for matrices, content of a matrix, and primitive matrices. Distinct from FreeModule/PID and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Transvection.lean
Description: Transvection matrices (elementary shear matrices): `Matrix.transvection i j r = 1 + r * eᵢⱼ` for `i ≠ j`. Proves transvections generate `SL(n, R)` for Euclidean domains, and the Smith/Hermite normal form. Distinct from Matrix/RowCol and FreeModule/PID.

Name: Trace.lean
Description: Matrix trace: `Matrix.trace A = ∑ i, A i i`. Proves `trace (A * B) = trace (B * A)`, `trace Aᵀ = trace A`, and the trace as a linear functional. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and InnerProductSpace/Trace.

Name: ZPow.lean
Description: Integer powers of matrices: `A ^ (n : ℤ)` for invertible matrices — defined as `A^n` for `n ≥ 0` and `(A⁻¹)^(-n)` for `n < 0`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and GeneralLinearGroup/Basic.

Name: WithConv.lean
Description: Matrices with convolution structure: matrix multiplication viewed as convolution in certain index types (e.g., `ZMod n`), and circulant matrices as a special case. Distinct from Matrix/Circulant and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Vec.lean
Description: Vectors as column/row matrices: `Matrix.col v` (column vector) and `Matrix.row v` (row vector), and the isomorphisms `(Fin 1 → α) ≃ α`. Distinct from Matrix/StdBasis and Matrix/Notation.

Name: ToLinearEquiv.lean
Description: Invertible matrices as linear equivalences: `Matrix.toLinearEquiv A h` — an invertible matrix `A` (with `det A ≠ 0`) gives a linear equivalence. Distinct from Matrix/ToLin (general map) and GeneralLinearGroup/Basic.

Name: Stochastic.lean
Description: Stochastic matrices: row-stochastic (`∑ j, A i j = 1`) and doubly stochastic matrices, Birkhoff–von Neumann theorem (doubly stochastic = convex combination of permutation matrices). Distinct from Analysis/Convex/Birkhoff and Matrix/Permutation.

Name: StdBasis.lean
Description: Standard basis matrices: `Matrix.stdBasisMatrix i j r` — the matrix with `r` in position `(i, j)` and 0 elsewhere. Proves they form a basis of `Matrix m n R`. Distinct from Basis/Fin and Matrix/Defs.

Name: SpecialLinearGroup.lean
Description: Special linear group `SL(n, R)`: the subgroup of `GL(n, R)` of matrices with determinant 1. The surjectivity of the determinant map, and `SL(2, ℤ)` as the modular group. Distinct from GeneralLinearGroup/Basic and Matrix/ProjectiveSpecialLinearGroup.

Name: ToLin.lean
Description: Matrices as linear maps: `Matrix.toLin e f A` — interpreting a matrix as a linear map given bases `e, f`. Proves `toLin` is a ring isomorphism for square matrices. Distinct from Matrix/ToLinearEquiv (isomorphism case) and Matrix/Basis.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric matrices: `Matrix.IsSymm A` (satisfying `Aᵀ = A`). The ring of symmetric matrices and interactions with quadratic forms. Distinct from Matrix/Hermitian (Hermitian = symmetric + complex conjugate) and Matrix/ConjTranspose.

Name: Swap.lean
Description: Row and column swap operations: elementary swap matrices `Matrix.swap i j` and their determinant `det (swap i j) = -1`. Distinct from Matrix/Transvection (shear maps) and Matrix/RowCol.

Name: Nondegenerate.lean
Description: Non-degenerate matrices and bilinear forms: `Matrix.Nondegenerate A` (the bilinear form `(x, y) ↦ xᵀ * A * y` is non-degenerate, equivalently `det A ≠ 0`). Distinct from Matrix/PosDef and BilinearForm/Properties.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for matrices: `Matrix m n α`, matrix multiplication `Matrix.mul`, `Matrix.det`, `Matrix.transpose`, and the `Ring`/`Semiring` instances. The foundational file for the Matrix folder.

Name: ConjTranspose.lean
Description: Conjugate transpose: `Matrix.conjTranspose A = Aᴴ = (star ∘ A)ᵀ`. Proves `(A * B)ᴴ = Bᴴ * Aᴴ`, the star-algebra structure, and Hermitian matrix properties. Distinct from Matrix/Symmetric (real transpose) and Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: Circulant.lean
Description: Circulant matrices: `Matrix.circulant v` — a matrix where each row is a cyclic shift of `v`. Proves circulant matrices are diagonalised by the DFT matrix and form a commutative algebra. Distinct from Matrix/Diagonal and Matrix/Permutation.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual of a matrix module: the dual basis of the standard matrix basis and the pairing `(A, B) ↦ trace(Aᵀ * B)`. Distinct from Dual/Basis and Matrix/Trace.

Name: DotProduct.lean
Description: Dot product of matrix rows/columns: `Matrix.dotProduct v w = ∑ vᵢ * wᵢ`, and its role in matrix multiplication `(A * B) i j = dotProduct (A i) (Bᵀ j)`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and InnerProductSpace/PiL2.

Name: Diagonal.lean
Description: Diagonal matrices: `Matrix.diagonal v` (zero off-diagonal), `Matrix.diag A` (diagonal entries), `det (diagonal v) = ∏ vᵢ`, and `diagonal` as a ring homomorphism. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Matrix/IsDiag.

Name: CharP.lean
Description: Characteristic of matrix rings: the characteristic of `Matrix n n R` equals the characteristic of `R`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and RingTheory/CharP.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for matrices: `Matrix.baseChange f A` — applying a ring map `f : R → S` entry-wise. Proves `det (f.map A) = f (det A)` and similar. Distinct from Matrix/Reindex and Charpoly/BaseChange.

Name: Adjugate.lean
Description: Adjugate matrix: `Matrix.adjugate A` — the transpose of the matrix of cofactors. Proves `A * adjugate A = det A * 1` (Cramer's rule). Used to express the matrix inverse as `A⁻¹ = adjugate A / det A`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Matrix/SemiringInverse.

Name: AbsoluteValue.lean
Description: Absolute values on matrices: norms derived from absolute values on the coefficient ring, and the sub-multiplicativity `‖A * B‖ ≤ ‖A‖ * ‖B‖`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/Normed and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Block.lean
Description: Block matrices: `Matrix.fromBlocks A B C D`, block triangular matrices, and the determinant formula `det (fromBlocks A 0 C D) = det A * det D`. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Matrix/Diagonal.

Name: BilinearForm.lean
Description: Matrix of a bilinear form in a basis: `BilinForm.toMatrix e B` — the matrix `(B(eᵢ, eⱼ))`, and the relationship `B(x, y) = xᵀ * M * y`. Distinct from BilinearForm/Basic and Matrix/DotProduct.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Matrices associated to a basis: `LinearMap.toMatrix e f` — expressing a linear map as a matrix in given bases, and `Basis.toMatrix`. Distinct from Matrix/ToLin (the inverse direction) and Basis/Basic.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional vector space structure on matrix spaces: `finrank R (Matrix m n R) = m * n`, and the matrix ring as a finite-dimensional algebra. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Dimension/Finrank.

Name: Kronecker.lean
Description: Kronecker product: `Matrix.kroneckerMap (· * ·) A B` — the block matrix with `(i,j)` block `A i j * B`. Proves `(A ⊗ B) * (C ⊗ D) = (A * C) ⊗ (B * D)` and `det(A ⊗ B) = (det A)^n * (det B)^m`. Distinct from Matrix/Block and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: IsDiag.lean
Description: Diagonal matrices as a predicate: `Matrix.IsDiag A` (off-diagonal entries are zero), closed under multiplication, and the relationship between `IsDiag` and `diagonal`. Distinct from Matrix/Diagonal (the construction) and Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: InvariantBasisNumber.lean
Description: Invariant basis number for matrix rings: `Matrix n n R` satisfies IBN iff `R` does, and matrix rings over fields satisfy IBN. Distinct from Dimension/StrongRankCondition and Matrix/Defs.

Name: MvPolynomial.lean
Description: Matrices over multivariate polynomial rings: determinant as a multivariate polynomial, `Matrix.detRowAlternating`, and the generic matrix. Distinct from Matrix/Polynomial (univariate) and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Module structure on matrices: `Matrix m n R` as an `R`-module, `Matrix.lmul` (left multiplication) and `Matrix.rmul` as linear maps, and `Module.End` identification. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and Matrix/ToLin.

Name: LDL.lean
Description: LDL* decomposition in the algebraic setting: `A = L * D * Lᴴ` with `L` unit lower triangular and `D` diagonal, for Hermitian positive definite matrices. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/LDL (normed version) and Matrix/PosDef.

Name: Integer.lean
Description: Integer matrices `Matrix n n ℤ`: specific results about integral matrices, Smith normal form computations over ℤ, and determinants of integer matrices. Distinct from FreeModule/Int and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Hadamard.lean
Description: Hadamard product (entrywise product) of matrices: `Matrix.hadamard A B = A * B` (pointwise), also called the Schur product. The Schur product theorem: the Hadamard product of positive semidefinite matrices is positive semidefinite. Distinct from Matrix/PosDef and Analysis/Complex/Hadamard.

Name: Gershgorin.lean
Description: Gershgorin circle theorem: every eigenvalue of `A` lies in some Gershgorin disk `{z | |z - Aᵢᵢ| ≤ ∑_{j≠i} |Aᵢⱼ|}`. Bounds on spectral radius. Distinct from Matrix/Hermitian and Analysis/Matrix/Spectrum.

Name: FixedDetMatrices.lean
Description: Matrices with fixed determinant: the space `{A : Matrix n n R | det A = c}` and its structure. Used in the study of special linear groups. Distinct from Matrix/SpecialLinearGroup and Matrix/Defs.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Matrix rings and ideals: the ideals of `Matrix n n R` are of the form `Matrix n n I` for ideals `I ⊆ R`. The Morita equivalence: `Matrix n n R` and `R` have equivalent module categories. Distinct from Matrix/Defs and RingTheory/MatrixAlgebra.

Name: HermitianFunctionalCalculus.lean
Description: Functional calculus for Hermitian matrices in the algebraic setting: applying a function to a Hermitian matrix via its eigendecomposition. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/HermitianFunctionalCalculus (analytic version) and Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: Hermitian.lean
Description: Hermitian matrices in the algebraic setting: `Matrix.IsHermitian A` (satisfying `Aᴴ = A`). Proves the ring of Hermitian matrices and interactions with spectrum. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/Hermitian (analytic spectral theory) and Matrix/ConjTranspose.

Name: TensorPower
Description: The n-fold tensor power M⊗ⁿ of a module with the symmetric group action, used in the construction of tensor algebras.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric tensor powers: the symmetric algebra `Sym^n M` as the symmetric subspace of `M^⊗n`, and the symmetrisation map. Distinct from TensorPower/Basic and SymmetricAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Pairing.lean
Description: Canonical pairing between tensor powers and their duals: `(M^⊗n)* ≃ (M*)^⊗n` for finite-dimensional `M`. Distinct from TensorPower/Basic and PiTensorProduct/Dual.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Tensor powers `⨂[R]^n M = M^⊗n`: the `n`-fold tensor product of `M` with itself, with the canonical symmetric group action. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic (two factors) and TensorAlgebra/Grading.

Name: TensorAlgebra
Description: The tensor algebra T(M) of a module: the universal associative algebra on M, graded by tensor degree.

Name: Grading.lean
Description: ℕ-grading of the tensor algebra: `TensorAlgebra.GradedAlgebra` — degree-`n` component is `M^⊗n`. Distinct from TensorAlgebra/Basic and ExteriorAlgebra/Grading.

Name: ToTensorPower.lean
Description: Map from tensor algebra to tensor powers: `TensorAlgebra.toTensorPower` — the canonical quotient map to the `n`-fold tensor power `M^⊗n` in degree `n`. Distinct from TensorAlgebra/Grading and TensorPower/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Tensor algebra `TensorAlgebra R M`: the free associative `R`-algebra on `M`, universal for `R`-linear maps `M → A`. The inclusion `TensorAlgebra.ι`, the universal property `TensorAlgebra.lift`, and the graded structure. Distinct from TensorAlgebra/Grading and SymmetricAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis of the tensor algebra: when `M` has a basis, the tensor algebra has a basis given by tensor monomials. Distinct from TensorAlgebra/Basic and ExteriorAlgebra/Basis.

Name: Transvection
Description: Transvection (shear) matrices: elementary matrices of the form I + aEᵢⱼ that generate the special linear group.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Transvections as linear maps: `Transvection i j c` — the linear endomorphism `id + c • (eᵢ ⊗ eⱼ*)` (adding `c` times the `j`-th coordinate to the `i`-th). Proves transvections generate `SL(n, R)` for fields. Distinct from Matrix/Transvection (matrix version) and GeneralLinearGroup/Basic.

Name: TensorProduct
Description: Tensor products of modules over commutative rings: the universal bilinear map, adjunction with Hom, and base change.

Name: Graded
Description: Graded tensor products of graded modules: the tensor product M ⊗ N of graded modules with (M ⊗ N)_n = ⊕_{i+j=n} Mᵢ ⊗ Nⱼ. The Koszul sign rule for the graded commutativity isomorphism M ⊗ N ≅ N ⊗ M in a graded-commutative setting. Used in the exterior algebra and in differential graded algebras.

Name: Internal.lean
Description: Internal graded tensor products.

Name: External.lean
Description: External graded tensor products.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Tensor product distributes over products: `(M × N) ⊗ P ≃ₗ (M ⊗ P) × (N ⊗ P)`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Pi and DirectSum/TensorProduct.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Tensor product of quotient modules: `(M ⧸ N) ⊗ P ≃ₗ (M ⊗ P) ⧸ (N ⊗ P)` (right-exactness of tensor product). Distinct from Quotient/Basic and TensorProduct/RightExactness.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Tensor product and opposite modules: `M ⊗[R] N ≃ₗ N ⊗[Rᵒᵖ] M` (or with the opposite ring). Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Multilinear/TensorProduct.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Tensor product over a pi type: `M ⊗ (Π i, Nᵢ) ≃ₗ Π i, (M ⊗ Nᵢ)` (for finite products), and related distributivity. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and PiTensorProduct/Basic.

Name: RightExactness.lean
Description: Right exactness of tensor product: `M ⊗ ·` is right-exact — if `A → B → C → 0` is exact then `M⊗A → M⊗B → M⊗C → 0` is exact. The flatness criterion. Distinct from TensorProduct/Quotient and Algebra/Flat.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Tensor product tower (base change): for rings `R → S → T` and an `S`-module `M`, `M ⊗[S] T ≃ₗ M` and `(M ⊗[R] S) ⊗[S] T ≃ₗ M ⊗[R] T`. The associativity of iterated tensor products. Distinct from TensorProduct/Associator and TensorProduct/Subalgebra.

Name: Vanishing.lean
Description: Vanishing of tensor products: conditions under which `M ⊗ N = 0` (e.g., `M` is torsion and `N` is torsion-free over a domain). Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and RightExactness.

Name: Subalgebra.lean
Description: Tensor product of subalgebras: when `A` and `B` are subalgebras of `C` that commute elementwise, `A ⊗ B → C` is an algebra map. Distinct from TensorProduct/Tower and Algebra/TensorProduct.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Tensor product of submodules: `N₁ ⊗ N₂` as a submodule of `M₁ ⊗ M₂` (via `TensorProduct.map`), and span properties. Distinct from Span/TensorProduct and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Tensor product and matrix rings: `(Matrix m m R) ⊗ (Matrix n n R) ≃ₐ Matrix (m × n) (m × n) R`. Distinct from Matrix/Kronecker and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis of a tensor product: `Basis.tensorProduct e f` — combining bases of `M` and `N` to get a basis of `M ⊗ N` indexed by `ι × κ`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Basis/Basic.

Name: Decomposition.lean
Description: Decomposition theorems for tensor products: every element of `M ⊗ N` can be written as a finite sum of simple tensors, and the minimal rank decomposition. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and TensorProduct/Finiteness.

Name: Associator.lean
Description: Associativity of the tensor product: `TensorProduct.assoc R M N P : (M ⊗ N) ⊗ P ≃ₗ M ⊗ (N ⊗ P)`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Tensor product `M ⊗[R] N`: the universal property for balanced maps, `TensorProduct.mk`, `tmul`, and basic algebra of tensor products (commutativity, unit). Distinct from TensorProduct/Defs (definition) and TensorProduct/Associator.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of the tensor product `TensorProduct R M N`: constructed as the free abelian group on `M × N` quotiented by bilinearity. Contains the low-level construction. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic (API) and Multilinear/TensorProduct.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free modules and tensor products: `(ι →₀ R) ⊗ M ≃ₗ (ι →₀ M)`, and tensor products of free modules are free. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and FreeModule/Basic.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Linear maps on tensor products: `TensorProduct.map f g : M ⊗ N →ₗ M' ⊗ N'` from linear maps `f : M →ₗ M'` and `g : N →ₗ N'`. Functoriality `TensorProduct.mapBimoduleEquiv`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Multilinear/TensorProduct.

Name: DirectLimit.lean
Description: Tensor product commutes with direct limits: `(colim Mᵢ) ⊗ N ≃ colim (Mᵢ ⊗ N)`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Algebra/DirectLimit.

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: Finite generation and tensor products: `M ⊗ N` is finitely generated when `M` and `N` are, and rank-1 elements of the tensor product. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Dimension/Constructions.

Name: SymmetricAlgebra
Description: The symmetric algebra Sym(M): the universal commutative algebra on M, isomorphic to a polynomial ring when M is free.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis of the symmetric algebra: when `M` has basis `{e₁, …, eₙ}`, `Sym R M` has basis `{e₁^{a₁} ⋯ eₙ^{aₙ}}` indexed by monomials (i.e., `Sym R M ≅ R[X₁, …, Xₙ]`). Distinct from SymmetricAlgebra/Basic and Matrix/MvPolynomial.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Symmetric algebra `Sym R M`: the commutative quotient of the tensor algebra by `v ⊗ w = w ⊗ v`. Universal property `SymmetricAlgebra.lift`, and `SymmetricAlgebra.ι`. Distinct from TensorAlgebra/Basic and ExteriorAlgebra/Basic.

Name: SesquilinearForm
Description: Sesquilinear (conjugate-bilinear) forms used in the study of Hermitian forms on complex vector spaces and C*-algebras.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star-sesquilinear forms: the interaction between `star` on `R` and sesquilinear forms, and self-adjoint sesquilinear forms `B(x,y) = star (B(y,x))`. Distinct from SesquilinearForm/Basic and CStarAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Sesquilinear forms: `SesquilinearForm R M` — maps `M × M → R` that are additive in both arguments, linear in the second, and conjugate-linear in the first. Distinct from BilinearForm/Basic (linear in both) and Matrix/SesquilinearForm.

Name: RootSystem
Description: Root systems and their Weyl groups: the combinatorial data underlying semisimple Lie algebras and algebraic groups.

Name: GeckConstruction
Description: The Geck construction: builds a semisimple Lie algebra from a root system, following Geck's paper. Constructs the Lie algebra lieAlgebra, defines its cartanSubalgebra, and proves the result is semisimple and simple under suitable conditions. Contains isSl2Triple and instHasTrivialRadical. Distinct from RootSystem (which defines the root system itself) and Lie/Semisimple (which studies semisimple algebras in general without constructing them from root data).

Name: Relations.lean
Description: Relations in Geck's root system construction.

Name: Semisimple.lean
Description: Semisimple Lie algebra from Geck's construction.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for Geck's construction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Geck's construction of root systems.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis from Geck's construction.

Name: Finite
Description: Finite root systems: the classification theorem for irreducible finite root systems (types A_n, B_n, C_n, D_n, E_6, E_7, E_8, F_4, G_2) via Dynkin diagrams. The Weyl group as a finite reflection group. The connection to semisimple Lie algebras (each type corresponds to a simple Lie algebra). Distinct from GeckConstruction (a specific root system construction).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for finite root systems.

Name: Nondegenerate.lean
Description: Non-degeneracy of the bilinear form on a root system.

Name: CanonicalBilinear.lean
Description: Canonical bilinear form on a root system.

Name: G2.lean
Description: G2 root system.

Name: OfBilinear.lean
Description: Root systems from a bilinear form: constructing a root pairing from an inner product space with a set of vectors satisfying the root axioms. The standard way to construct crystallographic root systems. Distinct from RootSystem/Defs and RootSystem/Basic.

Name: IsValuedIn.lean
Description: Root pairings valued in a subring: conditions for the Cartan matrix entries to lie in a sub-ring (e.g., ℤ ⊆ ℝ). Distinct from RootSystem/CartanMatrix and RootSystem/OfBilinear.

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Irreducible root systems: a root system is irreducible iff it cannot be partitioned into two orthogonal subsets. The ADE/BCFG classification is stated here. Distinct from RootSystem/Base and RootSystem/Basic.

Name: Reduced.lean
Description: Reduced root systems: a root system is reduced iff `α ∈ Φ` implies `2α ∉ Φ`. The non-reduced case (`BCₙ`) and the reduction to the reduced case. Distinct from RootSystem/Basic and RootSystem/Irreducible.

Name: WeylGroup.lean
Description: Weyl group of a root system: the group `W` generated by the reflections `sₐ(x) = x - ⟨x, α∨⟩α`. Proves `W` is finite, acts faithfully on the roots, and permutes the bases. Distinct from RootSystem/Basic and GroupTheory reflection files.

Name: RootPositive.lean
Description: Positive roots: a choice of positive system decomposes the root system into positive and negative roots `Φ = Φ⁺ ∪ (-Φ⁺)`. Proves the positive system is unique given a base. Distinct from RootSystem/Base and RootSystem/WeylGroup.

Name: RootPairingCat.lean
Description: Category of root pairings `RootPairingCat R`: objects are `RootPairing ι R M N`, morphisms are `RootPairing.Hom`. Limits, colimits, and the forgetful functor to modules. Distinct from RootSystem/Hom and CategoryTheory files.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Morphisms of root pairings: `RootPairing.Hom p₁ p₂` — module maps preserving the pairing structure. The category of root pairings. Distinct from RootSystem/Basic and RootSystem/RootPairingCat.

Name: BaseExists.lean
Description: Existence of a base for a root system: every finite reduced root system admits a base of simple roots. Proved using a generic hyperplane argument. Distinct from RootSystem/Base and RootSystem/Irreducible.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for root systems: a root pairing over `R` extends to one over `S` for a ring map `R → S`. Distinct from RootSystem/Defs and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Base.lean
Description: Base (simple roots) of a root system: `RootSystem.Base Φ B` — a subset `B` of roots such that every root is a non-negative or non-positive integer combination of elements of `B`. Distinct from RootSystem/Basic and RootSystem/BaseExists.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Root systems: `RootPairing ι R M N` — the data of roots `α ∈ M`, coroots `α∨ ∈ N`, and the paring `⟨αᵢ, α∨ⱼ⟩ = Aᵢⱼ` (Cartan matrix entries). Core API and the `RootSystem` abbreviation. Distinct from RootSystem/Defs (definitions) and RootSystem/CartanMatrix.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for root pairings: `RootPairing ι R M N` — the type of root data with index set `ι`, coefficient ring `R`, and dual modules `M`, `N`. Contains `RootSystem` as the special case `M = N*`. Distinct from RootSystem/Basic (API).

Name: Chain.lean
Description: Root strings (chains): the `α`-chain through `β` is `{β + n α | mᵢₙ ≤ n ≤ mₐₓ}`. Proves the string length is at most 4 and the midpoint formula. Distinct from RootSystem/CartanMatrix and RootSystem/Basic.

Name: CartanMatrix.lean
Description: Cartan matrix of a root pairing: `RootPairing.cartanMatrix p` — the matrix `(⟨αᵢ, α∨ⱼ⟩)`. Proves the Cartan matrix has entries in `{0, ±1, ±2, ±3}` and determines the root system. Distinct from RootSystem/Basic and RootSystem/Chain.

Name: Span
Description: The span of a set as the smallest submodule containing it, with generators, membership criteria, and span lattice.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Span and tensor products: `Submodule.span R s ⊗ N = Submodule.span R (s ⊗ N)` — the tensor product of a span with a module. Distinct from Span/Basic and TensorProduct/Submodule.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Submodule.span R s`: expressed as the intersection of all submodules containing `s`, or equivalently as the range of `Finsupp.linearCombination`. Distinct from Span/Basic (API) and Finsupp/LinearCombination.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Span of a set in a module: `Submodule.span R s` — the smallest submodule containing `s`. Core API: `Submodule.span_eq`, `Submodule.span_mono`, `mem_span_iff`. Distinct from Span/Defs (type definition) and Finsupp/Span.

Name: SModEq
Description: Congruence modulo a submodule: the relation x ≡ y [SMOD N] meaning x - y ∈ N, used in p-adic and adelic contexts.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Congruence modulo powers of a submodule: `x ≡ y [SMOD N^n]` and the `N`-adic filtration. Used in the theory of formal power series and completions. Distinct from SModEq/Basic and RingTheory/Ideal/Filtration.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise congruence modulo submodules: `SModEq` applied pointwise to families or finitely-supported functions. Distinct from SModEq/Basic and Finsupp/Supported.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Congruence modulo a submodule: `SModEq N x y` (written `x ≡ y [SMOD N]`) — `x - y ∈ N`. Congruence arithmetic and the relationship with quotient modules. Distinct from SModEq/Pointwise and RingTheory congruence files.

Name: Complex
Description: Linear algebra over ℂ: the real-linear vs complex-linear distinction, conjugate-linear maps, and complex structure on real spaces.

Name: Module.lean
Description: ℂ-module structure from an ℝ-module: `Complex.ofReal` as an ℝ-algebra map, and the complexification `M ⊗[ℝ] ℂ` of a real module. Provides `Complex.instModule` and the forgetful functor from ℂ-modules to ℝ-modules. Distinct from Complex/FiniteDimensional and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Orientation.lean
Description: Orientation of complex vector spaces: the canonical orientation of ℂ and of ℂⁿ (determined by `{1, i}` as an ordered ℝ-basis), and `Complex.orientation`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Orientation and Complex/Module.

Name: Determinant.lean
Description: Determinant of ℂ-linear maps viewed as ℝ-linear maps: `Complex.det_conjAe` — the determinant of the realification of a ℂ-linear map is `|det_ℂ|²`. Distinct from Complex/Module and Matrix/Determinant files.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional complex vector spaces viewed as real spaces: `finrank ℝ E = 2 * finrank ℂ E` when `E` is a ℂ-vector space. Instances connecting `FiniteDimensional ℝ` and `FiniteDimensional ℂ`. Distinct from Complex/Module and Dimension/DivisionRing.

Name: CliffordAlgebra
Description: Clifford algebras Cl(V, Q) associated to quadratic forms, generalizing the quaternions and providing a framework for spinors.

Name: Grading.lean
Description: ℤ/2-grading of Clifford algebras: the decomposition `CliffordAlgebra Q = CliffordAlgebra.even Q ⊕ CliffordAlgebra.odd Q` into even and odd parts. Proves the grading is compatible with multiplication. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Even and ExteriorAlgebra/Grading.

Name: Fold.lean
Description: Folding (recursion principle) for Clifford algebras: `CliffordAlgebra.foldl` — a recursion scheme for computing on `CliffordAlgebra Q` by folding over the generators. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Basic (universal property) and CliffordAlgebra/Contraction.

Name: EvenEquiv.lean
Description: Equivalence between even Clifford subalgebra and a smaller Clifford algebra: `CliffordAlgebra.evenEquiv` — the even part of `Cl(V ⊕ ℝ, Q ⊕ (-1))` is isomorphic to `Cl(V, Q)`. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Even and CliffordAlgebra/Equivs.

Name: Inversion.lean
Description: Invertible elements in Clifford algebras: conditions for `ι v` to be invertible (when `Q v ≠ 0`), and the pin group as the group of invertible elements preserving the subspace `ι(V)` by conjugation. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/SpinGroup and CliffordAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star structure on Clifford algebras: the reversion `CliffordAlgebra.reverse` as a star operation, making `CliffordAlgebra Q` a star algebra. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Conjugation (other involutions) and Topology/Algebra/Star.

Name: SpinGroup.lean
Description: Spin group: `CliffordAlgebra.spinGroup Q` — the group of even invertible elements of `CliffordAlgebra Q` that preserve the vector subspace under conjugation, with the double cover `Spin(n) → SO(n)`. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Inversion and CliffordAlgebra/Even.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product of Clifford algebras: `CliffordAlgebra (Q₁.prod Q₂) ≃ₐ CliffordAlgebra Q₁ ⊗ CliffordAlgebra Q₂` — the Clifford algebra of an orthogonal sum is the (graded) tensor product. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Equivs and CliffordAlgebra/Basic.

Name: CategoryTheory.lean
Description: Category-theoretic structure of Clifford algebras: the functor from quadratic modules to Clifford algebras, and morphisms of Clifford algebras induced by isometries of quadratic forms. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Basic and QuadraticForm/Isometry.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Clifford algebras: `CliffordAlgebra Q` — the quotient of the tensor algebra by `v⊗v = Q(v)`. The universal property `CliffordAlgebra.lift`, the inclusion `CliffordAlgebra.ι`, and `ι_sq_scalar`. The foundational file for the CliffordAlgebra folder.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for Clifford algebras: `CliffordAlgebra.baseChange` — the Clifford algebra of `Q ⊗ id` over an extended ring is the base change of `CliffordAlgebra Q`. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Basic and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Conjugation.lean
Description: Conjugation in Clifford algebras: the grade involution `CliffordAlgebra.involute` (negating odd-degree elements), the reversion `CliffordAlgebra.reverse` (reversing products), and the Clifford conjugation (composition). Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Grading and CliffordAlgebra/Star.

Name: Even.lean
Description: Even subalgebra of a Clifford algebra: `CliffordAlgebra.even Q` — the degree-0 part in the ℤ/2-grading. Proves it is a subalgebra and gives the Lipschitz group as a subgroup. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Grading and CliffordAlgebra/EvenEquiv.

Name: Equivs.lean
Description: Equivalences between Clifford algebras: `CliffordAlgebra.equivEven` (even subalgebra of `Cl(V⊕ℝ)` is `Cl(V)`), and specific low-dimensional identifications. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/EvenEquiv and CliffordAlgebra/Prod.

Name: Contraction.lean
Description: Contraction maps in Clifford algebras: left and right contractions `CliffordAlgebra.contractLeft`, expressing the action of the dual on the Clifford algebra. Used in the spinor representation. Distinct from CliffordAlgebra/Basic and ExteriorAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Dimension
Description: Dimension theory: invariance of basis cardinality, rank-nullity theorem, and dimension of subspaces and quotients.

Name: Torsion
Description: Dimension theory for torsion modules: the rank of a module over a domain (the dimension of M ⊗ Frac(R)), the relationship between rank and the torsion submodule, and the rank-nullity theorem generalized to modules. Distinct from the main Dimension folder (which handles torsion-free modules and vector spaces).

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite torsion modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Dimension of torsion modules.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Rank after localisation: `Module.rank (Localization S) (Localization S ⊗[R] M) = Module.rank R M` (localisation preserves rank for finitely generated modules over Noetherian rings). Distinct from Dimension/Basic and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Rank of linear maps: `LinearMap.rank f = rank (range f)`, rank-nullity theorem `rank f + rank (ker f) = rank M`, and the rank-sum formula. Distinct from Dimension/RankNullity (specific rank-nullity) and Dimension/Finrank.

Name: FreeAndStrongRankCondition.lean
Description: Free modules and the strong rank condition: a module satisfying `StrongRankCondition R` has well-defined rank, and `Module.Free R M` implies the strong rank condition. Distinct from Dimension/StrongRankCondition and FreeModule/StrongRankCondition.

Name: OrzechProperty.lean
