Description: Orzech property: a module satisfying the Orzech property has no proper submodule isomorphic to itself. Used to prove the invariant basis number property. Distinct from Dimension/StrongRankCondition and FreeModule/StrongRankCondition.

Name: Subsingleton.lean
Description: Dimension of subsingleton modules: `finrank R M = 0` when `M` is a subsingleton, and related degenerate dimension facts. Distinct from Dimension/Finrank and Dimension/Basic.

Name: StrongRankCondition.lean
Description: Strong rank condition: `StrongRankCondition R` asserts that `R^m ↪ₗ R^n` implies `m ≤ n`. Proved for commutative Noetherian rings and division rings. Distinct from Dimension/OrzechProperty and FreeModule/StrongRankCondition.

Name: RankNullity.lean
Description: Rank-nullity theorem: `finrank R M = finrank R (LinearMap.ker f) + finrank R (LinearMap.range f)` for a linear map `f : M → N` with `M` finite-dimensional. Distinct from Dimension/LinearMap (general rank) and Dimension/Finrank.

Name: DivisionRing.lean
Description: Dimension theory over division rings: every vector space over a division ring has a well-defined dimension, bases exist (Zorn), and any two bases have the same cardinality. Distinct from Dimension/Basic (general rings) and Basis/VectorSpace.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Rank of standard constructions: `rank (M × N) = rank M + rank N`, `rank (M →ₗ N) ≤ rank M * rank N`, and ranks of direct sums and tensor products. Distinct from Dimension/Basic and Dimension/Finrank.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Module rank: `Module.rank R M = ⨆ {ι | ∃ basis : Basis ι R M}, #ι` (supremum of cardinalities of linearly independent sets). Core results: `rank_le_card_basis`, `rank_span`. Distinct from Dimension/Finrank (finite-dimensional case) and Dimension/DivisionRing.

Name: ErdosKaplansky.lean
Description: Erdős–Kaplansky theorem: for a field `k` and infinite set `ι`, `dim_k(k^ι) = #k^ω * #ι`. This gives the dimension of the full product space. Distinct from Dimension/DivisionRing and Dimension/Free.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Rank of free modules: `Module.rank R (ι →₀ R) = #ι`, and rank of free modules equals the cardinality of any basis. Distinct from Dimension/Basic and FreeModule/Basic.

Name: Finrank.lean
Description: `FiniteDimensional.finrank R M : ℕ` — the rank as a natural number when finite, 0 otherwise. Key lemmas: `finrank_eq_rank`, `finrank_pos_iff`, and `finrank_quotient_add_finrank_submodule`. Distinct from Dimension/Basic (cardinal rank) and Dimension/Finite.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite-rank modules: conditions equivalent to `Module.rank R M < ℵ₀`, preservation under submodule and quotient, and rank-nullity in the finite setting. Distinct from Dimension/Finrank (integer finrank) and FiniteDimensional/Defs.

Name: ConvexSpace
Description: Convex spaces (abstract convex combination structures) axiomatizing convexity without requiring an ambient vector space.

Name: AffineSpace.lean
Description: Convex spaces as affine spaces: affine spaces over ℝ with midpoints are convex spaces (in the sense of `ConvexSpace`), and the interaction between affine combinations and convex structure. Distinct from AffineSpace/Combination and Analysis/Convex/Basic.

Name: Charpoly
Description: Characteristic polynomials of endomorphisms, the Cayley-Hamilton theorem, and minimal polynomials.

Name: ToMatrix.lean
Description: Characteristic polynomial via matrices: `LinearMap.charpoly f = Matrix.charpoly (f.toMatrix B)` for any basis `B`. Proves the characteristic polynomial is independent of the choice of basis. Distinct from Charpoly/BaseChange and Matrix/Polynomial.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The Cayley-Hamilton theorem: every square matrix (or linear endomorphism) satisfies its own characteristic polynomial. For a matrix A with charpoly p(t) = det(tI - A), the theorem states p(A) = 0. Proved via the adjugate matrix identity. Contains the minimal polynomial as a divisor of the characteristic polynomial, and the Cayley-Hamilton statement in the language of Mathlib's Matrix and LinearMap types.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for characteristic polynomials: `charpoly (f ⊗ id) = charpoly f` under scalar extension, and the compatibility of `charpoly` with ring homomorphisms. Distinct from Charpoly/ToMatrix and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Alternating
Description: Alternating multilinear maps: maps that vanish when two arguments are equal, with the exterior product as the universal example.

Name: Uncurry
Description: Uncurrying alternating multilinear maps: converting a curried alternating map M₁ →ₗ M₂ →ₗ … →ₗ N into a single alternating map M₁ × … × Mₙ →ₗ N. Used to build alternating maps from iterated linear maps and vice versa. Distinct from the main Alternating folder (which defines the type and its algebraic properties).

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Uncurrying alternating maps over Fin.

Name: DomCoprod.lean
Description: Domain coproduct of alternating maps: `AlternatingMap.domCoprod f g` — an alternating map on `ι₁ ⊕ ι₂` assembled from `f` on `ι₁` and `g` on `ι₂`. Used in the Leibniz rule for the exterior product. Distinct from Alternating/Basic and ExteriorAlgebra/Grading.

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying and uncurrying of alternating maps: `AlternatingMap.curryLeft` reduces an alternating map on `ι` arguments to a linear map into alternating maps on `ι \ {0}`. Used in the universal property of the exterior algebra. Distinct from Alternating/Basic and Multilinear/Curry.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Alternating multilinear maps: `AlternatingMap R M N ι` — multilinear maps that vanish when two arguments are equal, equivalently that change sign under transpositions. Core API: `AlternatingMap.map_swap`, `AlternatingMap.map_eq_zero_of_eq`. Distinct from Alternating/Curry (currying) and ExteriorAlgebra/Basic.

Name: AffineSpace
Description: Affine spaces and affine maps: points acted on freely and transitively by a vector space, without a distinguished origin.

Name: Simplex
Description: Simplices in affine spaces: an affinely independent set of n+1 points spanning an n-dimensional simplex. Barycentric coordinates, the centroid, and the face maps. Used in topology (geometric realization of the standard simplex) and in the geometry of numbers. Distinct from AffineSubspace (unbounded) — a simplex is bounded.

Name: Centroid.lean
Description: Centroid of an affine simplex.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Affine simplices.

Name: AffineSubspace
Description: Affine subspaces: subsets of an affine space closed under affine combinations (if x, y ∈ S then (1-t)x + ty ∈ S for all t). The direction of an affine subspace (the underlying vector subspace), the lattice of affine subspaces, and the affine span of a set. Distinct from Simplex (a specific bounded affine subspace).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Affine subspace definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Affine subspaces: `AffineSubspace k P`.

Name: Midpoint.lean
Description: Midpoint of two affine points: `midpoint k x y = (x + y) / 2` (in a module over a ring with `1/2`). Proves `midpoint x y ∈ segment k x y` and basic symmetry/affine equivariance. Distinct from AffineSpace/MidpointZero (the zero case) and AffineSpace/Combination.

Name: MidpointZero.lean
Description: Midpoint with a distinguished zero: specialised midpoint theory when the affine space has a base point (module setting). Distinct from AffineSpace/Midpoint (general affine midpoint) and AffineSpace/Defs.

Name: Independent.lean
Description: Affinely independent sets: `AffineIndependent k p` — no point is an affine combination of the others. Characterised by linear independence of differences, and the equivalence with `AffineBasis` for spanning sets. Distinct from AffineSpace/Basis (also requires spanning) and LinearAlgebra/LinearIndependent.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrices for affine maps: the matrix of an affine map with respect to an affine basis, and the composition law in matrix form. Distinct from AffineSpace/Basis (coordinate functions) and LinearAlgebra/Matrix/ToLin.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restriction of affine maps to affine subspaces: `AffineMap.restrict f` restricts `f` to a subspace where `f` maps it into another. Distinct from AffineSpace/AffineMap (general maps) and AffineSpace/AffineEquiv.

Name: Slope.lean
Description: Slope in affine spaces over a field: `slope f a b = (b - a)⁻¹ • (f b - f a)` — the affine analogue of divided differences. Proves `slope` is symmetric and the affine version of the mean value theorem. Distinct from Analysis/Calculus/DSlope and AffineSpace/Combination.

Name: Ordered.lean
Description: Ordered affine spaces: betweenness in an ordered field, `AffineOrderedField`, and the relationship between affine combinations with positive weights and convex combinations. Distinct from AffineSpace/Combination and Analysis/Convex/Between.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on affine subsets: translating a set `s +ᵥ v`, the orbit of an affine subspace under the translation group. Distinct from AffineSpace/AffineMap and Mathlib's general Pointwise files.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Affine bases: `AffineBasis ι k P` — an affinely independent spanning family of `ι`-many points. The barycentric coordinate functions `AffineBasis.coord i`, and the decomposition of any point as an affine combination with barycentric coordinates summing to 1. Distinct from AffineSpace/Independent and LinearAlgebra/Basis.

Name: Centroid.lean
Description: Centroid of a finite family of points: `Finset.centroid k f s = s.centerMass (fun _ => 1) f` as the average of the points. Proves `centroid_mem_convexHull` and affine equivariance. Distinct from AffineSpace/Combination and Analysis/Convex/Combination.

Name: AffineEquiv.lean
Description: Affine equivalences `AffineEquiv k P₁ P₂`: bijections that are affine maps with affine inverses. Defines `AffineEquiv.refl`, `.symm`, `.trans`, and the group structure of self-equivalences. Distinct from AffineSpace/AffineMap (maps not necessarily invertible) and Topology/Algebra/ContinuousAffineEquiv.

Name: AffineMap.lean
Description: Affine maps `AffineMap k P₁ P₂`: maps compatible with the affine structure, the linear part `AffineMap.linear`, composition, and the identification of affine maps with pairs (linear map, translation). Distinct from AffineSpace/AffineEquiv (invertible case) and AffineSpace/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for affine spaces: `AddTorsor V P` (a set `P` acted on freely and transitively by the vector space `V`), `AffineSpace k V P`, and the subtraction `p -ᵥ q : V`. The foundational file for the AffineSpace folder.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional affine spaces: the affine dimension `finrank` of an affine subspace, and the fact that two affine subspaces of the same dimension spanning the same space are equal. Distinct from AffineSpace/Defs and LinearAlgebra/Dimension/Finrank.

Name: Ceva.lean
Description: Ceva's theorem for affine geometry: in a triangle, the cevians are concurrent iff the product of the ratios of the divided sides is 1. Proved purely affinely over a field. Distinct from AffineSpace/Combination (general combinations) and Geometry files.

Name: Combination.lean
Description: Affine combinations: `Finset.affineCombination k f w` — the weighted combination `∑ wᵢ • f(i)` when `∑ wᵢ = 1`. Proves the affine span is the set of affine combinations. Distinct from AffineSpace/Centroid (equal weights) and AffineSpace/Basis (barycentric coordinates).

Name: BilinearForm
Description: Bilinear forms over rings: symmetry, non-degeneracy, matrix representation, and orthogonal complement.

Name: Orthogonal.lean
Description: Orthogonality with respect to a bilinear form: `BilinForm.orthogonal B s` — the set of vectors orthogonal to all of `s`. Proves `B.orthogonal s` is a submodule and the radical. Distinct from BilinearForm/Properties and InnerProductSpace/Orthogonal.

Name: IsometryEquiv.lean
Description: Isometry equivalences of bilinear forms: `BilinForm.IsometryEquiv` — a linear equivalence that also preserves the bilinear form. The group of isometries. Distinct from BilinearForm/Isometry (maps) and QuadraticForm/Isometry.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Bilinear forms on tensor products: `BilinForm.tmul B₁ B₂` on `M₁ ⊗ M₂`, defined by `(B₁ ⊗ B₂)(x₁⊗x₂, y₁⊗y₂) = B₁(x₁,y₁) * B₂(x₂,y₂)`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and BilinearForm/Basic.

Name: Properties.lean
Description: Symmetry, skew-symmetry, and reflexivity of bilinear forms: `BilinForm.IsSymm`, `BilinForm.IsAlt`, `BilinForm.IsRefl`. Proves symmetric forms are reflexive and the decomposition into symmetric and alternating parts. Distinct from BilinearForm/Basic and QuadraticForm/Basic.

Name: DualLattice.lean
Description: Dual lattice of a bilinear form: for a non-degenerate bilinear form on a lattice, the dual lattice `{x | ∀ y ∈ L, B(x,y) ∈ ℤ}`. Used in number theory (discriminant of a number field). Distinct from BilinearForm/Orthogonal and Dual/Defs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bilinear forms `BilinForm R M`: the module of `R`-bilinear maps `M → M → R`. Core API: `BilinForm.comp`, `BilinForm.restrict`, `BilinForm.nondegenerate`. Distinct from BilinearForm/Properties (symmetric, alternating) and SesquilinearForm/Basic.

Name: Isometry.lean
Description: Isometries of bilinear forms: `BilinForm.IsometryEquiv.symm` and maps preserving the form `B(f(x), f(y)) = B(x, y)`. Distinct from BilinearForm/IsometryEquiv (equivalences) and BilinearForm/Hom.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of bilinear forms: `BilinForm.comp f B` (pullback of `B` by `f`), and the functor from modules to bilinear forms. Distinct from BilinearForm/Isometry (isometries) and BilinearForm/Basic.

Name: Basis
Description: Bases of free modules and vector spaces: existence (via Zorn's lemma), change-of-basis matrices, and the canonical bijection between bases and linear maps.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Bases of product modules: `Basis.prod e₁ e₂` — combining bases of `M` and `N` to get a basis of `M × N` indexed by `ι₁ ⊕ ι₂`. Distinct from Basis/Basic and DirectSum/Basis.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Bases for modules over the opposite ring: `Basis.mulOpposite` transfers a basis from `R`-modules to `Rᵐᵒᵖ`-modules. Distinct from Basis/Basic and InnerProductSpace/MulOpposite.

Name: Flag.lean
Description: Flag of subspaces from a basis: the complete flag `⟨span {e₀}, span {e₀, e₁}, …, M⟩` associated to an ordered basis. Distinct from Basis/Basic and LinearAlgebra/Eigenspace/Triangularizable.

Name: VectorSpace.lean
Description: Bases for vector spaces (modules over a division ring): existence (Zorn's lemma), and the fact that every vector space has a basis. Proves `exists_basis`. Distinct from Basis/Basic (construction API) and Dimension/DivisionRing.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Bases for submodules: extending a basis of a submodule to a basis of the ambient module, and the splitting of short exact sequences with free quotient. Distinct from Basis/Basic and FreeModule/PID.

Name: SMul.lean
Description: Scalar multiplication and bases: how a basis for an `R`-module `M` interacts with extensions of scalars and `SMul` structures. Distinct from Basis/Basic and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of bases: any two bases of a module (over a division ring or satisfying the invariant basis number property) have the same cardinality. Proves `Basis.card_eq_card` and connects to `rank`. Distinct from Basis/Basic and Dimension/Basic.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Bilinear forms with respect to a basis: the matrix of a bilinear form in a given basis, and the dual basis for a non-degenerate bilinear form. Distinct from Basis/Basic and BilinearForm/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for bases of modules: `Basis.mk`, `Basis.ext`, coordinate functions `Basis.repr`, the `Basis.equivFun` isomorphism with `ι → R`, and `Basis.constr` (constructing linear maps by specifying values on basis elements). The main API file for the Basis folder.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Bases indexed by `Fin n`: `Basis.fin`, `Matrix.stdBasis`, and the standard basis `e i` of `Fin n → R` where `(e i) j = if i = j then 1 else 0`. Distinct from Basis/Basic (general index) and Matrix/StdBasis.

Name: Exact.lean
Description: Bases and exact sequences: if `0 → M → N → P → 0` is exact and `M`, `P` have bases then `N` has a basis (the Bass–Quillen context). Distinct from Basis/Basic and FreeModule/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Basis ι R M`: a linear equivalence `M ≃ₗ[R] ι →₀ R` (the free module on `ι`). Contains the type definition, `Basis.repr`, and the basic properties. Split from Basis/Basic for import reasons. Distinct from Basis/Basic (full API) and LinearIndependent/Defs.

Name: Finsupp
Description: Linear algebra using finitely supported functions (Finsupp): the canonical free module construction and its universal property.

Name: SumProd.lean
Description: Finsupp and sum/product types: `Finsupp.sumElim`, `Finsupp.prodCoprod` — isomorphisms between `(ι₁ ⊕ ι₂) →₀ R` and `(ι₁ →₀ R) × (ι₂ →₀ R)`. Distinct from Finsupp/Pi and DirectSum/Finsupp.

Name: Span.lean
Description: Span via Finsupp: the span of a set `s ⊆ M` equals the image of `linearCombination` on finitely-supported functions with support in `s`. Characterisation: `Submodule.span_eq_range_linearCombination`. Distinct from Finsupp/LinearCombination and Span/Defs.

Name: VectorSpace.lean
Description: Free vector spaces via Finsupp: `ι →₀ k` as a vector space over a field, existence of bases indexed by arbitrary types. Connects `Finsupp` theory to the vector space case. Distinct from Finsupp/Defs and Basis/VectorSpace.

Name: Supported.lean
Description: Finsupp functions supported on a subset: `Finsupp.supported R M s` — the submodule of `ι →₀ M` with support in `s ⊆ ι`. Proves `supported_univ = ⊤`. Distinct from Finsupp/Span and Finsupp/Defs.

Name: LinearCombination.lean
Description: Linear combinations via `Finsupp`: `Finsupp.linearCombination R f` — the linear map sending `c : ι →₀ R` to `∑ i, c i • f i ∈ M`. Used as the evaluation map for spans. Distinct from Finsupp/LSum and Finsupp/Span.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Linear structure on `ι →₀ M`: `Finsupp.lmapDomain`, `Finsupp.lcoeFun`, and the free module `ι →₀ R` as the free `R`-module on `ι`. Core definitions split for import purposes. Distinct from Finsupp/Span and Basis/Defs.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Finsupp and pi types: the isomorphism `(⊕ᵢ Mᵢ) ≃ₗ (ι →₀ R)` for specific families and embeddings of `Fin n → R` into `Finsupp`. Distinct from Finsupp/Defs and Basis/Fin.

Name: LSum.lean
Description: `Finsupp.lsum`: the linear map `(ι →₀ R) →ₗ M` determined by a function `ι → M` (summing over the support). The universal property of the free module. Distinct from Finsupp/Defs and Finsupp/LinearCombination.

Name: FiniteDimensional
Description: Finite-dimensional vector spaces: characterizations, finite bases, and decidability of linear independence.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for finite-dimensional spaces: `finrank_map_le`, injectivity/surjectivity equivalence for endomorphisms of finite-dimensional spaces, and `LinearMap.bijective_iff_finrank`. Distinct from FiniteDimensional/Basic and Dimension/RankNullity.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: `FiniteDimensional k V`: a vector space `V` over a field `k` with `finrank k V < ∞`, or equivalently `Module.Finite k V`. The definitional core. Distinct from FiniteDimensional/Basic (API) and Dimension/Finite.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for finite-dimensional vector spaces: `FiniteDimensional.finrank_eq_zero_iff`, `finrank_top`, `Subspace.finrank_add_finrank_orthogonal`, and the equivalence `FiniteDimensional k V ↔ finrank k V < ∞`. The main API file for FiniteDimensional.

Name: FreeProduct
Description: Free products of modules and algebras in the linear algebra context.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Free product of algebras (coproduct in the category of `R`-algebras): `FreeProduct R A B` — the algebra generated by copies of `A` and `B` with no relations between them. Universal property and basic API. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic (which is the monoidal product, not the coproduct).

Name: FreeModule
Description: Free modules over rings: the universal property, rank, and comparison with vector spaces over fields.

Name: Finite
Description: Finitely generated free modules: free modules of finite rank, the rank as a natural number, and the existence of a finite basis. The isomorphism Rⁿ ≅ Rᵐ implies n = m for a commutative ring (the invariant basis number property). Used in the classification of finitely generated modules over a PID. Distinct from FreeModule (no finiteness).

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix representation of finite free modules.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotients of finitely-generated free modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Finitely-generated free modules.

Name: CardQuotient.lean
Description: Cardinality of quotients of free modules.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: Norm on free modules: the operator norm induced by a basis and the equivalence of norms on finite-rank free modules. Distinct from FreeModule/Basic and Analysis/NormedSpace.

Name: ModN.lean
Description: Free modules mod n: `(ℤ/nℤ)^m` and quotients of free modules by `n * M`. Used in the classification theorem. Distinct from FreeModule/IdealQuotient and FreeModule/Int.

Name: StrongRankCondition.lean
Description: Free modules satisfy the strong rank condition: for a commutative Noetherian ring `R`, the module `R^m` does not surject onto `R^n` for `m < n`. Distinct from Dimension/StrongRankCondition and FreeModule/Basic.

Name: PID.lean
Description: Structure theorem for finitely generated modules over a PID: every such module is isomorphic to `R^r ⊕ R/d₁ ⊕ ⋯ ⊕ R/dₖ` with `d₁ | d₂ | … | dₖ`. The Smith normal form. Distinct from FreeModule/Int (ℤ case) and Matrix/Transvection.

Name: Determinant.lean
Description: Determinant for free modules: `Basis.det e` — the alternating multilinear form on a free module of finite rank detecting the volume element. Distinct from Matrix/Determinant files and Alternating/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `Module.Free R M`: a module admitting a basis. Core API: `Module.Free.exists_basis`, the tensor product of free modules is free. Distinct from FreeModule/PID (structure theorem over PIDs) and Basis/VectorSpace.

Name: Int.lean
Description: Free ℤ-modules: every submodule of `ℤⁿ` is free (of rank ≤ n), and the classification of finitely generated abelian groups follows. Distinct from FreeModule/PID (PID case) and FreeModule/Basic.

Name: IdealQuotient.lean
Description: Quotients of free modules by ideals: `(R^n / I * R^n)` as a module, and the structure theorem for finitely generated modules over PIDs in terms of quotients by principal ideals. Distinct from FreeModule/PID and Quotient/Basic.

Name: ExteriorPower
Description: Exterior powers ∧ᵏM of a module: the k-th graded piece of the exterior algebra, classifying alternating k-linear forms.

Name: Pairing.lean
Description: Pairing between exterior powers: the natural pairing `⋀ⁿ M × ⋀ⁿ M* → R` via the determinant of the evaluation matrix. Used to identify `⋀ⁿ M*` with the dual of `⋀ⁿ M`. Distinct from ExteriorPower/Basic and PerfectPairing/Basic.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis of the `n`-th exterior power: when `M` has basis `e₁, …, eₘ`, `ExteriorPower R M n` has basis `{e_I | I ∈ Finset.powersetCard n (Fin m)}`. Used to compute dimensions. Distinct from ExteriorPower/Basic and ExteriorAlgebra/Basis.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `ExteriorPower R M n` (the `n`-th exterior power of `M`): the degree-`n` graded component of `ExteriorAlgebra R M`, with the wedge product map `ExteriorPower.ι`. Distinct from ExteriorAlgebra/Basic (the full algebra) and ExteriorPower/Basis.

Name: Dual
Description: Dual modules and dual spaces: the natural pairing, double dual embedding, and finite-dimensional reflexivity.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Dual module: `Module.Dual R M = M →ₗ[R] R`, the evaluation pairing `Module.dualPairing`, and the natural map `ι : M → M**` (double dual embedding). Distinct from Dual/Basis (dual basis) and Dual/Lemmas.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for dual modules: annihilators, the codual, and the relationship between submodules of `M` and `M*`. Proves `Submodule.dualAnnihilator_gc` (Galois connection). Distinct from Dual/Defs and BilinearForm/Orthogonal.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Dual module and base change: `(M ⊗[R] S)* ≃ M* ⊗[R] S` for flat `S`-algebras. Distinct from Dual/Defs and TensorProduct/Tower.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Dual basis: `Basis.dualBasis e` — the dual basis `eᵢ* : M →ₗ R` satisfying `eᵢ*(eⱼ) = δᵢⱼ`. Proves the dual basis exists for finite bases and `Basis.dualBasis_eq`. Distinct from Dual/Defs and BilinearForm/DualLattice.

Name: DirectSum
Description: Direct sums of families of modules: the coproduct in the category of modules with universal property and component projections.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Direct sum as finitely-supported functions: `⨁ᵢ M = {f : ι → M | finsupp.support f finite}`, and the isomorphism with `ι →₀ M` when `M` is a module. Distinct from Finsupp/Defs and DirectSum/Basis.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor product distributes over direct sums: `(⨁ᵢ Mᵢ) ⊗ N ≃ₗ ⨁ᵢ (Mᵢ ⊗ N)`. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and DirectSum/Basis.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Bases of direct sums: a basis of `⨁ᵢ Mᵢ` from bases of each summand `Mᵢ`, via the canonical injection. Distinct from DirectSum/Finite and Basis/Prod.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Direct sums of finitely many modules: equivalences `⨁ᵢ Mᵢ ≃ₗ Πᵢ Mᵢ` (for finite `ι`) and finiteness conditions. Distinct from DirectSum/Basis and Dimension/Constructions.

Name: ExteriorAlgebra
Description: The exterior (Grassmann) algebra ∧(M): the universal alternating algebra on M, graded by exterior degree.

Name: Grading.lean
Description: Graded structure of the exterior algebra: `ExteriorAlgebra.gradedAlgebra` — the ℕ-grading by wedge degree, and `ExteriorAlgebra.ι` maps degree-1 elements. Distinct from ExteriorAlgebra/Basic and CliffordAlgebra/Grading.

Name: OfAlternating.lean
Description: Linear maps from the exterior algebra via alternating maps: `ExteriorAlgebra.liftAlternating` — a linear map `ExteriorAlgebra R M → N` is the same as an alternating map `M →ₗ[R] N` (for the degree-1 component). Distinct from ExteriorAlgebra/Basic and Alternating/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Exterior algebra `ExteriorAlgebra R M`: the quotient of the tensor algebra by `v⊗v = 0`. Universal property `ExteriorAlgebra.lift`, the wedge product, and `ExteriorAlgebra.ι_sq_zero`. Distinct from ExteriorAlgebra/Grading and CliffordAlgebra/Basic (Clifford generalises exterior).

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis of the exterior algebra: when `M` has a basis `e₁, …, eₙ`, the exterior algebra has the wedge products `e_{i₁} ∧ ⋯ ∧ e_{iₖ}` (for strictly increasing sequences) as a basis. Distinct from ExteriorAlgebra/Basic and ExteriorPower/Basis.

Name: Eigenspace
Description: Eigenspaces and generalized eigenspaces of linear operators, eigenvalue theory, and the primary decomposition theorem.

Name: Semisimple.lean
Description: Semisimple endomorphisms: `f` is semisimple iff the minimal polynomial is squarefree, equivalently iff `M` decomposes as a direct sum of eigenspaces. Distinct from Eigenspace/Triangularizable and Eigenspace/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Eigenspaces of operators on product spaces: the eigenspace of `f × g` at `(μ, ν)` is `eigenspace f μ × eigenspace g ν`. Distinct from Eigenspace/Basic and Dimension/Constructions.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: Generalised eigenspaces (nil-spaces): `Module.End.genEigenspace f μ k = ker (f - μ)^k` and the stabilisation of the ascending chain of generalised eigenspaces. Used in the Jordan normal form. Distinct from Eigenspace/Basic and Eigenspace/Triangularizable.

Name: Triangularizable.lean
Description: Triangularizable operators: an endomorphism is triangularizable over an algebraically closed field iff the minimal polynomial splits. The Jordan–Chevalley decomposition setup. Distinct from Eigenspace/Semisimple and Eigenspace/Basic.

Name: Minpoly.lean
Description: Minimal polynomial and eigenvalues: the minimal polynomial divides any polynomial annihilating the operator, and its roots are exactly the eigenvalues. Distinct from Eigenspace/Charpoly and RingTheory/Minpoly.

Name: Charpoly.lean
Description: Characteristic polynomial and eigenvalues: eigenvalues are roots of the characteristic polynomial, and over algebraically closed fields all roots are eigenvalues (Cayley–Hamilton). Distinct from Eigenspace/Basic and Charpoly/ToMatrix.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Eigenspaces and eigenvalues: `Module.End.eigenspace f μ = ker (f - μ • id)`, `HasEigenvalue f μ`, and the spectrum as eigenvalues over an algebraically closed field. Proves eigenvectors with distinct eigenvalues are linearly independent. Distinct from Eigenspace/Triangularizable and Eigenspace/Charpoly.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Eigenvalues and eigenspaces of matrices: `Matrix.HasEigenvalue A μ` iff `det(A - μ * 1) = 0`, and explicit computation. Distinct from Eigenspace/Basic (abstract endomorphisms) and Analysis/Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: ContinuousLinearMap.lean
Description: Eigenspaces of continuous linear maps on Banach spaces: `ContinuousLinearMap.eigenspace`, the relationship between the algebraic and topological eigenspace, and spectral theory connections. Distinct from Eigenspace/Basic and Analysis/CStarAlgebra/Spectrum.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Products of modules: the direct product M x N as a module, with projections and the universal property.

Name: PiTensorProduct.lean
Description: The Pi tensor product of a family of modules: the universal multilinear map out of a family, with basis elements.

Name: Ray.lean
Description: Rays in a module: equivalence classes of nonzero vectors under positive scaling, used in oriented geometry.

Name: Projection.lean
Description: Projection maps: idempotent linear maps and the splitting of a module as a direct sum of image and kernel.

Name: PID.lean
Description: Modules over a PID: the structure theorem classifying finitely generated modules as direct sums of cyclic modules.

Name: LinearPMap.lean
Description: Linear partial maps: linear maps defined on a submodule, with the graph and domain extension theorems.

Name: LinearDisjoint.lean
Description: Linearly disjoint submodules: a condition on pairs of submodules generalizing field linear disjointness.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi-modules: the product of an indexed family of modules, with component projections and the universal property.

Name: Orientation.lean
Description: Orientations of vector spaces: a choice of basis class up to positive determinant, and oriented volume forms.

Name: Reflection.lean
Description: Reflections in a vector space: linear involutions fixing a hyperplane, with connections to Coxeter groups.

Name: Transvection.lean
Description: Transvection matrices: elementary matrices of the form I + c times e_{ij} and their generation of SL(n).

Name: Trace.lean
Description: The trace of an endomorphism: the sum of diagonal entries of a matrix or the canonical contraction map.

Name: Vandermonde.lean
Description: The Vandermonde matrix: its determinant as a product of differences, with applications to polynomial interpolation.

Name: UnitaryGroup.lean
Description: The unitary group U(n): matrices satisfying A times A-star = I, and the special unitary group SU(n).

Name: SymplecticGroup.lean
Description: The symplectic group Sp(2n): matrices preserving a nondegenerate skew-symmetric form.

Name: SModEq.lean
Description: Congruence modulo a submodule: the SModEq relation and its use in p-adic module theory.

Name: Semisimple.lean
Description: Semisimple modules and linear maps: modules splitting as direct sums of simple submodules, and semisimple endomorphisms.

Name: StdBasis.lean
Description: The standard basis: the canonical basis vectors of a Pi-module, with coordinate projections and dual basis.

Name: SpecialLinearGroup.lean
Description: The special linear group SL(n, R): matrices of determinant 1, and its action on projective space.

Name: LeftExact.lean
Description: Left-exactness of Hom: the functor Hom(M, -) is left-exact and the long exact sequence in Ext.

Name: Countable.lean
Description: Countable-dimensional vector spaces and their bases, with the countable version of Zorn's lemma.

Name: ConvexSpace.lean
Description: Convex spaces as modules: the formalization of convex combination axioms inside a linear space.

Name: Determinant.lean
Description: The determinant of a linear map: the unique scalar satisfying det(fg) = det(f)det(g), with the Leibniz formula.

Name: CrossProduct.lean
Description: The cross product in R^3: the bilinear map satisfying |u x v| = |u||v|sin(theta).

Name: Contraction.lean
Description: Contraction maps between tensor products and Hom: the natural isomorphism M-dual tensor N isomorphic to Hom(M, N).

Name: BilinearMap.lean
Description: Bilinear maps and their currying: the equivalence between bilinear maps and linear maps into Hom.

Name: AnnihilatingPolynomial.lean
Description: Annihilating polynomials of linear maps: polynomials p such that p(T) = 0, with the minimal polynomial as the generator.

Name: Coevaluation.lean
Description: Coevaluation maps in duality: the canonical map R to M tensor M-dual used in the rigid monoidal structure on free modules.

Name: Center.lean
Description: The center of an endomorphism algebra: the commutant of the image of a ring in End(M).

Name: DFinsupp.lean
Description: Dependently-typed Finsupp in linear algebra: the algebraic theory of direct sums of modules via DFinsupp.

Name: Isomorphisms.lean
Description: Standard module isomorphism theorems: the first, second, and third isomorphism theorems for modules.

Name: InvariantBasisNumber.lean
Description: The invariant basis number property: rings where all bases of a free module have the same cardinality.

Name: Lagrange.lean
Description: Lagrange interpolation: the unique polynomial of degree < n passing through n given points, with the explicit formula.

Name: JordanChevalley.lean
Description: Jordan-Chevalley decomposition: splitting an endomorphism as a sum of a semisimple and a nilpotent part that commute.

Name: Goursat.lean
Description: Goursat's lemma for submodules: classifying submodules of M direct sum N via pairs of submodules and a quotient isomorphism.

Name: FixedSubmodule.lean
Description: Fixed submodules of group actions: the submodule fixed by a group action on a module.

Name: FiniteSpan.lean
Description: Finitely spanned submodules: submodules with a finite generating set and their properties.

Name: GeneralLinearGroup.lean
Description: The general linear group GL(M) as units of End(M): the group of invertible endomorphisms and its matrix representation.

Name: FreeAlgebra.lean
Description: Free algebras as modules: the underlying free module structure of the free algebra and basis elements by word length.

Name: Lean
Description: The interactive theorem prover and programming language in which Mathlib is written. Used to state definitions, theorems, and proofs with machine-checkable correctness, enabling formal verification of entire mathematical theories. All math fields formalized in Mathlib rely on Lean.

Name: Meta
Description: Meta-programming utilities for Lean 4: tools for inspecting and constructing terms, hypotheses, and proof states programmatically.

Name: Tactic
Description: Meta-level tactic utilities: functions for running tactics programmatically, inspecting the current goal state, applying lemmas, and creating new goals. Used when writing tactics that call other tactics or need fine-grained control over the proof state. Distinct from Lean/Elab/Tactic (which operates at the elaboration level with syntax).

Name: Rewrite.lean
Description: Tactic-level rewrite utilities.

Name: RefinedDiscrTree
Description: The refined discrimination tree: an indexing data structure for efficiently looking up lemmas by the shape of the conclusion or a hypothesis. An improvement over the standard discrimination tree that handles more patterns. Used by simp, apply, and exact? to search the lemma database. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Tactic (tactic execution utilities).

Name: Initialize.lean
Description: Initializing the refined discrimination tree.

Name: Lookup.lean
Description: Lookup in the refined discrimination tree.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Refined discrimination tree basics.

Name: Encode.lean
Description: Encoding expressions for the refined discrimination tree.

Name: KAbstractPositions.lean
Description: k-abstract positions: `kAbstractPositions e k` — finding the positions in expression `e` where `k` appears, used by `conv` and `rw` to identify rewrite sites. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Basic.

Name: RefinedDiscrTree.lean
Description: Refined discrimination tree: an enhanced discrimination tree with better handling of universe polymorphism and type-class arguments, used in Mathlib's lemma search. Distinct from Lean/Meta/DiscrTree.

Name: Simp.lean
Description: Simp set utilities: helpers for constructing and querying `SimpTheorems` sets, adding/removing lemmas, and running `simp` programmatically from tactic code. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core Meta utilities for Mathlib: helpers for `MetaM` — `mkFreshMVar`, `isDefEq`, `inferType`, `whnf`, `reduce`, and wrapping of common pattern-matching operations. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Simp.

Name: CongrTheorems.lean
Description: Congruence theorem infrastructure: `mkCongrTheorem` and related utilities for generating and applying congruence lemmas in `congr`-style tactics. Used by `gcongr` and `simp`. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Basic.

Name: DiscrTree.lean
Description: Discrimination tree utilities: wrappers around Lean's `DiscrTree` for storing and retrieving lemmas by their head symbol/shape. Used in `simp`, `norm_num`, and the `fun_prop` tactic. Distinct from Lean/Meta/RefinedDiscrTree.

Name: PrettyPrinter
Description: Pretty printing infrastructure: delaborators and formatters that control how Lean expressions are displayed to the user.

Name: Delaborator.lean
Description: Delaborator utilities: helpers for the Lean 4 delaborator (the inverse elaborator converting `Expr` back to `Syntax`) — custom delaborators for Mathlib notations and structured output. Distinct from Lean/Elab/Term.

Name: MessageData
Description: Utilities for constructing and formatting MessageData — the structured diagnostic messages shown in Lean's infoview and error panel.

Name: Trace.lean
Description: Trace message infrastructure: helpers for `withTraceNode` and structured trace output used in debugging tactic implementations and `set_option trace.*`. Distinct from Lean/MessageData/ForExprs.

Name: ForExprs.lean
Description: MessageData formatting for expressions: utilities for pretty-printing `Expr` values in tactic error messages and informational output. Distinct from Lean/MessageData/Trace.

Name: Elab
Description: Elaboration utilities: helpers for writing tactics and commands that run during the elaboration phase of Lean 4.

Name: Tactic
Description: Elaboration utilities for tactic mode: helpers for accessing the tactic state, modifying goals, and interacting with the metavariable context during tactic elaboration. Used by tactic authors to write new tactics in Lean 4. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Tactic (meta-level tactic utilities that work below the elaborator).

Name: Meta.lean
Description: Meta-level tactic elaboration.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic tactic elaboration utilities.

Name: Term.lean
Description: Term elaboration utilities for Mathlib: helpers for `TermElabM` — elaborating expressions, resolving names, and working with the term elaboration monad. Distinct from Lean/Meta/Basic.

Name: InfoTree.lean
Description: InfoTree utilities for Mathlib elaboration: helpers for traversing and querying the `InfoTree` (the elaboration metadata tree storing tactic states, term types, etc.) used in tactic widgets and linters. Distinct from Lean/Elab/Term.

Name: Expr
Description: Utilities for working with Lean 4 Expr: syntactic traversal, substitution, free variable detection, and expression comparison.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: Rational number expressions: helpers for recognising and constructing Lean expressions representing `Rat` literals, used in `norm_num` and `decide` extensions. Distinct from Lean/Expr/Basic.

Name: ReplaceRec.lean
Description: Recursive expression replacement with free-variable control: `Expr.replaceRec` — a variant of `Expr.replace` that handles bound variables correctly during recursive substitution. Distinct from Lean/Expr/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Expression utilities for Mathlib metaprogramming: helpers for constructing, destructuring, and querying Lean `Expr` values — `mkApp`, `mkConst`, `getAppFn`, `getAppArgs`, and pattern-matching helpers. Distinct from Lean/Expr/ExtraRecognizers.

Name: ExtraRecognizers.lean
Description: Extra expression recognisers: additional `Expr.is*` predicates not in core Lean — `isNatLit`, `isIntLit`, `isStringLit`, `isMulApp`, `isAddApp`, etc. Used in tactic implementations. Distinct from Lean/Expr/Basic.

Name: Linter.lean
Description: Linter infrastructure for Lean 4: the Linter monad, lint commands, and utilities for writing custom lint checks.

Name: Json.lean
Description: JSON encoding and decoding in Lean 4: the Json type, serialization to string, and basic parsers.

Name: GoalsLocation.lean
Description: Goals location data: tracking which hypotheses and goals are relevant to a tactic call in the infoview.

Name: LocalContext.lean
Description: Local context utilities: iterating over local declarations, filtering hypotheses, and modifying the local context.

Name: Thunk.lean
Description: Thunks in Lean 4: delayed computations and their use for lazy evaluation in tactics.

Name: Name.lean
Description: Name utilities for Lean 4: name manipulation, suffix checking, and generating fresh names in the elaborator.

Name: Meta.lean
Description: Meta-level utilities: whnf reduction, inferring types, checking definitional equality, and working with universe levels.

Name: EnvExtension.lean
Description: Environment extensions: persistent state for Lean attributes and tactic data stored across elaboration.

Name: CoreM.lean
Description: CoreM monad utilities: logging, error recovery, and environment manipulation in the Lean 4 kernel.

Name: ContextInfo.lean
Description: Context info for interactive Lean: metadata attached to terms for hover, go-to-definition, and infoview display.

Name: Environment.lean
Description: Lean 4 environment utilities: inspecting declarations, checking axioms, and traversing the declaration namespace.

Name: FoldEnvironment.lean
Description: Folding over the Lean environment: iterating over all declarations efficiently for linting and analysis.

Name: Expr.lean
Description: Expression utilities for Lean 4: traversal, substitution, abstraction, instantiation, and expression comparison.

Name: Exception.lean
Description: Exception handling in Lean 4: converting exceptions to messages, re-throwing, and suppressing expected errors.

Name: Mathport
Description: The tool that translates mathlib3 (written in Lean 3) into mathlib4 (Lean 4). Used to preserve decades of formalized results across major system upgrades, ensuring continuity of verified knowledge. All math fields previously formalized in mathlib3 rely on Mathport to remain available.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation for the Mathport directory: describes the Lean 3 to Lean 4 porting infrastructure and translation conventions.

Name: Logic
Description: Formalizes propositional and predicate logic, natural deduction, models, and set-theoretic foundations. Used to clarify the rules of inference, prove consistency and independence results, and study formal systems themselves. Relied on by set theory, model theory, computability, and the metamathematics underlying all of Mathlib.

Name: IsEmpty
Description: The IsEmpty typeclass for types provably having no inhabitants, with instances and elimination principles.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `IsEmpty α`: a proposition asserting `α → False`. The core typeclass. Distinct from IsEmpty/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: IsEmpty API: `IsEmpty α` — a type with no elements. Properties: `IsEmpty.elim`, `not_nonempty_iff`, `Unique.instIsEmpty`. Distinct from IsEmpty/Defs.

Name: Godel
Description: Gödel's incompleteness theorems: formalization of the first and second incompleteness theorems for sufficiently strong formal systems.

Name: GodelBetaFunction.lean
Description: Gödel's beta function: `Nat.godel_beta a b i` — a primitive recursive function encoding sequences of naturals, used in the arithmetisation of syntax for incompleteness theorems. Distinct from Logic/Function/Iterate.

Name: Small
Description: Small types relative to a universe: types that are equivalent to a type in a smaller universe, enabling universe-polymorphic constructions.

Name: List.lean
Description: Smallness of list types: `Small (List α)` from `Small α`. Distinct from Small/Basic.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Smallness of set types: `Small (Set α)` and `Small {x : α | P x}` results. Distinct from Small/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Small types: `Small.{u} α` — the type `α` is (essentially) in universe `u`, i.e., there exists a type `β : Type u` with `α ≃ β`. Properties and standard instances. Distinct from Small/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Small.{u} α`: a type-class asserting `α` is essentially small. Distinct from Small/Basic (API).

Name: Nontrivial
Description: The Nontrivial typeclass asserting the existence of two distinct elements, used as a hypothesis across algebra and analysis.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Nontrivial α`: the typeclass `∃ (x y : α), x ≠ y`. The core definition. Distinct from Nontrivial/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Nontrivial types: `Nontrivial α` — a type with at least two distinct elements. Properties and interaction with algebraic structures. Distinct from Nontrivial/Defs.

Name: Encodable
Description: Encodable types: countable types that embed into ℕ, with canonical encodings for products, sums, and lists.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Encodability of pi types: `Encodable (Π i : Fin n, α i)` from `Encodable α`. Distinct from Encodable/Basic.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Encodability of lattice operations: `Encodable` instances for `WithBot`, `WithTop`, and ordered structures. Distinct from Encodable/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `Encodable α` — a type with an injection into `ℕ`. Instances for `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `ℚ`, `List α`, `Finset α`. Used for countability arguments. Distinct from Encodable/Pi.

Name: Embedding
Description: Injective maps packaged as bundled embeddings, with the typeclass structure and composition laws.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Embeddings of set subtypes: `Set.embeddingOfSubset`, embeddings from `Set.inclusion`, and the interaction between `Embedding` and `Set.range`. Distinct from Embedding/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Function embeddings: `Function.Embedding α β` — injective functions `α ↪ β`. Composition, products, and the `EmbeddingLike` typeclass. Distinct from Embedding/Set.

Name: Function
Description: Set-theoretic and logical properties of functions: injectivity, surjectivity, bijectivity, involutions, and the axiom of choice.

Name: FromTypes.lean
Description: Functions from type-indexed families: `Function.fromTypes` — building functions from component-wise data. Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: FiberPartition.lean
Description: Fiber partition: `Function.FiberPartition f` — the partition of the domain into fibers `f⁻¹ {y}`. Distinct from Function/Coequalizer.

Name: Iterate.lean
Description: Function iteration: `f^[n] = f ∘ f ∘ ⋯ ∘ f` (`n` times), `Function.Iterate.comp`, and the orbit under iteration. Distinct from Function/Conjugate.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift and functions: lifting functions to higher universes via `ULift`. `ULift.equiv`. Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: OfArity.lean
Description: Functions of fixed arity: `Function.OfArity α β n` — the type of `n`-ary functions `α → α → ⋯ → β`. Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: DependsOn.lean
Description: Dependence on variables: `Function.DependsOn f i` — `f` genuinely depends on its `i`-th argument (not constant in it). Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: Coequalizer.lean
Description: Function coequalisers: `Function.Coequalizer f g` — the quotient by the equivalence relation generated by `f x = g x`. Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic function properties: `Function.Injective`, `Function.Surjective`, `Function.Bijective`, `Function.LeftInverse`, `Function.RightInverse`, and `Function.Involutive`. Core function API.

Name: CompTypeclasses.lean
Description: Typeclasses for composition: `Function.Comp.instFunLike` and related instances for composed functions. Used in the `fun_prop` tactic. Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core function definitions: `Function.comp`, `Function.id`, `Function.const`, `Function.flip`. The foundational file for Logic/Function. Distinct from Function/Basic.

Name: Conjugate.lean
Description: Conjugation of functions: `Function.Semiconj f g h` (`f ∘ g = h ∘ f`) and `Function.Commute f g` (`f ∘ g = g ∘ f`). Distinct from Function/Iterate.

Name: Equiv
Description: Equivalences (bijections packaged with an inverse): the Equiv type, its algebra, and canonical equivalences between standard types.

Name: Fin
Description: Equivalences involving Fin: canonical bijections between Fin n and other types, including Fin (m + n) ≅ Fin m ⊕ Fin n, Fin (m * n) ≅ Fin m × Fin n, and equivalences between function types. Used in combinatorics and in the formalization of matrix index sets.

Name: Rotate.lean
Description: Rotation equivalences for Fin.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Equivalences for Fin types.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Equivalences for `Option`: `Option α ≃ α ⊕ Unit`, `Equiv.optionEquivSumPUnit`. Distinct from Equiv/Sum.

Name: Pairwise.lean
Description: Equivalences and pairwise properties: `Pairwise.equiv` — transferring pairwise disjointness along equivalences. Distinct from Equiv/Basic.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Equivalences for multisets: bijections between `Multiset α` and `Finsupp ℕ` representations. Distinct from Equiv/Finset.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Equivalences for natural numbers: `Nat.equivPairNat` (pairing function `ℕ ≃ ℕ × ℕ`), `Equiv.optionEquivSumPUnit`, and Cantor pairing. Distinct from Equiv/Basic.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Equivalences for sets: `Set.equivSubtype`, `Equiv.Set.image`, and `Equiv.Set.sep`. Distinct from Equiv/Basic and Logic/Embedding/Set.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Equivalences for sum types: `Equiv.sumComm`, `Equiv.sumAssoc`, `Equiv.sumPUnit`. Distinct from Equiv/Prod.

Name: PartialEquiv.lean
Description: Partial equivalences: `PartialEquiv α β` — a bijection between subsets `s ⊆ α` and `t ⊆ β`. The foundation for `PartialHomeomorph`. Distinct from Equiv/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Equivalences for products: `Equiv.prodComm`, `Equiv.prodAssoc`, `Equiv.prodSumDistrib`. Distinct from Equiv/Sum.

Name: List.lean
Description: Equivalences for lists: `List.equiv` — bijections between `List α` and various other types. Distinct from Equiv/Array.

Name: Bool.lean
Description: Equivalences involving `Bool`: `Bool ≃ Fin 2`, `Bool ≃ {True, False}`. Distinct from Equiv/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Equiv α β`: a bijection as a pair of inverse functions `toFun : α → β` and `invFun : β → α` with `leftInv`/`rightInv`. The core type definition. Distinct from Equiv/Basic (API).

Name: Array.lean
Description: Equivalences for arrays: `Array.equiv` — bijections between `Array α` and `(Σ n, Fin n → α)`. Distinct from Equiv/List.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for `Equiv α β` (bijections): composition `e.trans`, `e.symm`, `Equiv.congr`, `Equiv.ofBijective`. The key workhorse file for the Equiv folder.

Name: Fintype.lean
Description: Equivalences for `Fintype` types: `Fintype.equivFin`, `Fintype.equivOfFintype`, and the uniqueness of `Fintype` structure. Distinct from Equiv/Finset.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functoriality of `Equiv`: `Equiv.map f e` — applying a functor to an equivalence. Distinct from Equiv/Basic.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Equivalences and embeddings: `Equiv.toEmbedding` — an `Equiv` gives a `Function.Embedding`. Distinct from Equiv/Basic and Logic/Embedding/Basic.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Equivalences for finsets: bijections between `Finset α` and structured types, and `Finset.equivFin`. Distinct from Equiv/Fintype.

Name: Relation.lean
Description: Logical properties of binary relations: reflexivity, symmetry, transitivity, their closures, and relational composition.

Name: Pairwise.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions: predicates holding for all pairs of distinct elements in a set, used in independence and disjointness.

Name: OpClass.lean
Description: Operator typeclasses: a unifying framework for heterogeneous operations between different types.

Name: UnivLE.lean
Description: Universe level comparisons: the UnivLE typeclass asserting that one universe is at most another.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: The Unique typeclass: types with exactly one element, and the equivalence with contractibility.

Name: Relator.lean
Description: The Relator: lifting relations on types to relations on function types, used in parametricity and transfer.

Name: Nonempty.lean
Description: Nonemptiness: the Nonempty typeclass, Classical.choice, and interactions with functions and products.

Name: ExistsUnique.lean
Description: Exists-unique quantification: the definition, elimination, and uniqueness arguments.

Name: Denumerable.lean
Description: Denumerable types: countably infinite types with a canonical bijection to N.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic logical lemmas: classical propositional logic, De Morgan's laws, and utility facts about connectives.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Additional logical lemmas supplementing Logic/Basic: specialized facts about quantifiers and connectives.

Name: IsEmpty.lean
Description: The IsEmpty typeclass: types proven to have no elements, with elimination and the vacuous truth principle.

Name: Hydra.lean
Description: The hydra game: a terminating game used to illustrate ordinal induction and well-foundedness arguments.

Name: Testing
Description: Provides regression tests, benchmarking, and linting to ensure the library remains consistent and performant. Used to prevent errors in formalized results and to guarantee that definitions and theorems behave as intended. All math fields in Mathlib benefit from Testing to maintain trustworthiness.

Name: Plausible
Description: Plausible (Lean's QuickCheck): a property-based testing framework that generates random counterexamples to conjectures before attempting a formal proof.

Name: Testable.lean
Description: Testable propositions: `Testable p` — a proposition `p` that can be checked by generating random counterexamples. The `#check` command. Translates `∀ x, p x` into a sampling loop. Distinct from Testing/Plausible/Sampleable.

Name: Sampleable.lean
Description: Sampleable typeclass for property-based testing: `Sampleable α` — a type that can be randomly sampled. Instances for `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `List`, `Fin n`, and tuples. The core sampling infrastructure for `#check`. Distinct from Testing/Plausible/Testable.

Name: Functions.lean
Description: Function generation for plausible/QuickCheck testing: random function generators for the `Plausible` testing framework, used to test universally quantified statements about functions. Distinct from Testing/Plausible/Sampleable.

Name: Tactic
Description: Comprises the collection of proof-assistant tactics that automate reasoning steps such as rewriting, induction, and case splitting. Used to construct formal proofs more efficiently and to create custom proof automation. All mathematical domains in Mathlib rely on Tactic for day-to-day proving.

Name: Positivity
Description: The positivity tactic: automatically proves goals of the form 0 < e or 0 ≤ e by structural recursion on the expression.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: `positivity` extension for finite sums and products: proving `0 ≤ ∑ i, f i` when each `f i ≥ 0`. Distinct from Positivity/Basic and NormNum/BigOperators.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core of the `positivity` tactic: the `Positivity.Result` type (`positive`, `nonneg`, `nonzero`), the `@[positivity]` attribute, and the proof search strategy. Distinct from Positivity/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic `positivity` extensions: proving `0 ≤ a^n`, `0 ≤ ‖x‖`, `0 < n!`, and other common positivity goals. Distinct from Positivity/Core (the framework) and Positivity/Finset.

Name: Order
Description: Tactics for order-theoretic goals: order and related automation for inequalities in partially and totally ordered structures.

Name: Graph
Description: The graph-based backend for the order tactic: represents the order-theoretic hypotheses as a directed graph and uses graph reachability to decide inequality goals. The transitive closure of the hypothesis graph gives the tactic its completeness for linear order goals. Distinct from the main Order tactic (which calls this backend).

Name: Tarjan.lean
Description: Tarjan's algorithm for the order tactic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order graph representation for the order tactic.

Name: ToInt.lean
Description: Embedding order goals into ℤ: translating `SuccOrder`/`PredOrder` goals into integer arithmetic for `linarith`. Distinct from Tactic/Order/Preprocessing and Tactic/Linarith/Frontend.

Name: Preprocessing.lean
Description: Preprocessing for the `order_tac` tactic: normalising order hypotheses before dispatching to a solver. Distinct from Tactic/Order/CollectFacts and Tactic/Linarith/Preprocessing.

Name: CollectFacts.lean
Description: Fact collection for the `order_tac` tactic: extracting order hypotheses from the context. Distinct from Tactic/Order/Preprocessing and Tactic/Linarith/Parsing.

Name: ReduceModChar
Description: The reduce_mod_char tactic: simplifies expressions in characteristic p by replacing integer literals with their residues mod p.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Extension mechanism for `reduce_mod_char`: allowing modules with characteristic-p arithmetic to be simplified. Distinct from Tactic/NormNum/ModEq and Tactic/Ring/Basic.

Name: Push
Description: The push_neg tactic (moves negations inward past quantifiers) and push_cast (moves coercions inward past arithmetic operations).

Name: Attr.lean
Description: The `@[push_cast]` and `@[push_neg]` attribute infrastructure: registers lemmas used by `push_cast` (pushing coercions inward) and `push_neg` (pushing negations inward). Distinct from Tactic/Ring/Basic.

Name: Monotonicity
Description: The mono tactic: proves inequalities by applying monotonicity lemmas compositionally, reducing a compound inequality to simpler ones.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas tagged `@[mono]`: monotonicity facts for arithmetic operations, `min`/`max`, and common functions used by the `mono` tactic. Distinct from Monotonicity/Basic and GCongr/CoreAttrs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The `mono` tactic: proves monotonicity goals using `@[mono]`-tagged lemmas, applying them to reduce `f a ≤ f b` to `a ≤ b` goals. Distinct from GCongr/Core (gcongr tactic) and Monotonicity/Attr.

Name: Attr.lean
Description: The `@[mono]` attribute: marks lemmas as monotonicity lemmas for the `mono` tactic. Distinct from GCongr/CoreAttrs and Monotonicity/Basic.

Name: Measurability
Description: The measurability tactic: automatically proves measurability of composite functions by chaining registered measurability lemmas.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation for the `measurability` tactic: the tactic entry point that discharges `Measurable f` goals by applying tagged `@[measurability]` lemmas. Distinct from Tactic/FunProp/Core (the general framework) and Tactic/Continuity/Init.

Name: NormNum
Description: The norm_num tactic and its extension framework: decides numerical goals involving arithmetic operations on concrete numbers.

Name: NatLog.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for natural logarithm: evaluating `Nat.log b n` (floor log base b) for literals. Distinct from NormNum/NatSqrt and SpecialFunctions/Exp.

Name: NatSqrt.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for integer square roots: evaluating `Nat.sqrt n` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/IsSquare and NormNum/NatLog.

Name: OfScientific.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for scientific notation literals: handling `1.5e3`-style numeric literals in `norm_num` goals. Distinct from NormNum/Basic.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for modular arithmetic: deciding `a ≡ b [MOD n]` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/DivMod and Tactic/Simproc/Divisors.

Name: NatFactorial.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for factorials: evaluating `n!` and `n.choose k` for small numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/BigOperators and NormNum/NatFib.

Name: NatFib.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for Fibonacci numbers: evaluating `Nat.fib n` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/NatFactorial and SpecificLimits/Fibonacci.

Name: Ordinal.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for ordinal arithmetic: evaluating ordinal expressions involving `ω`, `+`, `*`, `^` for concrete ordinals. Distinct from NormNum/Basic and SetTheory/Ordinal files.

Name: Prime.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for primality: deciding `Nat.Prime p` for numeric literals via a certified primality test. Distinct from NormNum/IsCoprime and NumberTheory/Primes.

Name: RealSqrt.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for real square roots: evaluating `Real.sqrt n` for perfect squares. Distinct from NormNum/NatSqrt and SpecialFunctions/Sqrt.

Name: Result.lean
Description: The `NormNum.Result` type and its API: the data structure representing a `norm_num` verification (either a numeric value or a proof of a boolean property). Distinct from NormNum/Core.

Name: Parity.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for parity: deciding `Even n` and `Odd n` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/Basic and NormNum/DivMod.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for powers: evaluating `a ^ n` for numeric literals, including large powers via fast exponentiation. Distinct from NormNum/Basic and NormNum/PowMod.

Name: PowMod.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for modular exponentiation: evaluating `a ^ n % m` efficiently. Distinct from NormNum/Pow and NormNum/ModEq.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core infrastructure for `norm_num`: the `NormNum.Result` type, the `@[norm_num]` attribute, and the dispatch mechanism for extending `norm_num`. Distinct from NormNum/Basic (user-facing extensions).

Name: DivMod.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for integer division and remainder: evaluating `n / m` and `n % m` for literals. Distinct from NormNum/Basic and NormNum/GCD.

Name: Eq.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for equality and inequality of numeric expressions. Distinct from NormNum/Ineq and NormNum/Basic.

Name: Abs.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for absolute value: evaluating `|n|` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/Core and NormNum/Ineq.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic `norm_num` extensions: arithmetic on numeric literals (`+`, `*`, `-`, `^`), comparison (`<`, `≤`, `=`), and the main `norm_num` tactic entry. Distinct from NormNum/Core (the meta-framework).

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for `∑` and `∏`: evaluating finite sums and products over explicit ranges. Distinct from NormNum/Basic and Tactic/Simproc/Factors.

Name: GCD.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for GCD and LCM: evaluating `Nat.gcd m n` and `Nat.lcm m n` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/IsCoprime and NormNum/DivMod.

Name: IsCoprime.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for coprimality: proving `IsCoprime m n` (or `Nat.Coprime m n`) for numeric literals via GCD computation. Distinct from NormNum/GCD.

Name: IsSquare.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for perfect squares: deciding `IsSquare n` for natural number literals. Distinct from NormNum/NatSqrt and NormNum/Irrational.

Name: LegendreSymbol.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for the Legendre symbol: computing `(a / p)` for explicit primes `p`. Distinct from NumberTheory/LegendreSymbol and NormNum/Prime.

Name: Ineq.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for strict and non-strict inequalities: evaluating `a < b`, `a ≤ b` for numeric expressions. Distinct from NormNum/Eq and NormNum/Basic.

Name: Inv.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for inversion in a field: evaluating `n⁻¹` and `1/n` for numeric literals. Distinct from NormNum/Basic and Tactic/FieldSimp/Lemmas.

Name: Irrational.lean
Description: `norm_num` extension for irrationality: proving `Irrational (√n)` for nonsquare positive integers. Distinct from NormNum/RealSqrt and NormNum/IsSquare.

Name: Nontriviality
Description: The nontriviality tactic: automatically discharges or reduces goals that require a type to be nontrivial (have at least two distinct elements).

Name: Core.lean
Description: The `nontriviality` tactic: derives `Nontrivial α` from the context or from the goal when the hypothesis `(1 : α) ≠ 0` (or similar) is needed. Distinct from Tactic/Positivity/Core.

Name: TacticAnalysis
Description: Infrastructure for analyzing and introspecting tactic proofs, used for linting, explode, and related meta-level tools.

Name: Declarations.lean
Description: Declarations for tactic analysis tools: data structures for recording tactic usage statistics and coverage in Mathlib. Distinct from Linter/UnusedTactic.

Name: Simps
Description: The @[simps] attribute: automatically generates @[simp] lemmas for the fields of a structure based on its definition.

Name: NotationClass.lean
Description: The `@[notation_class]` attribute used by `@[simps]`: marks typeclasses whose notation should be unfolded in `@[simps]` lemmas. Distinct from Simps/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The `@[simps]` attribute: automatically generates `@[simp]` lemmas for the projections of a structure. Distinct from Simps/NotationClass and Tactic/Attr/Core.

Name: Widget
Description: Interactive infoview widgets: custom displays in the Lean 4 VSCode panel that make proof state more readable for specific tactics.

Name: LibraryRewrite.lean
Description: The library rewrite widget: suggests applicable `rw` lemmas from Mathlib for the current goal, shown in the Infoview. Distinct from Widget/InteractiveUnfold.

Name: InteractiveUnfold.lean
Description: The interactive unfold widget: Infoview panel allowing the user to click on a term to unfold its definition. Distinct from Widget/LibraryRewrite.

Name: SelectInsertParamsClass.lean
Description: Parameter class for select-insert widgets: the typeclass `SelectInsertParams` used by interactive widgets that insert selected terms. Distinct from Widget/SelectPanelUtils.

Name: StringDiagram.lean
Description: The string diagram widget: renders string diagrams for monoidal/traced categories in the Lean Infoview. Distinct from Widget/CommDiag and Tactic/CategoryTheory/Coherence.

Name: SelectPanelUtils.lean
Description: Utilities for select-panel widgets: shared infrastructure for Infoview panels where the user selects from a list of options. Distinct from Widget/SelectInsertParamsClass.

Name: CommDiag.lean
Description: The commutative diagram widget: renders commutative diagrams in the Lean Infoview from `comm_diag` tactic blocks. Distinct from Widget/StringDiagram.

Name: Calc.lean
Description: The `calc` widget: an interactive Infoview panel for the `calc` tactic that shows the current state of the calculation chain. Distinct from Widget/Conv and Widget/GCongr.

Name: CongrM.lean
Description: The `congrM` widget: interactive panel for the `congr` tactic showing subgoals. Distinct from Widget/GCongr.

Name: GCongr.lean
Description: The `gcongr` widget: interactive panel showing which subgoals the `gcongr` tactic generates. Distinct from Tactic/GCongr/Core and Widget/CongrM.

Name: Conv.lean
Description: The `conv` widget: Infoview panel for the `conv` tactic that highlights the focused subterm. Distinct from Widget/Calc.

Name: Translate
Description: Translation and porting utilities: tools that help bridge Lean 3 and Lean 4 tactic syntax during the mathlib4 migration.

Name: ToAdditive.lean
Description: The `to_additive` tactic/attribute: main entry point that generates the additive version of a multiplicative declaration. Distinct from Translate/Core and Translate/GuessName.

Name: TagUnfoldBoundary.lean
Description: The `@[to_additive (attr := ...)]` boundary tag: marking where the `to_additive` translation should stop unfolding. Distinct from Translate/UnfoldBoundary.

Name: UnfoldBoundary.lean
Description: Unfold boundary declarations for `to_additive`: marking definitions that should be expanded during the multiplicative-to-additive translation. Distinct from Translate/TagUnfoldBoundary.

Name: ToDual.lean
Description: The `to_dual` attribute: generates the order-dual version of a monotonicity or lattice lemma. Distinct from Translate/ToAdditive and Translate/Core.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core translation engine for `to_additive` and `to_dual`: traversing a declaration and replacing multiplicative operations with additive ones (or order-dual). Distinct from Translate/Attributes and Translate/ToAdditive.

Name: Attributes.lean
Description: Attributes used in the `to_additive` / `to_dual` translation framework: `@[to_additive]`, `@[to_dual]` and their registration. Distinct from Translate/Core.

Name: Reorder.lean
Description: Argument reordering for `to_additive`: some multiplicative lemmas have arguments in different order from their additive counterparts; this module handles the remapping. Distinct from Translate/Core.

Name: GuessName.lean
Description: Name guessing for `to_additive`: converting a multiplicative Lean name to the expected additive name (e.g., `mul_comm` → `add_comm`). Distinct from Translate/Core.

Name: Ring
Description: The ring tactic: proves equalities in commutative (semi)rings by normalization, and ring_nf for simplification.

Name: NamePowerVars.lean
Description: Naming power variables in `ring` output: similar to `NamePolyVars` but for expressions of the form `a^n`. Distinct from Ring/NamePolyVars and Ring/Common.

Name: PNat.lean
Description: `ring` extension for `ℕ+`: handling the positive naturals type in ring normal forms. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Tactic/NormNum/Basic.

Name: RingNF.lean
Description: The `ring_nf` tactic: applying the ring normalisation to a subterm (not necessarily the whole goal), putting it in polynomial normal form. Distinct from Ring/Basic (the full `ring` tactic).

Name: NamePolyVars.lean
Description: Naming polynomial variables in `ring` output: mapping expression trees to human-readable variable names in `ring_nf` output. Distinct from Ring/Common and Ring/NamePowerVars.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core of the `ring` tactic: proving equalities in commutative (semi)rings by normalising to a canonical polynomial form. Distinct from Ring/RingNF (the normalisation form) and Ring/Common.

Name: Common.lean
Description: Shared utilities for `ring` and `ring_nf`: the polynomial normal form data structures. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Ring/RingNF.

Name: Compare.lean
Description: The `ring_nf` comparison mode: comparing normal forms for inequality goals (used in `positivity`). Distinct from Ring/Basic and Tactic/Positivity/Core.

Name: Relation
Description: Tactics for relational goals: rfl, symm, trans with automation for equivalence relations and congruence closure.

Name: Symm.lean
Description: The `symm` tactic extension: applying symmetry via `@[symm]`-tagged lemmas for non-standard symmetric relations. Distinct from Tactic/Relation/Rfl.

Name: Rfl.lean
Description: The `rfl` tactic extension: handling reflexivity for relations beyond `Eq`, using `@[refl]`-tagged lemmas. Distinct from Tactic/Relation/Symm.

Name: Simproc
Description: Simp procedures (simproc): custom definitional reducers registered into the simp set that go beyond rewriting rules.

Name: FinsetInterval.lean
Description: Simproc for `Finset.Icc`/`Ioc`/`Ico`/`Ioo` of numeric literals: computing the explicit finset. Distinct from Simproc/Divisors and Order/SuccPred/IntervalSucc.

Name: VecPerm.lean
Description: Simproc for vector permutations: simplifying `List.perm` and `Finset.perm` goals on explicit lists. Distinct from Simproc/Factors and Combinatorics/Perm.

Name: Factors.lean
Description: Simproc for prime factorisation: reducing `n = p₁^a₁ * ⋯ * pₖ^aₖ` statements. Distinct from Simproc/Divisors and NormNum/Prime.

Name: Divisors.lean
Description: Simproc for divisibility: `decide`-style simplification of `m ∣ n` for concrete numerals via the `@[simproc]` framework. Distinct from NormNum/DivMod and Simproc/Factors.

Name: ExistsAndEq.lean
Description: Simproc for `∃ x, x = a ∧ P x`: simplifying existential statements with equality hypotheses. Distinct from Simproc/Divisors.

Name: Sat
Description: SAT and propositional decision procedures: decide and native_decide extensions for decidable propositions.

Name: FromLRAT.lean
Description: Importing LRAT (Linear Resolution Asymmetric Tautology) certificates: converting external SAT solver certificates into Lean proofs of propositional tautologies. Distinct from Tactic/Linarith/Verification.

Name: Linter
Description: Linting tactics and lint checks: #check_failure, unused variable warnings, and style enforcement for Mathlib contributions.

Name: TextBased
Description: Text-based linters: linting checks that operate on the raw source text rather than the elaborated term, catching style issues like trailing whitespace, long lines, incorrect unicode usage, and missing docstrings. Complement to the type-checked linters in the parent Linter folder.

Name: UnicodeLinter.lean
Description: Unicode linter: checks for appropriate Unicode use.

Name: PrivateModule.lean
Description: Linter for private module names: checks that `private` is not misused and that private declarations are not re-exported. Distinct from Linter/UpstreamableDecl and Linter/MinImports.

Name: Style.lean
Description: Style linter: checks spacing, line length, indentation, and other formatting rules in Mathlib source files. Distinct from Linter/Whitespace and Linter/Header.

Name: TacticDocumentation.lean
Description: Linter for tactic documentation: verifies that tactics registered in the tactic library have documentation entries. Distinct from Linter/DocString and Linter/Lint.

Name: PPRoundtrip.lean
Description: Pretty-printer roundtrip linter: checks that `pp.roundtrip` holds — the pretty-printed output of expressions re-parses to the original. Distinct from Linter/Style and Linter/DocString.

Name: MinImports.lean
Description: Linter for minimal imports: checks whether any imported module is unused (its declarations are not referenced). Distinct from Linter/DirectoryDependency and Linter/UpstreamableDecl.

Name: Multigoal.lean
Description: Linter for multi-goal tactics: warns when a tactic is used in a context with multiple goals without explicitly focusing. Distinct from Linter/Style and Linter/UnusedTactic.

Name: OldObtain.lean
Description: Linter for old-style `obtain`: flags usage of `obtain` without a pattern match (should use `intro` or `rcases`). Distinct from Linter/HaveLetLinter and Linter/Style.

Name: UpstreamableDecl.lean
Description: Linter for declarations that could be upstreamed to Batteries or Lean: identifies lemmas in Mathlib that belong in a more foundational library. Distinct from Linter/MinImports and Linter/DirectoryDependency.

Name: ValidatePRTitle.lean
Description: Linter validating PR title format: checks that PR titles follow the `feat`/`fix`/`refactor`/`chore` conventions for Mathlib contributions. Distinct from Linter/Header.

Name: Whitespace.lean
Description: Whitespace linter: checks for trailing whitespace and mixed tabs/spaces in source files. Distinct from Linter/EmptyLine and Linter/Style.

Name: UnusedTacticExtension.lean
Description: Extension mechanism for the unused-tactic linter: allows registering additional tactic forms that the unused-tactic linter should recognise. Distinct from Linter/UnusedTactic.

Name: TextBased.lean
Description: Text-based linters: checks that run on the raw source text (rather than the parsed syntax tree), such as Unicode usage and ASCII-only identifiers. Distinct from Linter/Style and Linter/Whitespace.

Name: UnusedInstancesInType.lean
Description: Linter for unused typeclass instances in type signatures: warns when an `[inst : C]` argument is not used in the definition body. Distinct from Linter/UnusedTactic and Linter/MinImports.

Name: UnusedTactic.lean
Description: Linter for unused tactics: identifies `try`, `skip`, or other no-op tactic invocations that do not change the goal. Distinct from Linter/UnusedTacticExtension and Linter/Multigoal.

Name: Lint.lean
Description: Main linter dispatch: the `#lint` command and `Mathlib.Linter.all` that runs all enabled linters on a file. Distinct from Linter/FlexibleLinter and individual linter files.

Name: DirectoryDependency.lean
Description: Linter checking directory dependency rules: ensures files only import from allowed directories per Mathlib's layered structure. Distinct from Linter/MinImports and Linter/UpstreamableDecl.

Name: DocPrime.lean
Description: Linter checking `'`-suffixed names: verifies that declarations named `foo'` have a docstring explaining why they differ from `foo`. Distinct from Linter/DocString and Linter/Header.

Name: DocString.lean
Description: Linter checking documentation strings: verifies that `theorem`, `def`, `structure`, and `class` declarations have docstrings in the required format. Distinct from Linter/DocPrime and Linter/Header.

Name: DeprecatedSyntaxLinter.lean
Description: Linter for deprecated Lean 4 syntax: catches use of old syntax patterns that should be migrated. Distinct from Linter/DeprecatedModule and Linter/Style.

Name: CommandRanges.lean
Description: Linter checking command source ranges: verifies that commands do not span unexpected source positions (formatting lint). Distinct from Linter/Whitespace and Linter/Header.

Name: CommandStart.lean
Description: Linter checking command start positions: verifies that commands begin at expected column/line positions per Mathlib style. Distinct from Linter/CommandRanges and Linter/Style.

Name: DeprecatedModule.lean
Description: Linter for deprecated module imports: warns when a file imports a module marked `@[deprecated]`. Distinct from Linter/FindDeprecations and Linter/DeprecatedSyntaxLinter.

Name: HashCommandLinter.lean
Description: Linter for `#check`, `#eval`, `#print` commands: warns when debug commands are left in Mathlib files. Distinct from Linter/Lint and Linter/TacticDocumentation.

Name: HaveLetLinter.lean
