Description: Linter for `have`/`let` style: checks that `have` and `let` in tactic mode use the recommended syntax. Distinct from Linter/Style and Linter/OldObtain.

Name: Header.lean
Description: Linter checking file headers: verifies that each Mathlib file begins with the standard copyright header and module docstring. Distinct from Linter/DocString and Linter/Style.

Name: GlobalAttributeIn.lean
Description: Linter for `attribute [X] in` scoping: checks that global attribute additions (`@[simp]` etc.) follow Mathlib conventions for scoped vs. global registration. Distinct from Tactic/Attr/Core.

Name: EmptyLine.lean
Description: Linter for empty lines: checks for missing or extra blank lines between declarations per Mathlib style. Distinct from Linter/Whitespace and Linter/CommandStart.

Name: FindDeprecations.lean
Description: Linter finding deprecation usage: scans the file for use of deprecated identifiers and reports them. Distinct from Linter/DeprecatedModule and Linter/UpstreamableDecl.

Name: FlexibleLinter.lean
Description: Infrastructure for flexible/configurable linters: the base class and dispatch mechanism for linters that can be enabled/disabled per file or declaration. Distinct from Linter/Lint.

Name: CategoryTheory
Description: Tactics specialized for category theory: slice_lhs, coherence, and associativity/unitality automation for functors and natural transformations.

Name: Monoidal
Description: The monoidal tactic: proves equalities of morphisms in a monoidal category by normalizing both sides with respect to associativity, unitality, and functoriality of the tensor product. Handles the pentagon and triangle coherence conditions automatically. Distinct from Coherence (which handles all coherence morphisms) — Monoidal focuses on the specific equational theory of monoidal categories.

Name: Normalize.lean
Description: Normalisation for the monoidal tactic.

Name: PureCoherence.lean
Description: Pure coherence for the monoidal tactic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monoidal coherence tactic basics.

Name: Datatypes.lean
Description: Data types for the monoidal tactic.

Name: Coherence
Description: The coherence tactic: proves that any two parallel compositions of coherence morphisms in a monoidal or bicategory are equal, formalizing Mac Lane's coherence theorem. Allows users to skip explicit coherence proofs by relying on the fact that all coherence diagrams commute. Distinct from the Monoidal and Bicategory tactics (which handle specific equality goals, not just coherence).

Name: Normalize.lean
Description: Normalisation for the monoidal coherence tactic.

Name: PureCoherence.lean
Description: Pure coherence for monoidal categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Coherence tactic core.

Name: Datatypes.lean
Description: Data types for the coherence tactic.

Name: Bicategory
Description: The bicategory tactic: a tactic that proves equalities of 2-cells in a bicategory by normalization, handling all the coherence isomorphisms (associators and unitors) automatically. Extends the monoidal tactic to the bicategorical setting. Used in proofs about bicategories where explicit coherence manipulation would be prohibitively tedious.

Name: Normalize.lean
Description: Normalisation for the bicategory coherence tactic.

Name: PureCoherence.lean
Description: Pure coherence for bicategories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bicategory coherence tactic basics.

Name: Datatypes.lean
Description: Data types for the bicategory coherence tactic.

Name: MonoidalComp.lean
Description: The `monoidal_comp` tactic: normalises tensor product compositions in a monoidal category, resolving pentagon and triangle identities. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/Coherence and Tactic/CategoryTheory/BicategoricalComp.

Name: IsoReassoc.lean
Description: The `iso_reassoc` tactic: reassociates isomorphism compositions, resolving `f ≫ (g ≫ h) = (f ≫ g) ≫ h` goals. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/CancelIso and Tactic/CategoryTheory/Reassoc.

Name: Elementwise.lean
Description: The `elementwise` attribute/tactic: given a natural transformation `η : F ⟶ G`, produces the pointwise statement `∀ x, η.app X x = ...`. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/ToApp.

Name: ToApp.lean
Description: The `to_app` tactic/lemma: converts a natural transformation equality to its pointwise component form `∀ X, η.app X = ε.app X`. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/Elementwise.

Name: Slice.lean
Description: The `slice_lhs`/`slice_rhs` tactics: extract and rewrite a slice (sub-composition) of a morphism composition. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/Reassoc and Tactic/CategoryTheory/CheckCompositions.

Name: Reassoc.lean
Description: The `reassoc` attribute and tactic: given `f ≫ g = h`, adds `f ≫ g ≫ k = h ≫ k` as a simp lemma. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/IsoReassoc and Tactic/CategoryTheory/Slice.

Name: CancelIso.lean
Description: The `cancel_iso` tactic (or `slice_lhs`/`slice_rhs`): cancels isomorphisms in category theory goals. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/IsoReassoc and Tactic/CategoryTheory/Reassoc.

Name: BicategoryCoherence.lean
Description: The `bicategory_coherence` tactic: proves coherence goals in bicategories by reducing them to the free bicategory. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/Coherence (monoidal) and Tactic/CategoryTheory/BicategoricalComp.

Name: BicategoricalComp.lean
Description: The `bicategory_comp` tactic: normalises composition expressions in a bicategory, resolving associators and unitors. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/BicategoryCoherence and Tactic/CategoryTheory/MonoidalComp.

Name: Coherence.lean
Description: The `coherence` tactic for monoidal categories: proves goals that follow from the coherence theorem by rewriting via associators and unitors. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/BicategoryCoherence.

Name: CheckCompositions.lean
Description: The `check_compositions` tactic: verifies that a sequence of morphisms in a category is composable, used for diagram chasing. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/Reassoc and Tactic/CategoryTheory/Slice.

Name: CategoryStar.lean
Description: Star-shaped category tactic support: infrastructure for `*`-categories (star autonomous categories) and their diagrammatic reasoning. Distinct from Tactic/CategoryTheory/MonoidalComp.

Name: CancelDenoms
Description: The cancel_denoms tactic: clears denominators in hypotheses and goals involving division in a field.

Name: Core.lean
Description: The `cancel_denoms` tactic: clears denominators in hypotheses and goals involving division by nonzero elements in a field. Used as a preprocessing step for `linarith` and `ring`. Distinct from Tactic/FieldSimp/Lemmas.

Name: Continuity
Description: The continuity and fun_prop tactics: automatically proves continuity of composite functions by chaining continuity lemmas.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation for the `continuity` tactic: the tactic entry point that discharges continuity goals by combining `Continuous` lemmas. Distinct from Tactic/FunProp/Core (the general function-property framework).

Name: ComputeAsymptotics
Description: Tactics and utilities for computing asymptotic orders of growth, used in analysis and number theory.

Name: Multiseries
Description: Multiseries for asymptotic computation: formal objects representing functions as generalized power series in multiple asymptotic scales, used by the compute_asymptotics tactic to determine the asymptotic order of composite expressions. Handles compositions of exponentials, logarithms, and powers via Hardy field arithmetic.

Name: Monomial
Description: Monomial multiseries: the simplest multiseries, representing a single asymptotic term x^a · (log x)^b · exp(c·x^d) · (constant) as a formal object. The building block from which composite asymptotic expansions are built via addition and multiplication. Used internally by the compute_asymptotics tactic to normalize asymptotic expressions.

Name: Predicates.lean
Description: Monomial predicates.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monomials in multiseries.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Multiseries definitions.

Name: Majorized.lean
Description: Majorization in multiseries.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis for multiseries asymptotics.

Name: Corecursion.lean
Description: Corecursion for multiseries.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for the `compute_asymptotics` tactic: rewrite rules for simplifying `IsBigO`/`IsLittleO` goals by applying asymptotic arithmetic. Distinct from Analysis/Asymptotics/Lemmas and Tactic/Positivity/Basic.

Name: ArithMult
Description: Arithmetic multiplicativity tactics: automation for goals about multiplicative arithmetic functions.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation for the `arith_mult` tactic: attribute and dispatch setup for arithmetic multiplicativity goals (e.g., `f (m * n) = f m * f n`). Distinct from Tactic/NormNum/Basic and Tactic/Ring/Basic.

Name: Algebra
Description: Algebraic automation tactics: tools that discharge algebraic goals using typeclass assumptions about rings, fields, and modules.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas supporting the `algebra` tactic: auxiliary algebraic identities used in the tactic's normalisation procedure. Distinct from Tactic/Algebra/Basic and Tactic/Ring/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The `ring` and `field_simp`-adjacent `algebra` normalisation tactic: simplification of algebraic expressions in a `CommRing` or `Field`. Distinct from Tactic/Ring/Basic (the `ring` tactic) and Tactic/Algebra/Lemmas.

Name: Bound
Description: The bound tactic: proves numerical bounds by propagating inequalities through monotone operations.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation and dispatch for the `bound` tactic: the tactic entry point and proof search strategy for norm and inequality goals. Distinct from Tactic/Bound/Attribute and Tactic/Positivity/Core.

Name: Attribute.lean
Description: The `@[bound]` attribute: registers lemmas for use by the `bound` tactic (automated norm/inequality solving). Distinct from Tactic/Bound/Init and Tactic/GCongr/CoreAttrs.

Name: Attr
Description: Tactic attribute infrastructure: @[simp], @[ext], @[norm_cast], and other attribute definitions that customize tactic behavior.

Name: Register.lean
Description: Registration utilities for tactic attributes: `registerSimpAttr`, `registerNormNumAttr`, and similar registration functions for adding lemmas to tactic databases. Distinct from Tactic/Attr/Core.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core infrastructure for Mathlib's attribute system: `@[simp]`, `@[ext]`, `@[norm_cast]` registration helpers and the `AttributeExtension` typeclass. Distinct from Tactic/Attr/Register and Tactic/Simps/Basic.

Name: GRewrite
Description: The grw (generalized rewrite) tactic: rewrites using relations other than equality, such as iff or setoid equivalence.

Name: Elab.lean
Description: Elaboration layer for `grewrite`: parsing the tactic syntax and calling the core. Distinct from GRewrite/Core.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core of the `grewrite` tactic: generalised rewriting that can rewrite under binders and in positions not accessible by `rw`. Distinct from GRewrite/Elab and Tactic/Ring/RingNF.

Name: GCongr
Description: The gcongr (generalized congruence) tactic: proves order inequalities by congruence, applying monotonicity under compatible operations.

Name: ForwardAttr.lean
Description: The `@[gcongr_forward]` attribute: marks lemmas that `gcongr` uses in the forward direction (weakening hypotheses). Distinct from GCongr/CoreAttrs and GCongr/Core.

Name: CoreAttrs.lean
Description: The `@[gcongr]` attribute: marks lemmas for use by the `gcongr` tactic. Distinct from GCongr/Core and GCongr/ForwardAttr.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core engine of the `gcongr` tactic: monotone congruence lemma application. Given a goal `f a ≤ f b`, decomposes it using `@[gcongr]`-tagged lemmas. Distinct from GCongr/CoreAttrs and Tactic/Monotonicity/Basic.

Name: LinearCombination
Description: The linear_combination tactic: proves an equality by supplying an explicit linear combination of hypotheses, with ring closing the residual.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for the `linear_combination` tactic: the key ring identity `a - b = 0` iff `a = b`, used to verify user-supplied linear combination certificates. Distinct from Tactic/Ring/Basic and Tactic/Linarith/Verification.

Name: Linarith
Description: The linarith tactic: a complete decision procedure for linear arithmetic inequalities over ordered fields, implemented via Fourier-Motzkin elimination.

Name: Oracle
Description: The arithmetic oracle backend for linarith: an external call (using Lasserre-type SDP or Positivstellensatz certificates) that finds a nonnegativity certificate for a polynomial inequality, extending linarith to nonlinear cases. Distinct from the main Linarith folder (the Fourier-Motzkin core) — the oracle handles cases beyond the linear fragment.

Name: SimplexAlgorithm
Description: The simplex algorithm as a Positivstellensatz oracle for linarith: given a system of linear inequalities, the simplex method either finds a feasible point (disproving unsatisfiability) or returns a Farkas certificate (a nonneg linear combination proving infeasibility). This certificate is then checked by linarith to close the goal.

Name: PositiveVector.lean
Description: Positive vector certificates.

Name: SimplexAlgorithm.lean
Description: Main simplex algorithm implementation.

Name: Datatypes.lean
Description: Simplex algorithm data types.

Name: Gauss.lean
Description: Gaussian elimination in the simplex algorithm.

Name: SimplexAlgorithm.lean
Description: Simplex algorithm oracle for linarith.

Name: FourierMotzkin.lean
Description: Fourier–Motzkin elimination for linarith.

Name: Parsing.lean
Description: Parsing hypotheses for `linarith`: extracting linear combinations from Lean expressions. Distinct from Linarith/Datatypes and Linarith/Frontend.

Name: Preprocessing.lean
Description: Preprocessing for `linarith`: normalising hypotheses (multiplying out, moving terms across inequalities, handling `min`/`max`). Distinct from Linarith/Parsing and Linarith/Verification.

Name: Verification.lean
Description: Verification (proof term generation) for `linarith`: turning a Farkas certificate into a valid Lean proof term. Distinct from Linarith/Datatypes and Linarith/Frontend.

Name: NNRealPreprocessor.lean
Description: Preprocessing for `linarith` in `ℝ≥0` and `ℝ≥0∞`: coercion of nonneg-real hypotheses to `ℝ` for the linear arithmetic solver. Distinct from Linarith/Preprocessing and Linarith/Frontend.

Name: Datatypes.lean
Description: Data structures for `linarith`: the internal representation of linear arithmetic hypotheses, the Farkas lemma certificate, and the proof term. Distinct from Linarith/Frontend and Linarith/Verification.

Name: Frontend.lean
Description: Entry point for the `linarith` tactic: parsing the goal, collecting hypotheses, and dispatching to the Farkas-lemma solver. Distinct from Linarith/Datatypes and Linarith/Preprocessing.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas used internally by `linarith`: arithmetic facts about `≤`, `<`, and `0` used to build the certificate. Distinct from Linarith/Preprocessing and Linarith/Verification.

Name: FieldSimp
Description: The field_simp tactic: simplifies expressions in a field by clearing denominators and applying field axioms.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas tagged `@[field_simp]`: division, inversion, and fraction simplification rules used by `field_simp`. Distinct from Tactic/FieldSimp/Attr and Tactic/Ring/Basic.

Name: Discharger.lean
Description: Discharger for `field_simp`: the subroutine that proves side conditions (nonzero denominators) generated during `field_simp` simplification. Distinct from Tactic/FieldSimp/Attr and Tactic/CancelDenoms/Core.

Name: Attr.lean
Description: The `@[field_simp]` attribute: marks lemmas for use by the `field_simp` tactic (simplification in fields). Distinct from Tactic/FieldSimp/Lemmas and Tactic/FieldSimp/Discharger.

Name: Explode
Description: The #explode command: displays a proof term as a Fitch-style natural deduction derivation for human readability.

Name: Pretty.lean
Description: Pretty printing for the `#explode` command: the rendering of the proof tree as a table showing each proof step, its rule, and its hypotheses. Distinct from Tactic/Explode/Datatypes.

Name: Datatypes.lean
Description: Data structures for the `#explode` command: the proof tree representation used to display a human-readable decomposition of a Lean proof. Distinct from Tactic/Explode/Pretty.

Name: FunProp
Description: The fun_prop tactic: a general-purpose prover for functional properties (continuity, measurability, differentiability) registered via attributes.

Name: Theorems.lean
Description: Theorem database for `fun_prop`: the collection of registered function-property lemmas (composition, identity, constants) consulted during proof search. Distinct from FunProp/Decl and FunProp/Attr.

Name: Mor.lean
Description: Morphism handling in `fun_prop`: recognising and processing `ContinuousLinearMap`, `LinearMap`, and other bundled morphism types in function-property goals. Distinct from FunProp/FunctionData.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Type utilities for `fun_prop`: helpers for recognising function types, function-property typeclasses, and their arguments. Distinct from FunProp/Elab and FunProp/FunctionData.

Name: ToBatteries.lean
Description: Bridging `fun_prop` to Batteries: interface to lemmas in the Batteries library (upstream of Mathlib) used in `fun_prop` proof search. Distinct from FunProp/Theorems.

Name: FunctionData.lean
Description: Function data extraction for `fun_prop`: decomposing a function expression into its head, arguments, and property. Distinct from FunProp/Elab and FunProp/Types.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core engine of the `fun_prop` tactic: the proof search strategy combining tagged lemmas to prove goals of the form `Continuous f`, `Measurable f`, `Differentiable f`, etc. Distinct from FunProp/Attr and Tactic/Continuity/Init.

Name: Attr.lean
Description: The `@[fun_prop]` attribute: marks theorems as function-property lemmas (continuity, measurability, differentiability) for use by the `fun_prop` tactic. Distinct from FunProp/Core.

Name: Elab.lean
Description: Elaboration for `fun_prop`: the Lean 4 metaprogramming layer converting `fun_prop` goals into the internal representation. Distinct from FunProp/Core and FunProp/Decl.

Name: Decl.lean
Description: Declaration infrastructure for `fun_prop`: storing function-property declarations and their associated lemma databases. Distinct from FunProp/Core and FunProp/Theorems.

Name: Finiteness
Description: The finiteness tactic: automatically proves that a type or set is finite using registered instances and closure properties.

Name: Attr.lean
Description: The `@[finiteness]` attribute: marks lemmas for the `finiteness` tactic, which discharges goals of the form `Finite s` or `Fintype α`. Distinct from Tactic/FunProp/Attr and Tactic/Positivity/Core.

Name: Positivity.lean
Description: Entry point for the positivity tactic: imports all positivity extensions and registers the main tactic.

Name: Polyrith.lean
Description: The polyrith tactic: calls an external oracle to find a polynomial arithmetic certificate for an equality goal.

Name: ProdAssoc.lean
Description: The prod_assoc tactic: rewrites product types to reassociate parentheses.

Name: PPWithUniv.lean
Description: The pp_with_univ option: a pretty-printing flag showing universe levels in type expressions.

Name: PNatToNat.lean
Description: The pnat_to_nat simp set: lemmas for pushing coercions from PNat to Nat through arithmetic.

Name: OfNat.lean
Description: The ofNat tactic: normalizing numeric literals and their typeclass instances.

Name: Observe.lean
Description: The observe tactic: a debugging tool that asserts an intermediate fact and adds it as a hypothesis.

Name: Peel.lean
Description: The peel tactic: strips matching quantifiers from a hypothesis and the goal simultaneously.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order tactic: an automated decision procedure for goals in linearly ordered types.

Name: Propose.lean
Description: The propose tactic: suggests possible intermediate lemmas that could help close a goal.

Name: Rename.lean
Description: The rename_i tactic: renames inaccessible hypotheses to user-chosen names.

Name: ReduceModChar.lean
Description: The reduce_mod_char tactic: simplifies ring expressions in characteristic p.

Name: Replace.lean
Description: The replace tactic: replaces a hypothesis with a new statement, requiring proof of the new from the old.

Name: RenameBVar.lean

Name: Recover.lean

Name: Push.lean
Description: The push_neg and push_cast tactics: moving negations and type coercions inward through compound expressions.

Name: ProxyType.lean
Description: The proxy_type tactic: replaces a type by an equivalent simpler one for tactic purposes.

Name: Recall.lean
Description: The recall tactic: reasserts a known fact from the environment with its name for documentation purposes.

Name: Qify.lean
Description: The qify tactic: casts a goal about N or Z into a goal about Q, enabling field arithmetic.

Name: LinearCombinationPrime.lean
Description: The linear_combination' variant: a more flexible linear combination tactic allowing non-ring residuals.

Name: LinearCombination.lean
Description: The linear_combination tactic: proves equalities by expressing the goal as a linear combination of hypotheses.

Name: Measurability.lean
Description: The measurability tactic: proves measurability goals by applying registered measurability instances.

Name: Linter.lean
Description: Custom linters for Mathlib: checks for style issues, deprecated names, and common proof anti-patterns.

Name: Linarith.lean
Description: The linarith tactic: a decision procedure for linear arithmetic over ordered fields via Farkas' lemma.

Name: ITauto.lean
Description: The itauto tactic: an intuitionistic propositional tautology checker.

Name: IrreducibleDef.lean
Description: The irreducible_def attribute: marks a definition as irreducible for the simp tactic.

Name: Lift.lean
Description: The lift tactic: lifts a hypothesis of a coerced value to a hypothesis about the original type.

Name: Lemma.lean

Name: MinImports.lean

Name: Nontriviality.lean

Name: NoncommRing.lean

Name: NthRewrite.lean

Name: NormNum.lean

Name: MoveAdd.lean

Name: ModCases.lean

Name: MkIffOfInductiveProp.lean

Name: Monotonicity.lean

Name: Module.lean

Name: RewriteSearch.lean

Name: ToExpr.lean

Name: ToDual.lean

Name: ToLevel.lean

Name: ToFun.lean
Description: The toFun simp lemmas: simp rules for unfolding coercions from bundled morphisms to plain functions.

Name: ToAdditive.lean
Description: The @[to_additive] attribute: automatically generates additive versions of multiplicative definitions and theorems.

Name: TautoSet.lean
Description: The tauto_set tactic: a decision procedure for set-theoretic identities.

Name: Tauto.lean
Description: The tauto tactic: a classical propositional tautology checker.

Name: TFAE.lean
Description: The tfae_have and tfae_finish tactics: prove that a list of statements are all equivalent.

Name: TermCongr.lean
Description: Term-mode congruence lemmas: utilities for applying congruence in term-mode proofs.

Name: Trace.lean
Description: The trace tactic: prints a message during elaboration for debugging tactic proofs.

Name: WithoutCDot.lean
Description: The without_cdot tactic: a utility for rewriting without using the cdot notation.

Name: Variable.lean
Description: The variable? command: suggests variable declarations for the current context.

Name: Zify.lean
Description: The zify tactic: casts a goal involving N into Z, enabling subtraction and ring tactics.

Name: WLOG.lean
Description: The wlog tactic: introduces a without-loss-of-generality assumption, generating a symmetry side goal.

Name: Use.lean
Description: The use tactic: provides witnesses for existential goals.

Name: TypeCheck.lean
Description: The type_check command: verifies that a term has a given type during elaboration.

Name: TryThis.lean
Description: The try_this infrastructure: displays suggestions in the infoview that the user can click to apply.

Name: UnsetOption.lean
Description: The unset_option command: restores a Lean option to its default value.

Name: TypeStar.lean
Description: The TypeStar notation: * as shorthand for the universe-polymorphic Sort or Type.

Name: SetLike.lean
Description: SetLike tactic utilities: simp lemmas and automation for working with bundled subobjects.

Name: Set.lean
Description: The set tactic: introduces a local definition for a subterm, replacing all occurrences.

Name: SimpRw.lean
Description: The simp_rw tactic: like simp but uses rewrite lemmas under binders.

Name: SimpIntro.lean
Description: The simp_intro tactic: simultaneously introduces hypotheses and simplifies.

Name: ScopedNS.lean
Description: Scoped notation and attribute management: tools for managing notation and instances in local scopes.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: The ring and ring_nf tactics: normalizing expressions in commutative (semi)rings.

Name: Rify.lean
Description: The rify tactic: casts a goal about N, Z, or Q into R, enabling real analysis tactics.

Name: Says.lean
Description: The says tactic: documents what a tactic call produces, for transparent proof communication.

Name: RSuffices.lean
Description: The rsuffices tactic: a reversed suffices introducing the sufficient condition before its proof.

Name: SplitIfs.lean
Description: The split_ifs tactic: case-splits all if-then-else expressions in the goal and hypotheses.

Name: SuppressCompilation.lean
Description: The suppress_compilation attribute: prevents a declaration from being compiled to native code.

Name: SudoSetOption.lean
Description: The sudo set_option command: sets internal Lean options not normally user-accessible.

Name: TacticAnalysis.lean
Description: Tactic analysis tools: profiling tactic execution time and identifying slow steps.

Name: SwapVar.lean
Description: The swap_var tactic: exchanges the names of two hypotheses in the local context.

Name: SuccessIfFailWithMsg.lean
Description: The success_if_fail_with_msg tactic: succeeds iff a tactic fails with a specific error message.

Name: StacksAttribute.lean
Description: The @[stacks] attribute: links a theorem to its corresponding entry in the Stacks project.

Name: Spread.lean
Description: The spread tactic: distributes anonymous constructor arguments across hypothesis fields.

Name: Substs.lean
Description: The substs tactic: applies subst to all variables appearing as equalities in the context.

Name: Subsingleton.lean
Description: The subsingleton tactic: closes goals of the form a = b when the type has at most one element.

Name: CongrExclamation.lean
Description: The congr! tactic: a more powerful congruence tactic handling function applications more aggressively.

Name: ComputeDegree.lean
Description: The compute_degree tactic: automatically computes and verifies the degree of a polynomial expression.

Name: Constructor.lean
Description: The constructor tactic: applies the unique constructor of a structure or inductive type.

Name: CongrM.lean
Description: The congrm tactic: proves congruence by matching goal subterms with a user-provided template.

Name: Common.lean
Description: Common tactic imports: the set of tactics imported by default across all Mathlib files.

Name: ClearExclamation.lean
Description: The clear! tactic: clears a hypothesis and all hypotheses that depend on it.

Name: ClearExcept.lean
Description: The clear_except tactic: clears all hypotheses except the named ones.

Name: Coe.lean
Description: Coercion tactics: norm_cast, push_cast, pull_cast for normalizing coercion expressions.

Name: Clear_.lean
Description: The clear_ tactic: a version of clear that does not fail on missing hypotheses.

Name: Continuity.lean
Description: The continuity tactic entry point: imports all continuity extensions.

Name: DefEqAbuse.lean
Description: The def_eq_abuse tactic: exploits definitional equality to change the goal's syntactic form.

Name: DeclarationNames.lean
Description: Declaration name utilities: checking naming conventions and generating names for auto-generated declarations.

Name: DeprecateTo.lean
Description: The @[deprecated_to] attribute: marks a declaration as deprecated with a forwarding alias.

Name: DefEqTransformations.lean
Description: Definitional equality transformations: rewriting the goal using definitional equalities without proof obligations.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core tactic infrastructure: fundamental tactic combinators and the Tactic monad utilities.

Name: Contrapose.lean
Description: The contrapose tactic: transforms P implies Q into not Q implies not P.

Name: ContinuousFunctionalCalculus.lean
Description: Tactics for continuous functional calculus in C*-algebras: automation for f(a) where f is continuous.

Name: Convert.lean
Description: The convert tactic: closes a goal by converting it to a new goal related by congruence.

Name: Conv.lean
Description: The conv tactic: a structured rewriting mode for targeting specific subterms.

Name: ApplyWith.lean
Description: The apply_with tactic: applies a lemma with specific configuration options for unification.

Name: ApplyFun.lean
Description: The apply_fun tactic: applies an injective function to both sides of an equality or inequality.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic tactic utilities used throughout Mathlib: assumption, contradiction, and simple automation.

Name: ArithMult.lean
Description: Arithmetic multiplicativity tactics: automation for multiplicative arithmetic functions in number theory.

Name: ApplyCongr.lean
Description: The apply_congr tactic: proves goals under binders using a congruence lemma.

Name: AdaptationNote.lean
Description: The adaptation_note command: documents where a proof was changed to adapt to a Lean or Mathlib update.

Name: Abel.lean
Description: The abel tactic: proves equalities in abelian groups by normalization.

Name: ApplyAt.lean
Description: The apply_at tactic: applies a lemma to transform a specific hypothesis rather than the goal.

Name: Algebraize.lean
Description: The algebraize tactic: converts category-theoretic or morphism properties into explicit algebraic statements.

Name: Bound.lean
Description: The bound tactic: proves numerical bounds by systematically applying monotonicity.

Name: Check.lean
Description: The #check tactic: elaborates a term and reports its type in the infoview.

Name: Change.lean
Description: The change tactic: replaces the goal with a definitionally equal expression.

Name: Clean.lean
Description: The clean tactic: simplifies the goal by removing trivial hypotheses and normalizing syntax.

Name: Choose.lean
Description: The choose tactic: destructs an existential hypothesis, introducing witnesses as new definitions.

Name: CasesM.lean
Description: The casesM tactic: performs cases on all hypotheses matching a given pattern.

Name: ByContra.lean
Description: The by_contra tactic: introduces a negation hypothesis for a proof by contradiction.

Name: ByCases.lean
Description: The by_cases tactic: splits into two cases based on whether a decidable proposition holds.

Name: Cases.lean
Description: Extensions to the cases tactic: case naming, recursive cases, and pattern-matching utilities.

Name: CancelDenoms.lean
Description: The cancel_denoms tactic: multiplies through to clear denominators in inequalities and equalities.

Name: DepRewrite.lean
Description: The dep_rewrite tactic: rewrites in dependent types where the standard rw tactic fails.

Name: Generalize.lean
Description: The generalize tactic: replaces a term in the goal by a fresh variable.

Name: GCongr.lean
Description: The gcongr tactic: generalized congruence for inequalities, applying monotonicity lemmas.

Name: GRewrite.lean
Description: The grw tactic: rewrites using a relation other than equality.

Name: GeneralizeProofs.lean
Description: The generalize_proofs tactic: abstracts proof terms in the goal into named hypotheses.

Name: FunProp.lean
Description: The fun_prop tactic entry point: dispatches to registered provers for functional properties.

Name: Find.lean
Description: The #find command: searches for lemmas matching a given pattern or type.

Name: FinCases.lean
Description: The fin_cases tactic: enumerates all cases for a variable ranging over a finite type.

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: The finiteness tactic: proves that a type or set is finite using registered finiteness instances.

Name: FindSyntax.lean
Description: The syntax for #find queries: the parser for pattern-based lemma search.

Name: Group.lean
Description: The group tactic: proves equalities in groups using the group axioms by normalization.

Name: InferParam.lean
Description: The infer_param tactic: fills in implicit parameters by unification during tactic proofs.

Name: Hint.lean
Description: The hint tactic: tries a list of tactics and reports which ones make progress.

Name: IntervalCases.lean
Description: The interval_cases tactic: case-splits on all integer values in a bounded interval.

Name: Inhabit.lean
Description: The inhabit tactic: derives an Inhabited instance from an existence proof.

Name: HigherOrder.lean
Description: Higher-order unification tactics: tools for goals requiring higher-order pattern matching.

Name: GuardHypNums.lean
Description: The guard_hyp_nums tactic: asserts a specific number of hypotheses are present.

Name: GuardGoalNums.lean
Description: The guard_goal_nums tactic: asserts a specific number of goals are present.

Name: HaveI.lean
Description: The haveI tactic: introduces a typeclass instance as a local hypothesis.

Name: Have.lean
Description: Extensions to the have tactic: named intermediate goals and structured proof steps.

Name: Eqns.lean
Description: The @[eqns] attribute: registers custom simp equations for a definition, overriding the default equation lemmas.

Name: ENatToNat.lean
Description: The enat_to_nat simp set: lemmas for pushing coercions from ENat to Nat through expressions.

Name: Eval.lean
Description: The eval and #eval tactic utilities: evaluating expressions and checking decidable propositions.

Name: ErwQuestion.lean
Description: The erw? tactic: searches for applicable exact-rewrite lemmas.

Name: DSimpPercent.lean
Description: Diagnostics for dsimp percentage: measures what fraction of a simp call is definitional.

Name: DeriveEncodable.lean
Description: The derive_encodable command: auto-derives Encodable instances for inductive types.

Name: DeriveCountable.lean
Description: The derive_countable command: auto-derives Countable instances for inductive types.

Name: DeriveTraversable.lean
Description: The derive_traversable command: auto-derives Traversable instances for inductive functors.

Name: DeriveFintype.lean
Description: The derive_fintype command: auto-derives Fintype instances for finite inductive types.

Name: ExistsI.lean
Description: The existsI tactic: provides a witness for an existential with an instance argument.

Name: FBinop.lean
Description: The fbinop tactic: handles goals involving heterogeneous binary operations.

Name: FastInstance.lean
Description: The fast_instance tactic: quickly synthesizes typeclass instances, bypassing slow search paths.

Name: FieldSimp.lean
Description: The field_simp tactic: simplifies field expressions by clearing denominators.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Field tactic utilities: support lemmas for the field_simp and related field tactics.

Name: FailIfNoProgress.lean
Description: The fail_if_no_progress tactic: fails if the wrapped tactic makes no progress.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: The ext tactic: proves equality by extensionality, applying registered ext lemmas.

Name: Explode.lean
Description: The #explode command: displays a proof term in Fitch-style natural deduction format.

Name: ExtractGoal.lean
Description: The extract_goal command: outputs the current goal as a standalone lemma for copy-pasting.

Name: ExtendDoc.lean
Description: The @[extend_doc] attribute: appends documentation to an existing declaration.

Name: Util
Description: Contains miscellaneous helper functions, generic theorems, and small extensions to the type system that don't belong to a specific mathematical area. Used to support everyday formalization tasks like dealing with decidable equality and simplifying expressions. All math fields indirectly rely on Util for basic infrastructure.

Name: AtomM
Description: The AtomM monad for atomization: a state monad that caches and names subexpressions as atoms, used internally by tactics like polyrith and linear_combination.

Name: Recurse.lean
Description: Recursive atom extraction monad: `AtomM.recurse` — utilities for the `AtomM` monad (which extracts atomic sub-expressions from a Lean expression) to handle recursive descent into sub-terms. Used by `ring_nf` and `linarith` normalisation.

Name: Qq.lean
Description: The Qq quotation library: typed metaprogramming in Lean 4 using quoted expressions with universe polymorphism.

Name: Simp.lean
Description: Simp utilities: helpers for controlling and inspecting the simp set and simp lemma registration.

Name: SleepHeartbeats.lean
Description: Sleep by heartbeats: a utility for controlling elaboration time in tests and benchmarks.

Name: PrintSorries.lean
Description: The #print_sorries command: reports all sorry-containing declarations in the current file.

Name: Notation3.lean
Description: Notation3: a Lean 4 macro for defining complex notation patterns with binders and precedences.

Name: ParseCommand.lean
Description: Parse command utilities: infrastructure for defining new top-level commands in Lean 4.

Name: PPOptions.lean
Description: Pretty-printing options: controlling how terms, types, and universe levels are displayed.

Name: TransImports.lean
Description: Transitive imports: computing the full import closure of a Lean file.

Name: WhatsNew.lean
Description: The #what's_new command: shows declarations added by importing a given module.

Name: WithWeakNamespace.lean
Description: The with_weak_namespace utility: opens a namespace without requiring the namespace to exist.

Name: TermReduce.lean
Description: Term reduction utilities: beta, eta, and delta reduction for Lean 4 expressions in tactics.

Name: Superscript.lean
Description: Superscript notation: defines Unicode superscript characters as notation for powers and indices.

Name: SynthesizeUsing.lean
Description: The synthesize_using utility: synthesizing typeclass instances using a custom tactic.

Name: Tactic.lean
Description: Tactic utilities in Util: miscellaneous helpers for writing and combining tactics.

Name: MemoFix.lean
Description: Memoized fixed points: efficient computation of fixed points by caching intermediate results.

Name: AtomM.lean
Description: The AtomM monad: caches subexpressions as atoms for efficient comparison in tactics.

Name: CompileInductive.lean
Description: Compilation of inductive types: utilities for computing the compiled form of an inductive in the kernel.

Name: CountHeartbeats.lean
Description: The count_heartbeats command: measures the elaboration cost of a declaration in heartbeats.

Name: AtLocation.lean
Description: The at location utility: running a tactic at a specific hypothesis location.

Name: AddRelatedDecl.lean
Description: The addRelatedDecl utility: programmatically adding declarations related to an existing one.

Name: AliasIn.lean
Description: The aliasIn utility: creating an alias for a declaration in a different namespace.

Name: AssertNoSorry.lean
Description: The assert_no_sorry command: fails if the current file contains any sorry.

Name: FormatTable.lean
Description: Table formatting utilities: creating aligned text tables for use in documentation and error messages.

Name: GetAllModules.lean
Description: The getAllModules utility: enumerating all modules in the current Lean environment.

Name: LongNames.lean
Description: Long name utilities: detecting and reporting overly long declaration names.

Name: Export.lean
Description: Export utilities: tools for exporting Lean declarations to external formats.

Name: Delaborators.lean
Description: Custom delaborators: pretty-printer extensions that display terms in more readable notation.

Name: DischargerAsTactic.lean
Description: The discharger_as_tactic utility: wrapping a discharger function as a tactic for use in simp.

Name: ElabWithoutMVars.lean
Description: The elab_without_mvars utility: elaborating a term while ensuring no metavariables remain.

Name: Topology
Description: Formalizes topological spaces, continuity, compactness, connectedness, metric spaces, and uniform structures. Used to study properties preserved by continuous deformation, limits of sequences, and the convergence of nets. Relied on by analysis, geometry, algebraic topology, probability, and dynamical systems.

Name: MetricSpace
Description: Metric spaces: the metric typeclass, Cauchy sequences, completeness, compactness criteria (Heine-Borel, totally bounded), and the completion functor.

Name: Ultra
Description: Ultrametric spaces: metric spaces satisfying the strong triangle inequality d(x,z) ≤ max(d(x,y), d(y,z)). Every triangle is isoceles. Open balls are closed; two balls are either disjoint or nested. Examples: p-adic numbers, function spaces over finite fields. Distinct from ProperSpace (compactness condition) and Pseudo (weakening of identity axiom).

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Product ultrametric spaces.

Name: TotallySeparated.lean
Description: Ultrametric spaces are totally separated.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ultrametric spaces.

Name: ContinuousMaps.lean
Description: Continuous maps on ultrametric spaces.

Name: Pseudo
Description: Pseudometric spaces: spaces with a distance function d(x, y) ≥ 0 satisfying the triangle inequality and d(x, x) = 0, but not necessarily d(x, y) = 0 ⟹ x = y. Seminormed spaces give pseudometrics. The quotient by d(x,y) = 0 gives a metric space. Used as the setting for Lp seminorms before quotienting by the null-function relation. Distinct from the genuine metric (which requires d(x,y) = 0 ⟹ x = y).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Pseudo-metric lemmas.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Product pseudo-metric spaces.

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝ pseudo-metric.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pseudo-metric spaces.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of pseudo-metric spaces.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Pseudo-metric space definitions.

Name: ProperSpace
Description: Proper metric spaces: metric spaces where every closed ball {x | d(x, a) ≤ r} is compact. Equivalently, every bounded sequence has a convergent subsequence (Bolzano-Weierstrass). Examples: ℝⁿ, compact spaces. The Hopf-Rinow theorem: a connected Riemannian manifold is proper iff it is complete and geodesically complete. Distinct from Pseudo and Ultra (different weakening/strengthening of the metric axioms).

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝ is a proper metric space.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Proper metric space lemmas.

Name: Kuratowski.lean
Description: Kuratowski embedding: every separable metric space embeds isometrically into `ℓ∞(ℕ)` (bounded sequences). Used to realise abstract metric spaces as subsets of a universal space. Distinct from MetricSpace/GromovHausdorff and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Isometry.lean
Description: Isometries in metric spaces: `Isometry f` (`dist (f x) (f y) = dist x y`), the Mazur–Ulam theorem (surjective isometries of normed spaces over ℝ are affine), and `IsometryEquiv`. Distinct from MetricSpace/IsometricSMul and MetricSpace/Bilipschitz.

Name: Lipschitz.lean
Description: Lipschitz maps: `LipschitzWith K f` — `dist (f x) (f y) ≤ K * dist x y`. Proves Lipschitz implies uniformly continuous, composition, restriction, and the space of Lipschitz functions. Distinct from MetricSpace/Holder (Hölder exponent < 1) and MetricSpace/Antilipschitz.

Name: PartitionOfUnity.lean
Description: Partitions of unity in metric spaces: existence via Lipschitz bump functions, and the Lipschitz partition of unity subordinate to a locally finite open cover. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Paracompact (abstract partition of unity) and MetricSpace/Lipschitz.

Name: MetricSeparated.lean
Description: Metric separation: `MetricSeparated s t` — `inf_{x ∈ s, y ∈ t} dist x y > 0`. Properties under finite unions and compact sets, and the Lebesgue number lemma. Distinct from MetricSpace/Infsep (separation within a set) and MetricSpace/Lipschitz.

Name: IsometricSMul.lean
Description: Isometric scalar multiplication: `IsometricSMul M α` — the scalar action `c • ·` is an isometry for each `c`. Instances for normed spaces. Distinct from MetricSpace/Isometry (isometries in general) and Topology/Algebra/MulAction.

Name: HausdorffDistance.lean
Description: Hausdorff distance between sets: `Metric.hausdorffDist s t = max (sup_{x ∈ s} dist x t, sup_{y ∈ t} dist y s)` and `infDist x s = inf_{y ∈ s} dist x y`. Proves the Hausdorff distance is a metric on the set of nonempty closed bounded sets. Distinct from MetricSpace/GromovHausdorff and MetricSpace/Closeds.

Name: HausdorffDimension.lean
Description: Hausdorff dimension: `dimH s` — the critical exponent of the Hausdorff measure, `dimH s = inf {d | H^d(s) = 0}`. Proves `dimH (countable set) = 0`, `dimH [0,1]^n = n`, and the dimension of Lipschitz images. Distinct from MetricSpace/CoveringNumbers and MeasureTheory/Measure/Hausdorff.

Name: Holder.lean
Description: Hölder continuous maps: `HolderWith C r f` — `dist (f x) (f y) ≤ C * dist x y ^ r` for `r ∈ (0,1]`. Proves Hölder maps are uniformly continuous, and composition/composition-with-Lipschitz lemmas. Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz (`r = 1`) and MetricSpace/HolderNorm.

Name: Infsep.lean
Description: Infimum separation: `Metric.infsep s = inf_{x ≠ y ∈ s} dist x y` — the minimum pairwise distance in a set. Proves `infsep > 0` for finite sets and characterisations of Delaunay-type separation. Distinct from MetricSpace/MetricSeparated and MetricSpace/Bounded.

Name: HolderNorm.lean
Description: The Hölder norm: the Banach space structure on `α →ₕ[C, r] β` (Hölder functions with a given constant and exponent) with norm `‖f‖ = ‖f 0‖ + C`. Distinct from MetricSpace/Holder (the Hölder condition without norm structure) and MetricSpace/Lipschitz.

Name: Perfect.lean
Description: Perfect metric spaces: `IsPerfect s` — closed sets with no isolated points. Proves every perfect nonempty complete metric space contains a homeomorphic copy of the Cantor set (hence is uncountable). Distinct from Topology/Instances/CantorSet and MetricSpace/Polish.

Name: ThickenedIndicator.lean
Description: Thickened indicator function of a set: `thickenedIndicator δ s x = max 0 (min 1 (1 - δ⁻¹ * infDist x s))` — a Lipschitz function that is 1 on `s` and 0 outside the `δ`-thickening. Distinct from MetricSpace/Thickening (the thickened set) and MetricSpace/HausdorffDistance.

Name: Snowflaking.lean
Description: Snowflaking of a metric space: given `(X, d)` and `r ∈ (0,1)`, the snowflake `(X, d^r)` is again a metric space (Hölder reparametrisation). Used in embedding theorems and fractal geometry. Distinct from MetricSpace/Holder and MetricSpace/Dilation.

Name: Thickening.lean
Description: Thickenings of sets: `Metric.thickening δ s = {x | infDist x s < δ}` (open δ-neighborhood). Proves thickenings are open, `closure s ⊆ thickening δ s`, and Hausdorff distance characterisation. Distinct from MetricSpace/ThickenedIndicator and MetricSpace/HausdorffDistance.

Name: UniformConvergence.lean
Description: Uniform convergence in metric spaces: the sup metric on bounded functions `BoundedContinuousFunction`, and uniform convergence of sequences of continuous functions. Distinct from UniformSpace/UniformConvergence (uniform space version) and ContinuousMap/Compact.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring metric space structure along equivalences: `Equiv.metricSpace e` given an equiv `e : α ≃ β` with `β` a metric space. Distinct from Homeomorph/TransferInstance (topological transfer) and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Similarity.lean
Description: Similarity maps: `IsSimilarity f` — maps satisfying `dist (f x) (f y) = r * dist x y` for a fixed `r > 0`. The iterated function systems arising from contracting similarities define fractal sets. Distinct from MetricSpace/Dilation (extended metric) and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Polish.lean
Description: Polish spaces: completely metrizable separable metric spaces. Proves that Borel subsets of Polish spaces are Polish (Alexandrov's theorem), and that the Cantor space and Baire space are Polish. Distinct from Topology/GDelta/MetrizableSpace and MetricSpace/Perfect.

Name: PiNat.lean
Description: Metric on `ℕ → α` (the Baire space when `α` is discrete): the ultrametric `dist x y = (min n | x n ≠ y n)⁻¹`, completeness, and homeomorphism between Baire space and irrational numbers. Distinct from MetricSpace/Polish and Topology/Instances/Irrational.

Name: ProperSpace.lean
Description: Proper metric spaces: `ProperSpace α` — closed bounded sets are compact (the Heine–Borel property). Proves `ℝⁿ` and finite-dimensional normed spaces are proper. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/LocallyCompact (locally compact) and Topology/Instances/RealVectorSpace.

Name: ShrinkingLemma.lean
Description: Shrinking lemma: given a locally finite open cover of a normal space, there exists a locally finite open cover where each element has closure contained in the original. Used in partition of unity arguments. Distinct from MetricSpace/PartitionOfUnity and Topology/Compactness/Paracompact.

Name: Sequences.lean
Description: Sequential properties in metric spaces: a metric space is first-countable (hence sequences determine the topology), `SeqContinuous` equals `Continuous`, and sequentially compact iff compact. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Sequences (abstract sequential closures) and MetricSpace/Cauchy.

Name: Cauchy.lean
Description: Cauchy sequences and completeness in metric spaces: `CauchySeq` (filter-based), the completion of a metric space, and the Banach fixed-point theorem in complete metric spaces. Distinct from MetricSpace/CauSeqFilter (sequence vs filter) and MetricSpace/Contracting.

Name: CantorScheme.lean
Description: Cantor scheme construction: a scheme (tree of closed sets) satisfying diameter → 0 and nonempty intersection properties defines a continuous embedding. Used to construct homeomorphisms to the Cantor set or Baire space. Distinct from Topology/Instances/CantorSet and MetricSpace/Polish.

Name: CauSeqFilter.lean
Description: Cauchy sequences as Cauchy filters: the equivalence between Cauchy sequences (in the `CauSeq` sense) and Cauchy filters in a metric space, and the completion characterisation. Distinct from MetricSpace/Cauchy (filter-based) and MetricSpace/Completion.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Metric space completion: `UniformSpace.Completion α` with the extended metric, proving it is a complete metric space containing `α` as a dense subspace. Distinct from MetricSpace/Cauchy (Cauchy sequences) and UniformSpace/Completion (uniform space version).

Name: Closeds.lean
Description: The space of closed subsets of a metric space: `Closeds X` with the Hausdorff metric, compactness of `Closeds X` when `X` is compact, and the Blaschke selection theorem. Distinct from MetricSpace/HausdorffDistance (the Hausdorff metric itself) and Sets/Closeds.

Name: BundledFun.lean
Description: Spaces of bundled functions with metric structure: `BundledFun` — metrics on function types arising from bounding properties (e.g., the space of Lipschitz functions or bounded functions). Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz and ContinuousMap/Compact.

Name: Antilipschitz.lean
Description: Antilipschitz maps: `AntilipschitzWith K f` — `‖f x - f y‖ ≥ K⁻¹ * ‖x - y‖`. Proves antilipschitz maps are injective with closed range, and composition with Lipschitz maps. Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Metric space instances for algebraic structures: `MetricSpace.instNormedAddCommGroup`, and the metric on `α × β` as the max of metrics. Proves `isometry_of_uniformEmbedding`. Distinct from MetricSpace/Basic (metric topology) and Analysis/NormedSpace.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Metric space topology: `MetricSpace.toTopologicalSpace`, open balls `Metric.ball x r`, the fact that metric spaces are Hausdorff and first-countable, and Cauchy sequences in metric spaces. The main API file bridging `Dist` and the topology. Distinct from MetricSpace/Defs (type classes) and EMetricSpace/Basic.

Name: Bounded.lean
Description: Bounded sets in metric spaces: `Metric.Bounded s` (contained in some ball), `Metric.diam s`, and the equivalence with `Bornology.IsBounded`. Proves `Metric.bounded_iff_diam_lt_top`. Distinct from EMetricSpace/Diam (extended diameter) and Bornology/Basic.

Name: Bilipschitz.lean
Description: Bilipschitz maps: maps that are both Lipschitz and antilipschitz (i.e., satisfy `K⁻¹ * d(x,y) ≤ d(fx, fy) ≤ K * d(x,y)`). Proves bilipschitz maps are homeomorphisms onto their image. Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz and MetricSpace/Antilipschitz.

Name: Congruence.lean
Description: Congruences on metric spaces: metric preserving equivalence relations and the quotient metric space. Distinct from MetricSpace/Gluing (gluing metric spaces) and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Gluing.lean
Description: Gluing metric spaces: given two metric spaces sharing a common subspace, construct their pushout metric space. Used in the GH-distance construction. Distinct from MetricSpace/GromovHausdorff and MetricSpace/Congruence.

Name: Equicontinuity.lean
Description: Equicontinuity in metric spaces: `EquicontinuousAt F x` — a family `F` of maps is equicontinuous at `x` when all maps share a common modulus of continuity. The Arzelà–Ascoli theorem. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Equicontinuity (algebraic version) and UniformSpace/Equicontinuity.

Name: GromovHausdorff.lean
Description: Gromov–Hausdorff distance between compact metric spaces: `GromovHausdorff.ghDist X Y` — the infimum of Hausdorff distances over all isometric embeddings into a common metric space. Proves `GHDist` is a metric on the set of isometry classes of compact metric spaces. Distinct from MetricSpace/GromovHausdorffRealized.

Name: HausdorffAlexandroff.lean
Description: Hausdorff–Alexandroff one-point compactification: `Alexandroff.instTopologicalSpace` — the one-point compactification of a locally compact Hausdorff space is compact Hausdorff. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/LocallyCompact and Topology/Compactification/StoneCech.

Name: GromovHausdorffRealized.lean
Description: Realising the Gromov–Hausdorff distance: the infimum defining `ghDist` is achieved (a realising coupling exists). Proves the space of compact metric spaces is itself a complete metric space under `ghDist`. Distinct from MetricSpace/GromovHausdorff (the distance definition) and MetricSpace/Completion.

Name: DilationEquiv.lean
Description: Dilation equivalences: `DilationEquiv α β` — bijective dilations (homothetic maps) and their inverses. The group structure under composition. Distinct from MetricSpace/Dilation (not necessarily invertible) and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Cover.lean
Description: Covers of metric spaces: ε-nets and ε-dense sets, and their use in compactness arguments (a metric space is compact iff totally bounded and complete). Distinct from MetricSpace/CoveringNumbers (cardinality) and Compactness/Compact.

Name: Contracting.lean
Description: Contracting maps: `ContractingWith K f` — `dist (f x) (f y) ≤ K * dist x y` with `K < 1`. The Banach fixed-point theorem: a contracting map on a complete metric space has a unique fixed point. Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz and MetricSpace/Cauchy.

Name: CoveringNumbers.lean
Description: Covering numbers `covNhd ε s`: the minimum number of ε-balls needed to cover `s`. Properties: monotonicity, the metric entropy, and connection to Hausdorff dimension. Distinct from MetricSpace/Cover (covers without counting) and MetricSpace/HausdorffDimension.

Name: Dilation.lean
Description: Dilations: `Dilation f` — maps satisfying `edist (f x) (f y) = r * edist x y` for a fixed ratio `r > 0`. Proves dilations are bijections onto their image. Distinct from MetricSpace/DilationEquiv (invertible) and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Type class definitions for metric spaces: `Dist α`, `PseudoMetricSpace`, `MetricSpace`, `SemiMetricSpace`, and the hierarchy. Defines `dist`, `nndist`, `edist` and their key axioms (symmetry, triangle inequality, separation). Distinct from MetricSpace/Basic (topology from dist).

Name: Maps
Description: Continuous, open, closed, and proper maps between topological spaces, and their interactions with subspace and quotient topologies.

Name: Strict
Description: Strictly continuous maps: maps that are continuous and have the additional property that they reflect the filter structure more faithfully than plain continuity. The strict topology on C_b(X, E) (bounded continuous functions), which is coarser than the sup-norm topology but finer than the pointwise topology. Distinct from Proper (properness is about compact preimages, not filter structure).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Strict maps: closed maps with closed fibers.

Name: Proper
Description: Proper maps between topological spaces: continuous maps f : X → Y such that preimages of compact sets are compact (equivalently, f is closed and has compact fibers). Proper maps are closed. The composition of proper maps is proper. A proper bijection is a homeomorphism when the codomain is Hausdorff. Distinct from Strict (a different strengthening of closed maps).

Name: UniversallyClosed.lean
Description: Universally closed maps are proper.

Name: CompactlyGenerated.lean
Description: Proper maps on compactly generated spaces.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Proper maps: preimages of compact sets are compact.

Name: OpenQuotient.lean
Description: Open quotient maps: `IsOpenQuotientMap f` — a quotient map that is also open. Proves that open quotient maps preserve second-countability and regularity, and the topology of the quotient is characterised. Distinct from Topology/Maps/Basic and Topology/Hom/Open.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Open, closed, and proper maps: `IsOpenMap f`, `IsClosedMap f`, `IsProperMap f` (continuous with compact fibres and preimages of compact sets compact). Proves that proper maps are closed, and characterisations in terms of filters. Distinct from Topology/Hom/Open and Topology/Homeomorph/Defs.

Name: OpenPartialHomeomorph
Description: Open partial homeomorphisms: partially defined bijections between open sets that are locally homeomorphisms, used in manifold theory.

Name: Continuity.lean
Description: Continuity properties of functions defined via open partial homeomorphisms: a function is continuous iff it is continuous in every chart, and the gluing lemma. Distinct from OpenPartialHomeomorph/Basic and OpenPartialHomeomorph/IsImage.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `OpenPartialHomeomorph X Y`: a homeomorphism `e : e.source → e.target` between open sets, with `e.source ⊆ X` and `e.target ⊆ Y`. The type definition and `PartialHomeomorph` coercion. Distinct from OpenPartialHomeomorph/Basic (main API).

Name: IsImage.lean
Description: Image sets under open partial homeomorphisms: `OpenPartialHomeomorph.IsImage e s t` — `e` maps `s` homeomorphically to `t`. Used for charts on submanifolds. Distinct from OpenPartialHomeomorph/Basic and OpenPartialHomeomorph/Continuity.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Open partial homeomorphisms: `OpenPartialHomeomorph X Y` — a homeomorphism between open subsets of `X` and `Y`, the basic API and composition. Used as the model for manifold charts. Distinct from OpenPartialHomeomorph/Defs (type definition) and FiberBundle/Trivialization.

Name: Composition.lean
Description: Composition of open partial homeomorphisms: `OpenPartialHomeomorph.trans e₁ e₂` — composing two compatible charts and proving the domain/range properties. Distinct from OpenPartialHomeomorph/Basic and OpenPartialHomeomorph/Constructions.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of open partial homeomorphisms: restricting to sub-domains, trivial partial homeomorphisms (identity on open sets), and homeomorphisms viewed as partial homeomorphisms on the entire space. Distinct from OpenPartialHomeomorph/Basic and OpenPartialHomeomorph/Composition.

Name: Metrizable
Description: Metrizable topological spaces: spaces whose topology is induced by a metric, with Urysohn's metrization theorem.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Metrizability results for ℝ and its relatives: ℝ is completely metrizable and separable (hence Polish), `ℝⁿ` is Polish, and the subspace topology on any subset of ℝ is metrizable. Distinct from Topology/Instances/RealVectorSpace and Metrizable/Basic.

Name: Uniformity.lean
Description: Metrizability from uniform structure: a uniform space is metrizable iff its uniformity has a countable basis (Birkhoff–Kakutani theorem). Distinct from Metrizable/Basic (topological metrizability) and UniformSpace/Basic.

Name: Urysohn.lean
Description: Urysohn metrisation theorem: every second-countable regular Hausdorff (T3) space is metrizable. Distinct from Metrizable/Basic (abstract metrizability) and Topology/Separation/Regular.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Metrizable topological spaces: `MetrizableSpace X` — the topology is induced by some metric. Proves products of metrizable spaces are metrizable, and metrizable spaces are regular. Distinct from Metrizable/Urysohn (Urysohn metrisation theorem) and Metrizable/CompletelyMetrizable.

Name: CompletelyMetrizable.lean
Description: Completely metrizable spaces: `CompletelyMetrizableSpace X` — the topology is induced by a complete metric. Proves Gδ subsets of completely metrizable spaces are completely metrizable. Distinct from Metrizable/Basic and Topology/MetricSpace/Polish.

Name: ContinuousMap.lean
Description: Metrizability of `C(X, Y)`: when `X` is compact and `Y` is metrizable, `C(X, Y)` with the compact-open topology is metrizable. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Compact (completeness) and Metrizable/Basic.

Name: Homotopy
Description: Homotopy of paths and maps: path homotopies with fixed endpoints, homotopy equivalence, and the fundamental groupoid.

Name: TopCat
Description: Homotopy in the category TopCat: the homotopy relation on continuous maps as a natural transformation of functors in the 2-category of topological spaces, homotopy equivalences, and the homotopy category. The functoriality of homotopy groups. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy (path homotopies with fixed endpoints for the fundamental groupoid) — this folder treats homotopy categorically.

Name: ToSSet.lean
Description: Singular functor `Top → sSet`.

Name: ZerothHomotopy.lean
Description: π₀ of a topological space.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Homotopy in TopCat.

Name: Path.lean
Description: Paths in TopCat.

Name: Lifting.lean
Description: Homotopy lifting property: for a fibration `p : E → B`, any homotopy `H : X × [0,1] → B` with a lift at time 0 lifts to a homotopy `H̃ : X × [0,1] → E`. Distinct from Topology/Covering/Basic (covering-specific lifting) and Topology/Homotopy/Basic.

Name: HSpaces.lean
Description: H-spaces: topological spaces with a continuous binary operation having a two-sided homotopy identity. Groups are H-spaces; loop spaces are H-spaces. Used in the theory of infinite loop spaces. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Basic and Topology/Algebra/Monoid.

Name: LocallyContractible.lean
Description: Locally contractible spaces: every point has a contractible neighbourhood. Proves that manifolds and CW-complexes are locally contractible, and that locally contractible spaces are locally path-connected. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Contractible (global) and Topology/Connected/LocPathConnected.

Name: Product.lean
Description: Products of homotopies: the homotopy group of a product `πₙ(X × Y) ≅ πₙ(X) × πₙ(Y)`, and the homotopy-type of product spaces. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/HomotopyGroup (group structure) and Topology/Homotopy/Basic.

Name: Path.lean
Description: Path homotopy: `Path.Homotopy p q` — a homotopy rel endpoints between paths `p q : Path x y`. The fundamental groupoid `FundamentalGroupoid X` with composition of path-homotopy classes. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Basic (general homotopy) and Topology/Connected/PathConnected.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Homotopy of continuous maps: `ContinuousMap.Homotopy f g` — a continuous deformation of `f` into `g` through `[0,1]`. Defines relative homotopy, the homotopy relation, and homotopy equivalence of maps. The foundational file for the Homotopy folder.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: Affine homotopies: the straight-line homotopy `H(x,t) = (1-t)f(x) + t g(x)` between maps into a convex subset of a topological vector space. Proves that any two maps into a convex set are homotopic. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Basic (general homotopy) and Analysis/Convex/Contractible.

Name: Contractible.lean
Description: Contractible spaces: `ContractibleSpace X` — the identity map is homotopic to a constant map. Proves convex subsets of topological vector spaces are contractible, and contractible implies path-connected and simply connected. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Basic and Convex/Contractible.

Name: HomotopyGroup.lean
Description: Homotopy groups `πₙ(X, x₀)`: the group of homotopy classes of based loops/spheres. Defines `HomotopyGroup n X x₀`, proves it is a group for `n ≥ 1` and abelian for `n ≥ 2`, and the long exact sequence of a fibration. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Path (path homotopy) and Topology/Covering/Basic.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Homotopy equivalences: `ContinuousMap.HomotopyEquiv f g` — maps `f : X → Y` and `g : Y → X` with `g ∘ f ≃ id` and `f ∘ g ≃ id`. Proves this is an equivalence relation and that homotopy equivalences induce isomorphisms on all homotopy groups. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Basic and Homeomorph/Defs.

Name: Homeomorph
Description: Homeomorphisms between topological spaces: bicontinuous bijections, the homeomorphism typeclass, and canonical homeomorphisms.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring topological structure along homeomorphisms: `Homeomorph.transferInstance` — given `h : X ≃ₜ Y`, a topological algebraic structure on `Y` transfers to a compatible structure on `X`. Used to define standard topologies on type aliases. Distinct from Homeomorph/Lemmas.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas about homeomorphisms: composition, products `Homeomorph.prodAssoc`, `Homeomorph.prodComm`, sums, and the homeomorphism between subspace and image. The main lemma library for constructing homeomorphisms. Distinct from Homeomorph/Defs (definitions) and Homeomorph/TransferInstance.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Homeomorph X Y` (`X ≃ₜ Y`): a bicontinuous bijection — an `Equiv` with both the forward and inverse maps continuous. Contains `Homeomorph.mk`, `Homeomorph.symm`, and the `FunLike` instance. The definitional core of the homeomorphism theory.

Name: LocallyConstant
Description: Locally constant functions: functions constant on each connected component, and their sheaf-theoretic properties.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Locally constant functions: `LocallyConstant X Y` — functions that are constant on a neighbourhood of each point, equivalently continuous maps into the discrete space `Y`. Proves the fibers are clopen and `LocallyConstant.comap`. Distinct from Topology/LocallyConstant/Algebra and ContinuousMap/LocallyConstant.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebraic structures on locally constant functions: `LocallyConstant X A` is a ring/module/algebra when `A` is, with pointwise operations. Distinct from Topology/LocallyConstant/Basic (definition and topological properties) and ContinuousMap/Algebra.

Name: Instances
Description: Topology on specific mathematical structures: R, C, Z, N, Fin n, and products thereof, with their standard metric and order topologies.

Name: NNReal
Description: The topology on ℝ≥0 = {x : ℝ | 0 ≤ x}: the subspace topology from ℝ, making ℝ≥0 a locally compact metrizable space. Continuity of +, ×, and the coercion to ℝ. The completion of ℚ≥0. Used extensively as a target for norms and measures (‖x‖ ∈ ℝ≥0). Distinct from ENNReal (which adds +∞).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: NNReal topology lemmas.

Name: Real
Description: The topology on ℝ: the metric topology from |x - y|, equivalent to the order topology. Connectedness, local compactness, completeness, and the intermediate value theorem. Canonical instances for ℝ as a topological ring, metric space, and normed field. The primary concrete topological space in Mathlib — most analysis builds on these instances.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: ℝ topology lemmas.

Name: EReal
Description: The topology on ℝ ∪ {-∞, +∞}: the order topology on the extended reals, a compact ordered space. Continuity of arithmetic with the conventions ∞ + (-∞) = indeterminate, ∞ · 0 = 0. Used in optimization (inf over empty set = +∞) and in conjugate functions in convex analysis. Distinct from ENNReal (only nonneg) and NNReal (finite nonneg).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: EReal topology lemmas.

Name: AddCircle
Description: The additive circle ℝ/aℤ (or ℝ/ℤ as the unit circle): the quotient of ℝ by the discrete subgroup aℤ, with the quotient topology making it a compact abelian topological group. The winding number, the covering map ℝ → ℝ/ℤ, and Fourier series on the circle (the characters are e^{2πinx}). Distinct from Instances/Real (ℝ itself) and from the geometric S¹ (though homeomorphic to it).

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝ/ℤ as a topological group.

Name: DenseSubgroup.lean
Description: Dense subgroups of the circle.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Additive circle `ℝ/ℤ` definitions.

Name: ENNReal
Description: The topology on ℝ≥0 ∪ {+∞}: the order topology on the extended nonnegative reals, making them a compact space. Continuity of arithmetic operations with the conventions ∞ + x = ∞ and 0 · ∞ = 0. The primary codomain for measures. Distinct from Instances/NNReal (finite nonneg reals) and Instances/EReal (which also includes -∞).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: ENNReal topology lemmas.

Name: RealVectorSpace.lean
Description: Topology on finite-dimensional real vector spaces: all norms are equivalent, every finite-dimensional normed space is locally compact and complete, and the topology is unique. Proves `FiniteDimensional.proper_isROrC`. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Complex and Analysis/InnerProductSpace/PiL2.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Shrinking a topological space to a smaller universe: `Shrink X` is homeomorphic to `X` when `X` is small. Used for universe-polymorphic constructions. Distinct from Topology/Homeomorph/Defs and Topology/Instances/Discrete.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: Topology on ℚ: the subspace topology from ℝ, which coincides with the metric topology from `|p - q|`. Proves ℚ is countable, dense in ℝ, and not locally compact. Distinct from Topology/Instances/RatLemmas and Topology/Instances/Real.

Name: RatLemmas.lean
Description: Supplementary lemmas about the topology of ℚ: sequential density of ℚ in ℝ, rational approximation, and ℚ-indexed nets. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Rat (basic instances) and Topology/Instances/Real.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: Topology on `ZMod n` (integers mod n): the discrete topology for finite `n`, and the p-adic topology arising as the profinite completion of ℤ. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Int (ℤ) and NumberTheory/Padics.

Name: ZMultiples.lean
Description: Topology on `AddSubgroup.zmultiples x` (the cyclic subgroup generated by `x`): the subspace topology from the ambient topological group. Used in Pontryagin dual calculations. Distinct from Topology/Instances/ZMod and Topology/Algebra/OpenSubgroup.

Name: Sign.lean
Description: Topology on the sign type `SignType = {-1, 0, 1}`: the discrete topology and instances for sign as a topological space. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Discrete and Order/Basic.

Name: TrivSqZeroExt.lean
Description: Topology on trivial square-zero extensions `TrivSqZeroExt R M` (the split extension `R ⋉ M`): the product topology, and that it is a topological ring when `R` is. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Algebra and Topology/Instances/Matrix.

Name: PNat.lean
Description: Topology on ℕ+ (positive naturals): the discrete topology and the embedding `ℕ+ → ℕ → ℝ`. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Nat and Topology/Instances/Discrete.

Name: Discrete.lean
Description: Discrete topology instances: types with a decidable equality carry the discrete topology, making them T1 and zero-dimensional. Provides `DiscreteTopology` instances for `Fin n`, `ℕ`, `ℤ`, `Bool`. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Nat (ℕ-specific) and Topology/Separation/Basic.

Name: ENat.lean
Description: Topology on `ℕ∞ = ℕ ⊔ {∞}` (extended naturals): the order topology, compactness, and continuous functions from `ℕ∞`. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Nat and Topology/Order/WithTop.

Name: CantorSet.lean
Description: The Cantor set as a topological space: a compact totally disconnected perfect metrizable space, homeomorphic to `{0,1}^ℕ` (the product of countably many two-point discrete spaces). Proves `IsCantorSet` characterisation. Distinct from Topology/Connected/TotallyDisconnected and Topology/MetricSpace/Perfect.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Topology on ℂ: the topology from the norm, completeness, and `ℂ ≃ₜ ℝ × ℝ`. Provides `Complex.instTopologicalSpace` and proves `ℂ` is locally compact, connected, and second-countable. Distinct from Analysis/Complex/Basic (analytic structure) and Topology/Instances/RealVectorSpace.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Topology on matrix spaces: `Matrix n m α` with the product topology is a topological ring when `α` is, continuous matrix multiplication, and the homeomorphism `Matrix n m α ≃ₜ (Fin n → Fin m → α)`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/Normed and Topology/Instances/RealVectorSpace.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Topology on ℕ: the discrete topology, convergent sequences are eventually constant, and the embedding `ℕ → ℝ` is a topological embedding. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Int (integers) and Topology/Instances/Discrete.

Name: Int.lean
Description: Topology on ℤ: the discrete topology, `ℤ` as a topological ring, and the fact that ℤ is countable and locally compact. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Discrete (discrete topology in general) and Topology/Instances/Nat.

Name: Irrational.lean
Description: Topology of irrational numbers: `Irrational` as a Gδ-dense subspace of ℝ, homeomorphic to ℕ^ℕ (the Baire space). Distinct from Topology/GDelta/Basic and Topology/MetricSpace/Polish.

Name: Spectral
Description: Spectral spaces (sober spaces with a basis of compact opens): the topology on the prime spectrum of a ring and Stone spaces.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Spectral maps: `IsSpectralMap f` — continuous maps between spectral spaces that send compact open sets to compact open sets (quasi-compact and quasi-separated morphisms). Distinct from Spectral/Basic and Topology/Maps/Basic.

Name: Prespectral.lean
Description: Prespectral spaces: intermediate axioms on the way to being a full spectral space. Distinct from Spectral/Basic (full spectral space) and Topology/Category/Locale.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Spectral spaces: `SpectralSpace X` — compact T0 spaces where the compact open sets form a basis closed under finite intersection. Proves `Spec R` is spectral, and spectral spaces are sober. Distinct from Spectral/Hom (spectral maps) and Topology/Category/Locale.

Name: ConstructibleTopology.lean
Description: Constructible topology on a spectral space: the topology generated by compact open sets and their complements. Proves the constructible topology is compact Hausdorff, and the original topology is recovered from it. Distinct from Spectral/Basic and AlgebraicGeometry/Spec.

Name: Sheaves
Description: Sheaves of types and algebraic structures on topological spaces, with restriction maps, gluing axioms, and sheafification.

Name: SheafCondition
Description: The sheaf condition for presheaves on a topological space: a presheaf F is a sheaf iff for every open cover {Uᵢ} of U and compatible sections sᵢ ∈ F(Uᵢ), there is a unique section s ∈ F(U) restricting to each sᵢ. Formulated as an equalizer condition. The gluing axiom and locality axiom. Used to verify that the structure sheaf of a scheme is indeed a sheaf. Distinct from CategoryTheory/Sites (Grothendieck topology generalization).

Name: Sites.lean
Description: Sheaf condition via Grothendieck sites.

Name: UniqueGluing.lean
Description: Unique gluing condition for sheaves.

Name: PairwiseIntersections.lean
Description: Sheaf condition via pairwise intersections.

Name: EqualizerProducts.lean
Description: Sheaf condition via equaliser of products.

Name: OpensLeCover.lean
Description: Sheaf condition via opens ≤ cover.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Presheaves on a topological space: `TopCat.Presheaf C X` as a contravariant functor from `Opens X` to a category `C`. Defines restriction maps, sections over open sets, and the `stalkFunctor`. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf (with gluing axiom) and Sheaves/Stalks.

Name: PresheafOfFunctions.lean
Description: Presheaves of functions: `continuousFunctions X Y` as a presheaf of sets (or rings/algebras) on `X` where sections over `U` are continuous functions `U → Y`. Distinct from Sheaves/SheafOfFunctions (satisfying the gluing condition) and ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: Points.lean
Description: Points of a topological space from a topos-theoretic perspective: geometric points (stalks at points) and the fact that enough points distinguish sheaves (the Comparison Lemma). Distinct from Sheaves/Stalks (stalk construction) and Topology/Category/Locale.

Name: MayerVietoris.lean
Description: Mayer–Vietoris sequence for sheaves: given `U ∪ V = X`, the sequence of global sections `F(X) → F(U) × F(V) → F(U ∩ V)` is exact. Used as a computational tool for sheaf cohomology. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf and AlgebraicTopology cohomology.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Sheaves over a point or over a category: `Sheaf.over` — the slice category of sheaves over a fixed object, and the associated adjunction. Distinct from Sheaves/Functors (general functoriality) and Sheaves/Sheaf.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: Sheaves on the one-point space `PUnit`: the category of sheaves on `PUnit` is equivalent to the category of all objects (since the only open cover is trivial). Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf and Sheaves/Stalks.

Name: Skyscraper.lean
Description: Skyscraper sheaves: `skyscraperSheaf p A` — the sheaf on `X` with stalk `A` at a point `p` and trivial stalks elsewhere. Used as an injective object in sheaf cohomology. Distinct from Sheaves/Stalks (general stalks) and Sheaves/Sheaf.

Name: Stalks.lean
Description: Stalks of presheaves/sheaves: `F.stalk x = colim_{x ∈ U} F(U)` (filtered colimit over opens containing `x`). Proves the stalk functor is exact for sheaves of abelian groups, and the germ map `F(U) → F.stalk x`. Distinct from Sheaves/Presheaf and Sheaves/Skyscraper.

Name: SheafOfFunctions.lean
Description: The sheaf of continuous functions: `sheafOfFunctions X Y` — the presheaf of continuous functions on `X` satisfies the gluing condition because continuous functions glue. Distinct from Sheaves/PresheafOfFunctions (no gluing) and ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: Sheaf.lean
