Description: Sheaves on a topological space: `Sheaf C X` — a presheaf satisfying the gluing axiom (sections on an open cover agreeing on overlaps glue uniquely to a global section). Proves the sheaf condition via the equaliser diagram. The main definition file for the Sheaves folder.

Name: Sheafify.lean
Description: Sheafification: the left adjoint to the inclusion of sheaves into presheaves. Constructs `Sheafify.sheafify F` (the associated sheaf) and proves the universal property: the sheafification map `F → aF` is universal among maps to sheaves. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf and Sheaves/Limits.

Name: CommRingCat.lean
Description: Sheaves of commutative rings `Sheaf TopologicalSpace.Opens (CommRingCat)`: the structure sheaf of a topological ring, localisation as a sheaf on Spec R. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf (abstract sheaves) and AlgebraicGeometry/StructureSheaf.

Name: Flasque.lean
Description: Flasque (flabby) sheaves: a sheaf `F` is flasque if every restriction map `F(U) → F(V)` (for `V ⊆ U`) is surjective. Proves flasque sheaves have trivial higher cohomology. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf and Sheaves/Limits.

Name: Alexandrov.lean
Description: Sheaves on Alexandrov spaces: on a space with the Alexandrov topology (from a preorder), sheaves correspond to monotone functors, and the sheaf condition simplifies. Distinct from Topology/Separation/AlexandrovDiscrete and Sheaves/Sheaf.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Abelian structure on sheaf categories: `Sheaf.instAbelianCategory` — the category of sheaves of abelian groups on a topological space is abelian. Proves kernels and cokernels in the sheaf category are computed stalkwise. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf (sheaf definition) and Sheaves/Limits.

Name: AddCommGrpCat.lean
Description: Sheaves of abelian groups `Sheaf TopologicalSpace.Opens (AddCommGrp)`: the forgetful functor to presheaves, global sections, and restriction maps. Distinct from Sheaves/CommRingCat (ring-valued sheaves) and Sheaves/Abelian.

Name: Forget.lean
Description: Forgetful functors for sheaves: `Sheaf.forget` forgets the sheaf condition and gives a presheaf, and `SheafOfFunctions.forget` forgets the algebraic structure. Distinct from Sheaves/Presheaf and Sheaves/Sheaf.

Name: LocallySurjective.lean
Description: Locally surjective morphisms of sheaves: `IsLocallySurjective f` — a morphism of sheaves is locally surjective iff it is surjective on stalks. Distinct from Sheaves/Stalks and Sheaves/Sheafify.

Name: LocalPredicate.lean
Description: Local predicates on sheaves: `LocalPredicate P` — a property `P` of sections is local if it can be checked on a cover. Used to define sheaves of functions satisfying local properties (like continuity). Distinct from Sheaves/PresheafOfFunctions and Sheaves/Sheaf.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in sheaf categories: `Sheaf.instHasLimits` — the category of sheaves has all limits (computed objectwise in the presheaf category and sheafified for colimits). Distinct from Sheaves/Abelian and Sheaves/Sheafify.

Name: Functors.lean
Description: Functors on sheaf categories: direct image `f_*F` and inverse image `f^*F` for a continuous map `f`, and the adjunction `f^* ⊣ f_*`. Distinct from Sheaves/Presheaf (presheaf functors) and Sheaves/Stalks.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation/import file for the Sheaves folder: aggregates key imports so that `import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Init` brings in the core sheaf theory. Distinct from Sheaves/Sheaf (main definition file).

Name: VectorBundle
Description: Vector bundles over topological spaces: locally trivial families of vector spaces with continuous transition functions.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Bundle of linear maps between two vector bundles: `Bundle.ContinuousLinearMap F₁ E₁ F₂ E₂` — the vector bundle whose fibre over `b` is `E₁ b →L[F] E₂ b`. Distinct from VectorBundle/Basic and VectorBundle/Constructions.

Name: Riemannian.lean
Description: Riemannian vector bundles: vector bundles whose fibers carry an inner product varying continuously over the base. Introduces `IsContinuousRiemannianBundle F E` (inner product continuous on base point) and `RiemannianBundle E` (data of an inner product on fibers). Shows the inner product of two continuous fiber sections is continuous.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional vector bundles: `FiniteDimensional F (E x)` for each fibre — existence of local frames, and the structure group reduces to `GL(n, F)`. Distinct from VectorBundle/Basic and VectorBundle/Riemannian.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Vector bundles: `VectorBundle F E` — a fiber bundle whose fibres are (topological) vector spaces isomorphic to `F`, with local trivialisations that are linear maps. Proves the transition functions are continuous linear equivalences. Distinct from FiberBundle/Basic (general fiber bundles) and VectorBundle/Constructions.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions on vector bundles: direct sum `E₁ ⊕ E₂`, tensor product `E₁ ⊗ E₂`, and pullback `f*E` of vector bundles. Distinct from VectorBundle/Basic (single bundle) and VectorBundle/Hom (bundle of homomorphisms).

Name: UniformSpace
Description: Uniform spaces and uniform continuity: the entourage-based axiomatization that generalizes both metric spaces and topological groups.

Name: Ultra
Description: Ultrauniform spaces: uniform spaces whose uniformity has a base of equivalence relations (instead of just symmetric entourages). Ultrauniform spaces are exactly the uniform spaces underlying profinite groups and ultrametric spaces. The completion of an ultrauniform space is again ultrauniform. Distinct from MetricSpace/Ultra (the metric special case) and from the general UniformSpace folder.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of ultrauniform spaces.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion of ultrauniform spaces.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ultrauniform spaces.

Name: OfFun.lean
Description: Uniform structure from a function: given `f : α → β` with `β` a uniform space, the initial uniform structure making `f` uniformly continuous. Distinct from UniformSpace/Defs and Topology/Defs/Induced.

Name: Path.lean
Description: Paths in uniform spaces: uniform continuity of paths, the uniform structure on `Path x y`, and uniform convergence of path sequences. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Path (homotopy of paths) and UniformSpace/CompactConvergence.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Product uniform structure: `Pi.uniformSpace` — the product of uniform spaces with the entourages generated by finitely many component entourages. Proves Tychonoff's theorem for uniform spaces and that the product is complete iff each factor is. Distinct from UniformSpace/Basic and Topology/Constructions.

Name: OfCompactT2.lean
Description: The unique uniformity on a compact Hausdorff space: a compact T2 space has a unique uniformity compatible with its topology (the neighbourhood uniformity). Distinct from UniformSpace/Compact and Topology/Separation/Hausdorff.

Name: HeineCantor.lean
Description: Heine–Cantor theorem: a continuous map from a compact uniform space to a uniform space is uniformly continuous. Distinct from UniformSpace/Compact (compactness results) and MetricSpace/Lipschitz.

Name: LocallyUniformConvergence.lean
Description: Locally uniform convergence: `TendstoLocallyUniformly F f l` — `F` converges to `f` uniformly on compact subsets. Proves that locally uniform limits of continuous functions are continuous. Distinct from UniformSpace/UniformConvergence (global uniform) and ContinuousMap/Compact.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Uniform structure on matrix spaces: `Matrix n m α` inherits the product uniform structure from `α`, and matrix multiplication is uniformly continuous. Distinct from Topology/Instances/Matrix (topological) and UniformSpace/Pi.

Name: ProdApproximation.lean
Description: Approximating elements of a product uniform space: given a Cauchy filter on `α × β`, it can be approximated using Cauchy filters on each factor. Distinct from UniformSpace/Pi and UniformSpace/Cauchy.

Name: UniformConvergenceTopology.lean
Description: The uniform convergence topology on function spaces: `UniformFun X Y` — the space of functions `X → Y` with the topology of uniform convergence, and `UniformFunOn` for uniform convergence on compact subsets. Proves the adjunction with the discrete topology. Distinct from UniformSpace/UniformConvergence (the convergence concept).

Name: UniformEmbedding.lean
Description: Uniform embeddings: `UniformEmbedding f` — an injective uniformly continuous map whose inverse is uniformly continuous on the image. Proves uniform embeddings into complete spaces have an extension. Distinct from UniformSpace/Basic and MetricSpace/Isometry.

Name: Uniformizable.lean
Description: Uniformisable topological spaces: `UniformizableSpace X` — spaces whose topology is induced by some uniformity. Equivalent to complete regularity. Distinct from UniformSpace/Defs and Topology/Separation/CompletelyRegular.

Name: UniformConvergence.lean
Description: Uniform convergence of functions: `TendstoUniformly F f l` — `F` converges uniformly to `f` along filter `l`. Proves uniform limits of continuous functions are continuous. Distinct from UniformSpace/UniformConvergenceTopology (the topology on function spaces) and UniformSpace/LocallyUniformConvergence.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Uniform structure on ℝ: the standard uniform structure from the metric, completeness, and the Bolzano–Weierstrass theorem. Distinct from UniformSpace/CompareReals and Topology/Instances/RealVectorSpace.

Name: Separation.lean
Description: Separation quotient of a uniform space: `SeparationQuotient α` — quotienting a non-separated uniform space by the equivalence relation `x ~ y ↔ (x,y) ∈ ⋂ entourages` to get a separated (Hausdorff) uniform space. Distinct from UniformSpace/CompleteSeparated and Topology/Separation/Basic.

Name: UniformApproximation.lean
Description: Uniform approximation in function spaces: a function is in the uniform closure of a set `S` of functions iff it is uniformly approximated by elements of `S`. Stone–Weierstrass via uniform approximation. Distinct from ContinuousMap/StoneWeierstrass and UniformSpace/UniformConvergence.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Uniform equivalences: `UniformEquiv α β` — a bijection that is both uniformly continuous with uniformly continuous inverse. Proves the group structure and `UniformEquiv.toHomeomorph`. Distinct from UniformSpace/Basic and Homeomorph/Defs.

Name: Cauchy.lean
Description: Cauchy filters in uniform spaces: `Cauchy f` — a filter that is proper and `f ×ˢ f ≤ uniformity`, and `CauchySeq`. Proves completeness in terms of Cauchy filters, and that Cauchy sequences in metric spaces match. Distinct from UniformSpace/Basic and UniformSpace/Completion.

Name: Closeds.lean
Description: Closed sets in uniform spaces: the uniform closure and the fact that a set is closed iff it equals its uniform closure. Distinct from Topology/Sets/Closeds (topological) and UniformSpace/Basic.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Compactness in uniform spaces: a compact uniform space has a unique uniformity compatible with its topology, and compact uniform spaces are complete. Proves the Heine–Cantor theorem: continuous maps from compact uniform spaces are uniformly continuous. Distinct from UniformSpace/Basic and Compactness/Compact.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for uniform spaces: entourages, the uniformity filter `uniformity α`, `UniformContinuous`, `UniformEmbedding`, and the equivalence between uniform continuity and the ε-δ condition. The main workhorse file for the UniformSpace folder.

Name: AbsoluteValue.lean
Description: Uniform structure from an absolute value: the uniform space defined by `d(x,y) = |x - y|` for a ring with an absolute value (as in p-adic or archimedean absolute values). Distinct from Analysis/AbsoluteValue/Equivalence and UniformSpace/Basic.

Name: AbstractCompletion.lean
Description: Abstract completion of a uniform space: `AbstractCompletion α` — a complete uniform space `β` with a dense uniform embedding `i : α → β`, satisfying the universal property. Distinct from UniformSpace/Completion (concrete construction) and Topology/Algebra/LinearMapCompletion.

Name: Ascoli.lean
Description: Arzelà–Ascoli theorem in uniform spaces: a family of maps is precompact in the compact-open uniform topology iff it is equicontinuous and pointwise precompact. Distinct from MetricSpace/Equicontinuity and ContinuousMap/Compact.

Name: CompactConvergence.lean
Description: Compact-convergence uniform structure: the uniform structure on `C(X, Y)` of uniform convergence on compact subsets. Proves this coincides with the compact-open topology when `Y` is a metric space. Distinct from UniformSpace/UniformConvergence and ContinuousMap/Compact.

Name: Dini.lean
Description: Dini's theorem in uniform spaces: a monotone decreasing net of continuous functions on a compact space converging pointwise to a continuous limit converges uniformly. Distinct from UniformSpace/UniformConvergence and Compactness/Compact.

Name: DiscreteUniformity.lean
Description: Discrete uniform structure: the discrete uniformity on a type (where the only entourage containing the diagonal is the whole space), and the fact that discrete spaces are complete. Distinct from UniformSpace/Defs and Topology/Instances/Discrete.

Name: Equicontinuity.lean
Description: Equicontinuity in uniform spaces: `UniformEquicontinuous F` — a family of maps `X → Y` with a common uniform modulus of continuity. The Arzelà–Ascoli theorem: equicontinuous families are precompact in the compact-open uniform structure. Distinct from MetricSpace/Equicontinuity and UniformSpace/Ascoli.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for uniform spaces: `UniformSpace α` as a filter `uniformity` of entourages satisfying reflexivity, symmetry, and transitivity conditions. Contains `UniformSpace.Core` and the typeclass. Distinct from UniformSpace/Basic (main API).

Name: CompareReals.lean
Description: Comparison of uniform structures on ℝ: the absolute value uniformity, the standard metric uniformity, and the order uniformity coincide. Distinct from UniformSpace/Real and Topology/Instances/Rat.

Name: CompleteSeparated.lean
Description: Complete separated (Hausdorff) uniform spaces: `SeparatedSpace α` — the uniformity is Hausdorff (intersection of all entourages is the diagonal). Proves that completions of separated spaces are separated. Distinct from UniformSpace/Separation and UniformSpace/Completion.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion of a uniform space: `UniformSpace.Completion α` as the quotient of Cauchy filters, with the induced uniform structure. Proves the universal property: every uniformly continuous map to a complete space extends uniquely. Distinct from UniformSpace/AbstractCompletion and MetricSpace/Completion.

Name: Semicontinuity
Description: Semicontinuous functions (upper and lower semicontinuous) and their roles in optimization and measure theory.

Name: Hemicontinuity.lean
Description: Hemicontinuity of set-valued maps: `LowerHemicontinuous F` (the graph is open-lower-semicontinuous) and `UpperHemicontinuous F`. Used in fixed-point theorems for set-valued maps. Distinct from Semicontinuity/Defs (single-valued) and Topology/Order/Monotone.

Name: Lindelof.lean
Description: Semicontinuity in Lindelöf spaces: a family of lsc functions on a Lindelöf space has a pointwise infimum that is also lsc (by countability argument). Distinct from Semicontinuity/Basic and Topology/Compactness/Lindelof.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results about semicontinuous functions: `LowerSemicontinuousOn`, `UpperSemicontinuousOn`, their sum/product closure properties, and the infimum of a family of lsc functions is lsc. Distinct from Semicontinuity/Defs (definitions) and Topology/Order/LiminfLimsup.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions of semicontinuity: `LowerSemicontinuous f` (lsc: `{x | f x ≤ c}` is closed for all `c`), `UpperSemicontinuous f` (usc), and their `At`/`On`/`Within` variants. Distinct from Semicontinuity/Basic (lemmas) and Topology/Order/Basic.

Name: Order
Description: Ordered topological spaces: the order topology, upper and lower semicontinuity, and the topology of the extended reals.

Name: Hom
Description: Continuous order-preserving maps: the OrderTopHom type combining continuity and monotonicity, and its composition. Used when building maps between ordered topological spaces that must respect both structures simultaneously. Distinct from Topology/Order (the spaces themselves) and Order/Hom (order-preserving maps without topology).

Name: Esakia.lean
Description: Esakia maps: order homeomorphisms preserving opens.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order topology homomorphisms.

Name: Category
Description: Categorical aspects of ordered topological spaces: the category of topological spaces with a compatible order, and functors relating it to TopCat and to the category of ordered sets. Used to study the Scott topology categorically and to relate order-theoretic and topological constructions. Distinct from Topology/Order (the individual ordered-space instances).

Name: FrameAdjunction.lean
Description: Frame-topology adjunction.

Name: AlexDisc.lean
Description: Alexandroff-discrete spaces in the order category.

Name: NhdsSet.lean
Description: Neighbourhood sets in ordered topological spaces: `nhdsSet s` (the filter of all sets containing `s` in their interior) and its properties in linear orders (determined by the endpoints of `s`). Distinct from Topology/Order/LeftRightNhds and Topology/Defs/Filter.

Name: MonotoneConvergence.lean
Description: Monotone convergence in ordered topological spaces: a bounded monotone sequence in a complete order converges, and `⨆ n, f n = lim f n` for increasing bounded sequences. Distinct from Topology/Order/LiminfLimsup and MeasureTheory/MonotoneConvergence.

Name: OrderClosedExtr.lean
Description: Extrema of functions on order-closed sets: the maximum of a continuous function on an order-closed compact set is attained. Distinct from Topology/Order/OrderClosed (set definition) and Topology/Order/Compact.

Name: OrderClosed.lean
Description: Order-closed sets: a set `s` is order-closed if `x ≤ y ≤ z` with `x, z ∈ s` implies `y ∈ s` (i.e., closed under betweenness). Characterisation: order-closed closed sets are exactly the unions of closed intervals. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic and Topology/Order/IsLocallyClosed.

Name: MonotoneContinuity.lean
Description: Continuity of monotone functions: a monotone map is continuous iff it has no jump discontinuities, and on a dense order it is continuous iff `f` and `f⁻¹` preserve intervals. Distinct from Topology/Order/Monotone (discontinuity structure) and Topology/Order/MonotoneConvergence.

Name: LocalExtr.lean
Description: Local extrema of continuous functions: `IsLocalMin f x` (a neighbourhood of `x` where `f x ≤ f y`), and the fact that a local minimum of a differentiable function has zero derivative. Distinct from Topology/Order/Compact (global extrema) and Calculus/DerivativeTest.

Name: LiminfLimsup.lean
Description: `liminf` and `limsup` in ordered topological spaces: `Filter.liminf f l`, `Filter.limsup f l`, their continuity properties, and the Fatou lemma for filters. Distinct from Topology/Order/Filter (general filter order topology) and Topology/Order/MonotoneConvergence.

Name: Monotone.lean
Description: Monotone functions in ordered topological spaces: a monotone map between ordered topological spaces is measurable, and has at most countably many discontinuities. Distinct from Topology/Order/MonotoneContinuity (continuity criteria) and Topology/Order/LeftRightLim.

Name: LowerUpperTopology.lean
Description: Lower and upper topologies on an ordered set: the lower topology (open sets are upper sets) and upper topology (open sets are lower sets), and the Alexandrov topology. Distinct from Topology/Order/ScottTopology and Topology/Order/LawsonTopology.

Name: PartialSups.lean
Description: Partial suprema in ordered topological spaces: `partialSups f n = ⨆ i ≤ n, f i` — the running maximum of a sequence — and its convergence and continuity properties. Distinct from Topology/Order/MonotoneConvergence and Order/Monotone files.

Name: T5.lean
Description: T5 (completely normal) ordered spaces: a linear order with the order topology is T5 (every two separated subsets have disjoint open neighbourhoods). Distinct from Topology/Order/IsNormal (T4) and Topology/Separation/Basic.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Topology generated by successor and predecessor: in a linear order with successors (e.g., ℤ, ℕ), the order topology from `succ`/`pred` and its relationship to the discrete topology or coarser topologies. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic and Topology/Instances/Int.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Topology on `WithTop α` and `WithBot α`: the order topology on `α ⊔ {⊤}`, continuity of `WithTop.some`, and the fact that `WithTop α` is compact when `α` is. Distinct from Topology/Instances/ENat and Topology/Order/Basic.

Name: UpperLowerSetTopology.lean
Description: Topology on upper and lower sets: `UpperSet.instTopologicalSpace` and `LowerSet.instTopologicalSpace` — the induced subspace topology on the lattice of upper/lower sets of an ordered space. Distinct from Topology/Order/LowerUpperTopology (the order topology) and Topology/Sets/Order.

Name: ScottTopology.lean
Description: Scott topology on a partial order or dcpo: open sets are the Scott-open sets (upper sets closed under directed suprema). Proves the Scott topology is `T₀` and that continuous functions between dcpos are Scott-continuous iff continuous for the Scott topology. Distinct from Topology/Order/LawsonTopology.

Name: ProjIcc.lean
Description: Projection onto intervals: `projIcc a b h x = max a (min b x)` — the nearest-point projection onto a closed interval `[a,b]`. Proves continuity of `projIcc` and its use in homotopy retractions. Distinct from Topology/Order/Compact (compactness of intervals) and Analysis/Convex/MetricSpace.

Name: Priestley.lean
Description: Priestley spaces: compact totally order-disconnected spaces — used in the duality between bounded distributive lattices and Priestley spaces. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic and Topology/Separation/Profinite.

Name: Rolle.lean
Description: Rolle's theorem in topological order settings: a continuous function on `[a,b]` with `f(a) = f(b)` has a local extremum in `(a,b)`, and a derivative version. Distinct from Topology/Order/LocalExtr and Analysis/Calculus/MeanValue.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Order topology on ℝ: the equivalence between the order topology and the metric topology on ℝ, and specific limit/continuity results for real-valued functions. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic (abstract ordered spaces) and Topology/Instances/Rat.

Name: ExtendFrom.lean
Description: Extending functions from a dense subset to the closure using order-monotonicity: if `f` is monotone on a dense subset, it extends to a monotone function on the closure. Used for extending monotone functions to their closure domain. Distinct from Topology/Order/MonotoneContinuity and Topology/Order/Monotone.

Name: DenselyOrdered.lean
Description: Topology of densely ordered spaces: in a dense linear order with the order topology, the open sets are the unions of open intervals, and the space is connected. Proves `DenselyOrdered → ConnectedSpace` for the order topology. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic and Topology/Connected/Basic.

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Filter-based order topology: `nhds` in an ordered topological space via the filter of order-intervals, `Filter.liminf`, `Filter.limsup`, and `tendsto` criteria via upper/lower sets. Distinct from Topology/Order/LiminfLimsup and Topology/Defs/Filter.

Name: ExtrClosure.lean
Description: Extrema in closures: extreme values of a function on a set are achieved on the closure. Relates `IsMinOn`/`IsMaxOn` to `IsClosed` assumptions. Distinct from Topology/Order/LocalExtr (local extrema) and Topology/Order/Compact.

Name: CountableSeparating.lean
Description: Countably separating ordered spaces: a second-countable ordered space has a countable dense subset, and the order topology is the same as the Euclidean topology. Distinct from Topology/Order/DenselyOrdered and Topology/Metrizable/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordered topological spaces: `OrderTopology α` — the topology coincides with the order topology generated by open rays. Proves `OrderTopology ℝ`, the intermediate value theorem, and that `[a,b]` is compact in a linear order. The foundational file for Topology/Order.

Name: AtTopBotIxx.lean
Description: Limits at `+∞` and `-∞` and their interaction with intervals: `Filter.atTop`, `Filter.atBot`, tendsto results through intervals `[a, ∞)`, and the neighbourhood filters at `+∞` in an ordered topological space. Distinct from Topology/Order/LiminfLimsup and Order/Filter.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Compactness in ordered topological spaces: every nonempty closed bounded subset of a complete linear order is compact, and the extreme value theorem (continuous function on a compact ordered space attains its bounds). Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic and Compactness/Compact.

Name: Bornology.lean
Description: Bornology of ordered spaces: the order bornology where bounded sets are order-bounded (between two elements). Distinct from Bornology/Basic (abstract bornology) and Topology/Order/Basic.

Name: HullKernel.lean
Description: Hull-kernel topology on a set of ideals or primes: `hullKernel` — the topology on `Spec R` (prime ideals of a ring) where closed sets are `V(I) = {p | I ⊆ p}`. Used in algebraic geometry and spectral space theory. Distinct from Topology/Spectral/Basic and AlgebraicGeometry/Spec.

Name: LeftRight.lean
Description: Left and right limits: `Filter.atLeft x` and `Filter.atRight x` — the punctured left and right neighbourhood filters in a linear order. Proves `tendsto_atRight_nhdsWithin` and related filter convergence facts. Distinct from Topology/Order/LeftRightLim and Topology/Order/LeftRightNhds.

Name: LawsonTopology.lean
Description: Lawson topology on a semilattice: the join of the Scott topology and the lower topology (Alexandrov of reverse order). A dcpo with the Lawson topology is compact Hausdorff iff it is a domain. Distinct from Topology/Order/ScottTopology and Topology/Order/LowerUpperTopology.

Name: LeftRightNhds.lean
Description: Left and right neighbourhood filters `nhdsWithin (Iio x)` and `nhdsWithin (Ioi x)`: their relationship to `atLeft`/`atRight` and the `nhds` filter in an order topology. Distinct from Topology/Order/LeftRight (punctured filters) and Topology/Order/AtTopBotIxx.

Name: LeftRightLim.lean
Description: Left and right limits `leftLim f x` and `rightLim f x`: the limits of `f` as approached from left/right in a linear order. Proves `leftLim_eq_rightLim_iff_continuousAt` and monotone function left/right limits always exist. Distinct from Topology/Order/LeftRight (filters) and Topology/Order/MonotoneContinuity.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Topological lattices: `TopologicalLattice α` — `sup` and `inf` are jointly continuous. Instances for ℝ, `EReal`, and function lattices. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic (ordered spaces) and ContinuousMap/Lattice.

Name: IsLocallyClosed.lean
Description: Locally closed sets in ordered spaces: intersections of open and closed sets in a linearly ordered space, and their characterisation via half-open intervals. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic and Topology/GDelta/Basic.

Name: IntermediateValue.lean
Description: The intermediate value theorem for connected spaces: a continuous function on a connected topological space that takes values above and below a target must hit that target. The classical form: if f : [a, b] → ℝ is continuous and f(a) < c < f(b) then ∃ x ∈ (a,b), f(x) = c (IsPreconnected.intermediate_value). Contains the Brouwer fixed-point theorem for [0,1] as a corollary, and applications to root-finding.

Name: IsNormal.lean
Description: Normality of ordered topological spaces: every linear order with the order topology is normal (T4). Proves `LinearOrder.instNormalSpace`. Distinct from Topology/Separation/Basic (abstract normality) and Topology/Order/T5.

Name: IsLUB.lean
Description: Topology at least upper bounds: the `nhds` filter at a `LUB` of a set, and that in an order topology the neighbourhood of a supremum is determined by sequences approaching it from below. Distinct from Topology/Order/LiminfLimsup and Order/Bounds.

Name: Sets
Description: Topological properties of subsets: open, closed, dense, nowhere dense, meager, and residual sets.

Name: Opens.lean
Description: The type of open sets `TopologicalSpace.Opens α`: a `Frame` (complete lattice where `⊓` distributes over `⨆`), with `Opens.comap f` for continuous `f`, and the basis of open sets. Distinct from Sets/Closeds and Topology/Defs/Basic.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order properties of topological sets: the lattice of open/closed sets, `Opens.gc` (the Galois connection between open and closed sets), and monotone operations on collections of sets. Distinct from Sets/Opens and Topology/Order/Basic.

Name: VietorisTopology.lean
Description: Vietoris topology on the hyperspace of closed sets: `Closeds X` with the topology generated by sets `{F | F ∩ U ≠ ∅}` and `{F | F ⊆ U}`. Proves compactness of the Vietoris hyperspace when `X` is compact. Distinct from Sets/Closeds and MetricSpace/HausdorffDistance.

Name: OpenCover.lean
Description: Open covers and their properties: `OpenCover X` — a collection of open sets whose union is `X`, finite subcovers, and the Lebesgue number of a cover in metric spaces. Distinct from Compactness/Compact and MetricSpace/MetricSeparated.

Name: Closeds.lean
Description: The type of closed sets `TopologicalSpace.Closeds α`: a `CompleteLattice` where `⊓` is intersection and `⊔` is closure of union. Proves continuity of the closure map and the Vietoris topology on `Closeds`. Distinct from Sets/Compacts (compact closed sets) and MetricSpace/Closeds.

Name: CompactOpenCovered.lean
Description: Compact-open covered sets: `CompactOpenCovered X` — sets that can be covered by finitely many compact-open sets, used in the theory of locally compact spaces and profinite groups. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Basic (compact-open topology) and Sets/Compacts.

Name: Compacts.lean
Description: The type of compact sets `TopologicalSpace.Compacts α`: a `SemilatticeSup` (compact under finite union), with the Hausdorff metric when `α` is a metric space. Distinct from Sets/Closeds (closed but not necessarily compact) and Compactness/Compact.

Name: Separation
Description: Separation axioms: T0, T1, Hausdorff (T2), regular (T3), and normal (T4) spaces, with the Urysohn lemma.

Name: LinearUpperLowerSetTopology.lean
Description: Separation properties of the upper and lower set topologies on a linear order: the lower topology on a linear order is T0 but not T1, and the Alexandrov topology derived from a linear order. Distinct from Topology/Order/LowerUpperTopology and Separation/AlexandrovDiscrete.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for separation axioms: closure of finite sets, closedness of diagonal maps, and technical lemmas used across the Separation folder. Distinct from Separation/Basic (main definitions) and Separation/Hausdorff.

Name: Hausdorff.lean
Description: Hausdorff separation (T2): `T2Space X` — distinct points have disjoint open neighbourhoods. Proves compact subsets of Hausdorff spaces are closed, Hausdorff spaces are T1, and the product of Hausdorff spaces is Hausdorff. Distinct from Separation/Basic (all axioms) and Separation/Regular.

Name: NotNormal.lean
Description: Examples of non-normal spaces: the Niemytzki (Moore) plane, the Sorgenfrey plane, and other standard counterexamples showing that regular + Lindelöf does not imply normal in general. Distinct from Separation/Basic and Topology/Order/IsNormal.

Name: SeparatedNhds.lean
Description: Separated neighbourhoods: `SeparatedNhds s t` — `s` and `t` have disjoint open neighbourhoods. Characterises normal spaces by `SeparatedNhds` for disjoint closed sets. Distinct from Separation/Basic and Separation/Regular.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular (T3) and normal (T4) spaces: `RegularSpace X` (points and closed sets are separated by open sets), `NormalSpace X` (disjoint closed sets have disjoint open neighbourhoods), Urysohn's lemma, and Tietze extension. Distinct from Separation/Hausdorff and Separation/Basic.

Name: Profinite.lean
Description: Profinite spaces: compact Hausdorff totally disconnected spaces. Proves profinite spaces are inverse limits of finite discrete spaces, and every compact Hausdorff space is a quotient of a profinite space. Distinct from Topology/Connected/TotallyDisconnected and Topology/Algebra/ClopenNhdofOne.

Name: CompletelyRegular.lean
Description: Completely regular (Tychonoff) spaces: `CompletelyRegularSpace X` — points and closed sets are separated by continuous functions. Proves that completely regular spaces embed into `[0,1]^I` and the Stone–Čech compactification is Tychonoff. Distinct from Separation/Basic and Compactification/StoneCech.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic separation axioms: `T0Space`, `T1Space`, `T2Space` (Hausdorff), `T3Space` (regular), `T4Space` (normal), and their implications. Proves points are closed in T1, limits are unique in T2, and Urysohn's lemma in T4. The foundational separation file.

Name: AlexandrovDiscrete.lean
Description: Alexandrov discrete spaces: topological spaces where arbitrary intersections of open sets are open (T0 + Alexandrov = equivalent to preorders). Distinct from Topology/Separation/Basic and Topology/Order/ScottTopology.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Separation and connectedness: a connected Hausdorff space with more than one point has cardinality at least `𝔠`, and connected + totally disconnected iff singleton. Distinct from Topology/Connected/Basic and Topology/Separation/Basic.

Name: GDelta.lean
Description: Gδ sets and separation: in a T1 space every singleton is a Gδ set, and in a perfectly normal space every closed set is a Gδ. Distinct from Topology/GDelta/Basic and Separation/Basic.

Name: DisjointCover.lean
Description: Disjoint open covers and separation: a space is normal iff every finite open cover can be shrunk to a closed cover with the same shape. Used in partition of unity arguments. Distinct from Separation/Basic and Compactness/Paracompact.

Name: CountableSeparatingOn.lean
Description: Countably separating families of functions on a topological space: the existence of countably many continuous functions separating points, characterising second-countable T3 spaces. Distinct from Separation/Basic and Metrizable/Urysohn.

Name: Hom
Description: Continuous group and ring homomorphisms as bundled structures: the category of topological groups and topological rings.

Name: Open.lean
Description: Open maps and quotient maps: `IsOpenMap f` (images of open sets are open), `IsQuotientMap f` (a surjective map whose topology is the coinduced one). Proves composition of open maps is open and the relationship between quotient maps and identifications. Distinct from Topology/Maps/Basic.

Name: ContinuousEvalConst.lean
Description: Constant-evaluation continuity: `ContinuousEvalConst (X Y)` — for each fixed `x : X`, the map `f ↦ f x` from `C(X, Y)` to `Y` is continuous. Weaker than joint continuity of evaluation. Distinct from Topology/Hom/ContinuousEval (joint continuity) and ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: ContinuousEval.lean
Description: Continuous evaluation maps: `ContinuousEval (X Y)` — the evaluation `ev : C(X,Y) × X → Y` is continuous. Characterises spaces where the compact-open topology makes evaluation continuous (exponential objects in `Top`). Distinct from Topology/Hom/ContinuousEvalConst and ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: Compactness
Description: Compact and locally compact spaces: equivalent characterizations, Tychonoff's theorem, the tube lemma, and proper maps.

Name: LocallyFinite.lean
Description: Locally finite families of sets: `LocallyFinite f` (every point has a neighbourhood meeting only finitely many `f i`). Proves that locally finite families of closed sets have closed union, and their interaction with compactness. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Paracompact (which uses locally finite covers).

Name: LocallyCompact.lean
Description: Locally compact spaces: `LocallyCompactSpace X` (every point has a compact neighbourhood). Proves the locally compact Hausdorff space regularity, the one-point compactification, and that proper maps have compact fibres. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Compact and Topology/Compactification/StoneCech.

Name: Lindelof.lean
Description: Lindelöf spaces: `LindelofSpace X` (every open cover has a countable subcover). Proves that second-countable spaces are Lindelöf, and that Lindelöf regular spaces are paracompact. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Paracompact and Topology/Compactness/SigmaCompact.

Name: NhdsKer.lean
Description: Neighbourhood kernel (intersection of all neighbourhoods) in compact spaces: `nhdsKer x = ⋂ U ∈ 𝓝 x, U` and its properties. In a T1 space the kernel is `{x}`. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Filter (neighbourhood filter definitions) and Topology/Separation/Basic.

Name: SigmaCompact.lean
Description: σ-compact spaces: `SigmaCompactSpace X` (a countable union of compact sets). Proves that Euclidean spaces, locally compact σ-compact spaces are σ-compact, and that σ-compact regular spaces are Lindelöf. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/LocallyCompact and Topology/Compactness/Lindelof.

Name: PseudometrizableLindelof.lean
Description: A pseudometrizable space is Lindelöf iff it is second countable. Connects the bornological/metrizability theory with the Lindelöf covering property. Distinct from Topology/Metrizable/Urysohn and Topology/Compactness/Lindelof.

Name: Paracompact.lean
Description: Paracompact spaces: `ParacompactSpace X` (every open cover has a locally finite open refinement). Proves that metric spaces and Lindelöf regular spaces are paracompact, and that paracompact spaces admit partitions of unity. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Lindelof and Topology/MetricSpace/PartitionOfUnity.

Name: HilbertCubeEmbedding.lean
Description: Embedding of a second-countable compact Hausdorff space into the Hilbert cube `[0,1]^ℕ`: the Urysohn metrisation theorem consequence that every compact metrizable space embeds in the Hilbert cube. Distinct from Topology/Metrizable/Urysohn and Topology/MetricSpace/Completion.

Name: CompactlyCoherentSpace.lean
Description: `CompactlyCoherentSpace X`: a topological space where compactness is 'coherent' (determined by coherent systems of compact sets). Used in spectral space theory. Distinct from Topology/Spectral/Basic and Topology/Compactness/CompactlyGeneratedSpace.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Compact topological spaces and compact sets: `IsCompact s` (every open cover has a finite subcover), the finite intersection property characterisation, images of compact sets under continuous maps are compact, and products of compact spaces are compact (Tychonoff). The foundational file for the Compactness folder.

Name: Bases.lean
Description: Compactness from bases: Alexander's sub-base theorem (a space is compact iff every sub-basic open cover has a finite subcover), and compactness criteria via network bases. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Compact (general compactness) and Topology/Defs/Basic.

Name: CompactlyGeneratedSpace.lean
Description: `CompactlyGeneratedSpace X`: a space where the topology is the finest making all compact subsets compact — equivalently, `U` open iff `U ∩ K` is open for every compact `K`. Proves that first-countable spaces and locally compact spaces are compactly generated. Distinct from Topology/Category/CompactlyGenerated.

Name: DeltaGeneratedSpace.lean
Description: `DeltaGeneratedSpace X`: a space generated by the images of continuous maps from the unit interval `[0,1]`. Proves that manifolds are Δ-generated. Distinct from Topology/Category/DeltaGenerated (categorical treatment) and Topology/Compactness/CompactlyGeneratedSpace.

Name: CountablyCompact.lean
Description: Countably compact and sequentially compact spaces: `CountablyCompact X` (every countable open cover has a finite subcover), `SequentiallyCompact X` (every sequence has a convergent subsequence), and the relations between these. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Compact and Topology/MetricSpace/Sequences.

Name: CompactSystem.lean
Description: Systems of compact sets: directed systems of compact sets and their properties in compactness arguments. Used in inverse limit constructions and profinite group theory. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Compact (single compact sets) and Topology/Compactness/LocallyCompact.

Name: Compactification
Description: Compactifications: the one-point (Alexandroff) compactification and the Stone-Cech compactification.

Name: OnePoint
Description: The one-point (Alexandroff) compactification: adjoining a single point ∞ to a locally compact Hausdorff space X to obtain a compact Hausdorff space X* = X ∪ {∞}. The open neighborhoods of ∞ are complements of compact sets in X. The one-point compactification of ℝⁿ is Sⁿ. Distinct from the Stone-Čech compactification (Topology/Ultrafilter) — the one-point compactification is smaller and only Hausdorff when X is locally compact.

Name: Sphere.lean
Description: One-point compactification is a sphere.

Name: ProjectiveLine.lean
Description: Projective line as one-point compactification of ℝ.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: One-point compactification: `OnePoint X = X ∪ {∞}`.

Name: StoneCech.lean
Description: Stone–Čech compactification `βX` of a topological space `X`: universal property (every continuous map `X → K` with `K` compact Hausdorff extends uniquely to `βX → K`), the embedding `X → βX` is a dense embedding when `X` is completely regular, and `βX` is compact Hausdorff. Distinct from Topology/Category/Compactum and Topology/Separation/CompletelyRegular.

Name: Constructions
Description: Standard topological constructions: product topology, subspace topology, quotient topology, and coproduct topology.

Name: SumProd.lean
Description: Topology on sums and products: distributivity `(X ⊕ Y) × Z ≃ₜ (X × Z) ⊕ (Y × Z)`, and related homeomorphisms between combinations of sums and products. Distinct from Topology/Homeomorph/Lemmas (general homeomorphism lemmas) and Topology/Compactness/Compact.

Name: Connected
Description: Connected and path-connected spaces: the connected components, the intermediate value theorem, and locally connected spaces.

Name: PathConnected.lean
Description: Path-connected spaces and path-connected sets: `IsPathConnected s` (any two points are joined by a path in `s`), `PathConnectedSpace X`. Proves path-connected implies connected and the continuous image of a path-connected set is path-connected. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Path (homotopy of paths).

Name: PathComponentOne.lean
Description: Path component of the identity element in a topological group: the path-connected component of `1 : G` is a normal subgroup of `G`. Used in the topology of Lie groups. Distinct from Topology/Connected/PathConnected and Topology/Homotopy/HomotopyGroup.

Name: TotallyDisconnected.lean
Description: Totally disconnected spaces: `TotallyDisconnectedSpace X` (every connected subset is a singleton). Proves that Cantor sets, profinite groups, and the p-adic integers are totally disconnected. Distinct from Topology/Connected/CardComponents and Topology/Algebra/ClopenNhdofOne.

Name: Separation.lean
Description: Separation properties of connected spaces: a connected Hausdorff space cannot be written as a disjoint union of two clopen sets; the spectrum of a connected C*-algebra is connected. Distinct from Topology/Separation/Basic (Hausdorff axioms) and Topology/Connected/Clopen.

Name: LocPathConnected.lean
Description: Locally path-connected spaces: `LocPathConnectedSpace X` — every point has a path-connected neighbourhood. Proves that in a locally path-connected space, connected components and path components coincide. Distinct from Topology/Connected/PathConnected and Topology/Connected/LocallyConnected.

Name: CardComponents.lean
Description: Cardinality of connected components: `connectedComponents X` as the quotient by the equivalence of lying in the same connected component, and cardinality bounds. Distinct from Topology/Connected/Basic (connectedness) and Topology/Connected/TotallyDisconnected.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Connected topological spaces and connected sets: `IsConnected s` (nonempty and not a disjoint union of two nonempty open subsets), `ConnectedSpace X`, and the continuous image of a connected set is connected. The foundational file for the Connected folder.

Name: LocallyConnected.lean
Description: Locally connected spaces: `LocallyConnectedSpace X` — every point has a connected neighbourhood. Proves that connected components are open in a locally connected space. Distinct from Topology/Connected/LocPathConnected (path version) and Topology/Connected/Basic.

Name: Clopen.lean
Description: Clopen sets and connectedness: `X` is connected iff the only clopen sets are `∅` and `X`. Proves the clopen components of a locally connected space. Distinct from Topology/Connected/Basic and Topology/Algebra/ClopenNhdofOne.

Name: Baire
Description: The Baire category theorem: a complete metric space (or locally compact Hausdorff space) is a Baire space, with applications to nowhere-differentiable functions.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas derived from the Baire category theorem: a nonempty Baire space has no countable partition into nowhere dense closed sets, the uniform boundedness principle in Banach spaces follows from Baire, and open dense sets have dense intersection. Distinct from Topology/Baire/CompleteMetrizable and Topology/Baire/LocallyCompactRegular.

Name: LocallyCompactRegular.lean
Description: Baire category theorem for locally compact regular (in particular Hausdorff) spaces: every countable intersection of dense open sets is dense. Provides `BaireSpace` instance for `LocallyCompactSpace` with `T2Space`. Distinct from Topology/Baire/CompleteMetrizable (metric setting).

Name: BaireMeasurable.lean
Description: Baire-measurable sets and functions: sets in the σ-algebra generated by open sets modulo meager (first category) sets. Proves that Baire-measurable functions have points of continuity on a comeager set. Distinct from Topology/Baire/Lemmas (the Baire category theorem) and MeasureTheory measurability.

Name: CompleteMetrizable.lean
Description: Baire category theorem for completely metrizable spaces: every countable intersection of dense open sets is dense in a completely metrizable space. Proves `BaireCategoryTheorem` for `CompleteSpace`. Distinct from Topology/Baire/LocallyCompactRegular (compact setting) and Topology/Baire/Lemmas.

Name: Algebra
Description: Topological algebraic structures: topological groups, rings, modules, and vector spaces with their continuity axioms.

Name: ProperAction
Description: Proper group actions on topological spaces: an action G ↷ X is proper if the map (g, x) ↦ (gx, x) is a proper map (preimages of compact sets are compact). Proper actions have closed orbits and compact stabilizers. The quotient X/G is Hausdorff when the action is proper. Used in the construction of locally symmetric spaces.

Name: CompactlyGenerated.lean
Description: Proper actions on compactly generated spaces.

Name: ProperlyDiscontinuous.lean
Description: Properly discontinuous group actions.

Name: AddTorsor.lean
Description: Proper action on add torsors.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Proper group actions: `ProperlyDiscontinuous G X`.

Name: RestrictedProduct
Description: Restricted products (adèles): the restricted product ∏'_v K_v = {(xᵥ) | xᵥ ∈ O_v for almost all v} of local fields K_v with respect to compact open subrings O_v. The adèle ring 𝔸_K and the idèle group 𝔸_K× of a number field K. Used in class field theory and automorphic forms.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of restricted product rings.

Name: TopologicalSpace.lean
Description: Topological space structure on restricted products.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Restricted direct product (adeles) topology.

Name: Nonarchimedean
Description: Nonarchimedean topological algebraic structures: topological rings with a neighborhood base of open subgroups (equivalently, the topology is compatible with a nonarchimedean absolute value). p-adic numbers ℚ_p and their rings of integers ℤ_p are the primary examples. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Valued (which uses valuations explicitly) and from the Archimedean case (ℝ, ℂ).

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion of non-archimedean rings.

Name: TotallyDisconnected.lean
Description: Non-archimedean spaces are totally disconnected.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Non-archimedean topological rings and fields.

Name: AdicTopology.lean
Description: Adic topology: topology from an ideal filtration.

Name: Bases.lean
Description: Bases for non-archimedean topologies.

Name: Order
Description: Topological ordered algebraic structures: ordered topological groups (ℝ, ℝ≥0), the interaction between the order topology and the group structure, and the intermediate value theorem for continuous monotone maps. The order topology on ℝ equals the metric topology. Distinct from Topology/Order (topological spaces with an order, without algebra).

Name: Module.lean
Description: Ordered topological modules.

Name: LiminfLimsup.lean
Description: Liminf/limsup in ordered topological groups.

Name: UpperLower.lean
Description: Upper/lower sets in ordered topological spaces.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of functions in ordered topological spaces.

Name: Group.lean
Description: Ordered topological groups: `TopologicalLattice` and related order-topology interactions.

Name: ArchimedeanDiscrete.lean
Description: Discrete archimedean ordered groups.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: Archimedean ordered topological groups.

Name: Floor.lean
Description: Floor function in ordered topological groups: continuity and measurability of floor.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Ordered topological fields.

Name: Ring
Description: Topological rings: rings where addition and multiplication are continuous. The completion of a topological ring (when it is uniform), the I-adic topology on a ring, and the p-adic integers ℤ_p as the I-adic completion of ℤ. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Algebra (which additionally has a scalar field) and from Normed/Ring (which has a norm).

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Closed ideals in topological rings.

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝ as a topological ring.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Topological rings: `TopologicalRing`, continuity of multiplication and subtraction.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Compact topological rings.

Name: ValuativeRel
Description: Topological rings with a valuative relation: the topology defined by a valuation v : R → Γ via the filter of sets {x | v(x) > γ}. The topological ring associated to a valuation ring. Used in non-archimedean geometry. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Valued (which uses the Valued typeclass) — this folder uses the more general relation-based approach.

Name: ValuativeTopology.lean
Description: Valuative relation topology.

Name: Valued
Description: The Valued typeclass: a topological ring R equipped with a valuation v : R → Γ₀ (a linearly ordered group with zero) making R into a non-archimedean topological ring via v. Contains the Valued instance for p-adic integers and the relationship between the valuation topology and the ring topology. Distinct from Valuation/Discrete (DVRs, a special case) and Nonarchimedean (which doesn't name the valuation).

Name: ValuedField.lean
Description: Valued fields.

Name: WithVal.lean
Description: WithVal: type carrying a valuation.

Name: WithZeroMulInt.lean
Description: WithZero and multiplications with integers.

Name: ValuativeRel.lean
Description: Valuative relations in valued topology.

Name: LocallyCompact.lean
Description: Locally compact valued fields.

Name: NormedValued.lean
Description: Normed valued fields.

Name: ValuationTopology.lean
Description: Topology from a valuation.

Name: SeparationQuotient
Description: The separation quotient of a topological algebraic structure: quotienting a topological group or ring by the closure of the identity {e}⁻ to obtain a Hausdorff (T₂) group or ring. Every continuous homomorphism from a topological group to a Hausdorff group factors through the separation quotient. Used to Hausdorff-ify completions.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of separation quotients.

Name: Section.lean
Description: Sections of the separation quotient map.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Separation quotient of a topological space.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional separation quotients.

Name: Star
Description: Topological star algebras: *-algebras where the star operation is continuous. Includes C*-algebras (Analysis/CStarAlgebra) as a special case. Contains the continuity of * and the interaction with the topology. Distinct from Algebra/Star (algebraic star without topology) and CStarAlgebra (with the C*-identity and completeness).

Name: Unitary.lean
Description: Unitary elements in topological star algebras.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Real star topology.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Star linear maps in topological algebras.

Name: Constructions
Description: Standard constructions of topological algebraic structures: products, subgroups, quotients, and completions of topological groups and rings. The product topology makes the product of topological groups a topological group. The quotient by a closed normal subgroup. Distinct from Topology/Constructions (topological constructions without algebra).

Name: DomMulAct.lean
Description: Domain multiplication action topology.

Name: Group
Description: Topological groups: groups where multiplication (a,b) ↦ ab and inversion a ↦ a⁻¹ are continuous. Uniform structure from the group (left and right uniformities), the completion of a topological group, and the open mapping theorem for locally compact groups. The primary folder for Lie group foundations and harmonic analysis setup.

Name: OpenMapping.lean
Description: Open mapping theorem for topological groups.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise topology on group sets.

Name: GroupTopology.lean
Description: Group topology: the finest topology making a group topological.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix groups with topology.

Name: TopologicalAbelianization.lean
Description: Topological abelianisation.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of topological rings.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient topological groups.

Name: SubmonoidClosure.lean
Description: Submonoid closure in topological groups.

Name: ClosedSubgroup.lean
Description: Closed subgroups of topological groups.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Compact topological groups.

Name: AddTorsor.lean
Description: Topological group action on an add torsor.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Topological groups: `TopologicalGroup` axioms, translation homeomorphisms, uniformity.

Name: DiscontinuousSubgroup.lean
Description: Discontinuous subgroups of topological groups.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extension of topological groups.

Name: CompactOpen.lean
Description: Compact-open topology on function groups.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Topological group definitions: `TopologicalGroup`, `TopologicalAddGroup` typeclasses.

Name: Algebra
Description: Topological algebras: algebras over a topological ring where the ring operations and scalar multiplication are continuous. Normed algebras (Analysis/Normed/Algebra) are the complete metrizable case. Contains the typeclass TopologicalAlgebra and basic instances. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Ring (rings without a scalar field) and from Analysis/Normed/Algebra (which requires a norm).

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ-topological algebras.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Topological algebra equivalences.

Name: Category
Description: Categorical constructions for topological algebraic structures: the categories TopGrp (topological groups), TopRing (topological rings), and TopModule, with limits, colimits, and forgetful functors. Used when studying topological groups categorically. Distinct from Algebra/Category (algebraic categories without topology).

Name: ProfiniteGrp
Description: The category ProfiniteGrp of profinite groups: compact Hausdorff totally disconnected topological groups, equivalently inverse limits of finite discrete groups. The forgetful functor to Profinite (forgetting the group structure) and to Grp (forgetting the topology). The absolute Galois group Gal(K̄/K) is the canonical example. Used in class field theory.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in the category of profinite groups.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Profinite completion of a group.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of profinite groups.

Name: InfiniteSum
Description: Infinite sums in topological groups: the sum ∑ f(i) converges if the net of partial sums converges. Summable functions, the Summable typeclass, and the HasSum predicate. Rearrangement independence for absolutely convergent series in Banach spaces. Distinct from Analysis/SpecificLimits (specific convergent series) — this folder provides the general framework.

Name: Nonarchimedean.lean
Description: Infinite sums in non-archimedean spaces.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order aspects of infinite sums.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Infinite sums in topological modules.

Name: NatInt.lean
Description: Infinite sums of ℕ and ℤ-valued series.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Infinite sums in ℝ: convergence criteria and standard examples.

Name: TsumUniformlyOn.lean
Description: Uniform convergence of sums.

Name: UniformOn.lean
Description: Uniformly convergent sums.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Infinite sums in rings: `∑ f * ∑ g = ∑ (f * g)` (Cauchy product).

Name: SummationFilter.lean
Description: Summation via a filter.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of infinite sums.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Infinite sum definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Infinite sums in topological groups: `HasSum`, `Summable`, and `tsum` definitions.

Name: ConditionalInt.lean
Description: Conditionally convergent integer sums.

Name: DiscreteConvolution.lean
Description: Discrete convolution as an infinite sum.

Name: Group.lean
Description: Infinite sums in topological groups.

Name: GroupCompletion.lean
Description: Infinite sums in group completions.

Name: ENNReal.lean
Description: Infinite sums in `ℝ≥0∞`.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Infinite sums in topological fields.

Name: Module
Description: Topological modules: modules over a topological ring where scalar multiplication and addition are continuous. Locally convex topological vector spaces (Analysis/LocallyConvex) are the real/complex case. Contains the typeclass and basic instances for topological modules. Distinct from Normed/Module (which requires a norm).

Name: Spaces
Description: Specific topological module spaces: completions of locally convex spaces, the weak topology σ(E, E') on a locally convex space, and the strong topology on the dual E' = L(E, 𝕜). Used in functional analysis (reflexivity, Mackey topology, nuclear spaces). Distinct from Module (the general topological module typeclass).

Name: UniformConvergenceCLM.lean
Description: Uniform convergence on CLMs.

Name: WeakBilin.lean
Description: Weak bilinear topology.

Name: WeakDual.lean
Description: Weak dual topology.

Name: PointwiseConvergenceCLM.lean
Description: Pointwise convergence topology on CLMs.

Name: CharacterSpace.lean
Description: Character space of a topological algebra.

Name: CompactConvergenceCLM.lean
Description: Compact-convergence topology on CLMs.

Name: ContinuousLinearMap.lean
Description: Space of continuous linear maps.

Name: Multilinear
Description: Continuous multilinear maps on topological modules: the space of continuous k-linear maps E₁ × … × Eₖ → F with the topology of uniform convergence on bounded sets. Used in calculus on locally convex spaces (analysis beyond the Banach setting). Distinct from Normed/Module/Multilinear (which requires norms).

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topology on continuous multilinear maps.

Name: Bounded.lean
Description: Bounded multilinear maps: continuity from separate boundedness.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous multilinear maps: the topology on multilinear maps and norm estimates.

Name: Alternating
Description: Continuous alternating multilinear maps on topological modules: maps E^k → F that are multilinear, continuous, and vanish when two arguments coincide. The topology on this space. Used in the theory of differential forms on smooth manifolds modeled on locally convex topological vector spaces beyond Banach spaces.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topology on continuous alternating maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous alternating maps.

Name: PerfectPairing.lean
Description: Perfect pairings of topological modules.

Name: PerfectSpace.lean
Description: Perfect topological vector spaces.

Name: ModuleTopology.lean
Description: Module topology: the finest topology for which all linear maps are continuous.

Name: LinearPMap.lean
Description: Partial linear maps.

Name: LocallyConvex.lean
Description: Locally convex topological vector spaces.

Name: Simple.lean
Description: Simple topological modules.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring topological module instances.

Name: UniformConvergence.lean
Description: Uniform convergence topology on spaces of linear maps.

Name: TopDualPairing.lean
Description: Topological dual pairing.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star operations on topological modules.

Name: StrongTopology.lean
Description: Strong operator topology on continuous linear maps.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Compact operators and topological modules.

Name: Complement.lean
Description: Topological complements of subspaces.

Name: ClosedSubmodule.lean
Description: Closed submodules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous linear maps in topological modules: `ContinuousLinearMap`, `ContinuousLinearEquiv`, and the strong operator topology.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of topological modules.

Name: Determinant.lean
Description: Determinant in topological modules.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Topological linear maps.

Name: LinearMapPiProd.lean
Description: Pi and product linear maps.

Name: FiniteDimensionBilinear.lean
Description: Bilinear maps in finite dimensions.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Continuous linear equivalences.

Name: FiniteDimension.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional topological modules: unique topology and local compactness.

Name: Monoid
Description: Topological monoids: monoids where multiplication is jointly continuous. The topological monoid of continuous endomorphisms. Instances for ℕ and ℝ≥0 as topological monoids. The coarsest topological algebraic structure — all topological groups are topological monoids. Distinct from Group (which adds continuity of inversion).

Name: FunOnFinite.lean
Description: Functions on finite sets in topological monoids.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Topological monoid definitions.

Name: AddChar.lean
Description: Additive characters of topological monoids.

Name: IsUniformGroup
Description: Uniform groups: topological groups where left and right uniformities coincide (e.g., abelian groups). The IsUniformGroup typeclass. In a uniform group, Cauchy sequences are well-defined and the completion is also a group. All normed spaces and metrizable topological groups are uniform. Distinct from Topology/UniformSpace (the general uniform space theory).

Name: DiscreteSubgroup.lean
Description: Discrete subgroups of uniform groups.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Ordered uniform groups.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Uniform group definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Uniform topological groups.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of uniform groups.

Name: MetricSpace
Description: Metric space structure on topological algebraic objects: when a topological group or ring is metrizable, the interaction between the metric and the algebraic operations. Invariant metrics on groups (d(xa, xb) = d(a,b)), and the equivalence of metrizability with first-countability for topological groups.

Name: Lipschitz.lean
Description: Lipschitz maps in metric spaces.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Topology on univariate polynomial rings `Polynomial R`: evaluation `Polynomial.eval x` is continuous in `x` when `R` is a topological ring, and `aeval` is continuous. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/MvPolynomial (multivariate) and Analysis/Polynomial/Basic.

Name: OpenSubgroup.lean
Description: Open subgroups of topological groups: `OpenSubgroup G` — a subgroup that is also open. Proves open subgroups are also closed (of finite index), and characterises groups in which every open subgroup is closed. Used for profinite group theory. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ClopenNhdofOne.

Name: PontryaginDual.lean
Description: Pontryagin dual of a locally compact abelian group: `PontryaginDual G = ContinuousMonoidHom G circle` with the compact-open topology. Proves `PontryaginDual.instTopologicalGroup` and the evaluation map is continuous. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ContinuousMonoidHom and Fourier/BoundedContinuousFunctionChar.

Name: Semigroup.lean
Description: `TopologicalSemigroup M`: continuity of multiplication `M × M → M` without an identity requirement. Basic instances and the fact that closed subsemigroups exist. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Monoid (with identity) and Topology/Algebra/Constructions.

Name: ProperConstSMul.lean
Description: Proper constant scalar-multiplication actions: conditions under which the map `x ↦ c • x` is a proper map (preimages of compact sets are compact). Used in quotient constructions for vector spaces. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ConstMulAction (general constant actions) and Topology/Algebra/MulAction.

Name: MulAction.lean
Description: Continuous group actions: `ContinuousSMul M α` — the scalar multiplication `M × α → α` is jointly continuous. Proves orbits are continuous images, the action topology, and that proper actions have closed orbits. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ConstMulAction and Topology/Algebra/ProperConstSMul.

Name: Monoid.lean
Description: `TopologicalMonoid M`: continuity of the multiplication map `M × M → M`. Proves that limits of nets in a topological monoid respect multiplication, sum of convergent series, and instances for products and submonoids. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Semigroup (without identity) and Topology/Algebra/Constructions.

Name: MvPolynomial.lean
Description: Topology on multivariate polynomial rings `MvPolynomial σ R`: the product topology from the coefficient ring makes `MvPolynomial σ R` a topological ring, and evaluation maps are continuous. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Polynomial (univariate) and Topology/Algebra/Algebra.

Name: NonUnitalStarAlgebra.lean
Description: Non-unital topological *-algebras: the combined typeclass of `TopologicalNonUnitalAlgebra` and `ContinuousStar`, providing the algebra of compact operators as the main example. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/NonUnitalAlgebra (without star) and Topology/Algebra/Star.

Name: NonUnitalAlgebra.lean
Description: Non-unital topological algebras: `TopologicalNonUnitalAlgebra 𝕜 A` — a non-unital topological ring with continuous scalar multiplication. Provides instances for closed subalgebras of unital topological algebras. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Algebra (unital) and CStarAlgebra/Classes.

Name: UniformFilterBasis.lean
Description: Topological groups with uniform structure from a filter basis: `UniformGroupFilterBasis G` — conditions for a filter basis to generate a compatible uniform structure on a group. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/FilterBasis (topological but not uniform) and Topology/UniformSpace/Basic.

Name: UniformField.lean
Description: Uniform structure on topological fields: `UniformField 𝕜` — the completion of a uniform field is a field, and inversion is uniformly continuous on bounded-away-from-zero sets. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Field (topological, not uniform) and Topology/UniformSpace/Completion.

Name: UniformMulAction.lean
Description: Uniformly continuous group actions: `UniformContinuousConstSMul M α` — the map `c • ·` is uniformly continuous for each fixed `c`. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/MulAction (continuous but not necessarily uniform) and Topology/Algebra/UniformRing.

Name: WithZeroTopology.lean
Description: Topology on `WithZero α`: the topology making `WithZero (αˣ) ≃ α` (for a topological group `α`) a topological monoid with zero. Used for non-archimedean valued fields. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/GroupWithZero and Order/WithTop topology.

Name: UniformRing.lean
Description: Uniform topological rings and their completions: `UniformAddGroup R` with a compatible ring structure, the completion `Completion R` inherits the ring structure. Proves `UniformSpace.Completion.instRing`. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/UniformField and Topology/Algebra/GroupCompletion.

Name: StarSubalgebra.lean
Description: Closed *-subalgebras in topological *-algebras: `StarSubalgebra.topologicalClosure` is again a *-subalgebra, and the closure of a *-subalgebra is the smallest closed *-subalgebra containing it. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Star and CStarAlgebra/Hom.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Continuous star operations: `ContinuousStar α` — the involution `star : α → α` is continuous. Proves that `ContinuousStar` passes to products, subspaces, and completions. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/NonUnitalStarAlgebra (with algebra structure) and CStarAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of functions in the topological algebraic setting: `HasCompactSupport f` (closure of `{x | f x ≠ 0}` is compact), operations preserving compact support (sum, product, convolution), and the `tsupport` construction. Distinct from Distribution/Support and Topology/ContinuousMap/CompactlySupported.

Name: UniformConvergence.lean
Description: Uniform convergence of nets/sequences of maps in topological algebraic structures: `UniformConvergence.instTopologicalRing` (a ring of uniformly convergent functions is a topological ring), and the uniform limit of continuous homomorphisms is a homomorphism. Distinct from Topology/UniformSpace/UniformConvergence.

Name: TopologicallyNilpotent.lean
Description: Topologically nilpotent elements: `IsTopologicallyNilpotent a` meaning `a^n → 0` in a topological ring. Proves the set of topologically nilpotent elements is an ideal, and characterises topological nilpotency in terms of the spectral radius in Banach algebras. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Monoid and RingTheory/Nilpotent.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Topology on localisations of topological rings: the universal property of `Localization S A` in the topological category, and continuity of the localisation map. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Field (field as a localization) and RingTheory/Localization files.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Product, subspace, and quotient topologies for topological algebraic structures: `TopologicalGroup` instances for `α × β`, subgroups, and quotient groups, plus `Pi` instances. The standard constructions file for the Topology/Algebra folder.

Name: ConstMulAction.lean
Description: Proper and cocompact constant-multiplication actions: `ProperConstSMul 𝕜 X` (the map `x ↦ c • x` is proper for each `c ≠ 0`) and consequences for quotient topology. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/MulAction (general continuous actions) and Topology/Algebra/ProperConstSMul.

Name: ContinuousAffineEquiv.lean
Description: Continuous affine equivalences `X ≃ᵃ[k] Y`: homeomorphisms that are simultaneously affine isomorphisms. Defines `ContinuousAffineEquiv`, proves the group structure under composition, and converts between affine and linear equivalences. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ContinuousAffineMap (not necessarily invertible).

Name: ContinuousMonoidHom.lean
Description: Continuous monoid homomorphisms `ContinuousMonoidHom M N`: topology on the space of continuous homomorphisms, pointwise product, and the Pontryagin dual as a special case. Proves `ContinuousMonoidHom.compContinuous`. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/PontryaginDual and Topology/Algebra/Monoid.

Name: ContinuousAffineMap.lean
Description: The type `X →ᵃ[k] Y` of continuous affine maps: definition, the linear part `ContinuousAffineMap.linear`, composition, and the `ContinuousLinearMap`-to-`ContinuousAffineMap` coercion. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ContinuousAffineEquiv (invertible) and Topology/Algebra/Affine (continuity facts).

Name: AffineSubspace.lean
Description: Topological properties of affine subspaces: an affine subspace is a closed set in a Hausdorff topological vector space, the topology on an affine subspace induced from the ambient space, and the closure of an affine subspace is affine. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Affine (continuous affine maps) and LinearAlgebra/AffineSpace files.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: Topological properties of affine spaces and affine maps: continuity of the weighted combination map `(t, x, y) ↦ (1-t) • x + t • y`, and the fact that affine subspaces are closed when the underlying module is a Hausdorff topological vector space. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/ContinuousAffineMap (the type of continuous affine maps) and Topology/Algebra/AffineSubspace.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: `TopologicalAlgebra 𝕜 A`: a topological ring `A` that is also a `𝕜`-algebra with continuous scalar multiplication. Proves that continuous algebra homomorphisms are closed under composition, and provides the product and subspace topology instances. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Constructions (general topological algebraic constructions).

Name: ClopenNhdofOne.lean
Description: Clopen (simultaneously closed and open) neighbourhoods of the identity in a topological group: existence in totally disconnected compact groups, and the fact that such neighbourhoods form a neighbourhood basis. Used in the structure theory of profinite groups. Distinct from Topology/Connected/TotallyDisconnected and Topology/Separation/Profinite.

Name: AsymptoticCone.lean
Description: Asymptotic cone of a subset `s` of a topological vector space: the set of limit directions of sequences in `s` rescaled to infinity. Used in convex analysis and optimisation to characterise recession directions. Distinct from Analysis/Calculus/TangentCone (tangent cone at a point) and Analysis/Convex/Basic.

Name: IntermediateField.lean
Description: Topological properties of intermediate fields in a field extension: an intermediate field of a topological field extension is itself a topological field with the induced topology, and the closure of an intermediate field. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Field and FieldTheory/IntermediateField files.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator functions in the topological-algebra setting: continuity and measurability of `Set.indicator s f`, and the topology of pointwise convergence for indicator-based approximations. Distinct from MeasureTheory/Indicator files and Topology/ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: IsOpenUnits.lean
Description: The group of units `αˣ` is open in a topological ring `α` when the topology makes inversion continuous: `IsOpenUnits`. Proves `isOpen_setOf_isUnit` under appropriate conditions. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/GroupWithZero (zero included) and Topology/ContinuousMap/Units.

Name: LinearTopology.lean
Description: Linear topologies on rings: topologies where the neighbourhood filter at 0 has a basis of (two-sided) ideals or open submodules. Used in the theory of adic completions and profinite rings. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/FilterBasis and Topology/Algebra/UniformRing.

Name: LinearMapCompletion.lean
Description: Extension of continuous linear maps to uniform completions: a continuous linear map `f : E →L[𝕜] F` with `F` complete extends uniquely to `Completion E →L[𝕜] F`. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/GroupCompletion (additive groups) and Topology/UniformSpace/AbstractCompletion.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Topological fields: `TopologicalField 𝕜` — a field whose ring operations and inversion `x ↦ x⁻¹` (on `𝕜ˣ`) are continuous. Proves `ContinuousOn.inv` and that completion of a topological field is a topological field. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/GroupWithZero and Topology/Algebra/UniformField.

Name: Equicontinuity.lean
Description: Equicontinuity for families of maps between topological groups: `EquicontinuousAt` specialised to group homomorphisms, and the Arzelà–Ascoli theorem in the topological group setting. Distinct from Topology/MetricSpace/Equicontinuity (metric version) and Topology/UniformSpace/Equicontinuity.

Name: FilterBasis.lean
Description: Topological groups defined by a filter basis at the identity: `GroupFilterBasis G` — a collection of subsets of `G` satisfying the axioms to generate a group topology. Proves that the resulting topology is a topological group topology. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/UniformFilterBasis (uniform version) and Topology/Algebra/Monoid.

Name: GroupWithZero.lean
Description: Topological groups with zero: `TopologicalGroupWithZero G₀` — continuity of multiplication and the map `x ↦ x⁻¹` on `G₀ \ {0}`. Provides instances for normed fields and division rings. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/Field (which requires a field) and Topology/Algebra/IsOpenUnits.

Name: GroupCompletion.lean
Description: Uniform completion of a topological abelian group: `UniformCompletion G` inherits the group structure, and the completion map is a dense embedding of topological groups. Proves `UniformSpace.Completion.instAddCommGroup`. Distinct from Topology/Algebra/LinearMapCompletion and Topology/UniformSpace/Completion.

Name: Category
Description: Categorical aspects of topology: the category Top of topological spaces, functors from Top to algebraic categories, and compactly generated spaces.

Name: Profinite
Description: The category Profinite of profinite spaces: compact Hausdorff totally disconnected spaces, equivalently inverse limits of finite discrete spaces. Dual to the category of Boolean rings (Stone duality). The absolute Galois group of a field is profinite. Used as the site for condensed mathematics. Distinct from CompHaus (profinite = CompHaus + totally disconnected).

Name: Nobeling
Description: Nöbeling's theorem for profinite spaces: the ℤ-module of locally constant integer-valued functions LocallyConstant S ℤ is a free ℤ-module for every profinite set S. Constructs an explicit basis via a closed embedding Nobeling.isClosedEmbedding and a combinatorial argument on clopen covers. The main result is LocallyConstant.freeOfProfinite. Used in the cohomology of profinite groups and in condensed mathematics.

Name: Successor.lean
Description: Successor step in Nöbeling's theorem.

Name: ZeroLimit.lean
Description: Zero/limit step in Nöbeling's theorem.

Name: Span.lean
Description: Span condition in Nöbeling's theorem.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Nöbeling's theorem for profinite spaces.

Name: Induction.lean
Description: Inductive step in Nöbeling's theorem.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in Profinite.

Name: Extend.lean
