Description: Extension along profinite maps.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projective objects in Profinite.

Name: Product.lean
Description: Products in Profinite.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Profinite spaces: compact Hausdorff totally disconnected.

Name: AsLimit.lean
Description: Profinite space as a cofiltered limit of finite sets.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epis in Profinite.

Name: CofilteredLimit.lean
Description: Cofiltered limits of profinite spaces.

Name: Stonean
Description: Stonean spaces (extremally disconnected compact Hausdorff spaces): compact Hausdorff spaces where the closure of every open set is open. Gleason's theorem: Stonean spaces are the projective objects in CompHaus. The Stone space of a complete Boolean algebra is Stonean. Used in the construction of the injective hull in CompHaus. Distinct from Profinite (profinite spaces need not be Stonean).

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epis in Stonean.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in Stonean.

Name: Adjunctions.lean
Description: Adjunctions involving Stonean spaces.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Stonean spaces: extremally disconnected compact Hausdorff spaces.

Name: TopCat
Description: The category TopCat of topological spaces: the primary category of topology in Mathlib. Limits (products with product topology, equalizers as subspaces), colimits (coproducts as disjoint unions, coequalizers as quotients), adjunctions (discrete and indiscrete functors), and the forgetful functor to Type. Distinct from the more specialized subcategories CompHaus, Profinite, Stonean.

Name: Limits
Description: Explicit limits and colimits in TopCat: products (with the product topology, Tychonoff), equalizers (subspace of the product), pullbacks (fiber products), coproducts (disjoint unions), coequalizers (quotient spaces), and pushouts. The concrete formulas distinguishing TopCat limits from the abstract categorical limits. Used to verify TopCat is bicomplete.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in TopCat.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Pullbacks in TopCat.

Name: Konig.lean
Description: König's lemma via TopCat limits.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Limits in TopCat.

Name: Cofiltered.lean
Description: Cofiltered limits in TopCat.

Name: Opens.lean
Description: Opens of a topological space as a category.

Name: OpenNhds.lean
Description: Open neighbourhood functor.

Name: Sphere.lean
Description: Spheres in TopCat.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: Yoneda lemma for TopCat.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: Universe lifting for TopCat.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on TopCat.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: TopCat: category of topological spaces.

Name: Adjunctions.lean
Description: Adjunctions involving TopCat.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epis in TopCat.

Name: GrothendieckTopology.lean
Description: Grothendieck topology from TopCat.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epi/mono in TopCat.

Name: CompHaus
Description: The category CompHaus of compact Hausdorff spaces: the full subcategory of Top on compact T₂ spaces, with continuous maps as morphisms. Stone duality: CompHaus is dual to the category of commutative unital C*-algebras (Gelfand-Naimark). The free compact Hausdorff space on a set (Stone-Čech compactification). Distinct from Profinite (a full subcategory of CompHaus).

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in CompHaus.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projective objects in CompHaus.

Name: Frm.lean
Description: Frame of opens in CompHaus.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: CompHaus: category of compact Hausdorff spaces.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epimorphisms in CompHaus.

Name: CompHausLike
Description: A typeclass generalizing CompHaus: a CompHausLike structure captures properties shared by CompHaus, Profinite, LightProfinite, and Stonean spaces, enabling results to be proved uniformly. Used to avoid code duplication across the related categories of compact spaces.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in CompHausLike.

Name: SigmaComparison.lean
Description: Sigma comparison in CompHausLike.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epis in CompHausLike.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: CompHausLike: categories similar to CompHaus.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian structure on CompHausLike.

Name: LightProfinite
Description: Light profinite spaces: inverse limits of finite sets indexed by ℕ (countable inverse systems). A subcategory of Profinite that is more concrete and constructive. Light condensed sets (Condensed/Light) use light profinite spaces as the site. Distinct from Profinite (arbitrary inverse systems, not just countable ones).

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Injective objects in light profinite category.

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extension along light profinite maps.

Name: Sequence.lean
Description: Sequential limits in light profinite sets.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in the light profinite category.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Light profinite sets: countable limits of finite sets.

Name: AsLimit.lean
Description: Light profinite as a limit.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epis in light profinite sets.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian structure on light profinite sets.

Name: Sequential.lean
Description: The category of sequential spaces: `SequentialSpace` objects and continuous maps, the reflection functor from `Top`, and the adjunction. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/CountablyCompact (countable compactness) and Topology/Category/CompactlyGenerated.

Name: Locale.lean
Description: The category of locales (pointless topology): `Locale` — a complete Heyting algebra. The functor from topological spaces to locales (sending a space to its frame of open sets) and the adjunction with the spectrum functor. Distinct from Topology/Spectral (spectral spaces) and AlgebraicGeometry.

Name: TopCommRingCat.lean
Description: The category `TopCommRingCat` of topological commutative rings: objects, morphisms (continuous ring homomorphisms), limits, colimits, and the forgetful functor to `CommRingCat`. Distinct from Topology/Category/UniformSpace and RingTheory categorical files.

Name: UniformSpace.lean
Description: The category `UniformSpaceCat` of uniform spaces and uniformly continuous maps: the forgetful functor to `Top`, limits and colimits, and the completion functor as a left adjoint. Distinct from Topology/UniformSpace/Basic (uniform space theory) and Topology/Category/Born.

Name: TopPair.lean
Description: The category of topological pairs `(X, A)` with `A ⊆ X` closed: objects, morphisms (continuous maps of pairs), and the long exact sequence in algebraic topology context. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/Basic and AlgebraicTopology/RelativeHomology files.

Name: CompactlyGenerated.lean
Description: The category of compactly generated spaces: `CompactlyGeneratedSpace` (Kelley spaces), the reflection from `Top` to `CGTop`, and the fact that CW-complexes are compactly generated. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/CompactlyGeneratedSpace and Topology/Category/DeltaGenerated.

Name: Born.lean
Description: The category `Born` of bornological spaces and bounded maps: objects are `Bornology`, morphisms are `BoundedMap`, limits and colimits. Distinct from Topology/Bornology/Hom (the morphisms) and Topology/Category/CompactlyGenerated.

Name: Compactum.lean
Description: Compacta (compact Hausdorff spaces) as algebras for the ultrafilter monad: `Compactum` — the equivalence between compact Hausdorff spaces and algebras of the ultrafilter monad. Distinct from Topology/Category/CompactlyGenerated and Topology/Compactification/StoneCech.

Name: FinTopCat.lean
Description: The category `FinTopCat` of finite topological spaces: objects are `Fintype` types with a topology, morphisms are continuous maps, and the equivalence with finite preorders via the specialisation order. Distinct from Topology/Category/Locale and Topology/Separation/AlexandrovDiscrete.

Name: DeltaGenerated.lean
Description: The category of Δ-generated spaces (spaces generated by the singular simplices): objects, morphisms, the reflection functor, and the fact that Δ-generated spaces are compactly generated. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/DeltaGeneratedSpace (the typeclass) and Topology/Category/CompactlyGenerated.

Name: Bornology
Description: Bornological spaces: structures specifying which sets are bounded, generalizing the bounded sets of metric spaces.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Bornology on product, subtype, and quotient spaces: `Bornology.instProd`, `Bornology.induced`, and `Bornology.cofinite`. Also contains the bornology on `Fin n → α`. Distinct from Bornology/Basic (base definitions) and Bornology/Hom.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Bounded maps between bornological spaces: `LocallyBoundedMap f` (preimage of bounded sets is bounded) and `BoundedMap f` (image of bounded sets is bounded). Provides composition and the category of bornological spaces. Distinct from Bornology/Constructions and Topology/Category/Born.

Name: Real.lean
Description: The bornology on ℝ and subsets of ℝ: the standard bornology on ℝ coincides with the metric bornology, `IsBounded s ↔ BoundedOrder s`, and intervals are bounded. Distinct from Bornology/Basic (abstract bornology) and Topology/Instances/Discrete.

Name: Absorbs.lean
Description: Absorption: `Absorbs 𝕜 s t` — the set `s` absorbs `t` meaning `∃ r > 0, ∀ c, r ≤ ‖c‖ → t ⊆ c • s`. Proves basic properties of absorption used in bornology and locally convex space theory. Distinct from Bornology/Basic (bounded sets) and Analysis/LocallyConvex/Bounded.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `Bornology α`: a bornology on `α` is a filter of 'bounded' sets closed under subsets and finite unions, with every singleton bounded. Defines `IsBounded s`, the bornology from a metric, and the indiscrete bornology. The foundational file for the Bornology folder.

Name: BoundedOperation.lean
Description: Boundedness of algebraic operations: `BoundedAdd`, `BoundedMul`, etc. — conditions under which addition or multiplication of two bounded sets is bounded. Used to make algebraic structures into `BoundedSpace`. Distinct from Bornology/Basic (bornology definition) and Bornology/Constructions.

Name: EMetricSpace
Description: Extended metric spaces (emetric spaces): metric spaces where the distance may take the value +inf, used in measure theory.

Name: PairReduction.lean
Description: Reduction of properties in extended metric spaces to pairwise conditions: a set is bounded iff pairwise distances are bounded, and related simplifications. Distinct from EMetricSpace/Diam (diameter) and EMetricSpace/Basic.

Name: Lipschitz.lean
Description: Lipschitz maps in the extended metric setting: `LipschitzWith K f` for `K : ℝ≥0` and `f : α → β` extended-metric spaces. Proves composition, restriction, and that Lipschitz maps are uniformly continuous. Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz (ordinary metric) and EMetricSpace/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Extended metric on finite products: `EMetric.instPseudoEMetricSpacePi` — the sup-extended-metric on `Π i, α i`. Distinct from MetricSpace (product metric) and EMetricSpace/Basic.

Name: Paracompact.lean
Description: Paracompactness of extended metric spaces: every extended metric space is paracompact, generalising the metric space result. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/Paracompact (abstract paracompactness) and MetricSpace/Basic.

Name: BoundedVariation.lean
Description: Functions of bounded variation in extended metric spaces: `EDist.BoundedVariationOn f s` using `ℝ≥0∞`-valued variation, and the total variation as an extended real number. Distinct from MetricSpace/Lipschitz (Lipschitz condition) and Analysis/BoundedVariation files.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Extended metric spaces (EMetricSpace): the topology generated by balls `EMetric.ball x r` with `r : ℝ≥0∞`, and the equivalence with `PseudoEMetricSpace` plus the T0 axiom. Core API: `EMetric.mem_ball`, `EMetric.isOpen_ball`. Distinct from EMetricSpace/Defs and MetricSpace/Basic.

Name: Diam.lean
Description: Diameter in extended metric spaces: `EMetric.diam s = ⨆ (x y ∈ s), edist x y ∈ ℝ≥0∞`. Proves subadditivity, `diam (closure s) = diam s`, and characterisation of bounded sets. Distinct from MetricSpace/Bounded (finite-diameter sets) and EMetricSpace/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `EMetricSpace α`: a pseudo-emetric space (`edist : α → α → ℝ≥0∞` satisfying triangle inequality) plus separation. Contains `EDist`, `PseudoEMetricSpace`, and the typeclass hierarchy down to `MetricSpace`. Distinct from EMetricSpace/Basic (topology from edist).

Name: Defs
Description: Core definitions of topology: the TopologicalSpace typeclass, open sets, neighborhoods, and the basic axioms.

Name: Sequences.lean
Description: Sequential characterisations of topological notions: `SeqClosure` (closure via sequences), `IsSeqClosed`, `IsSeqCompact`, and their relationship to the filter-based notions in first-countable/metrizable spaces. Distinct from Topology/Compactness/CountablyCompact and Topology/MetricSpace/Sequences.

Name: Ultrafilter.lean
Description: Ultrafilter-based characterisations: compactness via ultrafilter convergence (`IsCompact.ultrafilter_le_nhds`), and the ultrafilter monad perspective on compact Hausdorff spaces. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Filter (neighbourhood filters) and Topology/Category/Compactum.

Name: Induced.lean
Description: Induced and coinduced topologies: `TopologicalSpace.induced f τ` (coarsest topology making `f` continuous) and `TopologicalSpace.coinduced f τ` (finest topology making `f` continuous). Used for subspace and quotient topologies. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Basic and Topology/Homeomorph/Defs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions of topology: `TopologicalSpace α`, open sets, `IsOpen`, `IsClosed`, `nhds` (neighbourhood filter), `closure`, `interior`, `frontier`. The Lean 4 definitions that everything else in the Topology folder depends on. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Filter (filter-based characterisations).

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Filter-based characterisations of topological concepts: `nhds x` as the neighbourhood filter, `tendsto`, and the filter definition of continuity `ContinuousAt`. Bridges the filter library with the topological space definitions. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Basic (set-theoretic definitions).

Name: GDelta
Description: G-delta sets (countable intersections of open sets) and F-sigma sets (countable unions of closed sets), with their topological properties.

Name: MetrizableSpace.lean
Description: Gδ sets in metrizable spaces: in a Polish (completely metrizable separable) space, every Borel set is a continuous image of a Gδ set, and Gδ subspaces of Polish spaces are Polish. Distinct from Topology/GDelta/Basic and Topology/MetricSpace/Polish.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Gδ sets (countable intersections of open sets): `IsGδ s`, closure under finite unions and countable intersections, and the fact that closed sets in a metric space are Gδ. Used in the Baire category theorem and descriptive set theory. Distinct from GDelta/MetrizableSpace and Topology/Baire files.

Name: FiberBundle
Description: Fiber bundles: locally trivial fibrations with a structure group, generalizing vector bundles and covering spaces.

Name: IsHomeomorphicTrivialBundle.lean
Description: Criterion for a fiber bundle to be homeomorphically trivial: `IsHomeomorphicTrivialBundle E F` — the total space is homeomorphic to `B × F` over `B`. Distinct from FiberBundle/Trivialization (local trivialisations) and FiberBundle/Basic.

Name: Trivialization.lean
Description: Trivialisations of fiber bundles: `Trivialization F π` — a homeomorphism from `π⁻¹ U` to `U × F` over an open set `U`. Proves the coherence conditions and how trivialisations determine the bundle topology. Distinct from FiberBundle/Basic (the bundle structure) and FiberBundle/Constructions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fiber bundles: `FiberBundle F E` — a surjection `π : E → B` with structure group, local trivialisations homeomorphic to `U × F`, and the bundle topology. Proves the local trivialisation gives `E` its topology. Distinct from FiberBundle/Trivialization (trivialization details) and VectorBundle/Basic.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions on fiber bundles: pullback bundle `f*E` along a continuous map `f : B' → B`, product bundles `E₁ ×_B E₂`, and trivial bundles. Distinct from FiberBundle/Basic (single bundle) and VectorBundle/Constructions.

Name: Convenient
Description: Convenient (Frolicher) spaces: a cartesian-closed setting for infinite-dimensional smooth calculus beyond Banach spaces.

Name: HomSpace.lean
Description: Function spaces in convenient calculus: the space of smooth maps between convenient vector spaces with the topology of uniform convergence on bounded sets, and the exponential law `C∞(U, C∞(V, W)) ≅ C∞(U × V, W)`. Distinct from Topology/Convenient/Category and ContinuousMap/Compact.

Name: OpenClosed.lean
Description: Open and closed sets in the convenient calculus topology: characterisations of open/closed in terms of smooth curves and the bornological topology. Distinct from Topology/Convenient/GeneratedBy and Topology/Bornology/Basic.

Name: GeneratedBy.lean
Description: The topology on a set generated by a family of maps: `TopologicalSpace.generateFrom` applied to preimages, and the universal property. Used in the convenient calculus framework. Distinct from Topology/Defs/Induced and Topology/Convenient/ContinuousMapGeneratedBy.

Name: Category.lean
Description: Category of convenient vector spaces (Frölicher–Kriegl convenient calculus): the category with smooth maps as morphisms, Cartesian closedness. Distinct from Topology/Convenient/HomSpace and Analysis/LocallyConvex.

Name: ContinuousMapGeneratedBy.lean
Description: Topologies generated by maps to convenient spaces: the initial topology making a family of maps continuous, characterised in the convenient calculus framework. Distinct from Topology/Convenient/GeneratedBy (general generated topologies) and Topology/Hom/ContinuousEval.

Name: ContinuousMap
Description: The space of continuous maps C(X, Y) with the compact-open topology, and its algebraic structure when Y is a ring or group.

Name: Bounded
Description: Bounded continuous functions C_b(X, E): the Banach space of continuous bounded functions from X to a normed space E, with the sup norm ‖f‖ = sup_x ‖f(x)‖. The Stone-Weierstrass theorem for bounded continuous functions. Distinct from the parent ContinuousMap folder (which covers C(X,Y) with the compact-open topology, not necessarily bounded) and from Lp spaces (where functions need not be continuous).

Name: Normed.lean
Description: Normed space structure on bounded continuous functions.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star structure on bounded continuous functions.

Name: ArzelaAscoli.lean
Description: Arzelà–Ascoli theorem for bounded continuous functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bounded continuous functions API.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomial functions as continuous maps: the embedding `Polynomial.toContinuousMapOn` of polynomials into `C([a,b], ℝ)`, and the Weierstrass approximation as density of this image. Distinct from ContinuousMap/StoneWeierstrass (general density) and Topology/Algebra/Polynomial.

Name: SecondCountableSpace.lean
Description: Second-countability of `C(X, Y)`: when `X` is compact metrizable and `Y` is second countable, the space `C(X, Y)` is second countable. Used in measure-theoretic arguments about function spaces. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Compact and Topology/Metrizable/Basic.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Continuous maps from a sigma type (disjoint union): `ContinuousMap.Sigma` — maps `(⊕ᵢ Xᵢ) → Y` correspond to families of continuous maps `Xᵢ → Y`. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Basic (general) and Topology/Constructions/SumProd.

Name: LocallyConvex.lean
Description: Locally convex topology on `C(X, E)` for locally convex `E`: the compact-open topology makes `C(X, E)` locally convex, and the evaluation seminorms generate this topology. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Compact and Analysis/LocallyConvex/WithSeminorms.

Name: Ordered.lean
Description: Order properties of continuous maps: `ContinuousMap.instPreorder`, monotone continuous maps, and the fact that the pointwise order on `C(X, β)` is compatible with the compact-open topology. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Lattice and Topology/Order/Monotone.

Name: Periodic.lean
Description: Periodic continuous functions: `Function.Periodic f p` — `f(x + p) = f(x)`, and `C(ℝ/pℤ, ℝ)` as a space of periodic functions. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Basic (general maps) and Analysis/Complex/Periodic.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star operations on continuous map spaces: `ContinuousMap.instStar` for `C(X, A)` when `A` has a continuous star involution, making `C(X, A)` a *-algebra when `A` is one. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Algebra (ring structure) and Topology/Algebra/Star.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in the ring `C(X, R)`: `ContinuousMap.unitsLift` — continuous maps into the units `Rˣ` correspond to units of `C(X, R)`. Proves `ContinuousMap.isUnit_iff`. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Algebra (ring structure) and Topology/Algebra/IsOpenUnits.

Name: Weierstrass.lean
Description: Weierstrass approximation theorem: polynomials are dense in `C([a,b], ℝ)`. Proved via Bernstein polynomials or as a corollary of Stone–Weierstrass. Distinct from ContinuousMap/StoneWeierstrass (general version) and ContinuousMap/Polynomial (embedding).

Name: ZeroAtInfty.lean
Description: Continuous functions vanishing at infinity `C₀(X, A)` (`ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap X A`): functions for which `‖f x‖ → 0` as `x` leaves every compact set. Proves `ZeroAtInftyContinuousMap.instNormedAddCommGroup` and the C*-algebra structure. Distinct from ContinuousMap/CompactlySupported and ContinuousMap/ContinuousMapZero.

Name: StarOrdered.lean
Description: Order on the self-adjoint elements of `C(X, A)` for a star-ordered `A`: `ContinuousMap.instStarOrderedRing`, positivity in `C(X, ℝ)` means pointwise nonneg. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Star (star structure) and ContinuousMap/Lattice.

Name: StoneWeierstrass.lean
Description: Stone–Weierstrass theorem: a subalgebra (or sublattice) of `C(X, ℝ)` that separates points and contains constants is dense. Proves `ContinuousMap.subalgebra_topologicalClosure_eq_top_of_separatesPoints`. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Polynomial (polynomial case) and ContinuousMap/Weierstrass.

Name: T0Sierpinski.lean
Description: Continuous maps to the Sierpiński space: maps `X → Sierpinski` correspond to open sets of `X`. The Sierpiński space as the classifying space for open sets in the category of T₀ topological spaces. Distinct from Topology/Separation/Basic (T0 axiom) and Topology/Category/Locale.

Name: CocompactMap.lean
Description: Cocompact maps: `CocompactMap f` — preimages of compact sets are compact (equivalently, maps between locally compact spaces that extend continuously to one-point compactifications). Distinct from ContinuousMap/Compact (maps into compact spaces) and Topology/Compactness/LocallyCompact.

Name: Compact.lean
Description: Continuous maps on compact spaces: `C(X, Y)` with the compact-open topology is a complete metric space when `X` is compact and `Y` is a complete metric space. Proves the Arzelà–Ascoli theorem for `C(X, Y)`. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Basic and Topology/UniformSpace/Ascoli.

Name: CompactlySupported.lean
Description: Compactly supported continuous functions `ContinuousMap.HasCompactSupport`: functions `f : X → A` where `closure {x | f x ≠ 0}` is compact. Operations (sum, product) preserving compact support, and density in `C₀(X)`. Distinct from ContinuousMap/BoundedCompactlySupported and Topology/Algebra/Support.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebraic structure on `C(X, A)`: when `A` is a topological ring/group/module, `C(X, A)` inherits the corresponding structure pointwise. Proves `ContinuousMap.instRing`, `ContinuousMap.instModule`, and continuity of the ring operations in the compact-open topology. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Basic (definition) and CStarAlgebra/ContinuousMap.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for `ContinuousMap X Y` (`X →C Y`): composition, the identity, constant maps, and the compact-open topology `ContinuousMap.compactOpen`. Proves `ContinuousMap.continuous_comp`, `Continuous.comp`, and the evaluation map is continuous on compact sets. The main API file for continuous maps.

Name: BoundedCompactlySupported.lean
Description: Bounded compactly supported continuous functions `BoundedCompactlySupported X A`: functions that are both bounded and have compact support. Distinct from ContinuousMap/CompactlySupported (support only) and ContinuousMap/ZeroAtInfty (decay at infinity).

Name: ContinuousMapZero.lean
Description: Continuous maps vanishing at a basepoint: `ContinuousMapZero X Y` — maps sending a chosen basepoint `⋆ : X` to `0 : Y`. The `C₀`-version of continuous maps for pointed spaces, used in non-unital C*-algebra theory. Distinct from ContinuousMap/ZeroAtInfty (vanishing at infinity).

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Continuous maps on intervals: `C([a, b], ℝ)` as a complete metric space, the intermediate value theorem for continuous maps, and specific interval-map constructions. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Compact (general compact domain) and Topology/Order/MonotoneContinuity.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on `C(X, β)` when `β` is a topological lattice: `ContinuousMap.instLattice` (pointwise sup and inf), the sublattice property, and the Stone–Weierstrass hypothesis that a sublattice separating points is dense. Distinct from ContinuousMap/StoneWeierstrass and ContinuousMap/Algebra.

Name: LocallyConstant.lean
Description: Locally constant functions as continuous maps: the inclusion `LocallyConstant X A → C(X, A)` (locally constant implies continuous), and characterisation of locally constant maps on connected spaces. Distinct from Topology/LocallyConstant/Basic and ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: ContinuousSqrt.lean
Description: Continuous square root of continuous functions: when `f : X → ℝ≥0` is continuous and everywhere nonneg, the function `√f` is continuous. Instances for `C(X, ℝ≥0)` and connections to C*-algebra functional calculus. Distinct from ContinuousMap/Basic and SpecialFunctions/Sqrt.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `ContinuousMap X Y` as a bundled continuous function, `FunLike` instance, and the `Continuous` predicate. Contains the type definition, coercion, and the basic `ext` lemma. The definitional core split off from ContinuousMap/Basic.

Name: Ideals.lean
Description: Ideals in `C(X, 𝕜)` for compact Hausdorff `X`: the Gelfand–Kolmogorov theorem — maximal ideals of `C(X, ℝ)` correspond to points of `X` via evaluation. Distinct from ContinuousMap/StoneWeierstrass (density) and CStarAlgebra/GelfandDuality.

Name: CWComplex
Description: CW complexes: topological spaces built by attaching cells of increasing dimension, the standard model for homotopy theory.

Name: Classical
Description: Classical CW complexes in the sense of Whitehead: a Hausdorff space X with a filtration by skeleta X⁰ ⊆ X¹ ⊆ … where Xⁿ is obtained from Xⁿ⁻¹ by attaching n-cells via maps Sⁿ⁻¹ → Xⁿ⁻¹. The closure finiteness and weak topology axioms (C and W in CW). Distinct from Abstract (categorical formulation).

Name: Graph.lean
Description: Graphs as CW complexes.

Name: Subcomplex.lean
Description: Subcomplexes of CW complexes.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Classical CW complexes.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite CW complexes.

Name: Abstract
Description: Abstract CW complexes: the categorical/combinatorial definition of a CW complex as a filtered topological space with attaching maps from spheres. The abstract framework for proving homotopy invariance results without reference to specific embeddings. Distinct from Classical (the concrete Euclidean definition).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Abstract CW complexes.

Name: Covering
Description: Covering spaces and the monodromy action: lifting theorems, deck transformations, and the Galois correspondence with the fundamental group.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient covering spaces: when a group `G` acts freely and properly discontinuously on `E`, the quotient map `E → E/G` is a covering. Provides `QuotientCoveringMap` and the deck transformation group. Distinct from Topology/Covering/Basic and Topology/Algebra/MulAction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Covering maps and covering spaces: `IsCoveringMap p` — `p : E → X` is surjective with each point of `X` having an evenly covered neighbourhood. Proves unique path lifting, the homotopy lifting property, and that covering maps are local homeomorphisms. Distinct from Topology/Covering/Quotient and FiberBundle/Basic.

Name: AddCircle.lean
Description: The covering map `ℝ → ℝ/ℤ` (or `ℝ → S¹`): the quotient projection is a covering map, its fibres are cosets of ℤ, and the deck transformations are integer translations. Distinct from Topology/Covering/Basic (abstract covering spaces) and Analysis/Complex/CoveringMap.

Name: OpenPartialHomeomorph.lean
Description: Open partial homeomorphisms: partially defined maps that are homeomorphisms on their domain, used in manifold charts.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Topology on ordered types: the order topology, upper and lower semicontinuity, and topology of the extended reals.

Name: NoetherianSpace.lean
Description: Noetherian topological spaces: spaces where every open set is compact, equivalently every ascending chain of opens stabilizes.

Name: OmegaCompletePartialOrder.lean
Description: Topology on omega-cpos: the Scott topology and its interaction with the order structure.

Name: Path.lean
Description: Paths in topological spaces: continuous maps from [0,1] to X, path concatenation, and path components.

Name: Perfect.lean
Description: Perfect sets: closed sets with no isolated points, with the Cantor-Bendixson theorem.

Name: Partial.lean
Description: Partial continuous maps: maps defined on an open or closed subset with continuity on that subset.

Name: PartitionOfUnity.lean
Description: Partitions of unity: families of continuous functions summing to 1, used to patch local constructions together.

Name: LocallyFinsupp.lean
Description: Locally finitely supported functions: functions vanishing outside a locally finite set, used in distribution theory.

Name: NatEmbedding.lean
Description: Embeddings of N into topological spaces: topological properties of sequences viewed as maps from the naturals.

Name: LocallyClosed.lean
Description: Locally closed sets: intersections of an open and a closed set, with the constructible topology.

Name: LocallyFinite.lean
Description: Locally finite families: collections of sets where each point has a neighborhood meeting only finitely many.

Name: NhdsSet.lean
Description: The neighborhood filter of a set: the infimum of neighborhood filters at points in the set.

Name: NhdsWithin.lean
Description: Neighborhoods within a set: the filter of neighborhoods of x restricted to S, used for one-sided limits.

Name: Neighborhoods.lean
Description: Neighborhood filters and basic neighborhood properties: the fundamental objects of point-set topology.

Name: NhdsKer.lean
Description: The neighborhood kernel: the largest open set contained in a neighborhood filter.

Name: Piecewise.lean
Description: Piecewise-defined continuous maps: gluing two continuous maps along a clopen partition.

Name: Subpath.lean
Description: Subpaths: restrictions of paths to subintervals, with reparametrization.

Name: TietzeExtension.lean
Description: Tietze extension theorem: a continuous real-valued function on a closed subset of a normal space extends to the whole space.

Name: Sober.lean
Description: Sober spaces: spaces where every irreducible closed set has a unique generic point, the topological model of Spec(R).

Name: Specialization.lean
Description: Specialization preorder: x specializes to y if y is in the closure of {x}, used in the Zariski topology.

Name: UrysohnsBounded.lean
Description: Bounded Urysohn functions: continuous functions separating a closed set from a point with controlled range.

Name: UrysohnsLemma.lean
Description: Urysohn's lemma: in a normal space, any two disjoint closed sets can be separated by a continuous function.

Name: Ultrafilter.lean
Description: Ultrafilters as points of the Stone-Cech compactification: the ultrafilter monad and convergence.

Name: UnitInterval.lean
Description: The unit interval [0,1]: its topology as a subspace of R, convexity, and use in path homotopy.

Name: Semicontinuous.lean
Description: Semicontinuous functions: upper and lower semicontinuous functions, with the Baire class hierarchy.

Name: SeparatedMap.lean
Description: Separated maps: morphisms where the diagonal is closed, the relative version of the Hausdorff property.

Name: PreorderRestrict.lean
Description: Preorder restriction topology: the topology induced on a subtype by restricting a preorder topology.

Name: QuasiSeparated.lean
Description: Quasi-separated spaces: spaces where the intersection of two quasi-compact opens is quasi-compact.

Name: Sion.lean
Description: Sion's minimax theorem: the min-max and max-min of a convex-concave function on compact spaces coincide.

Name: SmallInductiveDimension.lean
Description: Small inductive dimension: the topological dimension defined inductively via separating boundaries.

Name: Sequences.lean
Description: Sequential topology: sequences, sequential continuity, and conditions under which sequences determine the topology.

Name: ShrinkingLemma.lean
Description: The shrinking lemma: every locally finite open cover of a normal space has a shrinking.

Name: LocalAtTarget.lean
Description: Local properties at the target: a morphism has a property locally at each point of the codomain.

Name: Constructible.lean
Description: Constructible sets: finite Boolean combinations of locally closed sets, used in algebraic geometry.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard topological constructions: product, subspace, quotient, and disjoint union topologies.

Name: Coherent.lean
Description: Coherent topological spaces: spaces where every open set is a finite union of quasi-compact opens.

Name: CompactOpen.lean
Description: The compact-open topology on C(X, Y): the standard function space topology used in algebraic topology.

Name: Covering.lean
Description: Covering maps and the lifting property: the unique path lifting theorem and deck transformations.

Name: DenseEmbedding.lean
Description: Dense embeddings: embeddings whose image is dense, with the extension theorem to completions.

Name: Continuous.lean
Description: Continuity: the fundamental definition, continuity at a point, and the lattice of continuous functions.

Name: ContinuousOn.lean
Description: Continuity on a set: ContinuousOn f s and its relationship to continuity and the subspace topology.

Name: Bases.lean
Description: Bases and subbases for topologies: generating topologies from collections of open sets.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The core of topology in Lean: TopologicalSpace definition, open and closed sets, and basic topological facts.

Name: AlexandrovDiscrete.lean
Description: Alexandrov-discrete spaces: topological spaces where arbitrary intersections of open sets are open.

Name: ApproximateUnit.lean
Description: Approximate units in topological algebras: nets or sequences acting as approximate identity for multiplication.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Closure, interior, and boundary of sets: the closure operator, interior, and their duality.

Name: ClusterPt.lean
Description: Cluster points (accumulation points): points in the closure of a punctured neighborhood, used for limits.

Name: Clopen.lean
Description: Clopen sets: sets that are both open and closed, with disconnectedness and Stone-Cech compactification.

Name: ClopenBox.lean
Description: Clopen boxes in products of Stone spaces: basic clopen sets in the product topology on profinite spaces.

Name: DerivedSet.lean
Description: Derived sets: the set of accumulation points, used in the Cantor-Bendixson analysis.

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Irreducible topological spaces: spaces that cannot be written as a union of two proper closed subsets.

Name: IsClosedRestrict.lean
Description: IsClosed on restrictions: conditions for a set to be closed relative to a subspace.

Name: IndicatorConstPointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise limits of indicator functions: approximation results for indicator functions in topological spaces.

Name: Inseparable.lean
Description: Topologically indistinguishable points: points with the same neighborhood filters, used in the T0 separation axiom.

Name: KrullDimension.lean
Description: Krull dimension of topological spaces: the supremum of lengths of chains of irreducible closed sets.

Name: List.lean
Description: Topological properties of lists: continuity of list operations with respect to the product topology.

Name: IsLocalHomeomorph.lean
Description: Local homeomorphisms: maps that are homeomorphisms on a neighborhood of every point.

Name: JacobsonSpace.lean
Description: Jacobson spaces: topological spaces where every closed set is the closure of its set of closed points.

Name: ExtendFrom.lean
Description: Extending continuous functions: conditions under which a function on a dense subset extends continuously.

Name: ExtremallyDisconnected.lean
Description: Extremally disconnected spaces: spaces where the closure of every open set is open (Stonean spaces).

Name: DiscreteQuotient.lean
Description: Discrete quotients: quotients of topological spaces by open equivalence relations, used in profinite completions.

Name: DiscreteSubset.lean
Description: Discrete subsets: subsets whose subspace topology is discrete, related to locally finite sets.

Name: Germ.lean
Description: Germs of functions at a point: equivalence classes of functions agreeing on some neighborhood.

Name: Gluing.lean
Description: Gluing topological spaces: the pushout of two spaces along a common subspace.

Name: FiberPartition.lean
Description: Fiber partitions: partitioning a space into fibers of a continuous map.

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Filters and topology: the neighborhood filter, convergence, cluster points, and filter-based limits.

Name: RepresentationTheory
Description: Studies how groups and algebras act on vector spaces, including characters, irreducible modules, and induced representations. Used to linearize symmetry problems and classify group structures via linear algebra. Relied on by group theory, number theory (automorphic representations), physics (quantum mechanics), and combinatorics.

Name: Rep
Description: Representations of groups as linear actions on vector spaces, packaged as module objects in the category of representations.

Name: Res.lean
Description: Restriction of representations: `Rep.res H G ρ` — restricting a `G`-representation to a subgroup `H ≤ G`. The restriction-induction adjunction. Distinct from Rep/Basic.

Name: Iso.lean
Description: Isomorphisms in `Rep k G`: characterisation of isomorphisms as equivariant bijections, and standard isomorphisms (associator, unitor for the monoidal structure). Distinct from Rep/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The category `Rep k G` of `k`-linear representations of a group `G`: objects are `k[G]`-modules, morphisms are equivariant `k`-linear maps. The monoidal structure `⊗` and the forgetful functor to `k`-modules. Distinct from Rep/Iso and AlgebraRepresentation/Basic.

Name: Homological
Description: Homological algebra in representation theory: projective and injective resolutions, group cohomology, and Ext groups for modules.

Name: GroupHomology
Description: Group homology H_n(G, M): the derived functors of the coinvariants functor M ↦ M_G = M/IM where I is the augmentation ideal, computed via the bar resolution. H₁(G, ℤ) ≅ G^{ab} (abelianization). The Lyndon-Hochschild-Serre spectral sequence for group extensions. Distinct from GroupCohomology (dual via contravariant functor).

Name: LongExactSequence.lean
Description: Long exact sequence in group homology.

Name: LowDegree.lean
Description: Low-degree group homology: H₀, H₁, H₂.

Name: Shapiro.lean
Description: Shapiro's lemma for homology.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Group homology: `H_n(G, M)`.

Name: FiniteCyclic.lean
Description: Group homology of finite cyclic groups.

Name: Functoriality.lean
Description: Functoriality of group homology.

Name: GroupCohomology
Description: Group cohomology Hⁿ(G, M): the derived functors of the fixed-point functor M ↦ M^G, computed via the bar resolution. H¹(G, M) classifies crossed homomorphisms (mod coboundaries), which in the context of Galois representations classifies extensions. H²(G, M) classifies group extensions with abelian kernel M. Distinct from GroupHomology (dual construction).

Name: LongExactSequence.lean
Description: Long exact sequence in group cohomology.

Name: LowDegree.lean
Description: Low-degree group cohomology: H⁰, H¹, H².

Name: Shapiro.lean
Description: Shapiro's lemma: `H^n(G, CoInd M) ≅ H^n(H, M)`.

Name: Hilbert90.lean
Description: Hilbert's theorem 90: `H¹(G, Kˣ) = 0`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Group cohomology: `H^n(G, M)`.

Name: FiniteCyclic.lean
Description: Group cohomology of finite cyclic groups.

Name: Functoriality.lean
Description: Functoriality of group cohomology.

Name: Resolution.lean
Description: Free resolutions for group cohomology: the bar resolution `B_*(G) → ℤ → 0` and the normalised bar complex for computing `H^n(G, M) = Ext^n_{ℤ[G]}(ℤ, M)`. Distinct from Homological/FiniteCyclic.

Name: FiniteCyclic.lean
Description: Group cohomology of finite cyclic groups: `H^n(ℤ/mℤ, M)` computed via the norm and difference maps on the periodic resolution. Tate cohomology for finite cyclic groups. Distinct from Homological/Resolution.

Name: AlgebraRepresentation
Description: Representations of algebras: left modules over a k-algebra as the primary objects, with morphisms being module maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Algebra representations: `AlgebraRepresentation k G A V` — a `k`-algebra homomorphism from the group algebra `k[G]` to `End_k(V)`, equivalently a `k[G]`-module structure on `V`. Distinct from Rep/Basic and GroupAction/Ring.

Name: Invariants.lean
Description: Invariants of a representation: the subspace V^G of G-fixed vectors, with the Reynolds operator averaging over G.

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Irreducible representations: representations with no proper nonzero invariant subspaces, the building blocks.

Name: Induced.lean
Description: Induced representations Ind_H^G(W): extending a representation of a subgroup H to the full group G.

Name: Intertwining.lean
Description: Intertwining operators: G-equivariant linear maps between representations, the morphisms in the representation category.

Name: Maschke.lean
Description: Maschke's theorem: the group algebra k[G] is semisimple when the group order is invertible in k, so every representation is completely reducible.

Name: Subrepresentation.lean
Description: Subrepresentations: G-invariant subspaces and their lattice structure.

Name: Tannaka.lean
Description: Tannaka duality: recovering a group from its category of representations via fiber functors.

Name: Semisimple.lean
Description: Semisimple representations: direct sums of irreducible representations, and the Wedderburn decomposition of the group algebra.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Submodules in representation theory: the submodule structure of a representation viewed as a group algebra module.

Name: Character.lean
Description: Characters of representations: the trace function chi_V(g) = Tr(rho(g)), with orthogonality relations.

Name: Coinduced.lean
Description: Coinduced representations CoInd_H^G(W): the right adjoint to restriction, dual to the induced representation.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Group actions as representations: the correspondence between G-sets and permutation representations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic representation theory: definitions of representations as group homomorphisms to GL(V) and the module structure.

Name: Coinvariants.lean
Description: Coinvariants of a representation: the quotient V_G = V / span{gv - v}, the largest quotient on which G acts trivially.

Name: FinGroupCharZero.lean
Description: Representation theory of finite groups in characteristic zero: character theory, class functions, and the character table.

Name: FiniteIndex.lean
Description: Representations of groups with finite-index subgroups: restriction-induction results for finite-index subgroups.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences of representations: isomorphisms in the representation category and their properties.

Name: FDRep.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional representations (FDRep): the monoidal category of fd representations with the forgetful functor.

Name: Probability
Description: Builds on measure theory to define random variables, expectation, independence, and stochastic processes. Used to model randomness, quantify risk, and prove limit theorems like the law of large numbers. Relied on by statistics, finance, machine learning, physics, and operations research.

Name: Moments
Description: Moments and moment generating functions of random variables, Markov's inequality, and characteristic functions.

Name: MGFAnalytic.lean
Description: Analyticity of the moment generating function: when the MGF converges in a strip, it is analytic and its derivatives give moments. Distinct from Moments/Basic.

Name: IntegrableExpMul.lean
Description: Integrability of `exp(tX)` for bounded `X`: conditions under which the MGF is finite on an interval. Distinct from Moments/Basic and Moments/SubGaussian.

Name: SubGaussian.lean
Description: Sub-Gaussian random variables: `ProbabilityTheory.SubGaussian X c` — `E[e^{tX}] ≤ e^{t²c²/2}`. Concentration inequalities (Hoeffding). Distinct from Moments/Basic.

Name: Variance.lean
Description: Variance: `ProbabilityTheory.variance X μ = E[X²] - E[X]²`. Variance of sums, Chebyshev's inequality, and the law of large numbers setup. Distinct from Moments/Covariance.

Name: Tilted.lean
Description: Tilted moments: moments under exponentially tilted measures `μ_t(A) = e^{tX}/E[e^{tX}] * μ(A)`, and the cumulant generating function. Distinct from Moments/Basic and MeasureTheory/Measure/Tilted.

Name: ComplexMGF.lean
Description: Complex moment generating function: `E[e^{itX}]` — the characteristic function of `X`, and its analyticity properties. Distinct from Moments/Basic and MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Moments and moment generating function: `ProbabilityTheory.mgf X μ t = E[e^{tX}]` and `ProbabilityTheory.cgf X μ t = log mgf`. Basic properties and the Markov/Chebyshev inequalities. Distinct from Moments/Variance.

Name: Covariance.lean
Description: Covariance of random variables: `ProbabilityTheory.covariance X Y μ = E[XY] - E[X]E[Y]`. Cauchy–Schwarz for covariance and the covariance matrix. Distinct from Moments/Variance.

Name: CovarianceBilinDual.lean
Description: Dual of the covariance bilinear form: the Riesz representation of the covariance functional. Distinct from Moments/CovarianceBilin.

Name: CovarianceBilin.lean
Description: Covariance as a bilinear form: the covariance `Cov(X,Y)` is a positive semidefinite bilinear form on `L²(μ)`. Distinct from Moments/Covariance.

Name: Martingale
Description: Martingales and stopping times: optional sampling, Doob's inequalities, upcrossing lemma, and the martingale convergence theorem.

Name: OptionalSampling.lean
Description: Optional sampling theorem (Doob): for a martingale `f` and bounded stopping times `τ ≤ σ`, `E[f σ | ℱ τ] = f τ`. Distinct from Process/Stopping and Martingale/Basic.

Name: OptionalStopping.lean
Description: Doob's optional stopping theorem: for a martingale (Mₙ) and a bounded stopping time τ, the expected value E[M_τ] equals E[M₀]. Conditions include: τ bounded, the martingale uniformly integrable, or the stopped process bounded. Formalized as MeasureTheory.Martingale.expected_stoppedValue_mono. The mathematical theorem that no betting strategy (stopping rule) can turn a fair game into a guaranteed profit.

Name: Upcrossing.lean
Description: Doob's upcrossing inequality: bounds on the expected number of upcrossings, used to prove a.e. convergence. Distinct from Martingale/Convergence.

Name: Convergence.lean
Description: Martingale convergence theorem: an L¹-bounded submartingale converges a.e. and in L¹. Distinct from Martingale/Basic and Martingale/Upcrossing.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Martingales: `ProbabilityTheory.Martingale f ℱ μ` — `E[f n | ℱ m] = f m` for `m ≤ n`. Submartingales (`≥`) and supermartingales (`≤`). Doob's optional sampling theorem setup. Distinct from Martingale/Convergence.

Name: BorelCantelli.lean
Description: Martingale proof of Borel–Cantelli: the second Borel–Cantelli lemma proved using the martingale convergence theorem. Distinct from MeasureTheory/OuterMeasure/BorelCantelli.

Name: Centering.lean
Description: Doob decomposition: every adapted integrable process decomposes as `f n = martingale n + predictable n`. The centering lemma. Distinct from Martingale/Basic.

Name: Process
Description: Stochastic processes: filtrations, adapted processes, and the general framework for random processes indexed by time.

Name: PartitionFiltration.lean
Description: Filtrations from partitions: the filtration generated by a sequence of refining partitions. Used in discrete-time stochastic processes. Distinct from Process/Filtration.

Name: LocalProperty.lean
Description: Local properties of stochastic processes: properties that can be checked pathwise (almost surely). Distinct from Process/Adapted.

Name: Stopping.lean
Description: Stopping times: `ProbabilityTheory.IsStoppingTime ℱ τ` — `{τ ≤ n} ∈ ℱ n` for all `n`. The stopped σ-algebra `ℱ τ` and stopped process. Distinct from Process/HittingTime.

Name: Predictable.lean
Description: Predictable processes: `ProbabilityTheory.Predictable ℱ f` — `f (n+1)` is `ℱ n`-measurable (measurable one step ahead). Distinct from Process/Adapted.

Name: Kolmogorov.lean
Description: Kolmogorov continuity theorem: for a stochastic process satisfying `E[|X_t - X_s|^p] ≤ C|t-s|^{1+α}`, there exists a continuous modification. Distinct from Process/FiniteDimensionalLaws.

Name: Filtration.lean
Description: Filtrations: `MeasureTheory.Filtration ι m μ` — an increasing family of sub-σ-algebras `ℱ n ≤ ℱ (n+1)`. Natural filtration of a process and the conditional expectation `E[f | ℱ n]`. Distinct from Process/Adapted.

Name: Adapted.lean
Description: Adapted processes: `ProbabilityTheory.Adapted ℱ f` — `f n` is measurable with respect to `ℱ n`. Distinct from Process/Filtration and Process/Predictable.

Name: HittingTime.lean
Description: Hitting times: `ProbabilityTheory.hittingTime f s t` — the first time `f n ∈ s` for `n ≤ t`. Measurability of hitting times and their relationship to stopping times. Distinct from Process/Stopping.

Name: FiniteDimensionalLaws.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional laws of a stochastic process: the consistent family of measures `μ_{t₁,…,tₙ}` on finite-dimensional marginals. Kolmogorov's extension theorem setup. Distinct from Process/Filtration.

Name: ProbabilityMassFunction
Description: Probability mass functions on discrete types: the PMF type, support, expectation, and connections to the counting measure.

Name: Integrals.lean
Description: Integration against a PMF: `∫ x, f x ∂p.toMeasure = ∑ x, p x * f x`. Distinct from ProbabilityMassFunction/Basic.

Name: Monad.lean
Description: Monad structure on PMF: `PMF.bind p f` (monadic bind), `PMF.pure` (Dirac), and the Giry monad instance. Distinct from ProbabilityMassFunction/Basic.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard PMF constructions: uniform PMF on a `Fintype`, Bernoulli, and normalisation. Distinct from ProbabilityMassFunction/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Probability mass functions: `PMF α` — a function `α → ℝ≥0∞` with `∑ p = 1`. Coercion to measures and the `Monad` structure. Distinct from ProbabilityMassFunction/Constructions.

Name: Binomial.lean
Description: Binomial PMF: `PMF.binomial n p` — `k ↦ C(n,k) pᵏ (1-p)^{n-k}`. Distinct from Distributions/Binomial (which is measure-theoretic) and ProbabilityMassFunction/Basic.

Name: Kernel
Description: Probability kernels (Markov kernels): measurable maps from a space to probability measures, used in conditional distributions and Markov chains.

Name: Disintegration
Description: Disintegration of measures: given a measure μ on X × Y, a regular conditional distribution is a kernel κ : X → M(Y) such that μ = ∫ κ(x, ·) dμ_X(x). Existence requires Polish spaces (the Disintegration theorem). Used in Bayesian statistics (posterior distributions). Distinct from Kernel/IonescuTulcea (constructing measures from transition kernels).

Name: MeasurableStieltjes.lean
Description: Measurable Stieltjes functions for CDF.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integral of disintegration.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: Uniqueness of disintegration.

Name: StandardBorel.lean
Description: Disintegration on standard Borel spaces.

Name: CDFToKernel.lean
Description: CDF to kernel conversion.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Disintegration theorem: `μ = ∫ κ_y dμ_Y(y)`.

Name: Density.lean
Description: Density of disintegrated measures.

Name: CondCDF.lean
Description: Conditional CDF from disintegration.

Name: IonescuTulcea
Description: The Ionescu-Tulcea theorem: given a sequence of transition kernels κₙ : X₀ × … × Xₙ → M(Xₙ₊₁), there exists a unique probability measure on the product space X₀ × X₁ × … that makes (Xₙ) a Markov chain with the given transitions. The foundational existence theorem for stochastic processes. Distinct from Kernel/Disintegration (decomposing existing measures).

Name: Traj.lean
Description: Full trajectory measures via Ionescu–Tulcea.

Name: PartialTraj.lean
Description: Partial trajectories in the Ionescu–Tulcea theorem.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Maps in the Ionescu–Tulcea construction.

Name: Category
Description: The category of probability kernels (the Kleisli category of the Giry monad): objects are measurable spaces, morphisms are Markov kernels κ : X → M(Y). Composition is convolution of kernels. This is the categorical semantics of probabilistic programming. Distinct from Kernel/Composition (individual kernel operations).

Name: Stoch.lean
Description: Stochastic category: Markov kernels.

Name: SFinKer.lean
Description: Category of σ-finite kernels.

Name: Composition
Description: Composition and combination of probability kernels: sequential composition κ ∘ η (Markov chain transition), parallel composition κ ⊗ η, and the integral ∫ κ(·|x) dμ(x). Used to build complex stochastic systems from simpler kernels. Distinct from Kernel/Category (which organizes these into a categorical structure).

Name: MeasureComp.lean
Description: Measure composition via kernels.

Name: MapComap.lean
Description: Map and comap for kernels.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: General kernel composition lemmas.

Name: MeasureCompProd.lean
Description: Measure composition product.

Name: RadonNikodym.lean
Description: Radon–Nikodym for kernel compositions.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product of kernels.

Name: ParallelComp.lean
Description: Parallel composition of kernels.

Name: CompMap.lean
Description: Composition-map of kernels.

Name: Comp.lean
Description: Composition of kernels.

Name: AbsolutelyContinuous.lean
Description: Absolutely continuous kernels.

Name: CompNotation.lean
Description: Notation for kernel composition.

Name: KernelLemmas.lean
Description: Kernel composition lemmas.

Name: IntegralCompProd.lean
Description: Integral of the composition product.

Name: CompProd.lean
Description: Composition-product of kernels.

Name: Posterior.lean
Description: Posterior distribution via Bayes' theorem: for a prior kernel and likelihood kernel, the posterior kernel `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel.posterior`. Distinct from Kernel/CondDistrib.

Name: Proper.lean
Description: Proper kernels: kernels for which the disintegration theorem holds. Distinct from Kernel/Basic.

Name: MeasurableIntegral.lean
Description: Measurability of kernel integrals: `∫ x, f x ∂(κ a)` is measurable in `a`. Distinct from Kernel/Integral and Kernel/MeasurableLIntegral.

Name: MeasurableLIntegral.lean
Description: Measurability of lower kernel integrals: `∫⁻ x, f x ∂(κ a)` is measurable in `a`. Distinct from Kernel/MeasurableIntegral.

Name: SetIntegral.lean
Description: Set integrals with respect to kernels: `∫ x in s, f x ∂(κ a)` and the kernel version of `restrict`. Distinct from Kernel/Integral.

Name: WithDensity.lean
Description: Kernels with density: `κ.withDensity f` — the kernel `a ↦ (κ a).withDensity (f a)`. Distinct from Kernel/Basic and MeasureTheory/Measure/WithDensity.

Name: RadonNikodym.lean
Description: Radon–Nikodym theorem for kernels: a dominated kernel has a density kernel. Distinct from Kernel/Basic and MeasureTheory/Measure/AbsolutelyContinuous.

Name: Representation.lean
Description: Representation of measures via kernels: every finite measure on a product space disintegrates as a kernel. Distinct from Kernel/CondDistrib.

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Irreducible kernels: `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel.Irreducible κ` — every state is accessible from every other. Used in the ergodic theorem for Markov chains. Distinct from Kernel/Invariance.

Name: CondDistrib.lean
Description: Conditional distribution kernel: `ProbabilityTheory.condDistrib X Y μ` — the regular conditional distribution of `X` given `Y`. Distinct from Kernel/Condexp and Probability/Conditional.

Name: Condexp.lean
Description: Conditional expectation via kernels: expressing `E[f | m]` using a probability kernel. The disintegration theorem for kernels. Distinct from Kernel/CondDistrib and MeasureTheory/Function/ConditionalLExpectation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Probability kernels API: `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel α β` — measurable maps `α → Measure β`. Composition `κ ∘ η`, the Markov kernel category, and `ProbabilityTheory.compProd`. Distinct from Kernel/Defs.

Name: CompProdEqIff.lean
Description: Conditions for equality of kernel compositions: characterising when `κ ∘ η = ξ` in terms of disintegration. Distinct from Kernel/Basic.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integration with respect to a kernel: `∫ x, f x ∂(κ a)` and the Fubini theorem for kernel integrals. Distinct from Kernel/MeasurableIntegral and Kernel/SetIntegral.

Name: Invariance.lean
Description: Invariant measures for kernels: `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel.Invariant κ μ` — `μ = κ.bind μ`. The ergodic theory framework for kernels. Distinct from Kernel/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of probability kernels: `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel α β = {f : α → Measure β // Measurable f}`. The `Markov` (probability kernel) and `IsFiniteKernel` typeclasses. Distinct from Kernel/Basic (API).

Name: Deterministic.lean
Description: Deterministic kernels: `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel.deterministic f` — the kernel `a ↦ δ_{f(a)}` (Dirac mass at `f(a)`) for a measurable map `f`. Distinct from Kernel/Basic.

Name: Decision
Description: Decision theory: measurable decision rules, loss functions, and Bayes-optimal decisions under a prior distribution.

Name: Risk
Description: Statistical decision theory and risk: the risk function R(θ, δ) = E_θ[L(θ, δ(X))] of a decision rule δ under loss function L. Admissibility (no uniformly better rule exists), Bayes risk, and the complete class theorem. Used in mathematical statistics as the foundation for optimal estimation.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Risk measure definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Risk measures in decision theory.

Name: Combinatorics
Description: Probabilistic combinatorics: the probabilistic method, random graphs, and the Lovász local lemma.

Name: BinomialRandomGraph
Description: The Erdős-Rényi random graph G(n, p): a graph on n vertices where each edge is included independently with probability p. Threshold phenomena: properties that appear suddenly as p crosses a critical threshold. The expected number of triangles and other subgraphs. Connects combinatorics/SimpleGraph with probability theory.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Binomial random graph G(n,p) definitions.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation overview for the Probability/Combinatorics folder: describes the planned content on probabilistic combinatorics (random graphs, probabilistic method). This is a markdown documentation file.

Name: Independence
Description: Independence of events, sigma-algebras, and random variables, with equivalent characterizations via product measures.

Name: Process
Description: Independence of stochastic processes: a process (Xₜ)_{t≥0} has independent increments if Xₜ₄ - Xₜ₃ and Xₜ₂ - Xₜ₁ are independent when [t₁,t₂] ∩ [t₃,t₄] = ∅. Brownian motion as a process with independent Gaussian increments. Distinct from Probability/Process (general stochastic processes without independence conditions).

Name: HasIndepIncrements
Description: The HasIndepIncrements typeclass: a stochastic process (Xₜ) has independent increments if Xₜ₄ - Xₜ₃ and Xₜ₂ - Xₜ₁ are independent whenever the intervals [t₁,t₂] and [t₃,t₄] are disjoint. Brownian motion has independent Gaussian increments; the Poisson process has independent Poisson increments. Used to define Lévy processes.

Name: IsGaussianProcess.lean
Description: Gaussian processes with independent increments.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Independent increments basics.

Name: HasIndepIncrements.lean
Description: Processes with independent increments.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Independence for stochastic processes.

Name: Kernel
Description: Independence with respect to probability kernels: the kernel-based formulation of independence, where conditional independence P(A ∩ B | C) = P(A|C)P(B|C) is expressed using the kernel abstraction. Used in the Markov property and in Bayesian networks. Distinct from Probability/Independence (unconditional independence).

Name: IndepFun.lean
Description: Independence of kernel functions.

Name: Indep.lean
Description: Independence for probability kernels.

Name: Integration.lean
Description: Integration and independence: `∫ f * g dμ = ∫ f dμ * ∫ g dμ` for independent integrable `f, g`. The variance formula `Var(f+g) = Var(f) + Var(g)`. Distinct from Independence/Integrable.

Name: Integrable.lean
Description: Integrability for independent random variables: independent integrable functions have integrable product, and `E[fg] = E[f]E[g]`. Distinct from Independence/Integration.

Name: ZeroOne.lean
Description: Kolmogorov's zero-one law: a tail event (measurable with respect to `⋂_n σ(Xₙ, Xₙ₊₁, …)`) for a sequence of independent random variables has probability 0 or 1. Distinct from Independence/Basic.

Name: Kernel.lean
Description: Independence for probability kernels: `ProbabilityTheory.Kernel.Indep κ₁ κ₂ η` — independence of kernels in a Markov kernel context. Distinct from Independence/Basic and Kernel/Basic.

Name: InfinitePi.lean
Description: Independence for infinite product measures: the coordinate projections on an infinite product are mutually independent. Distinct from Independence/Basic and MeasureTheory/Constructions/Projective.

Name: BoundedContinuousFunction.lean
Description: Independence tested against bounded continuous functions: `f` and `g` are independent iff `∫ φ(f) ψ(g) dμ = ∫ φ(f) dμ * ∫ ψ(g) dμ` for all bounded continuous `φ, ψ`. Distinct from Independence/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Independence of σ-algebras and random variables: `ProbabilityTheory.IndepSets`, `iIndep`, `Indep` for pairs and families. `IndepFun f g μ` iff `μ ∘ (f, g)⁻¹ = μ_f × μ_g`. Distinct from Independence/Conditional.

Name: Conditional.lean
Description: Conditional independence: `ProbabilityTheory.CondIndep m₁ m₂ m μ` — `m₁` and `m₂` are conditionally independent given `m`. Distinct from Independence/Basic and Kernel/Condexp.

Name: CharacteristicFunction.lean
Description: Independence via characteristic functions: random variables are independent iff the joint characteristic function factors. Distinct from Independence/Basic and MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction.

Name: Distributions
Description: Specific probability distributions: Bernoulli, Gaussian, Poisson, and their properties (density, moments, characteristic functions).

Name: Poisson
Description: The Poisson distribution Pois(λ): the distribution of rare events, with P(X=k) = e^{-λ}λᵏ/k!. Poisson limit theorem (Binomial(n, λ/n) → Pois(λ)). The sum of independent Poissons is Poisson. The Poisson process as the continuous-time counting process. Distinct from Distributions/Gaussian (continuous distribution).

Name: PoissonLimitThm.lean
Description: Poisson limit theorem: Binomial(n, λ/n) → Poisson(λ).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Poisson distribution: `poisson λ k = e^{-λ} λ^k / k!`.

Name: Gaussian
Description: The Gaussian (normal) distribution N(μ, σ²): the density (2πσ²)^{-1/2} e^{-(x-μ)²/(2σ²)}, its moment generating function, the sum of independent Gaussians is Gaussian, and the central limit theorem (normed sums converge to N(0,1)). The multivariate Gaussian on ℝⁿ. Distinct from Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Gaussian (the integral ∫e^{-x²}dx = √π).

Name: IsGaussianProcess
Description: The IsGaussianProcess typeclass: a stochastic process (Xₜ) is Gaussian if every finite marginal (Xₜ₁, ..., Xₜₙ) has a multivariate Gaussian distribution. Brownian motion (Wiener process) as the canonical example. The Gaussian process is characterized by its mean function m(t) = E[Xₜ] and covariance kernel k(s,t) = Cov(Xₛ, Xₜ). Distinct from HasGaussianLaw (a single variable, not a process).

Name: Independence.lean
Description: Independence for Gaussian processes.

Name: Def.lean
Description: IsGaussianProcess definition.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: IsGaussianProcess predicate.

Name: HasGaussianLaw
Description: The HasGaussianLaw typeclass: asserts that a given random variable X has a Gaussian (normal) distribution with specified mean μ and variance σ². Contains the moment generating function, the closure under linear transformations (aX + b is Gaussian), and the characterization via the Fourier transform. Distinct from IsGaussianProcess (for entire stochastic processes, not individual random variables).

Name: Independence.lean
Description: Independence for Gaussian laws.

Name: Def.lean
Description: HasGaussianLaw definition.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: HasGaussianLaw predicate.

Name: Multivariate.lean
Description: Multivariate Gaussian distributions.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Real-valued Gaussian distributions.

Name: Fernique.lean
Description: Fernique's theorem for Gaussian measures.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Gaussian (normal) distribution basics.

Name: CharFun.lean
Description: Characteristic function of the Gaussian.

Name: Pareto.lean
Description: Pareto distribution: the measure on `[x_m, ∞)` with density `αx_m^α / x^{α+1}` — a power-law distribution. Distinct from Distributions/Exponential.

Name: Geometric.lean
Description: Geometric distribution: `ProbabilityTheory.geometricPMF p k = (1-p)^k p` — the number of failures before the first success. Memoryless property. Distinct from Distributions/Binomial.

Name: SetBernoulli.lean
Description: Bernoulli distribution on a set: the probability measure giving weight `p` to a set `s` and `1-p` to its complement. Distinct from Distributions/TwoValued.

Name: Uniform.lean
Description: Uniform distribution: `ProbabilityTheory.uniformMeasure s` — the uniform probability measure on a set `s`, with density `1/μ(s)`. Distinct from Distributions/Beta and MeasureTheory/Measure/Restrict.

Name: TwoValued.lean
Description: Two-valued distributions: probability measures supported on exactly two points, generalising Bernoulli. Distinct from Distributions/SetBernoulli and ProbabilityMassFunction/Basic.

Name: Gamma.lean
Description: Gamma distribution: `ProbabilityTheory.gammaMeasure a r` with density `r^a x^{a-1} e^{-rx} / Γ(a)`. Connection to the Gamma function integral. Distinct from Distributions/Beta and Distributions/Exponential.

Name: Binomial.lean
Description: Binomial distribution: `ProbabilityTheory.binomialPMF n p k = C(n,k) p^k (1-p)^{n-k}` as a probability mass function. Mean `np`, variance `np(1-p)`. Distinct from ProbabilityMassFunction/Binomial.

Name: Beta.lean
Description: Beta distribution: `ProbabilityTheory.betaMeasure a b` — the measure on `[0,1]` with density `x^{a-1}(1-x)^{b-1}/B(a,b)`. Mean, variance, and the beta–Dirichlet relationship. Distinct from Distributions/Gamma.

Name: Cauchy.lean
Description: Cauchy distribution: the measure on ℝ with density `1/(π(1+x²))`. Heavy-tailed, no mean or variance. Distinct from Distributions/Exponential.

Name: Fernique.lean
Description: Fernique's theorem: a Gaussian measure on a Banach space satisfies `∫ exp(α‖x‖²) dμ < ∞` for sufficiently small `α > 0`. Distinct from Distributions/Gamma and Moments/SubGaussian.

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: Exponential distribution: `ProbabilityTheory.expMeasure λ` — the measure with density `λ e^{-λx}` on `[0,∞)`. Mean `1/λ`, memoryless property. Distinct from Distributions/Gamma.

Name: IdentDistribIndep.lean
Description: Identically distributed and independent sequences: i.i.d. random variables and their product measure construction.

Name: IdentDistrib.lean
Description: Identically distributed random variables: two variables have the same law iff they agree on all measurable sets.

Name: HasLawExists.lean
Description: Existence of random variables with a given law: constructing a random variable on a probability space with a specified distribution.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Probability theory notation: P[X in A], E[X], and almost-sure notation for events and expectations.

Name: UniformOn.lean
Description: The uniform distribution on a finite set: definition, expectation, and sampling properties.

Name: StrongLaw.lean
Description: The strong law of large numbers: for a sequence of i.i.d. integrable real-valued random variables X₁, X₂, …, the Cesàro averages (X₁ + … + Xₙ)/n converge almost surely to the common expectation E[X₁]. Formalized as ProbabilityTheory.strong_law_ae. The rigorous foundation for why long-run frequencies converge to true probabilities — why insurance, polling, and statistical inference work.

Name: ProductMeasure.lean
Description: Product probability measures: the measure on a product space making coordinates independent.

Name: HasLaw.lean
Description: The HasLaw typeclass: asserting that a random variable has a specified probability distribution.

Name: CentralLimitTheorem.lean
Description: The central limit theorem: normalized partial sums of i.i.d. variables converge in distribution to the standard normal.

Name: CDF.lean
Description: Cumulative distribution functions: the CDF of a real-valued random variable, its right-continuity, and the Stieltjes measure.

Name: BorelCantelli.lean
Description: The Borel-Cantelli lemmas: convergence of probability series implies almost sure eventual absence, with the independent converse.

Name: ConditionalExpectation.lean
Description: Conditional expectation E[X | F]: the projection of X onto F-measurable functions, with the tower property.

Name: Density.lean
Description: Probability density functions: the Radon-Nikodym derivative of a probability measure with respect to Lebesgue measure.

Name: CondVar.lean
Description: Conditional variance Var[X | F]: the conditional expectation of the squared deviation, with the law of total variance.

Name: ConditionalProbability.lean
Description: Conditional probability P(A | B) = P(A intersect B)/P(B): basic properties, Bayes' theorem, and the conditional measure.

Name: SetTheory
Description: About sets, functions, relations, cardinals, ordinals, and the Zermelo-Fraenkel axioms. Used as a foundational language and to investigate infinite sets, cardinal arithmetic, and the consistency of axioms. Relied on by logic, topology, measure theory, and every field that uses infinite collections.

Name: Ordinal
Description: Ordinal numbers and transfinite induction: ordinal arithmetic, limit ordinals, and the well-ordering of ordinals.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Ordinal notations: a computable representation of certain ordinals (up to `ε₀` or beyond) as a data type, used for proof-theoretic purposes. Distinct from Ordinal/CantorNormalForm.

Name: Principal.lean
Description: Principal ordinals: `Ordinal.Principal op α` — ordinals closed under a binary operation `op`. Used to characterise additive and multiplicative principal ordinals. Distinct from Ordinal/FixedPoint.

Name: FixedPointApproximants.lean
Description: Approximants to fixed points: the sequence `f^n(α)` converging to the fixed point `nfp f α`. Distinct from Ordinal/FixedPoint.

Name: FundamentalSequence.lean
Description: Fundamental sequences for limit ordinals: `Ordinal.fundamentalSeq α` — an ω-sequence converging to the limit ordinal `α`. Used in proof-theoretic ordinal analysis. Distinct from Ordinal/Basic.

Name: Univ.lean
Description: The ordinal of the universe: `Ordinal.univ` — the ordinal corresponding to the class of all sets. Distinct from Ordinal/Basic and Cardinal/UnivLE.

Name: Veblen.lean
Description: Veblen functions: `Ordinal.veblen` — the hierarchy of fixed-point enumerations `φ_α` with `φ₀ = ω^·` and `φ_{α+1}` enumerating fixed points of `φ_α`. Reaches `Γ₀`. Distinct from Ordinal/FixedPoint.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank function for sets: `Set.rank s` — the von Neumann rank of a set in the cumulative hierarchy. Distinct from ZFC/Rank and Ordinal/Basic.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Order topology on ordinals: the ordinal space with the order topology, compactness of successor ordinals, and the Cantor–Bendixson theorem for ordinal spaces. Distinct from Topology/Order/Basic.

Name: CantorNormalForm.lean
Description: Cantor normal form: every ordinal `α` has a unique representation `ω^{β₁} k₁ + ⋯ + ω^{βₙ} kₙ` with `β₁ > ⋯ > βₙ` and `kᵢ ∈ ℕ⁺`. Distinct from Ordinal/Arithmetic.

Name: Commute.lean
Description: Commutativity in ordinal arithmetic: conditions under which `a + b = b + a` or `a * b = b * a` for ordinals (which generally do not hold). Distinct from Ordinal/Arithmetic.

Name: Arithmetic.lean
Description: Ordinal arithmetic: `a + b`, `a * b`, `a ^ b` for ordinals; the natural (Hessenberg) operations, and the cantor normal form. Distinct from Ordinal/Exponential and Ordinal/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordinals: `Ordinal` as well-ordered types modulo isomorphism. The ordering `a < b`, successor `succ a`, limit ordinals, `ω`, and transfinite induction. The foundational file for the Ordinal folder.

Name: Family.lean
Description: Families of ordinals: suprema of families, the `iSup` for ordinal-indexed families, and bounding arguments. Distinct from Ordinal/Basic.

Name: FixedPoint.lean
Description: Fixed points of ordinal functions: `Ordinal.nfp f` — the next fixed point of a normal function `f` above a given ordinal. The fixed-point lemma for normal functions. Distinct from Ordinal/FixedPointApproximants.

Name: Enum.lean
Description: Enumeration of ordinals: `Ordinal.enumOrd S` — the order-isomorphism between ordinals and closed unbounded classes. The club filter. Distinct from Ordinal/Basic.

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: Ordinal exponentiation: `a ^ b` via transfinite iteration, properties including `ω ^ ω = ε₀`. Distinct from Ordinal/Arithmetic and Ordinal/FixedPoint.

Name: ZFC
Description: A formalization of Zermelo-Fraenkel set theory inside Lean: the ZFSet type with membership, separation, and the axiom of choice.

Name: PSet.lean
Description: Pre-sets: `PSet` — a type of `Set`-like structures (protosets / iterative sets before quotienting). The quotient `PSet / equiv = ZFSet`. Distinct from ZFC/Basic.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Von Neumann rank in ZFC: `ZFSet.rank s` — the smallest ordinal `α` with `s ∈ V_{α+1}`. The cumulative hierarchy `V_α`. Distinct from ZFC/Ordinal and Ordinal/Rank.

Name: VonNeumann.lean
Description: Von Neumann hierarchy: `ZFSet.V α` — the cumulative hierarchy `V₀ = ∅`, `V_{α+1} = 𝒫(V_α)`, `V_λ = ⋃_{α<λ} V_α`. Every set has finite rank. Distinct from ZFC/Rank.

Name: Ordinal.lean
Description: Ordinals in ZFC: `ZFSet.IsOrdinal` — von Neumann ordinals (transitive sets well-ordered by membership), and their identification with `SetTheory.Ordinal`. Distinct from ZFC/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: ZFC set theory in Lean: `ZFSet` — the type of sets in the von Neumann hierarchy, set membership `∈`, comprehension `{x ∈ s | p x}`, union, power set, and regularity. The foundational file for the ZFC folder.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: Cardinals in ZFC: `ZFSet.card s` — the cardinal of a ZF set as an ordinal. Cantor's theorem `#s < #(𝒫 s)`. Distinct from ZFC/Basic and SetTheory/Cardinal/Basic.

Name: Class.lean
Description: Proper classes in ZFC: `Class` — a `Set ZFSet`, allowing proper classes like `V` (the universe) and `Ord` (the class of ordinals). Distinct from ZFC/Basic.

Name: Cardinal
Description: Cardinal numbers and their arithmetic: comparison via injections, the Schroeder-Bernstein theorem, and cardinal exponentiation.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order structure on cardinals: the linear order `#α ≤ #β ↔ α ↪ β`, suprema of sets of cardinals, and the cofinality characterisation. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: Ordinal.lean
Description: Cardinals from ordinals: `Cardinal.card o` (the cardinality of an ordinal), `ord : Cardinal → Ordinal` (the least ordinal of a given cardinality), and the Hartogs number. Distinct from Ordinal/Basic.

Name: NatCount.lean
Description: Counting elements of countable types: `Cardinal.toNat`, `Set.ncard`, and the relationship between `Set.Finite.toFinset.card` and cardinals. Distinct from Cardinal/Finite.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Cardinality of free algebraic structures: `#(FreeGroup α)`, `#(FreeMagma α)`, etc., in terms of `#α`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: HasCardinalLT.lean
Description: `HasCardinalLT α β`: a predicate asserting `#α < #β`, and its properties. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: Pigeonhole.lean
Description: Pigeonhole principle for cardinals: if `#f.domain > #f.codomain` then `f` is not injective. The infinite pigeonhole principle. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: ToNat.lean
Description: `Cardinal.toNat : Cardinal → ℕ` — maps finite cardinals to their natural number value and infinite cardinals to 0. Distinct from Cardinal/ENat.

Name: UnivLE.lean
Description: Universe-lifting and cardinal inequalities: conditions under which `#α ≤ #β` in a different universe, using `Cardinal.lift`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: Subfield.lean
Description: Cardinality of subfields: `#(Subfield K)` and bounds on the number of subfields of a given cardinality. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular and inaccessible cardinals: `Cardinal.IsRegular κ` (cf(κ) = κ), weakly inaccessible (`κ` regular and limit), and strongly inaccessible (weakly inaccessible and `2^λ < κ` for all `λ < κ`). Distinct from Cardinal/Cofinality.

Name: SchroederBernstein.lean
Description: Schröder–Bernstein theorem: if `#α ≤ #β` and `#β ≤ #α` then `#α = #β`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic and Cardinal/Embedding.

Name: Finsupp.lean
