Description: Cardinality of Finsupp: `#(ι →₀ R)` in terms of `#ι` and `#R`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic and LinearAlgebra/Finsupp/Defs.

Name: Cofinality.lean
Description: Cofinality of a cardinal or ordinal: `Cardinal.cof κ` — the smallest cardinality of a cofinal subset. Regular cardinals: `cof κ = κ`. König's cofinality theorem: `cof(2^κ) > κ`. Distinct from Cardinal/Regular.

Name: Continuum.lean
Description: Continuum: `Cardinal.continuum = 2^ℵ₀ = #ℝ`. The continuum hypothesis `CH : 2^ℵ₀ = ℵ₁` as a `Prop`, and the Cantor–Bernstein theorem for ℝ. Distinct from Cardinal/Arithmetic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core cardinal API: `Cardinal.mk α` (cardinality of a type), `Cardinal.lift` (universe lifting), `#α ≤ #β ↔ ∃ f : α ↪ β`, and `#α = #β ↔ α ≃ β`. Distinct from Cardinal/Defs.

Name: Aleph.lean
Description: Aleph function: `Cardinal.aleph` — the enumeration of infinite cardinals: `ℵ₀ = aleph 0`, `ℵ₁ = aleph 1`, etc. Properties: `aleph` is a strictly increasing continuous function on ordinals. Distinct from Cardinal/Regular.

Name: Arithmetic.lean
Description: Cardinal arithmetic: `a + b`, `a * b`, `a ^ b` for cardinals; `max(a, b)` for infinite sums, and König's theorem `∑ aᵢ < ∏ bᵢ` when `aᵢ < bᵢ`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: CountableCover.lean
Description: Countable covers in cardinal theory: conditions under which a set can be covered by countably many small pieces. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic.

Name: ENat.lean
Description: Cardinals and extended naturals: `Cardinal.toENat` — the map from cardinals to `ℕ∞` (sending infinite cardinals to `∞`). Distinct from Cardinal/ToNat.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite cardinals: `Cardinal.lt_aleph0_iff_fintype`, identification of finite cardinals with `ℕ`, and the coercion `#(Fin n) = n`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic and Cardinal/NatCount.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embeddings and cardinal bounds: `Cardinal.mk_le_of_injective`, the Schröder–Bernstein theorem in cardinal form. Distinct from Cardinal/SchroederBernstein and Cardinal/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Cardinal`: as a quotient type `Quotient (⟨α, β, α ≃ β⟩)` via type equivalence. The `LinearOrder` on cardinals and `ℵ₀`. Distinct from Cardinal/Basic (API).

Name: Divisibility.lean
Description: Divisibility of cardinals: `a ∣ b` in the semiring of cardinals. Distinct from Cardinal/Arithmetic.

Name: Descriptive
Description: Descriptive set theory: Borel sets, analytic sets, Polish spaces, and their topological and measure-theoretic properties.

Name: Tree.lean
Description: Trees in descriptive set theory: `Tree A` — sets of finite sequences from `A` closed under taking initial segments, used to define analytic and co-analytic sets. Distinct from Topology/MetricSpace/PiNat and Combinatorics/Quiver/Arborescence.

Name: Lists.lean
Description: Lists in set theory: the type of hereditary lists (lists of lists of ...) used to model sets in type theory.

Name: RingTheory
Description: Formalizes rings, ideals, quotient rings, polynomial rings, localization, and unique factorization domains. Used to solve polynomial equations, factor numbers, and study algebraic integers. Relied on by algebraic geometry, commutative algebra, number theory, and invariant theory.

Name: Perfectoid
Description: Perfectoid rings and spaces: characteristic-p-like structures that allow tilting between mixed and equal characteristic, foundational in modern p-adic geometry.

Name: Untilt.lean
Description: PreTilt.untilt: the multiplicative map from `PreTilt O p` to a `p`-adically complete ring `O`, defined as the limit of `p^n`-th powers of arbitrary lifts of the `n`-th component from the perfection of `O/p`. `mk_untilt_eq_coeff_zero`: the mod-`p` map composed with untilt equals taking the zeroth component.

Name: FontaineTheta.lean
Description: Fontaine's θ map: the ring homomorphism `fontaineTheta : 𝕎 R♭ →+* R` from the Witt vectors of the tilt of a `p`-adically complete ring `R`. `fontaineTheta_teichmuller`: θ([x]) equals the untilt of x. `fontaineTheta_surjective`: θ is surjective. Does not require R to be perfectoid.

Name: BDeRham.lean
Description: The de Rham period rings `BDeRhamPlus` (B_dR⁺) and `BDeRham` (B_dR): generalized versions following Scholze, defined for any `p`-adically complete ring `R`. When `R = PadicComplexInt` (integers of ℂ_p), these coincide with the classical de Rham period rings used in p-adic Hodge theory.

Name: Polynomial
Description: Polynomial rings over general rings: irreducibility, roots, factorization, and the relationship between polynomial properties and the base ring.

Name: Hermite
Description: Hermite polynomials Hₙ(X): orthogonal polynomials with weight e^{-x²}, satisfying the recurrence Hₙ₊₁ = 2X Hₙ - 2nHₙ₋₁. Used in the proof of the Lindemann-Weierstrass theorem (the auxiliary polynomials in the original proof of transcendence of e and π are Hermite-type). Distinct from Cyclotomic and Eisenstein (different polynomial families).

Name: Gaussian.lean
Description: Gaussian integral via Hermite polynomials.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Hermite polynomials `Hₙ(x)` and their orthogonality.

Name: Resultant
Description: The resultant Res(f, g) of two polynomials: the determinant of the Sylvester matrix, equal to the product of f evaluated at the roots of g (times the leading coefficient). Res(f, g) = 0 iff f and g share a common root. Used to eliminate variables in systems of polynomial equations and to define the discriminant as Res(f, f'). Distinct from Cyclotomic and Eisenstein (irreducibility criteria).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Resultant of two polynomials.

Name: Cyclotomic
Description: Cyclotomic polynomials Φₙ(X): the minimal polynomial over ℚ of a primitive nth root of unity, with integer coefficients. Irreducibility of Φₙ over ℚ, the factorization Xⁿ - 1 = ∏_{d|n} Φ_d, and Φₙ mod p. Used in algebraic number theory (rings of integers of cyclotomic fields) and in proving infinitely many primes ≡ 1 (mod n). Distinct from NumberTheory/Cyclotomic (the number field ℚ(ζₙ) itself).

Name: Factorization.lean
Description: Factorisation of cyclotomic polynomials.

Name: Roots.lean
Description: Roots of cyclotomic polynomials: primitive `n`-th roots.

Name: Expand.lean
Description: Cyclotomic polynomial expansion identities.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cyclotomic polynomials: `Polynomial.cyclotomic n R`.

Name: Eval.lean
Description: Evaluating cyclotomic polynomials.

Name: Eisenstein
Description: Eisenstein's irreducibility criterion: a polynomial f = aₙXⁿ + … + a₀ is irreducible over ℤ if there exists a prime p with p ∤ aₙ, p | aᵢ for i < n, and p² ∤ a₀. Generalizations to arbitrary rings and the Eisenstein criterion for Newton polygons. Used to prove irreducibility of cyclotomic polynomials (via a substitution). Distinct from Cyclotomic and Resultant (different irreducibility/compatibility tools).

Name: Distinguished.lean
Description: Distinguished polynomials.

Name: IsIntegral.lean
Description: Integrality via Eisenstein polynomials.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Eisenstein's criterion: if `p | aᵢ` for `i < n` and `p ∤ aₙ` and `p² ∤ a₀`, then `p` is irreducible.

Name: Criterion.lean
Description: Eisenstein's irreducibility criterion.

Name: ScaleRoots.lean
Description: Scaling roots of a polynomial: the polynomial scaleRoots p c = cⁿ·p(X/c) obtained by scaling the variable by c, and its application to clearing denominators in factorisation. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: RationalRoot.lean
Description: The rational root theorem: if a rational number p/q in lowest terms is a root of a₀ + a₁X + ··· + aₙXⁿ ∈ ℤ[X], then p ∣ a₀ and q ∣ aₙ. Distinct from Polynomial/Roots and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: SeparableDegree.lean
Description: The separable degree of a polynomial: the degree of the separable part (the largest separable divisor), and the interaction with the Frobenius endomorphism in characteristic p. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and CharP/Frobenius.

Name: Selmer.lean
Description: Irreducibility of Selmer polynomials: `X_pow_sub_X_sub_one_irreducible` — the Selmer polynomials `X^n - X - 1` are irreducible (over any suitable ring). TODO: show these polynomials have full Galois group S_n.

Name: Pochhammer.lean
Description: Pochhammer polynomials (rising factorials): (X)_n = X(X+1)···(X+n-1), with the recurrence, the connection to binomial coefficients, and the interaction with falling factorials and Stirling numbers. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Opposite polynomial rings: the isomorphism (R[X])^op ≅ (R^op)[X] of the opposite of a polynomial ring with the polynomial ring over the opposite ring. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: Radical.lean
Description: The radical of a polynomial in ring theory: the squarefree part of p (the product of distinct irreducible factors), and the interaction with the radical ideal of (p) in R[X]. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Radical/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient rings of polynomial rings: the ring R[X]/(f) and its interaction with the minimal polynomial (k[X]/(f) is a field iff f is irreducible), the CRT, and the first isomorphism theorem. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: UniversalFactorizationRing.lean
Description: Universal factorization ring: for a commutative ring `R` and monic polynomial `p` of degree `n = m + k`, the universal `R`-algebra representing the functor `S ↦ factorizations of p into (monic deg m) × (monic deg k) in S`. Constructs the universal ring via `MvPolynomial.mapEquivMonic` and related machinery.

Name: UniqueFactorization.lean
Description: Unique factorisation in polynomial rings: R[X] is a UFD when R is (Gauss's theorem), with the factorisation algorithm using content and primitive part. Distinct from Polynomial/GaussLemma and UniqueFactorizationDomain/Basic.

Name: Wronskian.lean
Description: The Wronskian of polynomials: the determinant W(p₁,…,pₙ) of the matrix [p₁ p₂ … ; p₁' p₂' … ; …], with the criterion W = 0 iff the polynomials are linearly dependent over the constants. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Derivative.

Name: Vieta.lean
Description: Vieta's formulas: for a monic polynomial ∏(X - rᵢ) = Xⁿ - e₁Xⁿ⁻¹ + e₂Xⁿ⁻² - ···, the elementary symmetric polynomials eₖ = eₖ(r₁,…,rₙ) as the coefficients, and Newton's power sum identities. Distinct from Polynomial/SmallDegreeVieta and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: SmallDegreeVieta.lean
Description: Vieta's formulas for small degree polynomials: explicit formulas expressing e₁ = ∑ rᵢ, e₂ = ∑ rᵢrⱼ, etc. in terms of coefficients for polynomials of degree ≤ 4. Distinct from Polynomial/Vieta and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: ShiftedLegendre.lean
Description: Shifted Legendre polynomials: P̃_n(X) = P_n(2X-1) where P_n are the Legendre polynomials, with the orthogonality on [0,1] and the explicit formula via the Rodriguez formula. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Tower of polynomial rings: the isomorphism R[X][Y] ≅ R[X, Y] and the interaction with the tower of algebras (IsScalarTower R R[X] R[X][Y]). Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Adjoin/Tower.

Name: Subring.lean
Description: Subrings and polynomial rings: for a subring S ⊆ R, the polynomial ring S[X] embeds into R[X], and the interaction of subring conditions with polynomial rings. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: DegreeLT.lean
Description: The degreeLT n submodule in ring theory: Polynomial.degreeLT R n as the R-submodule of polynomials of degree < n, and the Chinese Remainder Theorem for polynomial rings R[X]/(f·g) ≅ R[X]/(f) × R[X]/(g) for coprime f, g. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: ContentIdeal.lean
Description: The content ideal of a polynomial: the ideal (a₀, a₁, …, aₙ) ⊆ R generated by all coefficients of p, with the multiplicativity in a Prüfer domain (content(pq) = content(p)·content(q) as ideals). Distinct from Polynomial/Content and FractionalIdeal/Basic.

Name: GaussLemma.lean
Description: Gauss's lemma in ring theory: a primitive polynomial over a GCD domain R is irreducible in R[X] iff it is irreducible in K[X] (K = fraction field of R). Distinct from DedekindDomain/GaussLemma and Polynomial/Content.

Name: Dickson.lean
Description: Dickson polynomials: the Dickson polynomial of the first kind D_n(X, a) satisfying D_n(t + a/t, a) = tⁿ + (a/t)ⁿ, used in permutation polynomial theory and cryptography. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Chebyshev.

Name: Bernstein.lean
Description: Bernstein polynomials: B(n, k, X) = C(n,k) · X^k · (1-X)^(n-k), the partition of unity property (∑_k B(n,k,X) = 1), and applications to uniform approximation (Weierstrass approximation setup). Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ring-theoretic properties of polynomial rings: the Noetherian property (Hilbert basis theorem), integral closure (R integrally closed → R[X] integrally closed), and the interaction with prime ideals in R[X] vs R. Distinct from Algebra/Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Content.lean
Description: Content of a polynomial: the GCD of the coefficients content(p) ∈ R for p ∈ R[X] in a GCD domain, with Gauss's lemma content(pq) = content(p)·content(q). Distinct from Polynomial/ContentIdeal and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: Chebyshev.lean
Description: Chebyshev polynomials: the polynomials T_n and U_n defined by T_n(cos θ) = cos(nθ) and U_n(cos θ) = sin((n+1)θ)/sin θ, with the recurrence, orthogonality, and minimal polynomial connection. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: IsIntegral.lean
Description: Integrality of polynomial roots: if p ∈ R[X] is monic and p(α) = 0, then α is integral over R. Contains the connection between the minimal polynomial being monic and integrality. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: IrreducibleRing.lean
Description: Irreducibility conditions for polynomials in ring theory: conditions under which the polynomial ring R[X] is an integral domain or irreducible, and the interaction with the irreducibility of R. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Nilpotent polynomials in ring theory: p ∈ R[X] is nilpotent iff all coefficients of p are nilpotent, and the nilradical of R[X] equals (nilradical R)[X]. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Nilpotent/Basic.

Name: Morse.lean
Description: Galois groups of Morse polynomials: a Morse polynomial is one whose roots have at most one collision modulo each maximal ideal. Proves `Polynomial.Splits.surjective_toPermHom_of_iSup_inertia_eq_top`: if a group acting transitively on roots is generated by inertia subgroups, it surjects onto S_n.

Name: HilbertPoly.lean
Description: Hilbert polynomials: for a field `F` of characteristic 0, `p : F[X]` and `d : ℕ`, the unique polynomial `Polynomial.hilbertPoly p d` such that for all large enough `n`, `h(n)` equals the coefficient of `X^n` in the power series expansion of `p/(1 - X)^d`. Formalizes the connection between Hilbert polynomials and power series.

Name: GaussNorm.lean
Description: The Gauss norm for polynomials over a valued ring: ‖∑ aᵢXⁱ‖ = max|aᵢ|ᵥ, with the multiplicativity ‖pq‖ = ‖p‖·‖q‖ when the valuation is non-Archimedean. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and HahnSeries/Valuation.

Name: IntegralNormalization.lean
Description: Integral normalisation of polynomials: for a polynomial p ∈ R[X] of degree n with leading coefficient a, the polynomial aⁿ⁻¹ · p(X/a) is monic with coefficients in the integral closure of R in R[1/a]. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals of polynomial rings in ring theory: the interaction between ideals of R[X] and those of R (Lying-over, Going-up), and the structure of maximal ideals of k[X₁,…,Xₙ] (Nullstellensatz). Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ideal/Maps.

Name: OrderOfVanishing
Description: The order of vanishing of a ring element at a prime or valuation, used in algebraic geometry and valuation theory.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Order of vanishing in Noetherian rings: properties of the order of vanishing including API for computing it in discrete valuation rings. Builds on `OrderOfVanishing.Basic` and `DiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order of vanishing: defines the order of vanishing of a ring element as the length of `R / (x)R`, and extends this to the field of fractions. Distinct from the p-adic valuation; uses module length rather than ideal chains.

Name: OreLocalization
Description: Ore localization: localization of noncommutative rings at multiplicative sets satisfying the Ore conditions.

Name: OreSet.lean
Description: Ore sets: the OreSet S predicate asserting the left and right Ore conditions (for all a, s there exist a', s' with s'a = a's'), which are necessary and sufficient for the Ore localization to exist. Distinct from OreLocalization/Basic.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Ring-theoretic properties of the Ore localization: the interaction with ideals, modules, and the ring structure of the Ore localization. Distinct from OreLocalization/Basic and OreLocalization/OreSet.

Name: NonZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Ore localization at non-zero-divisors: when S = nonZeroDivisors R, the Ore localization produces the classical ring of fractions (the total quotient ring). Distinct from OreLocalization/Basic and Ring/NonZeroDivisors.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ore localization of a non-commutative ring: the OreLocalization R S for an Ore set S (satisfying the left/right Ore conditions), with the ring structure and the universal property (every S-inverting map factors through). Foundation for all OreLocalization files.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of the Ore localization: bounds on |R[S⁻¹]|. Distinct from OreLocalization/Basic.

Name: QuasiFinite
Description: Quasi-finite ring maps: ring homomorphisms with finite fibers, intermediate between finite and finitely generated maps.

Name: Weakly.lean
Description: Algebra.WeaklyQuasiFiniteAt: a weaker notion than `QuasiFiniteAt` (isolated in fibers) used to state Zariski's main theorem. Proved equivalent to `QuasiFiniteAt` for algebras of finite type via ZMT. Should not be used outside the proof of Zariski's main theorem; all public API should use `QuasiFiniteAt`.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Quasi-finiteness for polynomial quotients: the map R → R[X]/(f) is quasi-finite iff f ≠ 0, and conditions for degree-1 polynomials to give étale maps. Distinct from QuasiFinite/Basic and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quasi-finite ring maps: a ring map f : R → A is quasi-finite if it is of finite type and each fiber A ⊗_R k(p) (for a prime p of R) is a finite k(p)-algebra. Foundation for all QuasiFinite files.

Name: Radical
Description: The radical of an ideal (intersection of primes containing it) and the Jacobson radical (intersection of maximal ideals).

Name: NatInt.lean
Description: Radical ideals in ℕ and ℤ: the radical of an ideal nℤ is (rad n)ℤ where rad n = ∏_{p | n} p (the squarefree kernel), and the explicit description. Distinct from Radical/Basic and Int/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The radical of an ideal: Ideal.radical I = {r | rⁿ ∈ I} = ∩{p prime | I ⊆ p}, with basic properties (radical is radical, radical of a product, Nullstellensatz). Distinct from Nilpotent/Basic (nil radical = radical of 0) and Jacobson/Radical.

Name: PolynomialLaw
Description: Polynomial laws between modules: generalized polynomial maps that are not necessarily linear, used in divided power theory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Polynomial laws between R-modules: a polynomial law f : M → N over R is a natural transformation T_M : Hom_R(M, R⊗S) → Hom_R(N, S) for all R-algebras S, generalising both linear maps (degree 1) and the norm map (degree [A:R]). Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: PowerSeries
Description: Formal power series rings R[[X]]: coefficient operations, inversion of units, and connections to p-adic analysis and generating functions.

Name: PiTopology.lean
Description: The pi topology on formal power series: the product topology on R[[X]] = (ℕ → R) making it into a complete topological ring with the maximal ideal m = (X) as a neighborhood basis of 0. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and AdicCompletion/Topology.

Name: Restricted.lean
Description: `IsRestricted`: a power series over a normed ring `R` is restricted for a parameter `c : ℝ` if `‖coeff R i f‖ * c ^ i → 0`. Defines the subring of restricted power series and provides basic API. Distinct from formal power series with bounded coefficients in the sup-norm sense.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order (X-adic valuation) of a formal power series: ord p = min{n | coeff n p ≠ 0} with ord(pq) = ord p + ord q in a domain, and the interaction with multiplication and the maximal ideal filtration. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and Valuation/Basic.

Name: Log.lean
Description: The logarithm series: log(1 + X) = ∑_{n≥1} (-1)^(n+1) Xⁿ/n in PowerSeries ℚ, with the functional equation log(exp X) = X and log(xy) = log x + log y (for x, y ≡ 1 mod X). Distinct from PowerSeries/Exp.

Name: NoZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Zero divisors in formal power series: R[[X]] is an integral domain if R is a domain. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and MvPowerSeries/NoZeroDivisors.

Name: WeierstrassPreparation.lean
Description: The Weierstrass preparation theorem: every power series p ∈ k[[X]] with p ≡ Xⁿ mod (X^(n+1)) factors as p = u · w where u is a unit and w is a Weierstrass polynomial (monic polynomial of degree n with lower coefficients in the maximal ideal). Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Ideal.

Name: WellKnown.lean
Description: Well-known formal power series identities: the series for 1/(1-X) = ∑ Xⁿ, geometric series ∑ aXⁿ = a/(1-X), the Euler product, and other standard combinatorial generating functions. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Binomial.

Name: Trunc.lean
Description: Truncation of formal power series: the ring map PowerSeries.trunc n : R[[X]] → R[X] truncating to the first n terms, with the interaction between truncation and the inverse. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and MvPowerSeries/Trunc.

Name: Schroder.lean
Description: Schröder numbers as power series: `PowerSeries.largeSchroderSeries` and `PowerSeries.smallSchroderSeries`. Proves `largeSchroderSeries_eq_one_add_X_mul_largeSchroderSeries_add_X_mul_largeSchroderSeries_sq`: the functional equation satisfied by the large Schröder numbers power series.

Name: Substitution.lean
Description: Substitution (composition) of formal power series: the composite f(g(X)) for power series f and g with g(0) = 0, with the monoid structure on {p | p.coeff 0 = 0} under composition and the interaction with the exponential and logarithm. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic.

Name: Inverse.lean
Description: Inverses in formal power series: p ∈ R[[X]] is a unit iff p.coeff 0 ∈ Rˣ, with the explicit formula for p⁻¹ via the geometric series. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and Ring/Units.

Name: CoeffMulMem.lean
Description: Membership conditions for Cauchy product coefficients: conditions ensuring the coefficients of the product pq lie in a specified submodule or ideal of R. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Derivative.lean
Description: The formal derivative of a power series: the ring derivation PowerSeries.derivative : R[[X]] →ₗ[R] R[[X]] satisfying the Leibniz rule, with the interaction with composition. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and Polynomial/Derivative.

Name: Catalan.lean
Description: The Catalan generating series: the unique power series C satisfying XC(X)² - C(X) + 1 = 0 with C(0) = 1, with the explicit formula Cₙ = C(2n,n)/(n+1) for the Catalan numbers. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Formal power series: PowerSeries R = ℕ →₀ R with the Cauchy product ring structure, the variable X, the coeff map, and the universal property (any truncation-compatible ring map is induced by a power series). Foundation for all PowerSeries files.

Name: Binomial.lean
Description: Binomial series in formal power series: the series (1 + X)^n = ∑_k C(n,k) X^k as an element of PowerSeries R for a commutative ring R, and the functional equation. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic.

Name: GaussNorm.lean
Description: The Gauss norm for formal power series: ‖∑ aₙXⁿ‖ = supₙ|aₙ|ᵥ on R[[X]] over a valued ring R, with the ultrametric inequality and multiplicativity. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and MvPowerSeries/GaussNorm.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals of formal power series rings: the maximal ideal m = (X) ⊆ R[[X]], the Weierstrass preparation theorem setup, and the ideal structure of R[[X]] (especially for R a field or DVR). Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Expand.lean
Description: Expansion of formal power series: the map PowerSeries.expand R n substituting Xⁿ for X, and the Frobenius-Expand interaction in characteristic p. Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic.

Name: Evaluation.lean
Description: Evaluation of formal power series: evaluating a power series at an element a of a complete topological ring (when ∑ aₙ xⁿ converges in the topology). Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and AdicCompletion/Basic.

Name: Exp.lean
Description: The exponential series: exp(X) = ∑ Xⁿ/n! in PowerSeries ℚ (or PowerSeries R for a ℚ-algebra R), with the functional equation exp(X+Y) = exp(X)exp(Y) and the inverse log(1+X). Distinct from PowerSeries/Basic and Nilpotent/Exp.

Name: Norm
Description: The norm map in ring extensions: the multiplicative map N_{L/K} defined as the determinant of the multiplication map.

Name: Transitivity.lean
Description: Transitivity of norms in algebra towers: for R ⊆ S ⊆ A, Nrm_{A/R}(x) = Nrm_{S/R}(Nrm_{A/S}(x)), the tower formula for the norm. Distinct from Norm/Basic and Algebra/Tower.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definition of the algebraic norm: Algebra.norm R A x = det(Lₓ : A → A) where Lₓ(a) = xa is left multiplication by x. Distinct from Norm/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results on the algebraic norm: Algebra.norm R A : A →*₀ R (the determinant of left-multiplication), multiplicativity norm(xy) = norm(x)norm(y), and the interaction with algebraMap (norm(r·1) = r^[A:R]). Distinct from Norm/Defs and Norm/Transitivity.

Name: MvPolynomial
Description: Multivariate polynomial rings R[X₁,...,Xₙ] over a ring: variables, monomials, evaluation maps, and symmetric polynomials.

Name: Symmetric
Description: Symmetric polynomials in MvPolynomial: polynomials invariant under all permutations of variables. The elementary symmetric polynomials e₁, …, eₙ, the Newton power sums pₖ = ∑ Xᵢᵏ, and the fundamental theorem: every symmetric polynomial is a polynomial in the eₖ. Used in Galois theory (the coefficients of a polynomial are symmetric in its roots) and in representation theory (characters of GL).

Name: NewtonIdentities.lean
Description: Newton's identities relating power sums and elementary symmetric polynomials.

Name: FundamentalTheorem.lean
Description: Fundamental theorem of symmetric polynomials.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Symmetric polynomials: `MvPolynomial.IsSymmetric`.

Name: MonomialOrder
Description: Monomial orders on multivariate polynomial rings: total orders on monomials Xᵅ compatible with multiplication (lex, graded lex, graded reverse lex). The leading monomial, leading coefficient, and leading term of a polynomial. Used in Gröbner basis theory (a basis with respect to a monomial order). Distinct from Symmetric (which studies symmetric polynomials without an order) and Data/Finsupp/MonomialOrder (the abstract order on Finsupp).

Name: DegLex.lean
Description: DegLex monomial order on multivariate polynomials.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Localization of multivariate polynomial rings: the localization (MvPolynomial σ R)[f⁻¹] for a polynomial f, and the interaction with the ideal theory. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: IrreducibleQuadratic.lean
Description: Irreducible quadratic multivariate polynomials: conditions for a degree-2 polynomial in MvPolynomial σ R to be irreducible (e.g., X² + Y² + Z² over ℝ). Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in multivariate polynomial rings: the Hilbert basis theorem (MvPolynomial σ R is Noetherian for Noetherian R and finite σ), and the prime/maximal ideal theory of MvPolynomial σ k. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Noetherian/Basic.

Name: WeightedHomogeneous.lean
Description: Weighted homogeneous polynomials: polynomials homogeneous with respect to a weight function w : σ → ℕ (so that each monomial ∏ Xᵢ^aᵢ has the same weighted degree ∑ w(i)aᵢ = d). Distinct from MvPolynomial/Homogeneous and MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Tower of multivariate polynomial rings: the isomorphism MvPolynomial σ (MvPolynomial τ R) ≅ MvPolynomial (σ ⊕ τ) R and the interaction with IsScalarTower. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Adjoin/Tower.

Name: MonomialOrder.lean
Description: Monomial orders on multivariate polynomials: total orders on monomials in MvPolynomial σ R compatible with multiplication (lex, grlex, grevlex), with the leading monomial and leading coefficient. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Groebner.

Name: Expand.lean
Description: The expand map for multivariate polynomials in ring theory: the ring map MvPolynomial.expand and its interaction with Frobenius in characteristic p. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: EulerIdentity.lean
Description: Euler's identity for homogeneous multivariate polynomials: for a homogeneous polynomial f of degree d, ∑ᵢ Xᵢ (∂f/∂Xᵢ) = d · f in MvPolynomial σ R. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/PDeriv (Algebra level).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multivariate polynomials in ring theory: the Noetherian property (R Noetherian → MvPolynomial σ R Noetherian for finite σ), the interaction with ideals, and ring-theoretic instances. Distinct from Algebra/MvPolynomial/Basic (the algebra-level file).

Name: Homogeneous.lean
Description: Homogeneous multivariate polynomials: the decomposition of a polynomial into homogeneous components, the ring of homogeneous polynomials of degree d, and the graded algebra structure on MvPolynomial σ R. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and GradedAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Groebner.lean
Description: Division algorithm with respect to monomial orders in multivariate polynomial rings: `MonomialOrder.div hb f` computes a division of `f` by a family `b` of polynomials with invertible leading coefficients. Provides the quotient family and remainder. Prerequisite for Gröbner basis theory.

Name: FreeCommRing.lean
Description: The free commutative ring: MvPolynomial σ ℤ is the free commutative ring on the type σ (the initial commutative ring with a σ-indexed set of elements). Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Algebra/MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: MvPowerSeries
Description: Multivariate formal power series rings: indexed by a monoid of exponents, with filtration and completion properties.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order of a multivariate power series: the smallest total degree of nonzero monomials appearing, with the interaction with multiplication (ord(pq) = ord(p) + ord(q) in a domain). Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Order.

Name: NoZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Zero divisors in multivariate power series: MvPowerSeries σ R is a domain if R is a domain. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/NoZeroDivisors.

Name: LinearTopology.lean
Description: The linear topology on multivariate power series: the topology defined by the filtration by the ideal generated by monomials of total degree ≥ n, and the completion. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and AdicCompletion/Topology.

Name: PiTopology.lean
Description: The pi topology on multivariate power series: the product topology on MvPowerSeries σ R from the discrete topology on R, making MvPowerSeries σ R into a complete topological ring. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/PiTopology.

Name: Trunc.lean
Description: Truncation of multivariate power series: the ring map MvPowerSeries.trunc n : MvPowerSeries σ R → MvPolynomial σ R truncating at total degree n. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Trunc.

Name: Substitution.lean
Description: Substitution in multivariate power series: substituting power series with zero constant term for variables in a power series, with conditions for convergence. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Substitution.

Name: Rename.lean
Description: Renaming variables in multivariate power series: the ring map MvPowerSeries.rename (f : σ → τ) : MvPowerSeries σ R → MvPowerSeries τ R. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and MvPolynomial/Rename.

Name: Evaluation.lean
Description: Evaluation of multivariate power series: evaluating a power series at a point in a complete topological ring (when each variable is substituted by an element in the maximal ideal). Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Evaluation.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences of multivariate power series: the isomorphism MvPowerSeries (σ ⊕ τ) R ≅ MvPowerSeries σ (MvPowerSeries τ R) and the permutation-of-variables isomorphism. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multivariate formal power series: MvPowerSeries σ R = ((σ →₀ ℕ) → R) with the Cauchy product ring structure, the variable Xᵢ, and the coefficient map. Foundation for all MvPowerSeries files.

Name: Expand.lean
Description: Expansion of multivariate power series: the expand map substituting Xᵢ^n for Xᵢ in a power series. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Expand.

Name: LexOrder.lean
Description: Lexicographic order valuation on multivariate power series: when `σ` has `WellFoundedGT`, the lex order on `σ →₀ ℕ` is a well-order defining a natural valuation `lexOrder` on `MvPowerSeries σ R` as the smallest exponent in the support.

Name: Inverse.lean
Description: Inverses in multivariate power series: a power series p is invertible iff its constant term p.coeff 0 is invertible, with the explicit recursive formula for p⁻¹. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Inverse.

Name: GaussNorm.lean
Description: Gauss norm for multivariate power series: the multiplicative norm ‖∑ aᵐ Xᵐ‖ = sup |aᵐ|ᵥ on MvPowerSeries over a valued ring. Distinct from MvPowerSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/GaussNorm.

Name: LocalRing
Description: Local rings (rings with a unique maximal ideal): the residue field, Nakayama's lemma, and the local-global principle.

Name: RingHom
Description: Local ring homomorphisms: ring maps f : R → S between local rings such that f(𝔪_R) ⊆ 𝔪_S (equivalently, f maps non-units to non-units). The induced map on residue fields. These are the morphisms in the category of local rings. Distinct from MaximalIdeal (the ideal) — this folder characterizes the correct notion of morphism between local rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Local ring homomorphisms.

Name: ResidueField
Description: The residue field of a local ring: the field R/𝔪 where 𝔪 is the maximal ideal, with the canonical projection R → R/𝔪. The residue characteristic (characteristic of R/𝔪), used to distinguish mixed characteristic (char R = 0, char R/𝔪 = p) from equal characteristic. Distinct from MaximalIdeal (the ideal itself) — this is the quotient field.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Residue field and ideals.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard residue field instances.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomials over the residue field.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Residue field `R / 𝔪`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Residue field definitions.

Name: Fiber.lean
Description: Fiber over the residue field.

Name: MaximalIdeal
Description: The maximal ideal of a local ring: the unique maximal ideal 𝔪 = {x | x not invertible}, characterized as the set of non-units. Nakayama's lemma: if M is a finitely generated module with 𝔪M = M then M = 0. Powers of the maximal ideal and the 𝔪-adic filtration. Distinct from ResidueField (the quotient R/𝔪) and RingHom (local homomorphisms).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Local ring maximal ideal definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Maximal ideal of a local ring.

Name: NonLocalRing.lean
Description: Non-local rings: conditions ensuring a ring is not local (e.g., it has more than one maximal ideal), and explicit counterexamples. Distinct from LocalRing/Basic.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Modules over local rings: projective ↔ free (every finitely generated projective module over a local ring is free), and the interaction with Nakayama's lemma. Distinct from LocalRing/Basic and Module/Projective.

Name: Subring.lean
Description: Subrings of local rings: conditions for a subring of a local ring to inherit the local ring structure. Distinct from LocalRing/LocalSubring and LocalRing/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotients of local rings: the quotient R/I of a local ring by a proper ideal I is a local ring with maximal ideal m/I. Distinct from LocalRing/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for local rings: LocalRing R and its equivalent characterisations (unique maximal ideal; nonunits form an ideal; 0 ≠ 1 and for every x, x or 1-x is a unit). Distinct from LocalRing/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic local ring theory: the LocalRing typeclass (unique maximal ideal), the residue field R/m, units are exactly elements not in m, and the interaction with localisation (localisation at a prime is local). Foundation for all LocalRing files.

Name: LocalSubring.lean
Description: Local subrings: a subring S ⊆ R of a local ring is local iff its maximal ideal is the contraction of the maximal ideal of R. Contains the localSubring typeclass. Distinct from LocalRing/Basic and LocalRing/Subring.

Name: Length.lean
Description: Length of modules over a local ring: the composition series length of a module of finite length over a Noetherian local ring, with the Krull-Schmidt theorem. Distinct from LocalRing/Basic and KrullDimension/LocalRing.

Name: Morita
Description: Morita theory: the equivalence of module categories for Morita-equivalent rings, with the characterization via progenerators.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Morita equivalence with matrix rings: R is Morita equivalent to Mₙ(R) via the progenerator Rⁿ (a free module of rank n), with the Morita context and the functor. Distinct from Morita/Basic and SimpleRing/Matrix.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Morita equivalence of rings: two rings R and S are Morita equivalent if their module categories Mod-R and Mod-S are equivalent, with the basic criterion (R is Morita equivalent to Mₙ(R) for all n). Foundation for Morita/Matrix.

Name: NonUnitalSubring
Description: Subrings of non-unital rings: the lattice of non-unital subrings and their generated subrings.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for non-unital subrings: the NonUnitalSubring R typeclass and its basic API. Distinct from NonUnitalSubring/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Non-unital subrings: subsets of a ring closed under addition, negation, and multiplication (not required to contain 1), with the induced non-unital ring structure and the lattice of non-unital subrings. Distinct from NonUnitalSubring/Defs.

Name: NonUnitalSubsemiring
Description: Subsemirings of non-unital semirings, including the generated subsemiring and lattice structure.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for non-unital subsemirings: the NonUnitalSubsemiring R typeclass. Distinct from NonUnitalSubsemiring/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Non-unital subsemirings: subsets closed under addition and multiplication, not required to contain 0 or 1, with the lattice structure. Distinct from NonUnitalSubsemiring/Defs.

Name: Nilpotent
Description: Nilpotent elements and ideals: the nilradical, reduced rings (no nilpotents), and properties preserved under nilpotent extensions.

Name: GeometricallyReduced.lean
Description: `IsGeometricallyReduced`: a `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced if `AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A` is reduced. `IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg`: it suffices to check finite-generated subalgebras. References Stacks tag 05DS.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for nilpotent elements: a unit + nilpotent is a unit; ring maps preserve nilpotents; the nil radical and prime radical interact correctly. Distinct from Nilpotent/Basic.

Name: Exp.lean
Description: The exponential of a nilpotent element: exp(x) = ∑ᵢ xⁱ/i! is well-defined (finite sum) when x is nilpotent in a ℚ-algebra, with the group property exp(x)exp(y) = exp(x+y) for commuting nilpotents. Distinct from Nilpotent/Basic and PowerSeries/Exp.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Nilpotent elements in a ring: IsNilpotent x (xⁿ = 0 for some n), the nil radical nilradical R = ∩ prime ideals, and the interaction with ring operations (sum of nilpotents is nilpotent in a commutative ring). Distinct from Nilpotent/Defs (the typeclass).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for nilpotent elements: the IsNilpotent predicate and the nilradical nilradical R. Distinct from Nilpotent/Basic (derived lemmas).

Name: Noetherian
Description: Noetherian rings and modules: the ascending chain condition, the Hilbert basis theorem, and finitely generated modules.

Name: OfPrime.lean
Description: Noetherian rings from prime ideal conditions: Eakin-Nagata theorem (if R ⊆ S with S Noetherian and a finitely generated R-module, then R is Noetherian), and variants. Distinct from Noetherian/Basic.

Name: Orzech.lean
Description: The Orzech property: a finitely generated module M over a Noetherian ring has the property that every surjective endomorphism is an isomorphism (a generalisation of Fitting's lemma). Distinct from Noetherian/Basic.

Name: UniqueFactorizationDomain.lean
Description: Noetherian UFDs: a Noetherian domain is a UFD iff every prime ideal contains a principal prime ideal (Kaplansky's theorem). Distinct from Noetherian/Basic and UniqueFactorizationDomain/Basic.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Nilpotence in Noetherian rings: the nil radical of a Noetherian ring is nilpotent (Nilradical^n = 0 for some n), by the ACC on ideals. Distinct from Noetherian/Basic and Nilpotent/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Noetherian rings and modules: the Hilbert basis theorem (R Noetherian → R[X] Noetherian), basic instances (PIDs, quotients, and localisations of Noetherian rings are Noetherian), and the interaction with finite generation. Distinct from Noetherian/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Noetherian structures: the IsNoetherian R M typeclass (ascending chain condition on submodules of M), and the equivalent conditions (every submodule is finitely generated). Distinct from Noetherian/Basic (derived results).

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Noetherian rings and ideal filters: the ascending chain condition on the ideal filter lattice and the interaction with the Noetherian property. Distinct from Noetherian/Defs and IdealFilter/Basic.

Name: TwoSidedIdeal
Description: Two-sided ideals in noncommutative rings: the lattice of two-sided ideals, quotient rings, and ring homomorphism kernels.

Name: Kernel.lean
Description: The kernel of a ring map as a two-sided ideal: ker(f) for a ring map f : R → S is a two-sided ideal, and the first isomorphism theorem R/ker(f) ≅ im(f). Distinct from TwoSidedIdeal/Basic and Ring/Equiv.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: The lattice of two-sided ideals: the complete lattice structure on TwoSidedIdeal R with meet (intersection) and join (sum of ideals), and the modular law. Distinct from TwoSidedIdeal/Basic and Ideal/Lattice.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on two-sided ideals: the product IJ, powers Iⁿ, and the interaction with the ring operations and quotient maps in the non-commutative setting. Distinct from TwoSidedIdeal/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Two-sided ideals in non-commutative rings: the TwoSidedIdeal R type (a sub-abelian-group closed under both left and right multiplication by ring elements), the quotient ring R/I, and the basic homomorphism theorems. Foundation for all TwoSidedIdeal files.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for two-sided ideals: products ∏ᵢ Iᵢ and sums ∑ᵢ Iᵢ of finite families of two-sided ideals, with the distributivity formula. Distinct from TwoSidedIdeal/Basic and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Instances for two-sided ideals: two-sided ideals in a commutative ring coincide with ordinary ideals; the zero ideal and the unit ideal as canonical instances; matrix ring two-sided ideals. Distinct from TwoSidedIdeal/Basic.

Name: UniqueFactorizationDomain
Description: Unique factorization domains (UFDs): irreducibility, the existence and uniqueness of prime factorizations, and the Gauss lemma.

Name: Moebius.lean
Description: The Möbius function in a UFD: the generalisation of μ : ℕ → {−1, 0, 1} to an arbitrary UFD — μ(u·∏ pᵢ) = (−1)ᵏ for a unit u and distinct primes pᵢ, 0 if any prime appears with multiplicity > 1. Contains the Möbius inversion formula. Distinct from UFD/Basic.

Name: Kaplansky.lean
Description: Kaplansky's theorem on UFDs: a domain R is a UFD iff every nonzero prime ideal contains a prime element, equivalently iff the ascending chain condition on principal ideals holds. Distinct from UFD/Basic and Noetherian/UniqueFactorizationDomain.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in UFDs: the principal ideals and the interaction between the UFD factorisation and the lattice of principal ideals (principal ideal domain ↔ UFD of dimension 1). Distinct from UFD/Basic and DedekindDomain/Factorization.

Name: Multiplicative.lean
Description: Multiplicative functions on UFDs: completely multiplicative functions f : R → M (with f(ab) = f(a)f(b)) and their interaction with the prime factorisation in a UFD. Distinct from UFD/Basic and UFD/Moebius.

Name: NormalizedFactors.lean
Description: Normalized factorisation in UFDs: the NormalizedGCDMonoid structure selecting a canonical representative for each associate class, with the normalizedFactors multiset and the interaction with GCDs and LCMs. Distinct from UFD/FactorSet and UFD/Basic.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: ℕ as a UFD: the UniqueFactorizationDomain instance for ℕ, the prime factorisation via Nat.factorization : ℕ →₀ ℕ (the finsupp of prime exponents), and the interaction with Nat.factors. Distinct from UFD/Basic and GCDMonoid/Nat.

Name: Multiplicity.lean
Description: The multiplicity of a prime in a UFD: emultiplicity p a = the exponent of p in the unique factorisation of a, with pⁿ ∣ a ↔ n ≤ emultiplicity p a and the interaction with GCDs. Distinct from UFD/FactorSet and UFD/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for UFDs: the UniqueFactorizationDomain typeclass and its formulations — every nonzero non-unit factors into primes; every irreducible is prime; the WFIrrFactors well-founded induction principle. Distinct from UFD/Basic.

Name: ClassGroup.lean
Description: The class group of a UFD: a UFD has trivial class group Cl(R) = 0 (every invertible fractional ideal is principal), and conversely. Contains the interaction with the Dedekind domain setting where Cl(R) measures the obstruction to unique factorisation. Distinct from UFD/Basic and DedekindDomain/PID.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic UFD theory: the UniqueFactorizationDomain typeclass instances (ℤ, k[X], PIDs are UFDs), the prime = irreducible equivalence in a UFD, and the interaction with GCDs and divisibility. Foundation for all UniqueFactorizationDomain files.

Name: FactorSet.lean
Description: Factor sets in UFDs: the multiset of prime factors Factorization.factors a ∈ Multiset (Associates R), with the interaction with divisibility (a ∣ b ↔ factors a ≤ factors b as multisets) and the GCDMonoid structure. Distinct from UFD/Basic and GCDMonoid/Multiset.

Name: GCDMonoid.lean
Description: UFDs are GCD monoids: a UniqueFactorizationDomain is a GCDMonoid with gcd(a, b) = ∏ pᵢ^min(eᵢ, fᵢ) and lcm(a, b) = ∏ pᵢ^max(eᵢ, fᵢ). Distinct from UFD/Basic and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finsupp representation of UFD factorisation: the prime factorisation as a finitely supported function factorization : Associates R →* (Associates R →₀ ℕ) sending each element to its prime factor exponents, with the group structure. Distinct from UFD/FactorSet and UFD/Basic.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite domains are fields: a finite integral domain is a field (hence a finite UFD is a field), and the interaction of UFD with finiteness conditions. Distinct from UFD/Basic and Field/IsField.

Name: TensorProduct
Description: Tensor products of algebras over a commutative ring: the universal bialgebra property and base change for ring maps.

Name: MonoidAlgebra.lean
Description: Tensor products of monoid algebras: the ring isomorphism k[G] ⊗_k k[H] ≅ k[G × H] and more generally MonoidAlgebra k G ⊗ MonoidAlgebra k H ≅ MonoidAlgebra k (G × H). Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and MonoidAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Maps between tensor products: the functoriality of ⊗_R in both arguments (given f : A → A' and g : B → B' there is f ⊗ g : A ⊗ B → A' ⊗ B'), the associativity and commutativity isomorphisms. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and TensorProduct/IsBaseChangeHom.

Name: IsBaseChangeRightExact.lean
Description: Right exactness of the tensor product: the functor A ⊗_R − is right exact (preserves right exact sequences), and the Tor functor Tor¹_R(A, −) measures the failure of left exactness. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Flat/Basic.

Name: MvPolynomial.lean
Description: Tensor products and multivariate polynomial rings: the isomorphism MvPolynomial σ R ⊗_R MvPolynomial τ R ≅ MvPolynomial (σ ⊕ τ) R, and more generally A ⊗_R B ≅ A ⊗ B when A and B are polynomial rings. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Tensor products with quotient algebras: the fundamental isomorphisms (R/I) ⊗_R M ≅ M/IM and (R/I) ⊗_R (R/J) ≅ R/(I + J). Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Tensor products of Pi algebras: the interaction of ⊗_R with Pi-indexed R-algebras and the canonical map ∏ᵢ (A ⊗_R Bᵢ) → A ⊗_R ∏ᵢ Bᵢ. Distinct from TensorProduct/IsBaseChangePi and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: Nontrivial.lean
Description: Nontriviality of tensor products of algebras: conditions ensuring A ⊗_R B ≠ 0 (e.g., when both A and B are nonzero and R → A is faithfully flat, or R is a field). Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: IsBaseChangePi.lean
Description: Base change for Pi algebras: the canonical isomorphism (∏ᵢ Aᵢ) ⊗_R B ≅ ∏ᵢ (Aᵢ ⊗_R B) when the product is finite. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Ring/Pi.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finiteness of tensor products: A ⊗_R B is finitely generated (resp. finitely presented) as an R-algebra when A and B are, with the associated dimension bounds. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Finiteness/Basic.

Name: DirectLimitFG.lean
Description: Tensor products and finitely generated direct limits: A ⊗_R (colim_{i : ι} Mᵢ) ≅ colim (A ⊗_R Mᵢ) when the Mᵢ are finitely generated and the system is directed. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Colimit/TensorProduct.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Tensor products of R-algebras: A ⊗_R B as an R-algebra (the ring structure on the tensor product of modules), with the universal property (pairs of R-algebra maps A → C and B → C with commuting images induce A ⊗_R B → C), and basic instances. Foundation for all TensorProduct files.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Tensor products of free modules and algebras: (Rᵐ) ⊗_R (Rⁿ) ≅ Rᵐⁿ, and the tensor product of free algebras R[Xᵢ] ⊗_R R[Yⱼ] ≅ R[Xᵢ, Yⱼ]. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: IsBaseChangeHom.lean
Description: The base change homomorphism: the canonical algebra map f_* : A ⊗_R B → C induced by algebra maps f : A → C and g : B → C with commuting images, and the universal property of tensor products. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic and TensorProduct/Maps.

Name: IsBaseChangeFree.lean
Description: Freeness preserved by base change: if M is a free R-module, then A ⊗_R M is a free A-module of the same rank (base change preserves freeness). Distinct from TensorProduct/Free and Flat/IsBaseChange.

Name: IncludeLeftSubRight.lean
Description: The left and right inclusions in a tensor product: the canonical ring maps A ↪ A ⊗_R B (a ↦ a ⊗ 1) and B ↪ A ⊗_R B (b ↦ 1 ⊗ b), and the subalgebras they generate. Distinct from TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: Trace
Description: The trace map in ring extensions: the linear map Tr_{L/K} defined as the trace of the multiplication operator.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Trace in quotient algebras: the interaction of Algebra.trace with the quotient map A → A/I and the induced trace on the quotient, used in the different ideal computation. Distinct from Trace/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definition of the algebraic trace: Algebra.trace R A x = Tr(Lₓ : A → A) where Lₓ(a) = xa is left multiplication, defined as the trace of the multiplication matrix. Distinct from Trace/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results on the algebraic trace: Algebra.trace R A : A →ₗ[R] R (additivity, R-linearity), the interaction with algebraMap (trace(r·1_A) = [A:R]·r), the trace form, and the connection to the norm (discriminant). Distinct from Trace/Defs and Norm/Basic.

Name: WittVector
Description: Witt vectors: a functorial ring construction providing the unique lift of characteristic-p rings to characteristic 0, central in p-adic Hodge theory.

Name: MulP.lean
Description: Multiplication by p in Witt vectors: the identity V(φ(x)) = p·x = φ(V(x)) relating Frobenius, Verschiebung, and multiplication by p, and the formula [p](x) = V(x) · ... in the p-typical formal group. Distinct from WittVector/Frobenius and WittVector/Verschiebung.

Name: StructurePolynomial.lean
Description: Witt structure polynomials: the polynomials Sₙ (for addition) and Pₙ (for multiplication) with integer coefficients satisfying wₙ(S₀,…,Sₙ) = wₙ(X₀,…,Xₙ) + wₙ(Y₀,…,Yₙ) and similarly for products. Used to define the ring operations in W(R). Distinct from WittVector/Defs and WittVector/Identities.

Name: MulCoeff.lean
Description: Coefficients of the Witt vector product: the explicit polynomial formula for the n-th coefficient of a · b in W(R) in terms of the components of a and b, derived from the Witt polynomial multiplication. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and WittVector/StructurePolynomial.

Name: Isocrystal.lean
Description: Isocrystals over a perfect field k: a finite-dimensional K₀-vector space (K₀ = Frac W(k)) equipped with a σ-semilinear automorphism φ, classified by the Newton polygon via the Dieudonné-Manin theorem. Distinct from WittVector/FrobeniusFractionField and WittVector/Basic.

Name: IsPoly.lean
Description: The IsPoly typeclass for Witt vector maps: a family of maps fₙ : W(R) → R is IsPoly if each fₙ is given by a polynomial in the components (a₀, a₁, …) of the input, ensuring functoriality. Used to verify that Frobenius, Verschiebung, and Teichmüller are natural. Distinct from WittVector/Basic.

Name: Verschiebung.lean
Description: The Verschiebung (shift map) on Witt vectors: the additive map V : W(R) → W(R) defined by V(a₀, a₁, a₂, …) = (0, a₀, a₁, …), satisfying FV = VF = p (where F is Frobenius) and the key identity V(φ(x)·y) = x·V(y). Distinct from WittVector/Frobenius and WittVector/MulP.

Name: WittPolynomial.lean
Description: The Witt polynomials: wₙ(X₀,…,Xₙ) = ∑ᵢ₌₀ⁿ pⁱ Xᵢ^(p^(n-i)) ∈ ℤ[X₀,…,Xₙ], with the integrality theorem (differences of sums/products of Witt polynomials are integer combinations of Witt polynomials), enabling the definition of W(R). Distinct from WittVector/Defs and WittVector/StructurePolynomial.

Name: Truncated.lean
Description: Truncated Witt vectors: Wₙ(R) = the ring of sequences (a₀, …, aₙ₋₁) with operations defined by the Witt polynomials w₀, …, wₙ₋₁, fitting into the projective system ⋯ → W₂(R) → W₁(R) → R. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and WittVector/InitTail.

Name: Teichmuller.lean
Description: The Teichmüller representative: the multiplicative section [·] : R → W(R) defined by [a] = (a, 0, 0, …), satisfying [ab] = [a]·[b], and providing the splitting W(R)/pW(R) ≅ R. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and WittVector/TeichmullerSeries.

Name: TeichmullerSeries.lean
Description: Teichmüller series: every element of W(𝔽_p) ≅ ℤₚ has a unique expansion ∑ₙ [aₙ] pⁿ in terms of Teichmüller representatives [aₙ] ∈ {0,…,p-1}, identifying W(𝔽_p) with ℤₚ. Distinct from WittVector/Teichmuller and WittVector/Compare.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for p-typical Witt vectors: the WittVector p R type (sequences in R indexed by ℕ) and the Witt polynomials wₙ = ∑ᵢ₌₀ⁿ pⁱ Xᵢ^(p^(n-i)) used to define the ring operations via ghost components. Distinct from WittVector/Basic.

Name: DiscreteValuationRing.lean
Description: Witt vectors as a discrete valuation ring: W(k) for a perfect field k is a DVR with uniformiser p and valuation vₚ(x) = the p-adic order, used as coefficient rings in p-adic Hodge theory. Distinct from WittVector/Complete and DiscreteValuationRing/Basic.

Name: Complete.lean
Description: Completeness of Witt vectors: W(k) for a perfect field k of characteristic p is a complete DVR with maximal ideal (p), residue field k, and fraction field of characteristic 0. This is the Cohen structure theorem for complete DVRs with perfect residue field. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and DiscreteValuationRing/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: p-typical Witt vectors: the ring W(R) = WittVector p R with the ring structure (addition and multiplication defined by the Witt polynomials), the ghost map, and the ring homomorphism W : CommRing → CommRing. Foundation for all WittVector files.

Name: Compare.lean
Description: Comparison of Witt vector constructions: the isomorphism between the p-typical Witt vectors W(R) and the truncated Witt vectors Wₙ(R) as a projective system, and the interaction with other ring constructions. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and WittVector/Truncated.

Name: Identities.lean
Description: Algebraic identities for Witt vectors: the key relations among Witt polynomials (Frobeniussatz, the interaction between ghost components and ring operations), used to verify the ring axioms for W(R). Distinct from WittVector/Basic and WittVector/StructurePolynomial.

Name: InitTail.lean
Description: Initial segment and tail of a Witt vector: the truncation map W(R) → Wₙ(R) keeping the first n components (init) and the tail starting at position n, with the splitting W(R) ≅ Wₙ(R) ×_{Wₙ(R)} W(R). Distinct from WittVector/Truncated and WittVector/Basic.

Name: FrobeniusFractionField.lean
Description: `WittVector.exists_frobenius_solution_fractionRing`: for an algebraically closed field `k` of characteristic `p` and `a`, `b` in the fraction field of `𝕎 k`, there exists a solution to `φ b * a = p ^ m * b` where `φ` is the Frobenius. Builds via `WittVector.frobeniusRotation` on `𝕎 k` before passing to the fraction field.

Name: Domain.lean
Description: Witt vectors as an integral domain: W(R) is an integral domain when R is an integral domain of characteristic p, with the p-adic valuation. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and WittVector/DiscreteValuationRing.

Name: Frobenius.lean
Description: The Frobenius endomorphism on Witt vectors: the ring homomorphism φ : W(R) → W(R) lifting the Frobenius of R, acting on ghost components as (w₀, w₁, …) ↦ (w₀^p, w₁^p, …). Contains the interaction with Verschiebung: φ ∘ V = p. Distinct from WittVector/Basic and CharP/Frobenius.

Name: ZMod
Description: The rings Z/nZ of integers modulo n: Chinese remainder theorem, units, characteristic, and Zeckendorf-style results.

Name: UnitsCyclic.lean
Description: The unit group of ZMod n: (ZMod n)ˣ ≅ (ℤ/nℤ)ˣ is cyclic iff n ∈ {1, 2, 4, pᵏ, 2pᵏ} for odd prime p, and is isomorphic to the multiplicative group of reduced residues modulo n with Euler's φ(n) elements. Distinct from ZMod/Torsion and CharP/CharAndCard.

Name: Torsion.lean
Description: Torsion theory for ZMod n-modules: every abelian group of exponent dividing n is a ZMod n-module, with the equivalence between ZMod n-modules and ℤ-modules annihilated by n. Distinct from ZMod/UnitsCyclic and CharP/Defs.

Name: Unramified
Description: Unramified ring maps: formally unramified morphisms, the module of Kahler differentials vanishing criterion, and etale as unramified plus flat.

Name: LocalStructure.lean
Description: Local structure of unramified maps: an unramified map of finite type is locally a quotient of an étale map by a nilpotent ideal (the local structure theorem for unramified maps). Distinct from Unramified/Basic and Etale/StandardEtale.

Name: LocalRing.lean
Description: Unramified maps and local rings: a formally unramified map f : R → A induces a separable extension of residue fields at each prime of A over a prime of R, and the localisation at a prime is unramified over the localisation at the lying-over prime. Distinct from Unramified/Basic and LocalRing/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Unramified structure on finite products: a finite product of formally unramified morphisms is formally unramified. Distinct from Unramified/Basic and Smooth/Pi.

Name: Locus.lean
Description: The unramified locus of a ring map: the open subset of Spec A where f : R → A is locally unramified, and its interaction with base change and the smooth locus. Distinct from Unramified/Basic and Smooth/Locus.

Name: Dedekind.lean
Description: Unramified extensions of Dedekind domains: a ring map between Dedekind domains is unramified iff for every prime P over p, the ramification index e(P|p) = 1 and the residue field extension is separable. Distinct from Unramified/Basic and DedekindDomain/Different.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Formally unramified ring maps: f : R → A is formally unramified if for every R-algebra T and square-zero ideal I ⊆ T, every R-algebra map A → T/I has at most one lift to A → T (equivalently ΩA/R = 0). Contains the Algebra.FormallyUnramified typeclass. Foundation for all Unramified files.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Unramified implies quasi-finite: every formally unramified map of finite type is quasi-finite (has finite fibers). Distinct from Unramified/Basic and QuasiFinite/Basic.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Unramified algebras over a field: a finite-type k-algebra is formally unramified iff it is a product of separable field extensions of k (equivalently étale over k). Distinct from Unramified/Basic and Etale/Field.

Name: Valuation
Description: Valuations on rings and fields: the value group, valuation rings, and the topology induced by a valuation.

Name: ValuativeRel
Description: Valuative relations and the valuative criterion: a morphism of schemes is separated iff it satisfies the valuative criterion for separatedness (unique lifting for DVR diagrams), and proper iff it satisfies the existence + uniqueness criterion. The ring-theoretic formulation of these criteria in terms of valuation rings. Distinct from Valuation/Discrete (specific DVR structure) — this folder encodes the valuative criterion as a ring property.

Name: Trivial.lean
Description: Trivial valuative relations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Valuative relations.

Name: Discrete
Description: Discrete valuations: valuations v : K× → ℤ (value group = ℤ), giving a discrete valuation ring (DVR) O_v = {x | v(x) ≥ 0}. The uniformizer π (generator of the maximal ideal), v(π) = 1. Every DVR arises this way. DVRs are the local rings of smooth points on algebraic curves. Distinct from the general Valuation folder (arbitrary ordered value group).

Name: RankOne.lean
Description: Rank-one valuations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Discrete valuations: `DiscreteValuation v`.

Name: Minpoly.lean
Description: Minimal polynomials and valuations: the Gauss norm of the minimal polynomial of an algebraic element x over a valued field (all conjugates of x have the same absolute value in the algebraic closure). Distinct from Valuation/Basic and RootsOfUnity/Minpoly.

Name: PrimeMultiplicity.lean
Description: The prime multiplicity as a valuation: the p-adic valuation vₚ on ℤ or ℚ (vₚ(n) = the exponent of p in the factorisation of n) as a Valuation instance, with the interaction with UFD factorisation. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and UFD/Multiplicity.

Name: IsTrivialOn.lean
Description: Trivial valuations on a subring: IsTrivialOn S v asserting v(s) = 1 for all nonzero s ∈ S (the valuation is trivial on S), used in the study of function fields and algebraic extensions. Distinct from Valuation/Basic.

Name: LocalSubring.lean
Description: The valuation subring as a local subring: O_v is a local ring with maximal ideal m_v and the associated local ring structure, with the interaction with the valuation topology. Distinct from Valuation/Integers and LocalRing/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Valuations on quotient rings: the valuation on R/I induced from a valuation on R that is trivial on I (v(i) = 1 for i ∈ I), with the interaction with the quotient topology. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: ValuationRing.lean
Description: Valuation rings: the ValuationRing typeclass for integral domains R where for every element x in the fraction field K, either x ∈ R or x⁻¹ ∈ R. Contains the equivalence with being the ring of integers of some valuation. Distinct from Valuation/Integers and Valuation/Basic.

Name: ValuationSubring.lean
Description: Valuation subrings of a field: the ValuationSubring K type for subrings R ⊆ K satisfying the valuation ring property, with the lattice structure (ordered by inclusion) and the interaction with the corresponding valuation. Distinct from Valuation/ValuationRing and Valuation/Integers.

Name: RamificationGroup.lean
Description: Ramification groups of a valuation subring: the decomposition subgroup and inertia subgroup within `ValuationSubring`. TODO: define higher ramification groups in lower numbering.

Name: RankOne.lean
Description: Rank-one valuations: a valuation is rank-one if its value group order-embeds into (ℝ, ≤), equivalently if the valuation ring is a DVR (for discrete valuations). Distinct from Valuation/Basic and DiscreteValuationRing/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Valuations on a ring: the Valuation R Γ₀ typeclass for maps v : R → Γ₀ (a linearly ordered commutative group with zero) satisfying v(xy) = v(x)v(y), v(x+y) ≤ max(v(x),v(y)), and v(0) = 0. Foundation for all Valuation files.

Name: DiscreteValuativeRel.lean
Description: Discrete valuative relations: valuative relations that have a maximal element less than one in the value group. In the rank-one case this is equivalent to the value group being isomorphic to `ℤˣ⁰` (the multiplicative integers).

Name: AlgebraInstances.lean
Description: Algebra instances for valuations: the interaction of a valuation v on R with algebra maps R → S, and the induced valuation on an algebra extension. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: Archimedean and non-Archimedean valuations: the Archimedean condition (ℕ is unbounded in the value group image) and the non-Archimedean condition (ultrametric inequality v(x+y) ≤ max(v(x),v(y))). Distinct from Valuation/Basic.

Name: ExtendToLocalization.lean
Description: Extending a valuation to a localization: given a valuation v on R and S = R[M⁻¹], the unique extension of v to S defined by v(a/s) = v(a)·v(s)⁻¹. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: Integers.lean
Description: The ring of integers of a valuation: the valuation subring O_v = {x ∈ K | v(x) ≤ 1} and its maximal ideal m_v = {x | v(x) < 1}, with the residue field O_v/m_v. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and Valuation/ValuationRing.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integral elements and valuations: x ∈ K is integral over O_v iff v(x) ≤ 1, connecting integrality with the valuation ring membership criterion. Distinct from Valuation/Integers and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extension of valuations: conditions under which a valuation v on R extends to a valuation on a ring extension S ⊇ R, and the interaction with the Chevalley extension theorem. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and Valuation/ExtendToLocalization.

Name: FiniteField.lean
Description: Valuations on finite fields: the only valuation on a finite field is the trivial valuation (v(x) = 1 for all x ≠ 0), since finite fields have no proper valuation rings. Distinct from Valuation/Basic and Field/ZMod.

Name: Spectrum
Description: The prime spectrum Spec(R): prime ideals as a topological space with the Zariski topology, and the structure sheaf.

Name: Prime
Description: The prime spectrum Spec(R): the set of prime ideals of R with the Zariski topology (closed sets = V(I) = {p | I ⊆ p}). Generic points, the Noetherian condition on Spec, and the functoriality Spec : CommRing^op → Top. The structure sheaf making Spec(R) an affine scheme. The algebraic foundation for AlgebraicGeometry/Spec. Distinct from Spectrum/Maximal (only maximal ideals).

Name: Module.lean
Description: Module sheaf on Spec R.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Noetherian rings and Spec.

Name: Jacobson.lean
Description: Jacobson radical and Spec R.

Name: LTSeries.lean
Description: Length of chains in Spec R (Krull dimension).

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Spec of tensor products.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Zariski topology on Spec R.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Spec of polynomial rings.

Name: RingHom.lean
Description: Ring homomorphisms and Spec.

Name: ChevalleyComplexity.lean
Description: Complexity of Chevalley's theorem.

Name: ConstructibleSet.lean
Description: Constructible sets in Spec R.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Prime spectrum `Spec R` API.

Name: Chevalley.lean
Description: Chevalley's theorem: constructible image of Spec.

Name: Homeomorph.lean
Description: Homeomorphisms of prime spectra.

Name: IsOpenComapC.lean
Description: Open sets via comap of continuous maps.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Spec R definitions.

Name: FreeLocus.lean
Description: Free locus in Spec R.

Name: Maximal
Description: The maximal spectrum MaxSpec(R): the subspace of Spec(R) consisting of maximal ideals with the Zariski topology. For a commutative ring, MaxSpec(R) classifies the simple R-modules up to isomorphism. The Jacobson radical is the intersection of all maximal ideals. In a Jacobson ring every prime is an intersection of maximal ideals so Spec = MaxSpec topologically. Distinct from Spectrum/Prime (the full prime spectrum).

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Maximal spectrum and localisation.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topology on the maximal spectrum.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Maximal spectrum `MaxSpec R`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Maximal spectrum definitions.

Name: RegularLocalRing
Description: Regular local rings: local rings whose maximal ideal is generated by dim R elements, the algebraic model of smooth points.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Regular local rings: the IsRegularLocalRing typeclass for Noetherian local rings satisfying dim R = μ(m) (Krull dimension = minimal number of generators of the maximal ideal), equivalently global dimension = Krull dimension. Distinct from KrullDimension/LocalRing and Regular/RegularSequence.

Name: RingHom
Description: Properties of ring homomorphisms (finite, finitely presented, flat, etale) organized as a modular predicate framework.

Name: LocallyStandardSmooth.lean
Description: Locally standard smooth ring maps: maps that are locally (after localisation) isomorphic to a standard smooth map R → R[X₁,…,Xₙ]/(f₁,…,fₘ) with an invertible Jacobian minor. Distinct from RingHom/Smooth and RingHom/StandardSmooth.

Name: OpenImmersion.lean
Description: `RingHom.IsStandardOpenImmersion`: a ring homomorphism that is a localization map away from some element. Also defines the equivalent `Algebra.IsStandardOpenImmersion`. Distinct from open immersions in algebraic geometry, which are the geometric counterpart.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integral ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Integral for maps f : R → A where every element of A is integral over f(R), with stability under composition and base change. Distinct from IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed and RingHom/Finite.

Name: Locally.lean
Description: Local properties of ring maps: the RingHom.Locally predicate for properties that can be checked after localisation at primes (the local-global principle for ring map properties). Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and RingHom/Finite.

Name: QuasiFinite.lean
Description: Quasi-finite ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.QuasiFinite packaging the quasi-finite condition, with stability under composition and localisation. Distinct from QuasiFinite/Basic and RingHom/Finite.

Name: Surjective.lean
Description: Surjective ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Surjective and the induced quotient ring isomorphism. Distinct from Ring/InjSurj and RingHom/Bijective.

Name: Unramified.lean
Description: Unramified ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Unramified for formally unramified maps (ΩA/R = 0), with stability and the interaction with étale. Distinct from Unramified/Basic and RingHom/Etale.

Name: Smooth.lean
Description: Smooth ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Smooth for formally smooth maps (liftings exist against square-zero extensions), with stability and the interaction with étale and unramified maps. Distinct from Smooth/Basic and RingHom/Etale.

Name: StandardSmooth.lean
Description: Standard smooth ring maps: maps R → R[X₁,…,Xₙ]/(f₁,…,fₘ) where the Jacobian matrix (∂fᵢ/∂Xⱼ) has an invertible m×m minor (giving a smooth map of relative dimension n-m). Distinct from RingHom/Smooth and Smooth/StandardSmooth.

Name: Etale.lean
Description: Étale ring maps as a ring hom morphism property: the RingHom.Etale predicate for the stable property of étale ring maps, with the interaction with composition and base change. Distinct from Etale/Basic and RingHom/Smooth.

Name: FaithfullyFlat.lean
Description: Faithfully flat ring maps: f : R → S is faithfully flat if S is flat as an R-module and the map Spec S → Spec R is surjective, with descent of exactness. Distinct from Flat/Basic and RingHom/Flat.

Name: Bijective.lean
Description: Bijective ring maps: conditions for a ring homomorphism to be bijective (injective + surjective) and the induced ring isomorphism. Distinct from RingHom/Injective and RingHom/Surjective.

Name: EssFiniteType.lean
Description: Essentially of finite type ring maps: a ring map is essentially of finite type if it is a localisation of a finite-type map, with the stability properties. Distinct from RingHom/FiniteType and Localization/Basic.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Finite for maps f : R → A such that A is a finitely generated R-module, with stability under composition and base change. Distinct from Finiteness/Defs and RingHom/FiniteType.

Name: Flat.lean
Description: Flat ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Flat for maps f : R → A where A is flat as an R-module, with stability under composition and base change. Distinct from Flat/Basic and RingHom/FaithfullyFlat.

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Injective ring maps as a morphism property: RingHom.Injective for injective ring homomorphisms, with the stability under composition and base change. Distinct from RingHom/Bijective and Ring/InjSurj.

Name: FinitePresentation.lean
Description: Ring maps of finite presentation as a morphism property: RingHom.FinitePresentation for f : R → A ≅ R[X₁,…,Xₙ]/(f₁,…,fₘ), with the stability under composition and localisation. Distinct from Finiteness/Defs and RingHom/FiniteType.

Name: FiniteType.lean
Description: Ring maps of finite type as a morphism property: RingHom.FiniteType for finitely generated algebra maps, with stability under composition, base change, and localisation. Distinct from Finiteness/Defs and RingHom/Finite.

Name: RamificationInertia
Description: Ramification and inertia for ring extensions, especially in the context of Dedekind domains and local rings.

Name: Ramification.lean
Description: `Ideal.ramificationIdx' q R`: the ramification index of a prime `q` of `S` over `R`, defined as the length of the quotient `Sq/pSq` as an `Sq`-module (where `Sq` is the localization of `S` at `q` and `p` is the preimage in `R`). Distinct from the classical definition via ideal factorization.

Name: Inertia.lean
Description: `Ideal.inertiaDeg' q R`: the inertia degree of a prime `q` over `R`, defined as the degree of the residue field of `q` over the residue field of its preimage `p` in `R`. `inertiaDeg_eq_inertiaDeg'`: agrees with the classical definition for maximal ideals. `inertiaDeg'_tower`: multiplicativity in towers.

Name: Regular
Description: Regular elements and regular sequences in rings: non-zero-divisors, regular rings (all localizations are regular local), and depth.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: `Hom(N, M)` is subsingleton iff there exists an `IsSMulRegular M r` element `r` in `ann(N)`. Used for depth computations: the condition holds when `Depth[I](M) = 0`. Proves this characterization and related API for smul-regular linear maps.

Name: ProjectiveDimension.lean
Description: For `M` a finitely generated module over a Noetherian local ring `R` and an `M`-regular element `x` in the maximal ideal, `projdim(M/xM) = projdim(M) + 1`. The analogous version for quotients by regular sequences is also provided.

Name: RegularSequence.lean
Description: Regular sequences: a sequence a₁, …, aₙ in R is M-regular if each aᵢ is a non-zero-divisor on M/(a₁,…,aᵢ₋₁)M, and this M/(a₁,…,aₙ)M ≠ 0. Foundation for depth theory and Cohen-Macaulay rings. Distinct from Regular/Depth and Regular/IsSMulRegular.

Name: IsSMulRegular.lean
Description: Regular elements for scalar multiplication: IsSMulRegular M a asserting that a acts injectively on M (a is M-regular), with the interaction with regular sequences and M-regular elements. Distinct from Regular/RegularSequence and Algebra/Regular/Defs.

Name: Category.lean
Description: Categorical constructions for `IsSMulRegular`: `LinearMap.exact_smul_id_smul_top_mkQ` proves exactness of `r • id : M → M` followed by the quotient `M → M / (r • ⊤)`, and related lemmas in `ModuleCat`.

Name: Depth.lean
Description: Deprecated module (since 2026-04-28). Depth API has been moved to `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.LinearMap`.

Name: Flat.lean
Description: Regular sequences and flat base change: if a₁, …, aₙ is an M-regular sequence and S is a flat R-algebra, then a₁, …, aₙ is an (M ⊗_R S)-regular sequence. Distinct from Regular/RegularSequence and Flat/Basic.

Name: SimpleRing
Description: Simple rings (nonzero rings with no proper two-sided ideals): the Artin-Wedderburn theorem and division rings.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Simple commutative rings are fields: a nonzero commutative simple ring is a field (every nonzero element is a unit). Distinct from SimpleRing/Basic and Field/IsField.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix rings are simple: Mₙ(D) for any division ring D is a simple ring. Contains the theorem and the interaction with the Morita equivalence Mₙ(D) ~ D. Distinct from SimpleRing/Basic and Morita/Matrix.

Name: Principal.lean
