Description: Simple rings and principal ideals: in a simple ring, every one-sided ideal is principal (generated by an idempotent element via the Wedderburn structure). Distinct from SimpleRing/Basic and Ideal/IsPrincipal.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of simple rings: the IsSimpleRing typeclass, basic instances, the interaction with Artinian rings (simple Artinian rings satisfy Wedderburn-Artin), and the relation to the Jacobson radical. Foundation for all SimpleRing files. Distinct from SimpleRing/Defs.

Name: Congr.lean
Description: Congruences on simple rings: the only ring congruences on a simple ring are the trivial congruence (equality) and the total congruence (all elements equivalent). Distinct from SimpleRing/Basic and Congruence/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definition of simple rings: IsSimpleRing R asserting R has no proper two-sided ideals other than 0 and R, with the basic API. Distinct from SimpleRing/Basic (derived results).

Name: Smooth
Description: Formally smooth ring maps: the lifting criterion, connection to the cotangent complex, and smoothness in algebraic geometry.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Smooth structure on finite products: a finite product of formally smooth morphisms is formally smooth. Distinct from Smooth/Basic and Etale/Pi.

Name: NoetherianDescent.lean
Description: Smooth algebras have Noetherian models: if `S` is a smooth `R`-algebra, there exists a finite-type `ℤ`-subalgebra `R₀` and a smooth `R₀`-algebra `S₀` such that `S ≃ₐ R ⊗[R₀] S₀`. The analogous result for étale algebras is also provided.

Name: Locus.lean
Description: The smooth locus of a ring map: the open subset of Spec A where f : R → A is locally smooth (the smooth locus), with its interaction with base change and the étale locus. Distinct from Smooth/Basic and Etale/Locus.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Formal smoothness under quotient: for a flat ring homomorphism `f : R →+* S` and a square-zero ideal `I` of `R`, if `R ⧸ I →+* S ⧸ IS` is formally smooth then `f` is formally smooth. Formalizes Stacks 031L.

Name: StandardSmoothOfFree.lean
Description: `IsStandardSmooth` iff `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S/R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`. This is a presentation-independent characterization. Also deduces relations between standard smooth and other local properties.

Name: StandardSmoothCotangent.lean
Description: Cotangent module of standard smooth maps: for a standard smooth map R → R[X₁,…,Xₙ]/(f₁,…,fₘ), the module of Kähler differentials ΩA/R is locally free of rank n-m. Distinct from Smooth/StandardSmooth and Kaehler/Basic.

Name: StandardSmooth.lean
Description: Standard smooth ring maps: the explicit smooth presentations R → R[X₁,…,Xₙ]/(f₁,…,fₘ) with an invertible m×m Jacobian minor, with the universal property and the relationship to étale maps (m = 0 gives étale). Distinct from Smooth/Basic and RingHom/StandardSmooth.

Name: Local.lean
Description: Formally smooth local algebras: key results about formal smoothness for local algebras, including that a formally smooth local algebra has free Kähler differentials and that the converse holds under finite-presentation assumptions.

Name: Fiber.lean
Description: Fibers of smooth ring maps: the fibers A ⊗_R k(p) of a smooth map f : R → A are smooth over the residue field k(p), and geometric regularity follows. Distinct from Smooth/Basic and Smooth/Field.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Formally smooth ring maps: f : R → A is formally smooth if for every R-algebra T and square-zero ideal I ⊆ T (I² = 0), every R-algebra map A → T/I lifts to A → T. Contains the Algebra.FormallySmooth typeclass and basic properties. Foundation for all Smooth files.

Name: AdicCompletion.lean
Description: Formally smooth algebras and adic completion: for a formally smooth `R`-algebra `A`, any algebra map `A →ₐ[R] S ⧸ I` lifts to a map `A →ₐ[R] S` when `S` is `I`-adically complete. Used in proving smoothness implies flatness over Noetherian rings.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Smooth ring maps over a field: a finite-type k-algebra A is smooth over k iff it is geometrically regular (all base changes A ⊗_k K are regular for fields K/k). Distinct from Smooth/Basic and KrullDimension/Regular.

Name: Kaehler.lean
Description: Smooth maps and Kähler differentials: f : R → A is smooth iff A is flat over R and ΩA/R is a locally free A-module of the correct rank (the Kähler criterion for formal smoothness). Distinct from Smooth/Basic and Kaehler/Basic.

Name: IntegralClosure.lean
Description: Smooth base change commutes with integral closure: `TensorProduct.toIntegralClosure : S ⊗[R] integralClosure R B →ₐ integralClosure S (S ⊗[R] B)` is bijective when `S` is `R`-smooth. Injective for flat `S`, bijective for polynomial and localization cases.

Name: Flat.lean
Description: Smooth implies flat: every formally smooth ring map of finite presentation is flat. Contains the proof and the interaction with the structure theorem. Distinct from Smooth/Basic and Flat/Basic.

Name: RootsOfUnity
Description: Roots of unity in rings and fields: cyclotomic polynomials, the structure of the group of nth roots, and primitive roots.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for roots of unity: the cyclotomic polynomial factorisation ∏_{d|n} Φ_d(X) = Xⁿ - 1, the order of a root of unity, and the interaction with the characteristic of a ring. Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic and RootsOfUnity/Minpoly.

Name: EnoughRootsOfUnity.lean
Description: The EnoughRootsOfUnity typeclass: asserts that a ring R contains all n-th roots of unity for n in a specified set (e.g., all n-th roots for n dividing some fixed N). Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic and RootsOfUnity/AlgebraicallyClosed.

Name: PrimitiveRoots.lean
Description: Primitive roots of unity in fields: conditions for the existence of primitive n-th roots (the group of n-th roots is cyclic of order n iff char ∤ n), and the primitive root theorem for finite fields. Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic and RootsOfUnity/CyclotomicUnits.

Name: Minpoly.lean
Description: The minimal polynomial of a primitive n-th root of unity: minpoly ℚ ζₙ = Φₙ (the n-th cyclotomic polynomial) of degree φ(n), with the irreducibility of Φₙ over ℚ. Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic and RootsOfUnity/PrimitiveRoots.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Roots of unity in a commutative ring: IsPrimitiveRoot ζ n asserting ζ is a primitive n-th root of unity (ζⁿ = 1 and ζᵏ ≠ 1 for 0 < k < n), the group μₙ(R) of n-th roots, and the orderOf-based characterisation. Foundation for all RootsOfUnity files.

Name: AlgebraicallyClosed.lean
Description: Roots of unity in algebraically closed fields: every algebraically closed field of characteristic 0 (or char ∤ n) contains primitive n-th roots of unity, with the explicit existence result. Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic and RootsOfUnity/Complex.

Name: CyclotomicUnits.lean
Description: Cyclotomic units: the elements (1 - ζᵏ) ∈ ℤ[ζₙ]ˣ for a primitive n-th root ζₙ and (k, n) = 1, forming a subgroup of the unit group of the cyclotomic ring. Used in the analytic class number formula and Iwasawa theory. Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Roots of unity in ℂ: the explicit primitive n-th root e^(2πi/n), the group isomorphism μₙ(ℂ) ≅ ZMod n, and the interaction with the circle group. Distinct from RootsOfUnity/Basic and RootsOfUnity/AlgebraicallyClosed.

Name: SimpleModule
Description: Simple modules (nonzero modules with no proper submodules): Schur's lemma, semisimple modules, and the Jordan-Hölder theorem.

Name: Isotypic.lean
Description: Isotypic components of a module: the isotypic component M_(S) corresponding to a simple module S is the sum of all submodules of M isomorphic to S, with the decomposition M ≅ ⊕_S M_(S) for semisimple M. Distinct from SimpleModule/Basic and SimpleModule/WedderburnArtin.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank of simple modules: a simple module over a field is 1-dimensional; the multiplicity of a simple module S in a semisimple module M equals dim Hom_R(S, M) / dim End_R(S). Distinct from SimpleModule/Isotypic and SimpleModule/Basic.

Name: WedderburnArtin.lean
Description: The Wedderburn-Artin theorem: a semisimple ring is isomorphic to a finite product of matrix rings ∏ᵢ Mₙᵢ(Dᵢ) over division rings Dᵢ. Contains the decomposition and the uniqueness up to reordering and isomorphism. Distinct from SimpleModule/Isotypic and SimpleRing/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simple modules: a nonzero module M is simple (IsSimpleModule R M) if its only submodules are 0 and M. Contains Schur's lemma (End_R(M) is a division ring for simple M), the characterisation as irreducible representations, and basic instances. Foundation for all SimpleModule files.

Name: InjectiveProjective.lean
Description: Simple modules and injective/projective: over a semisimple ring, every simple module is both projective (a direct summand of the regular module) and injective (the socle coincides with the module). Distinct from SimpleModule/Basic and Module/Projective.

Name: IsAlgClosed.lean
Description: Simple modules over algebras over algebraically closed fields: over an algebraically closed field k, every simple module over a finite-dimensional k-algebra is 1-dimensional (Burnside's theorem). Distinct from SimpleModule/Basic and RootsOfUnity/AlgebraicallyClosed.

Name: DiscreteValuationRing
Description: Discrete valuation rings (DVRs): PIDs that are also local rings, with the uniformizer generating the maximal ideal.

Name: TFAE.lean
Description: TFAE characterisations of DVRs: a Noetherian local domain is a DVR iff it is a PID iff it is integrally closed of dimension 1 iff its maximal ideal is principal. Distinct from DiscreteValuationRing/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Discrete valuation rings: the IsDiscreteValuationRing typeclass (a local PID with a unique nonzero prime ideal), with basic instances, the uniformiser, and the valuation v : K → ℤ ∪ {∞}. Distinct from DiscreteValuationRing/TFAE and Valuation/Basic.

Name: DividedPowerAlgebra
Description: Divided power algebras: rings with operations gamma_n(x) = x^n/n! defined formally, used in crystalline cohomology.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation of the divided power algebra: the DividedPowerAlgebra R M for an R-module M, with generators γₙ(x) (formal divided powers x^[n]) satisfying γₙ(x)γₘ(x) = C(n+m,n) γₙ₊ₘ(x) and γₙ(x+y) = ∑ᵢ₊ⱼ₌ₙ γᵢ(x)γⱼ(y). Distinct from DividedPowers/Basic.

Name: DedekindDomain
Description: Dedekind domains: integral domains where every nonzero ideal factors uniquely into prime ideals, the algebraic setting for rings of integers.

Name: Ideal
Description: Ideals in Dedekind domains: the unique factorization of every nonzero ideal into prime ideals, the group of fractional ideals under multiplication, the ideal class group as the quotient by principal ideals, and the norm of an ideal. The Chinese remainder theorem for coprime ideals in a Dedekind domain. Distinct from the top-level DedekindDomain folder (abstract definition) — this subfolder develops the full ideal arithmetic.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Dedekind domain ideal lemmas.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ideal theory in Dedekind domains.

Name: LinearDisjoint.lean
Description: Disjoint extensions with coprime different ideals: for a finite extension of Dedekind domains with linearly disjoint sub-extensions `R₁`, `R₂` over `A` having coprime differents, proves `𝔡(B/R₁) = 𝔡(R₂/A)`, `𝔡(B/A) = 𝔡(R₁/A) * 𝔡(R₂/A)`, and constructs an `R₁`-basis of `B` from an `A`-basis of `R₂`.

Name: IntegralClosure.lean
Description: Integral closure in Dedekind domain extensions: the integral closure of a Dedekind domain in a finite separable extension of its fraction field is Dedekind. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Instances for Dedekind domains: PIDs are Dedekind, the ring of integers of a number field is Dedekind (used as axiom), and the interaction with class groups and localizations. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic.

Name: SInteger.lean
Description: S-integers in a Dedekind domain: elements of the fraction field K integral at all primes not in a finite set S, forming a Dedekind domain Oₛ ⊆ K. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: SelmerGroup.lean
Description: The Selmer group of a Dedekind domain: the S-class group (removing the primes in S from the class group), used in the arithmetic of elliptic curves and Galois cohomology. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and DedekindDomain/SInteger.

Name: PID.lean
Description: PIDs as Dedekind domains and the class group: a Dedekind domain is a PID iff its class group is trivial, with the class group Cl(R) = FractionalIdeal/principal ideals. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and FractionalIdeal/Basic.

Name: GaussLemma.lean
Description: Gauss's lemma for Dedekind domains: the content of a product of polynomials equals the product of contents (for polynomials over a Dedekind domain), used to prove factorisation in K[X] implies factorisation in R[X]. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and Polynomial/GaussLemma.

Name: Different.lean
Description: The different ideal of a Dedekind domain extension: 𝔡_{B/A} as the inverse of the codifferent (the dual of the ring of integers), with the connection to ramification (p ramifies iff p ∣ 𝔡). Distinct from RamificationInertia/Ramification and DedekindDomain/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Dedekind domains: the IsDedekindDomain typeclass (Noetherian, integrally closed, Krull dimension ≤ 1), with basic instances and the interaction with the class group. Foundation for all DedekindDomain files.

Name: AdicValuation.lean
Description: The adic valuation on a Dedekind domain: the v_p-adic valuation at a nonzero prime p, with the product formula and the interaction with the factorisation of ideals. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Factorization and Valuation/Basic.

Name: FiniteAdeleRing.lean
Description: The finite adele ring of a Dedekind domain: the restricted product ∏'_p K_p of p-adic completions of the fraction field K, with the ring structure and topology. Distinct from DedekindDomain/AdicValuation and HahnSeries/Valuation.

Name: Factorization.lean
Description: Unique factorisation of ideals in Dedekind domains: every nonzero ideal factors uniquely as a product of prime ideals ∏ pᵢ^eᵢ, with the prime factorisation algorithm. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and UniqueFactorizationDomain/Basic.

Name: Dvr.lean
Description: Dedekind domains and discrete valuation rings: the localization of a Dedekind domain at a nonzero prime is a DVR, with the interaction between the ideal theory and the DVR structure. Distinct from DedekindDomain/Basic and DiscreteValuationRing/Basic.

Name: Derivation
Description: Derivations on rings: the module of derivations, the Leibniz rule, and connections to Kahler differentials.

Name: MapCoeffs.lean
Description: Derivations and coefficient maps: how derivations interact with the evaluation of polynomials (the chain rule D(f(a)) = f'(a) D(a) for polynomial f). Distinct from Derivation/Basic and Polynomial/Derivative.

Name: ToSquareZero.lean
Description: Derivations and square-zero extensions: the bijection between derivations D : A → M and algebra sections of the projection A ⋉ M → A, giving the universal square-zero extension. Distinct from Derivation/Basic and TrivSqZeroExt/Basic.

Name: Lie.lean
Description: The Lie algebra of derivations: the commutator [D₁, D₂] = D₁ ∘ D₂ - D₂ ∘ D₁ makes Der_R(A) into a Lie R-algebra. Distinct from Derivation/Basic and Algebra/Lie/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Derivations of algebras: Derivation R A M for R-linear maps D : A → M satisfying D(ab) = aD(b) + D(a)b, with the A-module structure on derivations and the interaction with ring maps. Foundation for all Derivation files.

Name: DifferentialRing.lean
Description: Differential rings: rings R equipped with a derivation ∂ : R → R (a ring endomorphism satisfying the Leibniz rule), the differential ring typeclass, and basic instances. Distinct from Derivation/Basic and Kaehler/Basic.

Name: Extension
Description: Ring extensions: integral extensions, the going-up and going-down theorems, and finite versus finitely generated extensions.

Name: Presentation
Description: Presentations of ring extensions by generators and relations: a surjection A[x₁,...,xₙ] → B with kernel I (the ideal of relations), the module of Kähler differentials as I/I², and the regularity criterion (B is smooth over A iff the presentation is regular). Distinct from Extension/Cotangent (derived/higher-order information) — this is the explicit generators-and-relations picture.

Name: Submersive.lean
Description: Submersive presentations.

Name: Core.lean
Description: Core presentation data.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Algebra presentations from ring extensions.

Name: Cotangent
Description: The cotangent complex and cotangent space of a ring map: the module of Kähler differentials Ω_{B/A} and the first step of the cotangent complex L_{B/A}. The exact triangle for a composition A → B → C of ring maps. Used to detect smoothness (Ω_{B/A} = 0 and L_{B/A} acyclic) and to study deformation theory. Distinct from Extension/Presentation (generators and relations, not the cotangent complex).

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free cotangent modules.

Name: LocalizationAway.lean
Description: Cotangent module under localisation.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Basis for the cotangent module.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change of the cotangent complex.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cotangent module `Ω_{A/R}` via the extension.

Name: Generators.lean
Description: `Algebra.Generators`: a family of generators of an `R`-algebra `S` consisting of a type `vars`, a value map `val : vars → S`, and a set-theoretic section `σ` of the surjection `R[vars] → S`. The foundation for presentations of algebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `Algebra.Extension`: an extension of an `R`-algebra `S` is an `R`-algebra `P` together with a surjection `P →ₐ[R] S`. `Algebra.Extension.Hom`: morphisms between extensions forming a commutative square. Foundation for cotangent complex theory.

Name: FilteredAlgebra
Description: Filtered algebras and associated graded rings: filtrations by ideals and the Rees algebra construction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `IsFiltration`: for a family of subsets `σ` of an abelian group `A`, an increasing series `F` in `σ` is a filtration if there is a companion series `F_lt` equal to the supremum of `F` with smaller index. `IsRingFiltration`: a ring filtration compatible with multiplication. Covers filtered groups, rings, and modules.

Name: DividedPowers
Description: Divided power structures: the divided power hull of an ideal and the divided power envelope, used in deformation theory.

Name: RatAlgebra.lean
Description: Divided powers in ℚ-algebras: in a ℚ-algebra, γₙ(x) = xⁿ/n! is the unique divided power structure on any ideal, and any ℚ-algebra carries divided powers. Distinct from DividedPowers/Basic and DividedPowers/Padic.

Name: SubDPIdeal.lean
Description: Sub-divided-power ideals: a sub-ideal J ⊆ I compatible with the divided power structure (γₙ(J) ⊆ J for all n ≥ 1), with the lattice of sub-DP-ideals. Distinct from DividedPowers/Basic.

Name: Padic.lean
Description: Divided powers in p-adic rings: the canonical divided power structure on the ideal pℤₚ of the p-adic integers (γₙ(pᵉx) = pᵉⁿxⁿ/n!), providing the motivating example. Distinct from DividedPowers/Basic and DividedPowers/RatAlgebra.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Divided power structures on ideals: the DividedPowers I typeclass equipping an ideal I ⊆ R with maps γₙ : I → R satisfying the divided power axioms (γₙ(x)γₘ(x) = C(n+m,n)γₙ₊ₘ(x), γ₁ = id, γ₀(x) = 1). Foundation for all DividedPowers files.

Name: DPMorphism.lean
Description: Morphisms of divided power structures: ring maps f : R → S compatible with divided powers (f(γₙ(x)) = γₙ(f(x))), with the category of divided power algebras. Distinct from DividedPowers/Basic.

Name: Etale
Description: Etale ring maps: formally etale = formally unramified + formally smooth, with the standard etale topology in algebraic geometry.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Étale structure on finite products: a finite product of formally étale morphisms is formally étale. Distinct from Etale/Basic and Algebra/Pi.

Name: Locus.lean
Description: The étale locus of a ring map: the open subset of Spec A where f : R → A is locally étale, with its interaction with base change. Distinct from Etale/Basic and Smooth/Locus.

Name: StandardEtale.lean
Description: Standard étale morphisms: étale maps of the form R → R[X]/(f) for a separable polynomial f (f'(x) is a unit in R[X]/(f)), with the associated universal property. Distinct from Etale/Basic and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: QuasiFinite.lean
Description: Étale implies quasi-finite: every étale ring map has finite fibers (is quasi-finite), via the structure theorem for étale algebras. Distinct from Etale/Basic and QuasiFinite/Basic.

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Descent for étale morphisms: étale properties satisfy faithfully flat descent — if A → B is faithfully flat and B → C is étale, then A → C is étale under appropriate conditions. Distinct from Etale/Basic and Flat/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Formally étale ring maps: a ring map f : R → A is formally étale if it is formally unramified (ΩA/R = 0) and formally smooth (lifts exist). Foundation for all Etale files.

Name: Kaehler.lean
Description: Étale maps and Kähler differentials: f : R → A is formally étale iff ΩA/R = 0 and A is formally smooth over R; the Kähler-differential criterion. Distinct from Etale/Basic and Kaehler/Basic.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Étale algebras over a field: a finite k-algebra is étale iff it is a product of separable field extensions of k. Distinct from Etale/Basic and Algebraic/Basic.

Name: Coprime
Description: Coprime elements and ideals: the Chinese remainder theorem, coprimality in UFDs, and Bezout's identity.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for coprime elements: coprimeness of products and powers (IsCoprime a b → IsCoprime a (b^n)), the interaction with primes, and the coprimeness criterion via GCDs. Distinct from Coprime/Basic.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Coprime ideals and the Chinese Remainder Theorem: two ideals I and J are coprime if I + J = R, with the CRT isomorphism R/(I ∩ J) ≅ R/I × R/J for pairwise coprime ideals. Distinct from Coprime/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Coprime elements in a commutative ring: IsCoprime a b asserting ∃ u v, u*a + v*b = 1, with basic properties — coprime pairs, symmetry, and the interaction with units. Foundation for all Coprime files.

Name: Algebraic
Description: Algebraic elements over a subring: minimal polynomials, algebraic extensions, and the algebraic closure.

Name: MvPolynomial.lean
Description: Algebraic elements in multivariate polynomial rings: when an element of MvPolynomial σ R is algebraic over R. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic and MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: LinearIndependent.lean
Description: Algebraic independence and linear independence: a set that is algebraically dependent contains elements that are algebraic over the ring generated by the others. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic and AlgebraicIndependent/Basic.

Name: StronglyTranscendental.lean
Description: Strongly transcendental elements: x is strongly transcendental over R if it remains transcendental over any purely transcendental sub-extension of R. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic and AlgebraicIndependent/Transcendental.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Algebraic conditions on Pi algebras: componentwise algebraic conditions for Pi-type algebra structures. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic and Algebra/Pi.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of algebraic extensions: the cardinality of the algebraic closure of a field, and bounds on |K̄| in terms of |K|. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic and AlgebraicIndependent/RankAndCardinality.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Algebraic elements over a ring: IsAlgebraic R x asserting that x satisfies a nonzero polynomial over R, with the basic properties (algebraic over a field ↔ integral for finite extensions). Foundation for all Algebraic files.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Algebraic vs integral elements: in an extension of fields, algebraic elements are integral over the algebraic closure. Contains the IsAlgebraic.isIntegral result. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for algebraic and transcendental elements: the IsAlgebraic R x and IsTranscendental R x predicates. Distinct from Algebraic/Basic (derived results).

Name: AlgebraicIndependent
Description: Algebraically independent sets and transcendence bases: the transcendence degree of a field extension.

Name: RankAndCardinality.lean
Description: Rank and cardinality of transcendence bases: all transcendence bases of a field extension K/k have the same cardinality (the transcendence degree tr.deg(K/k)). Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/TranscendenceBasis.

Name: TranscendenceBasis.lean
Description: Transcendence bases: a maximal algebraically independent set (transcendence basis) exists by Zorn's lemma, and any two transcendence bases have the same cardinality. Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/RankAndCardinality.

Name: Transcendental.lean
Description: Algebraically independent elements are transcendental: every element of an algebraically independent set is transcendental over the ring generated by the remaining elements. Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/Basic and Algebraic/StronglyTranscendental.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definition of algebraic independence: AlgebraicIndependent R v asserting that the evaluation map MvPolynomial ι R → A at v is injective. Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/Basic (derived results).

Name: Adjoin.lean
Description: Algebraic independence and adjunction: an algebraically independent set {xᵢ} over R gives rise to an isomorphism Algebra.adjoin R {xᵢ} ≅ MvPolynomial ι R. Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/Basic and Adjoin/Basic.

Name: AlgebraicClosure.lean
Description: Algebraic independence in algebraic closures: every algebraically independent set extends to a transcendence basis, and the algebraic closure of a purely transcendental extension is computable. Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/TranscendenceBasis.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results on algebraically independent sets: closure under subsets, the polynomial map criterion (the induced map R[Xᵢ] → A is injective iff the set is algebraically independent), and basic instances. Distinct from AlgebraicIndependent/Defs.

Name: AdicCompletion
Description: I-adic completions of rings: the inverse limit construction, completion of Z at a prime giving Z_p, and the Cohen structure theorem.

Name: LocalRing.lean
Description: `isLocalRing_of_isAdicComplete_maximal`: a commutative ring that is adic complete with respect to a maximal ideal is a local ring. Proves uniqueness of the maximal ideal from the completeness assumption.

Name: Functoriality.lean
Description: Functoriality of adic completion: a ring map f : R → S with f(I) ⊆ J induces a map R̂_I → Ŝ_J on completions. Distinct from AdicCompletion/Basic and AdicCompletion/RingHom.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: `IsHausdorff.of_le_jacobson`: for a Noetherian ring `R`, finite module `M`, and ideal `I` contained in the Jacobson radical of `R`, the `I`-adic topology on `M` is Hausdorff. Proves the Krull intersection theorem consequence.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: The I-adic topology on a ring R: the ring topology defined by the filtration {Iⁿ} as a neighborhood basis of 0, and the interaction with the completion. Distinct from AdicCompletion/Basic and AdicCompletion/Completeness.

Name: RingHom.lean
Description: Ring homomorphisms and adic completions: the interaction of ring maps with the adic completion functor and conditions for the completion map to be an isomorphism. Distinct from AdicCompletion/Functoriality and AdicCompletion/Basic.

Name: AsTensorProduct.lean
Description: The adic completion as a tensor product: R̂_I ⊗_R M ≅ M̂_I for finitely generated modules M over a Noetherian ring (the completion-as-tensor-product theorem). Distinct from AdicCompletion/Basic and AdicCompletion/Exactness.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: The R-algebra structure on the I-adic completion R̂_I and the interaction with algebraMap: the completion of an R-algebra is an R-algebra. Distinct from AdicCompletion/Basic and AdicCompletion/RingHom.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The I-adic completion of an R-module M: the inverse limit lim M/IⁿM, with the canonical map M → M̂, the universal property among I-adically complete modules, and the ring structure when M = R. Foundation for all AdicCompletion files.

Name: Exactness.lean
Description: Exactness of adic completion: the I-adic completion functor is exact on finitely generated modules over a Noetherian ring (Artin-Rees lemma). Distinct from AdicCompletion/Basic and Flat/Basic.

Name: Completeness.lean
Description: Completeness of the adic completion: R̂_I is I-adically complete (the completion of the completion is the completion). Distinct from AdicCompletion/Basic and AdicCompletion/Topology.

Name: Adjoin
Description: Adjoining elements to rings: the subring (or subalgebra) generated by a set, polynomial evaluation maps, and simple extensions.

Name: Polynomial
Description: Adjoining a root of a polynomial: the ring R[X]/(f) obtained by quotienting the polynomial ring by a principal ideal, with the universal property that maps R[X]/(f) → S correspond to elements a ∈ S with f(a) = 0. The relationship between adjunction by a polynomial and adjunction by an element (RingTheory/Adjoin). Distinct from the top-level Adjoin folder, which handles adjoining arbitrary elements; this subfolder is specific to polynomial roots.

Name: Bivariate.lean
Description: Bivariate adjoined polynomials.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ring of polynomials from adjoined elements.

Name: PowerBasis.lean
Description: Power bases for adjoined algebras: the PowerBasis R α structure asserting {1, α, α², …, α^(n-1)} is an R-basis for Algebra.adjoin R {α}, with the interaction with the minimal polynomial. Distinct from Adjoin/Polynomial and Adjoin/Singleton.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomial interpretation of adjoined algebras: the isomorphism Algebra.adjoin R {α} ≅ R[X]/(minpoly R α) when α is integral, and the evaluation map from the polynomial ring. Distinct from Adjoin/Field and Adjoin/PowerBasis.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Towers in adjunction: the isomorphism Algebra.adjoin R (S ∪ T) = Algebra.adjoin (Algebra.adjoin R S) T and the interaction with the IsScalarTower. Distinct from Adjoin/FGBaseChange and Adjoin/Basic.

Name: Singleton.lean
Description: Adjoining a single element: the algebra Algebra.adjoin R {x} = R[x] generated by one element, with the basic instances and the comparison with polynomial evaluation. Distinct from Adjoin/PowerBasis and Adjoin/Basic.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Adjoining elements in field extensions: the subalgebra k[α] ⊆ L for an algebraic element α ∈ L over a field k, isomorphic to k[X]/(minpoly k α). Distinct from Adjoin/Basic and Adjoin/Polynomial.

Name: Dimension.lean
Description: Dimension of adjoined algebras: the Krull dimension of the subalgebra R[α] for an algebraic element α, in terms of the degree of its minimal polynomial. Distinct from Adjoin/Basic and KrullDimension/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Adjoining a set of elements to form a subalgebra: Algebra.adjoin R S is the smallest R-subalgebra of A containing S, with its universal property and the map from the polynomial ring. Foundation for all Adjoin files.

Name: FGBaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for finitely generated adjoined subalgebras: the interaction of Algebra.adjoin with scalar extension along a ring map R → T. Distinct from Adjoin/FG and Adjoin/Tower.

Name: FG.lean
Description: Finite generation for adjoined subalgebras: Algebra.adjoin R S is finitely generated as an R-algebra when S is finite, and conditions for Algebra.adjoin R S = ⊤. Distinct from Adjoin/Basic and Finiteness/Basic.

Name: Coalgebra
Description: Coalgebras over a commutative ring: counit and comultiplication maps satisfying coassociativity and counit axioms.

Name: MonoidAlgebra.lean
Description: The coalgebra structure on a monoid algebra: MonoidAlgebra k G with comultiplication extending g ↦ g ⊗ g and counit extending g ↦ 1. Distinct from Coalgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/MonoidAlgebra.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Coalgebra homomorphisms: CoalgHom R C D maps preserving both comultiplication and counit. Distinct from Coalgebra/Equiv and Coalgebra/Basic.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of coalgebras: A ⊗_R B is a coalgebra with comultiplication Δ(a ⊗ b) = ∑ a₁ ⊗ b₁ ⊗ a₂ ⊗ b₂ (after reshuffling). Distinct from Coalgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/TensorProduct.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: The opposite coalgebra: the coalgebra C^copp with the swapped comultiplication Δ^op = σ ∘ Δ (where σ is the swap map). Distinct from Coalgebra/Basic.

Name: GroupLike.lean
Description: Group-like elements in a coalgebra: elements g satisfying Δ(g) = g ⊗ g and ε(g) = 1. Distinct from Coalgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/GroupLike.

Name: CoassocSimps.lean
Description: Coassociativity simp lemmas for coalgebras: technical lemmas for automated reasoning about the coassociativity axiom in Coalgebra computations. Distinct from Coalgebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Coalgebras over a commutative ring: the Coalgebra R C typeclass with comultiplication Δ : C → C ⊗_R C and counit ε : C → R satisfying coassociativity and counit axioms. Foundation for all Coalgebra files.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Coalgebra equivalences: the CoalgEquiv R C D type for coalgebra isomorphisms. Distinct from Coalgebra/Hom and Coalgebra/Basic.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: The convolution algebra on coalgebra homomorphisms: Hom_R(C, A) for a coalgebra C and algebra A forms an algebra under the convolution product (f * g) = μ_A ∘ (f ⊗ g) ∘ Δ. Distinct from Coalgebra/Hom and Coalgebra/Basic.

Name: Congruence
Description: Ring congruences: equivalence relations compatible with ring operations, alternative to ideals for defining quotients.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Ring congruences and ring homomorphisms: the kernel congruence of a ring map f gives a congruence, and the first isomorphism theorem for ring congruences. Distinct from Congruence/Basic and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Ring congruences on opposite rings: a ring congruence on R induces one on R^op and vice versa. Distinct from Congruence/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for ring congruences: the RingCon predicate, the quotient ring Quotient c, and the basic API. Distinct from Congruence/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ring congruences: the RingCon R type for equivalence relations on R compatible with both addition and multiplication, with the induced quotient ring structure and the lattice of ring congruences. Foundation for all Congruence files.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators and ring congruences: sums ∑ and products ∏ respect ring congruences (congruent summands give congruent sums). Distinct from Congruence/Basic and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Artinian
Description: Artinian rings and modules: the descending chain condition, semisimplicity of Artinian rings (Hopkins-Levitzki), and Wedderburn's theorem.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Artinian modules: basic properties — an Artinian module has finite length, the interaction with quotients and submodules, and the Fitting decomposition. Distinct from Artinian/Defs and Artinian/Ring.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Artinian rings: the Hopkins-Levitzki theorem (Artinian ↔ Noetherian with finite length), the decomposition into a product of local Artinian rings, and the nil radical. Distinct from Artinian/Module and Artinian/Defs.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Instances for Artinian structure: finite rings are Artinian, quotients and finite products of Artinian rings are Artinian, and field extensions of finite degree are Artinian. Distinct from Artinian/Defs and Artinian/Ring.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Artinian algebras: an algebra over an Artinian ring that is finitely generated as a module is Artinian. Contains the Artinian typeclass for algebras. Distinct from Artinian/Ring and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Artinian rings and modules: the IsArtinian typeclass (the descending chain condition on submodules) and the ArtinianRing typeclass. Distinct from Artinian/Module and Artinian/Ring.

Name: Bialgebra
Description: Bialgebras: structures carrying both algebra and coalgebra maps that are compatible via the Hopf algebra compatibility axiom.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Bialgebra homomorphisms: BialgHom R A B maps preserving both the algebra and coalgebra structure, with composition and the category of R-bialgebras. Distinct from Bialgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/Equiv.

Name: MonoidAlgebra.lean
Description: The bialgebra structure on a monoid algebra: MonoidAlgebra k G is a bialgebra with comultiplication Δ(g) = g ⊗ g and counit ε(g) = 1. Distinct from Bialgebra/Basic and Coalgebra/MonoidAlgebra.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of bialgebras: A ⊗_R B is a bialgebra when A and B are, with the componentwise algebra and the crossed comultiplication. Distinct from Bialgebra/Basic and Coalgebra/TensorProduct.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bialgebras over a commutative ring: the Bialgebra R A typeclass combining the Algebra R A and Coalgebra R A structures with the compatibility conditions (comultiplication and counit are algebra maps). Foundation for all Bialgebra files.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Bialgebra equivalences: the BialgEquiv R A B type for bialgebra isomorphisms, preserving both the algebra and coalgebra structures. Distinct from Bialgebra/Hom and Bialgebra/Basic.

Name: GroupLike.lean
Description: Group-like elements in a bialgebra: elements g satisfying Δ(g) = g ⊗ g and ε(g) = 1, forming a group under multiplication. Distinct from Bialgebra/Basic and Coalgebra/GroupLike.

Name: Invariant
Description: Rings of invariants under group actions: the Reynolds operator, Noether's bound, and the structure of invariant subrings.

Name: Profinite.lean
Description: Invariant subrings under profinite group actions: the interaction of profinite group actions with the invariant subring, and the Galois theory of infinite extensions. Distinct from Invariant/Basic and Galois/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for invariant rings: the IsInvariant predicate for subalgebras and the fixed-point subring typeclass. Distinct from Invariant/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Invariant subrings under group actions: the fixed-point subring A^G of a G-algebra A, with the basic instances and the interaction with the action on ideals (invariant ideals). Distinct from Invariant/Defs and Invariant/Profinite.

Name: Jacobson
Description: The Jacobson radical and Jacobson rings: rings where every prime ideal is an intersection of maximal ideals (Hilbert's Nullstellensatz).

Name: Radical.lean
Description: The Jacobson radical of a ring: Jac(R) = ∩{M maximal ideal of R} = {x | 1 - rx ∈ Rˣ for all r}, with Nakayama's lemma, the interaction with semisimple rings, and the nilradical containment Nil(R) ⊆ Jac(R). Foundation for all Jacobson files.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Jacobson rings: rings where every prime ideal is an intersection of maximal ideals (the Jacobson property). Contains the Jacobson ring typeclass, instances (fields, ℤ, k[X] are Jacobson), and Hilbert's Nullstellensatz context. Distinct from Jacobson/Radical and Ideal/Prime.

Name: Semiprimary.lean
Description: Semiprimary rings: rings where the Jacobson radical is nilpotent and the quotient by Jac(R) is semisimple Artinian. Contains the IsSemiprimary typeclass and its relation to Artinian rings. Distinct from Jacobson/Artinian and Jacobson/Radical.

Name: Artinian.lean
Description: The Jacobson radical of Artinian rings: for an Artinian ring, Jac(R) equals the nil radical; the quotient R/Jac(R) is semisimple Artinian (Wedderburn-Artin theorem setup). Distinct from Jacobson/Radical and Artinian/Ring.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: The Jacobson radical of an ideal I: Jac(I) = ∩{M maximal | I ⊆ M}, the intersection of all maximal ideals containing I. Contains the interaction with Nakayama's lemma and the nil radical. Distinct from Jacobson/Radical and Ideal/Maximal.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: The Jacobson radical of polynomial rings: Jac(R[X]) = (Jac(R))[X], and the interaction between being Jacobson (every prime is an intersection of maximals) and polynomial extensions. Distinct from Jacobson/Ring and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Int
Description: Ring-theoretic properties specific to Z: Euclidean structure, Bezout's identity in Z, and Z as initial object in the category of rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ring theory of ℤ: ℤ is a Euclidean domain, a PID, a UFD, and a Dedekind domain, with the associated ideal theory, the prime ideals (0) and (p), and the class group being trivial. Distinct from Ideal/Int and EuclideanDomain/Int.

Name: IntegralClosure
Description: The integral closure of a ring in an extension: integrality criterion, the integral closure is a ring, and normal domains.

Name: IsIntegralClosure
Description: The IsIntegralClosure typeclass: a ring A is the integral closure of R in S if A ⊆ S is the subring of all elements integral over R, characterized by the universal property among rings between R and S where all elements are integral. Used to unify different constructions of integral closures. Distinct from IsIntegral (individual elements) and Algebra (whole algebra).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: IsIntegralClosure definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: IsIntegralClosure characterisation.

Name: IsIntegral
Description: The IsIntegral predicate for a single element: x ∈ S is integral over R if there exists a monic polynomial f ∈ R[X] with f(x) = 0. Equivalent characterizations: x is integral iff R[x] is a finitely generated R-module. The minimal polynomial of an integral element. Distinct from IsIntegralClosure (the whole ring being the closure) and Algebra (the whole algebra being integral).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: IsIntegral definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: IsIntegral API.

Name: AlmostIntegral.lean
Description: Almost integral elements.

Name: Algebra
Description: Integral closure in the algebra context: an R-algebra B where every element satisfies a monic polynomial with coefficients in R. The Algebra.IsIntegral typeclass and its closure properties (integral elements form a subring; compositions of integral extensions are integral). Distinct from IsIntegral (the property of a single element) — this folder handles the whole algebra being integral.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Integral algebra and ideals.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Integral algebra definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Integral closure in algebra extensions.

Name: IntegralRestrict.lean
Description: Integral restriction of ring maps: the integral closure of the image of a ring map, and the map from the integral closure of R in S to the integral closure of f(R). Distinct from IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: IntegrallyClosed.lean
Description: Integrally closed domains: a domain R is integrally closed (IsIntegrallyClosed) if every element of its fraction field integral over R lies in R. Contains basic instances (UFDs, Dedekind domains are integrally closed) and the characterisation. Distinct from IntegralClosure/GoingDown and DedekindDomain/Basic.

Name: GoingDown.lean
Description: Going-Down for integral closure: in a flat extension of integrally closed domains, the Going-Down theorem holds. Contains conditions under which the lying-over prime can be chosen below a given prime. Distinct from IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed and Ideal/GoingDown.

Name: Localization
Description: Localization of commutative rings: inverting a multiplicative set, the universal property, and local properties.

Name: Away
Description: Localization away from an element: R[f⁻¹] = R[{f}⁻¹], inverting a single element f ∈ R. Isomorphic to R[X]/(fX - 1). Used to describe affine opens D(f) = Spec(R[f⁻¹]) in algebraic geometry. Simpler than localization at a prime: the result need not be local. Distinct from Localization/AtPrime (inverting a prime's complement).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Localisation away lemmas.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Localisation away from an element.

Name: AdjoinRoot.lean
Description: Localisation away via adjoin root.

Name: AtPrime
Description: Localization at a prime ideal: the local ring R_p = R[(R∖p)⁻¹], obtained by inverting all elements outside p. The localized ring is local with maximal ideal pR_p and residue field Frac(R/p). Stalk-local properties in algebraic geometry. Distinct from Localization/Away (inverting a single element) — localizing at a prime is the primary tool for local analysis.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extension under localisation at a prime.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Localisation at a prime ideal.

Name: LocalizationLocalization.lean
Description: Localization of a localization: (R[S⁻¹])[T'⁻¹] ≅ R[(ST)⁻¹] for compatible submonoids S and T. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Localization/Defs.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Modules over a localization: the localization M[S⁻¹] of an R-module M as an R[S⁻¹]-module, with the universal property. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: InvSubmonoid.lean
Description: The submonoid of invertible elements: the localization R[{a}⁻¹] = R[1/a] at a single element a, and the condition for this to equal the localization at a prime. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Ring/Units.

Name: Integer.lean
Description: Integer elements in a localization: elements of R[M⁻¹] that are integral over R lie in R when R is integrally closed. Distinct from Localization/Basic and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integral elements and localization: if x ∈ S is integral over R[M⁻¹], then there exists m ∈ M such that mx is integral over R. Distinct from Localization/Integer and Localization/Basic.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ as a localization: ℚ = IsLocalization ℤ (ℤ \ {0}) ℚ, the universal field of fractions of ℤ, and the canonical embedding ℤ ↪ ℚ. Distinct from Localization/FractionRing and Algebra/Rat.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Submodules and localization: the interaction of the submodule lattice with the localization functor — the localization of a submodule and the interaction with quotients. Distinct from Localization/Module and Localization/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Localization of Pi rings: (∏ᵢ Rᵢ)[M⁻¹] ≅ ∏ᵢ Rᵢ[Mᵢ⁻¹] for a product of localizations. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Ring/Pi.

Name: NormTrace.lean
Description: Norm and trace in localizations: the compatibility of Algebra.norm and Algebra.trace with localization (norm commutes with base change). Distinct from Localization/Basic and Norm/Basic.

Name: NumDen.lean
Description: Numerators and denominators: for every element x of the localization R[M⁻¹], there exist a ∈ R and m ∈ M with x = a/m, and the choice of a and m. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Localization/Defs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The localization of a ring: IsLocalization M S asserting that the ring map R → S is a localization at the submonoid M, with the universal property and the interaction with ring maps. Foundation for all Localization files.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of localizations: |R[M⁻¹]| ≤ max(|R|, |M|, ℵ₀) for a localization at a submonoid M. Distinct from Localization/Basic.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change and localization: the canonical isomorphism R[M⁻¹] ⊗_R A ≅ A[(f(M))⁻¹] for a ring map f : R → A. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Flat/IsBaseChange.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebra structure of localizations: the R-algebra structure on a localization S = R[M⁻¹] and its interaction with algebraMap. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: AsSubring.lean
Description: Localization as a subring: embedding R[M⁻¹] as a subring of the fraction field when M consists of non-zero-divisors, and the interaction with the integral closure. Distinct from Localization/Basic and FractionRing.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free modules and localization: a free R-module localizes to a free R[M⁻¹]-module of the same rank. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals and localization: the extended ideal I·R[M⁻¹], the contracted ideal I^c = I ∩ R, and the bijection between primes of R[M⁻¹] and primes of R not meeting M. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Ideal/Maps.

Name: FractionRing.lean
Description: The fraction ring (field of fractions): FractionRing R as the localization of a domain R at R \ {0}, with the universal property and the embedding R ↪ FractionRing R. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Field/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for localization: the IsLocalization typeclass, the localization map, and the construction of the localization as a quotient of R × M. Distinct from Localization/Basic (derived results).

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: Finiteness and localization: finitely generated modules and algebras remain finitely generated after localization. Distinct from Localization/Basic and Finiteness/Basic.

Name: LocalProperties
Description: Properties of ring maps that can be checked locally: flatness, finiteness, and smoothness via localizations at primes.

Name: ProjectiveDimension.lean
Description: Projective dimension as a local property: pd M = sup_p pd M_p for a finitely generated module M over a Noetherian ring. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Module/Projective.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projectivity as a local property: a finitely generated module M is projective iff all localisations Mₘ at maximal ideals are free (Nakayama + Serre). Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Module/Projective.

Name: Reduced.lean
Description: Reducedness as a local property: R is reduced (no nonzero nilpotents) iff all localisations R_p are reduced. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Nilpotent/Basic.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Local vanishing of submodules: a submodule N ⊆ M is 0 iff all localisations N_p = 0; more generally, M = N iff M_p = N_p for all p. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Localization/Module.

Name: Semilocal.lean
Description: Semilocal rings and local properties: rings with finitely many maximal ideals (semilocal rings), and how local properties simplify in the semilocal case. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and LocalRing/Basic.

Name: Exactness.lean
Description: Local exactness: a sequence of R-modules is exact iff it is exact after localisation at every prime ideal (the local-global principle for exactness). Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Local properties of ring maps: the RingHom.LocallyOfFiniteType and similar predicates for properties that can be checked after localisation at every prime, with the local-global principle. Foundation for all LocalProperties files.

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Injectivity as a local property: a module map f : M → N is injective iff all localisations fₘ : Mₘ → Nₘ at maximal ideals m are injective. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Localization/Module.

Name: IntegrallyClosed.lean
Description: Integral closure as a local property: R is integrally closed iff all localisations R_p at prime ideals p are integrally closed. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and IntegralClosure/IntegrallyClosed.

Name: InjectiveDimension.lean
Description: Injective dimension as a local property: injdim M = sup_p injdim M_p for a finitely generated module M over a Noetherian ring R. Distinct from LocalProperties/Basic and Module/Injective.

Name: Kaehler
Description: The module of Kahler differentials Omega_{A/R}: the universal derivation, the first fundamental sequence, and connections to smoothness.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Kähler differentials of polynomial rings: ΩR[X]/R is the free R[X]-module of rank 1 generated by dX, and ΩR[X₁,…,Xₙ]/R is free of rank n. Distinct from Kaehler/Basic and MvPolynomial/PDeriv.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Kähler differentials and tensor products: Ω(A⊗_RB)/R ≅ (ΩA/R ⊗_A (A⊗B)) ⊕ (ΩB/R ⊗_B (A⊗B)), the base change formula for differentials. Distinct from Kaehler/Basic and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Kähler differentials: the A-module ΩA/R of Kähler differentials, presented as the diagonal ideal I_A ⊆ A ⊗_R A (I_A = ker(μ : A ⊗ A → A)) with ΩA/R = I_A/I_A², and the universal R-derivation d : A → ΩA/R. Foundation for all Kaehler files.

Name: JacobiZariski.lean
Description: The Jacobi-Zariski exact sequence: for `R → S → T`, the exact sequence `H¹(L_{T/R}) → H¹(L_{T/S}) → T ⊗[S] Ω[S/R] → Ω[T/R] → Ω[T/S] → 0`. Maps are `H1Cotangent.map`, `H1Cotangent.δ`, `KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange`, and `KaehlerDifferential.map`.

Name: KrullDimension
Description: Krull dimension of rings: the supremum of lengths of prime ideal chains, with computations for polynomial rings and local rings.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Krull dimension of polynomial rings: dim R[X] = dim R + 1 for a Noetherian ring R (and dim R + 1 ≤ dim R[X] ≤ 2 dim R + 1 in the general case). Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: PID.lean
Description: Krull dimension of PIDs: a PID that is not a field has Krull dimension 1, and the equivalence PID ↔ Noetherian UFD ↔ Dedekind domain of dimension ≤ 1. Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic and DedekindDomain/PID.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: Zero-dimensional rings: rings where every prime ideal is maximal (fields, Artinian rings), with the characterisation as rings with Nil(R) = Jac(R) and R/Nil(R) von Neumann regular. Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic and Artinian/Ring.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Krull dimension and regular sequences: the interaction between the depth (regular sequence length) and the Krull dimension, used in the characterisation of Cohen-Macaulay and regular rings. Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic and Regular/Depth.

Name: NonZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Krull dimension and non-zero-divisors: the height theorem for non-zero-divisors (an element that is not a zero divisor raises the dimension by 1 in the quotient). Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic and Ideal/KrullsHeightTheorem.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Krull dimension of fields: a field has Krull dimension 0 (the only prime ideal is 0). Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Krull dimension of a ring: the supremum of lengths of prime ideal chains, with Ring.krullDimension R and basic instances (fields have dimension 0, PIDs have dimension ≤ 1). Foundation for all KrullDimension files.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Krull dimension of modules: Module.krullDimension M, defined via the prime ideal filtration of the annihilator, with the relation dim M ≤ dim R. Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic.

Name: LocalRing.lean
Description: Krull dimension of local rings: the Krull dimension of a Noetherian local ring and the characterisation of regular local rings (dim R = embedding dimension = μ(m)). Distinct from KrullDimension/Basic and RegularLocalRing/Defs.

Name: IdealFilter
Description: Ideal filters: Gabriel filters and topologies on rings used in noncommutative localization theory.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topology induced by ideal filters: the linear topology on R defined by an ideal filter as a neighbourhood basis of 0, and the completion with respect to this topology. Distinct from IdealFilter/Basic and AdicCompletion/Topology.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ideal filters (Gabriel filters): a filter F of ideals on a ring R closed under colon ideals ((I : a) ∈ F whenever I ∈ F and a ∈ R), used in the theory of torsion modules and localisation. Distinct from IdealFilter/Topology and Ideal/Basic.

Name: FormalGroup
Description: Formal groups: power series in one or more variables satisfying group axioms, connecting local fields and elliptic curves.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Formal group laws over a ring: a formal group law F(X, Y) ∈ R[[X, Y]] satisfying F(X, 0) = X, F(0, Y) = Y, and the associativity F(F(X,Y),Z) = F(X,F(Y,Z)), with the additive and multiplicative formal groups as examples. Distinct from PowerSeries/Substitution.

Name: FractionalIdeal
Description: Fractional ideals of a domain: R-submodules of the fraction field, their multiplication, and invertibility in Dedekind domains.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: The norm of a fractional ideal: N(I) = [R:I] (the index of I in R as an R-module), with multiplicativity N(IJ) = N(I)N(J). Distinct from FractionalIdeal/Basic and Norm/Basic.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on fractional ideals: intersection, sum, product, colon ideal, and the associated lattice structure on FractionalIdeal. Distinct from FractionalIdeal/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Inverse.lean
Description: Invertible fractional ideals: I is invertible iff I·I⁻¹ = R (where I⁻¹ = {x ∈ K | xI ⊆ R}), equivalently iff I is projective of rank 1. Contains the criterion and the group of Cartier divisors. Distinct from FractionalIdeal/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fractional ideals: FractionalIdeal R⁰ K for the fraction field K of R, with the ring structure (ideal multiplication), the ideal lattice, and the embedding of regular ideals. Foundation for all FractionalIdeal files.

Name: Extended.lean
Description: Extended fractional ideals: the extension I·S of a fractional ideal from R to an overring S, and the interaction with localization and ring maps. Distinct from FractionalIdeal/Basic.

Name: Finiteness
Description: Finitely generated algebras and modules: Noetherian criteria, the Hilbert basis theorem, and finite presentation.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Finiteness in the presence of nilpotents: finiteness conditions in rings with nilpotent elements (e.g., finite generation is preserved by nilpotent extensions). Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Nilpotent/Basic.

Name: NilpotentKer.lean
Description: Finiteness conditions with nilpotent kernels: when the kernel of a ring map is nilpotent, finiteness conditions transfer between source and target. Distinct from Finiteness/Nilpotent and Finiteness/Basic.

Name: Nakayama.lean
Description: Nakayama's lemma: if M is a finitely generated R-module with IM = M for an ideal I ⊆ Jac(R) (Jacobson radical), then M = 0; and the local criterion for finite generation. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Jacobson/Radical.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Finiteness in the lattice of submodules: the lattice of finitely generated submodules as a sublattice, and conditions for the lattice to be Noetherian. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Noetherian/Defs.

Name: ModuleFinitePresentation.lean
Description: Module finite presentation: the interaction between finitely presented modules and exact sequences (finite presentation is preserved under extensions), and the schanuel lemma. Distinct from Finiteness/Defs and Finiteness/Basic.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Small modules: modules isomorphic to a module in a small universe, and universe-polymorphic finiteness conditions. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic.

Name: Subalgebra.lean
Description: Finiteness for subalgebras: conditions for a subalgebra of a finite-type algebra to be of finite type (Noetherian case), and counterexamples in the general case. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Noetherian/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Finiteness for quotient modules: a quotient of a finitely generated module is finitely generated; conditions for quotients to be finitely presented. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Finiteness for product modules: M × N is finitely generated iff M and N are; and M × N is finitely presented iff M and N are. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Module/Prod.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Finitely generated projective modules: the dual basis lemma (M is finitely generated projective iff there exist xᵢ ∈ M and fᵢ ∈ M* with m = ∑ fᵢ(m)xᵢ). Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Module/Projective.

Name: Cofinite.lean
Description: Cofinite finiteness conditions: the cofinite filter on the lattice of submodules and the associated finiteness criterion. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Ideal/Lattice.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core finiteness definitions: Module.Finite (finitely generated as an R-module), Algebra.FiniteType (finitely generated as an R-algebra), and Module.FinitePresentation (finitely generated with finitely generated kernel). Foundation for all Finiteness files.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of finitely generated modules: |M| ≤ max(|R|, ℵ₀) for a finitely generated R-module over an infinite ring R. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic finiteness results: a finitely generated module over a Noetherian ring is finitely presented; quotients, submodules (Noetherian case), and direct sums of finitely generated modules are finitely generated. Distinct from Finiteness/Defs.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Finiteness and bilinear maps: if M and N are finitely generated R-modules, the module of R-bilinear maps Bil(M, N; P) is finitely generated under appropriate conditions. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Module/Hom.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finiteness and finitely supported functions: finsupp-based constructions of finitely generated modules and their interaction with the Module.Finite typeclass. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Module/Hom.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Finiteness for ideals: finitely generated ideals and the Noetherian characterisation (R is Noetherian iff every ideal is finitely generated). Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Noetherian/Defs.

Name: FiniteTypeLocal.lean
Description: Local characterisation of finite type: an R-algebra A is of finite type iff it is locally of finite type (each localisation at a prime is of finite type over the localised ring). Distinct from Finiteness/Defs and LocalProperties/Basic.

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Descent of finiteness conditions: if R → S is faithfully flat and an S-algebra A satisfies a finiteness property, then A satisfies the corresponding property as an R-algebra. Distinct from Finiteness/Basic and Flat/Basic.

Name: FinitePresentationLocal.lean
Description: Local characterisation of finite presentation: M is finitely presented iff its localisations Mₘ at all maximal ideals m are finitely presented. Distinct from Finiteness/Defs and LocalProperties/Projective.

Name: Flat
Description: Flat modules and flat ring maps: the Tor-vanishing criterion, faithfully flat maps, and the flat base change theorem.

Name: FaithfullyFlat
Description: Faithfully flat modules and ring maps: a flat module M is faithfully flat if M ⊗ N = 0 implies N = 0 (equivalently, the scalar-extension functor - ⊗_R M reflects exactness). Faithfully flat descent: properties of B-modules can be detected after a faithfully flat base change A → B. The strict strengthening of flatness used for descent theory. Distinct from the top-level Flat folder (flat modules, weaker condition).

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Descent via faithfully flat maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Faithfully flat modules: `IsFaithfullyFlat R M`.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Faithfully flat algebras.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank of flat modules: a finitely generated flat module over a Noetherian ring has locally constant rank (the function Spec R → ℕ, p ↦ rank M_p is locally constant). Distinct from Flat/Basic and Finiteness/Projective.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Flatness and localisation: localisation is a flat base change, the flat locus of a module (the open set of primes where M_p is flat), and the local criterion for flatness. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: Stability.lean
Description: Stability of flatness: the tensor product of flat modules is flat; the composition of flat maps is flat; base change preserves flatness. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Flat/IsBaseChange.

Name: TorsionFree.lean
Description: Flat vs torsion-free: over a PID, flat ↔ torsion-free; over a general Noetherian ring, flat → torsion-free → no associated primes with nilpotent localisation. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Flat/Domain.

Name: Tensor.lean
Description: Flat modules and tensor products: the interaction of the flatness condition with multi-tensor products, and the associativity of flat base change. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Flat/Stability.

Name: IsBaseChange.lean
Description: Flat base change: the interaction of flat base change R → S with limits and colimits, and the isomorphism M ⊗_R S ≅ M ⊗_R N ⊗_N S for flat N. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: CategoryTheory.lean
Description: Flat modules in category-theoretic terms: flatness as exactness of the tensor product functor in the module category, with the categorical Tor functor characterisation. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Abelian/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Flat modules and flat algebras: the Module.Flat R M typeclass (the functor M ⊗_R − is exact on R-modules), with basic instances (free modules are flat, localisations are flat). Foundation for all Flat files.

Name: Domain.lean
Description: Flat modules over integral domains: flat modules over a domain are torsion-free; finitely generated flat modules over a PID are free (Serre's theorem). Distinct from Flat/Basic and Module/PID.

Name: EquationalCriterion.lean
Description: The equational criterion for flatness (Lazard's criterion): M is flat iff every relation ∑ aᵢmᵢ = 0 in M is witnessed by a matrix equation over R. Contains the criterion and its equivalence with flatness. Distinct from Flat/Basic.

Name: Equalizer.lean
Description: Flatness via equalizers: the characterisation of flat modules in terms of the exactness of equalizer diagrams, used in categorical approaches to flatness. Distinct from Flat/Basic and Flat/EquationalCriterion.

Name: HopfAlgebra
Description: Hopf algebras: bialgebras equipped with an antipode map, generalizing group algebras and coordinate rings of algebraic groups.

Name: MonoidAlgebra.lean
Description: The Hopf algebra structure on a group algebra: k[G] is a Hopf algebra with comultiplication Δ(g) = g ⊗ g, counit ε(g) = 1, and antipode S(g) = g⁻¹. Distinct from HopfAlgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/MonoidAlgebra.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of Hopf algebras: A ⊗_R B is a Hopf algebra when A and B are, with componentwise antipode S(a ⊗ b) = S_A(a) ⊗ S_B(b). Distinct from HopfAlgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/TensorProduct.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Hopf algebras over a commutative ring: the HopfAlgebra R H typeclass for a bialgebra H equipped with an antipode S : H → H satisfying the Hopf identity. Foundation for all HopfAlgebra files.

Name: GroupLike.lean
Description: Group-like elements in a Hopf algebra: elements g with Δ(g) = g ⊗ g form a group under multiplication with S(g) = g⁻¹. Distinct from HopfAlgebra/Basic and Bialgebra/GroupLike.

Name: Ideal
Description: Ideals in commutative rings: the lattice of ideals, prime and maximal ideals, radical ideals, and ideal arithmetic.

Name: Norm
Description: The norm of an ideal in a Dedekind domain: N(I) = |R/I|, the number of residue classes. Multiplicativity N(IJ) = N(I)N(J), the norm of a prime ideal p above a rational prime p is N(p) = p^f where f is the residue field degree. Used in counting lattice points and in the analytic class number formula. Distinct from Ideal/Quotient (the structure of R/I) — this folder measures the size.

Name: RelNorm.lean
Description: Relative norm of an ideal.

Name: AbsNorm.lean
Description: Absolute norm of an ideal.

Name: Quotient
Description: Quotient rings R/I: the ring structure on equivalence classes, the universal property (ring maps from R/I correspond to ring maps from R vanishing on I), and the correspondence theorem for ideals (ideals of R/I correspond to ideals of R containing I). The first isomorphism theorem for rings. Distinct from Ideal/Norm (size of R/I) and Ideal/AssociatedPrime (prime structure) — this folder gives the algebraic construction.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Noetherian quotient rings.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Nilpotent elements in quotient rings.

Name: PowTransition.lean
Description: Power transition in quotient rings.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on quotient ideals.

Name: Index.lean
Description: Index of an ideal.

Name: ChineseRemainder.lean
Description: Chinese remainder theorem for quotient rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quotient ring API: `R ⧸ I`, the quotient map, and first isomorphism theorem.

Name: HasFiniteQuotients.lean
Description: Rings with finite quotients by nonzero ideals.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Quotient ring definition: `Ideal.Quotient`.

Name: AssociatedPrime
Description: Associated primes of a module: a prime p is associated to M if p = Ann(m) for some m ∈ M. The set Ass(M) of associated primes is finite for Noetherian M; the minimal elements are the minimal primes of Ann(M), while embedded primes are non-minimal associated primes. Used in primary decomposition: Ass(M) encodes which primes appear. Distinct from MinimalPrime (only the minimal elements of Ass).

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Associated primes under localisation.

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: Finitely many associated primes.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Associated primes: `Ideal.IsAssociated p M`.

Name: MinimalPrime
Description: Minimal primes of an ideal or ring: primes p minimal over a given ideal I (no prime q with I ⊆ q ⊊ p). Every ideal has finitely many minimal primes in a Noetherian ring. The intersection of minimal primes of I equals the radical √I. Used in primary decomposition and in the definition of the irreducible components of Spec(R). Distinct from AssociatedPrime (associated primes include non-minimal embedded primes).

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Minimal primes under localisation.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Finitely many minimal primes (Noetherian case).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Minimal primes of an ideal.

Name: Colon.lean
Description: Minimal primes and colon ideals.

Name: NatInt.lean
Description: Ideals of ℕ and ℤ: the ideal structure on these rings (every ideal of ℕ is of the form nℕ, every ideal of ℤ is of the form nℤ). Distinct from Ideal/Int and Int/Basic.

Name: Nonunits.lean
Description: Nonunits and ideals: the nonunit elements are exactly those lying in some maximal ideal; the Jacobson radical is the intersection of all maximal ideals. Distinct from Ideal/Maximal and Jacobson/Radical.

Name: Oka.lean
Description: Oka families of ideals: a collection F of ideals that is an Oka family (closed under a specific extension condition) gives a prime avoidance-like result. Used in proving existence of prime ideals. Distinct from Ideal/Prime and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Maximal.lean
Description: Maximal ideals: IsMaximal I (I ≠ R and no ideal strictly between I and R), the existence of maximal ideals by Zorn's lemma, and the quotient R/m being a field. Distinct from Ideal/Prime and Ideal/Basic.

Name: KrullsHeightTheorem.lean
Description: Krull's principal ideal theorem (Hauptidealsatz): in a Noetherian ring, every minimal prime over a principal ideal (a) has height ≤ 1, and more generally over (a₁, …, aₙ) has height ≤ n. Distinct from Ideal/Height and KrullDimension/Basic.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: The complete lattice of ideals: the lattice Ideal R with ∩ (intersection), + (sum), ·(product), and the modular law I ∩ (J + K) = (I ∩ J) + (I ∩ K) when I ⊇ J. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Maps between ideal lattices: the pushforward (map) and pullback (comap) of ideals along ring homomorphisms, with the interaction between prime ideals and the Zariski topology. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ideal/Lattice.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Ideals in product rings: the ideals of R × S are exactly I × J for ideals I ⊆ R and J ⊆ S, with the Chinese Remainder Theorem. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ring/Prod.

Name: Pure.lean
Description: `Ideal.Pure`: an ideal `I` of `R` is pure if `R ⧸ I` is `R`-flat (Stacks 04PR). `Ideal.inf_eq_mul_of_pure`: for pure `I`, `I ⊓ J = I * J` for every ideal `J`. Distinct from idempotent ideals (equivalent over Noetherian rings).

Name: Span.lean
Description: The ideal span of a set: Ideal.span S as the smallest ideal containing S, with the interaction with ring maps (span (f '' S) ⊆ map f (span S)), and the generation of ideals from a set. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Prime.lean
Description: Prime ideals: IsPrime I (I ≠ R and xy ∈ I → x ∈ I or y ∈ I), the prime spectrum Spec R as a topological space (Zariski topology), and basic instances (0 is prime in a domain). Distinct from Ideal/Maximal and Ideal/Basic.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on ideals: product IJ, powers Iⁿ, radical √I, the Chinese Remainder Theorem for pairwise coprime ideals, annihilators Ann(M), and the interaction with the ring operations. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ideal/Lattice.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Ideals over a subring: the interaction of ideals in an extension S ⊇ R with the base ring R — extended ideals IS, contracted ideals I^c = I ∩ R, and the going-up/going-down theorems. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ideal/GoingUp.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on ideals: the smul-set action g • I for a group-ring action and the interaction of the ideal structure with the pointwise multiplication. Distinct from Ideal/Operations and Group/Support.

Name: IsPrincipalPowQuotient.lean
Description: `Ideal.quotEquivPowQuotPowSucc`: for a principal ideal `I`, the `R`-linear equivalence `R ⧸ I ≃ₗ[R] I ^ n ⧸ I ^ (n + 1)`. Also provides plain equiv versions to avoid synthesis timeouts.

Name: Cotangent.lean
Description: The cotangent space of an ideal: the I/I²-module, which for a maximal ideal m gives the cotangent space m/m² = T*_p(Spec R) at the corresponding point. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Kaehler/Basic.

Name: CotangentBaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for the cotangent module: (I/I²) ⊗_{R/I} (S/IS) ≅ IS/(IS)² for a ring map R → S, used in the Jacobi-Zariski sequence. Distinct from Ideal/Cotangent and Kaehler/JacobiZariski.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for ideals: the Ideal R = Submodule R R type, left/right/two-sided ideals in non-commutative rings, and the operations span, comap, map. Distinct from Ideal/Basic (derived lemmas).

Name: Colon.lean
Description: The colon ideal: (I : J) = {r ∈ R | rJ ⊆ I}, the ideal quotient, with basic properties, the interaction with annihilators Ann(M) = (0 : M), and the saturated ideal. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ideal/Operations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic ideal theory: the Ideal R type as a sub-R-module of R, the prime and maximal ideal predicates, the quotient ring R/I, and basic operations (comap, map, radical). Foundation for all Ideal files.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Bases for ideals: a basis for a finitely generated ideal I = (a₁, …, aₙ) as an R-module, and the interaction with module bases. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for ideals: ∏ Iᵢ (product of ideals) and ∑ Iᵢ (sum) over a finite index set, with the interaction between the two operations. Distinct from Ideal/Operations and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Int.lean
Description: Ideal theory for ℤ: every ideal of ℤ is principal (of the form nℤ), prime ideals are (0) and (p) for primes p, and the interaction with the GCD. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Int/Basic.

Name: IsPrimary.lean
Description: Primary ideals: q is primary if xy ∈ q → x ∈ q or y ∈ q for some power, with the associated prime √q, the primary decomposition of a Noetherian ideal, and the uniqueness of primary decomposition. Distinct from Ideal/Prime and Ideal/Operations.

Name: IsPrincipal.lean
Description: Principal ideals: the Submodule.IsPrincipal typeclass, conditions for R to be a PID, and the interaction with the GCDMonoid structure. Distinct from Ideal/Basic and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: IdempotentFG.lean
Description: Finitely generated idempotent ideals: an idempotent ideal I² = I that is finitely generated is generated by an idempotent element e (with I = eR). Distinct from Ideal/Basic and Ring/Idempotent.

Name: GoingDown.lean
Description: Going-Down theorem: for an integral extension R ⊆ S with R integrally closed and S flat, if p ⊆ q are primes of R and Q is a prime of S over q, there exists a prime P over p with P ⊆ Q. Distinct from Ideal/GoingUp and IntegralClosure/GoingDown.

Name: GoingUp.lean
Description: Going-Up theorem: for an integral extension R ⊆ S, given primes p ⊆ q in R and a prime P over p in S, there exists a prime Q over q with P ⊆ Q (Cohen-Seidenberg theorem). Distinct from Ideal/GoingDown and Ideal/Over.

Name: Height.lean
Description: The height of a prime ideal: ht(p) = sup{n | ∃ chain p₀ ⊊ … ⊊ pₙ = p of prime ideals} = Krull dimension of R_p. Distinct from Ideal/Prime and KrullDimension/Basic.

Name: GradedAlgebra
Description: Graded algebras and modules: decomposition into graded pieces, the associated graded ring, and homogeneous maps.

Name: Homogeneous
Description: Homogeneous elements and ideals in graded algebras: an element is homogeneous if it lies in a single graded piece Aₙ; an ideal is homogeneous if it is generated by homogeneous elements (equivalently, closed under the grading projection). The quotient of a graded algebra by a homogeneous ideal is again graded. Essential for the algebraic geometry of projective varieties (Proj construction requires homogeneous ideals).

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Homogeneous submodules.

Name: Subsemiring.lean
Description: Homogeneous subsemirings.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Homogeneous ideals in graded algebras.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Homogeneous maps between graded algebras.

Name: Radical.lean
Description: Radicals in graded algebras: the radical of a homogeneous ideal is homogeneous, the graded Nullstellensatz, and the prime radical of a graded ring. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/Basic and Radical/Basic.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Noetherian graded algebras: a ℕ-graded algebra A = ⊕ Aₙ is Noetherian iff A₀ is Noetherian and A is finitely generated as an A₀-algebra. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/Basic and Noetherian/Basic.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of graded algebras: the graded tensor product A ⊗_R B with (A ⊗ B)ₙ = ⊕ᵢ₊ⱼ₌ₙ Aᵢ ⊗ Bⱼ, with the induced graded algebra structure. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/Basic and TensorProduct/Basic.

Name: RingHom.lean
