Description: Ring maps of graded algebras: graded ring homomorphisms (degree-preserving maps), their interaction with the graded structure, and the graded ring map typeclass. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/AlgHom and GradedAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Graded algebras: the GradedAlgebra ℳ typeclass for an internal grading A = ⊕ ℳ i with ℳ i · ℳ j ⊆ ℳ (i+j), the internal direct sum decomposition, and the associated graded ring. Foundation for all GradedAlgebra files.

Name: AlgHom.lean
Description: Graded algebra homomorphisms: algebra maps f : A → B preserving the grading (f(ℳ i) ⊆ ℕ i), with the category of graded R-algebras. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/Basic and GradedAlgebra/RingHom.

Name: HomogeneousLocalization.lean
Description: Homogeneous localisation: the localisation of a graded ring at a homogeneous prime ideal p, giving the local ring at the corresponding point of Proj. Contains the HomogeneousLocalization R ℳ p type and its local ring structure. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/Basic and Localization/Basic.

Name: FiniteType.lean
Description: Finitely generated graded algebras: a graded algebra generated in degrees ≤ 1 is of finite type over its degree-0 piece, with the Noetherian condition and Hilbert's basis theorem for graded rings. Distinct from GradedAlgebra/Basic and Finiteness/Defs.

Name: HahnSeries
Description: Hahn series: generalized power series with exponents in an ordered group, unifying formal Laurent series and Puiseux series.

Name: Multiplication.lean
Description: Ring structure on Hahn series: the Cauchy product (∑ aᵢ[g])(∑ bⱼ[h]) = ∑ (∑_{g+h=k} aᵢbⱼ)[k], which is well-defined due to well-orderedness of the supports. Contains the Ring instance. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and HahnSeries/Addition.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: The lexicographic order on Hahn series: the total order on HahnSeries Γ R induced by the lexicographic ordering of the support in Γ, making R[[Γ]] a totally ordered ring when R is. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic.

Name: PowerSeries.lean
Description: Hahn series and formal power series: the embedding PowerSeries R ↪ HahnSeries ℕ R as the special case of well-ordered ℕ-supported series. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Basic.

Name: Valuation.lean
Description: The valuation on Hahn series: the natural valuation v(f) = min{g | f.coeff g ≠ 0} making HahnSeries Γ R a valued ring with value group Γ. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and Valuation/Basic.

Name: Summable.lean
Description: Summable families of Hahn series: conditions for an infinite family {fᵢ} of Hahn series to have a well-defined sum (the union of supports is well-ordered), and the summability criterion. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and HahnSeries/Multiplication.

Name: HEval.lean
Description: `PowerSeries.heval`: the `R`-algebra homomorphism from `PowerSeries R` to Hahn series `R⟦Γ⟧` taking `X` to a fixed positive-order Hahn series, extending to formal infinite sums via `HahnSeries.SummableFamily.powerSeriesFamily`. TODO: `MvPowerSeries.heval`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Hahn series: HahnSeries Γ R of formal sums ∑_{g ∈ Γ} aₐ [g] with well-ordered support (suppressing terms with aₐ = 0), with the additive group structure. Foundation for all HahnSeries files.

Name: Addition.lean
Description: Additive group structure on Hahn series: the pointwise addition, negation, and the AddCommGroup instance on HahnSeries Γ R. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and HahnSeries/Multiplication.

Name: Binomial.lean
Description: Binomial Hahn series: the generalised binomial series (1 + X)^α for a rational or real exponent α ∈ ℚ, defined as a Hahn series with support in {0, 1, 2, …} ⊆ ℚ. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and PowerSeries/Binomial.

Name: HahnEmbedding.lean
Description: The Hahn embedding theorem: every totally ordered abelian group G embeds (as an ordered group) into a Hahn series group ℝ^{G/H} for a suitable convex subgroup H. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic and HahnSeries/Valuation.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: Cardinality of Hahn series rings: |HahnSeries Γ R| = max(|R|^|Γ|, ℵ₀) and related bounds. Distinct from HahnSeries/Basic.

Name: Nullstellensatz.lean
Description: Hilbert's Nullstellensatz: the ideal-variety correspondence over algebraically closed fields, with radical and maximal ideal versions.

Name: NormTrace.lean
Description: Norm and trace in ring extensions: the relationship between the norm map N_{L/K} and the trace Tr_{L/K}.

Name: NormalClosure.lean
Description: The normal closure of a ring extension: the smallest normal extension containing a given extension.

Name: OrzechProperty.lean
Description: The Orzech property: a module over a Noetherian ring with a surjective endomorphism must be an isomorphism.

Name: PiTensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of ring extensions: the multi-fold tensor product of algebras over a base ring.

Name: PicardGroup.lean
Description: The Picard group of a ring: the group of isomorphism classes of invertible (rank-1 projective) modules.

Name: Perfection.lean
Description: Ring perfection in characteristic p: the inverse limit along Frobenius, used in perfectoid geometry.

Name: NoetherNormalization.lean
Description: Noether normalization: every finitely generated k-algebra is integral over a polynomial subring.

Name: MatrixAlgebra.lean
Description: Matrix algebras: the ring M_n(R) of n x n matrices, its center, and the Morita equivalence with R.

Name: LittleWedderburn.lean
Description: Wedderburn's little theorem: every finite division ring is commutative, hence a finite field.

Name: LinearDisjoint.lean
Description: Linearly disjoint subalgebras of a ring: a condition ensuring the multiplication map A tensor_R B to AB is injective.

Name: MatrixPolynomialAlgebra.lean
Description: The isomorphism between matrix polynomial rings and polynomial matrix rings.

Name: Nakayama.lean
Description: Nakayama's lemma: if M is a finitely generated module with M = IM for an ideal I in the Jacobson radical, then M = 0.

Name: MvPolynomial.lean
Description: Ring-theoretic properties of multivariate polynomial rings: flatness, Noetherianness, and universal coefficient rings.

Name: Multiplicity.lean
Description: Multiplicity of an element in a ring: the p-adic valuation v_p(a) as a ring-theoretic concept.

Name: PolynomialAlgebra.lean
Description: Polynomial algebras: the isomorphism A[X] isomorphic to A tensor_R R[X] and base change properties.

Name: SurjectiveOnStalks.lean
Description: Surjectivity on stalks: a module map surjective on all localizations at prime ideals is surjective.

Name: Support.lean
Description: The support of a module: the set of prime ideals where the localization is nonzero.

Name: RingInvo.lean
Description: Ring involutions: anti-automorphisms of order 2, generalizing complex conjugation and matrix transpose.

Name: Teichmuller.lean
Description: Teichmuller representatives: the unique (p-1)-th roots of unity in Z_p lifting elements of F_p.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: Ring-theoretic properties of ZMod: Chinese remainder theorem, units, and the structure of integers mod n.

Name: ZariskisMainTheorem.lean
Description: Zariski's main theorem in commutative algebra: the ring-theoretic version about quasi-finite algebras.

Name: TotallySplit.lean
Description: Totally split primes: primes that factor into distinct linear factors in an extension, with characterizations.

Name: RingHomProperties.lean
Description: Ring homomorphism properties: the predicate framework for finite, finitely presented, and flat ring maps.

Name: PrincipalIdealDomain.lean
Description: Principal ideal domains (PIDs): every ideal is principal, unique factorization, and classification of finitely generated modules.

Name: Prime.lean
Description: Prime elements and irreducible elements in rings: their relationship in domains and unique factorization domains.

Name: PowerBasis.lean
Description: Power bases of ring extensions: a basis of the form {1, alpha, alpha^2, ..., alpha^(n-1)} for a simple extension.

Name: PrincipalIdealDomainOfPrime.lean
Description: Criteria for a ring to be a PID using prime element conditions.

Name: ReesAlgebra.lean
Description: The Rees algebra of an ideal: the blowup algebra encoding the filtration by powers of I.

Name: Radical.lean
Description: The radical of an ideal: the intersection of all primes containing I, with the nilradical as a special case.

Name: QuotSMulTop.lean
Description: Quotient by the product of scalar multiplication and the top ideal: a technical lemma for module quotients.

Name: Length.lean
Description: The length of a module: the supremum of lengths of chains of submodules, finite for Artinian and Noetherian modules.

Name: EssentialFiniteness.lean
Description: Essential finiteness: conditions under which a module is finitely generated modulo some submodule.

Name: DualNumber.lean
Description: Dual numbers in ring theory: the ring R[eps]/(eps^2) and its universal property for first-order deformations.

Name: Discriminant.lean
Description: The discriminant of a basis: det of Tr(b_i b_j), its relation to the different ideal and ramification.

Name: EuclideanDomain.lean
Description: Euclidean domains: the GCD algorithm, Bezout's identity, and the structure of Euclidean domains as PIDs.

Name: FinitePresentation.lean
Description: Finitely presented algebras: algebras with a finite presentation by generators and relations.

Name: FiniteLength.lean
Description: Modules of finite length: Artinian and Noetherian modules coincide, and the composition length.

Name: Filtration.lean
Description: Filtrations of modules: decreasing sequences of submodules, stable filtrations, and the Artin-Rees lemma.

Name: Conductor.lean
Description: The conductor of a ring extension: the largest ideal of the extension lying in the base ring.

Name: Bezout.lean
Description: Bezout rings: rings where every finitely generated ideal is principal, a generalization of PIDs.

Name: AlgebraTower.lean
Description: Tower of algebras: if A is an R-algebra and B is an A-algebra then B is an R-algebra, with the tower law.

Name: AdjoinRoot.lean
Description: Adjoining a root: the ring R[X]/(f) with the universal property for maps sending X to a root of f.

Name: Binomial.lean
Description: Binomial coefficients in rings: the ring-theoretic properties of C(n,k) and binomial theorem in general rings.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Complex numbers as a ring extension: C isomorphic to R[X]/(X^2+1) and the ring-theoretic properties of the extension.

Name: ClassGroup.lean
Description: The class group of a Dedekind domain: the group of invertible fractional ideals modulo principal ideals.

Name: ChainOfDivisors.lean
Description: Chains of divisors: strictly increasing sequences under divisibility and their length in a UFD.

Name: FiniteStability.lean
Description: Finite stability of ring properties: conditions under which a property is preserved under finite limits or colimits.

Name: IsAdjoinRoot.lean
Description: The IsAdjoinRoot typeclass: characterizing when a ring map makes the target isomorphic to R[X]/(f).

Name: IntegralDomain.lean
Description: Integral domains: rings with no zero divisors, with basic properties and the fraction field construction.

Name: Idempotents.lean
Description: Idempotent elements and orthogonal decompositions: e^2 = e and the Pierce decomposition of a ring.

Name: IsPrimary.lean
Description: Primary ideals and primary decomposition: the Lasker-Noether theorem for Noetherian rings.

Name: LaurentSeries.lean
Description: Formal Laurent series k((X)): the fraction field of k[[X]], with a valuation and connection to local fields.

Name: Lasker.lean
Description: The Lasker-Noether theorem: every ideal in a Noetherian ring has a primary decomposition.

Name: IsTensorProduct.lean
Description: The IsTensorProduct typeclass: characterizing when a bilinear map exhibits a module as the tensor product.

Name: HopkinsLevitzki.lean
Description: The Hopkins-Levitzki theorem: a one-sided Artinian ring is also Noetherian.

Name: FreeCommRing.lean
Description: The free commutative ring on a set of generators: the polynomial ring over Z and its universal property.

Name: Fintype.lean
Description: Ring-theoretic properties of finite types: the structure of rings with finitely many elements.

Name: FiniteType.lean
Description: Finitely generated algebras: the FiniteType typeclass and its closure under composition and base change.

Name: FreeRing.lean
Description: The free ring on a set of generators: the noncommutative polynomial ring over Z and its universal property.

Name: Henselian.lean
Description: Henselian rings and Hensel's lemma: local rings where simple roots of polynomials lift from the residue field.

Name: Grassmannian.lean
Description: The Grassmannian as a ring-theoretic object: the Plucker embedding and the coordinate ring of Gr(k, n).

Name: Frobenius.lean
Description: The Frobenius endomorphism in characteristic p: the ring map x to x^p and its role in perfection.

Name: Combinatorics
Description: About finite sets, counting, graphs, designs, and discrete structures. Used to calculate exact enumerations, prove existence of configurations, and optimize discrete systems. Relied on by computer science, probability, optimization, number theory, and discrete geometry.

Name: Quiver
Description: Quivers (directed multigraphs): paths in a quiver, the free category on a quiver, and representation-theoretic applications.

Name: Path
Description: Paths in a quiver: a path from vertex u to vertex v is a sequence of edges e₁ : u → v₁, e₂ : v₁ → v₂, …, eₙ : vₙ₋₁ → v. Path composition, the empty path, and the free category on a quiver (objects = vertices, morphisms = paths). Used in representation theory of bound quivers (quotients by path ideals).

Name: Weight.lean
Description: Weight function on quiver paths.

Name: Vertices.lean
Description: Vertices of a quiver path.

Name: Decomposition.lean
Description: Decomposition of quiver paths.

Name: Schreier.lean
Description: Schreier quiver: for a group `G` acting on a set `S`, the Schreier quiver has vertices `S` and an arrow `(s, g)` from `s` to `g • s`. The Schreier index formula. Distinct from Quiver/Covering.

Name: ReflQuiver.lean
Description: Reflexive quivers: quivers with a distinguished identity arrow at each vertex. The category of reflexive quivers. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: Push.lean
Description: Pushforward quiver: the quiver obtained by applying a function on vertices, pushing arrows along. Distinct from Quiver/Prefunctor.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric quivers: quivers with a reverse-arrow involution. The symmetrification `symmetrify V` adding a reverse arrow for each arrow. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: Subquiver.lean
Description: Subquivers: a subquiver of `V` is a collection of arrows closed under composition; the lattice of subquivers. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: SingleObj.lean
Description: Single-object quiver: `SingleObj M` — a quiver with one vertex and arrows given by a monoid `M`, giving the delooping construction. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: Prefunctor.lean
Description: Prefunctors: `Prefunctor V W` — a map on vertices and arrows (not requiring any categorical axioms). The category of quivers with prefunctor morphisms. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: Cast.lean
Description: Casting quiver paths: `Quiver.Path.cast` — reinterpreting a path along equalities of endpoints. Used for definitional equality arguments. Distinct from Quiver/Path.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quivers: `Quiver V` — a type of vertices `V` with a type of arrows `Hom a b` for each pair `a b : V` (directed multigraph). The foundational file for the Quiver folder.

Name: Arborescence.lean
Description: Arborescences (rooted spanning trees of a quiver): `Quiver.Arborescence T r` — a spanning subquiver that is a tree with every vertex reachable from root `r`. Distinct from Quiver/Path.

Name: Path.lean
Description: Paths in a quiver: `Quiver.Path a b` — finite sequences of composable arrows from `a` to `b`. Composition of paths and the path category `Quiver.Free V`. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: Covering.lean
Description: Covering quivers: a quiver morphism that is a local bijection on arrows, analogous to covering spaces. Distinct from Quiver/Schreier.

Name: ConnectedComponent.lean
Description: Connected components of a quiver: the equivalence classes under reachability. Distinct from Quiver/Basic.

Name: Optimization
Description: Combinatorial optimization: matching problems, flow networks, min-cut max-flow, and discrete optimization algorithms.

Name: ValuedCSP.lean
Description: Valued constraint satisfaction problems (VCSPs): `ValuedCSP Γ R` — optimisation over assignments satisfying constraints from `Γ` with costs in ordered semiring `R`. Distinct from Matroid/Basic.

Name: Matroid
Description: Matroids: abstract independence systems generalizing linear independence and graph-theoretic forests, with duality and the greedy algorithm.

Name: Rank
Description: The rank function of a matroid: r : 2^E → ℕ satisfying submodularity r(A∪B) + r(A∩B) ≤ r(A) + r(B) and other axioms. The equivalence of the rank axioms with the independence, circuit, and flat axioms for defining a matroid. Distinct from Minor (structural operations on matroids).

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Rank of finite matroids.

Name: ENat.lean
Description: ENat-valued rank function.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: Cardinal rank of a matroid.

Name: Minor
Description: Matroid minors: the minor M/e (contraction by e) and M\e (deletion of e), and the minor order on matroids. Robertson-Seymour-style results for matroids. A matroid is graphic iff it has no U_{2,4} minor (excluded minor characterization). Distinct from Rank (which studies the rank function without the minor operation).

Name: Order.lean
Description: Minor order on matroids.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Matroid restriction to a subset.

Name: Contract.lean
Description: Matroid contraction: `M / e` deletes `e` and contracts.

Name: Delete.lean
Description: Matroid deletion: `M \ e` removes element `e`.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation and imports for the Matroid folder: aggregating basic matroid infrastructure. Contains no significant new results.

Name: IndepAxioms.lean
Description: Axiom systems for matroids: the independence axiom system, basis axioms, rank axioms, and circuit axioms, and their equivalence. Distinct from Matroid/Basic (which uses independence).

Name: Loop.lean
Description: Loops and coloops in matroids: `M.Loop e` (elements in no basis), `M.Coloop e` (elements in every basis). Properties and the interaction with rank. Distinct from Matroid/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Direct sum of matroids: `M ⊕ N` — the matroid on `E₁ ⊕ E₂` where `I` is independent iff `I ∩ E₁` is independent in `M` and `I ∩ E₂` in `N`. Distinct from Matroid/Basic.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Maps of matroids: pushforward `M.map f` and pullback `M.comap f` of a matroid along a function `f : E → F`. Distinct from Matroid/Constructions.

Name: Circuit.lean
Description: Circuits of a matroid: minimal dependent sets. The circuit elimination axiom and the circuit-cocircuit intersection property. Distinct from Matroid/Basic and Matroid/Closure.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Matroids via independent sets: `Matroid E` — a hereditary antichain system (collection of independent sets closed under taking subsets) satisfying the augmentation axiom. Core API: `Matroid.Indep`, `Matroid.Base`, `Matroid.rank`. Distinct from Matroid/IndepAxioms.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Closure operator of a matroid: `M.closure X` — the set of elements dependent on `X`. Proves the closure axioms and the lattice of flats. Distinct from Matroid/Circuit and Matroid/Basic.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual matroid: `M*` — the matroid whose bases are complements of bases of `M`. Proves `(M*)* = M` and the dual circuit/cocircuit relationship. Distinct from Matroid/Basic.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard matroid constructions: uniform matroids `UniformMatroid r n`, graphic matroids from graphs, free matroids, and loopless matroids. Distinct from Matroid/Basic and Matroid/Map.

Name: SetFamily
Description: Set family theory: Sperner's theorem, the Kruskal-Katona theorem, and shadow and compression operators on families of sets.

Name: Compression
Description: Compression operations (shifting) on set families: the (i,j)-compression replaces a set A containing j but not i with (A∖{j})∪{i} when this doesn't already appear. Used to prove the Kruskal-Katona theorem (optimal shadows of set families) and the Erdős-Ko-Rado theorem via the shifting technique.

Name: UV.lean
Description: UV-compression: moving elements to improve a set family.

Name: Down.lean
Description: Down-compression of set families.

Name: LYM.lean
Description: LYM (Lubell–Yamamoto–Meshalkin) inequality: `∑_{A ∈ F} 1/C(n, |A|) ≤ 1` for an antichain `F ⊆ 2^{[n]}`. Implies Dilworth's theorem and Sperner's theorem. Distinct from SetFamily/Shadow.

Name: KruskalKatona.lean
Description: Kruskal–Katona theorem: among all `k`-uniform families of size `m`, the initial segment of the colex order has the smallest shadow. Distinct from SetFamily/Shadow and SetFamily/LYM.

Name: Shatter.lean
Description: Shattering and VC dimension: a family `F` shatters `S` if `{A ∩ S | A ∈ F} = 2^S`. The VC dimension `vc_dim F = max{|S| | F shatters S}`. The Sauer–Shelah lemma. Distinct from SetFamily/Intersecting.

Name: Shadow.lean
Description: Shadow of a set family: `∂F = {A \ {x} | A ∈ F, x ∈ A}` — all sets obtained by removing one element. The Kruskal–Katona context. Distinct from SetFamily/KruskalKatona.

Name: Kleitman.lean
Description: Kleitman's lemma: two monotone families `A, B ⊆ 2^{[n]}` (one increasing, one decreasing) satisfy a correlation inequality. Distinct from SetFamily/HarrisKleitman.

Name: FourFunctions.lean
Description: Four functions theorem (Ahlswede–Daykin): for monotone functions on a distributive lattice, a certain convexity-type inequality holds. Distinct from SetFamily/Kleitman.

Name: AhlswedeZhang.lean
Description: Ahlswede–Zhang identity: `∑_{A ⊆ [n]} |A| / (1 + ∑_{B ⊆ A} 1) = n/2`. A combinatorial identity for set families. Distinct from SetFamily/LYM.

Name: Intersecting.lean
Description: Intersecting families: `Intersecting A` — every two sets in `A` have nonempty intersection. The Erdős–Ko–Rado theorem. Distinct from SetFamily/Shadow.

Name: HarrisKleitman.lean
Description: Harris–Kleitman inequality (FKG inequality for sets): two increasing set families `A, B ⊆ 2^{[n]}` satisfy `|A ∩ B| / 2^n ≥ (|A| / 2^n)(|B| / 2^n)`. Distinct from SetFamily/Kleitman.

Name: Young
Description: Young tableaux and Young diagrams: the combinatorics of partitions, the RSK correspondence, and Schur polynomials.

Name: YoungDiagram.lean
Description: Young diagrams: `YoungDiagram` — partitions represented as sets of boxes `{(i,j) | j < λᵢ}`. The dominance partial order and the conjugate diagram. Distinct from Young/SemistandardTableau.

Name: SemistandardTableau.lean
Description: Semistandard Young tableaux: fillings of a Young diagram with positive integers, weakly increasing along rows and strictly increasing down columns. The RSK correspondence setup. Distinct from Young/YoungDiagram.

Name: Tiling
Description: Tiling problems: covering a region with tiles of fixed shapes, with applications to combinatorial geometry.

Name: Tile.lean
Description: Tilings: `Tiling T S` — a covering of `S` by non-overlapping translates of a tile `T`. Basic definitions and conditions for a collection to be a tiling. Distinct from Analysis/Convex/Basic.

Name: SimpleGraph
Description: Simple graphs (undirected, no self-loops): the SimpleGraph typeclass, adjacency, degree, connectivity, and graph coloring.

Name: Triangle
Description: Triangles in graphs: the triangle-free condition, the Ramsey number R(3,3) = 6, the triangle removal lemma (few triangles implies the graph can be made triangle-free by removing few edges), and the friendship theorem. Distinct from Coloring (chromatic number) and Regularity (the lemma used to prove triangle results).

Name: Removal.lean
Description: Triangle removal lemma.

Name: Tripartite.lean
Description: Tripartite graphs and triangle counting.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Triangles in simple graphs: `SimpleGraph.IsTriangle` and triangle counting.

Name: Counting.lean
Description: Triangle counting: the triangle-counting lemma.

Name: Regularity
Description: Szemerédi's regularity lemma: any dense graph can be partitioned into a bounded number of parts so that most pairs of parts form an ε-regular bipartite graph (edges distributed almost uniformly). The counting lemma and triangle removal lemma as corollaries. Used to prove Roth's theorem and Szemerédi's theorem via the graph-theoretic approach.

Name: Increment.lean
Description: Energy increment step in Szemerédi's proof.

Name: Lemma.lean
Description: Szemerédi regularity lemma.

Name: Uniform.lean
Description: Uniform pairs in Szemerédi regularity.

Name: Equitabilise.lean
Description: Equitabilisation of partitions.

Name: Bound.lean
Description: Szemerédi regularity bound: the quantitative version.

Name: Chunk.lean
Description: Regular partition chunks.

Name: Energy.lean
Description: Energy of a partition: the Szemerédi energy increment argument.

Name: Walk
Description: Walks, trails, paths, and cycles in graphs: a walk is a sequence v₀ e₁ v₁ … eₙ vₙ; a trail avoids repeated edges; a path avoids repeated vertices. The length, support, and reversal of walks. Eulerian trails (traverse all edges exactly once: exist iff ≤ 2 vertices of odd degree). Distinct from Connectivity (global connectivity) and Ends (infinite walks).

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on graph walks.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Maps on graph walks.

Name: Traversal.lean
Description: Traversal properties of walks.

Name: Subwalks.lean
Description: Subwalks of a graph walk.

Name: Chord.lean
Description: Chords in graph walks.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic walk API.

Name: Decomp.lean
Description: Decomposition of walks.

Name: Counting.lean
Description: Counting graph walks.

Name: UnitDistance
Description: Unit distance graphs: graphs where vertices are points in ℝ² and edges connect points at distance exactly 1. The chromatic number of the plane (Hadwiger-Nelson problem: 4 ≤ χ ≤ 7). The Moser spindle and other unit distance graphs requiring ≥ 4 colors. A specialized topic at the intersection of combinatorics and geometry.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unit distance graphs: vertices at distance 1.

Name: Connectivity
Description: Connectivity of graphs: connected graphs, k-connected graphs (remain connected after removing k-1 vertices), cuts, bridges, and articulation points. Menger's theorem: the minimum vertex cut equals the maximum number of internally disjoint paths. Contains depth-first search and spanning trees. Distinct from Walk (individual walks/paths) and Ends (infinite graph ends).

Name: Represents.lean
Description: Representatives of connected components.

Name: Subgraph.lean
Description: Connectivity in subgraphs.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite connectivity.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connected graphs and components.

Name: EdgeConnectivity.lean
Description: Edge connectivity: minimum edge cut.

Name: Coloring
Description: Graph coloring: a proper vertex coloring assigns colors to vertices so adjacent vertices get different colors. The chromatic number χ(G). Brook's theorem (χ(G) ≤ Δ(G) unless G is complete or odd cycle). The four-color theorem (planar graphs are 4-colorable). Distinct from Triangle (triangles specifically) and Regularity (structural decompositions).

Name: VertexColoring.lean
Description: Vertex colourings: `G.Coloring n`.

Name: EdgeLabeling.lean
Description: Edge labelings of graphs.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of graph colourings.

Name: Extremal
Description: Extremal graph theory: maximizing or minimizing edges subject to forbidden subgraph conditions. Turán's theorem (ex(n, Kᵣ) and the Turán graph), the Kővári-Sós-Turán theorem (forbidden complete bipartite subgraphs), and the Bondy-Simonovits theorem. Distinct from Regularity (Szemerédi's lemma used to prove extremal results).

Name: TuranDensity.lean
Description: Turán density: `π(H) = lim ex(n,H)/C(n,2)`.

Name: Turan.lean
Description: Turán's theorem: max edges in K_r-free graphs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Extremal graph theory: basic results.

Name: Ends
Description: Ends of infinite graphs: an end is an equivalence class of rays (infinite paths) that cannot be separated by a finite vertex set. The space of ends with the Freudenthal compactification. Used in the study of infinite groups (Stallings' theorem: a group with > 1 end splits). Distinct from Connectivity (finite connectivity) and Walk (finite paths).

Name: Properties.lean
Description: Properties of graph ends.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Ends of a graph: `G.End` — equivalence classes of rays.

Name: Matching.lean
Description: Matchings: `G.IsMatching M` — a subgraph where every vertex has degree ≤ 1. Perfect matchings and the matching number. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Hall.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Graph homomorphisms: `G →g H` — maps on vertices preserving adjacency. `SimpleGraph.Hom`, `SimpleGraph.Embedding`, `SimpleGraph.Iso`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Metric.lean
Description: Graph metric: `G.dist u v` — the length of the shortest path. Properties: triangle inequality, `dist u v = 0 ↔ u = v` in connected graphs. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Diam.

Name: Partition.lean
Description: Graph colourings and partitions: `G.Colorable n` (n-colourability), chromatic number `G.chromaticNumber`, and the relationship with independent set partitions. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Clique.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Graph operations: complement `Gᶜ`, disjoint union `G ⊕ H`, join (add all cross-edges), and their properties. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic and SimpleGraph/Prod.

Name: IncMatrix.lean
Description: Incidence matrix: `G.incMatrix R` — the `V × E` matrix with `1` at `(v, e)` iff `v ∈ e`. Kirchhoff's theorem: the number of spanning trees equals any cofactor of the Laplacian. Distinct from SimpleGraph/LapMatrix.

Name: Hasse.lean
Description: Hasse diagram of a partial order as a graph: `G.HasseGraph P` — edges connect `a` and `b` when `a ⋖ b` (a is covered by b). Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic and Order/SuccPred/Relation.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialisation/import file for SimpleGraph: aggregates imports so that `import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Init` provides the core theory. Contains no new results.

Name: LineGraph.lean
Description: Line graph: `G.lineGraph` — the graph on `G.edgeSet` where two edges are adjacent iff they share a vertex. Whitney's theorem: line graphs of connected graphs with ≥ 5 vertices are determined by their complement. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: LapMatrix.lean
Description: Laplacian matrix: `G.lapMatrix R = G.degMatrix R - G.adjMatrix R`. The Kirchhoff matrix-tree theorem. Distinct from SimpleGraph/AdjMatrix.

Name: Tutte.lean
Description: Tutte's theorem: a graph has a perfect matching iff for every `S ⊆ V`, the number of odd components of `G - S` is ≤ `|S|`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Matching.

Name: Trails.lean
Description: Trails and Eulerian circuits: a trail visits each edge at most once; an Eulerian trail visits each edge exactly once. Characterisation: Eulerian iff connected with even degree. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Walk.

Name: UniversalVerts.lean
Description: Universal vertices: a vertex adjacent to all other vertices. Properties and interaction with graph operations. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Walk.lean
Description: Walks in graphs: `G.Walk u v` — a finite sequence of adjacent vertices from `u` to `v`. Operations: appending, reversing, converting to paths. The walk type is foundational for connectivity. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Paths.

Name: VertexCover.lean
Description: Vertex covers: `G.IsVertexCover C` — every edge has at least one endpoint in `C`. König's theorem: in bipartite graphs, max matching = min vertex cover. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Matching.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Graph products: Cartesian product `G □ H`, tensor product `G × H`, and strong product. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Operations.

Name: Paths.lean
Description: Paths in graphs: `G.Path u v` — a walk with no repeated vertices. Properties: existence in connected graphs, the unique path in trees. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Walk and SimpleGraph/Acyclic.

Name: StronglyRegular.lean
Description: Strongly regular graphs: `G.IsStronglyRegular k λ μ` — every vertex has `k` neighbours, adjacent vertices share `λ` common neighbours, non-adjacent share `μ`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Graph sum (union): `G ⊔ H` — the graph with all edges of both `G` and `H`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Operations.

Name: Subgraph.lean
Description: Subgraphs: `G.Subgraph` — a sub-relation of `G.Adj`. The lattice of subgraphs, spanning subgraphs, and induced subgraphs. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Clique.lean
Description: Cliques and independent sets: `G.IsClique s` (all pairs adjacent), `G.IsAntichain s` (no pair adjacent). Clique number `G.clique_num` and chromatic number bounds. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Circulant.lean
Description: Circulant graphs: Cayley graphs of `ℤ/nℤ`, where adjacency depends only on the difference `|u - v|`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Cayley.

Name: CompleteMultipartite.lean
Description: Complete multipartite graphs: `completemultipartiteGraph P` for a partition `P` — adjacent iff in different parts. Turán graphs as complete `r`-partite. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Dart.lean
Description: Darts (directed edges): `G.Dart` — oriented edges `(u, v)` with `G.Adj u v`, used for the Euler characteristic and Eulerian circuit theory. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Copy.lean
Description: Subgraph copies (induced subgraph isomorphism): `G.HasCopy H` — `G` contains a copy of `H` as a subgraph. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Subgraph and SimpleGraph/Maps.

Name: AdjMatrix.lean
Description: Adjacency matrix: `G.adjMatrix R` — the `V × V` matrix with `1` at `(u, v)` iff `G.Adj u v`. The eigenvalue characterisation of regularity and connectivity. Distinct from SimpleGraph/LapMatrix.

Name: Acyclic.lean
Description: Acyclic graphs and forests: `G.IsAcyclic` — no cycle exists. Characterisation: a connected acyclic graph is a tree; `|E| = |V| - 1`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Paths and SimpleGraph/Trails.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simple graphs: `SimpleGraph V` — an irreflexive symmetric binary relation `Adj : V → V → Prop`. Core API: `SimpleGraph.Adj`, `SimpleGraph.degree`, `SimpleGraph.neighborSet`. The foundational file.

Name: Cayley.lean
Description: Cayley graphs: for a group `G` and generating set `S`, the Cayley graph `cayleygraph G S` has `G` as vertices and `g ∼ h ↔ g⁻¹h ∈ S`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Bipartite.lean
Description: Bipartite graphs: `G.IsBipartite` — the vertex set has a 2-colouring with no monochromatic edge. Characterisation: no odd cycle. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: FiveWheelLike.lean
Description: `IsFiveWheelLike`: a graph structure used to prove `colorable_iff_isCompleteMultipartite_of_maximal_cliqueFree` (maximally K_{r+1}-free graphs are r-colorable iff complete-multipartite) and the Andrásfai-Erdős-Sós theorem `colorable_of_cliqueFree_lt_minDegree`. Also defines `IsPathGraph3Compl`.

Name: Finsubgraph.lean
Description: Finitely generated subgraphs: `G.subgraphGeneratedBy s` for a finite set `s ⊆ V`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Subgraph.

Name: Girth.lean
Description: Girth of a graph: `G.girth` — the length of the shortest cycle. The girth-chromatic number inequality. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Acyclic and SimpleGraph/Trails.

Name: Hamiltonian.lean
Description: Hamiltonian cycles and paths: `G.IsHamiltonian` — a cycle visiting every vertex exactly once. Dirac's theorem: minimum degree ≥ n/2 implies Hamiltonian. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Trails.

Name: Hall.lean
Description: Hall's theorem for bipartite graphs: a bipartite graph has a perfect matching iff for every set `S` of vertices on one side, `|N(S)| ≥ |S|`. Distinct from Combinatorics/Hall/Basic.

Name: DeleteEdges.lean
Description: Edge deletion: `G.deleteEdges S` — removing edges `S ⊆ G.edgeSet`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Subgraph and SimpleGraph/Operations.

Name: DegreeSum.lean
Description: Handshaking lemma: `∑ v, G.degree v = 2 * G.edgeFinset.card`. The degree sequence and its parity constraint. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Finite.

Name: Density.lean
Description: Edge density of a graph: `G.edgeDensity A B = |E(A,B)| / (|A| * |B|)` between two vertex sets. Used in Szemerédi regularity. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Finite.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite graph theory: `Fintype V` instances, `G.edgeFinset`, `G.vertexCount`. Basic enumerative results for finite graphs. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Diam.lean
Description: Diameter of a graph: `G.diam` — the maximum distance between connected vertices. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Metric and SimpleGraph/Paths.

Name: Digraph
Description: Directed graphs (digraphs): reachability, strongly connected components, and topological sorting.

Name: Orientation.lean
Description: Orientations of simple graphs: an orientation is an assignment of a direction to each edge. `SimpleGraph.toDigraph` and conditions for an orientation to be acyclic. Distinct from Digraph/Basic and SimpleGraph/Acyclic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Directed graphs: `Digraph V` — a binary relation `Adj : V → V → Prop` (possibly with self-loops). Basic operations: image, preimage, union, complement. Distinct from Digraph/Orientation and SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Derangements
Description: Derangements (permutations with no fixed points): the subfactorial formula and inclusion-exclusion derivation.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Derangements of a finite type: a derangement is a permutation with no fixed points. The count is Dn = n! · ∑_{k=0}^{n} (-1)^k / k!, the nearest integer to n!/e. Formalized as Fintype.card_derangements with the explicit formula proved via inclusion-exclusion. Used in probability (hat-check problem), combinatorics, and analysis of random permutations.

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: Exponential generating function of derangements: `∑ D_n x^n/n! = e^{-x}/(1-x)`. Distinct from Derangements/Basic and Combinatorics/Enumerative/Bell.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Derangements: permutations with no fixed points. The recurrence `D_n = (n-1)(D_{n-1} + D_{n-2})` and the inclusion-exclusion formula `D_n = n! ∑_{k=0}^n (-1)^k/k!`. Distinct from Derangements/Exponential.

Name: Additive
Description: Additive combinatorics: sum sets, Cauchy-Davenport theorem, Freiman's theorem, and additive energy.

Name: Corner
Description: Corners in additive combinatorics: a corner is a triple (x, y), (x+d, y), (x, y+d) in ℤ² or a finite group. Ajtai-Szemerédi's corner theorem: every dense subset of [N]² contains a corner. Distinct from AP (one-dimensional APs) — corners are the 2-dimensional analogue and require different Fourier-analytic techniques.

Name: Roth.lean
Description: Corners theorem: dense subsets of `[N]²` contain corners.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Combinatorial corners: `IsCorner a b c` in a 2D grid.

Name: AP
Description: Arithmetic progressions in additive combinatorics: the density of sets containing k-term APs (Szemerédi's theorem), Roth's theorem (3-term APs in dense sets via Fourier analysis), and the Green-Tao theorem (primes contain arbitrarily long APs). Contains the AP regularity lemma setup. Distinct from Corner (2-dimensional APs).

Name: Three
Description: Three-term arithmetic progressions (3-APs): sets {a, a+d, a+2d} ⊂ G for d ≠ 0. Roth's theorem: any subset of {1,...,N} of density > 0 contains a 3-AP. The Fourier-analytic proof (bounding the 3-AP count via the Fourier transform) and Meshulam's theorem for finite abelian groups. Distinct from Combinatorics/Additive/AP (the general k-AP theory).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: 3-term arithmetic progressions: `ThreeAP a b c` iff `2b = a + c`.

Name: Behrend.lean
Description: Behrend's construction: sets without 3-term APs of size `exp(-c√(log n)) * n`.

Name: PluenneckeRuzsa.lean
Description: Plünnecke–Ruzsa inequality: if `|A + B| ≤ K|A|` then `|nB - mB| ≤ Kⁿ⁺ᵐ|A|`. Distinct from Additive/ETransform and Additive/DoublingConst.

Name: Randomisation.lean
Description: Probabilistic / randomisation arguments in additive combinatorics: random subset arguments proving existence of sets with good additive properties. Distinct from Additive/Energy.

Name: ETransform.lean
Description: E-transform (Plünnecke's trick): the e-transform of a set to reduce the doubling constant while preserving structure. Distinct from Additive/PluenneckeRuzsa and Additive/Energy.

Name: FreimanHom.lean
Description: Freiman homomorphisms: `FreimanHom A B s` — maps `f : α → β` preserving `s`-fold sumsets `f(a₁)+⋯+f(aₛ) = f(b₁)+⋯+f(bₛ)` whenever `a₁+⋯+aₛ = b₁+⋯+bₛ` in `A`. Distinct from Additive/ApproximateSubgroup.

Name: SubsetSum.lean
Description: Subset sums: `∑_{a ∈ S} a` for `S ⊆ A` and the sumset `Σ_A = {∑_{a ∈ S} a | S ⊆ A}`. Distinct from Additive/Dissociation and Additive/ErdosGinzburgZiv.

Name: VerySmallDoubling.lean
Description: Sets with very small doubling: if `|A + A| < 3|A|/2` then `A` is contained in a coset of a subgroup. Distinct from Additive/SmallTripling and Additive/DoublingConst.

Name: RuzsaCovering.lean
Description: Ruzsa's covering lemma: if `|A + B| ≤ K|B|` then `A ⊆ T + B - B` for some set `T` with `|T| ≤ K`. Distinct from Additive/CovBySMul and Additive/PluenneckeRuzsa.

Name: SmallTripling.lean
Description: Small tripling sets: sets with `|A + A + A| ≤ K|A|` and their structure (related to Freiman's theorem). Distinct from Additive/VerySmallDoubling.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Additive convolution of functions: `f ∗ g (x) = ∑_{a+b=x} f(a) g(b)` in an additive group. The `l2` norm, Young's inequality for additive convolution. Distinct from MeasureTheory/Group/Convolution.

Name: CovBySMul.lean
Description: Covering by dilates: `CovBySMul A B` — the set `B` can be covered by `K` translates of `A`. Used in Ruzsa's covering lemma. Distinct from Additive/RuzsaCovering.

Name: ApproximateSubgroup.lean
Description: Approximate subgroups: `K`-approximate subgroups `A` satisfying `A + A ⊆ K * A` (Freiman–Ruzsa type). Properties and the polynomial Freiman–Ruzsa conjecture context. Distinct from Additive/FreimanHom and Additive/SmallTripling.

Name: CauchyDavenport.lean
Description: Cauchy–Davenport theorem: for nonempty `A, B ⊆ ℤ/pℤ`, `|A + B| ≥ min(p, |A| + |B| - 1)`. Distinct from Additive/PluenneckeRuzsa and Additive/ErdosGinzburgZiv.

Name: Energy.lean
Description: Additive energy: `E(A) = |{(a,b,c,d) ∈ A⁴ | a+b=c+d}|` — a measure of additive structure in `A`. The Cauchy–Schwarz bound `|A+A|² ≥ |A|⁴/E(A)`. Distinct from Additive/DoublingConst.

Name: ErdosGinzburgZiv.lean
Description: Erdős–Ginzburg–Ziv theorem: any `2n-1` integers contain `n` with sum divisible by `n`. Distinct from Additive/CauchyDavenport and Additive/SubsetSum.

Name: Dissociation.lean
Description: Dissociated sets: a set `A` is dissociated if all subset sums `∑_{a ∈ S} ε_a a` (with `ε_a ∈ {0,1}`) are distinct. Distinct from Additive/Energy and Additive/SubsetSum.

Name: DoublingConst.lean
Description: Doubling constant: `doublingConst A = |A + A| / |A|` for a finite set `A` in an additive group. Properties and lower bounds. Distinct from Additive/PluenneckeRuzsa.

Name: Enumerative
Description: Enumerative combinatorics: binomial coefficients, Catalan numbers, Stirling numbers, generating functions, and formal power series methods.

Name: Partition
Description: Integer partitions: unordered decompositions of n as a sum of positive integers, with the partition function p(n). Young diagrams as visual representations. Partition-theoretic identities: Euler's theorem (partitions into odd parts = partitions into distinct parts), and the Rogers-Ramanujan identities. Distinct from Catalan (trees) and Young tableaux.

Name: Glaisher.lean
Description: Glaisher's theorem: the number of partitions of n into parts not divisible by d equals the number of partitions where no part is repeated d or more times. The case d = 2 gives Euler's partition theorem: partitions into odd parts biject with partitions into distinct parts. The bijection is explicit, using the binary representation of multiplicities. Foundational in the combinatorics of integer partitions.

Name: GenFun.lean
Description: Generating functions for partitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Integer partitions: definitions and basic properties.

Name: Catalan
Description: Catalan numbers Cₙ = C(2n,n)/(n+1): the number of binary trees with n+1 leaves, triangulations of a convex polygon, Dyck paths, and many other combinatorial objects. The recurrence Cₙ₊₁ = ∑ CᵢCₙ₋ᵢ and the generating function. Distinct from Enumerative/Partition (integer partitions) — Catalan numbers count plane trees.

Name: Tree.lean
Description: Catalan numbers count binary trees.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Catalan numbers: definitions and recurrence.

Name: Partition.lean
Description: Integer partitions: `Nat.Partition n` — multisets of positive integers summing to `n`. Distinct from Enumerative/Composition (ordered) and Order/Partition/Finpartition.

Name: InclusionExclusion.lean
Description: The inclusion-exclusion principle: |A₁ ∪ … ∪ Aₙ| = ∑|Aᵢ| - ∑|Aᵢ ∩ Aⱼ| + … + (-1)^{n+1}|A₁ ∩ … ∩ Aₙ|. The abstract Möbius inversion form and the Finset combinatorial version. Used to count derangements, compute Euler's totient function φ(n) = n ∏(1 - 1/p), and derive the Möbius function in number theory. The fundamental counting tool for finite families of overlapping sets.

Name: Pentagonal.lean
Description: Pentagonal number theorem: Euler's identity `∏_{n≥1}(1-qⁿ) = ∑_{k∈ℤ} (-1)^k q^{k(3k-1)/2}`. Distinct from Enumerative/Partition.

Name: Stirling.lean
Description: Stirling numbers: `stirling n k` (first kind: signed, number of permutations with k cycles; second kind: number of partitions into k nonempty blocks). Recurrences and generating functions. Distinct from Enumerative/Bell.

Name: Schroder.lean
Description: Schröder numbers: `schroeder n` counting lattice paths from `(0,0)` to `(n,n)` using steps `(1,0)`, `(0,1)`, `(1,1)` that stay below the diagonal. Distinct from Enumerative/Catalan.

Name: IncidenceAlgebra.lean
Description: Incidence algebra of a poset: `IncidenceAlgebra R P` — functions on intervals `[x, y] ⊆ P` with Dirichlet-style convolution. The Möbius function as the inverse of the zeta function. Distinct from NumberTheory/ArithmeticFunction/Moebius.

Name: Catalan.lean
Description: Catalan numbers: `catalan n = C(2n, n) / (n+1)` — counting Dyck paths, binary trees, triangulations. The recurrence `C_{n+1} = ∑ C_i C_{n-i}`. Distinct from Enumerative/DyckWord.

Name: Bell.lean
Description: Bell numbers: `Bell n` — the number of partitions of an `n`-element set. The exponential generating function `∑ Bell_n x^n/n! = e^{e^x - 1}`. Distinct from Enumerative/Partition and Enumerative/Stirling.

Name: Composition.lean
Description: Compositions of a natural number: `Composition n` — ordered tuples `(a₁, …, aₖ)` of positive integers summing to `n`. The bijection with subsets of `{1, …, n-1}`. Distinct from Enumerative/Partition (unordered).

Name: DyckWord.lean
Description: Dyck words: balanced strings of `(` and `)`, counted by Catalan numbers. The bijection with well-parenthesised expressions. Distinct from Enumerative/Catalan.

Name: DoubleCounting.lean
Description: Double counting principle: algebraic formulation of counting a set in two ways, giving identities among binomial coefficients. Distinct from Enumerative/Partition.

Name: Hall
Description: Hall's marriage theorem: a bipartite graph has a perfect matching iff Hall's condition holds, with generalizations.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Hall's marriage theorem (finite case): the finite version and its constructive proof via augmenting paths or bipartite matching. Distinct from Hall/Basic and SimpleGraph/Matching.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Hall's marriage theorem (general): for a finite set family `{Aᵢ}`, a system of distinct representatives exists iff `|⋃ᵢ∈S Aᵢ| ≥ |S|` for all finite `S`. Distinct from Hall/Finite.

Name: Graph
Description: General graph theory: graph homomorphisms, graph products, and structural graph theory.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Graph homomorphisms: maps `f : G →g H` preserving adjacency. Isomorphisms and the category of graphs. Distinct from Graph/Basic and SimpleGraph/Maps.

Name: Subgraph.lean
Description: Subgraphs of a multigraph: `G.Subgraph` — a sub-multigraph specifying a subset of edges and their vertices. Distinct from Graph/Delete and SimpleGraph/Subgraph.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice of graphs: the partial order on graphs by sub-multigraph inclusion, forming a complete lattice. Distinct from Graph/Subgraph and SimpleGraph/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Abstract graphs (multigraphs): `Graph V E` — a type of vertices `V`, a type of edges `E`, and incidence maps `G.source e` and `G.target e`. Distinct from SimpleGraph/Basic (simple graphs) and Digraph/Basic.

Name: Delete.lean
Description: Edge and vertex deletion in graphs: `G.deleteEdge e` and `G.deleteVert v`. Distinct from Graph/Subgraph and SimpleGraph/DeleteEdges.

Name: Extremal
Description: Extremal combinatorics: Turan's theorem, the Zarankiewicz problem, and Ramsey theory.

Name: RuzsaSzemeredi.lean
Description: Ruzsa–Szezeredi theorem: a 3-uniform hypergraph on `n` vertices whose edges can be decomposed into matchings has at most `o(n²)` edges. Implies sum-free set results. Distinct from SetFamily/KruskalKatona.

Name: Nullstellensatz.lean
Description: The combinatorial Nullstellensatz (Alon's theorem): a polynomial method proving existence of nonzero points in combinatorial problems.

Name: KatonaCircle.lean
Description: Katona's circle method: a symmetric chain decomposition argument proving the Erdos-Ko-Rado theorem for intersecting families.

Name: Schnirelmann.lean
Description: Schnirelmann density and the Schnirelmann-Mann theorem: additive basis results for subsets of the natural numbers.

Name: Pigeonhole.lean
Description: The pigeonhole principle: if n+1 objects are placed in n boxes, some box contains at least two objects, with finset variants.

Name: Hindman.lean
Description: Hindman's theorem: any finite coloring of N contains a monochromatic IP set (set of all finite sums of an infinite sequence).

Name: Compactness.lean
Description: Compactness arguments in combinatorics: proofs of infinite Ramsey-type results from their finite versions via ultrafilters.

Name: Colex.lean
Description: The colexicographic order on finite sets: the colex ordering on Finset N and its role in the Kruskal-Katona theorem.

Name: HalesJewett.lean
Description: The Hales-Jewett theorem: any finite coloring of the combinatorial lines of a cube contains a monochromatic line.

Name: Configuration.lean
Description: Combinatorial configurations: incidence geometries and their properties, including projective and affine planes.

Name: CategoryTheory
Description: Provides definitions of categories, functors, natural transformations, adjunctions, and monads. Used to unify constructions across different fields, prove universal properties, and formalize mathematical abstraction. Relied on by algebra, algebraic topology, algebraic geometry, logic, and theoretical computer science.

Name: PathCategory
Description: The free category on a quiver: objects are quiver vertices, morphisms are paths, and composition is path concatenation.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Morphism properties on path categories: characterises which morphisms of PathCategory Q satisfy a given MorphismProperty, with stability results under composition and retract. Distinct from PathCategory/Basic (which defines the path category and its universal property).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Defines the free category PathCategory Q on a quiver Q (directed graph): objects are vertices, morphisms are composable paths of edges. Contains the universal property (any quiver morphism Q → forget(C) extends uniquely to a functor PathCategory Q → C) and the path functor. Distinct from PathCategory/MorphismProperty (which classifies morphisms within this construction).

Name: ObjectProperty
Description: Properties of objects in a category: conditions on objects that are stable under isomorphism and their associated closure operations.

Name: FunctorCategory
Description: Object properties in functor categories: lifting object properties (e.g., injectivity, projectivity) from a target category C to the functor category [J, C] pointwise. Used to establish that injective sheaves are pointwise injective under suitable conditions.

Name: PreservesLimits.lean
Description: Object properties preserved by functors: limit-preservation conditions.

Name: LimitsClosure.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under all limits: LimitsClosure P and its interaction with finite and cofiltered limits. Distinct from ObjectProperty/LimitsOfShape (one fixed shape) and ObjectProperty/ColimitsClosure.

Name: LimitsOfShape.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under limits of a specific diagram shape J: the predicate LimitsOfShape J P. Distinct from ObjectProperty/LimitsClosure (all limit shapes simultaneously) and ObjectProperty/ColimitsOfShape.

Name: Local.lean
Description: Local ObjectProperty relative to a Grothendieck topology: a property P is local if it can be verified on a covering family. Used in sheaf theory to define local properties of objects. Distinct from ObjectProperty/SiteLocal (which specialises to a concrete site) and Sites/LocalProperties.

Name: Ind.lean
Description: ObjectProperty for ind-objects: defines which objects of Ind(C) are 'essentially in' a subcategory of C and establishes the closure under filtered colimits. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ColimitsClosure (general colimit closure without the Ind-category perspective).

Name: InheritedFromHom.lean
Description: Object properties inherited from hom-set conditions: predicates on X determined by the behaviour of Hom(X, −) or Hom(−, X), such as compactness or injectivity conditions. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Orthogonal (the explicit orthogonality formulation) and ObjectProperty/Basic.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under kernels in a preadditive or abelian category: KernelClosure P and basic stability results. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Extensions (triangulated extension closure) and ObjectProperty/ContainsZero.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Transfer of an ObjectProperty to the opposite category: the opposite property P.op and its basic instances. A structural file enabling duality arguments across ObjectProperty. Distinct from all other ObjectProperty siblings which work in the forward direction.

Name: ShiftAdditive.lean
Description: ObjectProperty closed under shifts in an additive shifted category: combines additive and shift structures, defining when P is stable under X ↦ X⟦n⟧ for all n. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Shift (single degree) and ObjectProperty/ClosureShift (iterative closure).

Name: SiteLocal.lean
Description: Object properties that are local for a specific Grothendieck site: specialises ObjectProperty/Local to a concrete site structure. Relates to CategoryTheory/Sites machinery and the local-to-global principle for properties. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Local (abstract topology).

Name: Small.lean
Description: The ObjectProperty Small: an object is small if its underlying set (via a concrete forgetful functor) is small in the universe sense. Contains basic instances and implications. Distinct from ObjectProperty/HasCardinalLT (cardinal-bound version) and Presentable files.

Name: Orthogonal.lean
Description: Orthogonality for ObjectProperty: defines when two properties P and Q are orthogonal (every morphism from a P-object to a Q-object is zero or factors through the localisation). Used in reflective subcategory theory. Distinct from ObjectProperty/EpiMono and ObjectProperty/InheritedFromHom.

Name: Retract.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under retracts: RetractClosure P, proving that if X ∈ P and Y is a retract of X then Y ∈ P. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ClosedUnderIsomorphisms (isomorphism closure, which is strictly stronger).

Name: Shift.lean
Description: The ObjectProperty obtained by shifting an existing property at a single degree n under the shift functor: Shift P n and its basic instances. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ClosureShift (the closure under all shifts) and ObjectProperty/ShiftAdditive.

Name: HasCardinalLT.lean
Description: The object property HasCardinalLT κ asserting that an object has presentability-rank strictly less than a cardinal κ. Used in the theory of locally presentable and accessible categories. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Small (set-theoretic smallness).

Name: ColimitsCardinalClosure.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under colimits indexed by categories of cardinality less than a given cardinal κ. Provides the CardinalColimitsClosure predicate used in the theory of locally presentable categories. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ColimitsClosure (all colimits) and ObjectProperty/ColimitsOfShape (one fixed shape).

Name: ColimitsClosure.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under all colimits: the ColimitsClosure P predicate asserting that if every object in a diagram satisfies P then the colimit does. Contains the interaction with filtered and finite colimits. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ColimitsOfShape (closure under a fixed shape J) and ObjectProperty/LimitsClosure.

Name: ColimitsOfShape.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under colimits of a specific diagram shape J: the predicate ColimitsOfShape J P and passage between different index shapes. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ColimitsClosure (all shapes simultaneously) and ObjectProperty/LimitsOfShape.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for ObjectProperty: the type P : ObjectProperty C of predicates on objects of a category C, closure under isomorphisms, the subtype of satisfying objects, and lattice operations. Foundation for all sibling ObjectProperty files.

Name: ClosedUnderIsomorphisms.lean
Description: The predicate ClosedUnderIsomorphisms P: if X satisfies P and X ≅ Y then Y satisfies P. Contains the typeclass, the instance that any IsIso-closed property is ClosedUnderIsomorphisms, and the basic interaction with full subcategories. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Equivalence (which transports a property along an equivalence of categories).

Name: ClosureShift.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under the shift functor of a shifted category: defines when P is stable under X ↦ X⟦n⟧ and gives the smallest shift-closed superproperty. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Shift (which defines the shifted property at a single degree) and ObjectProperty/ShiftAdditive (shift-closure in the additive setting).

Name: CompleteLattice.lean
Description: The complete lattice structure on ObjectProperty C: infima and suprema of families of object predicates and the Galois connection with full subcategories. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Basic (which only sets up the basic type and operations) and ObjectProperty/FullSubcategory.

Name: Extensions.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under extensions in a triangulated or exact category: ExtensionClosure P, proved to be the smallest property containing P and closed under distinguished triangles. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ColimitsClosure (colimit closure) and ObjectProperty/Orthogonal.

Name: FiniteProducts.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under finite products: FiniteProductsClosure P and basic instances for preadditive and abelian categories. Distinct from ObjectProperty/LimitsClosure (all limits) and ObjectProperty/ColimitsOfShape.

Name: FullSubcategory.lean
Description: The full subcategory FullSubcategory P on objects satisfying P, with the fully faithful inclusion functor and reflection of isomorphisms. Distinct from ObjectProperty/Basic (the predicate type) and ObjectProperty/Equivalence (equivalence transport).

Name: ContainsZero.lean
Description: The predicate ContainsZero P asserting that P contains the zero object of a pointed or preadditive category. Proves basic instances and closure under direct summands. Distinct from ObjectProperty/EpiMono (epi/mono closure) and ObjectProperty/Kernels.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Closure of an ObjectProperty under epimorphisms and monomorphisms: EpiClosure P and MonoClosure P, with instances for preadditive and abelian categories. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ContainsZero (zero-object closure) and ObjectProperty/Orthogonal.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Transfer of an ObjectProperty along a category equivalence: the induced property on the equivalent category and the proof that ClosedUnderIsomorphisms is preserved. Distinct from ObjectProperty/ClosedUnderIsomorphisms (within a single fixed category).

Name: MorphismProperty
Description: Properties of morphisms in a category: classes of maps stable under composition (monomorphisms, epimorphisms, isomorphisms, etc.).

Name: OfObjectProperty.lean
Description: Morphism properties derived from object properties: given an ObjectProperty P, the induced predicate on morphisms whose source or target (or both) satisfies P. Bridges the ObjectProperty and MorphismProperty hierarchies. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Basic (free predicates).

Name: OverAdjunction.lean
Description: Morphism properties for over-categories and their adjunctions: the interaction between W on C and the induced property on slice categories C/X, with adjunction-lifting results. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Comma (general comma) and MorphismProperty/Limits.

Name: Local.lean
Description: Morphism properties that are local for a Grothendieck topology: the Local typeclass asserting that W can be checked after pulling back along a covering family. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Descent and Sites/LocalProperties.

Name: LiftingProperty.lean
Description: The left and right lifting property for morphism properties: HasLeftLiftingProperty and HasRightLiftingProperty, and the proof that each class is the other's orthogonal complement. Foundation for weak factorization systems and model category theory. Distinct from MorphismProperty/WeakFactorizationSystem (combines both sides into a WFS).

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Stability of a MorphismProperty under limits and colimits: IsStableUnderLimitsOfShape, IsStableUnderFilteredColimits, and related typeclasses. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Composition (composition stability) and MorphismProperty/FunctorCategory.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: The quotient of a category by a MorphismProperty: the localised category C[W⁻¹] and the universal functor Q : C → C[W⁻¹] as a quotient construction. Distinct from Localization/Construction (which builds the full localization with its universal property) and MorphismProperty/IsInvertedBy.

Name: TransfiniteComposition.lean
Description: Closure of a MorphismProperty under transfinite composition: the saturation of W under ordinal-indexed colimit sequences and the proof that pushouts of W-maps are in the saturation. Key for the small object argument. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Composition (finite composition) and SmallObject/TransfiniteIteration.

Name: WeakFactorizationSystem.lean
Description: Weak factorization systems (L, R): defines WFS, the retract and lifting arguments, and mutual determination of L and R. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Factorization (strict/orthogonal factorization with uniqueness) and MorphismProperty/LiftingProperty.

Name: RetractArgument.lean
Description: The retract argument: if W is closed under retracts and satisfies a lifting property against a class, then W is saturated by that class. Fundamental to model category theory and the small object argument. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Retract (closure alone) and LiftingProperties/Basic.

Name: Representable.lean
Description: Representable morphism properties: W is representable if membership can be tested fibrewise via a classifying morphism. Used in fibered categories and algebraic stack theory. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Descent (descent vs representability) and ConcreteCategory files.

Name: Retract.lean
Description: Closure of a MorphismProperty under retracts: IsStableUnderRetracts and its basic consequences. Distinct from MorphismProperty/RetractArgument (which uses retract closure to prove a structural theorem) and MorphismProperty/Composition.

Name: IsSmall.lean
Description: The IsSmall predicate on a MorphismProperty: the total space of W-morphisms is small relative to a universe. Used to guarantee the small object argument terminates. Distinct from MorphismProperty/HasCardinalLT (cardinal bound) and SmallObject files.

Name: Composition.lean
Description: Stability of a MorphismProperty under composition: the IsStableUnderComposition typeclass and proofs that composites of W-morphisms lie in W. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Basic (predicate definition only) and MorphismProperty/Factorization.

Name: Concrete.lean
Description: Morphism properties for concrete categories defined by conditions on underlying set maps (injective, surjective, bijective). Distinct from MorphismProperty/Basic (abstract predicates) and ConcreteCategory/EpiMono.

Name: CommaSites.lean
Description: Morphism properties for comma categories equipped with Grothendieck topologies: locally defined morphism properties combining comma-category and site structure. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Comma (without sites) and Sites/MorphismProperty.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for MorphismProperty W: predicates on morphisms of a category, closure under composition and identities, the class IsIso of isomorphisms, and basic set operations (intersection, union, opposite). Foundation for all sibling MorphismProperty files.

Name: Comma.lean
Description: Morphism properties on comma categories (F ↓ G): defines the induced property on morphisms of the comma category in terms of base morphisms, with lifting and descent results. Distinct from MorphismProperty/CommaSites (adds site structure) and MorphismProperty/OfObjectProperty.

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Morphism properties satisfying descent: the Descent typeclass asserting that W can be checked after passing to a covering family, used in algebraic geometry and site theory. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Local and MorphismProperty/CommaSites.

Name: Ind.lean
Description: Morphism properties for Ind-categories: defines which morphisms in Ind(C) lie in the natural extension of a property from C. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Basic and the Ind construction in Presentable.

Name: IsInvertedBy.lean
Description: The predicate IsInvertedBy W F: a functor F sends every W-morphism to an isomorphism. Contains basic instances (localization functors invert W) and interaction with functor composition. Used as the universal property of localization. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Basic and Localization/Predicate.

Name: HasCardinalLT.lean
Description: The morphism property HasCardinalLT κ: morphisms of presentability-rank strictly less than a cardinal κ. Used in the theory of locally presentable categories and the small object argument. Distinct from ObjectProperty/HasCardinalLT (the object version).

Name: Factorization.lean
Description: Orthogonal factorization systems (L, R): the strict factorization with unique diagonal fills, distinct from weak factorization systems. Contains functorial factorizations and uniqueness results. Distinct from MorphismProperty/WeakFactorizationSystem (existence without uniqueness) and MorphismProperty/Composition.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Morphism properties lifted to functor categories [J, C]: the pointwise lifting of W and stability results under natural transformations. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Basic (single category) and MorphismProperty/Limits.

Name: Pi
Description: Pi categories: the product of a family of categories indexed by a type, with componentwise functors and natural transformations.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on a Pi-category ∏ i, C i when each C i is monoidal: componentwise tensor product, unit objects, and verification of the monoidal axioms. Distinct from Pi/Basic (category structure only) and Products/Basic (binary product case).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Products of categories indexed by a type I: the category ∏ i, C i with componentwise morphisms and composition, the projection functors πᵢ, and the universal property. Distinct from Pi/Monoidal (which adds a monoidal structure to this construction) and Products/Basic (finite product of two categories).

Name: Products
Description: Products of categories: the binary product C × D, the product functor, and the Cartesian structure on Cat.

Name: Bifunctor.lean
Description: Bifunctors as functors from a product category: the curry/uncurry equivalence between functors C × D → E and functors C → (D → E), and its interaction with limits and natural transformations. Distinct from Products/Basic (product category structure) and Functor/Currying.

Name: Unitor.lean
Description: The left and right unitor isomorphisms PUnit × C ≅ C and C × PUnit ≅ C for product categories. Distinct from Products/Associator and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Associator.lean
Description: The associator isomorphism (C × D) × E ≅ C × (D × E) of product categories as a functor and natural isomorphism. Distinct from Products/Unitor (left/right unit isomorphisms) and Monoidal/Category (abstract monoidal axioms).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The product category C × D: objects are pairs, morphisms are pairs of morphisms composed componentwise, with projection functors fst and snd and the universal property. Distinct from Products/Bifunctor (bifunctors as functors from the product) and Pi/Basic (infinite indexed products).

Name: Presentable
Description: Presentable categories: locally presentable and accessible categories, with the adjoint functor theorem.

Name: LocallyPresentable.lean
Description: `IsCardinalLocallyPresentable C κ` and `IsCardinalAccessibleCategory C κ` (relative to a regular cardinal κ), and their cardinal-free versions `IsLocallyPresentable` and `IsAccessibleCategory`. Proves that in locally presentable or accessible categories every object is presentable. Reference: Adámek-Rosický 1994.

Name: OrthogonalReflection.lean
Description: Orthogonal reflective localizations of presentable categories: the orthogonal complement of a small set of morphisms determines a reflective full subcategory of a locally presentable category. Distinct from Presentable/Adjunction (general adjoint functor results) and Presentable/Retracts.

Name: IsCardinalFiltered.lean
Description: The IsCardinalFiltered κ typeclass: a category is κ-filtered if every diagram of size < κ has a compatible cocone. Foundation for the accessibility theory at cardinal κ. Distinct from Filtered/Basic (the classical filtered condition) and Presentable/Basic.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in locally presentable categories: every locally presentable category is complete, and limits of accessible functors are accessible. Contains the completeness theorem. Distinct from Presentable/Adjunction (which applies completeness for adjoint existence).

Name: StrongGenerator.lean
Description: Strong generators in locally presentable categories: the StrongGenerator typeclass, its relation to dense generators, and existence proofs. Distinct from Presentable/Dense (dense generators) and Generator/StrongGenerator.

Name: Type.lean
Description: The locally presentable structure of the category Type: Type is locally presentable with ℵ₀-compact objects being the finitely presentable types. Distinct from Presentable/Presheaf (presheaf categories generally) and Types/Basic.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Locally presentable structure on presheaf categories: [C^op, Set] is locally presentable for any small category C. Contains the presentability of functor categories and the κ-compact objects as retracts of representables. Distinct from Presentable/Type (Set itself) and Sites/Sheafification.

Name: Retracts.lean
Description: Retracts in locally presentable categories: the class of κ-compact objects is closed under retracts, and accessibility is preserved under retract-closure. Distinct from Presentable/ColimitPresentation and ObjectProperty/Retract.

Name: CardinalFilteredPresentation.lean
Description: Presentations of objects by κ-filtered diagrams: defines κ-presentability and the associated cardinal-filtered colimit presentations. Distinct from Presentable/ColimitPresentation (all colimit presentations) and Presentable/IsCardinalFiltered.

Name: ColimitPresentation.lean
Description: Colimit presentations in presentable categories: every object is a colimit of a small diagram of κ-presentable objects. Contains the existence theorem and functoriality. Distinct from Presentable/CardinalFilteredPresentation (κ-filtered specialization).

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Adjunctions involving locally presentable categories: the special adjoint functor theorem (a cocontinuous functor between presentable categories has a right adjoint) and related accessibility results. Distinct from Presentable/OrthogonalReflection (reflective localizations) and Adjunction/AdjointFunctorTheorems.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for presentable and accessible categories: κ-compact objects, the IsAccessible and IsLocallyPresentable typeclasses, and the relationship between accessibility and presentability. Foundation for all sibling Presentable files.

Name: EssentiallyLarge.lean
Description: The theorem that locally presentable categories are essentially large: no locally presentable category is essentially small. Distinct from Presentable/Finite (finitely presentable specialisation) and Presentable/Basic.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finitely presentable objects and finitely accessible categories: the κ = ℵ₀ specialisation of accessibility, finite colimit presentations, and concrete examples. Distinct from Presentable/Basic (general κ) and Presentable/IsCardinalFiltered.

Name: Dense.lean
Description: Dense generators in presentable categories: the Dense typeclass for subcategories, and the theorem that every locally presentable category has a small dense generator. Distinct from Presentable/StrongGenerator and Generator/Basic.

Name: Directed.lean
Description: Directed colimits in presentable categories: the equivalence between filtered and directed colimits, and existence results for directed presentations. Distinct from Presentable/IsCardinalFiltered (filtered structure) and Presentable/ColimitPresentation.

Name: Preadditive
Description: Preadditive categories: categories enriched over abelian groups, with biproducts and the additive category axioms.

Name: Yoneda
Description: The additive Yoneda lemma: in a preadditive category, the functor Hom(X, -) : C → Ab is additive, and Nat(Hom(X,-), F) ≅ F(X) as abelian groups. Contains the additive Yoneda embedding and the representability criterion for additive functors. Distinct from the ordinary Yoneda lemma (CategoryTheory/Yoneda) — this one remembers the additive structure.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Yoneda and limits in preadditive categories.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Yoneda and projective objects.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Preadditive Yoneda: `Hom(-, X)` as a preadditive functor.

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Yoneda and injective objects in preadditive categories.

Name: Projective
Description: Projective objects in preadditive categories: objects P where Hom(P, -) is exact. In module categories, projectives are direct summands of free modules. Contains the projective cover and the Nakayama functor. Distinct from Abelian/Projective (which requires abelianness).

Name: Preserves.lean
Description: Functors preserving projective objects.

Name: Resolution.lean
Description: Projective resolutions in preadditive categories.

Name: LiftingProperties.lean
Description: Lifting properties of projective objects.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Projective objects in preadditive categories.

Name: Internal.lean
Description: Internal projective objects.

Name: Injective
Description: Injective objects in preadditive categories: objects I where Hom(-, I) is exact. In module categories, injectives are characterized by Baer's criterion (every map from an ideal extends to the whole ring). Contains the injective hull construction. Distinct from Abelian/Injective (which requires abelianness for resolutions to work).

Name: Preserves.lean
Description: Functors preserving injective objects.

Name: Resolution.lean
Description: Injective resolutions in preadditive categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Injective objects in preadditive categories.

Name: LiftingProperties.lean
Description: Lifting properties of injective objects.

Name: Mat.lean
Description: The matrix category Mat_ R over a semiring R: objects are finite index types, morphisms are matrices, and composition is matrix multiplication. Proves Mat_ is preadditive and relates to the Karoubi envelope. Distinct from Preadditive/Biproducts (abstract biproducts) and Idempotents/Karoubi.

Name: OfBiproducts.lean
Description: Preadditive structure derived from biproducts: the converse of Preadditive/Biproducts, deriving addition on hom-sets from the existence of binary biproducts satisfying the split short exact sequence condition. Distinct from Preadditive/Biproducts (the forward direction) and Preadditive/Basic.

Name: LeftExact.lean
Description: Left exact functors between preadditive categories: the LeftExact typeclass for additive functors preserving finite limits, and the relationship between left exactness and kernel preservation. Distinct from Preadditive/AdditiveFunctor (additive but not necessarily exact) and Limits/ExactFunctor.

Name: LiftToFinset.lean
Description: Lifting of additive functors to finitely supported families: maps the formal finite sum ∑ᵢ fᵢ in a preadditive category to an actual morphism via the Finset sum. Used internally for Mat_ and biproduct constructions. Distinct from Preadditive/Mat (the matrix category) and Preadditive/Biproducts.

Name: SingleObj.lean
Description: The preadditive category SingleObj M for a group M: the one-object category where End(*) = M, made preadditive when M is an abelian group. Distinct from Category/Cat (general category constructions) and Monad/Basic.

Name: Transfer.lean
Description: Transfer of preadditive structure along a category equivalence: if C ≃ D and D is preadditive, then C inherits a coherent preadditive structure. Distinct from Preadditive/EilenbergMoore (monad algebras) and Preadditive/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on the opposite category: if C is preadditive, Cᵒᵖ is preadditive with the same abelian group structure on hom-sets. A structural duality file. Distinct from all other Preadditive siblings which work in the forward direction.

Name: Schur.lean
