Description: Schur's lemma in preadditive categories: if X is a simple object then End(X) is a division ring. Contains the formalisation and basic corollaries on hom-spaces between distinct simples. Distinct from Preadditive/HomOrthogonal (the general orthogonality framework).

Name: Indization.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on Ind-categories: Ind(C) is preadditive if C is preadditive, and the inclusion C → Ind(C) is additive. Distinct from Preadditive/Basic (which defines preadditive) and Abelian/Indization.

Name: Biproducts.lean
Description: Biproducts in preadditive categories: the Biproduct typeclass, binary biproducts with their split short exact sequence, and finite biproducts. Proves biproducts are simultaneously products and coproducts. Distinct from Preadditive/OfBiproducts (converse direction) and Limits/Bicones.

Name: CommGrp_.lean
Description: Internal commutative group objects in a preadditive monoidal category and the equivalence between preadditive structure and commutative group objects in the endofunctor category. Distinct from Monoidal/CommGrp_ (the abstract monoidal version).

Name: AdditiveFunctor.lean
Description: Additive functors between preadditive categories: the Functor.Additive typeclass, preservation of zero morphisms and addition, composition of additive functors, and AdditiveEquivalence. Distinct from Linear/LinearFunctor (R-linear version) and Preadditive/FunctorCategory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Defines the Preadditive typeclass: each Hom-set carries an abelian group structure and composition is bilinear. Contains zero morphisms, subtraction, and the basic additive identities. Foundation for Abelian category theory and all additive constructions in Mathlib.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on functor categories [C, D] when D is preadditive: pointwise addition of natural transformations, zero natural transformation, and the Preadditive instance. Distinct from Preadditive/AdditiveFunctor (properties of individual additive functors) and Linear/FunctorCategory.

Name: HomOrthogonal.lean
Description: Hom-orthogonality in preadditive categories: the HomOrthogonal family property (Hom(Aᵢ, Aⱼ) = 0 for i ≠ j) and decomposition into orthogonal direct summands. Foundation for structure theorems including Schur's lemma. Distinct from Preadditive/Schur (Schur's lemma itself).

Name: EilenbergMoore.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on Eilenberg-Moore categories: if C is preadditive and the monad is additive, the category of algebras is preadditive. Distinct from Monad/Algebra (which defines algebras without additivity) and Preadditive/Transfer.

Name: EndoFunctor.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on the endofunctor category End(C): natural transformations form an abelian group, making End(C) a preadditive monoidal category under composition. Distinct from Endofunctor/Algebra and Preadditive/FunctorCategory (general functor categories).

Name: Monoidal
Description: Monoidal categories: a tensor product bifunctor with unit and associativity, and the coherence theorem for monoidal categories.

Name: Limits
Description: Limits and colimits in monoidal categories that interact well with the monoidal structure: conditions for tensor to preserve limits (e.g., - ⊗ X preserves colimits when C is a closed monoidal category). The monoidal structure on the category of (co)algebras. Distinct from CategoryTheory/Limits (limits in a general category).

Name: Shapes
Description: Specific limit shapes in monoidal categories: when products and equalizers are compatible with the tensor product (e.g., when ⊗ preserves products in each variable). The tensor product of monoid objects and the interaction of limits with the monoidal structure. Used to construct limits of algebras in monoidal categories.

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pullbacks in monoidal categories.

Name: HasLimits.lean
Description: HasLimits for monoidal categories.

Name: Preserves.lean
Description: Tensor product preserving limits.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in monoidal categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Limits in monoidal categories: tensor products commuting with limits.

Name: Cokernels.lean
Description: Cokernels in monoidal categories.

Name: Internal
Description: Internal monoids and comonoids in a monoidal category: a monoid (A, μ : A⊗A → A, η : I → A) satisfying associativity and unit diagrams internal to (C, ⊗). In Vect: algebras; in Ab: rings; in Cat: strict monoidal categories. Internal comonoids and their comodules. Used in Hopf algebra and coalgebra theory.

Name: Types
Description: Internal monoids in the category of types: a type M with a multiplication M × M → M and unit 1 : M satisfying associativity and unit laws — this is exactly an ordinary Lean monoid. The equivalence between internal monoids in Type and the Monoid typeclass. Used as a sanity check and as a bridge between categorical and algebraic definitions.

Name: Grp.lean
Description: Group objects in Type are groups.

Name: Grp_.lean
Description: Grp_ in Type.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monoid objects in Type.

Name: CommGrp_.lean
Description: Commutative group objects in Type are abelian groups.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Internal modules in a monoidal category.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in categories of monoid objects.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Monoid objects in functor categories.

Name: Functor
Description: Monoidal functors: lax monoidal functors (F, φ_{X,Y} : F(X) ⊗ F(Y) → F(X ⊗ Y), φ₀ : I → F(I)) and their strong (φ invertible) and strict (φ = id) variants. Monoidal natural transformations. The composition of monoidal functors. Used to compare monoidal structures across categories. Distinct from Monoidal/Action (where the functor targets End(C)).

Name: Types.lean
Description: Monoidal functors to Type.

Name: Types
Description: Monoidal structure on the category of types: Type u with the Cartesian monoidal structure (Type u × Type u → Type u), instances showing Type u is a symmetric monoidal category, and the closed structure (function types). Used as a concrete reference implementation. Distinct from Monoidal/Cartesian (the abstract theory).

Name: Coyoneda.lean
Description: Coyoneda for monoidal categories of types.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Type as a monoidal category.

Name: Rigid
Description: Rigid (autonomous) monoidal categories: monoidal categories where every object X has a left dual X* with evaluation and coevaluation maps. Pivotal categories (left = right dual). Compact closed categories (symmetric rigid). Used in the Drinfeld center and in quantum invariants. Distinct from Monoidal/Closed (internal hom, not duality) and Monoidal/Braided (symmetry, not rigidity).

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Rigid structure on functor categories.

Name: OfEquivalence.lean
Description: Rigid monoidal structure transferred along equivalences.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functors between rigid monoidal categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Rigid monoidal categories: objects with left and right duals.

Name: Braided.lean
Description: Rigid braided monoidal categories.

Name: Opposite
Description: Opposite monoidal categories: the opposite (C^op, ⊗^op) where X ⊗^op Y = Y ⊗ X, and the reversed monoidal category (C, ⊗^rev). Used to dualize monoidal results and to define right modules as left modules over the opposite. Distinct from Bicategory/Opposite (reversing 1-cells in a bicategory).

Name: Mon_.lean
Description: Monoid objects in monoidal opposite categories.

Name: Mon.lean
Description: Mon objects in opposite monoidal categories.

Name: Free
Description: The free monoidal category on a generating set of objects: the category of planar trees with leaves labeled by generators and the universal property. Used in coherence proofs and in the theory of operads. Every monoidal category is a quotient of the free one. Distinct from the free strict monoidal category (where associativity is strict equality).

Name: Coherence.lean
Description: Coherence for free monoidal categories: the Maclane coherence theorem.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Free monoidal categories: the free monoidal category on a set of generators.

Name: Cartesian
Description: Cartesian monoidal categories: monoidal categories where the tensor product is the categorical product and the unit is the terminal object. Every cartesian monoidal category is symmetric. Contains the diagonal map Δ : X → X ⊗ X and the augmentation ε : X → I. The canonical monoidal structure on Set, Type, and Grp. Distinct from Monoidal/Braided (non-cartesian braidings) and CopyDiscardCategory (abstract copy-discard structure).

Name: Mod.lean
Description: Module category is cartesian monoidal (over ℤ).

Name: Mod_.lean
Description: Module objects in cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: Grp_.lean
Description: Group objects in cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: InfSemilattice.lean
Description: Inf-semilattice structure from cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Over-categories as cartesian monoidal.

Name: ShrinkYoneda.lean
Description: Yoneda for cartesian monoidal categories (small case).

Name: Mon.lean
Description: Monoid category is cartesian monoidal.

Name: Mon_.lean
Description: Monoid objects in cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: CommGrp_.lean
Description: Commutative group objects in a cartesian monoidal category.

Name: CommMon_.lean
Description: Commutative monoid objects in cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cartesian monoidal categories: `MonoidalCategory C` where the tensor is the cartesian product.

Name: Cat.lean
Description: Cat is cartesian monoidal.

Name: Grp.lean
Description: Group category is cartesian monoidal.

Name: GrpLimits.lean
Description: Limits of group objects in cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: Comon_.lean
Description: Comonoid objects in cartesian monoidal categories.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Functor categories are cartesian monoidal.

Name: Braided
Description: Braided and symmetric monoidal categories: a braiding β_{X,Y} : X ⊗ Y → Y ⊗ X satisfying hexagon coherence. Symmetric monoidal: β² = id. Ribbon categories: braided with a twist. Used in quantum algebra (braided categories model quantum groups) and topology (braided monoidal categories give knot invariants). Distinct from Monoidal/Closed (internal hom).

Name: Reflection.lean
Description: Reflection through braided monoidal structures.

Name: Transport.lean
Description: Transporting braided monoidal structures.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Opposite braided monoidal category: the reverse braiding.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Braided monoidal categories: `BraidedCategory C` — the braiding `β_{X,Y} : X ⊗ Y ≅ Y ⊗ X`.

Name: Multifunctor.lean
Description: Multifunctors in braided monoidal categories.

Name: Action
Description: Monoidal actions: an action of a monoidal category M on a category C, i.e., a monoidal functor M → End(C). Module categories over a monoidal category. Used in representation theory (G-modules as actions of the group algebra) and in linear algebra (modules as actions of a ring viewed as a one-object preadditive category).

Name: LinearFunctor.lean
Description: Linear functors as monoidal actions.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Monoidal actions on opposite categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monoidal actions: a monoidal category acting on another category.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphism action in monoidal categories.

Name: ExternalProduct
Description: External tensor products of functors: given F : C → D and G : C' → D', the external product F ⊠ G : C × C' → D ⊗ D'. Used in the Künneth formula and in the product of representations. Distinct from internal tensor product (which requires the same source and target category) and from Day convolution (which uses a monoidal structure on the source).

Name: KanExtension.lean
Description: External product via Kan extension.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: External product in a monoidal category.

Name: DayConvolution
Description: The Day convolution monoidal structure on presheaf categories: for a monoidal category C, the functor category [C^op, Set] carries a monoidal structure (F ⊗_Day G)(c) = ∫^{a,b} C(a⊗b,c) × F(a) × G(b). The free cocompletion preserves monoidal structure. Used in operads and combinatorial species. Distinct from Monoidal/Closed (internal hom, not convolution).

Name: DayFunctor.lean
Description: The Day convolution functor.

Name: Closed.lean
Description: Closed structure for Day convolution.

Name: Braided.lean
Description: Day convolution: braided monoidal structure on presheaves.

Name: Closed
Description: Closed monoidal categories: monoidal categories with an internal hom [X, Y] right adjoint to - ⊗ X. The adjunction (- ⊗ X) ⊣ [X, -] and the evaluation map ev : [X,Y] ⊗ X → Y. Examples: Set (function spaces), Vect (linear maps), Chain complexes (internal Hom). Distinct from Monoidal/Braided (commutativity of ⊗) and CategoryTheory/Closed (the top-level folder).

Name: FunctorCategory
Description: The closed monoidal structure on a functor category [C, D] when D is closed monoidal: the internal hom [F, G] defined pointwise as [F, G](c) = [F(c), G(c)] in D. The evaluation and coevaluation natural transformations. Used in the categorical treatment of enriched functor categories and in the Day convolution setting.

Name: Groupoid.lean
Description: Closed groupoid functor categories.

Name: Complete.lean
Description: Complete closed functor categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic closed functor categories.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Closed ideals in monoidal closed categories.

Name: FunctorToTypes.lean
Description: Internal hom for functors to Type.

Name: Transport.lean
Description: Transporting closed monoidal structures along equivalences.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: Zero objects in closed monoidal categories.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Type is a closed monoidal category: `[X, Y] = X → Y`.

Name: Braided.lean
Description: Closed braided monoidal categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Closed monoidal categories: `MonoidalClosed C` — the internal hom `[X, -] ⊣ - ⊗ X`.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian closed = cartesian monoidal + closed.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Closed monoidal functors: preservation of the internal hom.

Name: Enrichment.lean
Description: Closed monoidal categories as enriched categories.

Name: Multifunctor.lean
Description: Multifunctors in the monoidal setting: functors of several monoidal arguments compatible with the monoidal structure and associativity. Defines MultifunctorCategory and the associated coherence data. Distinct from Functor/Trifunctor (three-variable case) and Monoidal/Functor.

Name: Mon_.lean
Description: Monoid objects in a monoidal category: the Mon_ typeclass with multiplication μ : X ⊗ X → X and unit η : 𝟙_ → X, satisfying associativity and unit axioms. Foundation for commutative monoids (CommMon_), groups (Grp_), and module theory. Distinct from Monoidal/CommMon_ and Monoidal/Comon_.

Name: OfHasFiniteProducts.lean
Description: The cartesian monoidal structure from finite products: proves that any category with finite products is monoidal with ⊗ = × and 𝟙_ = ⊤. Distinct from Monoidal/Discrete (discrete case) and Closed/Cartesian (cartesian closed extension).

Name: NaturalTransformation.lean
Description: Monoidal natural transformations between monoidal functors: the MonoidalNatTrans typeclass, compatibility with the tensorator, and vertical/horizontal composition. Distinct from Monoidal/Functor (the functors being connected) and general NaturalTransformation files.

Name: Mon.lean
Description: The category Mon_ C of monoid objects in a monoidal category C: morphisms preserving multiplication and unit, the category structure, and the forgetful functor. Distinct from Monoidal/Mon_ (the typeclass for individual monoid objects) and Monoidal/CommMon_.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: R-linear monoidal categories and R-linear monoidal functors: the compatibility between the monoidal tensor and the R-module structure on hom-sets. Distinct from Linear/LinearFunctor (linear functors without monoidal structure) and Monoidal/Preadditive.

Name: Hopf_.lean
Description: Hopf algebra objects in a symmetric monoidal category: a Bimon_ equipped with an antipode morphism S : X → X satisfying the Hopf identity (S convolution id = id convolution S = unit ∘ counit). Distinct from Monoidal/Bimon_ (bimonoids without antipode) and Monoidal/Grp_.

Name: Mod_.lean
Description: Module objects over a monoid Mon_ M in a monoidal category: the Mod_ M typeclass with action act : M ⊗ X → X and module axioms. Distinct from Monoidal/Bimod (bimodules over M) and Monoidal/Mon_.

Name: Mod.lean
Description: The category Mod_ M of left modules over a fixed monoid object M: the category structure, module morphisms, and basic functoriality. Distinct from Monoidal/Mod_ (the module typeclass for individual objects) and Monoidal/Bimod (bimodules).

Name: Tor.lean
Description: The Tor functor in a monoidal abelian category: Tor₀ and Tor₁ via derived tensor product, with the long exact Tor sequence. Distinct from Abelian/LeftDerived (general left derived functors) and Monoidal/Bimod.

Name: Subcategory.lean
Description: Monoidal subcategories: a full subcategory closed under tensor product and unit inherits a monoidal structure, with the inclusion as a strict monoidal functor. Distinct from Monoidal/Widesubcategory (wide subcategory) and ObjectProperty/FiniteProducts.

Name: Widesubcategory.lean
Description: Wide monoidal subcategories: a subcategory containing all objects but closed under tensor product on morphisms. Distinct from Monoidal/Subcategory (full subcategory) and ObjectProperty/FiniteProducts.

Name: Transport.lean
Description: Transport of monoidal structure along a category equivalence: given an equivalence C ≃ D and a MonoidalCategory structure on C, transfers it to D with all coherence. Distinct from Monoidal/Skeleton (skeleton-based strictification).

Name: Skeleton.lean
Description: The skeleton of a monoidal category as a strict monoidal category: proves every monoidal category is equivalent to a strict one via the skeleton. Distinct from Monoidal/Category (non-strict definition) and Limits/Skeleton.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Monoidal preadditive categories: the compatibility between the tensor product and the preadditive hom-set structure, defining MonoidalPreadditive and bilinearity of ⊗ on morphisms. Distinct from Monoidal/Linear (R-linear version) and Preadditive/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The monoidal structure on the opposite category Cᵒᵖ when C is monoidal: the reversed tensor X ⊗ᵒᵖ Y := Y ⊗ X, reversed associators, and the op-monoidal functor. Distinct from Monoidal/Category (forward direction) and Closed/Monoidal.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Ring objects in a preadditive monoidal category: a Mon_ in a preadditive monoidal category, providing the categorical analogue of a ring (multiplication distributes over addition). Contains the Ring_ typeclass and basic instances. Distinct from Monoidal/Mon_ (monoids without additivity) and RingTheory files.

Name: PushoutProduct.lean
Description: The pushout product of two morphisms f : A → B and g : C → D: the induced map f □ g from the pushout (A ⊗ D) ⊔_{A⊗C} (B ⊗ C) to B ⊗ D. Used in model category theory and the small object argument. Distinct from LiftingProperties/PushoutProduct (the pushout-product/hom adjunction) and SmallObject/Basic.

Name: CommComon_.lean
Description: Commutative comonoid objects in a symmetric monoidal category: the Comon_ typeclass with the symmetry constraint forcing cocommutativity. Contains basic instances. Distinct from Monoidal/Comon_ (non-commutative) and Monoidal/CommMon_.

Name: CoherenceLemmas.lean
Description: Technical coherence lemmas for monoidal categories: any two coherence morphisms (assembled from associators and unitors) between the same parenthesisations are equal. Used to avoid repeated verification in monoidal computations. Distinct from Monoidal/Category (which states the pentagon and triangle axioms).

Name: CommMon_.lean
Description: Commutative monoid objects in a symmetric monoidal category: extends Mon_ with a commutativity constraint μ ∘ β = μ. Contains the definition, the symmetric monoidal constraint, and basic instances. Distinct from Monoidal/Mon_ (non-commutative) and Monoidal/CommComon_.

Name: CommGrp_.lean
Description: Commutative group objects in a monoidal category: a CommMon_ equipped with an inverse morphism satisfying the group axioms. Distinct from Monoidal/Grp_ (non-commutative group objects) and Preadditive/CommGrp_.

Name: Center.lean
Description: The Drinfeld center Z(C) of a monoidal category: objects are pairs (X, γ) where γ is a half-braiding, the center is a braided monoidal category, and the forgetful functor to C is monoidal. Distinct from Monoidal/CommMon_ (commutative monoids) and Center/Basic.

Name: Bimod.lean
Description: Bimodule objects over a monoid Mon_ in a monoidal category: the bimodule typeclass, the tensor product of bimodules over the monoid, and the bicategory of monoids and bimodules. Distinct from Monoidal/Mod_ (left modules only) and Monoidal/Mon_.

Name: Arrow.lean
Description: The arrow category of a monoidal category as a monoidal category: the tensor product on arrows defined componentwise, with verification of the monoidal axioms. Distinct from Comma/Arrow (non-monoidal arrow category) and Monoidal/FunctorCategory.

Name: Category.lean
Description: Core definitions for monoidal categories: the MonoidalCategory typeclass with tensor product ⊗, unit 𝟙_, associator α, left/right unitors λ/ρ, and the pentagon and triangle axioms. The foundational file for all monoidal category theory in Mathlib. Distinct from Monoidal/Functor (monoidal functors) and Monoidal/NaturalTransformation.

Name: Bimon_.lean
Description: Bimonoid objects in a monoidal category: simultaneously a Mon_ and a Comon_ with compatibility between multiplication and comultiplication. Contains the definition, basic examples, and the relationship to Hopf algebras. Distinct from Monoidal/Hopf_ (adds antipode) and Monoidal/Mon_.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Pointwise monoidal structure on functor categories [C, D]: when D is monoidal, [C, D] is monoidal via (F ⊗ G)(X) := F(X) ⊗ G(X). Distinct from Monoidal/DayConvolution (convolution product) and Monoidal/Functor (monoidal functors themselves).

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Monoidal functors: LaxMonoidalFunctor, OplaxMonoidalFunctor, and MonoidalFunctor (strong), with tensorator μ, unit morphism ε, and coherence conditions. Contains composition and the monoidal equivalence typeclass. Distinct from Monoidal/NaturalTransformation (nat. trans. between monoidal functors) and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Grp_.lean
Description: Group objects in a monoidal category: Mon_ with an inverse morphism ι : X → X satisfying the group axioms. Contains the Grp_ typeclass and the forgetful functor to Mon_. Distinct from Monoidal/CommGrp_ (commutative version) and Monoidal/Mon_.

Name: Grp.lean
Description: The category Grp_ C of group objects in a fixed monoidal category C: the category structure, morphisms, and basic functoriality. Distinct from Monoidal/Grp_ (the typeclass for individual group objects) and Monoidal/Mon (the category Mon_).

Name: End.lean
Description: The monoidal category of endofunctors: endofunctors of a category form a strict monoidal category under composition. Contains the MonoidalCategory instance and the relationship to Mon_ objects. Distinct from Monoidal/FunctorCategory (general functor categories) and Preadditive/EndoFunctor.

Name: Conv.lean
Description: The convolution monoidal structure on functor categories: given a monoidal category C and any category D, defines a preliminary convolution product on [C, D]. Distinct from Monoidal/DayConvolution (the full Day convolution with sheafification) and Monoidal/FunctorCategory.

Name: Comon_.lean
Description: Comonoid objects in a monoidal category: dual of Mon_ with comultiplication Δ : X → X ⊗ X and counit ε : X → 𝟙_, satisfying coassociativity and counit axioms. Distinct from Monoidal/Mon_ (monoids) and Monoidal/Bimon_ (bimonoids).

Name: Discrete.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on discrete categories: a discrete category on a monoid M is monoidal with tensor product given by multiplication. Contains the discretization functor and its monoidal properties. Distinct from Monoidal/Category (abstract axioms) and Category/Pairwise.

Name: DayConvolution.lean
Description: Day convolution: the symmetric monoidal structure on [C^op, Set] (presheaves) induced by a monoidal structure on C. Contains the tensor product, unit, and the monoidal closed structure. Distinct from Monoidal/Conv (simpler convolution) and Sites/CartesianMonoidal.

Name: Linear
Description: Linear categories: categories enriched over a commutative ring, with R-module morphism sets.

Name: LinearFunctor.lean
Description: R-linear functors between R-linear categories: the Functor.Linear typeclass, preservation of scalar multiplication, and the category LinFunctor R C D. Distinct from Preadditive/AdditiveFunctor (additive, not linear) and Linear/FunctorCategory.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: The R-linear Yoneda lemma: the Yoneda embedding for R-linear categories landing in R-linear presheaves and the associated natural isomorphism Nat(よ(X), F) ≅ F(X). Distinct from Linear/Basic (definitions) and the standard Yoneda in Functor/Hom.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Defines the Linear R typeclass for R-linear categories: each Hom-set is an R-module and composition is R-bilinear. Foundation for all R-linear category theory. Distinct from Preadditive/Basic (abelian group structure only, without scalar multiplication) and Linear/LinearFunctor.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: R-linear structure on functor categories [C, D] when D is R-linear: pointwise scalar multiplication and addition of natural transformations. Distinct from Preadditive/FunctorCategory (additive but not R-linear) and Linear/LinearFunctor.

Name: Limits
Description: Limits and colimits: cones, cocones, the universal property, and specific limits (products, equalizers, pullbacks, filtered colimits).

Name: Preserves
Description: Functors that preserve limits or colimits: right adjoints preserve limits, left adjoints preserve colimits. Conditions for a functor to preserve specific classes (finite limits, filtered colimits, etc.). The general preservation API used throughout Mathlib to propagate limit results. Distinct from Limits/Constructions (building limits) and Limits/Final (replacing index categories).

Name: Shapes
Description: Preservation of specific limit shapes: instances showing that particular functors preserve terminal objects, products, pullbacks, equalizers, or filtered colimits. Organized by limit shape rather than by functor. Used to quickly look up whether a standard functor (e.g., a forgetful functor) preserves a needed limit type.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Preservation of products.

Name: Preorder.lean
Description: Preservation of preorder limits.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Preservation of over-limits.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Preservation of pullbacks.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: Preservation of zero objects.

Name: Terminal.lean
Description: Preservation of terminal objects.

Name: Square.lean
Description: Preservation of squares.

Name: Biproducts.lean
Description: Preservation of biproducts.

Name: BinaryProducts.lean
Description: Preservation of binary products.

Name: AbelianImages.lean
Description: Preservation of abelian images.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Preservation of equalisers.

Name: Multiequalizer.lean
Description: Preservation of multiequalisers.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Preservation of kernels.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Preservation of images.

Name: Creates
Description: Functors that create limits: F creates limits of shape J if whenever F ∘ D has a limit cone, D itself has a limit cone and F maps it to the limit of F ∘ D. Stronger than preservation. The forgetful functor from groups to sets creates all limits. Used to transfer limits from a base category to a structured category.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Creates pullbacks.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Creates limits in opposite categories.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Creates finite limits.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Limit preservation in over-categories.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Preservation of limits in opposite categories.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limit preservation lemmas: compositions and reflections of limit-preserving functors.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Limit preservation for presheaves.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: Yoneda functor preserves limits.

Name: Ulift.lean
Description: Limit preservation under ULift.

Name: SigmaConst.lean
Description: Preservation of sigma-indexed colimits.

Name: BifunctorCokernel.lean
Description: Bifunctors and cokernels.

Name: Bifunctor.lean
Description: Bifunctors preserving limits.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Preservation of limits: `PreservesLimit K F` — `F` sends limit cones to limit cones.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered colimit preservation.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: Limit preservation via Grothendieck fibrations.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Limit preservation in functor categories.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Preservation of finite limits: left-exact and right-exact functors.

Name: Indization
Description: The Ind-category Ind(C): the free cocompletion of C under filtered colimits, with objects being formal filtered diagrams in C. Every presheaf on C is a colimit of representables; Ind(C) ↪ Psh(C) is the subcategory of filtered-colimit-preserving presheaves. Used in the theory of accessible and locally presentable categories.

Name: LocallySmall.lean
Description: Local smallness of ind-categories.

Name: ParallelPair.lean
Description: Parallel pair diagrams in ind-categories.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products of ind-objects.

Name: IndObject.lean
Description: Ind-object characterisation: when a presheaf is an ind-object.

Name: Category.lean
Description: The category of ind-objects.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Equalisers of ind-objects.

Name: FilteredColimits.lean
Description: Filtered colimits of ind-objects.

Name: Types
Description: Limits and colimits in the category of types (Type u): explicit set-theoretic descriptions of products (Pi types), equalizers (subtypes), pullbacks, colimits (quotient types). Used to verify that Type u is complete and cocomplete, and as a computational reference for limits. Distinct from Limits/ConcreteCategory (forgetful-functor conditions) — this is the specific case of Type.

Name: Multiequalizer.lean
Description: Multiequalisers in Type: intersection of equaliser conditions.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in Type: `∏ᵢ Xᵢ`.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in Type: as subtypes of products.

Name: Multicoequalizer.lean
Description: Multicoequalisers in Type.

Name: Shapes.lean
Description: Specific limit shapes in Type.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: Yoneda lemma for limits in Type.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Pullbacks in Type: `{(x,y) | f x = g y}`.

Name: Pushouts.lean
Description: Pushouts in Type: amalgamated sum.

Name: ColimitType.lean
Description: Colimit type: the explicit type computing a colimit.

Name: ColimitTypeFiltered.lean
Description: Filtered colimit type: explicit construction of filtered colimits of types.

Name: Coequalizers.lean
Description: Coequalisers of types: explicit set-theoretic construction.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in Type: explicit computation as quotients.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in Type: equivalence classes of pairs.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Images in Type: range of a function.

Name: Coproducts.lean
Description: Coproducts in Type: disjoint union `⊕ᵢ Xᵢ`.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Equalisers in Type: `{x | f x = g x}`.

Name: Shapes
Description: Specific named limit and colimit types: terminal and initial objects, binary products and coproducts, equalizers and coequalizers, pullbacks and pushouts, zero object, biproducts (in preadditive categories), and kernels/cokernels. Each shape has its own universal property stated concretely. Distinct from Limits/Constructions (reducing arbitrary limits to shapes).

Name: Preorder
Description: Limits and colimits in preorder categories (thin categories): products = meets (greatest lower bounds), coproducts = joins (least upper bounds), pullbacks = binary meets, pushouts = binary joins, terminal = top, initial = bottom. The complete lattice structure corresponds exactly to having all limits and colimits. Used to verify order-theoretic results categorically.

Name: PrincipalSeg.lean
Description: Limits over principal segments of ordinals.

Name: TransfiniteCompositionOfShape.lean
Description: Transfinite compositions over preorder shapes.

Name: WellOrderContinuous.lean
Description: Well-order continuous limits.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Limits over preorders: colimits along functors from preorders.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Preorder limits over Fin n.

Name: HasIterationOfShape.lean
Description: Iterations over preorder shapes.

Name: Pullback
Description: Pullbacks and pushouts: the universal squares X ×_Z Y (fiber product) and X ⊔_Z Y (amalgamated sum). The pasting lemma for pullbacks (a square of squares is a pullback iff each sub-square is). Pullback-stable properties (epimorphisms, monomorphisms in suitable categories). The primary workhorse for constructions in algebraic geometry and topology.

Name: IsPullback
Description: The IsPullback predicate: a commutative square is a pullback iff the induced map into the product is an isomorphism. The isPullback API for recognising pullback squares without constructing a limit cone explicitly. Used throughout algebraic geometry when checking that a given square is Cartesian.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: IsPullback definitions.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels from pullback squares.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: IsPullback predicate.

Name: BicartesianSq.lean
Description: Bicartesian squares.

Name: Categorical
Description: Categorical properties of pullbacks: the pasting lemma (two adjacent pullback squares compose to a pullback), pullback of a mono is a mono, and the pullback functor f* as a right adjoint. Used when reasoning about base change and the Beck-Chevalley condition.

Name: CatCospanTransform.lean
Description: Cospan transformations in Cat.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Categorical pullback basics.

Name: Mono.lean
Description: Monomorphisms and pullbacks.

Name: Iso.lean
Description: Isomorphisms and pullbacks.

Name: HasPullback.lean
Description: HasPullback typeclass.

Name: Pasting.lean
Description: Pasting lemma for pullbacks.

Name: Square.lean
Description: Square diagrams and pullbacks.

Name: PullbackObjObj.lean
Description: The pullback object.

Name: PullbackCone.lean
Description: Pullback cone: the explicit cone for a pullback.

Name: EquifiberedLimits.lean
Description: Limits of equifibered transformations.

Name: CommSq.lean
Description: Commutative squares and pullbacks.

Name: ChosenPullback.lean
Description: Chosen pullback: canonical pullback choice.

Name: Assoc.lean
Description: Associativity of pullbacks.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connected pullback squares.

Name: Equifibered.lean
Description: Equifibered natural transformations: `η` is equifibered when all `η_X` are pullback squares.

Name: Equalizer.lean
Description: Equaliser from pullback.

Name: Cospan.lean
Description: Pullbacks via cospans.

Name: NormalMono
Description: Normal monomorphisms and normal epimorphisms: a monomorphism is normal if it is the kernel of some morphism; a normal epimorphism is the cokernel of some morphism. In an abelian category every mono is normal. Normal monos are exactly the regular monos in categories where kernels exist. Used in the definition of exact sequences.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Normal mono from equalizer.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normal monomorphisms: monomorphisms that are kernels.

Name: Opposites
Description: Limits and colimits in opposite categories: if C has colimits of shape J then C^op has limits of shape J^op, and vice versa. The duality between products/coproducts and between equalizers/coequalizers. Contains the typeclass instances propagating limit existence through the opposite functor.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in opposite categories.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Pullbacks in opposite categories.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels in opposite categories.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Equalisers in opposite categories.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in opposite categories.

Name: Reflexive.lean
Description: Reflexive coequalisers: coequalisers of maps with a common section.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products and coproducts: `∏ f` and `∐ f` over arbitrary index sets.

Name: SequentialProduct.lean
Description: Sequential products over ℕ.

Name: RegularMono.lean
Description: Regular monomorphisms: monomorphisms that are equalisers of some pair of morphisms.

Name: PiProd.lean
Description: Pi types as products.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels and cokernels: `kernel f`, `cokernel f` in preadditive categories.

Name: KernelPair.lean
Description: Kernel pairs: `KernelPair f` — the pullback of `f` with itself.

Name: MultiequalizerPullback.lean
Description: Multiequaliser as pullback.

Name: Multiequalizer.lean
Description: Multiequalisers: equalisers of multiple maps simultaneously.

Name: SingleObj.lean
Description: Limits in single-object categories.

Name: WidePullbacks.lean
Description: Wide pullbacks: limits over diagrams with a common codomain.

Name: WideEqualizers.lean
Description: Wide equalisers: equalisers of a family of morphisms.

Name: ZeroObjects.lean
Description: Zero objects: `IsZero X` — objects that are both initial and terminal.

Name: ZeroMorphisms.lean
Description: Zero morphisms and zero objects: `HasZeroMorphisms C` and the zero object.

Name: Terminal.lean
Description: Terminal and initial objects: `terminal C`, `initial C`, and uniqueness of maps to/from them.

Name: SplitEqualizer.lean
Description: Split equalisers: equalisers with a section.

Name: SplitCoequalizer.lean
Description: Split coequalisers: coequalisers that admit a splitting.

Name: StrongEpi.lean
Description: Strong epimorphisms: epimorphisms with the right lifting property against monomorphisms.

Name: StrictInitial.lean
Description: Strict initial object: `InitialMono C` — the initial object is a strict initial.

Name: Countable.lean
Description: Countable limits: limits over countably-indexed diagrams.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Limits over connected index categories.

Name: DisjointCoproduct.lean
Description: Disjoint coproducts: coproducts where summands are disjoint.

Name: Diagonal.lean
Description: Diagonal morphism: `Δ : X → X × X` and its relation to monomorphisms.

Name: ConcreteCategory.lean
Description: Limits in concrete categories: element-wise constructions.

Name: BinaryProducts.lean
Description: Binary products: `prod X Y`, `prod.fst`, `prod.snd`, and the universal property.

Name: BinaryBiproducts.lean
Description: Binary biproducts: `X ⊕ Y` in preadditive categories.

Name: CombinedProducts.lean
Description: Combined products: products built from families.

Name: Biproducts.lean
Description: Biproducts: `biproduct X Y` — objects that are both products and coproducts.

Name: End.lean
Description: Ends and coends: `∫ F` as a limit over the twisted arrow category.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: Limits in Grothendieck fibrations.

Name: FunctorToTypes.lean
Description: Limits for functors to types.

Name: IsTerminal.lean
Description: IsTerminal predicate: conditions for an object to be terminal.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Image factorisation: `imageFactorisation f` — factoring `f` as epi then mono.

Name: FiniteProducts.lean
Description: Finite products: `∏ᶠ` and `∐ᶠ` for finite index sets.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Limits preserved by equivalences.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Equalisers and coequalisers: `equalizer f g`, fork, and cofork.

Name: FiniteMultiequalizer.lean
Description: Finite multiequalisers: equalisers of finitely many maps.

Name: FiniteLimits.lean
Description: Finite limits: categories with all finite limits.

Name: FunctorCategory
Description: Limits and colimits in functor categories [J, C]: when C has limits/colimits, [J, C] has them computed pointwise (lim F evaluated at j is lim(F(-)(j))). The pointwise criterion for limit-preservation. Used to transfer completeness from C to diagram categories. Distinct from Limits/Constructions (reductions between limit types).

Name: Shapes
Description: Pointwise computation of specific limit shapes in functor categories [J, C]: products, equalizers, pullbacks, and terminal objects in [J, C] are computed objectwise when C has these limits. Contains the concrete instances (e.g., the terminal object in [J, C] is the constant functor at the terminal object of C).

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Pullbacks in functor categories.

Name: Terminal.lean
Description: Terminal objects in functor categories.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in functor categories.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Images in functor categories.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels in functor categories.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in functor categories.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite limits in functor categories.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epi/mono in functor categories: pointwise conditions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Limits in functor categories: computed objectwise.

Name: BinaryBiproducts.lean
Description: Binary biproducts in functor categories.

Name: Constructions
Description: Constructing limits and colimits from simpler pieces: products + equalizers ⟹ all limits, coproducts + coequalizers ⟹ all colimits, finite limits from pullbacks + terminal object, and similar reduction theorems. The practical toolkit for proving a category is complete or cocomplete. Distinct from Limits/Shapes (specific limit types).

Name: Over
Description: Constructing limits in over-categories C/X: the limit of a diagram F : J → C/X is computed by taking the limit of the underlying diagram in C and using the universal maps to X. Used to prove that slice categories inherit completeness from the base category, and in the construction of the Beck-Chevalley condition for base change.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in over-categories.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connected limits in over-categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Constructions in over-categories.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Pullbacks from equalisers and products.

Name: LimitsOfProductsAndEqualizers.lean
Description: All limits from products and equalisers.

Name: WeaklyInitial.lean
Description: Weakly initial objects: existence conditions.

Name: ZeroObjects.lean
Description: Zero objects from initial and terminal.

Name: WidePullbackOfTerminal.lean
Description: Wide pullbacks from terminal objects.

Name: FiniteProductsOfBinaryProducts.lean
Description: Finite products from binary products and terminal object.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epi/mono from limits: constructing epi/mono morphisms from limit data.

Name: BinaryProducts.lean
Description: Binary products from other limits: binary products from terminal object and equalisers.

Name: Equalizers.lean
Description: Equalisers from other limits.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered colimits from other colimits.

Name: EventuallyConstant.lean
Description: Limits of eventually constant diagrams.

Name: ConcreteCategory
Description: Limits and colimits in concrete categories (categories with a faithful functor to Set): conditions under which limits/colimits are computed set-theoretically, and when the forgetful functor preserves or reflects them. Relevant for ModuleCat, GroupCat, TopCat. Distinct from Limits/Types (limits in the category of types itself).

Name: WithAlgebraicStructures.lean
Description: Limits in concrete categories with algebraic structure.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in concrete categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Limits in concrete categories: computed as limits of underlying sets.

Name: FormalCoproducts
Description: The category of formal coproducts FormalCoproduct C: given a category C, freely adjoins coproducts to obtain a new category whose objects are indexed families of objects from C and whose morphisms are given by compatible families of maps. Proves that FormalCoproduct C has all small coproducts, with the inclusion functor incl : C → FormalCoproduct C. Also defines a Čech object functor in this context. Used in the construction of free cocompletions under coproducts.

Name: ExtraDegeneracy.lean
Description: Extra degeneracy for formal coproducts.

Name: Cech.lean
Description: Čech nerves via formal coproducts.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Formal coproducts: coproducts in categories where they may not exist by adjoining them formally.

Name: Final
Description: Final and initial functors: K : J → C is final if colim(F∘K) ≅ colim(F) for all F : C → D. Quillen's theorem A for final functors. Used to replace index categories with simpler cofinal ones. The terminal object is a final functor from the one-object category. Distinct from Limits/Constructions (which builds limits, not replaces index categories).

Name: Type.lean
Description: Finality in the category of types.

Name: ParallelPair.lean
Description: Final functors from parallel pairs.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Finality for connected diagrams.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Morphism properties defined via limit/colimit behavior: the induced MorphismProperty from a limit shape, and the interaction with Creates and Preserves. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Limits (morphism properties stable under limits) and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: MonoCoprod.lean
Description: Monomorphisms in coproducts: the MonoCoprod typeclass asserting that coproduct injections are monomorphisms (holds in toposes and categories with decidable object equality). Distinct from Limits/EpiMono (general epi/mono results) and EffectiveEpi files.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Limits in opposite categories via duality: limits in Cᵒᵖ are colimits in C and vice versa, with automatic dualization of existence results. Distinct from all other Limits siblings; this is the primary duality file for limits.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Limits indexed by a type (product over a type): ∏ᵢ Fᵢ as a limit over the discrete category on a type, with canonical projections. Distinct from Pi/Basic (the Pi category itself) and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in over-categories C/X: construction from limits in C, interaction with the projection functor, and preservation results. Distinct from Limits/Comma (comma categories generally) and Adjunction/Limits.

Name: IndYoneda.lean
Description: Ind-Yoneda lemma and its interaction with limits: the limit of a cofiltered diagram of representables in a presheaf category, and the pro-Yoneda pairing. Distinct from Limits/Yoneda (standard Yoneda for limits) and Presentable/Presheaf.

Name: HasLimits.lean
Description: The HasLimitsOfShape and HasLimits typeclasses: existence of limits over all diagrams of a given shape or all small shapes. Contains the limit functor and preservation instances. Distinct from Limits/IsLimit (universal property of a specific cone) and Limits/Creates.

Name: IsConnected.lean
Description: The predicate asserting that a specific limit cone is connected (the index diagram is connected in a suitable sense). Distinct from Limits/Connected (connectivity of index categories in general) and Limits/Cones.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in preorder categories viewed as categories: meets as products/limits, joins as coproducts/colimits, and the interaction with lattice operations. Distinct from Order library and Limits/Preorder.

Name: IsLimit.lean
Description: The IsLimit and IsColimit typeclasses: the universal property of a specific cone or cocone, the lift morphism, uniqueness, and the limit comparison map liftCone. Distinct from Limits/HasLimits (existence of limits in general) and Limits/Cones (cone definitions).

Name: SmallComplete.lean
Description: Small-complete categories: categories with limits over all small diagrams. Contains the SmallComplete typeclass and the relationship to presheaf completions. Distinct from Limits/HasLimits (existence of limits of a fixed shape) and Presentable/Limits.

Name: Skeleton.lean
Description: Limits in skeletal categories: transferring limit existence from a category to its skeleton and back. Distinct from Limits/Final (cofinal functors) and Limits/EssentiallySmall.

Name: Unit.lean
Description: Limits in the unit category PUnit: every diagram has limit PUnit (trivially). Provides instances for HasLimits PUnit. Distinct from WithTerminal/Cone and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: The Yoneda lemma for limits: the natural isomorphism Hom(lim F, X) ≅ lim Hom(F(−), X) and functoriality of the Yoneda embedding through limits. Distinct from Limits/Presheaf (presheaf constructions) and Functor/Hom.

Name: VanKampen.lean
Description: The van Kampen condition on pushout squares: a pushout is van Kampen if the functor from cones over the cocone is an equivalence. Used in adhesive categories and for computing fundamental groups via Seifert-van Kampen. Distinct from Adhesive/Basic (which uses van Kampen pushouts) and Limits/Comma.

Name: Presentation.lean
Description: Limit presentations: expressing an object as a limit of a diagram of simpler objects, and functorial presentations. Used in derived and homotopy theory contexts. Distinct from Presentable/ColimitPresentation (the colimit side) and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: Preorder.lean
Description: Limits in preorder categories: meets (finite limits) and joins (finite colimits) computed concretely from the preorder structure. Distinct from Limits/Lattice (lattice-specific) and Order library files.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in presheaf categories: limits are computed pointwise (objectwise), while colimits are the Yoneda extension. Distinct from Limits/FunctorToTypes (Type-valued case) and Sites/Sheafification.

Name: Sifted.lean
Description: Sifted colimits: the IsSifted typeclass (generalising filtered colimits and reflexive coequalisers), and the theorem that sifted colimits commute with finite products. Distinct from Limits/Filtered (filtered case) and Limits/FilteredColimitCommutesProduct.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in the category Set (Type): explicit constructions as Π-types, subtypes, and quotient types, with the instances for HasLimits and HasColimits. Distinct from Limits/FunctorToTypes (functor categories to Type) and Types/Basic.

Name: FunctorToTypes.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in functor categories to Type: limits as sections, colimits as quotients, and explicit constructions. Distinct from Limits/Set (limits in Set itself) and Limits/Presheaf (presheaf categories generally).

Name: Creates.lean
Description: Creation and reflection of limits: the CreatesLimits and ReflectsLimits typeclasses. Contains instances for forgetful functors of concrete categories. Distinct from Limits/HasLimits (existence) and Limits/Preserves (preservation).

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connected limits: a limit shape J is connected if the index category is connected, and connected limits commute with filtered colimits. Contains basic instances. Distinct from Limits/IsConnected (the isConnected predicate on cones) and Filtered/Basic.

Name: Elements.lean
Description: The category of elements el(F) of a presheaf F: objects are pairs (X, x ∈ F(X)), morphisms are maps preserving the element. Contains the Grothendieck construction for set-valued presheaves and the cofinality of the projection el(F) → C. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Grothendieck (full Grothendieck construction).

Name: EssentiallySmall.lean
Description: Limits over essentially small index categories: a locally small category with limits over small diagrams has limits over essentially small ones. Distinct from Limits/SmallComplete (existence over all small diagrams) and Limits/Final.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms arising from limits and colimits: equalizers are monomorphisms, coequalizers are epimorphisms, and related results. Distinct from Limits/MonoCoprod (monos in coproducts) and Abelian/Monomorphisms.

Name: ColimitLimit.lean
Description: The canonical map colim(lim Fᵢ) → lim(colim Fᵢ) and conditions (filtered colimits, finite limits) for it to be an isomorphism. Distinct from Limits/FilteredColimitCommutesFiniteLimit (the theorem itself) and Limits/Fubini.

Name: Bicones.lean
Description: Bicones for simultaneous limit/colimit diagrams: the Bicone F type (a cone and cocone sharing an apex), used to define biproducts in preadditive categories. Distinct from Limits/Cones (limit cones only) and Preadditive/Biproducts (which uses bicones).

Name: Comma.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in comma categories (F ↓ G): construction from limits in the base categories, interaction with projection functors. Distinct from Comma/Basic (defines comma categories) and Limits/Over (over-category limits).

Name: Cones.lean
Description: Limit cones and colimit cocones: the types Cone F and Cocone F, morphisms between them, and the functoriality of the cone construction in the diagram. Distinct from Limits/IsLimit (the universal property of a specific cone) and Limits/ConeCategory.

Name: ConeCategory.lean
Description: The category of cones Cone F over a diagram F: objects are cones, morphisms are cone morphisms, and the limit is the terminal cone. Distinct from Limits/Cones (which defines individual cones) and Limits/IsLimit (the universal property).

Name: FintypeCat.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in the category FintypeCat of finite types: existence results, explicit constructions, and the interaction with the forgetful functor to Type. Distinct from Limits/Set (all types) and FinCategory/Basic.

Name: FinallySmall.lean
Description: Finally small categories: a category J is finally small if it has a final functor from a small category, allowing colimits over J to be computed via small diagrams. Distinct from Limits/Final (defines finality) and Limits/EssentiallySmall.

Name: FormalCoproducts.lean
Description: Formal coproducts: the free category completion under coproducts of a given shape, used in the construction of colimit completions and Karoubi envelopes. Distinct from Limits/Set (concrete coproducts) and Idempotents/Karoubi.

Name: FullSubcategory.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in full subcategories: if a full subcategory is stable under limits, they are computed as in the ambient category. Distinct from Limits/Creates (creation of limits by a functor) and ObjectProperty/LimitsClosure.

Name: Fubini.lean
Description: Fubini theorem for limits: an iterated limit over J × K is isomorphic to a limit over J of limits over K. Distinct from Limits/ColimitLimit (colimit-limit commutation) and Limits/FilteredColimitCommutesFiniteLimit.

Name: Filtered.lean
Description: Filtered and cofiltered colimits/limits: the IsFiltered and IsCofiltered typeclasses (existence of upper bounds and coequalisers), and commutation of filtered colimits with finite limits. Distinct from Limits/FilteredColimitCommutesFiniteLimit (the commutation theorem) and Filtered/Basic.

Name: ExactFunctor.lean
Description: Exact functors in the limits/colimits context: LeftExact (preserves finite limits), RightExact (preserves finite colimits), and Exact (both). Distinct from Preadditive/LeftExact (additive left exact functors) and Abelian/Exact (exactness of sequences).

Name: FilteredColimitCommutesFiniteLimit.lean
Description: The theorem that filtered colimits commute with finite limits in Set and in suitable categories: the canonical map is an isomorphism. Distinct from Limits/ColimitLimit (which defines the map) and Limits/Filtered (filtered index categories).

Name: Final.lean
Description: Final and initial functors: F : J → K is final if colimits over K equal colimits over J. Contains the Final and Initial typeclasses, the criterion via the connected slice categories, and Quillen's Theorem A. Distinct from Limits/FinallySmall (size conditions) and Limits/Connected.

Name: FilteredColimitCommutesProduct.lean
Description: Commutation of filtered colimits with finite products: the canonical map colim(∏ᵢ Fᵢ) → ∏ᵢ(colim Fᵢ) is an isomorphism for filtered diagrams. Distinct from Limits/FilteredColimitCommutesFiniteLimit (all finite limits) and Limits/Pi.

Name: LiftingProperties
Description: Lifting properties of morphisms: left and right lifting properties, weak factorization systems, and model category axioms.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Lifting properties in over-categories: the induced lifting property on C/X from a lifting property in C, and stability under base change. Distinct from LiftingProperties/Basic (base category) and MorphismProperty/OverAdjunction.

Name: ParametrizedAdjunction.lean
Description: Parametrized adjunction lifting: lifting properties transferred along a parametrized adjunction (a functor adjunction depending on a parameter). Distinct from LiftingProperties/Adjunction (non-parametrized) and MorphismProperty/Comma.

Name: PushoutProduct.lean
Description: The pushout-product/pullback-hom adjunction: (f □ g) ⋔ h ↔ f ⋔ hᵍ, where □ is the pushout product. Distinct from Monoidal/PushoutProduct (which defines the pushout product) and LiftingProperties/Basic.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Lifting properties transferred along adjunctions: if f has the left lifting property against W and F ⊣ G, then F(f) has the left lifting property against the image of W under G. Distinct from LiftingProperties/Basic (definitions) and MorphismProperty/LiftingProperty.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for lifting properties: HasLeftLiftingProperty f g asserts every commutative square with f on the left and g on the right has a diagonal fill. Contains basic instances (isomorphisms have both lifting properties) and the 2-of-3 property. Distinct from MorphismProperty/LiftingProperty (the morphism-property version) and LiftingProperties/Adjunction.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Lifting properties and limits: the class of morphisms with a given lifting property is closed under limits of the left factor (products, pullbacks, inverse limits). Contains stability under colimits of the right class. Distinct from LiftingProperties/Basic (definitions) and MorphismProperty/Limits.

Name: Localization
Description: Localization of categories: inverting a class of morphisms, the calculus of fractions, and the derived category construction.

Name: Monoidal
Description: Monoidal structure on a localized category: given a localization functor L : C ⥤ D and a monoidal structure on C whose tensor product preserves the inverted class W, lifts the monoidal structure to D making L a monoidal functor. Defines LocalizedMonoidal and constructs the MonoidalCategory instance on the localization. Used to equip derived categories with a derived tensor product. Distinct from Localization/DerivabilityStructure (the framework for derived functors rather than monoidal structures).

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Monoidal functor to the localisation.

Name: Braided.lean
Description: Braided monoidal localisation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monoidal localisation: the localised category inherits a monoidal structure.

Name: DerivabilityStructure
Description: Derivability structures for constructing derived functors: a framework based on Maltsiniotis's theory, providing a general method to define right or left derived functors via functorial resolutions, without requiring enough injectives or projectives. Defines IsRightDerivabilityStructure, IsLeftDerivabilityStructure, and the Derives predicate. Distinct from DerivedCategory (which provides the target triangulated category) and HomotopyCategory (which provides the source).

Name: OfFunctorialResolutions.lean
Description: Derivability from functorial resolutions.

Name: PointwiseRightDerived.lean
Description: Pointwise right derived functor from derivability.

Name: Derives.lean
Description: Derived functor from a derivability structure.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Derivability structures: abstract framework for derived functors.

Name: Constructor.lean
Description: Constructing derivability structures.

Name: CalculusOfFractions
Description: The calculus of left/right fractions (Ore conditions for categories): conditions on a class W of morphisms ensuring the localized category C[W⁻¹] has an explicit description with morphisms as fractions. The Ore conditions are: (1) W is closed under composition, (2) every W-morphism can be completed to a commutative square. Used for derived categories where quasi-isomorphisms satisfy the conditions.

Name: OfAdjunction.lean
Description: Calculus of fractions from an adjunction.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Calculus of fractions for preadditive categories.

Name: ComposableArrows.lean
Description: Composable arrows in calculus of fractions.

Name: Fractions.lean
Description: Fraction descriptions in Ore localisation.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Preadditive localization: if C is preadditive and W is additive-compatible, C[W⁻¹] is preadditive and Q is additive. Distinct from Localization/Linear (R-linear version) and Preadditive/Basic.

Name: Predicate.lean
Description: The predicate formulation of localization: IsLocalization L W asserts that a given functor L is a localization at W, without explicitly constructing C[W⁻¹]. Contains the predicate and basic consequences. Distinct from Localization/Construction (explicit construction) and Localization/HasLocalization.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Localization of a product category: C₁[W₁⁻¹] × C₂[W₂⁻¹] ≅ (C₁ × C₂)[(W₁ × W₂)⁻¹]. Distinct from Localization/Pi (indexed products) and Localization/Composition.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Localization of a Pi-category: the localization (∏ᵢ Cᵢ)[∏ᵢ Wᵢ⁻¹] ≅ ∏ᵢ Cᵢ[Wᵢ⁻¹]. Distinct from Pi/Basic and Localization/Prod.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal localization: if C is monoidal and W is monoidal-stable, the localization C[W⁻¹] inherits a monoidal structure. Distinct from Localization/Linear (linear structure) and Monoidal/Transport.

Name: OfQuotient.lean
Description: Localization as a quotient: identifies C[W⁻¹] with the quotient category by the equivalence relation generated by W, and the passage between the two descriptions. Distinct from Localization/Construction (universal-property construction) and Quotient/Preadditive.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Localization in the opposite category: if L : C → D localizes at W, then Lᵒᵖ : Cᵒᵖ → Dᵒᵖ localizes at Wᵒᵖ. Distinct from Localization/Construction and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: StructuredArrow.lean
Description: Localization and structured arrow categories: the interaction of the localization functor with the structured arrow categories (X ↓ F). Distinct from Localization/Adjunction and Comma/Basic.

Name: Triangulated.lean
Description: Triangulated structure on localizations: if C is triangulated and W is triangulated-stable, C[W⁻¹] is triangulated, with the shift and distinguished triangles inherited from C. Distinct from Triangulated/Subcategory (subcategory triangulated structure) and Localization/Monoidal.

Name: Trifunctor.lean
Description: Trifunctors compatible with localization: a trifunctor inverting W in all three arguments descends to the localization. Distinct from Localization/Bifunctor (two arguments) and Functor/Trifunctor.

Name: SmallShiftedHom.lean
Description: Small shifted hom-sets in localizations: the graded version of SmallHom for shifted categories, used in triangulated localizations. Distinct from Localization/SmallHom (unshifted) and Triangulated/Subcategory.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: The quotient functor for localizations: properties of Q : C → C[W⁻¹] (full, dense, inverts W) and its universal property. Distinct from Localization/Construction (the construction) and MorphismProperty/Quotient.

Name: Resolution.lean
Description: Resolutions in the localization: defines a resolution of an object of C[W⁻¹] by objects of C, used in derived category constructions and computing derived functors. Distinct from Localization/Construction (general localization) and Abelian/RightDerived.

Name: SmallHom.lean
Description: Small hom-sets in localizations via a calculus of fractions: when W admits a set of chosen roofs, C[W⁻¹] has small hom-sets. Distinct from Localization/HomEquiv (the bijection formula) and Localization/LocallySmall.

Name: CalculusOfFractions.lean
Description: Calculus of left/right fractions: the Gabriel-Zisman condition under which morphisms in C[W⁻¹] can be represented by roofs, with the explicit hom-set formula. Contains the LeftCalculusOfFractions typeclass. Distinct from Localization/Construction (general construction) and Localization/SmallHom.

Name: Composition.lean
Description: Composition of localizations: if L₁ localizes at W₁ and L₂ localizes at L₁(W₂), then L₂ ∘ L₁ localizes at W₁ ∪ W₂. Distinct from Localization/Equivalence (equivalences of localizations) and Localization/LocalizerMorphism.

Name: Construction.lean
Description: The explicit construction of C[W⁻¹]: the universally W-inverting category, the localization functor Q : C → C[W⁻¹], and the proof that Q inverts exactly W. Distinct from Localization/Predicate (which only asserts a functor is a localization) and Localization/CalculusOfFractions.

Name: BousfieldTransfiniteComposition.lean
Description: Bousfield localization via transfinite composition: constructs the Bousfield localization using transfinite iteration of the small object argument. Distinct from Localization/Bousfield (the main Bousfield file) and SmallObject/TransfiniteIteration.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Adjunctions involving localized categories: the induced functor C[W⁻¹] → E from a W-inverting functor C → E, and its adjunction properties. Distinct from Localization/Construction (building the localization) and Localization/Equivalence.

Name: Bifunctor.lean
Description: Bifunctors compatible with localization: a bifunctor C × C → D that inverts W in both arguments descends to the localized category. Distinct from Localization/Trifunctor (three arguments) and Localization/Composition.

Name: Bousfield.lean
Description: Bousfield localization: the localization of C at a morphism property W constructed via the small object argument. Contains the main existence theorem and the resulting weak factorization system. Distinct from Localization/BousfieldTransfiniteComposition (the transfinite construction) and Localization/HasLocalization.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: R-linear localization: if C is R-linear and W is a linear morphism property, C[W⁻¹] is R-linear and Q is R-linear. Distinct from Localization/Preadditive (additive version) and Linear/Basic.

Name: LocalizerMorphism.lean
Description: Morphisms of localizers: a functor F : C → D compatible with morphism properties W_C and W_D, inducing a functor on localizations. Distinct from Localization/Composition (composing localizations) and Localization/Equivalence.

Name: LocallySmall.lean
Description: Local smallness of localized categories: conditions under which C[W⁻¹] has small hom-sets. Distinct from Localization/SmallHom (the hom-set formula) and Localization/CalculusOfFractions.

Name: HomEquiv.lean
Description: Hom-set equivalences in the localization: the canonical bijection between Hom_{C[W⁻¹]}(QX, QY) and the appropriate fraction set. Distinct from Localization/CalculusOfFractions (roofs) and Localization/SmallHom.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Equivalences of localizations: if L₁ and L₂ both localize C at W, they are equivalent via a canonical comparison functor. Distinct from Localization/Construction and Localization/Predicate.

Name: FiniteProducts.lean
Description: Preservation of finite products under localization: conditions under which Q preserves finite products and the localized category has finite products. Distinct from Localization/Adjunction and Localization/Linear.

Name: HasLocalization.lean
Description: The HasLocalization typeclass: asserts that a localization of C at W exists in a given universe. Contains existence and uniqueness up to equivalence. Distinct from Localization/Construction (the explicit construction) and Localization/Bousfield.

Name: Monad
Description: Monads and comonads: the Kleisli and Eilenberg-Moore categories, Beck's monadicity theorem, and monadic functors.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in Eilenberg-Moore categories: if C has limits of a given shape and T preserves them, the category of T-algebras has limits of that shape (created by U). Distinct from Monad/Equalizer and Monad/Coequalizer.

Name: Kleisli.lean
Description: The Kleisli category of a monad: objects are objects of C, morphisms X → Y are maps T X → Y (Kleisli morphisms), and the Kleisli inclusion C → Kleisli(T). Contains the adjunction Kleisli(T) ⊣ Algebra(T). Distinct from Monad/Algebra (Eilenberg-Moore) and Monad/Basic.

Name: EquivMon.lean
Description: The equivalence between monads on C and monoid objects in the monoidal category End(C) of endofunctors: every monad is a Mon_ in End(C). Distinct from Monad/Basic (monad definition) and Monoidal/End.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Monads on the category Type: the list monad, option monad, and state monad as instances, with their Eilenberg-Moore algebras identified as concrete algebraic structures (monoids, sets with distinguished element, etc.). Distinct from Monad/Basic (abstract definition) and Monad/Kleisli.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in Eilenberg-Moore categories: when C has products and T preserves them, the algebra category has products. Distinct from Monad/Limits (general limits) and Preadditive/Biproducts.

Name: Monadicity.lean
Description: Beck's monadicity theorem: G : D → C is monadic iff it has a left adjoint, reflects isomorphisms, and D has and G preserves the relevant coequalizers. Contains the precise Beck conditions. Distinct from Monad/Adjunction and Monad/Comonadicity.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for monads and comonads: the Monad typeclass (endofunctor T with μ : T² → T, η : Id → T satisfying the monad laws), the Comonad typeclass, and basic instances. Distinct from Monad/Adjunction (adjunction connection) and Monad/Algebra (algebras).

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Eilenberg-Moore algebras over a monad: the Algebra T typeclass with structure map a : T X → X, the category of algebras, the forgetful functor U : Algebra T → C, and the free algebra functor F : C → Algebra T. Distinct from Monad/Adjunction (the generating adjunction) and Endofunctor/Algebra.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: The connection between monads and adjunctions: every adjunction F ⊣ G generates a monad T = G ∘ F, and the comparison functor to the Eilenberg-Moore category. Contains Beck's theorem. Distinct from Monad/Monadicity (when the comparison is an equivalence) and Monad/Basic.

Name: Equalizer.lean
Description: Equalizers in Eilenberg-Moore categories: constructs equalizers in the category of algebras from equalizers in the base category. Distinct from Monad/Coequalizer and Monad/Limits.

Name: Comonadicity.lean
Description: Beck's comonadicity theorem: conditions under which a functor is comonadic (the comparison functor to the Eilenberg-Moore coalgebra category is an equivalence). Distinct from Monad/Monadicity (monadic version) and Monad/Adjunction.

Name: Coequalizer.lean
Description: Coequalizers in Eilenberg-Moore categories: the category of algebras has coequalizers when the base does and the monad preserves them. Distinct from Monad/Equalizer (equalizer version) and Monad/Limits.

Name: MarkovCategory
Description: Markov categories: categorical semantics for probability theory and stochastic processes, with copy and discard maps.

Name: Positive.lean
Description: Positive Markov categories: a Markov category satisfying the positivity axiom (a state that factors through the zero object is itself zero), used for a well-behaved categorical probability theory. Distinct from MarkovCategory/Basic (without positivity) and CopyDiscardCategory/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Markov categories: a symmetric monoidal category where every object is a cocommutative comonoid via copy (Δ : X → X ⊗ X) and delete (ε : X → 𝟙_), axiomatizing categorical probability theory. Contains the MarkovCategory typeclass and basic instances. Distinct from MarkovCategory/Positive (adds a positivity axiom).

Name: LocallyCartesianClosed
Description: Locally cartesian closed categories: categories where each slice category is cartesian closed, modeling dependent type theory.

Name: Over.lean
Description: The locally cartesian closed structure on over-categories: if C is locally cartesian closed, each slice C/X is cartesian closed. Distinct from LocallyCartesianClosed/Sections (global sections) and Closed/Cartesian (abstract cartesian closed categories).

Name: Sections.lean
Description: Sections of the locally cartesian closed structure: the global sections functor Γ : C/X → C and its right adjoint, and the interaction with exponentiable morphisms. Distinct from LocallyCartesianClosed/Over and LocallyCartesianClosed/ExponentiableMorphism.

Name: ChosenPullbacksAlong.lean
Description: Chosen pullbacks along a fixed morphism: the functor f* : C/Y → C/X for f : X → Y defined by chosen pullback squares, used to construct the locally cartesian closed structure. Distinct from LocallyCartesianClosed/ExponentiableMorphism (characterises when f* has a right adjoint) and LocallyCartesianClosed/Over.

Name: ExponentiableMorphism.lean
Description: Exponentiable morphisms: a morphism f : X → Y is exponentiable if the pullback functor f* : C/Y → C/X has a right adjoint Πf. Defines the ExponentiableMorphism typeclass and proves basic instances. Distinct from LocallyCartesianClosed/ChosenPullbacksAlong (constructing f*) and Closed/Cartesian.

Name: Sums
Description: Coproducts of categories: the categorical sum C + D and its universal property.

Name: Products.lean
Description: The distributivity of categorical coproducts over products: the canonical isomorphism C × (D ⊕ E) ≅ (C × D) ⊕ (C × E). Distinct from Sums/Basic and Products/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The categorical coproduct (disjoint union) C ⊕ D of two categories: objects are C.obj ⊕ D.obj (Sum type), morphisms only exist between same-summand objects, with inclusion functors inl and inr. Distinct from Sums/Associator and Sigma/Basic (indexed coproduct).

Name: Associator.lean
Description: The associator isomorphism (C ⊕ D) ⊕ E ≅ C ⊕ (D ⊕ E) for categorical coproducts. Distinct from Sums/Basic (defines the coproduct) and Products/Associator (product associator).

Name: Subpresheaf
Description: Subpresheaves and natural transformations that are objectwise injective, with applications to sheaf theory.

Name: OfSection.lean
Description: Subpresheaves generated by sections: the smallest subpresheaf containing a given set of sections, and the interaction with sheafification. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Basic (general subpresheaves) and Subpresheaf/Sieves.

Name: Sieves.lean
Description: Sieves as subpresheaves of the representable presheaf よ(X): identifies sieves on X with subpresheaves of よ(X) and relates the sieve sheaf condition to the subpresheaf sheaf condition. Distinct from Sites/Sieves (direct sieve definitions) and Subpresheaf/Basic.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subpresheaves as subobjects in the presheaf category: the correspondence between subpresheaves of F and subobjects of F in [Cᵒᵖ, Type], and the resulting lattice. Distinct from Subobject/Presheaf (general subobject lattice) and Subpresheaf/Basic.

Name: Image.lean
Description: The image subpresheaf of a natural transformation α : F → G: the objectwise image α_X(F(X)) ⊆ G(X). Contains the image factorization for natural transformations of presheaves. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Basic and Subpresheaf/Equalizer.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Subpresheaves of a presheaf F : Cᵒᵖ → Type: a subpresheaf G ≤ F assigns to each object X a subset G(X) ⊆ F(X) compatible with restrictions. Contains the definition, the order structure, and the induced natural transformation G → F. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Sieves (sieve-generated subpresheaves) and Subobject/Presheaf.

Name: Equalizer.lean
Description: Equalizers in the poset of subpresheaves: the intersection G₁ ∩ G₂ as the equalizer of the two inclusions. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Basic (basic operations) and Subpresheaf/Image.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite subpresheaves: subpresheaves that are finitely generated or have finite fibers. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Basic and Subpresheaf/OfSection.

Name: Subobject
Description: Subobjects in a category: the poset of monomorphisms into a fixed object, and the subobject classifier in a topos.

Name: Classifier
Description: The subobject classifier Ω in a topos: a special object with a 'true' map 1 → Ω such that every monomorphism A ↪ B corresponds uniquely to a characteristic map B → Ω. In Set, Ω = {0,1} = Bool. The subobject classifier is the internal logic of the topos. Distinct from the Subobject folder (which classifies subobjects in any category, without a classifier object).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Subobject classifier: `Ω` such that `Mono(X, Y) ≅ Hom(X, Ω)` (topos-theoretic).

Name: NoetherianObject.lean
Description: Noetherian objects: objects satisfying the ascending chain condition on subobjects. Contains the NoetherianObject typeclass and preservation under quotients. Distinct from Subobject/ArtinianObject (descending chain) and Subobject/Lattice.

Name: MonoOver.lean
Description: The category MonoOver X of monomorphisms into X: objects are pairs (A, m : A ↣ X), morphisms are triangles, and the projection to Subobject X. This is the unquotiented version on which Subobject/Basic is built. Distinct from Subobject/Basic (the quotient) and Abelian/Monomorphisms.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Subobjects and limits: interaction of subobject lattices with limit functors, and how limits of subobjects relate to subobjects of limits. Distinct from Subobject/Lattice (lattice operations) and Limits/EpiMono.

Name: WellPowered.lean
Description: Well-powered categories: categories where each object has only a set (not a proper class) of subobjects. Contains the WellPowered typeclass and instances for presheaf categories. Distinct from Subobject/Basic and Presentable/Limits.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Subobjects in the category Type: every subobject corresponds to a subtype (subset), and lattice operations are intersection and union. Distinct from Subobject/Basic (abstract) and Types/Basic.

Name: Presheaf.lean
