Description: Subobjects in presheaf categories: the subobject lattice of a presheaf corresponds to subpresheaves, including the subobject classifier. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Subobject (the explicit identification) and Subobject/Basic.

Name: Comma.lean
Description: Subobjects in comma categories: subobjects of an object in (F ↓ G) and their relationship to subobjects in the base categories. Distinct from Subobject/Basic and Comma/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Subobjects in a category: the type Subobject X as an equivalence class of monomorphisms into X, the partial order, and the subobject lattice. Foundation for all sibling Subobject files.

Name: ArtinianObject.lean
Description: Artinian objects: objects satisfying the descending chain condition on subobjects. Contains the ArtinianObject typeclass and basic properties. Distinct from Subobject/NoetherianObject (ascending chain condition) and Subobject/Lattice.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: The lattice structure on Subobject X: meets (intersection via pullback), joins (image of coproduct), and the bounded lattice axioms. Contains the complete lattice in the abelian case. Distinct from Subobject/Basic (which defines the type) and Abelian/Subobject.

Name: HasCardinalLT.lean
Description: Subobjects of cardinality less than a cardinal κ: the HasCardinalLT typeclass for subobjects. Used in the theory of locally presentable categories and generator arguments. Distinct from ObjectProperty/HasCardinalLT and Subobject/Basic.

Name: FactorThru.lean
Description: Factoring morphisms through subobjects: given a monomorphism m : S ↣ X and a morphism f : Y → X whose image lies in S, the factored morphism factorThru and its uniqueness. Distinct from Subobject/Basic (subobject type) and Abelian/Images.

Name: Topos
Description: Toposes: categories with a subobject classifier, finite limits, and power objects. Grothendieck toposes and elementary toposes.

Name: Sheaf.lean
Description: Sheaves in a topos with respect to the canonical topology: the sheaf condition relative to the subobject classifier Ω, and the identification of sheaves with the associated sheaf functor. Distinct from Sites/Sheaf (sheaves for general Grothendieck topologies) and Topos/Classifier.

Name: Classifier.lean
Description: The subobject classifier in a topos: the object Ω with universal subobject true : ⊤ → Ω, and the characteristic morphism χ_S : X → Ω of any subobject S ↣ X. Contains the ClassifiedBy predicate and the bijection between subobjects of X and morphisms X → Ω. Distinct from Topos/Sheaf and Subobject/Basic.

Name: WithTerminal
Description: The category C with a freely adjoined terminal (or initial) object, used in the Grothendieck construction.

Name: FinCategory.lean
Description: The finite category structure on WithTerminal and WithInitial: adjoining a terminal/initial to a finite category preserves finiteness. Distinct from WithTerminal/Basic and FinCategory/Basic.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for WithTerminal and WithInitial: morphism characterisation, functor extension, and basic computation lemmas supplementing Basic. Distinct from WithTerminal/Basic (definitions) and WithTerminal/Cone.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Adjoinment of a terminal or initial object: the categories WithTerminal C and WithInitial C obtained by freely adjoining ⊤ (resp. ⊥) to C. Contains the inclusion functor and the universal property (any functor C → D with a terminal object extends). Distinct from WithTerminal/Lemmas and WithTerminal/Cone.

Name: Cone.lean
Description: Cone constructions in WithTerminal categories: limit cones that use the adjoined terminal object and their interaction with cone morphisms. Distinct from WithTerminal/Basic and Limits/Cones.

Name: Types
Description: The category of types (Type u) in Lean: limits, colimits, and representable functors in this concrete category.

Name: Monomorphisms.lean
Description: Monomorphisms in Type: characterises Mono f ↔ Function.Injective f. Distinct from Types/Epimorphisms and Subobject/Types.

Name: Set.lean
Description: The full subcategory of Set in Type: the SmallCategory instance for finite types and the interaction between the categorical Set and Type. Distinct from Types/Basic (all types) and Limits/Set.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The category Type of Lean types: the CategoryTheory.Types instance (morphisms are functions, composition is function composition), and basic instances for limits, colimits, and concrete structure. Foundation for all files in this folder. Distinct from Types/Monomorphisms, Types/Epimorphisms, and Types/Set.

Name: Epimorphisms.lean
Description: Epimorphisms in Type: characterises Epi f ↔ Function.Surjective f. Distinct from Types/Monomorphisms and Limits/EpiMono.

Name: Triangulated
Description: Triangulated categories: the structure of distinguished triangles, the octahedral axiom, and the derived category as a triangulated category.

Name: TStructure
Description: T-structures on triangulated categories: a t-structure (D≤0, D≥0) is a pair of full subcategories satisfying orthogonality and truncation conditions, with heart D≤0 ∩ D≥0 abelian. Contains TStructure, IsLE, IsGE, the truncation functors truncLE, truncGE, truncLT, truncGT, the heart construction, and induced t-structures. The standard t-structure on D(A) recovers A as its heart. Distinct from DerivedCategory (which carries the t-structure) and Triangulated (the basic triangulated structure).

Name: SpectralObject.lean
Description: Spectral objects from a t-structure.

Name: Induced.lean
Description: Induced t-structure on a subcategory.

Name: TruncLTGE.lean
Description: Truncation <n and ≥n for t-structures.

Name: TruncLEGT.lean
Description: Truncation ≤n and >n for t-structures.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: t-structures on triangulated categories: `TStructure C` — the abstract t-structure data.

Name: AbelianSubcategory.lean
Description: Abelian subcategory from a t-structure: the heart.

Name: Heart.lean
Description: The heart of a t-structure: the abelian subcategory `D^{≤0} ∩ D^{≥0}`.

Name: ETrunc.lean
Description: E-truncation in a t-structure.

Name: Opposite
Description: The opposite triangulated category: reversing all morphisms and replacing the shift functor [1] by [-1]. Used to dualize triangulated results (e.g., the dual of a t-structure) and to compare the derived category of coherent sheaves with its opposite. Distinct from CategoryTheory/Opposites (opposite of a plain category).

Name: Subcategory.lean
Description: Subcategories of opposite triangulated categories.

Name: Triangle.lean
Description: Triangles in opposite triangulated categories.

Name: Triangulated.lean
Description: Octahedral axiom in opposite triangulated categories.

Name: Pretriangulated.lean
Description: Opposite pretriangulated structure.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Opposite triangulated categories: the opposite with swapped shift.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functors on opposite triangulated categories.

Name: OpOp.lean
Description: Double opposite of a triangulated category.

Name: Subcategory.lean
Description: Triangulated subcategories: full subcategories closed under shifts and cones of distinguished triangles, with the interaction with localization functors. Contains the Subcategory typeclass and basic instances. Distinct from Triangulated/Orthogonal and Localization/Triangulated.

Name: SpectralObject.lean
Description: `SpectralObject C ι`: the category of spectral objects in a pretriangulated category `C` indexed by a category `ι`. TODO: construct spectral objects from t-structures, define the analogous notion in abelian categories, and build the associated spectral sequence.

Name: Rotate.lean
Description: Rotation of distinguished triangles: the rotation axiom (X → Y → Z → X[1] distinguished implies Y → Z → X[1] → Y[1] distinguished) and the rotation functor. Distinct from Triangulated/Basic (which states the axiom) and Triangulated/TriangleShift.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: The Yoneda lemma in the triangulated setting: representable homological functors よ(X) and identification of morphisms via natural transformations of homological functors. Distinct from Triangulated/HomologicalFunctor and Limits/Yoneda.

Name: Triangulated.lean
Description: The full triangulated axioms including the octahedral axiom: proves that the octahedral axiom holds in standard examples (derived categories) and follows from the others in certain cases. Distinct from Triangulated/Pretriangulated (without octahedral axiom) and Triangulated/Basic.

Name: TriangleShift.lean
Description: The interaction of the shift functor with triangles: the shifted triangle T[n], sign changes on the third morphism, and commutativity of shift and rotation. Distinct from Triangulated/Rotate (the rotation axiom) and Shift/Basic.

Name: Pretriangulated.lean
Description: Pretriangulated categories: the triangle axioms without the octahedral axiom, with the basic consequences (the composition of two consecutive maps in a distinguished triangle is zero). Distinct from Triangulated/Basic (adds octahedral axiom) and Triangulated/Rotate.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Triangulated functors: the Functor.IsTriangulated typeclass asserting F commutes with shifts and sends distinguished triangles to distinguished triangles. Contains composition results. Distinct from Triangulated/Basic (triangle definitions) and Triangulated/Adjunction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for pretriangulated and triangulated categories: the Triangle typeclass (X → Y → Z → X[1]), distinguished triangles, the octahedral axiom, and the shift functor. Foundation for all files in this folder. Distinct from Triangulated/Pretriangulated (without octahedral axiom) and Shift/Basic.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Adjunctions between triangulated categories: a left or right adjoint of a triangulated functor is triangulated, with the CommShift transferred along the adjunction. Distinct from Triangulated/Functor (triangulated functors themselves) and Adjunction/Basic.

Name: Orthogonal.lean
Description: Semiorthogonal decompositions in triangulated categories: a pair (S, T) is semiorthogonal if Hom(S, T) = 0 and every object fits in a triangle with terms in S and T. Distinct from Triangulated/Subcategory (triangulated subcategories) and ObjectProperty/Orthogonal.

Name: HomologicalFunctor.lean
Description: Homological functors from triangulated categories: Functor.IsHomological asserts H : T → A sends distinguished triangles to long exact sequences. Contains the long exact sequence construction. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and Triangulated/Functor.

Name: Generators.lean
Description: Generators of triangulated categories: an object G generates the triangulated category if every object can be built from G by shifts and cones. Contains the generate predicate and existence theorems. Distinct from Generator/Basic and Triangulated/Subcategory.

Name: Subfunctor
Description: Subfunctors: natural transformations that are objectwise monomorphic, providing a notion of sub-presheaf.

Name: OfSection.lean
Description: Subfunctors generated by sections: the smallest subfunctor containing a given collection of sections. Distinct from Subfunctor/Basic and Subpresheaf/OfSection.

Name: Sieves.lean
Description: Sieves as subfunctors of representable functors: the identification of sieves on X with subfunctors of よ(X) in the general functor-category setting. Distinct from Sites/Sieves (direct sieve definitions) and Subpresheaf/Sieves.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subfunctors as subobjects in functor categories: the lattice of subobjects of F in a functor category. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Subobject (presheaf case) and Subobject/Basic.

Name: Image.lean
Description: The image subfunctor of a natural transformation α : F → G: the objectwise image. Contains the image factorization for natural transformations between general functors. Distinct from Subfunctor/Basic and Subpresheaf/Image.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Subfunctors of a functor F : C → D: a subfunctor G ≤ F assigns to each object X a subobject G(X) ≤ F(X), compatible with the functorial action. Contains the definition, order structure, and the induced natural transformation. Distinct from Subpresheaf/Basic (presheaf = Type-valued case) and Subobject/Basic.

Name: Equalizer.lean
Description: Equalizers of subfunctors: the intersection G₁ ∩ G₂ as an equalizer of the inclusion natural transformations. Distinct from Subfunctor/Basic and Subpresheaf/Equalizer.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite subfunctors: subfunctors with finite fibers or generated by finitely many sections. Distinct from Subfunctor/Basic and Subpresheaf/Finite.

Name: RegularCategory
Description: Regular categories: categories with pullback-stable regular epimorphisms, providing a setting for first-order logic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Regular categories: finitely complete categories where every morphism factors as a regular epimorphism followed by a monomorphism, and regular epimorphisms are stable under pullback. Defines the RegularCategory typeclass. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/RegularEpi (regular epi definition alone) and Abelian/Basic (which implies regularity).

Name: Quotient
Description: Quotient categories by a congruence relation on morphisms, and the universal property of the quotient functor.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on quotient categories: if C is preadditive and the congruence is additive (compatible with addition of morphisms), the quotient is preadditive. Distinct from Quotient/Linear (R-linear version) and Preadditive/Basic.

Name: LocallySmall.lean
Description: Local smallness of quotient categories: conditions ensuring the quotient C/~ has small hom-sets. Distinct from Quotient/Preadditive and Localization/LocallySmall.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: R-linear structure on quotient categories: if C is R-linear and the congruence is R-linear-compatible, the quotient category is R-linear. Distinct from Quotient/Preadditive (additive version) and Linear/Basic.

Name: Profunctor
Description: Profunctors (distributors): functors C^op x D to Set, composable via coend, and used in categorical systems theory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Profunctors (distributors) between categories: a profunctor P : C ⇸ D is a functor D^op × C → Set, with the coend-based composition. Contains the identity profunctor, the bicategory of categories and profunctors, and the Yoneda embedding as a profunctor. Distinct from Functor/Hom (hom as a profunctor) and Enriched/Basic.

Name: Shift
Description: Shift functors on categories: autoequivalences used in triangulated categories and graded algebra.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Shift structure on quotient categories: the shift descends to a quotient by a shift-compatible additive congruence. Distinct from Shift/Basic and Quotient/Preadditive.

Name: ShiftedHom.lean
Description: Shifted hom-sets: ShiftedHom X n Y := Hom(X, Y⟦n⟧) and the graded-category composition, used in the enriched structure of triangulated categories. Distinct from Shift/ShiftedHomOpposite (opposite-category version) and Localization/SmallShiftedHom.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Shift structure on the opposite category: if C has a shift by M, then C^op has a shift by M^op, with the opposite CommShift isomorphisms. Distinct from Shift/Basic and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pulling back shift structure along a functor: the shift on C induces a shift on D when D is equipped with a functor D → C. Distinct from Shift/Induced (along equivalences) and Shift/Basic.

Name: SingleFunctors.lean
Description: Single functors adapted to shifts: functors from a shifted category to an abelian category that restrict to a fixed degree, forming the basis for cohomological functors. Distinct from Shift/CommShift and Triangulated/HomologicalFunctor.

Name: Twist.lean
Description: The twist automorphism in a shifted category: the isomorphism relating different sign conventions for the shift, and its interaction with CommShift. Distinct from Shift/Basic and Triangulated/TriangleShift.

Name: ShiftedHomOpposite.lean
Description: Shifted hom-sets in the opposite category: ShiftedHom in C^op and compatibility with the opposite shift. Distinct from Shift/ShiftedHom (forward direction) and Shift/Opposite.

Name: ShiftSequence.lean
Description: A family of natural isomorphisms organizing the coherence between different shifts: the ShiftSequence structure used in triangulated and homological settings. Distinct from Shift/CommShift and Shift/InducedShiftSequence.

Name: CommShift.lean
Description: Functors commuting with shifts: the CommShift typeclass asserting that F commutes with the shift up to a natural isomorphism, and the coherence conditions between the commutation isomorphisms at different degrees. Distinct from Shift/Basic (defines the shift) and Shift/CommShiftTwo.

Name: CommShiftTwo.lean
Description: Two-variable commutation with shifts: defines when a bifunctor commutes with the shift in each variable simultaneously. Distinct from Shift/CommShift (single-variable) and GradedObject/Bifunctor.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Adjunctions and shift functors: the CommShift structure transfers along adjunctions (the left/right adjoint of a CommShift functor also CommShifts). Distinct from Shift/CommShift (defines CommShift) and Adjunction/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The shift functor on a category: HasShift C M asserts the existence of a monoidal functor from a monoid M to autoequivalences of C, giving the shift X ↦ X⟦n⟧ and coherence isomorphisms. Foundation for all sibling Shift files.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: R-linear shift functors: compatibility of the CommShift structure with R-linear structure on hom-sets. Distinct from Shift/Basic and Linear/Basic.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Shift structure on localized categories: the shift functor descends to C[W⁻¹] when W is stable under shifts. Distinct from Localization/Triangulated and Shift/Basic.

Name: Induced.lean
Description: Induced shift structure: transfers a shift functor along a fully faithful functor or equivalence. Distinct from Shift/Basic (defines shifts from scratch) and Shift/Pullback.

Name: InducedShiftSequence.lean
Description: Induced shift sequences: functorial families of isomorphisms relating the shift at different levels, induced from a base shift sequence. Distinct from Shift/Induced (single transfer) and Shift/ShiftSequence.

Name: SmallObject
Description: The small object argument: a transfinite construction that produces functorial factorizations in model categories.

Name: Iteration
Description: Transfinite iteration in the small object argument: constructing a functorial factorization by iterating a pushout construction through all ordinals. The limit at each ordinal stage and the colimit at limit ordinals. Used to produce cofibrant replacement in model categories. Distinct from the top-level SmallObject folder (which states the argument) — this subfolder formalizes the transfinite construction.

Name: FunctorOfCocone.lean
Description: Functor from a cocone in the small object iteration.

Name: Nonempty.lean
Description: Non-emptiness of the small object iteration.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Small object argument: transfinite iteration of the pushout-product construction.

Name: ExtendToSucc.lean
Description: Extension of small-object iterations to successor ordinals.

Name: TransfiniteCompositionLifting.lean
Description: Lifting properties preserved under transfinite composition: the left lifting property against a class W is preserved when composing a transfinite sequence of W-maps. Distinct from MorphismProperty/TransfiniteComposition (defines transfinite composition) and SmallObject/TransfiniteIteration.

Name: TransfiniteIteration.lean
Description: Transfinite iteration of endofunctors or morphism properties along ordinal-indexed chains: used in the small object argument and Bousfield localization. Distinct from SmallObject/Basic and Localization/BousfieldTransfiniteComposition.

Name: WellOrderInductionData.lean
Description: Well-order induction data for transfinite constructions: the induction hypothesis and successor/limit step data needed for building transfinite sequences by well-founded recursion. A technical support file for SmallObject/TransfiniteIteration. Distinct from SmallObject/TransfiniteIteration (which consumes this data).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The small object argument setup: functorial cylinder objects, the pushout-product structure, and the basic data needed for the small object argument in homotopical algebra. Distinct from SmallObject/Construction (the full WFS construction) and SmallObject/TransfiniteIteration.

Name: Construction.lean
Description: The full small object argument: given a set of morphisms I, constructs the (I-cell, I-inj) weak factorization system via transfinite composition of pushouts along I. Distinct from SmallObject/Basic (setup) and SmallObject/IsCardinalForSmallObjectArgument.

Name: IsCardinalForSmallObjectArgument.lean
Description: The IsCardinalForSmallObjectArgument typeclass: asserts that a cardinal κ is sufficiently large relative to the generating set I for the small object argument to converge at step κ. Distinct from SmallObject/Basic and Presentable/IsCardinalFiltered.

Name: Sites
Description: Grothendieck sites and sheaves: a coverage (or Grothendieck topology) on a category and the category of sheaves it defines.

Name: NonabelianCohomology
Description: Non-abelian Čech cohomology H¹ for a sheaf of groups on a site: defines 0-cochains, 1-cochains, 1-cocycles (satisfying the cocycle condition gᵢⱼgⱼₖ = gᵢₖ on triple overlaps), and the cohomologous equivalence relation. The cohomology set H1 classifies G-torsors over the site as a pointed set. Distinct from Sites/SheafCohomology (abelian coefficients and higher cohomology via derived functors).

Name: H1.lean
Description: Non-abelian H¹: torsors and Čech cocycles.

Name: Hypercover
Description: Hypercoverings: simplicial objects that are covers at each level, generalizing covering families. The Čech nerve of a cover is a hypercover. Homotopy descent for hypercoverings: a sheaf satisfies descent for a hypercover iff its sections form a homotopy limit. Used in étale cohomology and ∞-topos theory.

Name: Subcanonical.lean
Description: Subcanonical topologies from hypercovers.

Name: SheafOfTypes.lean
Description: Sheaves of types via hypercovers.

Name: ZeroFamily.lean
Description: Family of 0-hypercovers.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: 0-hypercovers: surjective maps as hypercovers.

Name: IsSheaf.lean
Description: Sheaf condition via hypercovers.

Name: Homotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy properties of hypercovers.

Name: Saturate.lean
Description: Saturation of hypercovers.

Name: One.lean
Description: 1-hypercovers: two-term hypercovers.

Name: SheafCohomology
Description: Cohomology of sheaves on a site: derived functors of the global sections functor Γ : Sh(C, J) → Ab, with cohomology functors H and cohomologyFunctor. Contains an initial Mayer-Vietoris sequence result (sequence_exact). An early-stage formalization with active development; the full long exact sequence in cohomology and comparison with Čech cohomology are not yet present. Distinct from Sites/NonabelianCohomology (non-abelian H¹) and Homology/DerivedCategory (the general machinery for derived functors).

Name: MayerVietoris.lean
Description: Mayer–Vietoris for sheaf cohomology.

Name: Cech.lean
Description: Čech cohomology: `H^n` computed via Čech complexes for a cover.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Sheaf cohomology: `H^n(X, F)` via injective resolutions of the sheaf `F`.

Name: Point
Description: Points of a topos: geometric morphisms Set → Sh(C, J) (or equivalently, flat continuous functors C → Set). A topos has enough points if its points jointly detect isomorphisms. Used to check sheaf conditions and to relate toposes to their classifying spaces.

Name: OfIsCofiltered.lean
Description: Points from cofiltered systems.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal points of a site.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Points of over-toposes.

Name: Skyscraper.lean
Description: Skyscraper point: the point associated to a single stalk.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Presheaf points.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Map of points.

Name: Category.lean
Description: Category of points of a topos.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Points of a Grothendieck topos: geometric morphisms from Set.

Name: Comap.lean
Description: Comap of a point.

Name: IsMonoidalW.lean
Description: Monoidal W-maps for points.

Name: Conservative.lean
Description: Conservative systems of points: enough points detect isomorphisms.

Name: CoversTop
Description: The condition that a covering family covers the top element: a site-theoretic condition used to ensure sheaves satisfy certain gluing conditions. Contains the interaction between covers and the terminal object of the site.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Covers-top in over-categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Sieves covering the top: `∀ X, covers_top S X`.

Name: Coherent
Description: Coherent topologies and coherent sheaves: the coherent topology on a coherent category (one with finite limits and a generating set of compact objects). A presheaf is a sheaf for the coherent topology iff it sends finite effective epimorphic families to limit cones. Used for the Zariski and étale topologies on coherent schemes.

Name: ReflectsPreregular.lean
Description: Reflecting preregular conditions.

Name: ReflectsPrecoherent.lean
Description: Reflecting precoherent conditions.

Name: LocallySurjective.lean
Description: Locally surjective maps on coherent sites.

Name: RegularSheaves.lean
Description: Sheaves on the regular topology.

Name: SheafComparison.lean
Description: Comparison of sheaves between coherent topologies.

Name: SequentialLimit.lean
Description: Sequential limits on coherent sites.

Name: RegularTopology.lean
Description: The regular Grothendieck topology.

Name: ExtensiveTopology.lean
Description: The extensive Grothendieck topology.

Name: CoherentTopology.lean
Description: The coherent Grothendieck topology.

Name: CoherentSheaves.lean
Description: Sheaves on the coherent topology.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Coherent topology on a category: the topology generated by jointly epimorphic families.

Name: Comparison.lean
Description: Comparison of coherent topologies.

Name: ExtensiveSheaves.lean
Description: Sheaves on the extensive topology.

Name: ExtensiveColimits.lean
Description: Extensive colimits and coherent topology.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Equivalences of coherent sites.

Name: Descent
Description: Descent theory for sheaves: conditions under which data on a cover glues to data on the covered object. Effective descent morphisms: maps p : U → X such that the pullback functor p* : Sh(X) → Sh(U) is monadic. Faithfully flat descent in algebraic geometry.

Name: IsPrestack.lean
Description: Prestacks: presheaves satisfying the first descent condition.

Name: IsStack.lean
Description: Stacks: sheaves of categories satisfying both descent conditions.

Name: Precoverage.lean
Description: Precoverages for sites.

Name: DescentData.lean
Description: Descent data for sheaves on a site.

Name: DescentDataAsCoalgebra.lean
Description: Descent data as a coalgebra structure.

Name: DescentDataPrime.lean
Description: Alternative formulation of descent data.

Name: DenseSubsite
Description: Dense subsites: a full subcategory D ↪ C such that every object of C is covered by objects of D. The comparison lemma: if D is dense in C, then Sh(C) ≃ Sh(D). Used to replace a site with a smaller generating subcategory without changing the sheaf category. Distinct from Sites/Coherent (which concerns specific covering conditions).

Name: OneHypercoverDense.lean
Description: Density via hypercovers.

Name: SheafEquiv.lean
Description: Sheaf equivalence between dense subsites.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Dense subsites: a functor whose image is topologically dense.

Name: InducedTopology.lean
Description: Topology induced by a dense subsite.

Name: PrecoverageToGrothendieck.lean
Description: Converting a precoverage to a Grothendieck topology: the generated topology from a precoverage, and conditions under which a presheaf that is a sheaf for the precoverage is a sheaf for the generated topology. Distinct from Sites/Precoverage and Sites/Grothendieck.

Name: Precoverage.lean
Description: Precoverages: weaker than coverages (no closure conditions required), used as a starting point before generating a Grothendieck topology. Distinct from Sites/Coverage and Sites/PrecoverageToGrothendieck.

Name: Plus.lean
Description: The plus construction F ↦ F⁺: the one-step approximation to sheafification, with F⁺(X) = colim_{S ∈ J(X)} Hom(S, F). Contains the Plus functor and the natural transformation F → F⁺. Distinct from Sites/Sheafification (the double-plus F⁺⁺) and Sites/CompatiblePlus.

Name: Preserves.lean
Description: Functors that preserve sheaves: the IsSheafPreserving typeclass and basic instances asserting a functor sends sheaves to sheaves. Distinct from Sites/PreservesSheafification (commutation with sheafification) and Sites/CoverPreserving.

Name: PreservesSheafification.lean
Description: Functors commuting with sheafification: conditions under which a functor Psh(C) → Psh(D) restricts to sheaves and commutes with the sheafification adjunction. Distinct from Sites/CompatibleSheafification and Sites/CoverPreserving.

Name: PreservesLocallyBijective.lean
Description: Functors preserving locally bijective morphisms: a functor between sheaf categories preserves locally bijective morphisms under suitable conditions. Distinct from Sites/LocallyBijective and Sites/PreservesSheafification.

Name: PreservesLimits.lean
Description: Preservation of limits by sheaf functors: the sheafification functor and pushforward functors preserve limits under appropriate conditions. Distinct from Sites/Limits (existence of limits) and Sites/CompatibleSheafification.

Name: LocalProperties.lean
Description: Local properties of morphisms of sheaves: a property of a sheaf morphism is local if it can be checked on a covering of the base. Contains the Local typeclass and basic examples. Distinct from Sites/LocallySurjective (a specific local property) and MorphismProperty/Local.

Name: LocallySurjective.lean
Description: Locally surjective morphisms of sheaves: a morphism f : F → G is locally surjective if every section of G is locally in the image of f. Contains LocallySurjective and the epimorphism characterisation in Sh(C, J). Distinct from Sites/LocallyInjective and Sites/EpiMono.

Name: LocallyInjective.lean
Description: Locally injective morphisms of sheaves: a morphism f : F → G is locally injective if restrictions to covering families are injective on sections. Distinct from Sites/LocallySurjective and Sites/EpiMono.

Name: MayerVietorisSquare.lean
Description: Mayer-Vietoris squares in a site: a commutative square that is a pushout in the site and gives rise to Mayer-Vietoris sequences for sheaf cohomology. Contains the MayerVietorisSquare definition and basic instances.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Over-categories of a site: the site structure on C/X induced from J, and the interaction between sheaves on C/X and sheaves on C via the base-change adjunction. Distinct from Sites/Pullback and Limits/Over.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Morphism properties for sheaf categories derived from the site structure: properties of sheaf morphisms that are local or compatible with the topology. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Local (abstract) and Sites/LocalProperties.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on sheaf categories: the tensor product of sheaves as the sheafification of the objectwise tensor, when the site has finite products. Distinct from Sites/CartesianMonoidal (cartesian case) and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Pretopology.lean
Description: Grothendieck pretopologies: a collection of covering families closed under pullback and composition (but not requiring sieve closure). Used to generate Grothendieck topologies more concretely. Distinct from Sites/Coverage (coverages) and Sites/Grothendieck (the resulting topology).

Name: Subcanonical.lean
Description: Subcanonical topologies: a topology J is subcanonical if all representable presheaves よ(X) are J-sheaves. Contains the Subcanonical typeclass and its relation to effective epimorphic descent. Distinct from Sites/Canonical (the finest subcanonical topology) and Sites/Grothendieck.

Name: Spaces.lean
Description: The site structure on topological spaces: the open-cover topology on the category of open sets of a space, and sheaves on a topological space as sheaves on the associated site. Distinct from Sites/Grothendieck (abstract topology) and Topology library files.

Name: Sieves.lean
Description: Sieves on an object X: a sieve on X is a subfunctor of よ(X), equivalently a set of morphisms into X closed under pre-composition. Contains the Sieve type, operations (intersection, union, pullback), and the maximal sieve. Distinct from Sites/Grothendieck (which uses sieves to define topologies) and Subpresheaf/Sieves.

Name: SubcanonicalOver.lean
Description: Subcanonical topologies on over-categories: if J is subcanonical, the induced topology on C/X is also subcanonical. Distinct from Sites/Subcanonical and Sites/Over.

Name: Whiskering.lean
Description: Whiskering (precomposition) of sheaves: if f : C → D is a functor, precomposition gives f* : Psh(D) → Psh(C), and the conditions for it to restrict to sheaves. Distinct from Sites/Pullback (for morphisms of sites) and Sites/CoverPreserving.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Sheaves on a site with values in Type: the category of type-valued sheaves Sh(C, J, Type), with limits and colimits computed pointwise. Distinct from Sites/Sheaf (general codomain D) and Types/Basic.

Name: Subsheaf.lean
Description: Subsheaves of a sheaf: a subpresheaf of a sheaf that is itself a sheaf. Contains the lattice of subsheaves and its correspondence with subobjects in Sh(C, J). Distinct from Subpresheaf/Basic (presheaf case) and Sites/Sheaf.

Name: RegularEpi.lean
Description: Regular epimorphisms as a coverage: the regular epimorphism coverage on a regular category and conditions under which a regular category becomes a site. Distinct from Sites/EffectiveEpimorphic and RegularCategory/Basic.

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pullback of sheaves along a morphism of sites: the pullback functor f* : Sh(D, K) → Sh(C, J) and its interaction with sheafification. Distinct from Sites/Adjunction (the full f* ⊣ f* adjunction) and Sites/Over.

Name: PseudofunctorSheafOver.lean
Description: `J.pseudofunctorOver A : Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Cᵒᵖ) Cat`: sends each `X : C` to the category of sheaves on `Over X` with values in `A`. Provides a pseudofunctorial packaging of the sheaf-over construction.

Name: Sheaf.lean
Description: The category of sheaves Sh(C, J) on a Grothendieck site: the full subcategory of Psh(C) satisfying the sheaf condition, with the sheafification adjunction a ⊣ i. The primary sheaf theory file in Mathlib. Distinct from Sites/IsSheafFor (individual sieve condition) and Sites/SheafOfTypes.

Name: SheafOfTypes.lean
Description: Sheaves of types (sets): the classical formulation of the sheaf condition for set-valued presheaves and its equivalence with the categorical definition. Distinct from Sites/Sheaf (general codomain) and Sites/IsSheafFor.

Name: Sheafification.lean
Description: The sheafification functor a : Psh(C) → Sh(C, J): the left adjoint to the inclusion of sheaves, constructed via the double plus construction F⁺⁺. Contains the adjunction a ⊣ i and the universal property. Distinct from Sites/Plus (one step) and Sites/CompatibleSheafification.

Name: SheafHom.lean
Description: Internal hom of sheaves: the sheaf [F, G] defined as sheafification of X ↦ Nat(F|_{C/X}, G|_{C/X}). Distinct from Sites/CartesianClosed (which uses internal hom to assert cartesian closure) and Sites/Sheaf.

Name: LocallyFullyFaithful.lean
Description: Locally fully faithful functors between sheaf categories: fully faithful on all hom-sheaves in the local sense. Distinct from Sites/LocallySurjective and Sites/LocallyBijective.

Name: ConstantSheaf.lean
Description: The constant sheaf functor: sends a set S to the sheaf whose sections over X are locally constant S-valued functions. Contains the adjunction between the constant sheaf and global sections. Distinct from Sites/GlobalSections (global sections functor alone) and Sites/Sheaf.

Name: ConcreteSheafification.lean
Description: Concrete sheafification for sites where objects have underlying sets: the explicit sheafification using compatible families directly. Distinct from Sites/Sheafification (abstract double-plus construction) and Sites/Plus.

Name: CompatibleSheafification.lean
Description: Compatibility of sheafification with categorical operations: sheafification preserves colimits and commutes with pullback along morphisms of sites. Distinct from Sites/Sheafification (the construction) and Sites/CompatiblePlus.

Name: Continuous.lean
Description: Continuous morphisms of sites: a functor f : C → D is continuous if it pulls back covering sieves to covering sieves. Contains the morphism-of-sites conditions. Distinct from Sites/CoverPreserving (an alternative formulation) and Sites/Adjunction.

Name: CoverLifting.lean
Description: Cover-lifting (cocontinuous) functors between sites: a functor f : C → D is cocontinuous if covers in C lift to covers in D. Used to define the other direction of morphisms of sites. Distinct from Sites/Continuous (continuous functors going the other way) and Sites/CoverPreserving.

Name: Coverage.lean
Description: Coverages on categories: a coverage assigns to each object X a collection of covering families closed under pullback and refinement. Distinct from Sites/Grothendieck (Grothendieck topologies, which require sieves) and Sites/Precoverage.

Name: CoproductSheafCondition.lean
Description: The sheaf condition via coproducts: an equivalent formulation of the sheaf condition using restriction maps indexed by coproducts rather than equalizers. Distinct from Sites/EqualizerSheafCondition (the standard equalizer form) and Sites/IsSheafFor.

Name: Canonical.lean
Description: The canonical Grothendieck topology on a category: the finest topology for which all representable presheaves よ(X) are sheaves. Contains the Canonical topology definition and the equivalence with the subcanonical condition. Distinct from Sites/Subcanonical (arbitrary subcanonical topologies) and Sites/Grothendieck.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Adjunctions between sheaf categories on different sites: the pushforward/pullback adjunction f* ⊣ f* for a morphism of sites f : (C, J) → (D, K). Distinct from Sites/Continuous (continuity of site morphisms) and Sites/CoverPreserving.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Abelian structure on the category of sheaves of abelian groups on a site: kernels, cokernels, and exactness for sheaf morphisms, with the sheafification of exact sequences. Distinct from Sites/Sheaf (sheaves without abelian structure) and Abelian/Basic.

Name: CartesianClosed.lean
Description: Cartesian closed structure on the sheaf category: if the site has finite limits, Sh(C, J) is cartesian closed with internal hom [F, G]. Distinct from Closed/Cartesian (abstract cartesian closed categories) and Sites/CartesianMonoidal.

Name: CompatiblePlus.lean
Description: Compatibility of the plus construction with limit structure: the plus construction F ↦ F⁺ preserves products and limits in the appropriate sense. Distinct from Sites/Plus (defines the plus construction) and Sites/CompatibleSheafification.

Name: Closed.lean
Description: Closed sieves and the closure operator for Grothendieck topologies: the J-closure j̄(S) of a sieve S, dense sieves, and the associated closure operator on the sieve lattice. Distinct from Sites/Sieves (sieve definitions) and Sites/Subcanonical.

Name: CartesianMonoidal.lean
Description: The cartesian monoidal structure on a sheaf category: the product of sheaves is a sheaf, making Sh(C, J) a cartesian monoidal category. Distinct from Sites/CartesianClosed (adds internal hom) and Monoidal/OfHasFiniteProducts.

Name: CoverPreserving.lean
Description: Cover-preserving functors: a functor f : C → D is cover-preserving if covering families in J map to covering families in K. Contains CoverPreserving and the interaction with the sheaf condition. Distinct from Sites/Continuous (sieve-based formulation) and Sites/CoverLifting.

Name: JointlySurjective.lean
Description: Jointly surjective families in sites: a family {Yᵢ → X} is jointly surjective if the induced map ∐ Yᵢ → X is epimorphic. Used in coverage conditions and the effective epimorphic topology. Distinct from Sites/EffectiveEpimorphic and EffectiveEpi/Basic.

Name: IsSheafFor.lean
Description: The local sheaf condition for a single sieve: IsSheafFor F S asserts that F(X) → Hom(S, F) is a bijection for the sieve S. Contains IsSheafFor and its basic properties. Distinct from Sites/Sheaf (the global condition over all covering sieves) and Sites/EqualizerSheafCondition.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: Grothendieck topologies: a GrothendieckTopology J on C assigns to each X a set of covering sieves (sub-presheaves of よ(X)) satisfying the maximality, stability, and transitivity axioms. The foundational file for all site-theoretic machinery in Mathlib. Distinct from Sites/Coverage (less structured) and Sites/Sieves.

Name: LeftExact.lean
Description: Left exact functors in the context of sites and sheaves: the sheafification functor is left exact (preserves finite limits), and left exact functors between abelian sheaf categories. Distinct from Sites/Abelian (the full abelian structure) and Limits/ExactFunctor.

Name: LocallyBijective.lean
Description: Locally bijective morphisms of sheaves: a morphism is locally bijective if it is both locally injective and locally surjective, equivalent to being an isomorphism in Sh(C, J). Distinct from Sites/LocallyInjective and Sites/LocallySurjective.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Sheaves as a localization of the presheaf category: identifies Sh(C, J) with the localization of Psh(C) at the local equivalences (W-local morphisms), and the sheafification as the localization functor. Distinct from Localization/Construction (general) and Sites/Sheafification.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in sheaf categories: limits in Sh(C, J) are computed pointwise in the presheaf category and are automatically sheaves. Distinct from Sites/Abelian (exact/abelian structure) and Limits/Presheaf.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms in sheaf categories: characterisation of epis in Sh(C, J) as locally surjective morphisms and monos as locally injective morphisms. Distinct from Sites/LocallySurjective and Sites/LocallyInjective.

Name: EffectiveEpimorphic.lean
Description: Effective epimorphic families as a Grothendieck coverage: the coverage where covering families are effective epimorphic, and the resulting topology. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and Sites/Coverage.

Name: CoversTop.lean
Description: Sieves covering the terminal object: a sieve on ⊤ is covering iff it is the maximal sieve (under standard conditions), used in global section computations. Distinct from Sites/IsSheafFor and Sites/GlobalSections.

Name: EqualizerSheafCondition.lean
Description: The equalizer sheaf condition: F is a sheaf for a covering sieve S iff F(X) → equalizer(∏ F(Y) ⇒ ∏ F(Y ×_X Z)) is an isomorphism. Distinct from Sites/IsSheafFor (the categorical sheaf condition) and Sites/CoproductSheafCondition.

Name: GlobalSections.lean
Description: The global sections functor Γ : Sh(C, J) → Set: Γ(F) = F(⊤), its right-exactness properties, and the adjunction with the constant sheaf functor. Distinct from Sites/ConstantSheaf (the left adjoint) and Sites/CoversTop.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite sites and sheaves: sheaf conditions for finite sites (finitely many objects, finite covers), and finiteness preservation by sheafification. Distinct from Sites/Coverage (general coverages) and FinCategory/Basic.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Equivalences of sites and their sheaf categories: a morphism of sites that is an equivalence induces an equivalence on sheaf categories. Contains the SiteEquivalence typeclass. Distinct from Sites/Adjunction and Sites/Continuous.

Name: Sigma
Description: Sigma (dependent coproduct) categories: the coproduct of a family of categories indexed by a type with natural projections.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The sigma-type construction for categories: the category ∐ᵢ Cᵢ whose objects are dependent pairs (i, X) with X : Cᵢ, and morphisms only between objects in the same component. Contains the inclusion functors and universal property. Distinct from Sums/Basic (binary categorical coproduct) and Pi/Basic (categorical product).

Name: ConcreteCategory
Description: Concrete categories: categories equipped with a faithful functor to Set, giving an underlying-set notion for objects.

Name: ReflectsIso.lean
Description: Reflection of isomorphisms by the forgetful functor: if forget(f) is an isomorphism in Type, then f is an isomorphism in C. Contains ReflectsIsomorphisms instances for common concrete categories. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/Forget and ConcreteCategory/EpiMono.

Name: Forget.lean
Description: Properties of the forgetful functor: faithfulness, the interaction with limits and colimits, and the HasForget class. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/Basic (defines the functor) and ConcreteCategory/ReflectsIso.

Name: UnbundledHom.lean
Description: Unbundled hom-sets for concrete categories: morphisms defined directly as structure-preserving functions without the BundledHom packaging. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/BundledHom (bundled version) and ConcreteCategory/Basic.

Name: Representable.lean
Description: Representable concrete categories: concrete categories where forget is representable (Nat(よ(A), F) ≅ F(A) for some corepresenting object A). Used in universal algebra theory. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/Basic and Functor/Hom.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms in concrete categories: if forget reflects mono/epi, a morphism is mono/epi iff its underlying function is injective/surjective. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/Basic and Types/Monomorphisms.

Name: Bundled.lean
Description: Bundled structures for concrete categories: the Bundled α type (a carrier type with a structure instance), used to define Grp, Ring, and Top as categories of bundled structures. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/BundledHom (morphisms) and ConcreteCategory/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Concrete categories and the forgetful functor: the ConcreteCategory typeclass, the forget : C → Type functor, faithfulness, and HasForget. Contains instances for Grp, Ring, and Top. Foundation for all files in this folder.

Name: Elementwise.lean
Description: The elementwise tactic lemmas: equalities in a concrete category that follow from pointwise equality on elements. Contains the @[elementwise] attribute and the elementwise tactic. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/Basic and ConcreteCategory/Representable.

Name: BundledHom.lean
Description: Morphisms between bundled structures: the BundledHom typeclass for a class of morphisms (e.g., group homomorphisms, ring homomorphisms) and the induced category structure. Distinct from ConcreteCategory/Bundled (the objects) and ConcreteCategory/UnbundledHom.

Name: ComposableArrows
Description: Composable sequences of arrows in a category, used to define simplicial objects and chain complexes categorically.

Name: Three.lean
Description: Composable arrows of length 3: sequences X₀ → X₁ → X₂ → X₃ with mk₃, projections, and the map operation. Distinct from ComposableArrows/Two and ComposableArrows/Four.

Name: Two.lean
Description: Composable arrows of length 2: pairs of morphisms X₀ → X₁ → X₂ with mk₂ and the composite morphism. Distinct from ComposableArrows/One and ComposableArrows/Three.

Name: One.lean
Description: Composable arrows of length 1: a single morphism X₀ → X₁, with mk₁ and the equivalence with the arrow category. Distinct from ComposableArrows/Basic and ComposableArrows/Two.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Composable arrows in a category: ComposableArrows C n is the type of sequences X₀ → X₁ → … → Xₙ, realized as functors [n] → C. Contains the definition, access to objects and morphisms, the map operation, and the mk constructors. Foundation for all sibling files.

Name: Four.lean
Description: Composable arrows of length 4: sequences X₀ → X₁ → X₂ → X₃ → X₄ with the mk₄ constructor and projections. Distinct from ComposableArrows/Basic and ComposableArrows/Three.

Name: Comma
Description: Comma categories and arrow categories: the category (F down G) of triples (a, b, f : Fa to Gb), used in adjunction theory.

Name: StructuredArrow
Description: Structured arrow categories (F ↓ d): for a functor F : C → D and object d ∈ D, the category of pairs (c ∈ C, f : F(c) → d). The costructured arrows reverse direction. Used in the proof of the adjoint functor theorem and in the definition of final functors. Distinct from Over (the case F = id and d is an object of C).

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functors on structured arrow categories.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Smallness of structured arrow categories.

Name: Final.lean
Description: Finality conditions for structured arrow categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Structured arrow category: `StructuredArrow T d` — morphisms in `C` from `d` to any object in the range of `T`.

Name: CommaMap.lean
Description: Comma maps for structured arrows.

Name: Presheaf
Description: Presheaf categories as comma categories: the category of presheaves Psh(C) = [C^op, Set] realised as a comma category, with the Yoneda embedding y : C → Psh(C) as a universal construction. Contains the colimit-density of representables (every presheaf is a canonical colimit of representables). Distinct from Sites/SheafCohomology (where covers are imposed).

Name: Colimit.lean
Description: Colimits of presheaves via comma categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Presheaves as comma categories: `PresheafOfTypes = (yoneda ↓ const)ᵒᵖ`.

Name: Over
Description: Slice categories (over-categories) C/X: objects are pairs (A, f : A → X), morphisms are triangles commuting over X. Dual: under-categories X/C. Contains the forgetful functor to C, pullback functors f* : C/Y → C/X, and the dependent sum functor f_! : C/X → C/Y. Used to state the Yoneda lemma in slice form. Distinct from Comma/StructuredArrow (non-strict version).

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pullback in over categories: base change functor `f* : Over Y → Over X`.

Name: StrictInitial.lean
Description: Strict initial object in over categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for over categories: `Over X` — objects over `X`.

Name: OverClass.lean
Description: OverClass typeclass: conditions for a category to be an over-category.

Name: Final.lean
Description: Final and initial functors from comma categories: conditions under which the projection (F ↓ G) → A or → B is final. Distinct from Comma/Basic (definitions) and Limits/Final (finality for general functors).

Name: LocallySmall.lean
Description: Local smallness of comma categories: conditions ensuring (F ↓ G) has small hom-sets. Distinct from Comma/Basic and Localization/LocallySmall.

Name: CardinalArrow.lean
Description: The arrow category with cardinal bounds: the full subcategory of Arrow C on morphisms of cardinality < κ. Used in presentability theory and the small object argument. Distinct from Comma/Arrow (all morphisms) and Presentable files.

Name: Arrow.lean
Description: The arrow category Arrow C: objects are morphisms f : X → Y, morphisms are commutative squares, with source and target functors to C. Contains limits and colimits in the arrow category. Distinct from Comma/Basic (general comma when F = G = Id) and Comma/CardinalArrow.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The comma category (F ↓ G) for functors F : A → C and G : B → C: objects are triples (X, Y, f : F(X) → G(Y)), morphisms are commutative squares, with projection functors. Foundation for Arrow, StructuredArrow, and CostructuredArrow. Distinct from Comma/Arrow and Comma/Final.

Name: CopyDiscardCategory
Description: Categories with explicit copy and discard maps, modeling classical information flow as opposed to linear or quantum information.

Name: Deterministic.lean
Description: Deterministic morphisms in a CD category: morphisms commuting with both copy and delete maps, corresponding to deterministic computations. Contains the Deterministic typeclass and its closure under composition. Distinct from CopyDiscardCategory/Basic and MarkovCategory/Positive.

Name: Widesubcategory.lean
Description: The wide subcategory of deterministic morphisms in a CD category. Distinct from CopyDiscardCategory/Deterministic (the definition) and Monoidal/Widesubcategory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Copy-discard categories (CD categories): symmetric monoidal categories where every object is a cocommutative comonoid via copy (Δ : X → X ⊗ X) and delete (ε : X → 𝟙_), generalising Markov categories. Distinct from CopyDiscardCategory/Cartesian (cartesian case) and MarkovCategory/Basic.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian copy-discard categories: proves that cartesian monoidal categories are CD categories via the diagonal and projection to the terminal. Distinct from CopyDiscardCategory/Basic and Closed/Cartesian.

Name: Distributive
Description: Distributive categories: categories where coproducts distribute over products, modelling distributive logic.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal distributive categories: a monoidal category where tensor distributes over coproducts, with the distributor isomorphism. Distinct from Distributive/Cartesian (cartesian product version) and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian distributive categories: categories with finite products and coproducts where products distribute over coproducts (X × (Y ⊕ Z) ≅ (X × Y) ⊕ (X × Z)). Distinct from Distributive/Monoidal (monoidal version).

Name: Discrete
Description: Discrete categories: categories where the only morphisms are identities, used as index categories for products and coproducts.

Name: SumsProducts.lean
Description: Sums and products in and over discrete categories: discrete colimits are coproducts, discrete limits are products, and the interaction with Pi and Sigma constructions. Distinct from Discrete/Basic and Limits/Pi.

Name: StructuredArrow.lean
Description: Structured arrows over discrete categories: the comma category (X ↓ Discrete F) for a discrete functor. Used in colimits over discrete index categories. Distinct from Discrete/Basic and Comma/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Discrete categories: the category Discrete α on a type α with only identity morphisms. Contains the functor Discrete.functor : (α → C) ≃ (Discrete α ⥤ C), limits and colimits (products and coproducts), and the discreteness instances. Foundation for all sibling files.

Name: Dialectica
Description: The Dialectica construction: a categorical formalization of Godel's Dialectica interpretation, used in proof theory.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on the Dialectica category: the symmetric monoidal structure on Dial(C) with componentwise tensor product, making it a model of linear logic. Distinct from Dialectica/Basic (defines the category) and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The Dialectica construction: the category Dial(C) has objects (U, X) and morphisms (f, F) : (U, X) → (V, Y) where f : U → V and F : U × Y → X, categorifying Gödel's Dialectica interpretation. Distinct from Dialectica/Monoidal (which gives Dial(C) its monoidal structure).

Name: Closed
Description: Closed monoidal categories: monoidal categories with an internal hom functor adjoint to the tensor product.

Name: FunctorCategory
Description: The closed monoidal structure on functor categories: if C is a closed monoidal category, the functor category [J, C] inherits a pointwise closed monoidal structure. The internal hom [F, G](j) = [F(j), G(j)]. Used in the Day convolution construction and in enriched category theory. Distinct from Monoidal/Closed (general internal hom construction).

Name: Groupoid.lean
Description: Closed structure for groupoid functor categories.

Name: Complete.lean
Description: Closure in complete functor categories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Closed functor categories: `[C, D]` is closed monoidal when `D` is.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal closed categories: the MonoidalClosed typeclass with internal hom [A, B] and the adjunction − ⊗ A ⊣ [A, −]. Contains the curry/uncurry isomorphism. Distinct from Closed/Cartesian (cartesian product version) and Closed/Functor.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in a closed monoidal category: a full subcategory I where X ⊗ Y ∈ I whenever Y ∈ I, with the interaction with the internal hom. Distinct from Closed/Monoidal and Monoidal/Subcategory.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: Zero objects in closed categories: conditions under which a closed category has a zero object and the interaction of the internal hom with the zero morphism. Distinct from Closed/Monoidal (general closed structure) and Preadditive/Basic.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Cartesian closed structure on the category Type: the internal hom A → B (the function type), with the CartesianClosed instance. Distinct from Closed/Cartesian (abstract definition) and Types/Basic.

Name: Enrichment.lean
Description: Self-enrichment of a closed category: a cartesian closed or monoidal closed category is naturally enriched over itself via the internal hom. Contains the selfEnrichment construction. Distinct from Closed/Cartesian and Enriched/Basic.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian closed categories: the CartesianClosed typeclass, exponential objects X^Y (internal hom), and the adjunction − × Y ⊣ (−)^Y. Contains instances for Set, Type, and presheaf categories. Distinct from Closed/Monoidal (the more general monoidal closed setting) and LocallyCartesianClosed/Over.

Name: FunctorToTypes.lean
Description: Internal hom for functor-to-types categories: the internal hom for presheaves valued in Type, computed pointwise. Distinct from Closed/Cartesian (abstract) and Limits/FunctorToTypes.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functors between closed categories preserving the closed structure: the ClosedFunctor typeclass asserting compatibility with the internal hom. Distinct from Closed/Monoidal (monoidal closed structure) and Monoidal/Functor.

Name: Adhesive
Description: Adhesive categories: categories where pushouts along monomorphisms are adhesive, used in graph rewriting theory.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subobjects in adhesive categories: uses the van Kampen pushout property to characterise monomorphisms and show the subobject lattice is distributive. Distinct from Adhesive/Basic and Subobject/Lattice.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Adhesive structure on over-categories: if C is adhesive, each slice category C/X is adhesive. Distinct from Adhesive/Basic (base category) and Limits/Over.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Adhesive categories: categories in which pushouts along monomorphisms are van Kampen squares. Contains the Adhesive typeclass, proves pushouts of monos exist and satisfy the van Kampen condition, and gives basic instances (toposes, presheaf categories). Distinct from Adhesive/Subobject (subobject consequences) and Adhesive/Over (slices).

Name: Action
Description: Group and monoid actions on categories: functors from a delooping to a category, with equivariant maps.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in the category of group actions Action G C: constructs them from limits in C and proves the forgetful functor creates them. Distinct from Action/Basic and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on the category of group actions: when C is monoidal, Action G C is monoidal via the diagonal action g • (x ⊗ y) := (g • x) ⊗ (g • y). Distinct from Action/Basic and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Continuous.lean
Description: Continuous group actions on objects in a category: actions of a topological group that are continuous in a suitable categorical sense. Distinct from Action/Basic (algebraic/discrete group actions) and Galois/Action.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Group (or monoid) actions on objects in a category: the Action G C typeclass for a group G acting on objects of C, the category Action G C of G-equivariant objects with equivariant morphisms, and the forgetful functor to C. Distinct from Action/Limits, Action/Monoidal, and Action/Concrete.

Name: Concrete.lean
Description: Concrete realisation of group actions: when C is a concrete category, the underlying set action of G on the carrier of an object. Distinct from Action/Basic and ConcreteCategory/Basic.

Name: Abelian
Description: Abelian categories: preadditive categories with kernels, cokernels, and the exact sequence axioms. The natural setting for homological algebra.

Name: Preradical
Description: Preradicals and radicals in abelian categories: a preradical is a subfunctor r of the identity endofunctor, assigning to each object X a subobject r(X) ⊆ X functorially. Contains the colon construction (r : s), IsIdempotent (idempotence of r), and the notion of a radical (an idempotent preradical r satisfying r(X/r(X)) = 0, the Radical type). Used to define torsion theories and to localize abelian categories. Distinct from SerreClass (an extension-closed approach to the same localization theory).

Name: Radical.lean
Description: Radicals: idempotent preradicals and Serre subcategories.

Name: Colon.lean
Description: Colon of preradicals: `(r : s)` — the preradical defined by the quotient.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Preradicals on abelian categories: sub-endofunctors of the identity; kernel and image.

Name: Projective
Description: Projective objects in abelian categories: objects P where every epimorphism B ↠ A and map P → A lifts to P → B. Projective resolutions … → P₁ → P₀ → X → 0 used to compute left-derived functors. Every free module is projective. Distinct from Injective (dual) and Preadditive/Projective (projective objects in preadditive, not necessarily abelian, categories).

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extending maps from projective objects: the lifting property.

Name: Resolution.lean
Description: Projective resolutions: `ProjectiveResolution X` as a projective complex over `X`.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Ext via projective resolutions: `Ext^n(X, Y)` from a projective resolution of `X`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Projective objects in abelian categories: `IsProjective X` and the projective lifting property.

Name: Dimension.lean
Description: Projective dimension: the minimum length of a projective resolution.

Name: SerreClass
Description: Serre classes and Serre quotient categories: a Serre class is a full subcategory closed under subobjects, quotients, and extensions (0 → X' → X → X'' → 0 with X', X'' ∈ C implies X ∈ C). Defines IsSerreClass, the associated morphism class isoModSerre, and constructs the Serre quotient as an abelian category (SerreClassLocalization.abelian). Used to define relative homology and to localize abelian categories at thick subcategories. Distinct from Preradical (a functor-based approach to the same localization theory).

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Localisation at a Serre class: the quotient abelian category `C/S`.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Morphism property from a Serre class: `S`-isomorphisms as a morphism property.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Serre classes: `IsSerreClass S` — a class of objects closed under subobjects, quotients, and extensions.

Name: Bousfield.lean
Description: Bousfield localisation at a Serre class: the localisation inverting `S`-isomorphisms.

Name: Injective
Description: Injective objects in abelian categories: objects I where every monomorphism A ↪ B and map A → I extends to B → I. Injective resolutions 0 → X → I⁰ → I¹ → … used to compute right-derived functors. Existence in Grothendieck categories (from GrothendieckCategory). Distinct from Projective (the dual lifting property).

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extending maps to injective objects: the lifting property of injective objects.

Name: Resolution.lean
Description: Injective resolutions: `InjectiveResolution X` as a specific injective complex quasi-isomorphic to `X`.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Ext via injective resolutions: `Ext^n(X, Y)` computed from an injective resolution of `Y`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Injective objects in abelian categories: `IsInjective X` and Baer's criterion.

Name: Dimension.lean
Description: Injective dimension: `injectiveDimension X` — the minimum length of an injective resolution.

Name: DiagramLemmas
Description: The classical diagram lemmas for abelian categories: the snake lemma, four lemma, five lemma, nine lemma, and horseshoe lemma. Each is a statement about exactness propagating through commutative diagrams. Distinct from ShortComplex (which proves them for triples) — this folder proves the full lemmas in any abelian category.

Name: KernelCokernelComp.lean
Description: Kernel-cokernel composition lemma: if `f ≫ g = 0` in an abelian category, there is a natural map `cokernel f → kernel g` whose kernel and cokernel are related to the homology.

Name: Four.lean
Description: Four lemma in abelian categories: `four_lemma` for commutative diagrams.

Name: GrothendieckAxioms
Description: The Grothendieck AB axioms for abelian categories: AB3 (arbitrary coproducts exist), AB4 (coproducts are exact), AB5 (filtered colimits are exact), and their duals AB3*–AB5*. AB5 is the key condition for the existence of injective resolutions. Distinct from GrothendieckCategory — this folder defines the axioms, the next builds on them.

Name: Indization.lean
Description: Grothendieck axioms for ind-categories: ind-objects of an AB5 category satisfy AB5.

Name: Sheaf.lean
Description: Grothendieck axioms for sheaf categories: sheaves on a site inherit AB3/AB5 from the target.

Name: Types.lean
Description: Grothendieck axioms for the type category: `Type u` satisfies AB5.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Grothendieck axioms for functor categories: AB5 for `[J, C]` when `C` satisfies AB5.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Grothendieck's axioms AB3–AB6 for abelian categories.

Name: Colim.lean
Description: AB3/AB5 colimit axioms: filtered colimits commute with finite limits in an AB5 category.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connected diagram colimits in Grothendieck axioms: colimits over connected diagrams.

Name: GrothendieckCategory
Description: Grothendieck abelian categories: AB5 categories with a generator. The main theorem is that every Grothendieck category has enough injectives (Grothendieck's theorem). The category of sheaves of abelian groups on a site is a Grothendieck category. Distinct from GrothendieckAxioms (axioms) and Injective (injective objects in any abelian category).

Name: ModuleEmbedding
Description: The Gabriel-Popescu theorem: a Grothendieck abelian category with a generator embeds fully faithfully and exactly into a category of modules R-Mod, realized as a localization of R-Mod at a hereditary torsion theory. Proves GabrielPopescu.full and GabrielPopescu.preservesInjectiveObjects. Provides a concrete representation theorem for Grothendieck categories and is a step toward, though distinct from, the Freyd-Mitchell embedding theorem for small abelian categories. Distinct from SerreClass (the localization theory underlying the construction).

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Opposite of the Gabriel–Popescu embedding.

Name: GabrielPopescu.lean
Description: Gabriel–Popescu theorem: every Grothendieck category embeds into a module category.

Name: HasExt.lean
Description: Ext groups in Grothendieck categories: existence of Ext via injective resolutions.

Name: Monomorphisms.lean
Description: Monomorphisms in Grothendieck categories: filtered colimits of monomorphisms are monomorphisms.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subobjects in Grothendieck categories: the subobject classifier and lattice properties.

Name: EnoughInjectives.lean
Description: Enough injectives in Grothendieck categories: every Grothendieck category has enough injectives.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Grothendieck abelian categories: AB5 + generator; Giraud's theorem and Gabriel–Popescu.

Name: ColimCoyoneda.lean
Description: Coyoneda colimits in Grothendieck categories: every object is a colimit of representables.

Name: Coseparator.lean
Description: Coseparators in Grothendieck categories: the generator/coseparator duality.

Name: Refinements.lean
Description: Refinements of exact sequences in abelian categories: technical subdivision lemmas used to pass from one diagram to a finer one without losing exactness. Distinct from Abelian/Exact (basic exact sequences) and Abelian/CommSq.

Name: RightDerived.lean
Description: Right derived functors in an abelian category: the right derived functors RⁱF of a left-exact functor F, computed via injective resolutions. Contains the δ-functor structure and the long exact sequence. Distinct from Abelian/LeftDerived (projective resolutions) and Abelian/Ext.

Name: Pseudoelements.lean
Description: Pseudoelements in abelian categories: Freyd's construction of generalised elements as equivalence classes of morphisms into X, enabling diagram-chasing proofs in abstract abelian categories. Contains the Pseudoelement typeclass and the pseudoelement versions of exactness and injectivity. Distinct from Abelian/Exact (sequence-level) and Abelian/Basic.

Name: NonPreadditive.lean
Description: Deriving abelian structure without assuming preadditive axioms explicitly: obtains the additive group structure on hom-sets from the existence of finite limits, colimits, and the image factorisation. Distinct from Abelian/Basic (standard axioms starting from Preadditive) and Preadditive/OfBiproducts.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Abelian structure on the opposite category: if A is abelian, A^op is abelian with kernels and cokernels swapped. Distinct from Abelian/Basic (forward direction) and Preadditive/Opposite.

Name: Transfer.lean
Description: Transfer of abelian structure along a category equivalence: if A ≃ B and B is abelian, then A inherits abelian structure coherently. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and Preadditive/Transfer.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: The Yoneda lemma in the abelian setting: Hom(−, X) is a homological functor sending short exact sequences to long exact Hom-sequences, and the natural Ext-connecting homomorphism. Distinct from Abelian/Ext (Yoneda Ext groups) and Limits/Yoneda.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subobjects in abelian categories: the subobject lattice is modular, lattice operations correspond to kernel and image constructions, and every subobject is a kernel. Distinct from Abelian/Monomorphisms (morphism characterisations) and Subobject/Lattice.

Name: ShortExact.lean
Description: Short exact sequences in abelian categories: the ShortExact predicate (0 → A → B → C → 0), the splitting criterion (right-split iff left-split iff B ≅ A ⊕ C), and the five and snake lemmas. Distinct from Abelian/Exact (composable-morphisms exactness, not just short sequences) and Abelian/CommSq.

Name: Subcategory.lean
Description: Abelian subcategories: full subcategories closed under kernels, cokernels, and extensions that inherit the abelian structure. Contains the conditions for a full subcategory to be abelian. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and ObjectProperty/Extensions.

Name: Monomorphisms.lean
Description: Monomorphisms and epimorphisms in abelian categories: mono ↔ kernel is zero, epi ↔ cokernel is zero, the normal form, and stability under pullback/pushout. Distinct from Abelian/Basic (general abelian axioms) and Subobject/MonoOver.

Name: Exact.lean
Description: Exact sequences in abelian categories: the Exact predicate for composable morphisms (image of f equals kernel of g), the five lemma, the snake lemma, and the construction of long exact sequences from short ones. Distinct from Abelian/ShortExact (the 0 → A → B → C → 0 packaging) and Abelian/CommSq.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: The Ext functor in an abelian category: Ext¹(A, B) as the group of equivalence classes of short exact sequences 0 → B → E → A → 0 (Yoneda Ext), with the long exact Ext sequence. Distinct from Abelian/LeftDerived (general derived functors) and Abelian/Yoneda.

Name: EpiWithInjectiveKernel.lean
Description: Epimorphisms whose kernel is injective in an abelian category: the EpiWithInjectiveKernel predicate and its role in splitting short exact sequences and estimating injective dimension. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and Abelian/Exact.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The Abelian typeclass: a preadditive category with all finite limits and colimits in which every monomorphism is a kernel map and every epimorphism is a cokernel map. Proves the canonical isomorphism coim(f) ≅ im(f) and the first isomorphism theorem. Foundation for all abelian category theory in Mathlib.

Name: CommSq.lean
Description: Commutative squares in abelian categories: pushouts as cokernel squares, pullbacks as kernel squares, and the interplay used in the four lemma and snake lemma. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and Abelian/Exact.

Name: Indization.lean
Description: Abelian structure on Ind-categories: Ind(A) is abelian if A is abelian, and the exact inclusion A → Ind(A) preserves kernels and cokernels. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and Preadditive/Indization.

Name: LeftDerived.lean
Description: Left derived functors in an abelian category: the left derived functors LᵢF of a right-exact functor F, computed via projective resolutions. Contains the δ-functor structure and the long exact sequence LᵢF(A) → LᵢF(B) → LᵢF(C) → Lᵢ₋₁F(A) → …. Distinct from Abelian/RightDerived (injective resolutions) and Abelian/Ext.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Images in abelian categories: the canonical image im(f) := ker(coker(f)), the image factorisation A → im(f) → B, and the canonical isomorphism coim(f) ≅ im(f). Distinct from Abelian/Basic (which uses images in its axioms) and Subobject/FactorThru.

Name: FreydMitchell.lean
Description: The Freyd-Mitchell embedding theorem: every small abelian category embeds exactly and fully faithfully into the category of R-modules for some ring R. Contains the statement and the key steps of the proof. Distinct from Abelian/Basic and Abelian/FunctorCategory.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: Abelian structure on functor categories [C, A] when A is abelian: pointwise kernels, cokernels, and exactness for natural transformations, with the Abelian instance. Distinct from Abelian/Basic (single category) and Preadditive/FunctorCategory.

Name: Adjunction
Description: Adjunctions between functors: unit-counit definition, the hom-set bijection, and adjoint functor theorems.

Name: Lifting
Description: Lifting adjunctions along functors: if F ⊣ G and U is monadic, constructing a lifted adjunction between the Eilenberg-Moore categories. The adjoint lifting theorem (Johnstone): under suitable conditions, an adjunction C ⇆ D lifts to an adjunction between categories of algebras. Distinct from the basic Adjunction folder (unit-counit definitions).

Name: Right.lean
Description: Lifting right adjoints: conditions for lifting a right adjoint through a forgetful functor.

Name: Left.lean
Description: Lifting left adjoints: conditions for lifting a functor to a left adjoint of a composed functor.

Name: PartialAdjoint.lean
Description: Partial adjoints: G has a partial left adjoint at X if the comma category (const_X ↓ G) has an initial object. Contains the PartialLeftAdjoint predicate and the passage to a global adjoint when partial adjoints exist everywhere. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic (global adjoint) and Adjunction/Comma.

Name: Quadruple.lean
Description: Quadruple adjunctions (F ⊣ G ⊣ H ⊣ K): four functors forming a consecutive adjoint chain, with the automatic consequences for preservation of limits and colimits. Distinct from Adjunction/Triple (three adjoints) and Adjunction/Basic.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Adjunctions in opposite categories: if F ⊣ G then G^op ⊣ F^op. Contains the op-adjunction construction and the interaction with limits/colimits in the opposite direction. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: Parametrized.lean
Description: Parametrized adjunctions: a family of adjunctions Fₓ ⊣ Gₓ varying functorially in a parameter x, with the parametrized adjunction typeclass and interaction with limits. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic (non-parametrized) and LiftingProperties/ParametrizedAdjunction.

Name: Reflective.lean
Description: Reflective subcategories: when the right adjoint G is fully faithful (equivalently the counit is an iso), the adjunction is a reflection. Contains the Reflective typeclass, the reflector, and the connection to localisation and the sheafification adjunction. Distinct from Adjunction/FullyFaithful (the ff characterisation) and Localization/Predicate.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: Uniqueness of adjoints: any two left adjoints of the same functor are naturally isomorphic, and dually for right adjoints. Contains the canonical comparison isomorphism. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Adjunction/FullyFaithful.

Name: Whiskering.lean
Description: Whiskering of adjunctions: given F ⊣ G and a functor H, produces the whiskered adjunction H ∘ F ⊣ G ∘ H' under appropriate conditions. Distinct from Adjunction/Mates (the full mates correspondence) and Adjunction/CompositionIso.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restricting adjunctions to full subcategories: if F ⊣ G and both functors restrict to full subcategories, the restricted pair is still adjoint. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Adjunction/Reflective.

Name: Triple.lean
Description: Triple adjunctions (F ⊣ G ⊣ H): a chain of three adjoints and automatic consequences such as H ∘ F being self-adjoint. Distinct from Adjunction/Quadruple (four adjoints) and Adjunction/CompositionIso.

Name: Mates.lean
Description: The mates correspondence: given adjunctions F ⊣ G and F' ⊣ G', natural transformations F' ∘ H → K ∘ F correspond bijectively to transformations H ∘ G → G' ∘ K. Contains the mate bijection and its 2-functoriality. Distinct from Adjunction/Whiskering and Adjunction/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for adjunctions: the Adjunction typeclass F ⊣ G with unit η : Id → G ∘ F, counit ε : F ∘ G → Id, and the triangle identities. Contains the adjunction from a hom-set bijection, the adjunction from unit/counit, and composition of adjunctions. Foundation for all adjunction theory.

Name: Comma.lean
Description: Adjunctions and comma categories: characterises F ⊣ G via initial objects in the comma categories (const_X ↓ G), and the adjunction between structured and costructured arrow categories. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Comma/Basic.

Name: Additive.lean
Description: Additive adjunctions between preadditive categories: adjunctions where both F and G are additive, and the induced preadditive structure on natural transformations. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Preadditive/AdditiveFunctor.
