Name: AdjointFunctorTheorems.lean
Description: The adjoint functor theorems: the General Adjoint Functor Theorem (limit-preserving + solution set condition implies a left adjoint exists) and the Special Adjoint Functor Theorem for complete well-powered categories. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Presentable/Adjunction.

Name: CompositionIso.lean
Description: Isomorphisms arising from compositions of adjunctions: the canonical isomorphism (F₂ ∘ F₁) ⊣ (G₁ ∘ G₂) and its naturality. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic (single adjunction) and Adjunction/Mates.

Name: FullyFaithfulLimits.lean
Description: Fully faithful adjunctions and limits: if the right adjoint G is fully faithful, limits in the source can be reflected from limits in the target via G. Distinct from Adjunction/FullyFaithful and Adjunction/Limits.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Adjunctions and limits: right adjoints preserve limits, left adjoints preserve colimits (RAPL/LAPC). Contains the preservation instances and the converse direction. Distinct from Adjunction/AdjointFunctorTheorems (existence of adjoints) and Adjunction/FullyFaithfulLimits.

Name: Evaluation.lean
Description: Adjunctions involving evaluation functors: the adjunction between evaluation at a fixed object and the constant-diagram functor, used in constructing limits and colimits. Distinct from Adjunction/Basic and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: FullyFaithful.lean
Description: Adjunctions where one functor is fully faithful: G is fully faithful iff the counit ε is an isomorphism (making the adjunction reflective), and F is fully faithful iff the unit η is an isomorphism. Distinct from Adjunction/Reflective (consequences of reflectivity) and Adjunction/Basic.

Name: Center
Description: The Drinfeld center of a monoidal category: the braided monoidal category of objects equipped with half-braidings.

Name: NegOnePow.lean
Description: The sign element (−1)^n in the center: the central element arising from the shift functor in a triangulated or graded category, producing the sign changes in triangulated diagrams and the Koszul sign rule. Distinct from Center/Basic and Triangulated/TriangleShift.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: The center of a preadditive monoidal category: the commutative ring structure on Z(C) when C is preadditive monoidal, and the canonical ring map from Z(C) to each End(X). Distinct from Center/Basic (the braided monoidal center) and Center/Linear.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: The center of a localised category: the canonical map Z(C) → Z(C[W⁻¹]) and conditions under which it is an isomorphism. Distinct from Center/Basic and Localization/Predicate.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The center of a monoidal category (Drinfeld center): objects are pairs (X, γ) where γ : X ⊗ − ≅ − ⊗ X is a half-braiding, and the center is a braided monoidal category. Distinct from Center/Preadditive (additive center structure) and Monoidal/Center.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: The R-linear structure on the center of an R-linear monoidal category and the interaction with linear monoidal functors. Distinct from Center/Basic and Center/Preadditive.

Name: Category
Description: Core category theory definitions: the Category typeclass, functors, natural transformations, and the Yoneda lemma.

Name: Cat
Description: The (large) category Cat of small categories: objects are small categories, morphisms are functors. Contains the Cartesian closed structure (internal hom = functor categories), the product of categories, and the inclusion of sets as discrete categories. The 2-categorical enrichment over Cat gives the tricategory structure on bicategories.

Name: Limit.lean
Description: Limits in Cat: limits of diagrams of categories.

Name: Op.lean
Description: Opposite categories in Cat.

Name: Terminal.lean
Description: Terminal category in Cat: `PUnit` as the terminal category.

Name: Colimit.lean
Description: Colimits in Cat: colimits of diagrams of categories.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Adjunctions in Cat: standard adjunctions between functor categories.

Name: AsSmall.lean
Description: Shrinking a large category to a small one: `AsSmall C`.

Name: CartesianClosed.lean
Description: Cat is cartesian closed: `[C, D]` is the internal hom.

Name: Preorder.lean
Description: Categories arising from preorders: the equivalence between preorders and thin categories (at most one morphism between any two objects), with the category-theoretic limits/colimits recovered as meets/joins. Distinct from Category/Basic and Limits/Preorder.

Name: Quiv.lean
Description: The category Quiv of quivers (directed graphs): morphisms are quiver morphisms (vertex and edge maps). Contains the free-category functor Quiv → Cat (left adjoint to the forgetful functor) and the forgetful functor. Distinct from PathCategory/Basic (the free category on a quiver) and Category/Basic.

Name: PartialFun.lean
Description: The category PartialFun of types and partial functions: morphisms are partial functions (functions defined on a subtype). Contains the composition of partial functions and the interaction with the category of types. Distinct from Category/Basic and Types/Basic.

Name: Pointed.lean
Description: The category Pointed of pointed types: objects are pairs (α, pt : α), morphisms are basepoint-preserving functions. Contains the smash product (monoidal structure) and the interaction with algebraic structures. Distinct from Category/Bipointed (two basepoints) and Category/Basic.

Name: TwoP.lean
Description: The two-point (walking morphism) category: the category with objects {0, 1} and exactly one non-identity morphism 0 → 1. Used as the indexing category for the arrow category and walking-morphism arguments. Distinct from Category/Init (empty/unit categories) and Category/Basic.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: The ULift construction for categories: lifts a category in universe u to universe max u v via ULift on objects, enabling universe-polymorphic constructions. Distinct from Category/Basic and the general Lean universe infrastructure.

Name: ReflQuiv.lean
Description: The category ReflQuiv of reflexive quivers: quivers with a distinguished identity edge at each vertex, and quiver morphisms preserving identity edges. Distinct from Category/Quiv (non-reflexive quivers) and Category/Basic.

Name: RelCat.lean
Description: The category RelCat of types and binary relations: morphisms are binary relations, composition is relational composition, and the identity is the equality relation. Distinct from Category/PartialFun (partial functions) and Category/Basic.

Name: Pairwise.lean
Description: The pairwise category of a cover: given a family {Uᵢ}, the category whose objects are single Uᵢ and pairwise intersections Uᵢ ∩ Uⱼ, with restriction morphisms. Used in the Mayer-Vietoris and Čech formulations of sheaves. Distinct from Category/Basic and Sites/Sieves.

Name: Cat.lean
Description: The (large) category Cat of small categories: objects are small categories, morphisms are functors. Contains the CartesianClosed structure on Cat (the internal hom [C, D] = functor category) and the 2-category structure. Distinct from Category/Basic (the Category typeclass) and Category/Grpd.

Name: Factorisation.lean
Description: Factorisation systems on a category as a categorical structure: orthogonal factorisation systems (L, R) with the unique diagonal fill property, packaged as a type with basic operations. Distinct from MorphismProperty/Factorization (morphism-property formulation) and Category/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for categories in Mathlib: the Category typeclass with Hom, comp, id and the associativity and unit axioms. Contains SmallCategory, LargeCategory, and the basic universe-polymorphic infrastructure. Foundation for all of category theory; every other file in Mathlib's category theory library depends on this.

Name: Bipointed.lean
Description: The category Bipointed of bipointed types: objects are types equipped with two distinguished points (pt₁, pt₂), morphisms are functions preserving both points. Distinct from Category/Pointed (one point) and Category/Basic.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initial and terminal categories: PEmpty as the initial (empty) category and PUnit as the terminal (one-object) category, with their universal properties in Cat. Distinct from Category/Basic and WithTerminal/Basic.

Name: KleisliCat.lean
Description: The Kleisli category construction for the category of types: KleisliCat T for a monad T on Type, as a concrete category with the Kleisli morphisms. Distinct from Monad/Kleisli (the abstract Kleisli category for an arbitrary monad on C) and Category/Basic.

Name: GaloisConnection.lean
Description: Categories arising from Galois connections: the adjunction between poset categories induced by a Galois connection, and the resulting pair of adjoint functors. Distinct from Category/Preorder and Galois/Basic.

Name: Grpd.lean
Description: The category Grpd of groupoids: objects are groupoids, morphisms are functors between them. Contains the interaction with Group, the fundamental groupoid construction, and the classifying space. Distinct from Category/Cat (all categories) and Groupoid/Basic.

Name: Bicategory
Description: Bicategories (weak 2-categories): 0-cells, 1-cells, 2-cells with composition, coherence, and the bicategory of spans.

Name: Monad
Description: Monads in a bicategory: a 1-cell t : A → A with 2-cells η : 1_A ⇒ t (unit) and μ : t∘t ⇒ t (multiplication) satisfying associativity and unit axioms. The Eilenberg-Moore object (algebra for the monad) when it exists. Generalizes ordinary monads (1-cells in Cat). Distinct from CategoryTheory/Monad (monads on a fixed category).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monads in bicategories: a monad in `B` on object `a` is a monoid in `B(a, a)`.

Name: Modification
Description: Modifications between pseudonatural transformations: 2-cells in the functor bicategory, consisting of a family of 2-cells in the target bicategory satisfying a naturality square. Modifications are the morphisms between pseudonatural transformations. Distinct from Bicategory/NaturalTransformation (one level up) and Bicategory/FunctorBicategory (the whole structure).

Name: Pseudo.lean
Description: Modifications between pseudonatural transformations.

Name: Oplax.lean
Description: Modifications between oplax natural transformations.

Name: Lax.lean
Description: Modifications between lax natural transformations.

Name: Strict
Description: Strict 2-categories: bicategories where composition and identity coherences are equalities rather than isomorphisms. Every bicategory is biequivalent to a strict 2-category (strictification theorem). Contains the coherence theorem for bicategories and the strictification functor. Distinct from the general Bicategory folder (which allows weak composition).

Name: Pseudofunctor.lean
Description: Pseudofunctors out of strict bicategories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Strict bicategories: bicategories where associators and unitors are identities.

Name: NaturalTransformation
Description: Pseudonatural transformations between pseudofunctors F, G : B → C: a family of 1-cells α_A : F(A) → G(A) with invertible 2-cell components satisfying a pseudonaturality square. The 1-cells in the functor bicategory. Distinct from Modification (2-cells between them) and ordinary NatTrans (the strict special case in Cat).

Name: Pseudo.lean
Description: Pseudonatural transformations between pseudofunctors.

Name: Oplax.lean
Description: Oplax natural transformations.

Name: Lax.lean
Description: Lax natural transformations between lax functors.

Name: Functor
Description: Pseudofunctors (homomorphisms of bicategories): maps F between bicategories preserving composition and identities up to coherent isomorphism. Contains lax functors (non-invertible coherence cells), oplax functors, and strict 2-functors. The Grothendieck construction produces a fibration from a pseudofunctor. Distinct from Bicategory/NaturalTransformation (morphisms between pseudofunctors).

Name: Cat
Description: The (strict) 2-category Cat of categories as a bicategory: objects are categories, 1-cells are functors, 2-cells are natural transformations. The strictification showing every bicategory is biequivalent to a strict 2-category maps into Cat. Used as the primary example of a bicategory and as the target of the Yoneda embedding for bicategories.

Name: ObjectProperty.lean
Description: Object properties in Cat-valued functors.

Name: Pseudofunctor.lean
Description: Pseudofunctors: functors between bicategories with invertible 2-cells.

Name: Prelax.lean
Description: Prelax functors: the data of a functor between bicategories before naturality.

Name: StrictPseudofunctor.lean
Description: Strict pseudofunctors: pseudofunctors where the comparison 2-cells are identities.

Name: StrictlyUnitary.lean
Description: Strictly unitary pseudofunctors: pseudofunctors that strictly preserve identity 1-cells.

Name: Lax.lean
Description: Lax functors between bicategories: laxly preserving composition up to a 2-cell.

Name: Cat.lean
Description: Functors between Cat as a bicategory: `Cat.instBicategory`.

Name: Oplax.lean
Description: Oplax functors between bicategories: the dual notion to lax functors.

Name: LocallyDiscrete.lean
Description: Functors from locally discrete bicategories: functors from a category viewed as a bicategory.

Name: Adjunction
Description: Adjunctions in a bicategory: a pair of 1-cells f : A → B, u : B → A with 2-cells η : 1_A ⇒ u∘f and ε : f∘u ⇒ 1_B satisfying the triangle identities. Generalizes ordinary adjunctions (in Cat) and dualizable objects in monoidal categories. Distinct from CategoryTheory/Adjunction (adjunctions between categories, a special case).

Name: Cat.lean
Description: Adjunctions in the bicategory Cat: ordinary adjunctions as bicategorical adjunctions.

Name: Mate.lean
Description: Mates in bicategories: the mate correspondence for adjunctions in a bicategory.

Name: Adj.lean
Description: Adjunctions in bicategories: `Adj f g` — unit/counit data satisfying the triangle identities in a bicategory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic bicategorical adjunctions: definitions and basic properties.

Name: Kan
Description: Kan extensions and Kan lifts in a bicategory: the left Kan extension of a 1-cell f : A → C along g : A → B is a 1-cell lan : B → C with a universal 2-cell η : f ⇒ lan ∘ g. Contains HasLeftKanExtension, lan, IsKan, IsAbsKan (absolute Kan extensions invariant under composition), and HasLeftKanLift. The API covers composition, whiskering, and relations to adjunctions; right extensions and lifts are obtained by formal duality. Distinct from CategoryTheory/Limits/Kan (the 1-categorical version).

Name: IsKan.lean
Description: Definition of Kan extensions in a bicategory.

Name: HasKan.lean
Description: Existence of Kan extensions in a bicategory.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Kan extensions in a bicategory as adjunctions.

Name: FunctorBicategory
Description: The bicategory of pseudofunctors between two fixed bicategories: objects are pseudofunctors, 1-cells are pseudonatural transformations, and 2-cells are modifications. The internal hom in the tricategory of bicategories. Distinct from Bicategory/Functor (individual pseudofunctors) and Bicategory/Modification (the 2-cells specifically).

Name: Pseudo.lean
Description: Pseudofunctor bicategory: the bicategory of pseudofunctors.

Name: Oplax.lean
Description: Oplax functor bicategory.

Name: Lax.lean
Description: Lax functor bicategory: the bicategory of lax functors between two bicategories.

Name: LocallyGroupoid.lean
Description: Locally groupoid bicategories: bicategories where every 2-morphism is invertible. Contains the LocallyGroupoid typeclass and its interaction with pseudofunctors. Distinct from Bicategory/LocallyDiscrete and Groupoid/Basic.

Name: LocallyDiscrete.lean
Description: Locally discrete bicategories: bicategories where each hom-category Hom(X, Y) is discrete (only identity 2-morphisms). Contains the LocallyDiscrete typeclass and the embedding of 2-categories into locally discrete bicategories. Distinct from Bicategory/LocallyGroupoid and Bicategory/Basic.

Name: InducedBicategory.lean
Description: Induced bicategory structure: pulling back a bicategory structure along a function on objects to produce a new bicategory. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and Bicategory/Extension.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: Opposite and co-opposite bicategories: B^op (reverse 1-morphisms), B^co (reverse 2-morphisms), and B^coop (reverse both). Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: The Yoneda lemma for bicategories: the pseudo-Yoneda embedding B → [B^op, Cat] and the bicategorical Yoneda equivalence Nat(よ(X), F) ≃ F(X). Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and Limits/Yoneda.

Name: SingleObj.lean
Description: The single-object bicategory delooping of a monoidal category: a monoidal category C gives a bicategory B(C) with one object *, Hom(*,*) = C, and tensor as composition. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Product.lean
Description: The product bicategory B × C: objects are pairs, 1-morphisms and 2-morphisms are componentwise, with the induced bicategorical structure. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and Products/Basic.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: The Grothendieck construction for bicategories: given a pseudofunctor F : B → Cat, the total bicategory ∫F with the fibration B. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Grothendieck (the 1-categorical version) and Bicategory/Basic.

Name: Coherence.lean
Description: The coherence theorem for bicategories: any two coherence 2-morphisms (built from associators and unitors) between the same 1-morphism composites are equal. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic (which states the axioms) and Monoidal/CoherenceLemmas.

Name: CatEnriched.lean
Description: Bicategories as Cat-enriched categories: the identification of bicategories with categories enriched over Cat, with the Cat-valued hom-objects Hom_B(X, Y). Distinct from Enriched/Basic (general V-enriched categories) and Bicategory/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for bicategories: the Bicategory typeclass with objects, 1-morphisms, 2-morphisms (2-cells), horizontal and vertical composition, the associator and unitors for 1-morphisms, and the interchange law. Foundation for all bicategory theory in Mathlib. Distinct from Bicategory/Coherence (coherence theorem) and Monoidal/Category (monoidal categories as one-object bicategories).

Name: End.lean
Description: The monoidal category of endomorphisms at an object X in a bicategory B: End_B(X) with 1-morphisms X → X and vertical composition as tensor product. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and Monoidal/End.

Name: Free.lean
Description: The free bicategory on a 2-graph (quiver with 2-cells): the bicategory generated by objects, generating 1-morphisms, and generating 2-morphisms with no relations, and its universal property. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic and PathCategory/Basic.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extensions and lifts in bicategories: `LeftExtension` (resp. `RightExtension`) defined as objects of `StructuredArrow` (resp. `CostructuredArrow`). `LeftExtension.extension` is an alias for `Comma.right`. Gives a uniform treatment of Kan extensions and lifts in the bicategorical setting.

Name: EqToHom.lean
Description: The eqToHom 2-morphism in a bicategory: the canonical invertible 2-cell from a proof that two 1-morphisms are equal. Technical infrastructure for equality coercions in bicategory computations. Distinct from Bicategory/Basic.

Name: GradedObject
Description: Graded objects in a category: objects indexed by a grading monoid, with graded morphisms and tensor products.

Name: Single.lean
Description: Single-degree graded objects: the inclusion functor C → GradedObject I C concentrating an object in degree i ∈ I, and its role as the monoidal unit and the interaction with the monoidal structure. Distinct from GradedObject/Monoidal and GradedObject/Unitor.

Name: Trifunctor.lean
Description: Trifunctors on graded objects: three-variable functors compatible with the grading (shifting degrees additively), used for the triple tensor product and its associativity. Distinct from GradedObject/Bifunctor (two variables) and Localization/Trifunctor.

Name: Unitor.lean
Description: The left and right unitors for the graded monoidal structure: the natural isomorphisms 𝟙 ⊗ X ≅ X and X ⊗ 𝟙 ≅ X for graded objects. Distinct from GradedObject/Associator and GradedObject/Monoidal.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on graded objects: GradedObject I C (functors I → C for an additive monoid I) is monoidal with the convolution tensor product ⊗ defined by (X ⊗ Y)(n) = ⊕_{i+j=n} X(i) ⊗ Y(j). Distinct from GradedObject/Bifunctor (the bifunctor alone) and GradedObject/Braiding.

Name: Associator.lean
Description: The associator for the graded tensor product: the natural isomorphism (X ⊗ Y) ⊗ Z ≅ X ⊗ (Y ⊗ Z) for graded objects, with explicit degree-shift bookkeeping. Distinct from GradedObject/Monoidal (the full monoidal structure) and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Bifunctor.lean
Description: Bifunctors on graded objects: functors of two graded arguments that shift degrees additively, used for the convolution tensor product. Distinct from GradedObject/Monoidal (assembles these into a monoidal structure) and Products/Bifunctor.

Name: Braiding.lean
Description: Braiding on graded objects: the symmetric or braided monoidal structure with the braiding isomorphism β : X ⊗ Y ≅ Y ⊗ X and Koszul sign conventions (signs depending on degrees). Distinct from GradedObject/Monoidal (non-braided monoidal structure) and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Generator
Description: Generators and cogenerators of a category: objects G such that Hom(G, -) is faithful, generalizing the notion of a spanning set.

Name: Sheaf.lean
Description: Generators of sheaf categories: conditions on a family of sheaves to be a generating family for Sh(C, J), and transfer of generators from presheaves via sheafification. Distinct from Generator/Presheaf and Sites/Sheaf.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Generators of presheaf categories: the representable presheaves {よ(X) | X ∈ C} form a generating family for [C^op, Set]. Distinct from Generator/Basic and Limits/Presheaf.

Name: Type.lean
Description: Generators in the category Type: the one-element type PUnit is a generator of Type (Hom(PUnit, −) is faithful). Distinct from Generator/Basic and Types/Basic.

Name: StrongGenerator.lean
Description: Strong generators: a set of objects S is a strong generator if for every non-zero morphism f there exists s ∈ S and g : s → source such that f ∘ g ≠ 0. Contains the StrongGenerator typeclass and relationship to dense and detecting families. Distinct from Generator/Basic and Presentable/StrongGenerator.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Generators in preadditive categories: characterisation via the faithfulness of the additive Hom functor and the generation of hom-groups by the image of the generator. Distinct from Generator/Basic and Generator/Abelian.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Generators of a category: an object G is a generator if Hom(G, −) is faithful, and a separating family {Gᵢ} if Hom(Gᵢ, −) jointly faithful. Contains the IsSeparating and IsDetecting predicates. Foundation for all sibling Generator files.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Generators in abelian categories: characterisation of generators via the surjectivity of Hom(G, −) and the relationship to the existence of enough projectives. Distinct from Generator/Basic and Abelian/Basic.

Name: Indization.lean
Description: Generators of Ind-categories: if a family generates C, its ind-extension generates Ind(C). Contains the transfer theorem and the interaction with filtered colimits. Distinct from Generator/Basic and Preadditive/Indization.

Name: HomologicalComplex.lean
Description: Generators of `HomologicalComplex C c`: for a complex shape `c` with no loop, if `C` has a separator then `HomologicalComplex C c` has a separating family; and a separator when suitable coproducts exist.

Name: Galois
Description: Galois theory in categorical form: Galois categories and fiber functors, recovering the etale fundamental group.

Name: GaloisObjects.lean
Description: Galois objects in a PreGaloisCategory: connected objects X for which Aut(X) acts transitively on F(X). Contains the GaloisObject typeclass and the Galois closure construction. Distinct from Galois/Basic and Galois/Decomposition.

Name: Full.lean
Description: Fullness of the fiber functor: Hom_C(X, Y) embeds injectively and surjectively into equivariant maps of fiber sets. Distinct from Galois/EssSurj and Galois/Basic.

Name: IsFundamentalgroup.lean
Description: The fundamental group of a Galois category: the profinite group π₁(C, F) = lim Aut(X) over Galois objects X, and the IsFundamentalGroup typeclass. Distinct from Galois/Basic and Galois/Prorepresentability.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: The profinite topology on the fundamental group: the limit topology on π₁(C, F) making the action on fibers continuous. Distinct from Galois/IsFundamentalgroup and Galois/Action.

Name: Prorepresentability.lean
Description: Prorepresentability of the fiber functor: F is prorepresented by the profinite fundamental group as a pro-object in C. Distinct from Galois/IsFundamentalgroup and Galois/Equivalence.

Name: Examples.lean
Description: Concrete examples of Galois categories: covering spaces (π₁ = fundamental group of the base), étale algebras over a field (π₁ = absolute Galois group), and finite G-sets for a discrete group G. Distinct from Galois/Basic (abstract axioms) and Galois/Equivalence.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The basic theory of Galois categories: the PreGaloisCategory typeclass for a category equipped with a fiber functor F : C → FinType satisfying Galois axioms (finite limits, finite coproducts, connected components, Galois closure). Foundation for all sibling Galois files.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Group actions in Galois category theory: the action of the fundamental group π₁ on the fiber functor F, and functoriality of the action with respect to morphisms. Distinct from Galois/Basic and Action/Basic.

Name: Decomposition.lean
Description: Decomposition of Galois objects: every connected Galois object X decomposes the fiber F(X) as a torsor under Aut(X), and general Galois objects split as coproducts of connected ones. Distinct from Galois/GaloisObjects and Galois/Basic.

Name: EssSurj.lean
Description: Essential surjectivity of the fiber functor: F : C → FinType is essentially surjective onto the category of finite π₁-sets. Distinct from Galois/Full (fullness) and Galois/Equivalence.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: The main equivalence of Galois category theory: a PreGaloisCategory C with fundamental group π₁ is equivalent to the category of finite continuous π₁-sets. Contains the statement and core arguments assembling Essential Surjectivity and Fullness. Distinct from Galois/EssSurj and Galois/Full.

Name: Groupoid
Description: Groupoids: categories where every morphism is an isomorphism. Fundamental groupoids and groupoid actions.

Name: Grpd
Description: The (large) category Grpd of groupoids: objects are groupoids, morphisms are functors. Contains the forgetful functor to Cat, the free groupoid on a quiver, and the fundamental groupoid functor Top → Grpd. The 2-categorical structure with natural isomorphisms as 2-cells.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The category Grpd of groupoids: objects are groupoids, morphisms are functors.

Name: FreeGroupoidOfCategory.lean
Description: The free groupoid on a category: the groupoid obtained by formally inverting all morphisms of a small category, with its universal property. Distinct from Groupoid/FreeGroupoid (starts from a quiver) and Localization/Construction.

Name: Subgroupoid.lean
Description: Subgroupoids of a groupoid: the Subgroupoid typeclass (a subcategory closed under inverses), the complete lattice of subgroupoids, and normal subgroupoids as kernels of groupoid functors. Distinct from Groupoid/Basic and Subobject/Basic.

Name: VertexGroup.lean
Description: The vertex group of a groupoid at an object x: the group Aut_G(x) of automorphisms at x, the group structure, and the interaction with groupoid functors. Distinct from Groupoid/Basic and Center/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for groupoids: the Groupoid typeclass (a category in which every morphism is invertible), the inv map, the groupoid axioms (right and left cancellation), and basic instances. Distinct from Category/Grpd (the category of groupoids) and Groupoid/Discrete.

Name: Discrete.lean
Description: Discrete groupoids: a groupoid with only identity morphisms, equivalently a set viewed as a groupoid. Contains the equivalence between discrete groupoids and types. Distinct from Groupoid/Basic and Discrete/Basic.

Name: FreeGroupoid.lean
Description: The free groupoid on a quiver: the groupoid generated by a directed graph (quiver) with no relations, universal among groupoids receiving a quiver morphism from the graph. Distinct from Groupoid/FreeGroupoidOfCategory (which starts from a category) and PathCategory/Basic.

Name: Join
Description: The join of categories: the category C star D with objects from both and new morphisms from every C-object to every D-object.

Name: Pseudofunctor.lean
Description: Pseudofunctor structure on the join: the join is pseudofunctorial in both arguments, giving a pseudofunctor Cat × Cat → Cat. Distinct from Join/Basic and Bicategory/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: The relationship between the join and categorical coproduct: the join C ⋆ D is built from the disjoint union C ⊕ D by adjoining the extra morphisms from C to D. Distinct from Join/Basic and Sums/Basic.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: The opposite of a join: (C ⋆ D)^op ≅ D^op ⋆ C^op. Contains the isomorphism and its interaction with limits. Distinct from Join/Basic and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The join C ⋆ D of two categories: objects are C.obj ⊕ D.obj, with Hom-sets inherited from C and D for same-summand objects, one canonical morphism from every C-object to every D-object, and no morphisms the other way. Foundation for the simplex category and simplicial arguments. Distinct from Join/Sum, Join/Final, and Join/Opposites.

Name: Final.lean
Description: Finality results for the join construction: the inclusion C → C ⋆ D is cofinal under suitable conditions, enabling colimit computations. Distinct from Join/Basic and Limits/Final.

Name: Idempotents
Description: Idempotent completion (Karoubi envelope): the universal category where all idempotents split, used in K-theory and stable categories.

Name: Karoubi.lean
Description: The Karoubi envelope (idempotent completion) of a category: Karoubi(C) has objects (X, e) with e an idempotent on X, morphisms compatible with the idempotents, and the embedding C → Karoubi(C). Contains the universal property (Karoubi(C) is the free idempotent-complete category on C). Distinct from Idempotents/Basic (individual idempotents) and Preadditive/Mat.

Name: HomologicalComplex.lean
Description: Idempotent completeness for categories of homological complexes: split idempotents in chain complexes and the Karoubi envelope of the bounded homotopy category. Distinct from Idempotents/Karoubi and Abelian/LeftDerived.

Name: SimplicialObject.lean
Description: `SimplicialObject C` and `CosimplicialObject C` are idempotent complete when `C` is idempotent complete. Instances derived from `CategoryTheory.Idempotents.FunctorCategories`.

Name: KaroubiKaroubi.lean
Description: The Karoubi envelope of the Karoubi envelope: the canonical equivalence Karoubi(Karoubi(C)) ≃ Karoubi(C), proving the Karoubi envelope is idempotent-complete. Distinct from Idempotents/Karoubi and Idempotents/FunctorExtension.

Name: Biproducts.lean
Description: Biproducts in the Karoubi envelope: Karoubi(C) has biproducts when C is preadditive, and the embedding C → Karoubi(C) preserves biproducts. Distinct from Idempotents/Karoubi (defines the Karoubi envelope) and Preadditive/Biproducts.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Idempotent morphisms and split idempotents: an idempotent e : X → X satisfies e ∘ e = e; it splits if e = i ∘ r with r ∘ i = id_Y. Contains IsIdempotent and IsSplitIdempotent predicates and the idempotent-complete (Karoubian) typeclass. Foundation for all Idempotents files.

Name: FunctorExtension.lean
Description: Extension of functors to the Karoubi envelope: any functor F : C → D with D idempotent-complete extends uniquely to F̄ : Karoubi(C) → D. Contains the extension and its universal property. Distinct from Idempotents/Karoubi and Idempotents/FunctorCategories.

Name: FunctorCategories.lean
Description: Idempotent completeness of functor categories and the Karoubi envelope of [C, D]: pointwise splitting of idempotents and the equivalence Karoubi([C, D]) ≃ [C, Karoubi(D)]. Distinct from Idempotents/FunctorExtension (extension of individual functors) and Idempotents/Karoubi.

Name: GuitartExact
Description: Guitart exact squares: a 2-categorical notion of exactness for squares of functors, generalizing both pullbacks and exact sequences.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Guitart exact squares in over-categories: the interaction of Guitart exactness with the slice category construction and base change. Distinct from GuitartExact/Basic and Limits/Over.

Name: VerticalComposition.lean
Description: Vertical composition of Guitart exact squares: two Guitart exact squares sharing a common edge compose vertically to a Guitart exact square. Distinct from GuitartExact/Basic and GuitartExact/KanExtension.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Guitart exactness in the opposite category: the opposite of a Guitart exact square is Guitart exact. Distinct from GuitartExact/Basic and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Guitart exact squares: a commutative square of functors (a 2-cell in the double category of profunctors) W : (u, f) → (g, v) is Guitart exact if the induced natural transformation of profunctors is an isomorphism. Contains the GuitartExact typeclass and basic instances including pullback squares. Foundation for all sibling files.

Name: KanExtension.lean
Description: Guitart exact squares and Kan extensions: a square is Guitart exact iff the right Kan extension of f along u equals the composition v ∘ g on the corresponding profunctors. Distinct from GuitartExact/Basic and the general Kan extension theory in Functor files.

Name: Functor
Description: Functors between categories: the definition, composition, full, faithful, and essentially surjective functors, and functor categories.

Name: ReflectsIso
Description: Functors that reflect isomorphisms: F : C → D reflects isos if F(f) being an iso implies f is an iso. Conservative functors. The condition under which a natural transformation that is objectwise an iso is itself a natural isomorphism. Used to transfer isomorphism results along forgetful functors. Distinct from faithful functors (which reflect equality, not invertibility).

Name: Jointly.lean
Description: Jointly reflecting isomorphisms: a family of functors that together reflect isos.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limit-reflecting and iso-reflecting conditions.

Name: Exact.lean
Description: Exact functors reflecting isomorphisms.

Name: Balanced.lean
Description: Balanced categories and reflecting isomorphisms.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic API for functors reflecting isomorphisms.

Name: KanExtension
Description: Kan extensions: the left Kan extension Lan_K F and right Kan extension Ran_K F of a functor F : C → D along K : C → C'. Universal properties: Lan_K F is left adjoint to precomposition by K. The pointwise formula (Lan_K F)(c') = colim_{K(c) → c'} F(c). Every adjunction arises as a Kan extension. Distinct from Bicategory/Kan (same concept in bicategories).

Name: DenseAt.lean
Description: Density at an object: conditions for a functor to be dense at a specific object.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise Kan extensions: computation of Kan extensions as (co)limits of comma categories.

Name: Preserves.lean
Description: Kan extensions preserved by functors.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Kan extension adjunction: left and right Kan extensions as adjoints to restriction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Kan extensions: `RanExt F G` and `LanExt F G` — the right/left Kan extension of `G` along `F`.

Name: Dense.lean
Description: Dense functors and Kan extensions: `F` is dense iff every presheaf is a Kan extension along `F`.

Name: Derived
Description: Derived functors: the total derived functor RF : D(A) → D(B) of a left-exact functor F : A → B between abelian categories, computed using injective resolutions. Contains the universal property of derived functors, the cohomological δ-functor, and the long exact sequence of derived functors. Distinct from Algebra/Homology (chain complex machinery) — this is the categorical construction.

Name: PointwiseRightDerived.lean
Description: Pointwise right derived functors.

Name: RightDerived.lean
Description: Right derived functors: `RDeriv F` using fibrant replacement.

Name: PointwiseLeftDerived.lean
Description: Pointwise left derived functors: `LDeriv F X` computed at each object.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Derived functors and adjunctions: derived functors of adjoint pairs.

Name: LeftDerived.lean
Description: Left derived functors via model categories: `LDeriv F` using cofibrant replacement.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: The Hom functor and Yoneda embedding: Hom(X, −) : C → Set, the contravariant Hom(−, X), and the Yoneda embedding よ : C → [C^op, Set]. Contains the Yoneda lemma Nat(よ(X), F) ≅ F(X) and the fully faithful embedding. Distinct from Functor/Basic and Linear/Yoneda.

Name: OfSequence.lean
Description: Functors defined by a sequence: packages a sequence of objects X₀, X₁, X₂, … with transition maps Xₙ → Xₙ₊₁ as a functor ℕ → C (where ℕ is the category with morphisms n → m for n ≤ m). Distinct from Functor/Basic and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: FunctorHom.lean
Description: The functor-hom objects in enriched functor categories: the V-enriched natural transformation object [F, G] as the equaliser of the product of V-hom-objects. Distinct from Functor/Hom (the set-valued Yoneda hom functor) and Enriched/Basic.

Name: Functorial.lean
Description: The Functorial typeclass: a typeclass for maps between structures that are automatically packaged as functors, avoiding repeated verification of functoriality. Distinct from Functor/Basic and ConcreteCategory/Forget.

Name: TwoSquare.lean
Description: Two-squares (horizontal natural transformations in the double category of categories and functors): a 2-cell filling a square of functors, used in Guitart-exact squares and Kan extension theory. Distinct from GuitartExact/Basic and Functor/Basic.

Name: TypeValuedFlat.lean
Description: Flat functors to Type: specialisation of Functor/Flat to the codomain D = Type, with the explicit filtered-colimit and finite-limit-preservation characterisation. Distinct from Functor/Flat (general codomain) and Types/Basic.

Name: RegularEpi.lean
Description: Regular epimorphisms of functors (natural transformations): characterises when a natural transformation is a regular epi in the functor category [C, D]. Distinct from Functor/EpiMono (general epi/mono) and EffectiveEpi/Basic.

Name: Trifunctor.lean
Description: Trifunctors: functors of three arguments C × D × E → F with the associated currying and interaction with limits in each variable separately. Distinct from Functor/Currying (two-variable currying) and Localization/Trifunctor.

Name: Const.lean
Description: The constant functor const X : C → D sending every object to X ∈ D and every morphism to id_X. Contains const as a functor C → (C ⥤ D), the right adjoint to colim, and its interaction with limits and cones. Distinct from Functor/Basic and Limits/Cones.

Name: Currying.lean
Description: Currying for functor categories: the equivalence [C × D, E] ≃ [C, [D, E]] and the curry/uncurry operations. Contains the natural isomorphism and the interaction with limits. Distinct from Functor/CurryingThree (three-variable) and Products/Bifunctor.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for functors: the Functor typeclass with obj (object map), map (morphism map), map_id, and map_comp (functoriality). Contains the identity functor 𝟭, functor composition ⋙, and the op functor. Foundation for all functor theory; every other Functor/* file depends on this.

Name: Category.lean
Description: The functor category [C, D] (also written C ⥤ D): objects are functors, morphisms are natural transformations, vertical composition, and the category instance. Contains basic limits and colimits in functor categories. Distinct from Functor/Basic (individual functors) and NaturalTransformation files.

Name: Flat.lean
Description: Flat functors F : C → D: a functor is flat if its category of elements el(F) is filtered, equivalently if it preserves finite limits. Contains the Flat typeclass, the connection to left Kan extensions, and the flat functor theorem for sheaves. Distinct from Functor/TypeValuedFlat (Type-valued case) and Filtered/Flat.

Name: FullyFaithful.lean
Description: Fully faithful functors: Full (surjective on hom-sets), Faithful (injective on hom-sets), and FullyFaithful (both). Contains the natural bijection characterisation, preimage of isomorphisms, and the equivalence between FullyFaithful and embedding. Distinct from Adjunction/FullyFaithful (adjunction consequences) and Functor/Basic.

Name: CurryingThree.lean
Description: Three-variable currying for functor categories: the equivalence [C × D × E, F] ≃ [C, [D, [E, F]]] and iterated curry/uncurry operations. Distinct from Functor/Currying (two-variable) and Monoidal/Multifunctor.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms of functors: conditions on a natural transformation η : F → G to be a monomorphism or epimorphism in the functor category. Distinct from Functor/FullyFaithful and Types/Monomorphisms.

Name: Enriched
Description: Enriched categories: categories whose hom-sets are objects in a monoidal category V, generalizing Ab-enriched and metric-enriched cases.

Name: Ordinary
Description: The ordinary category underlying an enriched category: the category obtained by applying the functor V(I, -) : V → Set to hom-objects, where I is the monoidal unit. Also the fully faithful embedding of ordinary categories into V-categories via the discrete enrichment. Distinct from Enriched/Limits (enriched limit theory).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordinary categories as self-enriched: `Cat.Enriched C` from a category `C`.

Name: Limits
Description: Limits and colimits in enriched categories: the enriched version of limits, where the universal property is stated using the enriched hom rather than Set-valued hom-sets. Contains conical limits (ordinary limits that happen to be enriched) and the enriched Yoneda lemma for limits. Distinct from Enriched/Ordinary (general enriched structure without limits).

Name: HasConicalPullbacks.lean
Description: Conical pullbacks in enriched categories.

Name: HasConicalTerminal.lean
Description: Conical terminal objects in enriched categories.

Name: HasConicalLimits.lean
Description: Conical limits in enriched categories: limits whose cone is conical (unenriched base).

Name: HasConicalProducts.lean
Description: Conical products in enriched categories.

Name: HomCongr.lean
Description: Congruence for V-enriched hom-objects: natural isomorphisms between V-valued hom-objects arising from equalities or isomorphisms of objects. Technical infrastructure for coherence in enriched computations. Distinct from Enriched/Basic and Functor/Hom.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite of a V-enriched category: if C is V-enriched then C^op is V-enriched with the same V-hom-objects but with composition reversed via the braiding. Distinct from Enriched/Basic and Monoidal/Opposite.

Name: FunctorCategory.lean
Description: The V-enriched functor category [C, D]_V: for V-enriched categories C and D, the functor category enriched over V with V-natural transformations. Distinct from Enriched/Basic (individual enriched categories) and Functor/Category.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Enriched category theory: the EnrichedCategory V typeclass for a category enriched over a monoidal category V, with V-valued hom-objects EnrichedHom X Y, V-composition morphisms, and unit morphisms. Foundation for V-linear categories and dg-categories. Distinct from Enriched/FunctorCategory and Linear/Basic.

Name: EnrichedCat.lean
Description: The category of V-enriched categories: objects are V-enriched small categories, morphisms are V-enriched functors. Contains the category EnrichedCat V and basic functoriality. Distinct from Enriched/Basic (individual enriched categories) and Category/Cat.

Name: Endofunctor
Description: Endofunctors and their algebras and coalgebras: initial algebras (least fixed points) and terminal coalgebras (greatest fixed points).

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebras over an endofunctor F : C → C (without requiring a monad structure): an F-algebra is a pair (X, a : F(X) → X), the category of F-algebras with structure-preserving morphisms, the initial F-algebra (if it exists), and the Lambek lemma (the structure map of an initial algebra is an isomorphism). Distinct from Monad/Algebra (monad algebras, which additionally require the monad unit and multiplication laws) and Monad/Basic.

Name: EffectiveEpi
Description: Effective epimorphisms and effective epimorphic families: covers in the categorical sense, used to define Grothendieck topologies.

Name: Extensive.lean
Description: Effective epimorphisms in extensive categories: in an extensive category (finite coproducts are disjoint and pullback-stable), characterises effective epimorphic families via surjectivity on components. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and Adhesive/Basic.

Name: Preserves.lean
Description: Preservation of effective epimorphisms by functors: conditions under which a functor sends effective epis to effective epis (e.g., left adjoints and functors preserving finite limits). Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and Functor/Basic.

Name: RegularEpi.lean
Description: The relationship between effective and regular epimorphisms: in a category with kernel pairs, regular epimorphisms are effective, and conversely in regular categories. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and RegularCategory/Basic.

Name: Enough.lean
Description: Categories with enough effective epimorphisms: every object admits an effective epi from a member of a given generating class. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and Generator/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Effective epimorphisms and effective epimorphic families: an epimorphism f : X → Y is effective if it is the coequalizer of its kernel pair; a family {Xᵢ → Y} is effectively epimorphic if it is a colimit covering. Contains the EffectiveEpi and EffectiveEpimorphicFamily typeclasses. Foundation for all sibling files.

Name: Comp.lean
Description: Composition of effective epimorphisms: if f : X → Y and g : Y → Z are effective epis, so is g ∘ f; and the 2-of-3 property. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and MorphismProperty/Composition.

Name: Coproduct.lean
Description: Effective epimorphic families from coproducts: conditions under which a family {Xᵢ → Y} is effectively epimorphic, and the interaction with coproduct injections and the Grothendieck topology generated. Distinct from EffectiveEpi/Basic and Sites/EffectiveEpimorphic.

Name: Equivalence
Description: Equivalences of categories: pairs of inverse-up-to-isomorphism functors, and the characterization via fully faithful plus essentially surjective.

Name: Symmetry.lean
Description: The symmetry of category equivalences: if C ≃ D then D ≃ C, with an explicit construction of the inverse equivalence from a given one. Contains the symm operation and the proof it is a two-sided quasi-inverse up to natural isomorphism. Distinct from the main Equivalence definitions (in core Functor files) and Localization/Equivalence.

Name: FinCategory
Description: Finite categories: categories with finitely many objects and morphisms, and limits/colimits over finite diagrams.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Finite categories: the FinCategory typeclass for categories with finitely many objects (Fintype on objects) and finitely many morphisms between any two objects (Fintype on hom-sets). Contains basic instances (discrete finite categories, walking morphisms, the category [n]) and the interaction with Fintype. Distinct from FinCategory/AsType.

Name: AsType.lean
Description: Realising a finite category as a category on a finite type: FinCatAsType C, a FinCategory equivalent to C whose objects are a Fin n for some n. Distinct from FinCategory/Basic (abstract finite category typeclass) and Discrete/Basic.

Name: Filtered
Description: Filtered categories and filtered colimits: the key property making colimits commute with finite limits in Set.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: Filtered structure on Grothendieck constructions: the Grothendieck construction ∫F is filtered when the base category and each fiber category are filtered. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and FiberedCategory/Grothendieck.

Name: Flat.lean
Description: Flat functors and filtered categories: the characterisation of flat functors via their category of elements el(F) being filtered, and the connection to preservation of finite limits. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and Functor/Flat.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Small filtered categories and their properties: size conditions on filtered categories and the existence of small cofinal subcategories in locally small filtered categories. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and Limits/FinallySmall.

Name: OfColimitCommutesFiniteLimit.lean
Description: The converse direction: a category J is filtered iff colimits over J commute with finite limits in Set. This criterion for filteredness complements the direct axioms. Distinct from Filtered/Basic (direct axioms) and Limits/FilteredColimitCommutesFiniteLimit.

Name: FinallySmall.lean
Description: Finally small filtered categories: a filtered category is finally small if there is a final functor from a small filtered category. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and Limits/FinallySmall.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connectedness of filtered categories: filtered categories are connected (IsConnected), and the interaction between filteredness and connectedness. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and Limits/Connected.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Filtered and cofiltered categories: the IsFiltered typeclass (every finite diagram has a compatible cocone) and IsCofiltered (every finite diagram has a cone), with basic instances (ℕ with ≤, opposite of a filtered category). Foundation for all Filtered files. Distinct from Limits/Filtered (which uses these in limit theory) and Presentable/IsCardinalFiltered.

Name: Final.lean
Description: Final functors from filtered categories: conditions under which a functor from a filtered category is final, enabling colimit substitution. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and Limits/Final.

Name: CostructuredArrow.lean
Description: Filtered structure on costructured arrow categories: the costructured arrow category (F ↓ X) is filtered when F is corepresentable or under suitable conditions. Used in the theory of pro-objects. Distinct from Filtered/Basic and Comma/Basic.

Name: FiberedCategory
Description: Fibered categories and the Grothendieck construction: associating a category over B to a pseudofunctor B^op to Cat.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: The Grothendieck construction: given a pseudofunctor F : S^op → Cat, constructs the total category ∫F with the fibration p : ∫F → S. Contains the Grothendieck construction and the 2-equivalence between fibrations over S and pseudofunctors S^op → Cat. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Fibered (abstract fibration) and Bicategory/Grothendieck.

Name: Fibered.lean
Description: The fibered category (Grothendieck fibration) condition: p : C → S is a (split) fibration if every morphism φ : S' → S in the base and every object X over S has a chosen cartesian lift. Contains the IsFibered typeclass and basic consequences. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Cartesian (individual morphism condition) and FiberedCategory/Grothendieck.

Name: HomLift.lean
Description: The HomLift predicate: a morphism f in C lies over φ : S → T in the base (i.e., p(f) = φ). Contains HomLift and the factorisation of morphisms in a based category through their base component. Distinct from FiberedCategory/BasedCategory (the overall structure) and FiberedCategory/Cartesian.

Name: HasFibers.lean
Description: The HasFibers typeclass: a fibered category equipped with chosen fiber categories and chosen cartesian lifts (a cleavage). Contains the cleavage structure, the transition functors φ* : Fiber(T) → Fiber(S) for each φ : S → T, and their composition. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Fiber (individual fiber) and FiberedCategory/Fibered.

Name: Cartesian.lean
Description: Cartesian morphisms in a based category: a morphism f in C over φ : S' → S in the base is cartesian if it satisfies the universal lifting property (any lift of φ ∘ ψ factors uniquely through f). Contains the IsCartesian typeclass and basic consequences. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Cocartesian (the dual) and FiberedCategory/Fibered.

Name: BasedCategory.lean
Description: Based categories: a category C equipped with a functor p : C → S to a base category S. Contains the BasedCategory typeclass and the notion of a morphism in C lying over a given morphism in S. The foundation for fibered category theory in Mathlib. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Fibered (the fibration condition) and FiberedCategory/HomLift.

Name: Fiber.lean
Description: The fiber category over a base object S: the strict subcategory of C consisting of objects over S and morphisms over id_S. Contains the Fiber typeclass and its category structure. Distinct from FiberedCategory/BasedCategory (the overall based structure) and FiberedCategory/HasFibers.

Name: Cocartesian.lean
Description: Cocartesian (opcartesian) morphisms in a based category: the dual of cartesian morphisms, where the universal lifting property runs in the opposite direction. Contains the IsCocartesian typeclass and the dual results. Distinct from FiberedCategory/Cartesian and FiberedCategory/Fibered.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: The opposite category C^op: reversing all morphisms, dualizing constructions, and the contravariant Yoneda embedding.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Noetherian objects in a category: objects where every ascending chain of subobjects stabilizes.

Name: NatTrans.lean
Description: Natural transformations between functors: components, naturality squares, vertical composition, and the functor category.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient categories: quotienting a category by a congruence relation on hom-sets, with the universal functor.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: The terminal category PUnit: the category with one object and one morphism, the unit for categorical products.

Name: PEmpty.lean
Description: The initial category PEmpty: the empty category with no objects, the initial object in Cat.

Name: NatIso.lean
Description: Natural isomorphisms: natural transformations where every component is an isomorphism, forming the 2-morphisms of Cat.

Name: IsConnected.lean
Description: Connected categories: categories where any two objects are linked by a zigzag of morphisms, used in colimit theory.

Name: InducedCategory.lean
Description: Induced categories: given a function f : C to D, the category on C with hom-sets inherited from D via f.

Name: HomCongr.lean
Description: Congruence on hom-sets: rewriting in hom-sets using isomorphisms on either side via conjugation.

Name: LocallyDirected.lean
Description: Locally directed categories: categories where each connected component of the slice is filtered.

Name: IsomorphismClasses.lean
Description: Isomorphism classes of objects: the skeleton of a category and the equivalence relation of being isomorphic.

Name: Iso.lean
Description: Isomorphisms in a category: the Iso type with forward and backward maps satisfying both composites are identity.

Name: RepresentedBy.lean
Description: Representable functors: functors F : C^op to Set with F isomorphic to Hom(-, X) for some representing object X.

Name: UnivLE.lean
Description: Universe level inequalities in category theory: managing universe polymorphism for small and large categories.

Name: Thin.lean
Description: Thin categories (preorder categories): categories where there is at most one morphism between any two objects.

Name: Subterminal.lean
Description: Subterminal objects: objects X with X x X isomorphic to X, equivalent to being a subobject of the terminal object.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: The Yoneda lemma: a natural bijection Nat(Hom(-, X), F) isomorphic to F(X), and the fully faithful Yoneda embedding.

Name: Widesubcategory.lean
Description: Wide subcategories: subcategories containing all objects of the ambient category but only some morphisms.

Name: Whiskering.lean
Description: Whiskering of natural transformations: pre- and post-composing a natural transformation with a functor.

Name: Square.lean
Description: Commutative squares in a category: the data of four objects and four morphisms making a commutative diagram.

Name: Simple.lean
Description: Simple objects in a category: objects with no proper subobjects, the building blocks for Jordan-Holder theory.

Name: ShrinkYoneda.lean
Description: Universe shrinking for the Yoneda embedding: making the Yoneda embedding land in a smaller universe.

Name: Retract.lean
Description: Retracts and retractions: a morphism r : Y to X with a section s : X to Y such that r composed with s equals id.

Name: SmallRepresentatives.lean
Description: Small representatives for isomorphism classes: choosing a set-sized skeleton of a locally small category.

Name: Skeletal.lean
Description: Skeletal categories: categories where every isomorphism is an identity, and the skeleton construction.

Name: SingleObj.lean
Description: The single-object category associated to a monoid: the delooping BM with one object and hom-monoid M.

Name: Groupoid.lean
Description: Groupoids as categories: all-isomorphism categories, the fundamental groupoid, and groupoid actions.

Name: Core.lean
Description: The core of a category: the wide subcategory consisting of all isomorphisms, forming a groupoid.

Name: ConnectedComponents.lean
Description: Connected components of a category: the quotient by the equivalence relation of being connected by a zigzag.

Name: Conj.lean
Description: Conjugation of morphisms by isomorphisms: rewriting f as the inverse of g composed with f composed with g.

Name: DinatTrans.lean
Description: Dinatural transformations: transformations between functors satisfying a hexagon identity, used for ends and coends.

Name: DifferentialObject.lean
Description: Differential objects: objects equipped with a differential d satisfying d squared = 0, used in dg-categories.

Name: Countable.lean
Description: Countable categories: categories with countably many objects and morphisms, and their limits and colimits.

Name: CommSq.lean
Description: Commutative squares in a category: the CommSq structure and its use in pullback and pushout arguments.

Name: Balanced.lean
Description: Balanced categories: categories where every morphism that is both mono and epi is an isomorphism.

Name: Adhesive.lean
Description: Adhesive categories: categories where pushouts along monomorphisms are van Kampen squares.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Actions of monoids and groups on categories: functors from the delooping BM to Cat.

Name: CofilteredSystem.lean
Description: Cofiltered systems: inverse systems indexed by a cofiltered category and their inverse limits.

Name: CodiscreteCategory.lean
Description: Codiscrete categories: categories where there is exactly one morphism between every pair of objects.

Name: CatCommSq.lean
Description: Commutative squares of categories and functors: the 2-categorical notion of a commutative square in Cat.

Name: Elements.lean
Description: The category of elements of a functor F : C to Set: pairs (c, x) with x in F(c), the Grothendieck construction for Set-valued functors.

Name: FintypeCat.lean
Description: The category FintypeCat of finite types: its limits, colimits, and the fully faithful embedding into Type.

Name: ExtremalEpi.lean
Description: Extremal epimorphisms: epimorphisms that do not factor through any proper monomorphism.

Name: Extensive.lean
Description: Extensive categories: categories where coproducts are disjoint and pullback-stable, satisfying the extensivity axiom.

Name: Grothendieck.lean
Description: The Grothendieck construction: building a category from a functor C to Cat, generalizing the category of elements.

Name: GradedObject.lean
Description: Graded objects in a category: formal coproducts of objects indexed by a grading type.

Name: GlueData.lean
Description: Glue data in a category: a system of objects and isomorphisms on overlaps satisfying cocycle conditions.

Name: EssentiallySmall.lean
Description: Essentially small categories: categories equivalent to a small category, with universe-independent results.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms: the categorical generalization of surjections and injections, with basic properties.

Name: Endomorphism.lean
Description: Endomorphisms: the monoid End(X) = Hom(X, X) and its relation to automorphisms and group objects.

Name: Elementwise.lean
Description: The elementwise tactic: a proof technique for natural transformation equalities by checking on all objects.

Name: EssentialImage.lean
Description: The essential image of a functor: the full subcategory of objects isomorphic to something in the image.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Equivalences of categories: the Equivalence structure and constructing equivalences from fully faithful essentially surjective functors.

Name: EqToHom.lean
Description: The eqToHom map: converting a proof of equality of objects into an isomorphism, and its coherence properties.

Name: Condensed
Description: Formalizes the theory of condensed sets and related condensed objects, providing a convenient framework for topological algebra. Used to integrate algebraic topology with functional analysis, especially for studying operations on locally compact abelian groups and liquid vector spaces. Relied on by functional analysis, algebraic topology, number theory, and geometric group theory.

Name: Light
Description: Light condensed sets: a set-size variant of condensed sets using light profinite spaces, providing a more constructive foundation.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Light condensed modules over a condensed ring: the category of light condensed `R`-modules and its abelian structure. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on light condensed abelian groups: the tensor product `⊗` making LightCondAb a symmetric monoidal category. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: InternallyProjective.lean
Description: `internallyProjective_iff_tensor_condition`: a light condensed `R`-module `P` is internally projective iff for every epimorphism `e : A → B`, every `S : LightProfinite`, and every `g : P ⊗ R[S] → B`, there exists a surjection `π : S' → S` and a lift `g' : P ⊗ R[S'] → A` making the diagram commute.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: `HasCountableLimits (LightCondMod R)`: the category of light condensed `R`-modules has countable limits. Provides basic limit instances for light condensed sets and modules.

Name: TopCatAdjunction.lean
Description: Adjunction between light condensed sets and topological spaces: the realisation–nerve adjunction `Top ⇌ LightCondSet` connecting light condensed mathematics to topology. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: TopComparison.lean
Description: Comparison between topological spaces and light condensed sets: conditions under which the comparison functor `Top → LightCondSet` is fully faithful or an equivalence (for nice topological spaces). Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Sequence.lean
Description: The free light condensed `R`-module `R[ℕ∪∞]` is internally projective. (`R` must be in `Type` since `ℕ∪∞` currently only exists in `LightProfinite.{0}`. TODO: generalize.)

Name: Small.lean
Description: Equivalence of light condensed objects with sheaves on a small site: for `LightCondensed` and related categories, provides the equivalence arising from the essentially-small nature of `LightProfinite`.

Name: CartesianClosed.lean
Description: Deprecated module (since 2026-04-08). Cartesian closed structure on light condensed sets has been moved elsewhere.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: `lightProfiniteToLightCondSet.PreservesEpimorphisms`: the yoneda sheaf functor from `LightProfinite` to `LightCondSet` preserves effective epimorphisms (i.e., continuous surjections map to epimorphisms of light condensed sets).

Name: AB.lean
Description: The category of light condensed `R`-modules over a ring satisfies the countable version of Grothendieck's AB4* axiom. Relies on `ModuleCat.AB` and `GrothendieckAxioms.Sheaf`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Light condensed sets: `LightCondSet` — sheaves on the site of countable profinite sets (light profinite sets) with the coherent topology. The light variant of condensed mathematics. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Functors.lean
Description: `lightProfiniteToLightCondSet : LightProfinite → LightCondSet`: the yoneda sheaf functor embedding test objects (light profinite sets) into light condensed sets. The primary functor connecting the two categories.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: `HasSheafify (coherentTopology LightProfinite.{u}) (Type u)` and similar instances for concrete categories. Not automatic because `LightProfinite.{u}` is a large category; obtained via the essentially-small equivalence from `CategoryTheory.Sites.Equivalence`.

Name: Epi.lean
Description: Epimorphisms in light condensed sets and abelian groups: conditions for a morphism in LightCondSet or LightCondAb to be epimorphic. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Explicit.lean
Description: `LightCondensed.ofSheafLightProfinite`: a finite-product-preserving presheaf on `LightProfinite` satisfying `EqualizerCondition` (F(B) is the equalizer of F(X) ⇉ F(X ×_B X) for each effective epi X → B) gives a light condensed set. Variants for concrete categories with limit-reflecting forgetful functors.

Name: Discrete
Description: Discrete condensed sets: the fully faithful embedding of Set into condensed sets, sending a set to the corresponding discrete sheaf.

Name: LocallyConstant.lean
Description: Locally constant sheaves as discrete condensed sets: the equivalence between locally constant sheaves on the pro-étale site and discrete condensed sets. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Discrete condensed modules: condensed modules over a ring that arise from discrete module structures. Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Colimit.lean
Description: A condensed (resp. light condensed) set `X` is discrete iff `X(S) ≅ colimᵢ X(Sᵢ)` for every profinite set `S = limᵢSᵢ`. Provides the API needed to prove this characterization.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Discrete condensed sets: condensed sets associated to ordinary sets/types (the discrete topology). The functor from sets to condensed sets. Active area of Mathlib; exact API requires verification.

Name: Characterization.lean
Description: Characterisation of discrete condensed objects: conditions under which a condensed set/abelian group is discrete (equivalent to a sheaf constant on profinite sets). Active area; exact content requires verification.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Condensed modules over a ring: sheaves of modules on the category of profinite spaces, with tensor product.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits and colimits of condensed sets: the category of condensed sets is complete and cocomplete.

Name: Functors.lean
Description: Functors between condensed categories: the six operations and their adjoints for condensed abelian groups.

Name: TopComparison.lean
Description: Comparison between condensed sets and topological spaces: the functor from Top to condensed sets and its properties.

Name: TopCatAdjunction.lean
Description: The adjunction between TopCat and condensed sets: the left adjoint to the underlying topological space functor.

Name: Solid.lean
Description: Solid abelian groups: condensed abelian groups satisfying an exactness condition making them amenable to homological algebra.

Name: Explicit.lean
Description: Explicit descriptions of condensed sets: computing sections over profinite spaces via ultrafilter limits.

Name: CartesianClosed.lean
Description: The cartesian closed structure of condensed sets: internal hom and its adjunction with the tensor product.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic definitions of condensed sets: sheaves on the category of profinite spaces with the coherent topology.

Name: AB.lean
Description: The AB axioms for condensed abelian groups: AB3, AB4, AB5, and AB6 properties and their consequences.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Equivalences of categories of condensed objects: comparing condensed sets with pyknotic sets and similar frameworks.

Name: Epi.lean
Description: Epimorphisms of condensed sets: characterization as surjective on underlying sets for each profinite space.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epimorphisms in condensed categories: surjective families and their role in the condensed topology.

Name: Computability
Description: Deals with models of computation, Turing machines, primitive recursion, undecidability, and complexity classes. Used to determine which problems can be solved algorithmically and to classify their inherent difficulty. Relied on by logic, theoretical computer science, formal verification, and constructive mathematics.

Name: TuringMachine
Description: Turing machines: formal definition, the halting problem, and reductions between computational problems.

Name: StackTuringMachine.lean
Description: Stack-based Turing machines: a Turing machine model using two stacks in place of a tape. Used as an intermediate model in the equivalence chain between Turing machines and partial recursive functions. Distinct from TuringMachine/PostTuringMachine and TuringMachine/Tape.

Name: Tape.lean
Description: Turing machine tapes: `TuringMachine.Tape Γ` — a bi-infinite tape over alphabet `Γ`, implemented as two lists (left and right of head) with a head symbol. Operations: `move`, `write`, `read`. Distinct from TuringMachine/Config.

Name: ToPartrec.lean
Description: Turing machines compute partial recursive functions: every partial recursive function is computed by a Turing machine and every Turing-computable function is partial recursive. The Church–Turing thesis formalisation. Distinct from TuringMachine/Computable and Primrec/Basic.

Name: Computable.lean
Description: Computable functions via Turing machines: `TuringMachine.Computable f` — `f` is computable iff some Turing machine halts on every input and outputs `f(input)`. Connection to partial recursive functions. Distinct from TuringMachine/ToPartrec and Primrec/Basic.

Name: Config.lean
Description: Turing machine configurations: `TuringMachine.Cfg` — the state type `(state, tape)` representing a snapshot of a Turing machine computation. Step function `TuringMachine.step`. Distinct from TuringMachine/Tape.

Name: PostTuringMachine.lean
Description: Post–Turing machines: a variant of the Turing machine model using a two-stack representation (Post's formulation). Equivalence with the standard multi-tape model. Distinct from TuringMachine/Config and TuringMachine/StackTuringMachine.

Name: Primrec
Description: Primitive recursive functions: the base functions and closure operations, and their relation to Turing computability.

Name: List.lean
Description: Primitive recursion on lists: `Primrec` instances for list operations (`List.length`, `List.append`, `List.map`, etc.) via encoding of lists as natural numbers. Distinct from Primrec/Basic and TuringMachine/ToPartrec.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Primitive recursive functions: `Primrec f` — the class of functions built from zero, successor, projections, composition, and primitive recursion. Core API: `Primrec.comp`, `Primrec.prec`, and instances for standard functions. Distinct from Primrec/List and TuringMachine/Computable.

Name: AkraBazzi
Description: The Akra-Bazzi theorem: a generalization of the Master theorem for solving divide-and-conquer recurrences with variable recursion ratios.

Name: SumTransform.lean
Description: Sum transform in the Akra–Bazzi proof: the auxiliary function `∑ᵢ aᵢ T(⌊n/bᵢ⌋)` and the integral estimate used to replace discrete sums by their continuous analogues. Distinct from AkraBazzi/AkraBazzi and AkraBazzi/GrowsPolynomially.

Name: GrowsPolynomially.lean
Description: Polynomial growth condition for the Akra–Bazzi method: `GrowsPolynomially f` — a function `f` grows polynomially if `f(c * n) = Θ(f(n))` for all `c > 0`. This is the required condition on the toll function `g` in the Akra–Bazzi theorem. Distinct from AkraBazzi/AkraBazzi.

Name: AkraBazzi.lean
Description: Akra–Bazzi method: the master theorem for divide-and-conquer recurrences of the form `T(n) = ∑ aᵢ T(n/bᵢ) + g(n)`. Computes the asymptotic complexity `T(n) = Θ(nᵖ(1 + ∫₁ⁿ g(u)/uᵖ⁺¹ du))` where `p` solves `∑ aᵢ/bᵢᵖ = 1`. Distinct from AkraBazzi/GrowsPolynomially (growth conditions) and AkraBazzi/SumTransform.

Name: Reduce.lean
Description: Computable reductions between decision problems: many-one reductions, Turing reductions, and their degree structure.

Name: RegularExpressions.lean
Description: Regular expressions: syntax, semantics (the language a regex matches), and the Kleene star.

Name: StateTransition.lean
Description: State transition systems: abstract machines with states and transition functions, the basis for automata theory.

Name: Primrec.lean
Description: Primitive recursive functions on N: the base functions (zero, succ, proj) and their closure under composition and primitive recursion.

Name: RE.lean
Description: Recursively enumerable sets: sets whose membership can be semi-decided by a Turing machine, and their relationship to computable functions.

Name: RecursiveIn.lean
Description: Relative computability: functions computable with an oracle, Turing reducibility, and the arithmetic hierarchy.

Name: TMToPartrec.lean
Description: Compilation from Turing machines to partial recursive functions: the proof that Turing-computable implies partial-recursive.

Name: TuringDegree.lean
Description: Turing degrees: equivalence classes of sets under mutual Turing reducibility, with the degree 0 and 0-prime.

Name: TuringMachine.lean
Description: Turing machines: the formal model with tapes, states, and transition functions, and basic computability results.

Name: Tape.lean
Description: Turing machine tapes: the data structure for two-directional infinite tapes with read/write/move operations.

Name: TMComputable.lean
Description: Efficiently computable functions: functions computed by Turing machines with polynomial time bounds.

Name: TMConfig.lean
Description: Turing machine configurations: the state-plus-tape snapshots representing a moment in a computation.

Name: PostTuringMachine.lean
Description: Post-Turing machines and Post's correspondence problem: an alternative machine model and an undecidability result.

Name: Encoding.lean
Description: Encodings of data types as natural numbers: canonical encodings for pairs, lists, and trees used in recursion theory.

Name: EpsilonNFA.lean
Description: Epsilon-NFAs: nondeterministic finite automata with epsilon-transitions, and the epsilon-closure construction.

Name: Halting.lean
Description: The halting problem: the undecidability of whether a Turing machine halts on a given input.

Name: Ackermann.lean
Description: The Ackermann function: a total computable function that is not primitive recursive, with the proof of its rapid growth.

Name: ContextFreeGrammar.lean
Description: Context-free grammars: production rules, derivations, the language of a grammar, and the Chomsky hierarchy.

Name: DFA.lean
Description: Deterministic finite automata: states, alphabet, transition function, accepting states, and the recognized language.

Name: Partrec.lean
Description: Partial recursive functions: the closure of primitive recursive functions under minimization (mu-operator).

Name: PartrecBasis.lean
Description: Basis theorems for partial recursive functions: generating all partial recursive functions from a small basis set.

Name: PartrecCode.lean
Description: Codes for partial recursive functions: a universal computable encoding, enabling the universal machine and the s-m-n theorem.

Name: Language.lean
Description: Formal languages: subsets of the free monoid with union, concatenation, Kleene star, and the Chomsky hierarchy.

Name: MyhillNerode.lean
Description: The Myhill-Nerode theorem: a language is regular iff its Nerode equivalence classes are finite, with the minimal DFA.

Name: NFA.lean
Description: Nondeterministic finite automata: the NFA model, the subset construction for determinization, and equivalence with DFAs.

Name: AlgebraicGeometry
Description: Formalizes schemes, sheaves, and morphisms between algebraic varieties from a modern Grothendieck-style viewpoint. Used to investigate solutions of polynomial equations, geometric properties of rings, and moduli problems. Relied on by number theory (e.g., arithmetic geometry), complex algebraic geometry, and mathematical physics.

Name: Modules
Description: Quasi-coherent and coherent sheaves of modules on schemes: the affine case via ring modules and the general gluing construction.

Name: Tilde.lean
Description: The tilde construction: `M̃` — the quasi-coherent sheaf on `Spec R` associated to an `R`-module `M`, with `M̃(D(f)) = Mf`. The equivalence `QCoh(Spec R) ≃ R-Mod`. Distinct from Modules/Sheaf.

Name: Sheaf.lean
Description: Sheaves of modules (quasi-coherent sheaves): `QCohSheaf X` — a sheaf `ℱ` of `𝒪_X`-modules satisfying the quasi-coherent condition (`ℱ(U) ≃ M̃` for affine opens). Distinct from Modules/Presheaf and Modules/Tilde.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Presheaves of modules on a scheme: `PresheafOfModules X` — a presheaf `M` of `𝒪_X`-modules on the opens of `X`. Basic operations and the global sections. Distinct from Modules/Sheaf and Topology/Sheaves/Presheaf.

Name: IdealSheaf
Description: Ideal sheaves on schemes: sub-sheaves of the structure sheaf corresponding to closed subschemes.

Name: IrreducibleComponent.lean
Description: Irreducible components via ideal sheaves: the ideal sheaf of an irreducible component and the primary decomposition. Distinct from IdealSheaf/Basic and Geometrically/Irreducible.

Name: Subscheme.lean
Description: Closed subschemes from ideal sheaves: `AlgebraicGeometry.IdealSheaf.toScheme ℐ` — the closed subscheme `V(ℐ)` defined by an ideal sheaf `ℐ`. Distinct from IdealSheaf/Basic and Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.

Name: Basic.lean
