Description: Ideal sheaves on schemes: `AlgebraicGeometry.IdealSheaf X` — a sheaf of ideals in `𝒪_X`, defining a closed subscheme. The correspondence between ideal sheaves and closed subschemes. Distinct from IdealSheaf/Subscheme.

Name: Functorial.lean
Description: Functoriality of ideal sheaves: pullback and pushforward of ideal sheaves along scheme morphisms. Distinct from IdealSheaf/Basic.

Name: Morphisms
Description: Morphisms of schemes and their properties: open immersions, closed immersions, affine, quasi-compact, separated, proper, and etale morphisms.

Name: QuasiSeparated.lean
Description: Quasi-separated morphisms: `IsQuasiSeparated f` — the diagonal `X → X ×_Y X` is quasi-compact. Distinct from Morphisms/Separated and Morphisms/QuasiCompact.

Name: QuasiFinite.lean
Description: Quasi-finite morphisms: `IsQuasiFinite f` — locally of finite type with finite fibres. Zariski's main theorem applies. Distinct from Morphisms/Finite and Morphisms/FiniteType.

Name: SchemeTheoreticallyDominant.lean
Description: Scheme-theoretically dominant morphisms: `IsSchemeTheoreticallyDominant f` — the scheme-theoretic image equals the target. Distinct from Morphisms/UniversallyInjective.

Name: RingHomProperties.lean
Description: Morphism properties from ring homomorphism properties: converting affine-local ring-hom properties (finite type, flat, etc.) to scheme morphism properties. Distinct from Morphisms/Basic.

Name: QuasiCompact.lean
Description: Quasi-compact morphisms: `IsQuasiCompact f` — preimages of affine opens are quasi-compact. Distinct from Morphisms/QuasiSeparated and Morphisms/FiniteType.

Name: OpenImmersion.lean
Description: Open immersions: `IsOpenImmersion f` — a morphism that is a homeomorphism onto an open subspace with isomorphic structure sheaf. The main examples: `Spec A[f⁻¹] → Spec A`. Distinct from Morphisms/Immersion.

Name: LocalIso.lean
Description: Local isomorphisms: morphisms that are isomorphisms locally on the source. Distinct from Morphisms/IsIso and Morphisms/OpenImmersion.

Name: Proper.lean
Description: Proper morphisms: `IsProper f` — separated, finite type, and universally closed. The valuative criterion for properness. Distinct from Morphisms/Separated and Morphisms/UniversallyClosed.

Name: Preimmersion.lean
Description: Preimmersions: `IsPreimmersion f` — a morphism injective on topological spaces and injective on structure sheaves (precursor to immersion). Distinct from Morphisms/Immersion and Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.

Name: UniversallyInjective.lean
Description: Universally injective (radicial) morphisms: `IsUniversallyInjective f` — injective + purely inseparable residue extensions. Distinct from Morphisms/UniversallyClosed.

Name: UniversallyClosed.lean
Description: Universally closed morphisms: `IsUniversallyClosed f` — `f` remains closed after any base change. Distinct from Morphisms/Proper and Morphisms/UniversallyOpen.

Name: WeaklyEtale.lean
Description: Weakly étale morphisms: `IsWeaklyEtale f` — flat with flat diagonal. Weaker than étale but stronger than flat. Distinct from Morphisms/Etale and Morphisms/Flat.

Name: UniversallyOpen.lean
Description: Universally open morphisms: `IsUniversallyOpen f` — open map after any base change. Distinct from Morphisms/UniversallyClosed and Morphisms/Flat.

Name: UnderlyingMap.lean
Description: Properties of the underlying continuous map: conditions on the underlying continuous map of a scheme morphism (open, closed, surjective, injective). Distinct from Morphisms/Basic.

Name: Smooth.lean
Description: Smooth morphisms: `IsSmooth f` — flat, locally of finite presentation, geometrically regular fibres. Equivalent to formally smooth + locally of finite type. Distinct from Morphisms/Etale and Morphisms/FormallyUnramified.

Name: Separated.lean
Description: Separated morphisms: `IsSeparated f` — the diagonal `X → X ×_Y X` is a closed immersion. The valuative criterion for separatedness. Distinct from Morphisms/QuasiSeparated and Morphisms/Proper.

Name: SurjectiveOnStalks.lean
Description: Morphisms surjective on stalks: `IsSurjectiveOnStalks f` — the map `f# : 𝒪_{Y,f(x)} → 𝒪_{X,x}` is surjective for all `x`. Distinct from Morphisms/UniversallyInjective.

Name: SmoothFiber.lean
Description: `Smooth.of_smooth_fiberToSpecResidueField`: a morphism of schemes that is flat, locally of finite presentation, and has smooth fibers over all residue fields is smooth. Connects pointwise fiber smoothness with global smoothness.

Name: Etale.lean
Description: Étale morphisms: `IsEtale f` — flat, unramified, locally of finite presentation. Equivalent to formally étale + locally of finite type. Distinct from Morphisms/Smooth and Morphisms/FormallyUnramified.

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Descent of morphism properties: if `f : X → Y` becomes `P` after faithfully flat base change, then `f` has `P`. Fpqc and étale descent. Distinct from Morphisms/Basic and Sites/Fpqc.

Name: FinitePresentation.lean
Description: Morphisms of finite presentation: `LocallyOfFinitePresentation f` — locally of finite type with a finitely presented diagonal. Distinct from Morphisms/FiniteType and Morphisms/QuasiCompact.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite morphisms: `IsFinite f` — affine with integral + finite type fibres, equivalently `f_*(𝒪_X)` is a finite `𝒪_Y`-algebra. Distinct from Morphisms/FiniteType and Morphisms/Integral.

Name: Constructors.lean
Description: Constructor lemmas for morphism properties: helper lemmas for building morphism property classes from affine-local conditions. Distinct from Morphisms/Basic and Morphisms/RingHomProperties.

Name: AffineAnd.lean
Description: Affine-and-`P` morphisms: the class of morphisms that are affine and satisfy property `P`. Used to build finite, integral, and other affine morphism classes. Distinct from Morphisms/Affine.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: Affine morphisms: `IsAffine f` — every preimage of an affine open is affine. The characterisation via `f_*(𝒪_X)` being quasi-coherent and `f` being the relative Spec. Distinct from Morphisms/Basic.

Name: ClosedImmersion.lean
Description: Closed immersions: `IsClosedImmersion f` — a morphism that is a homeomorphism onto a closed subspace with surjective `f#`. Characterised by a surjective `𝒪_Y → f_*(𝒪_X)`. Distinct from Morphisms/Immersion and Morphisms/Preimmersion.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Framework for morphism properties in algebraic geometry: `MorphismProperty` — a class of scheme morphisms closed under isomorphisms. Stability under base change, composition, and local conditions. The foundational file for the Morphisms folder.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integral morphisms: `IsIntegral f` — affine with integral ring maps. Integral morphisms are affine and universally closed. Distinct from Morphisms/Finite and Morphisms/Affine.

Name: Immersion.lean
Description: Immersions (open or closed): `IsImmersion f` — a morphism that factors as an open immersion followed by a closed immersion. Distinct from Morphisms/OpenImmersion and Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.

Name: LocalClosure.lean
Description: Local closure conditions: conditions on morphism properties that can be checked locally on the source or target. The affine-local criterion framework. Distinct from Morphisms/Basic.

Name: IsIso.lean
Description: Isomorphisms of schemes: `IsIso f` — scheme morphisms that are invertible. The recognition lemma: `f` is iso iff it is a homeomorphism with isomorphic structure sheaf maps. Distinct from Morphisms/OpenImmersion.

Name: FormallyUnramified.lean
Description: Formally unramified morphisms: `IsFormallyUnramified f` — the unique lifting condition against nilpotent thickenings. Distinct from Morphisms/Etale (formally unramified + locally finite type) and Morphisms/Smooth.

Name: Flat.lean
Description: Flat morphisms: `IsFlat f` — the sheaf map on stalks is flat. Flat = torsion-free in the curve case. Distinct from Morphisms/FlatDescent and Morphisms/FlatMono.

Name: FiniteType.lean
Description: Morphisms of finite type: `LocallyOfFiniteType f` — locally: `𝒪_Y(V) → 𝒪_X(f⁻¹V)` is of finite type. Distinct from Morphisms/FinitePresentation and Morphisms/Affine.

Name: FlatMono.lean
Description: Flat monomorphisms: a morphism that is both flat and a monomorphism is an open immersion. Distinct from Morphisms/Flat and Morphisms/OpenImmersion.

Name: FlatDescent.lean
Description: Descent along flat morphisms: fpqc descent for flat morphisms and quasi-coherent sheaves. Distinct from Morphisms/Flat and Morphisms/Descent.

Name: Sites
Description: Sites on schemes: the Zariski, etale, and flat topologies, with their associated sheaves and cohomology theories.

Name: Representability.lean
Description: Representability of functors on scheme sites: Yoneda lemma for sites, and conditions under which a presheaf on the Zariski/étale site is representable. Distinct from Sites/Pretopology.

Name: QuasiCompact.lean
Description: Quasi-compact condition on sites: the quasi-compact Grothendieck topology and its relationship with coherent sheaves. Distinct from Sites/BigZariski and Morphisms/QuasiCompact.

Name: Proetale.lean
Description: Pro-étale site: the site with pro-étale covers (inverse limits of étale covers). Used for the pro-étale fundamental group and better cohomological properties. Distinct from Sites/Etale.

Name: SmallAffineZariski.lean
Description: Small affine Zariski site: the site on affine opens of a scheme with Zariski covers by affine opens. Used for quasi-coherent sheaf descent. Distinct from Sites/Small and Sites/BigZariski.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Small Zariski site: the site on the lattice of open subsets of a scheme `X` (as opposed to all schemes over `X`). Distinct from Sites/BigZariski and Sites/SmallAffineZariski.

Name: SheafQuasiCompact.lean
Description: Sheaves on quasi-compact sites: descent for sheaves on quasi-compact covers and compactness of the site. Distinct from Sites/QuasiCompact and Modules/Sheaf.

Name: Pretopology.lean
Description: Pretopologies on the category of schemes: the data specifying coverings before sheafification. Distinct from Sites/MorphismProperty and Topology/Category/Locale.

Name: ElladicCohomology.lean
Description: ℓ-adic cohomology setup on the étale site: the projective system of étale sheaves `(ℤ/ℓⁿℤ)` and the ℓ-adic sheaf `ℤₗ`. Active area; exact scope requires verification.

Name: ConstantSheaf.lean
Description: Constant sheaves on scheme sites: the constant sheaf `ConstantSheaf A` on the étale or Zariski site and its cohomology. Distinct from Sites/Etale and Topology/Sheaves/Skyscraper.

Name: BigZariski.lean
Description: Big Zariski site: the Grothendieck site on all schemes with Zariski covers (families of open immersions covering the scheme). Distinct from Sites/Small and Sites/SmallAffineZariski.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Sites defined by morphism properties: the general construction of a Grothendieck site from a class of morphisms `P` (e.g., open immersions, étale). Distinct from Cover/MorphismProperty.

Name: Fpqc.lean
Description: Fpqc site: covers by faithfully flat quasi-compact morphisms. Fpqc descent for quasi-coherent sheaves and morphism properties. Distinct from Sites/Etale and Morphisms/Descent.

Name: Etale.lean
Description: Étale site: the Grothendieck site on a scheme `X` with étale covers. The étale fundamental group and the local-acyclicity of étale morphisms. Distinct from Sites/BigZariski and Morphisms/Etale.

Name: ProjectiveSpectrum
Description: The projective spectrum Proj(S) of a graded ring: the scheme structure on projective space and projective varieties.

Name: Scheme.lean
Description: The scheme structure on `Proj A`: the structure sheaf on `Proj A` making it a scheme, and the open affine cover `D₊(f) ≅ Spec A[f⁻¹]₀`. Distinct from ProjectiveSpectrum/StructureSheaf.

Name: StructureSheaf.lean
Description: Structure sheaf of `Proj A`: the sheaf `𝒪_{Proj A}` with sections `𝒪(D₊(f)) = A[f⁻¹]₀`. Distinct from ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme and Modules/Tilde.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topology of `Proj A`: the Zariski topology on homogeneous prime ideals, closed sets `V₊(I)`, distinguished opens `D₊(f)`. Distinct from ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Projective spectrum `Proj A`: the topological space of homogeneous prime ideals of a graded ring `A`, not containing the irrelevant ideal. Core definitions. Distinct from ProjectiveSpectrum/Topology and ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functoriality of `Proj`: a graded ring map `A → B` induces a morphism `Proj B → Proj A` (where defined). Distinct from ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.

Name: Proper.lean
Description: Properness of projective morphisms: `Proj A → Spec A₀` is proper for a finitely generated graded algebra `A`. Distinct from ProjectiveSpectrum/Scheme and Morphisms/Proper.

Name: Cover
Description: Covers and covering families in algebraic geometry: Zariski covers, etale covers, and faithfully flat descent.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Covers over a base scheme: covers of the morphism `f : X → S`, i.e., covers of `X` compatible with the structure map. Distinct from Cover/Open and Cover/MorphismProperty.

Name: QuasiCompact.lean
Description: Quasi-compact covers: finite open covers and covers by quasi-compact opens. Used in the characterisation of quasi-compact schemes. Distinct from Cover/Open and Morphisms/QuasiCompact.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma-type covers: covers indexed by a sigma type (disjoint union of index sets). Distinct from Cover/Open and Cover/Directed.

Name: Directed.lean
Description: Directed covers of schemes: `Cover.Directed` — a cover where the index type is directed, used for filtered colimits of schemes and affine-local conditions. Distinct from Cover/Open and Cover/QuasiCompact.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Covers defined by morphism properties: for a morphism property `P` (e.g., open immersion, étale), a `P`-cover is a family of morphisms with property `P` whose images cover the scheme. Distinct from Cover/Open and Sites/MorphismProperty.

Name: Open.lean
Description: Open covers of schemes: `Scheme.OpenCover X` — a family of open immersions `Uᵢ → X` covering `X`. The affine open cover and its relationship with affine-local properties. Distinct from Cover/QuasiCompact.

Name: AlgClosed
Description: Algebraic geometry over algebraically closed fields: the Nullstellensatz, the equivalence between radical ideals and affine varieties.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Algebraically closed fields in algebraic geometry: schemes over an algebraically closed field, the Nullstellensatz consequence that closed points are dense, and geometric properties that hold over algebraically closed fields. Distinct from FieldTheory/IsAlgClosed/Basic (the field theory) and AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Basic.

Name: EllipticCurve
Description: Elliptic curves: Weierstrass equations, the group law on points, the j-invariant, and connections to modular forms.

Name: Jacobian
Description: Elliptic curves in Jacobian coordinates (X : Y : Z) where the affine point (x, y) corresponds to (x : y : 1): a projective model where the group law has no exceptional cases. Faster for computer arithmetic than affine or standard projective coordinates. Distinct from Projective (which uses standard homogeneous coordinates (X : Y : Z) with Y² Z = X³ + AXZ² + BZ³).

Name: Point.lean
Description: Points in Jacobian coordinates.

Name: Formula.lean
Description: Addition formulas in Jacobian coordinates.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Jacobian coordinates for elliptic curves.

Name: Projective
Description: Elliptic curves in projective Weierstrass form Y²Z = X³ + AXZ² + BZ³: the group law in homogeneous coordinates, the point at infinity as (0 : 1 : 0), and the scheme-theoretic definition as a smooth projective curve of genus 1. The most natural algebro-geometric model. Distinct from Affine (no projective closure) and Jacobian (different projective embedding).

Name: Point.lean
Description: Points in projective coordinates.

Name: Formula.lean
Description: Addition formulas in projective coordinates.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Projective Weierstrass curves.

Name: Affine
Description: Elliptic curves in affine Weierstrass form y² = x³ + Ax + B: the group law via the chord-and-tangent construction in affine coordinates, the point at infinity, and the j-invariant. This is the computationally explicit model. Distinct from Projective (homogeneous coordinates) and Jacobian (a projective model optimized for efficient group law computation).

Name: Point.lean
Description: Points on affine Weierstrass curves.

Name: Formula.lean
Description: Addition formulas on affine Weierstrass curves.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Affine Weierstrass model of an elliptic curve.

Name: DivisionPolynomial
Description: Division polynomials ψₙ, φₙ, ωₙ of an elliptic curve: polynomials in x (and y) defined by explicit recurrences whose roots are the coordinates of n-torsion points. Contains ψ (the classical division polynomial), φ, ω, and twoTorsionPolynomial. Used to compute the n-torsion subgroup E[n] and to study isogenies of elliptic curves. Distinct from EllipticCurve/Affine and EllipticCurve/Projective (coordinate-system formalizations of the curve) and from the Weil pairing (a separate construction on torsion points).

Name: Degree.lean
Description: Degree of division polynomials.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Division polynomials ψₙ for elliptic curves.

Name: Reduction.lean
Description: Reduction of elliptic curves modulo a prime: good reduction, multiplicative reduction, additive reduction; the Néron model. Distinct from EllipticCurve/Weierstrass and NumberTheory/LocalField.

Name: VariableChange.lean
Description: Variable changes for Weierstrass models: the substitution `(x, y) ↦ (u²x + r, u³y + u²sx + t)` and how it transforms Weierstrass coefficients. Distinct from EllipticCurve/Weierstrass.

Name: Weierstrass.lean
Description: Weierstrass equations and elliptic curves: `WeierstrassCurve R` with coefficients `a₁, a₂, a₃, a₄, a₆`, the discriminant `Δ`, j-invariant, and `EllipticCurve R` (Weierstrass curve with `Δ ≠ 0`). The foundational file for the EllipticCurve folder.

Name: NormalForms.lean
Description: Normal forms for Weierstrass equations: short Weierstrass form `y² = x³ + ax + b` (char ≠ 2, 3), and other simplified forms. Distinct from EllipticCurve/Weierstrass and EllipticCurve/VariableChange.

Name: IsomOfJ.lean
Description: Isomorphisms between elliptic curves with the same j-invariant: over an algebraically closed field, two elliptic curves are isomorphic iff they have the same j-invariant. Distinct from EllipticCurve/ModelsWithJ and EllipticCurve/Weierstrass.

Name: LFunction.lean
Description: L-function of an elliptic curve: the Hasse–Weil L-function `L(E, s) = ∏_p L_p(E, p^{-s})⁻¹` and its basic properties. Distinct from NumberTheory/LSeries and EllipticCurve/Reduction.

Name: ModelsWithJ.lean
Description: Standard Weierstrass models with prescribed j-invariant: for `j ≠ 0, 1728`, explicit Weierstrass equations with the given j-invariant. Distinct from EllipticCurve/IsomOfJ and EllipticCurve/Weierstrass.

Name: Group
Description: Group schemes: schemes with a group structure, including additive and multiplicative group schemes and abelian schemes.

Name: Smooth.lean
Description: `AlgebraicGeometry.smooth_of_grpObj`: a group scheme `G` over a field `k` that is geometrically reduced and locally of finite type is smooth over `k`. Uses `AlgClosed.Basic` and `Geometrically.Reduced`.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: `AlgebraicGeometry.isCommMonObj_of_isProper_of_geometricallyIntegral`: a proper geometrically integral group scheme over a field is commutative. Uses Zariski's main theorem and `Geometrically.Integral`.

Name: Geometrically
Description: Geometric properties of morphisms that are preserved after base change to an algebraic closure (geometrically irreducible, reduced, etc.).

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Geometrically irreducible schemes: `Geometrically.IsIrreducible X` — `X ×_k k̄` is irreducible. Distinct from Geometrically/Integral and Geometrically/Connected.

Name: Reduced.lean
Description: Geometrically reduced schemes: `Geometrically.IsReduced X` — `X ×_k k̄` is reduced (no nilpotents). Distinct from Geometrically/Integral and Morphisms/UniversallyInjective.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Geometrically integral schemes: `Geometrically.IsIntegral X` — `X ×_k k̄` is integral (irreducible + reduced). Distinct from Geometrically/Irreducible and Geometrically/Reduced.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Geometric properties of schemes: the framework for properties that hold after base change to an algebraically closed field. Core definitions and the general `Geometrically` predicate. Distinct from Geometrically/Connected and Morphisms/Basic.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Geometrically connected schemes: `Geometrically.IsConnected X` — `X ×_k k̄` is connected. Distinct from Geometrically/Basic and Morphisms/Separated.

Name: RationalMap.lean
Description: Rational maps between schemes: partially defined morphisms, their domains of definition, and birational equivalence.

Name: QuasiAffine.lean
Description: Quasi-affine schemes: open subschemes of affine schemes, characterized by the quasi-affinity criterion.

Name: ResidueField.lean
Description: Residue fields of schemes at points: the field kappa(x) = O_{X,x}/m_x and the canonical map from the stalk.

Name: RelativeGluing.lean
Description: Relative gluing of schemes over a base: constructing a scheme over S by gluing local pieces with compatibility over S.

Name: Properties.lean
Description: Global properties of schemes: reducedness, integrality, normality, and their interaction with morphisms.

Name: PointsPi.lean
Description: Points of a product scheme: the universal property of fiber products and the description of closed points.

Name: Pullbacks.lean
Description: Pullbacks of schemes: the fiber product X x_S Y and its construction via the affine and gluing cases.

Name: PullbackCarrier.lean
Description: The underlying type of pullback schemes: computing the carrier set and its topology for the fiber product.

Name: StructureSheaf.lean
Description: The structure sheaf of Spec(R): sections over basic opens D(f) as localizations R_f, and the sheaf axioms.

Name: Stalk.lean
Description: Stalks of the structure sheaf: the local ring O_{X,x} as a colimit of sections, and the stalk map.

Name: ZariskisMainTheorem.lean
Description: Zariski's main theorem: a quasi-finite separated morphism factors as an open immersion followed by a finite morphism.

Name: ValuativeCriterion.lean
Description: The valuative criteria for separatedness and properness of morphisms of schemes.

Name: Scheme.lean
Description: The Scheme type: a locally ringed space locally isomorphic to Spec(R), the central object of algebraic geometry in Mathlib.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restriction of schemes to open subsets: the open subscheme U in X and its universal property.

Name: SpreadingOut.lean
Description: Spreading out results: a morphism of finite type defined over a limit can be descended to a finite-level approximation.

Name: Spec.lean
Description: The Spec functor: from commutative rings to affine schemes, left adjoint to the global sections functor.

Name: Over.lean
Description: The category of schemes over a fixed base S: S-schemes and S-morphisms, the slice category Sch/S.

Name: EffectiveEpi.lean
Description: Effective epimorphisms of schemes: surjective families that are universal colimiting cocones in the category of schemes.

Name: ColimitsOver.lean
Description: Colimits in the category of schemes over a base: pushouts and coproducts of S-schemes.

Name: FunctionField.lean
Description: The function field of an integral scheme: the stalk at the generic point, rational functions, and the degree of a finite morphism.

Name: Fiber.lean
Description: Fibers of a morphism of schemes: the scheme-theoretic fiber X x_S Spec(kappa(s)) over a point s in S.

Name: AffineSpace.lean
Description: Affine n-space as a scheme: Spec of the polynomial ring with coordinate ring and universal property.

Name: AffineScheme.lean
Description: Affine schemes: the full subcategory of Spec(R)s, the adjunction with CommRing, and the affine cover criterion.

Name: Artinian.lean
Description: Artinian schemes: schemes with Artinian local rings at every point, characterizing zero-dimensional schemes.

Name: AffineTransitionLimit.lean
Description: Limits of affine scheme transitions: inverse limits along affine morphisms and their properties.

Name: Noetherian.lean
Description: Noetherian schemes: schemes with Noetherian underlying topological space and Noetherian local rings, with finiteness results.

Name: LimitsOver.lean
Description: Limits in the category of schemes over a base: fiber products and equalizers of S-schemes.

Name: OpenImmersion.lean
Description: Open immersions of schemes: morphisms that are homeomorphisms onto an open subset and isomorphisms on stalks.

Name: Normalization.lean
Description: Normalization of schemes: the integral closure of a scheme in its function field, producing a normal scheme.

Name: Gluing.lean
Description: Gluing schemes along open subsets: the general gluing construction producing a scheme from local affine pieces.

Name: GammaSpecAdjunction.lean
Description: The Gamma-Spec adjunction: the counit being the canonical map X to Spec(Gamma(X)).

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in the category of schemes: products, equalizers, and their explicit constructions.

Name: GluingOneHypercover.lean
Description: Gluing schemes along a hypercover: a generalization of the gluing construction using a simplicial diagram of affines.

Name: Algebra
Description: Algebraic structures such as groups, rings, fields, modules, and algebras over a ring, together with their homomorphisms and constructions. Used to prove fundamental properties of numbers, polynomials, matrices, and symmetries, and to develop linear algebra and representation theory. Relied on by abstract algebra, number theory, algebraic geometry, algebraic topology, and most areas that use ring- or group-theoretic arguments.

Name: NoZeroSMulDivisors
Description: The NoZeroSMulDivisors typeclass: a module axiom asserting that scalar multiplication has no nontrivial zero divisors.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: NoZeroSMulDivisors for Pi types: the product of NoZeroSMulDivisors modules fails to satisfy the property (since (1,0) • (0,1) = 0), and the correct instances. Distinct from NoZeroSMulDivisors/Basic and Module/Pi.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: NoZeroSMulDivisors for product modules: the product M × N and when it inherits the property. Distinct from NoZeroSMulDivisors/Pi and NoZeroSMulDivisors/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results for NoZeroSMulDivisors: cancellation of scalar multiplication, the interaction with injective scalar maps, and instances for algebras over domains. Distinct from NoZeroSMulDivisors/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: The NoZeroSMulDivisors typeclass: asserting that r • m = 0 implies r = 0 or m = 0 for a scalar multiplication action, the module-theoretic analogue of an integral domain. Distinct from NoZeroSMulDivisors/Basic (derived results).

Name: Notation
Description: Notation definitions and typeclasses for algebraic operations: overloaded symbols for multiplication, addition, powers, and inverses.

Name: Pi
Description: Pi-type notation for algebraic operations: scoped notation enabling pointwise ∑ and ∏ over dependent function types, and operator precedence for Pi-indexed algebraic expressions.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for pi notation: the `Pi.instXxx` instances for algebraic typeclasses.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pi notation for algebra: notation and instances for pointwise operations on `Π i, α i`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product notation for algebra: the ∏ operator notation and its interaction with the ring and group structures. Distinct from Notation/Defs and BigOperators files.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation for the Notation folder: explains the role of algebraic notation files and the conventions for support, indicator, and product notation. Not a Lean source file.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Notation for support: the mulSupport and support of a function, with the notation {x | f x ≠ 1} and basic API. Distinct from Notation/Defs and Group/Support.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas about algebraic notation: basic consequences of the notational definitions, such as alternative spellings and simp lemmas for ∑, ∏, and •. Distinct from Notation/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core notation definitions for algebra: the operator symbols ∑, ∏, •, and their syntactic sugar, establishing the notation layer used throughout Mathlib's algebra files. Distinct from Notation/Lemmas.

Name: FiniteSupport.lean
Description: Notation for finite-support sums: the ∑ᶠ (finsum) notation for sums over types with finite support, distinct from Finset.sum. Distinct from Notation/Defs and FiniteSupport/Basic.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Notation for indicator functions: the notation 1[condition] or Set.indicator for the indicator function of a set, valued in a type with zero. Distinct from Notation/Defs and Group/Indicator.

Name: NonAssoc
Description: Non-associative algebraic structures: magmas, non-associative rings, and their basic properties without the associativity axiom.

Name: PreLie
Description: Pre-Lie algebras: non-associative algebras satisfying (a ▷ b) ▷ c - a ▷ (b ▷ c) = (a ▷ c) ▷ b - a ▷ (c ▷ b), the right-symmetry condition. The anti-symmetrization gives a Lie algebra. Arises in combinatorics (rooted trees), operad theory, and quantum field theory. Distinct from LieAdmissible: pre-Lie is strictly stronger.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pre-Lie algebras: `PreLieAlgebra R A` satisfying `(x ▷ y) ▷ z - x ▷ (y ▷ z) = (x ▷ z) ▷ y - x ▷ (z ▷ y)`. Used in deformation theory.

Name: LieAdmissible
Description: Lie-admissible algebras: non-associative algebras A where the commutator bracket [a,b] = ab - ba satisfies the Jacobi identity, making A into a Lie algebra. Every associative algebra is Lie-admissible. Distinct from PreLie algebras, which satisfy a weaker axiom.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Lie-admissible algebras: `LieAdmissible A` — a non-associative algebra whose commutator `[x, y] = xy - yx` satisfies the Jacobi identity.

Name: Polynomial
Description: Univariate polynomials over a semiring: degree, leading coefficient, evaluation, roots, and polynomial arithmetic.

Name: Module
Description: Polynomials as a module over the base ring: the R[X]-module structure where X acts by shift, and the connection to R-module endomorphisms (the Cayley-Hamilton theorem). Distinct from LinearAlgebra/Charpoly (which computes the characteristic polynomial of a matrix) and Polynomial/Eval (which evaluates polynomials at points).

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional polynomial modules.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor product of polynomial modules.

Name: AEval.lean
Description: Abstract evaluation `aeval` for polynomials acting on modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Polynomial module: `PolynomialModule R M = R[X] ⊗_R M`.

Name: Eval
Description: Evaluation of polynomials: eval r p (evaluating p at r ∈ R), aeval r p (evaluating in an R-algebra via a ring homomorphism), Polynomial.map f (applying f to coefficients), and the roots of a polynomial. Contains the factor theorem, multiple roots, and Vieta's formulas. Distinct from Degree (structural degree results) and Module (polynomial as a module).

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Evaluation and irreducibility.

Name: SMul.lean
Description: Scalar multiplication in polynomial evaluation.

Name: Subring.lean
Description: Polynomial evaluation in subrings.

Name: Degree.lean
Description: Evaluation and degree.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Polynomial evaluation as algebra map.

Name: Coeff.lean
Description: Evaluating polynomial coefficients.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Polynomial evaluation definitions.

Name: Degree
Description: Degree theory for polynomials: natDegree p (the degree as a natural number, 0 for zero), degree p (as a WithBot ℕ, -∞ for zero), leadingCoeff, and monic polynomials. Contains degree of sums, products (deg(fg) = deg f + deg g in a domain), and compositions. Distinct from Eval (evaluation at a point) and Module (polynomial as a module).

Name: SmallDegree.lean
Description: Polynomials of degree ≤ 2.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Degree of polynomial operations: sum, product.

Name: Monomial.lean
Description: Degree of monomials.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Degree of units in polynomial rings.

Name: TrailingDegree.lean
Description: Trailing degree of a polynomial.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support and degree of polynomials.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Polynomial degree definitions.

Name: Definitions.lean
Description: Polynomial degree API: `Polynomial.degree`, `natDegree`, `leadingCoeff`.

Name: CardPowDegree.lean
Description: CardPowDegree: cardinality-based degree measure for polynomials over finite fields.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Polynomial degree lemmas.

Name: IsMonicOfDegree.lean
Description: Monic polynomials of a given degree.

Name: Domain.lean
Description: Degree in polynomial domains.

Name: Monomial.lean
Description: Monomials in the polynomial ring: Polynomial.monomial n : R →ₗ[R] Polynomial R sending a to the single-term polynomial a * X^n, with the monomial basis and the decomposition lemmas. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Coeff.

Name: Monic.lean
Description: Monic polynomials: the Monic predicate (leadingCoeff p = 1), the product of two monics is monic, the monic part of a polynomial, and monic factorisation. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Coeff.

Name: OfFn.lean
Description: Constructing polynomials from coefficient arrays: Polynomial.ofFn building a polynomial from a function Fin n → R listing coefficients from constant to leading. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/CoeffList.

Name: Reverse.lean
Description: Polynomial reversal via finsupp.revAt: Polynomial.reverse defined by reversing the finsupp support, a related but distinct construction from Polynomial.mirror. Distinct from Polynomial/Mirror and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: PartialFractions.lean
Description: Partial fraction decomposition: the decomposition of a rational function p/q (with q squarefree and split into linear factors) as a sum of simple fractions over a field. Distinct from Polynomial/Div and Field/Basic.

Name: Inductions.lean
Description: Induction principles for polynomials: Polynomial.induction_on and Polynomial.rec for reasoning by induction on degree or on the coefficient structure (C + X * rest). Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Coeff.

Name: Identities.lean
Description: Polynomial identities: standard ring-theoretic identities expressed via polynomials — Sophie Germain identity, difference of powers factorisation, and Newton's identities for elementary symmetric polynomials. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ring/GeomSum.

Name: Laurent.lean
Description: Laurent polynomials: LaurentPolynomial R = ℤ →₀ R of formal sums with integer exponents and finite support, containing Polynomial R (non-negative exponents) as a subring. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: Mirror.lean
Description: The mirror of a polynomial: Polynomial.mirror p = X^natDegree(p) * p(X⁻¹), the polynomial with reversed coefficients, satisfying mirror(p*q) = mirror(p) * mirror(q). Distinct from Polynomial/Reverse (a related but distinct operation) and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Lifts.lean
Description: Lifting polynomials along ring maps: if f : R → S is surjective, any polynomial in Polynomial S lifts to one in Polynomial R; conditions for lifting along other classes of maps. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Ring/InjSurj.

Name: Splits.lean
Description: Splitting polynomials and splitting fields: the Splits k p predicate asserting p factors into linear factors over k, with the algebraic closure context and the basic instances. Distinct from Polynomial/Factors (UFD theory) and Polynomial/Roots.

Name: SpecificDegree.lean
Description: Submodules of polynomials of bounded degree: Polynomial.degreeLT n (polynomials of degree strictly less than n) and Polynomial.degreeLE n as R-submodules, with their finite bases {1, X, …, X^(n-1)}. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Coeff.

Name: SumIteratedDerivative.lean
Description: Sums of iterated derivatives: the Taylor-expansion identity p = ∑ₙ (Polynomial.derivative^[n] p)|_{x=0} / n! * X^n, and the associated summation formulas for iterated derivatives. Distinct from Polynomial/Derivative and Polynomial/Taylor.

Name: UnitTrinomial.lean
Description: Unit trinomials: polynomials of the form X^(a+b) ± X^b ± 1 with coefficients in {±1}, and conditions for their irreducibility over ℤ or ℚ. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Factors.

Name: Taylor.lean
Description: The Taylor shift of a polynomial: the map p ↦ p(X + C a) expressing p as a polynomial in (X - a), and the Taylor expansion identity. Distinct from Polynomial/Derivative and Polynomial/SumIteratedDerivative.

Name: Roots.lean
Description: Roots of polynomials: Polynomial.roots p as a multiset of roots in R, the root multiplicity, the bound card(roots p) ≤ natDegree p over integral domains, and the Vieta formulas. Distinct from Polynomial/Splits (factoring in a closure) and Polynomial/Factors.

Name: RingDivision.lean
Description: Polynomial division and the factor theorem in a commutative ring: Polynomial.divByMonic, the division algorithm p = q · d + r with deg r < deg d, and the remainder theorem (evaluating p at c gives the remainder upon division by (X - c)). The factor theorem: (X - c) divides p iff p(c) = 0. Contains Polynomial.roots as a Multiset and the bound on the number of roots of a nonzero polynomial over an integral domain.

Name: RuleOfSigns.lean
Description: Descartes' rule of signs: the number of positive real roots of a polynomial (counted with multiplicity) is at most the number of sign changes in its coefficient sequence, and the difference is even. The analogous bound for negative roots is obtained via p(-x). Formalized as Polynomial.card_roots_le_of_sign_changes. Used to bound the number of real roots without solving — a classical tool in polynomial analysis.

Name: Smeval.lean
Description: Semiring evaluation of polynomials: Polynomial.smeval R x evaluating p at x ∈ M where M is an R-algebra (or even just an R-module with a smul), generalising eval. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic (eval requires the ring structure).

Name: Sequence.lean
Description: Finite sequences of polynomials: auxiliary results about lists or vectors of polynomials and their products, used in resultant and discriminant computations. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and BigOperators/Fin.

Name: Homogenize.lean
Description: Homogenising a univariate polynomial: the map p(X) ↦ Y^deg(p) p(X/Y) converting a polynomial to a homogeneous bivariate polynomial. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Degrees.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: Cardinality of polynomial rings: |Polynomial R| = max(|R|, ℵ₀) for an infinite coefficient ring R. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic.

Name: CancelLeads.lean
Description: Cancellation of leading terms: the cancelLeads operation on two polynomials p q that cancels the leading term of p relative to q (used in the pseudo-division algorithm and Gröbner basis theory). Distinct from Polynomial/EraseLead and Polynomial/Div.

Name: Coeff.lean
Description: Coefficients of polynomials: Polynomial.coeff p n extracting the n-th coefficient, with lemmas for coeff of sums, products, scalar multiples, and the finsupp interaction. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Monomial.

Name: CoeffMem.lean
Description: Membership of coefficients in sub-structures: the predicate asserting all coefficients of p lie in a sub-semiring, subring, or ideal, with stability under ring operations. Distinct from Polynomial/Coeff and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: CoeffList.lean
Description: Representing polynomials as coefficient lists: converting between Polynomial R and List R of coefficients (least-significant first), and the interaction with toList and ofList. Distinct from Polynomial/Coeff and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of univariate polynomials: Polynomial R = ℕ →₀ R with the ring structure, the variable X, monomials C a * X^n, the evaluation map, and the universal property (any ring map R → S and choice of element extends to Polynomial R → S). Foundation for all Polynomial files.

Name: AlgebraMap.lean
Description: The algebra structure of polynomial rings: the R-algebra structure on Polynomial S for an R-algebra S via algebraMap, and the induced map Polynomial R → Polynomial S from a ring map R → S. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: The monomial basis of a polynomial ring: the collection {X^n | n : ℕ} as an R-basis for Polynomial R as a free R-module, with the basis map and its properties. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Bivariate.lean
Description: Bivariate polynomials: the ring Polynomial (Polynomial R) as two-variable polynomials and the isomorphism with Polynomial R[X][Y], with the interaction between the two variable orderings. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Polynomial.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operator results for polynomials: ∑ and ∏ of polynomial families, the product formula ∏ (X - Cᵢ) for roots, and the interaction of Finset.sum with polynomial evaluation. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and BigOperators/Fin.

Name: Factors.lean
Description: Factorisation theory for polynomials: the unique factorisation domain structure on Polynomial k for a field k, irreducible polynomials, and the interaction with the content and primitive part. Distinct from Polynomial/Splits (algebraic closure context) and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: Expand.lean
Description: Expanding polynomials: Polynomial.expand R n substitutes X^n for X in a polynomial, and the interaction with the Frobenius endomorphism in characteristic p (expand p ∘ Frobenius = Frobenius ∘ expand p). Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Expand.

Name: FieldDivision.lean
Description: Division of polynomials over a field: since k[X] is a Euclidean domain when k is a field, the exact division p / q, degree formula deg(p/q) = deg(p) - deg(q), and the fraction field k(X). Distinct from Polynomial/Div (general rings) and EuclideanDomain/Defs.

Name: HasseDeriv.lean
Description: The Hasse derivative (divided power derivative): the k-th Hasse derivative D^k satisfying D^k(X^n) = C(n,k) X^(n-k), well-defined in any characteristic unlike k! D^k. Distinct from Polynomial/Derivative (standard derivative, requires k! invertible) and Polynomial/SumIteratedDerivative.

Name: GroupRingAction.lean
Description: Group ring actions on polynomials: the action of a group G on Polynomial R via ring automorphisms g • p, and the fixed-point subring Polynomial R^G. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Action/Basic.

Name: Derivation.lean
Description: Derivations of polynomial rings: the universal property that R-derivations D : Polynomial R → M are determined by D(X), and the associated derivation module structure. Distinct from Polynomial/Derivative (the formal derivative map itself) and MvPolynomial/Derivation.

Name: DenomsClearable.lean
Description: Clearing denominators in rational polynomials: the DenomsClearable predicate and the operation of scaling a polynomial over a field of fractions to clear denominators. Distinct from Polynomial/Basic and Field/Basic.

Name: Derivative.lean
Description: The formal derivative of a polynomial: Polynomial.derivative : Polynomial R →ₗ[R] Polynomial R satisfying the Leibniz rule d(pq) = p'q + pq', with the iterated derivative and Taylor's theorem. Distinct from Polynomial/Derivation (universal derivation theory) and Polynomial/HasseDeriv.

Name: EraseLead.lean
Description: Erasing the leading term: Polynomial.eraseLead p removes the highest-degree term, with the recursion eraseLead p = p - C(leadingCoeff p) * X^natDegree p, and induction principles using eraseLead. Distinct from Polynomial/CancelLeads and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Div.lean
Description: Division of polynomials: the Euclidean division algorithm divByMonic and modByMonic, the remainder theorem p(a) = 0 ↔ (X - C a) ∣ p, and polynomial GCDs via the EuclideanDomain instance. Distinct from Polynomial/FieldDivision (over fields) and Polynomial/EraseLead.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise operations on sets and functions: scalar multiplication of sets, addition of sets, and their algebraic properties.

Name: Stabilizer.lean
Description: The stabilizer of a set under a group action: Stab_G(S) = {g ∈ G | g • S = S}, with the subgroup structure and the orbit-stabilizer theorem for sets. Distinct from Group/Conj and Action/Basic.

Name: Order
Description: Ordered algebraic structures: ordered groups, ordered rings, and ordered fields with compatibility between the order and ring operations.

Name: Nonneg
Description: The nonneg subtype {a : α // 0 ≤ a}: algebraic instances (semiring, group, field) for the subtype of nonneg elements of an ordered structure. Used to construct ℝ≥0 and ℚ≥0. Distinct from Order/Positive (which requires strict positivity) and NNReal (which is the specific case of ℝ≥0).

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on non-negative elements.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Module structure on non-negative elements.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Ring structure on non-negative elements.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Non-negative elements in an ordered group.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Non-negative elements in ordered fields.

Name: Floor.lean
Description: Floor for non-negative elements.

Name: Positive
Description: The positive subtype {a : α // 0 < a}: algebraic instances for strictly positive elements. Contains the group structure on positives (closed under multiplication), and the map to the nonneg subtype. Distinct from Order/Nonneg — strict positivity gives a group, not merely a monoid.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Positive cone of an ordered ring: `Positive R` — elements strictly greater than zero.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Positive elements in an ordered field: `{x : F | 0 < x}` as a group under multiplication.

Name: Monoid
Description: Ordered monoids: the primary typeclass layer for ordered algebraic structures. Contains OrderedAddCommMonoid, OrderedCommMonoid, and the key lemmas about monotone addition and multiplication. The root of the ordered algebra hierarchy — almost every ordered structure in Mathlib extends from here.

Name: Unbundled
Description: Unbundled ordered monoid lemmas: explicit-hypothesis proofs of monotonicity and order results that do not use the OrderedMonoid typeclass. Used when writing lemmas that should apply in settings where the full typeclass hierarchy cannot be used, or to bootstrap the typeclass instances themselves.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tags for unbundled ordered monoids.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Powers in unbundled ordered monoids: `n • a ≤ n • b` from `a ≤ b`.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: WithTop for unbundled ordered monoids.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in unbundled ordered monoids.

Name: OrderDual.lean
Description: Order dual for unbundled ordered monoids: the dual ordered monoid structure.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered monoid definitions: `CovariantClass` and `ContravariantClass` for monotonicity.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered monoid lemmas: `mul_le_mul` and variants without full typeclass assumptions.

Name: MinMax.lean
Description: Min/max in unbundled ordered monoids.

Name: ExistsOfLE.lean
Description: Existence lemmas from ordered monoid inequalities: `ExistsAddOfLE` typeclass for `a ≤ b → ∃ c, b = a + c`.

Name: Canonical
Description: Canonical ordered monoids (CanonicallyOrderedMonoid): ordered monoids where a ≤ b iff ∃ c, b = a + c (the order is determined entirely by the monoid). Examples: ℕ, ℝ≥0, ENNReal. Every element is ≥ 0, so there is no subtraction issue. Used pervasively in measure theory and probability. Distinct from Unbundled (which gives lemmas without the full typeclass).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Canonical ordered monoid definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Canonical ordered monoids.

Name: Submonoid.lean
Description: Ordered submonoids: the induced order on `Submonoid M` from the ordered monoid `M`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product ordered monoids.

Name: PNat.lean
Description: ℕ+ as an ordered monoid.

Name: ToMulBot.lean
Description: ToMulBot: extending a monoid with a bottom element.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Ordered monoid with top element.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in ordered monoids.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tags for ordered monoids.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for ordered monoids: `OrderedAddCommMonoid`, `StrictOrderedSemiring`, and related typeclasses.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordered monoid API.

Name: Associated.lean
Description: Associated elements in ordered monoids.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: Lexicographic ordered monoids.

Name: OrderDual.lean
Description: Order dual of an ordered monoid.

Name: NatCast.lean
Description: ℕ cast in ordered monoids.

Name: LocallyFiniteOrder.lean
Description: Locally finite ordered monoids.

Name: Interval
Description: Algebraic operations on order-intervals: the interval [a, b] as a set with addition [a,b] + [c,d] = [a+c, b+d] and scalar multiplication. Used in interval arithmetic and perturbation theory. Distinct from Order/LatticeIntervals (lattice structure on intervals) and Data/Set/Intervals (set-theoretic intervals).

Name: Set
Description: Algebraic operations on set-theoretic intervals: the interval arithmetic a + [b, c] = [a+b, a+c] and r • [a, b] = [ra, rb] for ordered rings. Used in analysis (perturbation bounds) and in convex analysis. Distinct from the parent Interval folder (order structure only) — this subfolder adds the algebraic ring/group operations.

Name: Monoid.lean
Description: Set intervals in monoids.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Set intervals and successor-predecessor.

Name: Group.lean
Description: Set intervals in groups.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard interval set instances.

Name: Finset
Description: Finite ordered intervals as Finset: Finset.Icc a b, Finset.Ioc, Finset.Ico, Finset.Ioo when the linear order is locally finite (LocallyFiniteOrder typeclass). Cardinality formulas (|Icc a b| = b - a + 1 in ℕ or ℤ) and summation over intervals. The algebraic interactions: how arithmetic operations interact with finite intervals.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Finset intervals and successor-predecessor.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Finite intervals in ordered sets.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Multisets and intervals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Intervals in ordered groups.

Name: Module
Description: Ordered modules: modules M over an ordered ring R where the order on M is compatible with scalar multiplication (r ≥ 0 and m ≥ 0 implies r • m ≥ 0). Contains the positive cone, the order dual, and PosMulStrictMono. Distinct from Normed/Module which requires a norm.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise order on module sets.

Name: HahnEmbedding.lean
Description: Hahn embedding theorem for ordered groups.

Name: PositiveLinearMap.lean
Description: Positive linear maps.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Type synonyms for ordered modules.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ-ordered modules.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Ordered field modules.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: Archimedean ordered modules.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Ordered algebra structure on modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordered modules: `OrderedSMul`.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Isomorphisms of ordered modules.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Ordered module definitions.

Name: SuccPred
Description: Successor and predecessor in ordered algebraic structures: the SuccOrder and PredOrder typeclasses in the presence of an algebraic structure, and the interaction of succ/pred with addition. Used in ℕ and ℤ. Distinct from Order/SuccPred which has no algebraic structure.

Name: WithBot.lean
Description: Succ-pred with bottom element.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tags for succ-pred ordered structures.

Name: PartialSups.lean
Description: Partial suprema and successor-predecessor orders.

Name: WithTop
Description: Extending an ordered algebraic structure with a top element +∞: addition and multiplication on WithTop α, with the convention ∞ + x = ∞ and 0 · ∞ = 0. Used for ENNReal and ENat. Distinct from Order/WithTop which only considers the order, not the algebra.

Name: Untop0.lean
Description: `untop0`: extracting the value from `WithTop`, defaulting to 0.

Name: Sub
Description: Truncated subtraction for ordered algebraic structures: a ∸ b = max(a - b, 0) for ordered groups, and its algebraic properties. Used in ℕ (where subtraction is truncated) and in abstract ordered groups without genuine inverses. Distinct from ordered ring subtraction which is the genuine difference.

Name: Unbundled
Description: Unbundled truncated subtraction lemmas: explicit-hypothesis versions of the OrderedSub (a ∸ b = max(a - b, 0)) theory. Used for ℕ-style truncated subtraction in contexts where the full OrderedSub typeclass is not available. Contains the basic identities (a ∸ b + b = max(a, b)) without typeclass overhead.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of ordered subtraction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered subtraction.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Truncated subtraction on products: `(a, b) - (c, d) = (a - c, b - d)` in a product ordered monoid.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Truncated subtraction with top.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Truncated subtraction in ordered groups.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Ordered subtraction definitions: `OrderedSub` typeclass for truncated subtraction.

Name: Ring
Description: Ordered rings and semirings: OrderedRing, OrderedCommRing, and LinearOrderedRing, combining ring axioms with order compatibility. Contains the sign function, the strict monotonicity of multiplication, and the characterization of ordered rings via the positive cone. The algebraic home for properties like a² ≥ 0.

Name: Unbundled
Description: Unbundled ordered ring lemmas: the multiplicative sign rules (neg × neg = pos), bounds on products, and order consequences of ring axioms, stated with explicit hypotheses instead of typeclass inference. Used to bootstrap OrderedRing instances and to handle universe polymorphism issues in the ordered ring hierarchy.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ unbundled ordered ring.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered ring lemmas: `mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left` and variants without full typeclass.

Name: Ordering
Description: Constructing a ring order from a positive cone: given a subset P ⊆ R satisfying P + P ⊆ P, P · P ⊆ P, P ∩ (-P) = {0}, P ∪ (-P) = R, the relation a ≤ b ↔ b - a ∈ P makes R into a linearly ordered ring. Used to put the standard order on ℤ, ℚ, ℝ via their natural positive cones. Distinct from Unbundled (lemmas about existing orders).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Ring ordering definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ring ordering basics.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Opposite of an ordered ring.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Powers in ordered rings: `x^n > 0` for `x > 0`, and `x^n ≤ y^n` for `0 ≤ x ≤ y`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product ordered rings.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Intervals in ordered rings.

Name: IsNonarchimedean.lean
Description: Non-archimedean ordered rings.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: ℕ as an ordered semiring: `Nat.instStrictOrderedSemiring` and related instances.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Type synonyms for ordered rings.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in ordered rings.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Ordered ring with top element.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ as a linear ordered field: `Rat.instLinearOrderedField`.

Name: StandardPart.lean
Description: Standard part in hyperreal-like ordered rings.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star and order in C*-algebras.

Name: Canonical.lean
Description: Canonical ordered ring facts: universal identities in `LinearOrderedField` derived from ordering.

Name: Cast.lean
Description: Cast lemmas for ordered rings.

Name: Cone.lean
Description: Positive cone of an ordered ring.

Name: Abs.lean
Description: Absolute value in ordered rings.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: Archimedean ordered rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordered ring basics.

Name: Idempotent.lean
Description: Idempotents in ordered rings.

Name: InjSurj.lean
Description: Injective/surjective maps of ordered rings.

Name: Int.lean
Description: ℤ as an ordered ring: the unique ordered commutative ring structure on ℤ.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Ordered ring definitions: `StrictOrderedRing`, `LinearOrderedRing`, and their axioms.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Ordered ring operations on Finsets: `Finset.prod_pos` and sums/products of positive elements.

Name: GeomSum.lean
Description: Geometric sum bounds in ordered rings.

Name: Star
Description: Ordered star algebras: star algebras where the order interacts with the star operation via 0 ≤ a ↔ ∃ b, a = b* b (the algebraic positivity condition). The algebraic counterpart of the C*-algebra condition. Distinct from Analysis/CStarAlgebra which requires completeness.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Star order on pi types.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Star order on products.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Star-ordered algebras.

Name: Conjneg.lean
Description: Conjugate-negative elements in star-ordered rings.

Name: BigOperators
Description: Order-aware big-operator lemmas: inequalities for ∑ and ∏ in ordered algebraic structures, including monotonicity of sums, the AM-GM inequality as a Finset statement, and comparison of products. Distinct from Algebra/BigOperators/Ring (algebraic identities) — this folder requires an order.

Name: Ring
Description: Order inequalities involving both sums and products in ordered rings: the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality (∑ aᵢbᵢ)² ≤ (∑ aᵢ²)(∑ bᵢ²) and the Chebyshev sum inequality. Products of sums and the rearrangement inequality. Requires both additive and multiplicative order compatibility. Distinct from Group (additive only) and GroupWithZero (no distribution).

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Multiset ring big operators.

Name: List.lean
Description: List ring big operators.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Ring big operators: ordered sums and products.

Name: GroupWithZero
Description: Order inequalities for big operators in ordered GroupWithZero: products of nonneg terms are nonneg (∏ f ≥ 0 when each f ≥ 0), and bounds on products via the AM-GM inequality stated at the Finset level. The zero-product property with order. Distinct from Group (no zero element) and Ring (adds distributivity).

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Group-with-zero big operators over Multisets.

Name: List.lean
Description: Group-with-zero big operators over Lists.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Group-with-zero big operators over Finsets.

Name: Group
Description: Order inequalities for big operators in ordered groups: if f ≤ g pointwise then ∑ f ≤ ∑ g (monotonicity), and the triangle inequality |∑ f| ≤ ∑ |f| for groups with absolute value. Used throughout analysis for bounding sums. Distinct from Ring and GroupWithZero variants (which additionally involve multiplication).

Name: LocallyFinite.lean
Description: Ordered big operators over locally finite sets.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Ordered big operators over Multisets.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Ordered big operators over Finsets.

Name: List.lean
Description: Ordered big operators over Lists.

Name: Expect.lean
Description: Expected value as a big operator: `Finset.expect s f = (∑ x in s, f x) / s.card` — the average.

Name: CauSeq
Description: Cauchy sequences in ordered fields: the CauSeq type (sequences where ∀ ε > 0, ∃ N, m n > N → |aₘ - aₙ| < ε), their equivalence relation, and the quotient completing the field. The algebraic foundation for the construction of ℝ from ℚ. Distinct from Topology/UniformSpace — this uses order rather than entourages.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion via Cauchy sequences: `CauSeq.Completion` — the quotient of Cauchy sequences by eventual equivalence, completing an ordered field.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for Cauchy sequences: `CauSeq.sum` and summability in the Cauchy sequence setting.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cauchy sequences in ordered fields: `CauSeq α β` — sequences satisfying the Cauchy criterion, the base for constructing ℝ.

Name: Archimedean
Description: The Archimedean property for ordered algebraic structures: ∀ x y > 0, ∃ n : ℕ, y ≤ n • x. Contains the Archimedean typeclass, the embedding of ℕ as cofinal in an Archimedean group, and the characterization of ℝ as the unique complete Archimedean ordered field.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Archimedean homomorphisms: order-preserving maps preserving the Archimedean property.

Name: IndicatorCard.lean
Description: Cardinality via Archimedean indicator: card bounds derived from the Archimedean property.

Name: Submonoid.lean
Description: Archimedean submonoids: submonoids of an Archimedean ordered group.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Archimedean ordered fields: `ArchimedeanField` — `∀ x, ∃ n : ℕ, x < n`. Includes ℝ, ℚ and their subfields.

Name: Class.lean
Description: Archimedean typeclass: `Archimedean α` — the typeclass for ordered groups satisfying the Archimedean property.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of the Archimedean property: `Archimedean α` and its basic consequences.

Name: AbsoluteValue
Description: Absolute values on rings: the AbsoluteValue typeclass |·| : R → S satisfying positivity, multiplicativity, and the triangle inequality. Subsumes both the real absolute value and p-adic valuations. Distinct from norm — absolute values are multiplicative, norms are not required to be.

Name: Euclidean.lean
Description: Euclidean absolute values: absolute values on Euclidean domains and their compatibility with the Euclidean algorithm.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The AbsoluteValue typeclass: a map |·| : α → β satisfying positivity, multiplicativity |ab| = |a||b|, and the triangle inequality |a + b| ≤ |a| + |b|. The triangle inequality formalizes that the straight-line path is shortest — a universal fact that holds in any metric space and underlies virtually all estimates in analysis. Contains instances for the standard absolute value on ℤ, ℚ, ℝ, and p-adic absolute values.

Name: Antidiag
Description: Antidiagonal Finsets: Finset.antidiag n = {(i,j) | i + j = n}, used to expand products of formal power series and polynomials (the coefficient of Xⁿ in f·g is ∑_{i+j=n} fᵢgⱼ). Distinct from BigOperators — this is the index set, not the summation machinery.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi antidiagonal: `Pi.antidiagonal n` — functions `ι → ℕ` summing to `n`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product antidiagonal: `Prod.antidiagonal n` — pairs summing to `n` in a product ordered monoid.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Nat antidiagonal: `Nat.antidiagonal n` — pairs `(i, j)` with `i + j = n`.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Antidiagonal for Finsupp: `Finsupp.antidiagonal n` — pairs `(f, g)` of Finsupps with `f + g = n`.

Name: FinsuppEquiv.lean
Description: Equivalences for Finsupp antidiagonals: bijections between `Finsupp.antidiagonal` and structured index sets.

Name: GroupWithZero
Description: Ordered GroupWithZero: structures combining a GroupWithZero (nonzero elements form a group) with an order compatible with multiplication. Subsumes ordered fields when restricted to nonzero elements, and ordered semirings in the additive direction.

Name: Unbundled
Description: Unbundled ordered GroupWithZero lemmas: explicit-hypothesis versions of the ordered GroupWithZero theory, parallel to Algebra/Order/Group/Unbundled. Used to avoid typeclass diamonds when ordered GroupWithZero instances would conflict.

Name: OrderIso.lean
Description: Order isomorphisms for ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered group-with-zero definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered group-with-zero basics.

Name: Action
Description: Ordered GroupWithZero actions: scalar multiplication by an element of an ordered GroupWithZero on an ordered type, with order-compatibility and the behaviour at zero (0 • x = 0). Used for ordered modules where the scalar field includes zero.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Action synonyms for ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Submonoid.lean
Description: Submonoids of ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Range.lean
Description: Range in ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: WithZero.lean
Description: WithZero construction for ordered groups.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Type synonyms for ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Canonical.lean
Description: Canonical ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds in ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: Lexicographic order on groups-with-zero.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Finset operations in ordered groups-with-zero.

Name: Hom
Description: Order-preserving algebra homomorphisms: monotone MonoidHoms and ring homomorphisms between ordered algebraic structures. Contains the OrderMonoidHom type and its composition. Distinct from Order/GaloisConnection (adjoint pairs) and plain Hom folders (no order).

Name: Submonoid.lean
Description: Order homs for submonoids.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tag instances for order homs.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Order homs for units.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Order-preserving ring homomorphisms.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order-preserving homomorphisms.

Name: Monoid.lean
Description: Order-preserving monoid homomorphisms.

Name: MonoidWithZero.lean
Description: Order-preserving monoid-with-zero homomorphisms.

Name: Group
Description: Ordered groups: OrderedAddCommGroup and OrderedCommGroup, groups with a compatible order where a ≤ b → c + a ≤ c + b. Contains absolute value |a| = max(a, -a), the positive and negative parts, and the lattice-ordered group structure. Distinct from Order/Field (no division) and Order/Ring (no multiplication).

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise order on sets of ordered group elements: the order A ≤ B (every a ∈ A is ≤ some b ∈ B, or similar) and set-arithmetic inequalities A + C ≤ B + C when A ≤ B in an ordered group. Used in interval arithmetic and in bounding sumsets.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Intervals in pointwise ordered groups.

Name: CompleteLattice.lean
Description: Complete lattice for pointwise ordered groups.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Pointwise bounds in ordered groups.

Name: Unbundled
Description: Unbundled ordered group lemmas: consequences of ordered group axioms stated without typeclass bundling, using explicit hypothesis variables instead of instance inference. Used when the typeclass hierarchy would create diamonds or universe issues, or when the ordered group properties are needed in a context where instances are not available. The 'escape hatch' for the bundled theory in Order/Group.

Name: Int.lean
Description: ℤ unbundled ordered group.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unbundled ordered group lemmas: basic inequalities without the full `OrderedAddCommGroup` typeclass.

Name: Abs.lean
Description: Unbundled absolute value: `|x|` defined without the ordered group typeclass, for Lean's general `LinearOrder`.

Name: Action
Description: Ordered group actions: SMul instances on an ordered type where the scalar is an ordered group element, satisfying order-compatibility (a ≤ b and 0 ≤ c implies c • a ≤ c • b). Used in ordered modules and in the interaction between a linear order and scalar multiplication.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Action synonyms for ordered groups.

Name: Flag.lean
Description: Flag actions in ordered groups.

Name: End.lean
Description: Ordered group action on endomorphisms.

Name: Int
Description: Ordered group results specialized to ℤ: the interaction between the integer group structure and its linear order — properties like a ≤ b ↔ a + 1 ≤ b for integers, and the floor/ceiling in terms of integer subtraction. Distinct from Data/Int/Order (which covers the order typeclass instances for ℤ) — this folder contains the algebraic consequences of ℤ being an ordered group.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Sums in ordered ℤ.

Name: MinMax.lean
Description: Min/max in ordered groups.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Multisets in ordered groups.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: ℕ as an ordered group: the canonical ordered additive monoid structure on ℕ.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator functions and ordered groups.

Name: Int.lean
Description: ℤ as an ordered group.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice-ordered groups: `LatticeOrderedGroup` — compatible lattice and group structures; `x ⊔ y + x ⊓ y = x + y`.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Opposite of an ordered group.

Name: PosPart.lean
Description: Positive and negative parts in lattice-ordered groups: `x⁺ = x ⊔ 0`.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Ordered group type synonyms.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in ordered groups.

Name: OrderIso.lean
Description: Order isomorphisms for ordered groups: `x ↦ a + x` is an order isomorphism.

Name: PartialSups.lean
Description: Partial suprema in ordered groups.

Name: PiLex.lean
Description: Lexicographic order on pi ordered groups.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds in ordered groups: `sup` and `inf` in lattice-ordered groups.

Name: CompleteLattice.lean
Description: Complete lattice ordered groups: `iSup` and `iInf` in a complete lattice-ordered abelian group.

Name: Cone.lean
Description: Positive cone: `OrderedGroup.cone G` — the set of non-negative elements defining the order.

Name: Abs.lean
Description: Absolute value in ordered groups: `|x| = max x (-x)`, `|x + y| ≤ |x| + |y|`, and abs-related lemmas.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Ordered group actions: `OrderedSMul` — scalar multiplication is monotone.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core ordered group API: `add_lt_add`, `neg_lt_neg`, and basic ordered-group manipulations.

Name: Cyclic.lean
Description: Cyclic ordered groups: `ℤ` and `ℤ/nℤ` as ordered groups.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Ordered group isomorphisms: `OrderIso` that are also group isomorphisms.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Ordered group operations on Finsets: `Finset.sup`, `Finset.inf`, and products in an ordered group.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in ordered groups: convex subgroups as 'ideals' in a lattice-ordered group.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for ordered groups: `OrderedAddCommGroup`, `LinearOrderedGroup`, and their axioms.

Name: DenselyOrdered.lean
Description: Densely ordered groups: an ordered group where between any two elements there is another.

Name: End.lean
Description: Ordered group endomorphisms: `OrderedAddCommGroup.End` as an ordered ring.

Name: Field
Description: Ordered fields: LinearOrderedField, the typeclass combining a field with a linear order where a > 0 and b > 0 implies ab > 0. Contains division inequalities, absolute value in a field, and the floor/ceiling in terms of division. Distinct from Order/Group (no multiplication) and Order/Ring (no inverses).

Name: Power.lean
Description: Powers in ordered fields: `x^n > 0` for `x > 0`, monotonicity of `x^n` in `n` and `x`.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise order operations in a field: `s * t`, `s / t` for `Set F` with order properties.

Name: Subfield.lean
Description: Subfields of ordered fields: an ordered subfield inherits the order.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ as an ordered field: `Rat.instLinearOrderedField` and specific rational number inequalities.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Ordered field structure on pi types: pointwise order and division on `Π i, F`.

Name: Canonical.lean
Description: Canonical ordered field facts: universal results holding in any ordered field (e.g., ℚ embeds into every characteristic-0 ordered field).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ordered fields — basic API: division lemmas, `div_pos`, `inv_lt`, and the interaction of order with field operations.

Name: GeomSum.lean
Description: Geometric sums in ordered fields: `∑_{k=0}^{n-1} r^k = (1 - rⁿ) / (1 - r)` and bounds.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of ordered fields: `LinearOrderedField`, `StrictOrderedField`, and their interaction with division.

Name: Floor
Description: Floor and ceiling functions in ordered rings: FloorRing with ⌊x⌋ = max {n : ℤ | n ≤ x} and CeilRing with ⌈x⌉ = min {n : ℤ | x ≤ n}. Contains fract (fractional part), Int.fract, and the properties ⌊x + n⌋ = ⌊x⌋ + n. Distinct from Data/Nat — these are integer-valued functions on ordered rings, not arithmetic on ℕ.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Floor ring API: `Int.floor_add_nat`, `Int.fract`, and the interaction between floor and ring operations.

Name: Semifield.lean
Description: Floor in a semifield: `Nat.floor` for `ℝ≥0` and semifield-specific floor lemmas.

Name: Semiring.lean
Description: Floor in a semiring: `FloorSemiring` typeclass and `Nat.floor`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Floor and ceiling definitions: `FloorRing` typeclass, `⌊x⌋` and `⌈x⌉`, basic axioms.

Name: Div.lean
Description: Floor division: `Nat.fdiv`, `Int.fdiv`, `Int.ediv` and their properties.

Name: Extended.lean
Description: Extended floor: floor function for `ℝ≥0∞` and `EReal`.

Name: Rearrangement.lean
Description: The rearrangement inequality: for sequences aᵢ and bᵢ sorted in the same order, ∑ aᵢbᵢ is maximised; if sorted in opposite orders, it is minimised. Distinct from Order/Chebyshev and Order/Monovary.

Name: Round.lean
Description: Rounding in ordered groups: round x = ⌊x + 1/2⌋ (the nearest integer), with basic properties and the interaction with floor and ceiling. Distinct from Order/Pi and Order/CompleteField.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: Ordered structure on PUnit: the trivial ordered algebraic structure on PUnit with a single element. Distinct from Order/Pi and Group/PUnit.

Name: Quantale.lean
Description: Quantales: complete lattices with an associative operation distributing over all joins, used in domain theory and topology. Contains the Quantale typeclass and basic instances. Distinct from Order/Algebra and Order/Kleene.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Successor and predecessor in ordered algebraic structures: the SuccOrder and PredOrder instances for ordered groups and rings where successor/predecessor make sense. Distinct from Order/Pi and Order/Algebra.

Name: UpperLower.lean
Description: Upper and lower sets in ordered algebraic structures: order ideals, filters, and their interaction with the algebraic operations. Distinct from Order/Pi and Order/Algebra.

Name: ZeroLEOne.lean
Description: The ZeroLEOne typeclass: ordered algebraic structures satisfying 0 ≤ 1, and the basic consequences for multiplication and the interaction with positivity. Distinct from Order/Algebra and Ring/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Ordered sum types: the lexicographic order on α ⊕ β (with all of α before β) and the interaction with algebraic operations. Distinct from Order/Pi and Sums/Basic.

Name: ToIntervalMod.lean
Description: Projection to an interval modulo a period: the map x ↦ x mod T landing in [a, a+T), used for fractional parts and circular arithmetic. Distinct from Order/Round and Group/ModEq.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Ordered Pi types: the pointwise order on ∏ i, f i and basic order results for Pi-indexed algebraic structures. Distinct from Order/Algebra and Module/Pi.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Ordered algebras: the OrderedAlgebra typeclass combining an ordered ring and an algebra structure, with the positivity condition on the algebraMap. Distinct from Order/Ring (ordered rings) and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Chebyshev.lean
Description: The Chebyshev sum inequality: for sequences aᵢ and bᵢ monotone in the same direction, n ∑ aᵢbᵢ ≥ (∑ aᵢ)(∑ bᵢ). Distinct from Order/Rearrangement and BigOperators/Fin.

Name: AddGroupWithTop.lean
Description: Ordered additive groups with a top element: the AddGroupWithTop typeclass (ordered additive group augmented with +∞) and the interaction of +∞ with addition and subtraction. Distinct from Order/WithTop (the lattice structure) and Order/Pi.

Name: AddTorsor.lean
Description: Ordered add torsors: an add torsor P over an ordered group G, with the induced order on P making translations order-preserving. Distinct from AddTorsor/Basic and Order/Pi.

Name: CompleteField.lean
Description: Complete ordered fields: the CompleteLinearOrder structure on a field (a field that is also a conditionally complete linear order), characterising ℝ up to isomorphism. Distinct from Order/Algebra and Field/Basic.

Name: Kleene.lean
Description: Kleene algebras: an idempotent semiring with a star operation a* = ∑ᵢ aⁱ satisfying 1 + aa* ≤ a* and completeness. Used in regular languages and program verification. Distinct from Order/Algebra and Ring/Idempotent.

Name: Monovary.lean
Description: Monovariance conditions: two sequences (or functions) are monovariant if they vary in the same direction. Contains the Monovary and Antivary predicates, used in the rearrangement inequality. Distinct from Order/Rearrangement and Order/Pi.

Name: Disjointed.lean
Description: Disjointedness conditions in ordered algebraic structures: elements satisfying a ⊓ b = 0 in a lattice ordered group, with the disjoint decomposition. Distinct from Order/Pi and Order/AddGroupWithTop.

Name: Invertible.lean
Description: Invertible elements in ordered algebraic structures: conditions under which the Invertible typeclass interacts with the ordering, and positivity of explicitly inverted elements. Distinct from Ring/Invertible (ring setting) and GroupWithZero/Invertible.

Name: LieRinehartAlgebra
Description: Lie-Rinehart algebras (Lie algebroids in algebraic form): pairs (A, L) of a commutative ring and a Lie algebra acting on A via derivations.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Lie-Rinehart algebras (Lie algebroids over a commutative ring): a pair (A, L) where L is an A-module and Lie algebra acting on A via a derivation ρ : L → Der(A), with the Leibniz rule. Generalises Lie algebras (A = k) and tangent sheaves. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Algebra/Defs.

Name: Lie
Description: Lie algebras over a commutative ring: the bracket operation, the Jacobi identity, ideals, and the universal enveloping algebra.

Name: Semisimple
Description: Semisimple Lie algebras: Lie algebras with trivial radical (no nonzero solvable ideals). Defines IsSemisimple, IsSimple, and HasTrivialRadical, and proves structural properties including that the lattice of ideals of a semisimple algebra forms a Boolean algebra. Provides the foundational definitions toward the Cartan-Killing classification; the ADE classification theorem is a goal not yet formalized. Distinct from Weights (representation-theoretic weight-space theory) and RootSystem (the associated combinatorial structure).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for semisimple Lie algebras: properties of the radical, Levi decomposition context.

Name: Defs.lean
