Description: Definition of semisimple Lie algebras: `LieAlgebra.IsSemisimple` — no nonzero abelian ideals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Semisimple Lie algebras — main API: `IsSemisimple L` — `L` is a direct sum of simple ideals; the Cartan criterion.

Name: Weights
Description: Weight theory for Lie algebra representations: a weight is a linear functional λ : H → k on a Cartan subalgebra H, with weight space Vλ = {v | h·v = λ(h)v for all h ∈ H}. Contains Weight, rootSpace, coroot, rootSystem, chainLength, and isSl2Triple. Develops the root system framework and weight-space decomposition needed as groundwork for the representation theory of semisimple Lie algebras. Distinct from AdjointAction (the self-representation ad : L → End L) and Semisimple (the algebra structure).

Name: Killing.lean
Description: Weights and the Killing form: the inner product on weight spaces induced by the Killing form.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Linear functionals as weights: weights are characters of the Cartan subalgebra, elements of `H*`.

Name: RootSystem.lean
Description: Root system of a semisimple Lie algebra: the roots form a root system in the dual of the Cartan subalgebra.

Name: IsSimple.lean
Description: Weights of simple modules: simple Lie algebra modules have a unique highest weight.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Weight spaces: `LieAlgebra.weight_space M χ = {m | ∀ x ∈ H, ρ(x)m = χ(x)m}` for a Cartan subalgebra `H`.

Name: Cartan.lean
Description: Cartan subalgebras and weight theory: the weight space decomposition `M = ⊕_χ M_χ` under a Cartan subalgebra.

Name: Chain.lean
Description: Root strings and weight chains: the `α`-string through weight `λ` is `{λ + kα | k ∈ ℤ} ∩ weights`.

Name: AdjointAction
Description: The adjoint representation of a Lie algebra: the action of L on itself by ad(x)(y) = ⁅x, y⁆, making L a module over itself. Contains the adjoint module, the Killing form B(x,y) = Tr(ad x ∘ ad y), and key structural lemmas including LieAlgebra.ad_nilpotent_of_nilpotent and LieAlgebra.ad_isSemisimple_of_isSemisimple, which are central components of Cartan's criterion for solvability and semisimplicity. Distinct from Weights and Semisimple (which extend representation theory beyond the adjoint).

Name: JordanChevalley.lean
Description: Jordan–Chevalley decomposition via adjoint action: `ad x = ad xₛ + ad xₙ` with semisimple `xₛ` and nilpotent `xₙ`.

Name: Derivation.lean
Description: Adjoint action as derivations: `ad x` is a derivation of `L`; the adjoint representation lands in `Der(L)`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Adjoint action of a Lie algebra: `LieAlgebra.adjoint L` — the representation `ad : L → End L` given by `ad x y = ⁅x, y⁆`.

Name: Derivation
Description: Derivations of Lie algebras: linear maps D : L → M satisfying D(⁅x,y⁆) = ⁅Dx,y⁆ + ⁅x,Dy⁆. The module of derivations Der(L), and the fact that inner derivations ad(x) form an ideal. Distinct from NonAssoc/LieAdmissible — derivations here act on the Lie algebra, not on an ambient associative algebra.

Name: Killing.lean
Description: Killing form via derivations: the Killing form `κ(x, y) = tr(ad x ∘ ad y)` and its non-degeneracy for semisimple algebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Derivations of a Lie algebra: `LieDerivation R A M` — `R`-linear Lie algebra derivations and their Lie bracket.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change of Lie derivations: `Der(A) ⊗_R S ≅ Der(A ⊗_R S)` for commutative algebras.

Name: OfAssociative.lean
Description: The Lie algebra of an associative algebra: for any associative R-algebra A, the commutator bracket [a, b] = ab - ba makes A into a Lie algebra. Contains the LieRing.ofAssociativeRing instance. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Lie/Matrix.

Name: Normalizer.lean
Description: The normalizer of a Lie subalgebra: Nor_L(H) = {x ∈ L | [x, H] ⊆ H}, and its role in the characterisation of Cartan subalgebras. Distinct from Lie/Subalgebra and Lie/CartanSubalgebra.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Products of Lie algebras: the Lie algebra structure on L₁ × L₂ with componentwise bracket and the projection Lie homomorphisms. Distinct from Lie/DirectSum and Lie/Basic.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank of a Lie algebra `L` over a nontrivial commutative ring `R` (with `L` finite free as `R`-module): the smallest `n` for which the `n`-th coefficient of the characteristic polynomial of `ad R L x` is not the zero polynomial. Regular elements are those for which this coefficient is nonzero.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient Lie algebras: the quotient L/I by a Lie ideal I with the induced bracket, and the first isomorphism theorem for Lie algebras. Distinct from Lie/Ideal and Lie/Basic.

Name: Loop.lean
Description: Loop Lie algebra: the base change `L ⊗_R R[t, t⁻¹]` of a Lie `R`-algebra `L` with the ring of Laurent polynomials. Central extensions attached to invariant inner products. When `L` is finite-dimensional simple, yields untwisted affine Kac-Moody Lie algebras. Implemented via `AddMonoidAlgebra`.

Name: LieTheorem.lean
Description: Lie's theorem: every finite-dimensional representation of a solvable Lie algebra over an algebraically closed field of characteristic 0 has a common eigenvector. Distinct from Lie/Solvable and Lie/Nilpotent.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Lie algebras of matrices: the Lie algebra structure on Mₙ(R) with the commutator bracket [A, B] = AB - BA, and the general and special linear Lie algebras gₗₙ(R) and sₗₙ(R). Distinct from Lie/Classical and Lie/Basic.

Name: NonUnitalNonAssocAlgebra.lean
Description: Non-unital non-associative algebras: the general typeclass for R-modules with a bilinear multiplication satisfying no further axioms, and the Lie algebra as a special case. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Jordan/Basic.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Nilpotent Lie algebras: a Lie algebra is nilpotent if its lower central series Lⁿ terminates at 0, with the IsNilpotent typeclass and the nilpotency class. Distinct from Lie/Solvable (derived series) and Lie/Engel.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Lie submodules: sub-R-modules of a Lie module M that are also closed under the Lie action (x • m ∈ N for x ∈ L, m ∈ N). Contains LieSubmodule, the quotient, and the lattice. Distinct from Lie/Ideal (ideals are Lie submodules of L acting on itself) and Lie/Basic.

Name: Subalgebra.lean
Description: Lie subalgebras: sub-R-modules of L closed under the Lie bracket, the LieSubalgebra type, the lattice of subalgebras, and the span of a set. Distinct from Lie/Ideal (subalgebras that are also ideals) and Lie/Basic.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of Lie modules: the tensor product M ⊗_R N of two Lie L-modules with the diagonal action x • (m ⊗ n) = (x • m) ⊗ n + m ⊗ (x • n). Distinct from Lie/Basic and Algebra/Bilinear.

Name: UniversalEnveloping.lean
Description: The universal enveloping algebra U(L) of a Lie algebra: the associative algebra with the universal property that Lie algebra maps L → A (A associative) correspond to algebra maps U(L) → A. Contains the PBW theorem. Distinct from Lie/Basic and FreeAlgebra/Cardinality.

Name: TraceForm.lean
Description: The trace form of a Lie algebra representation: the bilinear form B_ρ(x, y) = Tr(ρ(x) ∘ ρ(y)) for a representation ρ, and the Killing form as the trace form of the adjoint representation. Distinct from Lie/Killing and Lie/InvariantForm.

Name: SerreConstruction.lean
Description: The Serre construction of semisimple Lie algebras: constructing a semisimple Lie algebra from a Cartan matrix via generators and the Serre relations. Distinct from Lie/CartanMatrix and Lie/Free.

Name: SemiDirect.lean
Description: Semidirect products of Lie algebras: the semidirect product I ⋊ L for a Lie algebra L acting on an ideal I, with the Lie bracket (i₁, l₁), (i₂, l₂)] = ([l₁, i₂] - [l₂, i₁] + [i₁, i₂], [l₁, l₂]). Distinct from Lie/Ideal and Lie/Extension.

Name: SkewAdjoint.lean
Description: `skewAdjointLieSubalgebra`: the Lie subalgebra of endomorphisms that are skew-adjoint with respect to a given bilinear form on a module. Provides simple, explicit constructions of the classical Lie algebras (sp, so, etc.).

Name: Solvable.lean
Description: Solvable Lie algebras: a Lie algebra is solvable if its derived series L⁽⁰⁾ ⊇ L⁽¹⁾ = [L,L] ⊇ L⁽²⁾ = [L⁽¹⁾,L⁽¹⁾] ⊇ … terminates at 0, with extensions and quotients. Distinct from Lie/Nilpotent (lower central series) and Lie/IdealOperations.

Name: Sl2.lean
Description: The Lie algebra sl₂(k): the three-dimensional Lie algebra with basis {e, f, h} and brackets [h, e] = 2e, [h, f] = -2f, [e, f] = h. Contains the structure, representations, and the sl₂-triple theory. Distinct from Lie/Classical and Lie/Matrix.

Name: Killing.lean
Description: The Killing form of a Lie algebra: the symmetric invariant bilinear form κ(x, y) = Tr(ad x ∘ ad y), with the criterion for semisimplicity (κ non-degenerate). Distinct from Lie/TraceForm and Lie/CartanCriterion.

Name: CartanMatrix.lean
Description: Deprecated module (since 2025-12-13). Cartan matrix content has been moved to `Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Cartan`.

Name: CartanExists.lean
Description: Existence of Cartan subalgebras: every finite-dimensional Lie algebra over an algebraically closed field of characteristic 0 has a Cartan subalgebra. Distinct from Lie/CartanSubalgebra and Lie/Basic.

Name: CartanSubalgebra.lean
Description: Cartan subalgebras: maximal toral (ad-semisimple) nilpotent subalgebras, with the Cartan decomposition L = H ⊕ ⊕_α L_α into root spaces. Distinct from Lie/CartanExists and Lie/Nilpotent.

Name: Classical.lean
Description: The classical Lie algebras: the special linear algebra sₗₙ(k), the orthogonal algebras soₙ(k), and the symplectic algebras sp₂ₙ(k), with their root systems and Dynkin diagrams. Distinct from Lie/Matrix and Lie/CartanMatrix.

Name: Character.lean
Description: Characters of Lie algebra representations: one-dimensional representations (Lie algebra homomorphisms L → k) and the character group. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Lie/TraceForm.

Name: BaseChange.lean
Description: Base change for Lie algebras: the scalar extension L ⊗_R S of a Lie R-algebra L to a Lie S-algebra. Distinct from Lie/TensorProduct and Lie/Basic.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Abelian Lie algebras: Lie algebras where the bracket is identically zero ([x, y] = 0 for all x, y). Contains the IsLieAbelian typeclass, basic instances, and the abelianisation of a Lie algebra. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Lie/Solvable.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for Lie algebras: the LieRing typeclass (an additive group with a Lie bracket satisfying bilinearity, alternativity, and the Jacobi identity) and the LieAlgebra R L typeclass over a commutative ring R. Foundation for all Lie files.

Name: CartanCriterion.lean
Description: Cartan's criterion for solvability: a Lie algebra over a field of characteristic 0 is solvable iff the Killing form on [L, L] is zero. Distinct from Lie/Killing and Lie/Solvable.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: Bases for Lie algebras: the structure of a Lie algebra with a specified basis and the structure constants. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Lie/Free.

Name: Graded.lean
Description: Graded Lie algebras: Lie algebras with a ℤ-grading (or ℕ-grading) compatible with the bracket [Lᵢ, Lⱼ] ⊆ Lᵢ₊ⱼ. Distinct from Lie/Basic and GradedObject/Monoidal.

Name: Free.lean
Description: The free Lie algebra on a type: the universal Lie algebra generated by a set X with no relations, with the universal property and the connection to the free associative algebra via the commutator bracket. Distinct from Lie/Basic and FreeAlgebra/Cardinality.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Lie ideals: two-sided ideals I ⊆ L satisfying [L, I] ⊆ I, the quotient L/I, and the Lie algebra homomorphism theorem. Distinct from Lie/Subalgebra (subalgebras, which need not be ideals) and Lie/Basic.

Name: InvariantForm.lean
Description: Invariant bilinear forms on Lie algebras: a bilinear form β : L × L → k is invariant if β([x, y], z) = β(x, [y, z]) for all x, y, z. Distinct from Lie/TraceForm (which defines the Killing form) and Lie/Basic.

Name: IdealOperations.lean
Description: Operations on Lie ideals: the Lie bracket [I, J] of two ideals, the derived series [L, [L, [L, …]]], and the lower central series. Distinct from Lie/Ideal and Lie/Solvable.

Name: DirectSum.lean
Description: Direct sums of Lie algebras: the direct sum L₁ ⊕ L₂ with componentwise bracket, and the interaction with Lie ideal theory. Distinct from Lie/Basic and DirectSum/Basic.

Name: Cochain.lean
Description: Lie algebra cohomology cochains: the Chevalley-Eilenberg cochain complex C^n(L, M) = Hom(∧^n L, M) with the coboundary operator, whose cohomology is the Lie algebra cohomology H^n(L, M). Distinct from Lie/Basic and Homology/HomologicalComplex.

Name: Engel.lean
Description: Engel's theorem: if every element of a Lie algebra L acts nilpotently in the adjoint representation (ad x is nilpotent for all x), then L is nilpotent. Distinct from Lie/Nilpotent and Lie/EngelSubalgebra.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extensions of Lie algebras: a short exact sequence 0 → I → L → L/I → 0, with the classification via H²(L/I, I) and the associated 2-cocycle. Distinct from Lie/Ideal and Lie/Cochain.

Name: EngelSubalgebra.lean
Description: `LieSubalgebra.Engel R x`: the Engel subalgebra — all `y : L` killed by `(ad R L x)^n` for some `n`. `normalizer_engel`: Engel subalgebras are self-normalizing. Minimal Engel subalgebras are nilpotent and hence Cartan subalgebras (TODO).

Name: Jordan
Description: Jordan algebras: commutative non-associative algebras satisfying the Jordan identity (xy)(xx) = x(y(xx)).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Jordan algebras: the Jordan algebra typeclass (a commutative non-associative algebra satisfying the Jordan identity (a² * b) * a = a² * (b * a)), with basic instances and the connection to associative algebras. Distinct from Lie/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: MvPolynomial
Description: Multivariate polynomials in Algebra: the MvPolynomial type with ring operations, evaluation, and symmetric polynomial theory.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Nilpotent elements in multivariate polynomial rings: a polynomial p is nilpotent iff all its coefficients are nilpotent. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: NoZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Zero divisors in multivariate polynomial rings: MvPolynomial σ R is a domain if R is a domain. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Monad.lean
Description: The monad structure on MvPolynomial: MvPolynomial is a monad on CommRing (or CommAlgebra), with the monad operations given by substitution. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Monad/Basic.

Name: Funext.lean
Description: Functional extensionality for polynomials: two polynomials over an infinite integral domain that agree on all inputs are equal. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Eval.

Name: Invertible.lean
Description: Invertible elements in multivariate polynomial rings: units and the interaction with the invertible elements of the coefficient ring. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Ring/Invertible.

Name: PDeriv.lean
Description: Partial derivatives of multivariate polynomials: the partial derivative ∂/∂xᵢ : MvPolynomial σ R → MvPolynomial σ R, with the Leibniz rule and the Taylor expansion. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Derivation and MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Supported.lean
Description: Support-bounded multivariate polynomials: polynomials supported on a subset of σ (using only variables in a specified set), with the restriction and extension maps. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Variables and MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Variables.lean
Description: Variables of multivariate polynomials: the set of variables actually appearing in a polynomial (vars p ⊆ σ), with the interaction with operations and the degree bounds. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Coeff and MvPolynomial/Degrees.

Name: SchwartzZippel.lean
Description: The Schwartz-Zippel lemma: a nonzero polynomial of total degree d over a field k vanishes on at most d/|S| fraction of random points in Sⁿ for a finite set S. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Funext.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: The isomorphism MvPolynomial (Fin 1) R ≅ Polynomial R and more generally MvPolynomial (Fin n) R ≅ Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin (n-1)) R). Distinct from MvPolynomial/Equiv and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Rename.lean
Description: Renaming variables in multivariate polynomials: the ring map MvPolynomial.rename (f : σ → τ) : MvPolynomial σ R → MvPolynomial τ R substituting variable names. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Eval and MvPolynomial/Comap.

Name: Expand.lean
Description: Expanding multivariate polynomials: the expand n : MvPolynomial σ R → MvPolynomial σ R map substituting xᵢ^n for each variable xᵢ. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Expand.

Name: Comap.lean
Description: Pulling back along a variable map: given f : τ → σ, the algebra map MvPolynomial σ R → MvPolynomial τ R substituting f(i) for each variable xᵢ. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Rename and MvPolynomial/Eval.

Name: CommRing.lean
Description: The commutative ring structure on MvPolynomial σ R when R is a commutative ring: additional instances and commutativity-dependent lemmas. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Coeff.lean
Description: Coefficients of multivariate polynomials: the coefficient coeff m p of monomial m in p, its interaction with ring operations, and the MvPolynomial.coeff linear map. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Degrees.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of multivariate polynomials: MvPolynomial σ R = σ →₀ ℕ →₀ R with the ring structure, variables X i, monomials, and the universal property (any assignment of variables extends to a ring map). Foundation for all MvPolynomial files.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: Cardinality of multivariate polynomial rings: |MvPolynomial σ R| = max(|σ|, |R|, ℵ₀) for infinite σ or R. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: Counit.lean
Description: The counit of the MvPolynomial adjunction: the natural transformation MvPolynomial σ (MvPolynomial τ R) → MvPolynomial (σ ⊕ τ) R. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Monad.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Ring equivalences of multivariate polynomial rings: MvPolynomial (σ ⊕ τ) R ≅ MvPolynomial σ (MvPolynomial τ R) and permutation of variables. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Rename.

Name: Eval.lean
Description: Evaluation of multivariate polynomials: eval₂ (substituting variables) and eval (evaluating at a point), with the interaction with ring maps and the universal property. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and MvPolynomial/Rename.

Name: Division.lean
Description: Division in multivariate polynomial rings: divisibility and the interaction with the grading. Distinct from MvPolynomial/Basic and Polynomial/Div.

Name: Degrees.lean
Description: Degree bounds in multivariate polynomials: the total degree, individual variable degrees, and the interaction with multiplication (deg(fg) ≤ deg(f) + deg(g)). Distinct from MvPolynomial/Coeff and MvPolynomial/Variables.

Name: Derivation.lean
Description: Derivations of multivariate polynomial rings: R-derivations D : MvPolynomial σ R → M are determined by their values on variables. Distinct from MvPolynomial/PDeriv and MvPolynomial/Basic.

Name: MonoidAlgebra
Description: Monoid algebras R[M]: the free R-module on a monoid M with multiplication extending the monoid operation, generalizing group rings.

Name: NoZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Zero divisors in monoid algebras: MonoidAlgebra k G is a domain if k is a domain and G is a torsion-free cancellative monoid (no zero divisors). Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Division and MonoidAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Module structure on monoid algebras: MonoidAlgebra k G as a k-module and the module structure on representations (G-modules as MonoidAlgebra k G-modules). Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: MapDomain.lean
Description: Functoriality of monoid algebras: a monoid hom f : G → H induces a ring map MonoidAlgebra k G → MonoidAlgebra k H by mapping the basis elements. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Finsupp files.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite monoid algebra: MonoidAlgebra k Gᵒᵖ ≅ (MonoidAlgebra k G)ᵒᵖ, and the anti-isomorphism. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: ToDirectSum.lean
Description: The isomorphism MonoidAlgebra k G ≅ ⊕_G k (as k-modules): the direct sum decomposition of the monoid algebra into degree components. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and DirectSum/Basic.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of monoid algebra elements: the finite set of G-values where a monoid algebra element is nonzero, and the interaction with multiplication. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Notation/Support.

Name: PointwiseSMul.lean
Description: Pointwise scalar multiplication on monoid algebras: the action of k^× on MonoidAlgebra k G by pointwise scaling. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Lift.lean
Description: The universal property of monoid algebras: a monoid hom G → R^× and a ring map k → R extend uniquely to a ring map MonoidAlgebra k G → R. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and FreeAlgebra/Cardinality.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for monoid algebras: MonoidAlgebra k G = G →₀ k, the free k-module on G with convolution multiplication (f * g)(x) = ∑_{yz=x} f(y)g(z). The foundation for group rings and polynomial rings. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic (derived results).

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: Cardinality of monoid algebras: |MonoidAlgebra k G| = max(|k|, |G|, ℵ₀) for infinite k or G. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of monoid algebras: the ring structure on MonoidAlgebra k G, the inclusion G → MonoidAlgebra k G (as a monoid hom), the augmentation map, and the universal property. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Defs and MonoidAlgebra/Lift.

Name: Degree.lean
Description: Degree on monoid algebras: the degree of a monoid algebra element as the support in G, with a total ordering on G inducing a leading term. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in monoid algebras: augmentation ideals, the group algebra ideal theory, and the connection to subgroup theory. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: Grading.lean
Description: Graded structure on monoid algebras: the ℤ-grading (or G-grading) on MonoidAlgebra k G with Mₐ = k • δₐ. Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and GradedObject/Monoidal.

Name: Division.lean
Description: Division in monoid algebras: when G is a group, MonoidAlgebra k G is a domain if k is a domain and G is torsion-free (Kaplansky's conjecture in special cases). Distinct from MonoidAlgebra/Basic and MonoidAlgebra/NoZeroDivisors.

Name: Module
Description: Modules over (semi)rings: the module typeclass hierarchy, submodules, quotient modules, and module homomorphisms.

Name: Submodule
Description: Submodules: the Submodule type, additive subgroups of M closed under scalar multiplication. The lattice of submodules (inf = intersection, sup = span of union), the span of a set, and quotient modules M/N. The foundational substructure type for all of module theory. Distinct from Module/Equiv and LinearMap which are about morphisms, not sub-objects.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Image and preimage of submodules: `Submodule.map f N` and `Submodule.comap f N`.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Ordered submodule structure: the inclusion order on submodules and its interaction with operations.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice of submodules: intersection as meet, span of union as join; Noetherian / Artinian conditions.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Linear maps between submodules: restriction to submodules and quotient maps.

Name: RestrictScalars.lean
Description: Restricting scalars on submodules: viewing an `S`-submodule as an `R`-submodule.

Name: Union.lean
Description: Submodule union: `Submodule.sup N P` (= generated by `N ∪ P`) vs. set-theoretic union.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise submodule operations: `s • N` as a submodule and scalar-multiplication lemmas.

Name: Range.lean
Description: Range of a linear map as a submodule: `LinearMap.range f = f '' ⊤` and its basic API.

Name: Ker.lean
Description: Kernel and range: `LinearMap.ker`, `LinearMap.range`, first isomorphism theorem for linear maps.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Submodule R M`: a sub-`R`-module, closed under `+`, `0`, and scalar multiplication.

Name: EqLocus.lean
Description: Equality locus: `Submodule.eqLocus f g` — the submodule where two linear maps agree.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for submodules: `Submodule.span`, `Submodule.map`, `Submodule.comap`, lattice, and quotient modules.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Bilinear maps on submodules: the restriction of a bilinear form to a submodule.

Name: Invariant.lean
Description: Invariant submodules: submodules preserved under a family of endomorphisms.

Name: IterateMapComap.lean
Description: Iterated map/comap: `Submodule.iterateMapComap f n` — repeated application of `map` and `comap`.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Linear equivalences of submodules: `Submodule.equivOfEq`, `Submodule.orderIsoMapComap`.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finsupp and submodules: the submodule generated by finitely-supported functions.

Name: StablyFree
Description: Stably free modules: modules M such that M ⊕ Rⁿ ≅ Rᵐ for some n, m. Defines IsStablyFree and proves basic properties including IsStablyFree.of_free_prod. Every free module is stably free, but the converse fails in general; the Quillen-Suslin theorem (projective modules over polynomial rings are free) is not formalized here. Distinct from FreeModule (a stronger condition than stably free) and Projective (every projective module over a polynomial ring is stably free, by Serre's theorem).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Stably free modules: `Module.StablyFree M` — `M ⊕ Rⁿ ≅ Rᵐ` for some `n, m`; properties and Serre's theorem context.

Name: ZLattice
Description: Z-lattices: full-rank free ℤ-submodules Λ ⊆ V of a finite-dimensional real or complex vector space. Contains the covolume, the dual lattice Λ* = {v | ∀ λ ∈ Λ, ⟨v, λ⟩ ∈ ℤ}, and the connection to the geometry of numbers. Distinct from SetTheory/Ordinal lattices — these are geometric lattices in the number-theoretic sense.

Name: Summable.lean
Description: Summability over a ℤ-lattice: conditions under which `∑_{v ∈ L} f(v)` converges.

Name: Covolume.lean
Description: Covolume of a ℤ-lattice: `ZLattice.covolume L` — the volume of the fundamental domain of `L`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: ℤ-lattices: `ZLattice K L` — a full-rank discrete subgroup of `K`-vector space, with fundamental domain and covolume.

Name: Torsion
Description: Torsion theory for modules: the torsion submodule Tor(M) = {m | ∃ r ≠ 0, r·m = 0}, torsion-free modules, and p-torsion for prime p. Contains the annihilator of a module element, the primary decomposition for finitely generated modules over a PID, and torsion elements in the context of Ext groups.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Torsion of pi modules: `torsion (Π i, Mᵢ) = Π i, torsion Mᵢ`.

Name: PrimaryComponent.lean
Description: Primary torsion components: `Module.primaryComponent M p` — the `p`-primary part `{m | p^n • m = 0 for some n}`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Torsion of product modules: `torsion (M × N) = torsion M × torsion N`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Torsion submodule: `Module.torsion R M = {m | ∃ r ≠ 0, r • m = 0}`; basic API and the torsion exact sequence.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Torsion over a field: every module over a field is torsion-free; torsion of field extensions.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Torsion-free modules: `Module.IsTorsionFree M` — zero torsion; characterisation for Noetherian rings.

Name: Presentation
Description: Presentations of modules by generators and relations: a module M is presented by a surjection Rⁿ → M with kernel (relations) K ⊆ Rⁿ. Contains finitely presented modules, the Schanuel lemma, and the relationship to projective resolutions. Distinct from FreeModule (no relations) and StablyFree (weaker freeness condition).

Name: RestrictScalars.lean
Description: Scalar restriction for module presentations: a presentation of an `S`-module viewed as an `R`-module presentation.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free module as a presentation: the canonical presentation of a free module with basis as generators and no relations.

Name: Tensor.lean
Description: Tensor product of presentations: combining two module presentations to get a presentation of the tensor product.

Name: Tautological.lean
Description: Tautological presentation: the presentation of `M` with generators `M` itself and relations from kernel.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite module presentations: `Module.Presentation.finite` — a presentation with finitely many generators and relations.

Name: Cokernel.lean
Description: Cokernel presentation: the cokernel of a linear map as a presented module.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Module presentations: `Module.Presentation R M` — a generator set `G` and relation set `R` defining `M = F(G) / R`.

Name: DirectSum.lean
Description: Direct sum of module presentations: combining two presentations into a presentation of the direct sum.

Name: Differentials.lean
Description: Presentation of the module of differentials: `Ω_{A/R}` via generators `da` and the Leibniz relations.

Name: Equiv
Description: Module isomorphisms: LinearEquiv R M N, bijective linear maps with a two-sided linear inverse. Contains the group of automorphisms, composition, flip, and the canonical equivalences (e.g., M ≃ₗ M ⊕ 0). Distinct from Module/LinearMap (no inverse required) and Group/Equiv (no linearity required).

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Linear equivalences with the opposite ring: `LinearEquiv.toOpposite` — an `R`-module equivalence gives an `Rᵒᵖ`-module equivalence.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `LinearEquiv R M N`: a bijective `R`-linear map with `R`-linear inverse.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for linear equivalences: `LinearEquiv.trans`, `.symm`, the automorphism group `GL(M)`, and standard isomorphisms.

Name: Congruence
Description: Congruences on modules: equivalence relations on M compatible with addition and scalar multiplication, an alternative to submodules for constructing quotient modules. Contains the correspondence between congruences and submodules, and the quotient module construction. Distinct from Module/Submodule which uses the additive subgroup formalism.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Congruence relations on modules: `ModuleCongruence R M` — an additive congruence compatible with scalar multiplication.

Name: LocalizedModule
Description: Localization of modules: given a multiplicative set S ⊆ R and an R-module M, the localized module S⁻¹M with elements m/s. Contains the universal property, the base change isomorphism S⁻¹M ≅ S⁻¹R ⊗_R M, and stalks of quasi-coherent sheaves. Distinct from RingTheory/Localization which localizes the ring itself.

Name: Int.lean
Description: Integer localisation: `LocalizedModule.int M` — localising at the nonzero integers.

Name: IsLocalization.lean
Description: Universal property of localised modules: the characterisation via `IsLocalizedModule`.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Submodules of localised modules: `(S⁻¹R) ⊗_R N` for submodules `N ≤ M`.

Name: Exact.lean
Description: Exactness of localisation: localisation is an exact functor (flat module).

Name: AtPrime.lean
Description: Localisation at a prime ideal: `LocalizedModule.atPrime p M` — `M` localised at the complement of `p`.

Name: Away.lean
Description: Localisation away from an element: `LocalizedModule.away f M` inverting `f` in `M`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Localised modules: `LocalizedModule S M` — the `S⁻¹R`-module of fractions `m/s` with `m ∈ M`, `s ∈ S`.

Name: LinearMap
Description: Linear maps: LinearMap R M N, the morphisms of the category of R-modules. Contains the LinearMap type, kernel, range, the first isomorphism theorem, composition, zero map, identity, and the R-module structure on Hom(M, N). The foundational morphism type for all of linear algebra.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Linear maps on product modules: `LinearMap.prod`, `LinearMap.fst`, `LinearMap.snd`.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomial actions as linear maps: `Polynomial.aeval f` as a linear map via the module structure.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star-linear maps: `LinearMap.star` for star-algebras and `StarLinearEquiv`.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: Linear maps over ℚ: specific results for `ℚ`-linear maps.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `LinearMap R M N`: a function preserving `+` and `r •` with `LinearMapClass` instances.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for linear maps: `LinearMap.comp`, kernel, range, and the module structure on `Hom_R(M, N)`.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphisms: the ring `End_R(M) = LinearMap R M M` and its unit group `GL(M)`.

Name: DivisionRing.lean
Description: Linear maps over division rings: specific results when the scalar ring is a division ring.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projective modules: an R-module P is projective if every surjection M → P splits, equivalently if Hom_R(P, −) is exact. Contains the Projective typeclass, free modules are projective, and lifting results. Distinct from Module/Injective and Module/Basic.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: Module structure on PUnit: the trivial R-module with a single element. Distinct from Module/Basic.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ-module structure: every divisible torsion-free abelian group is a ℚ-vector space, and the canonical ℚ-module structure on ℚ itself. Distinct from Module/Basic and Field/Rat.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Binary product modules: the R-module structure on M × N with componentwise operations. Distinct from Module/Pi (arbitrary products) and Module/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi modules: the R-module structure on ∏ i, M i when each M i is an R-module, with componentwise scalar multiplication. Distinct from Module/Basic and Module/Prod.

Name: PID.lean
Description: Structure theorem for modules over a PID: a finitely generated module M over a PID R decomposes as M ≅ R^r ⊕ R/d₁ ⊕ … ⊕ R/dₖ (with d₁ ∣ d₂ ∣ … ∣ dₖ). Distinct from Module/DedekindDomain and Module/Basic.

Name: PointwisePi.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on pi-type modules: scalar multiplication and addition applied pointwise to functions i → M i. Distinct from Module/Pi and Module/Basic.

Name: RingHom.lean
Description: Linear maps induced by ring homomorphisms: restriction of scalars along a ring map f : R → S, turning an S-module into an R-module via f. Distinct from Module/Basic and Algebra/RestrictScalars.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring module instances along linear equivalences. Distinct from Module/Basic and Ring/TransferInstance.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift for modules: the R-module structure on ULift M. Distinct from Module/Basic.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: ZMod n-modules: every abelian group of exponent dividing n is a ZMod n-module, and the equivalence between ZMod n-modules and ℤ-modules annihilated by n. Distinct from Module/Basic and CharP/Defs.

Name: SpanRankOperations.lean
Description: Operations on span rank: how the rank changes under submodule operations (sum, intersection, quotient). Distinct from Module/SpanRank and Module/Lattice.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-level shrinking for modules. Distinct from Module/Basic and Algebra/Shrink.

Name: SnakeLemma.lean
Description: The snake lemma for modules: a commutative diagram with exact rows gives a six-term exact sequence of cokernels and kernels, with the connecting homomorphism. Distinct from Module/Basic and Abelian/Exact.

Name: SpanRank.lean
Description: The rank of the span of a set: the minimum number of generators for a submodule, and results bounding the rank. Distinct from Module/Basic and Module/SpanRankOperations.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite module: if M is a left R-module, the opposite module M^op is a right R-module, and conversely. Distinct from Module/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: CharacterModule.lean
Description: The character module Hom_ℤ(M, ℚ/ℤ): the Pontryagin dual of an abelian group, with Hom-tensor adjunction and the injectivity of ℚ/ℤ. Distinct from Module/Basic and Module/Injective.

Name: DedekindDomain.lean
Description: Modules over Dedekind domains: the structure theorem for finitely generated modules over a Dedekind domain (classification up to isomorphism via the rank and Steinitz class). Distinct from Module/PID and Module/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for modules: the Module R M typeclass (an additive commutative group M with an R-scalar multiplication satisfying the four module axioms), and the basic instances. Distinct from Module/Basic (derived lemmas) and Module/Hom.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of modules: |M| = |R|^rank for finitely generated free modules over a PID, and basic cardinality results. Distinct from Module/Basic and Module/PID.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic module theory: consequences of the Module typeclass — zero acts as zero, the interaction of smul with negation, subtraction, and addition, and the tensor product identity. Foundation for all Module files. Distinct from Module/Defs.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for modules: ∑ i, c i • x i and the interaction of linear combinations with the module operations, Finset.sum_smul and smul_sum. Distinct from Module/Basic and BigOperators/Module.

Name: Bimodule.lean
Description: Bimodules over rings: an abelian group M that is simultaneously a left R-module and a right S-module with compatible actions (r • (m • s) = (r • m) • s). Distinct from Module/Basic and Monoidal/Bimod.

Name: End.lean
Description: The endomorphism ring of a module: End_R(M) = {f : M →ₗ[R] M} with composition as multiplication, forming a ring. Contains the ring structure and the action of End_R(M) on M. Distinct from Module/Hom and Group/End.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: The lattice of submodules: the complete lattice structure on Submodule R M, with meets (intersection) and joins (sum of submodules), and the modular law. Distinct from Module/Basic and Subobject/Lattice.

Name: MinimalAxioms.lean
Description: Minimal axiom sets for modules: reduced axioms from which the full Module typeclass can be derived. Distinct from Module/Defs.

Name: NatInt.lean
Description: Natural number and integer module structures: every AddCommMonoid is a ℕ-module and every AddCommGroup is a ℤ-module, with explicit smul definitions. Distinct from Module/Basic and Ring/Nat.

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Injective modules: an R-module Q is injective if every injective R-linear map M → N extends along any map M → Q. Contains the Injective typeclass, Baer's criterion, and the injective hull. Distinct from Module/Projective and Module/Basic.

Name: FinitePresentation.lean
Description: Finitely presented modules: a module is finitely presented if it has a presentation with finitely many generators and finitely many relations, with stability under exact sequences. Distinct from Module/Basic and Presentable/Finite.

Name: GradedModule.lean
Description: Graded modules over a graded ring: a module M graded by an additive monoid with Mᵢ as the degree-i component, compatible with the ring grading RᵢMⱼ ⊆ Mᵢ₊ⱼ. Distinct from Module/Basic and GradedObject/Monoidal.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Linear maps between modules: the LinearMap R M N type (= M →ₗ[R] N) with the module structure on Hom_R(M, N), composition, and the evaluating-at-a-point map. Distinct from Module/Basic and Module/End.

Name: Star
Description: Star algebras and C*-algebras: the star operation (involution), star rings, and the basic properties of complex conjugation.

Name: RingQuot.lean
Description: Star structure on ring quotients: when I is a star-stable ideal (star(I) ⊆ I), the quotient ring R/I inherits a star making the quotient map a *-ring homomorphism. Distinct from Star/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: SelfAdjoint.lean
Description: Self-adjoint elements: IsSelfAdjoint a ↔ star a = a, the real subspace selfAdjoint R, the Jordan decomposition into self-adjoint and skew-adjoint parts, and the functional calculus setup. Distinct from Star/Basic and Star/Unitary.

Name: StarAlgHom.lean
Description: Star algebra homomorphisms: AlgHom maps f : A →ₐ[R] B additionally satisfying f(star a) = star(f a), the StarAlgHom type, and their composition and basic properties. Distinct from Star/Basic and Algebra/Hom.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise star on sets: star (s : Set A) = {star a | a ∈ s}, with the interaction with set union, intersection, and the associated star-closed sets. Distinct from Star/Basic and Group/Support.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Star structure on binary products: the star on A × B with star(a, b) = (star a, star b). Distinct from Star/Pi (arbitrary products) and Star/Basic.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: Star structure on ℚ: the trivial star (star q = q, since ℚ is a commutative field with positive definite norm) and the associated *-algebra instance. Distinct from Star/Basic and Field/Rat.

Name: StarProjection.lean
Description: Star projections: self-adjoint idempotents p = star p and p² = p, the lattice of projections in a *-algebra, and the associated range submodules. Distinct from Star/SelfAdjoint and Ring/Idempotent.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Star structure on tensor products: the star on A ⊗_R B defined by star(a ⊗ b) = star a ⊗ star b, making the tensor product a *-algebra. Distinct from Star/Basic and Algebra/Bilinear.

Name: Unitary.lean
Description: Unitary elements in a *-algebra: u is unitary if star u = u⁻¹ (equivalently u * star u = star u * u = 1), forming the unitary group unitary R. Distinct from Star/SelfAdjoint and Ring/Units.

Name: UnitaryStarAlgAut.lean
Description: Star algebra automorphisms induced by unitaries: the conjugation a ↦ u * a * star u for a unitary u ∈ unitary R forms a star algebra automorphism, and the group hom unitary R → StarAlgAut R. Distinct from Star/Unitary and Ring/Aut.

Name: StarRingHom.lean
Description: Star ring homomorphisms: ring maps R →+* S additionally satisfying f(star a) = star(f a), the StarRingHom type, and their interaction with StarAlgHom. Distinct from Star/StarAlgHom (algebra maps) and Star/Basic.

Name: Subalgebra.lean
Description: Star subalgebras: subalgebras of a *-algebra closed under the star involution, with the lattice structure, intersection, and the generated star subalgebra. Distinct from Star/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Subsemiring.lean
Description: Star subsemirings: subsemirings closed under the star involution, a weaker notion than star subalgebras. Distinct from Star/Subalgebra and Star/Basic.

Name: CentroidHom.lean
Description: Star centroid homomorphisms: centroid maps of a *-ring that commute with the star involution. Distinct from Star/Basic and Ring/CentroidHom.

Name: CHSH.lean
Description: The CHSH inequality in a C*-algebra: for unitaries A, B, C, D satisfying the CHSH algebra relations, the norm bound ‖AB + AC + DB - DC‖ ≤ 2√2. Used in the algebraic foundation of quantum information theory. Distinct from Star/Basic and Star/Unitary.

Name: Conjneg.lean
Description: Conjugate-negation in *-rings: the element conjneg a = -(star a), appearing in the decomposition of elements into self-adjoint and skew-adjoint parts. Distinct from Star/Basic and Star/SelfAdjoint.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions for *-rings and *-algebras: the Star typeclass (star : R → R), the InvolutiveStar typeclass (star ∘ star = id), StarAddMonoid (star is an additive monoid hom), StarRing (star is an anti-ring-hom), and StarAlgebra. Foundation for all Star files.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for star structures: star (∑ᵢ fᵢ) = ∑ᵢ star (fᵢ) and star (∏ᵢ fᵢ) = ∏ᵢ star (f_{σ(i)}) (reversed). Distinct from Star/Basic and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Center.lean
Description: Self-adjoint central elements: elements z ∈ Z(R) with star z = z, forming a commutative sub-*-ring. Distinct from Star/Basic and Ring/Center.

Name: Free.lean
Description: The free *-algebra on a type: the free involutive algebra FreeStarAlgebra R s generated by a set s, with the universal property (any *-algebra map from s extends uniquely). Distinct from Star/Basic and FreeAlgebra/Cardinality.

Name: NonUnitalSubalgebra.lean
Description: Non-unital star subalgebras: non-unital subalgebras of a *-algebra that are closed under the star involution, with the lattice structure. Distinct from Star/Basic and Algebra/NonUnitalSubalgebra.

Name: NonUnitalSubsemiring.lean
Description: Non-unital star subsemirings: subsemirings closed under the star involution, a weaker notion than NonUnitalSubalgebra. Distinct from Star/NonUnitalSubalgebra and Star/Subsemiring.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Star structure on Pi types: the componentwise star on ∏ i, A i with star(f)(i) = star(f i). Distinct from Star/Basic and Ring/Pi.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Star linear maps: R-linear maps f : M → N between star modules satisfying f(star m) = star(f m), forming the star-linear map type. Distinct from Star/Basic and Module/Hom.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Star modules: R-modules M equipped with a star involution compatible with the star on R, the self-adjoint and skew-adjoint submodules, and the real subspace decomposition. Distinct from Star/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: MonoidHom.lean
Description: Star monoid homomorphisms: monoid maps preserving the star operation, and the StarHomClass typeclass for maps commuting with star. Distinct from Star/Basic and Group/Basic.

Name: Squarefree
Description: Squarefree elements: elements not divisible by the square of any irreducible, with characterizations in UFDs and polynomial rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Squarefree elements: an element r is squarefree if p² ∣ r implies p is a unit, with closure under units, interaction with the unique factorisation, and characterisation in terms of radical ideals. Distinct from Prime/Defs and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: SkewPolynomial
Description: Skew polynomial rings R[X; sigma, delta]: polynomials with a twisted multiplication using an endomorphism and a derivation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Skew polynomial rings (Ore extensions): the ring R[X; σ, δ] with the Ore relation Xr = σ(r)X + δ(r) for a ring endomorphism σ and σ-derivation δ. Contains the ring structure, the skew division algorithm, and basic instances. Distinct from SkewMonoidAlgebra/Basic (no derivation) and Polynomial/Basic (σ = id, δ = 0).

Name: Vertex
Description: Vertex algebras: algebraic structures arising in conformal field theory and the representation theory of affine Lie algebras.

Name: VertexOperator.lean
Description: Vertex operators in vertex operator algebras: the formal distribution Y(a, z) = ∑ₙ aₙ z^(-n-1) ∈ (End V)[[z, z⁻¹]], the locality axiom, and the basic structure of a vertex algebra. Distinct from Vertex/HVertexOperator.

Name: HVertexOperator.lean
Description: `HVertexOperator`: heterogeneous vertex operators — `R`-linear maps from an `R`-module `V` to `HahnModule Γ W`. Includes composition of heterogeneous vertex operators (values in Hahn series on lex-order product). Ext lemma. Allows composites and scalar multiplication by multivariable Laurent series.

Name: Tropical
Description: Tropical algebra: the semiring (R union {inf}, min, +), used in tropical geometry and optimization.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: The tropical semiring as a lattice: Tropical R (the min-plus semiring where ⊕ = min and ⊗ = +) with the lattice order, the idempotent semiring axioms, and the connection to complete lattices. Distinct from Tropical/BigOperators and Order/Kleene.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators in the tropical semiring: tropical sums ∑ᵀ (= min) and tropical products ∏ᵀ (= +) over finite sets, with the tropical distributivity. Distinct from Tropical/Lattice and BigOperators/Fin.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of tropical algebra: the tropical semiring (ℝ ∪ {+∞}, min, +) where addition is min and multiplication is +. In the tropical setting the binomial theorem takes the form (x ⊕ y)ⁿ = xⁿ ⊕ yⁿ (since tropical addition is idempotent). The classical binomial theorem (x+y)ⁿ = ∑ C(n,k) xᵏ yⁿ⁻ᵏ is formalized in Algebra/BigOperators. This file provides the tropical algebraic foundation.

Name: TrivSqZeroExt
Description: The trivial square-zero extension R times M: a ring built from a ring R and an R-module M with multiplication (r,m)(s,n) = (rs, rn+ms).

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Ideals in the trivial square-zero extension: the lattice of ideals in R ⋉ M and the interaction with the square-zero ideal {0} × M and the projection to R. Distinct from TrivSqZeroExt/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The trivial square-zero extension R ⋉ M: the ring R × M with multiplication (r₁, m₁) * (r₂, m₂) = (r₁r₂, r₁m₂ + m₁r₂), making {0} × M a square-zero two-sided ideal. Used for dual numbers (M = R) and first-order deformations. Foundation for TrivSqZeroExt/Ideal.

Name: QuadraticAlgebra
Description: Quadratic algebras: finitely generated algebras with quadratic relations, including Clifford and exterior algebras.

Name: NormDeterminant.lean
Description: The norm as a determinant in a quadratic algebra: expressing N(x + yι) as the determinant of the 2×2 matrix of left multiplication, and the multiplicativity of the norm. Distinct from QuadraticAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for quadratic algebras: the QuadraticAlgebra R a typeclass for the algebra R[X]/(X² - a X - b) (or similar), with the generator ι satisfying a quadratic relation. Distinct from QuadraticAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of quadratic algebras: the norm map N : R[ι] → R, the trace Tr : R[ι] → R, and the algebra axioms for the quadratic extension. Distinct from QuadraticAlgebra/Defs and QuadraticAlgebra/NormDeterminant.

Name: Prime
Description: Prime and irreducible elements in monoids and rings, with the ascending chain condition and unique factorization.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for primes and irreducibles: the equivalence Prime ↔ Irreducible in a UFD, primality of powers, and applications to ℕ and ℤ. Distinct from Prime/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for prime and irreducible elements: Prime p (p ≠ 0, p ∉ Rˣ, and p ∣ ab → p ∣ a or p ∣ b) and Irreducible p (p ∉ Rˣ and the only factorisations p = ab have a ∈ Rˣ or b ∈ Rˣ). Distinct from Prime/Lemmas and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: PresentedMonoid
Description: Monoids defined by generators and relations: the presented monoid and its universal property.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Presented monoids: PresentedMonoid rels, the monoid generated by a type with generators subject to a set of relations, with the universal property (any assignment of generators satisfying all relations extends to a unique monoid hom). Distinct from FreeMonoid/Basic and Group/Basic.

Name: SkewMonoidAlgebra
Description: Skew monoid algebras: generalizations of monoid algebras where the multiplication is twisted by a monoid action.

Name: Single.lean
Description: Single-element basis of a skew monoid algebra: the basis elements δ_g ∈ k[G; σ] and their twisted multiplication rule δ_g * δ_h = δ_{gh} with the σ-twist on coefficients. Distinct from SkewMonoidAlgebra/Basic and MonoidAlgebra/Defs.

Name: Support.lean
Description: The support of a skew monoid algebra element: the finite set of G-indices where the element has nonzero coefficient, with the interaction with the multiplication. Distinct from SkewMonoidAlgebra/Basic and MonoidAlgebra/Support.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Skew monoid algebras (twisted group rings): the skew monoid algebra k[G; σ] where multiplication is twisted by a monoid endomorphism σ : G → G, with the ring structure and basic instances. Foundation for all SkewMonoidAlgebra files.

Name: Lift.lean
Description: The universal property of the skew monoid algebra: a σ-twisted monoid map G → Rˣ and a ring map k → R extend uniquely to a ring map k[G; σ] → R. Distinct from SkewMonoidAlgebra/Basic and MonoidAlgebra/Lift.

Name: Ring
Description: Rings and semirings: the basic typeclass hierarchy, subrings, ring homomorphisms, and the fundamental operations.

Name: Submonoid
Description: Multiplicative submonoids of a ring: subsets containing 1 and closed under multiplication, used as the denominator set in localization (RingTheory/Localization). Distinct from Ring/Subring (which also requires closure under addition and subtraction).

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise submonoid operations in rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Submonoids of rings.

Name: Semireal
Description: Semireal rings: rings R where -1 is not a sum of squares, a weakening of the condition for formally real fields. Contains the semireal closure and connections to orderings of rings. Distinct from OrderedRing (which imposes a global order) — semireality is a weaker positivity condition.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Semireal rings: `IsSemireal R` — `−1` is not a sum of squares.

Name: Subsemiring
Description: Subsemirings: the Subsemiring type, subsets closed under + and * and containing 0 and 1, without requiring subtraction. The image of any RingHom is a subsemiring. Distinct from Subring (requires subtraction) and Submonoid (multiplicative only).

Name: Order.lean
Description: Ordered subsemirings: order inherited by subsemirings.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise subsemiring operations: `s * t` for subsemirings.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Subsemiring of the opposite semiring.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Subsemiring API: `Subsemiring.closure`, `Subsemiring.map`, and the lattice.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Subsemiring R`: a subset closed under `+`, `*`, `0`, `1`.

Name: Subring
Description: Subrings: the Subring type, subsets of a ring closed under +, *, -, and containing 0 and 1. The lattice of subrings, the subring generated by a set, and the inclusion RingHom. Distinct from Submonoid (multiplicative only) and Subsemiring (no subtraction).

Name: Order.lean
Description: Ordered subrings: the order structure inherited by a subring.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise subring operations: `s * t` as a subring product.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in a subring.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Subring of the opposite ring.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Subring API: `Subring.closure`, `Subring.map`, lattice of subrings.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Subring R`: a subring of `R` closed under `+`, `*`, `-`, `0`, `1`.

Name: IntPolynomial.lean
Description: Integer polynomials in a subring.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise ring operations on sets: A + B = {a + b | a ∈ A, b ∈ B}, A * B = {a * b}, and their interaction with ring axioms (distributivity of * over +). Used in additive combinatorics (sum-product phenomenon) and in ideal theory (product of sets of ideals). Distinct from Group/Pointwise (multiplicative only).

Name: Set.lean
Description: Pointwise set ring operations.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Pointwise Finset ring operations.

Name: Divisibility
Description: Divisibility in rings: a ∣ b (there exists c with b = a * c), associates (a ∣ b and b ∣ a), and units vs non-units. The ring-level foundation for unique factorization theory. Distinct from Group/Irreducible (irreducibility) and RingTheory/UniqueFactorizationDomain (UFD theory).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Divisibility lemmas.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Divisibility in rings: `a ∣ b`.

Name: Action
Description: Ring actions: the SMul of a ring on a module or algebra, compatibility with both addition and multiplication (distributivity), and the resulting algebra structure. Distinct from Group/Action (which lacks multiplicative structure) and Module/LinearMap (which requires a pre-existing module structure).

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise ring actions on sets: lifting a ring action on α to Set α, with set-arithmetic distributivity (r • (A + B) = r • A + r • B). Used in module theory to describe how a ring element scales a set of module elements, and in linear algebra (scaling a subspace).

Name: Set.lean
Description: Pointwise set ring action.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Pointwise Finset ring action.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ-ring actions.

Name: Invariant.lean
Description: Invariant elements under ring actions.

Name: Subobjects.lean
Description: Subobjects under ring actions.

Name: Submonoid.lean
Description: Submonoid ring actions.

Name: Group.lean
Description: Group ring actions.

Name: ConjAct.lean
Description: Conjugation ring action: the conjugation action of units on a ring, and its properties.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ring actions: `SMulCommClass`, `IsScalarTower`, and basic lemmas for ring-module interactions.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Field actions: the action of a field on a ring extension.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphism ring action.

Name: Int
Description: The ring structure on ℤ at the algebraic (non-number-theoretic) level: ℤ as the initial ring, the unique ring homomorphism ℤ → R for any ring R (the characteristic map), and casting lemmas. Distinct from Data/Int (divisibility, gcd) and NumberTheory (prime factorization, congruences).

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of ℤ.

Name: Parity.lean
Description: Parity in ℤ.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: ℤ ring definitions.

Name: Hom
Description: Ring homomorphisms: RingHom R S, the primary morphism type for rings and semirings. Contains kernel (as an ideal), range (as a subring), composition, the identity RingHom, and instances making the category of rings. Distinct from Algebra/Algebra/Hom (AlgHom requires an algebra structure) and Module/LinearMap (no multiplication required).

Name: InjSurj.lean
Description: Injectivity and surjectivity of ring maps: `RingHom.injective_iff` and conditions for surjectivity.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `RingHom R S`: preserves `0`, `1`, `+`, `*`. `RingHomClass` typeclass.

Name: Periodic.lean
Description: Periodic elements in a ring: Function.Periodic f T asserting f(x + T) = f(x), and the period subgroup {T | f periodic with period T}. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Field/Periodic.

Name: Parity.lean
Description: Even and odd elements in rings: IsEven a (∃ r, a = r + r) and IsOdd a (∃ r, a = r + r + 1), with ring-arithmetic closure and the interaction with the natural number cast. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Group/Even.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi-type ring structure: the Ring (and Semiring, CommRing) structure on ∏ i, R i with componentwise operations and the projection ring homomorphisms. Distinct from Ring/Prod (binary product) and Ring/Basic.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: Ring structure on PUnit: the unique (zero) ring with a single element satisfying 0 = 1. Distinct from Ring/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Binary product rings: the Ring structure on R × S with componentwise operations, the projection maps fst and snd as ring maps, and the diagonal embedding. Distinct from Ring/Pi (arbitrary products) and Ring/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite ring R^op: reversed multiplication (aᵒᵖ * bᵒᵖ = (ba)ᵒᵖ), the anti-isomorphism MulOpposite.opRingEquiv, and bimodule duality. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Group/Opposite.

Name: MinimalAxioms.lean
Description: Minimal axiom sets for rings: reduced axiom systems (e.g., just one distributivity direction, or just left inverses) from which the full Ring typeclass follows. Distinct from Ring/Defs.

Name: IsFormallyReal.lean
Description: Formally real rings: the IsFormallyReal typeclass asserting no nontrivial representation of 0 as a sum of squares (foundation for ordered field theory). Distinct from Ring/SumsOfSquares and Ring/Basic.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Natural number casts in rings: Nat.cast : ℕ → R as a ring map, the lemmas Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_mul, and the interaction with CharP and CharZero. Distinct from Ring/Basic and CharZero/Defs.

Name: NonZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Non-zero-divisors of a ring: the submonoid nonZeroDivisors R = {a | ∀ b, ab = 0 → b = 0}, the map to the fraction ring, and the equivalence with integral domain in the commutative case. Distinct from Ring/Basic and GroupWithZero/NonZeroDivisors.

Name: NegOnePow.lean
Description: Powers of −1 in a ring: the negOnePow n = (-1)^n function, the sign alternation (-1)^(n+1) = -(-1)^n, and applications to alternating sums and signs in algebra. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Field/NegOnePow.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring ring instances along equivalences: Function.Injective.ring and Equiv.ring for transporting semiring, ring, and commutative ring structure. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Ring/InjSurj.

Name: Torsion.lean
Description: Torsion elements in rings: elements r with n • r = 0 for some positive n, the additive torsion ideal, and torsion-free rings (in which the natural map ℤ → R is injective). Distinct from Ring/Basic and Group/Torsion.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift for rings: the Ring structure on ULift R with the isomorphism ULift R ≅ R. Distinct from Ring/Basic.

Name: WithZero.lean
Description: The WithZero construction applied to multiplicative structures: building a ring from a group by adjoining a zero that absorbs under multiplication. Distinct from Ring/Basic and GroupWithZero/WithZero.

Name: Units.lean
Description: The unit group Rˣ of a ring: elements with a two-sided multiplicative inverse, the group structure, the embedding Rˣ ↪ R, units in polynomial rings (constants), and the interaction with nonZeroDivisors. Distinct from Ring/Basic and GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: SumsOfSquares.lean
Description: Sums of squares in rings: the IsSumOfSquares predicate, closure under addition and multiplication, and the connection to formally real rings (IsFormallyReal) and positive semidefinite forms. Distinct from Ring/IsFormallyReal and Ring/Basic.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular elements in rings as non-zero-divisors: the interaction between the Regular/Defs typeclass and the ring structure, showing regular elements are exactly the non-zero-divisors in a CommRing. Distinct from Regular/Defs and Ring/NonZeroDivisors.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℤ and ℚ as rings: the canonical ring maps ℤ → R (for any ring) and ℚ → R (for any CharZero field), and the uniqueness of these maps. Distinct from Ring/Basic and CharZero/Defs.

Name: Semiconj.lean
Description: Semiconjugacy in rings: SemiconjBy a x b (ax = xb) and its role in the theory of twisted homomorphisms and skew rings. Distinct from Ring/Commute (commutativity, where a = b) and Ring/Center.

Name: Subgroup.lean
Description: Additive subgroups of rings: conditions for an additive subgroup H ≤ (R, +) to be a subring or (left/right/two-sided) ideal, and the interaction with the multiplicative structure. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Group/Basic.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-level shrinking for rings: the Shrink construction for Ring instances. Distinct from Ring/Basic.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: The centralizer of a subset: Centralizer S = {r ∈ R | ∀ s ∈ S, rs = sr} as a subring. Contains the interaction with the center (Centralizer R = Z(R)) and iterated centralizers. Distinct from Ring/Center and Ring/Basic.

Name: Center.lean
Description: The center of a ring: Z(R) = {z | ∀ r, zr = rz} as a commutative subring, basic properties, and instances for matrix rings. Distinct from Ring/Centralizer (centralizer of a subset) and Group/Center.

Name: CentroidHom.lean
Description: Centroid homomorphisms: the centroid Cent(R) of a (possibly non-associative) ring R consists of maps f : R → R satisfying f(ab) = f(a)b = af(b), forming the multiplier ring. Distinct from Ring/Center and Ring/Basic.

Name: Commute.lean
Description: Commutativity in rings: Commute a b (ab = ba), properties of commuting elements (sums, products, powers), and the centraliser C_R(a). Distinct from Ring/Center (global commutativity) and Ring/Semiconj.

Name: CharZero.lean
Description: Characteristic-zero ring results: the Nat.cast and Int.cast maps are injective in a CharZero ring, cancellation lemmas, and the interaction with the ordered structure. Distinct from Ring/Basic and CharZero/Defs.

Name: BooleanRing.lean
Description: Boolean rings: rings where every element satisfies x² = x, with the derived commutativity (every Boolean ring is commutative) and the lattice structure. Contains the equivalence with Boolean algebras. Distinct from Ring/Idempotent and Ring/Basic.

Name: Associated.lean
Description: Associated elements in a ring: a ~ᵤ b (a = u * b for a unit u ∈ Rˣ), the associated equivalence relation, and the quotient monoid Associates R. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Divisibility/Units.

Name: AddAut.lean
Description: Additive automorphisms of a ring: the group of additive automorphisms AddAut R and its interaction with the ring multiplication (an additive automorphism is ring-linear). Distinct from Ring/Aut (ring automorphisms) and Ring/Basic.

Name: Associator.lean
Description: The ring associator: the trilinear map assoc(a, b, c) = (a*b)*c - a*(b*c) measuring failure of associativity, used in the theory of non-associative rings and alternative algebras. Distinct from Ring/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic ring theory: consequences of the ring axioms — zero annihilates all products, negation interacts with multiplication, the binomial theorem, and the basic interaction with subrings. Foundation for all Ring/* files. Distinct from Ring/Defs.

Name: Aut.lean
Description: Ring automorphisms: the group RingAut R of ring automorphisms (ring self-isomorphisms) with composition, the Galois action on extensions, and the conjugation automorphisms. Distinct from Ring/AddAut (additive automorphisms only) and Ring/Equiv.

Name: Idempotent.lean
Description: Idempotent elements in rings: e² = e, the Pierce decomposition via central idempotents, the correspondence between idempotents and direct product decompositions, and the Boolean ring of idempotents. Distinct from Ring/Center and DirectSum/Idempotents.

Name: GrindInstances.lean
Description: Instance registrations for ring arithmetic in the grind tactic: lemmas about ring operations tagged for automation by the grind tactic. Distinct from Ring/Basic.

Name: Identities.lean
Description: Algebraic ring identities: the Brahmagupta-Fibonacci identity (a²+b²)(c²+d²) = (ac-bd)²+(ad+bc)², Sophie Germain identity, and other polynomial identities in arbitrary rings. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Ring/GeomSum.

Name: Invertible.lean
Description: The Invertible typeclass for rings: an element a : R with an explicit inverse ⅟a satisfying a * ⅟a = 1 and ⅟a * a = 1, and the interaction with Rˣ and nonZeroDivisors. Distinct from Ring/Units (the Rˣ group) and GroupWithZero/Invertible.

Name: InjSurj.lean
Description: Injective and surjective ring maps: transfer of ring structure along injective maps (Function.Injective.ring) and surjective maps (Function.Surjective.ring). Distinct from Ring/Basic and Ring/TransferInstance.

Name: GeomSum.lean
Description: Geometric sum formulas in rings: the identity (x^n - 1) = (x - 1) * ∑ᵢ₌₀ⁿ⁻¹ xⁱ and the related formulas for x^n - y^n and the sum of a geometric series. Distinct from Ring/Identities and Field/GeomSum.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for rings and semirings: the NonUnitalNonAssocSemiring, Semiring, Ring, and CommRing typeclasses with their axioms (distributivity, zero and one). Foundation for all Ring files.

Name: CompTypeclasses.lean
Description: Typeclass composition for ring homomorphisms: the RingHomCompTriple α β γ typeclass packaging the fact that f ∘ g = h as a typeclass for automation. Distinct from Ring/Defs.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Ring equivalences (ring isomorphisms): the RingEquiv type, its group structure under composition, the API for converting between ring maps and equivalences, and basic instances. Distinct from Ring/Basic and Ring/Aut.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Ring structure on Fin n and ZMod n: the identification of the ring operations on ZMod n with modular arithmetic on Fin n, and the basic instances. Distinct from Ring/Basic and CharP/Defs.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Extensionality for ring homomorphisms: two ring maps R → S are equal iff they agree on a generating set (ring hom ext). Distinct from Ring/Basic.

Name: Regular
Description: Regular elements in algebraic structures: left, right, and two-sided regular elements (non-zero-divisors) in monoids and rings.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Regularity in binary product rings: (a, b) is regular in R × S iff a is regular in R and b in S. Distinct from Regular/Pi (arbitrary products) and Regular/Basic.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Powers of regular elements: if a is (left/right) regular then a^n is (left/right) regular for all n ≥ 1. Distinct from Regular/Basic.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: Regularity for ULift: the (left/right) regular element structure on ULift R. Distinct from Regular/Basic.

Name: SMul.lean
Description: Regular elements and scalar multiplication: if a is left-regular in R then the smul-by-a map M → M is injective. Distinct from Regular/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for regular elements: IsLeftRegular a (ax = ay → x = y), IsRightRegular a (xa = ya → x = y), and IsRegular a (both). Foundation for all Regular files.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results for regular elements: products of regular elements are regular, units are regular, and the interaction with cancellativity and injectivity. Distinct from Regular/Defs.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Regularity in Pi rings: (aᵢ)ᵢ is (left/right) regular in ∏ᵢ Rᵢ iff each aᵢ is (left/right) regular in Rᵢ. Distinct from Regular/Basic and Ring/Pi.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Regularity in the opposite ring: a is left-regular in R iff aᵒᵖ is right-regular in R^op. Distinct from Regular/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: Homology
Description: Homological algebra within the Algebra folder: chain complexes, exact sequences, and homology in an algebraic context.

Name: ShortComplex
Description: Short complexes 0 → X₁ → X₂ → X₃ → 0: the fundamental building block of homological algebra. Contains the ShortComplex type (a triple of composable maps with zero composite), exactness, the snake lemma, and the five lemma. Distinct from full chain complexes — this is the minimal setting for exact sequences.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Preadditive structure: the category `ShortComplex C` is preadditive when `C` is.

Name: PreservesHomology.lean
Description: Functors preserving homology: conditions for a functor to preserve the homology of a short complex.

Name: ModuleCat.lean
Description: Short complexes of modules: specific results for `ShortComplex (ModuleCat R)`.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits of short complexes: products and equalisers computed componentwise.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Linear structure on short complexes: `k`-linear short complexes and their hom spaces.

Name: ShortExact.lean
Description: Short exact sequences: `ShortExact S` — `f` is mono, `g` is epi, and `S` is exact.

Name: SnakeLemma.lean
Description: Snake lemma: given a morphism of short exact sequences, there is a connecting homomorphism making a 6-term exact sequence.

Name: RightHomology.lean
Description: Right homology: `ShortComplex.rightHomology S = coker (image f → ker g)` (cokernel-based definition).

Name: QuasiIso.lean
Description: Quasi-isomorphisms of short complexes: maps inducing isomorphisms on homology.

Name: Retract.lean
Description: Retracts of short complexes: a short complex that is a retract of an exact complex is itself exact.

Name: ConcreteCategory.lean
Description: Short complexes in concrete categories: element-wise computation of exactness.

Name: Exact.lean
Description: Exact short complexes: `ShortComplex.Exact S` — `ker g = image f`; characterisation in terms of left/right homology.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Short complexes: `ShortComplex C` — a triple `X → Y → Z` with `f ≫ g = 0`; the category and basic API.

Name: Ab.lean
Description: Short complexes of abelian groups: instances and lemmas for `ShortComplex Ab`.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Short complexes in abelian categories: `ShortComplex C` is exact iff it computes zero homology in any abelian `C`.

Name: Homology.lean
Description: Homology of a short complex: `ShortComplex.homology S = ker g / im f`, the main homology group.

Name: LeftHomology.lean
Description: Left homology: `ShortComplex.leftHomology S = ker g / im f` (kernel-based definition).

Name: HomologicalComplex.lean
Description: Short complexes and homological complexes: extracting a short complex at degree `n` from a chain complex.

Name: ExactFunctor.lean
Description: Exact functors preserve short exact sequences: `F.map S` is exact when `S` is exact and `F` is exact.

Name: FunctorEquivalence.lean
Description: Functor equivalences for short complexes: natural equivalences between short complex categories.

Name: ModelCategory
Description: A lifting lemma for cochain complexes: given a commutative square of cochain maps in which one side has a degreewise lift satisfying a cocycle condition, constructs an explicit global lift using cocycle₁', exists_hom, and hasLift as the main result. A component of the broader program to formalize the Quillen model structure on chain complexes (weak equivalences = quasi-isomorphisms, fibrations = degreewise surjections, cofibrations = degreewise injections with projective cokernels). Distinct from DerivedCategory (the homotopy category of that model structure) and HomotopyCategory.

Name: Lifting.lean
Description: Lifting properties in the chain complex model category: the lifting of maps against (trivial) cofibrations/fibrations.

Name: SpectralSequence
Description: Spectral sequences: a SpectralSequence is a sequence of pages (Eᵣ, dᵣ) where each Eᵣ is a bigraded chain complex with differential dᵣ of degree (r, 1−r). Defines SpectralSequence and pageFunctor, and organizes spectral sequences into a category. Provides the abstract definition and categorical framework; construction of specific spectral sequences from filtered complexes, double complexes, or fibrations is not yet present. Distinct from SpectralObject (the exact-couple machinery that inputs to a spectral sequence) and DerivedCategory (a key source of examples).

Name: ComplexShape.lean
Description: Complex shapes for spectral sequences: the bigraded complex shape underlying the `(p,q)` indexing.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic spectral sequence API: the abstract definition and the `E^r` page notation.

Name: SpectralObject
Description: Spectral objects: a comprehensive framework generalizing spectral sequences in an abelian category, abstracting the page data, differentials dᵣ, cycles, boundaries, and convergence to a graded target. Defines SpectralObject, page, d, convergesTo, and hasSpectralSequence. More general and powerful than SpectralSequence — a spectral object packages the exact-couple input from which the individual pages are extracted. Distinct from SpectralSequence (the pages-and-differentials presentation) and DerivedCategory (which provides key examples via filtrations).

Name: Homology.lean
Description: Homology from a spectral object: `H_n(F)` computed from the filtration and the spectral sequence.

Name: HasSpectralSequence.lean
Description: Typeclass for having a spectral sequence: `HasSpectralSequence F` — the filtered complex `F` gives rise to a convergent spectral sequence.

Name: SpectralSequence.lean
Description: Spectral sequence: the full structure assembling the pages `E^r`, differentials `d^r`, and convergence to `H_*`.

Name: Page.lean
Description: Pages of a spectral sequence: `E^r_{p,q}` for all `r ≥ 1`, with differentials and the `E^{r+1}` formula.

Name: FirstPage.lean
Description: First page of a spectral sequence: `E^1_{p,q}` computed from the filtered complex.

Name: Cycles.lean
Description: Cycles in a spectral object: the cycle subobjects `Z^r_{p,q}` of the spectral sequence.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Spectral objects: a `SpectralObject C` is a filtered complex or exact couple giving rise to a spectral sequence.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epi/mono conditions in a spectral sequence: conditions on the differentials for degeneration.

Name: Differentials.lean
Description: Differentials in a spectral sequence: `d^r : E^r_{p,q} → E^r_{p-r,q+r-1}` and their computation.

Name: LeftResolution
Description: Left resolutions of objects in an abelian category: projective resolutions P• → X → 0 used to compute left-derived functors. Contains the existence of projective resolutions in module categories and the comparison theorem for resolutions.

Name: Transport.lean
Description: Transport of left resolutions: moving a resolution along a quasi-isomorphism.

Name: Reduced.lean
Description: Reduced left resolutions: augmentation-free resolutions used in group cohomology.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Left resolutions: `LeftResolution M` — a projective/free resolution of `M` from the left.

Name: Embedding
Description: Embeddings of complexes and their induced maps: including complexes along index-set embeddings, extending complexes with zero objects, and the restriction/extension adjunction. Used to compare complexes indexed over different ordered sets.

Name: RestrictionHomology.lean
Description: Homology of restricted complexes: `H(restrict e C) ≅ H(C)` for appropriately supported complexes.

Name: StupidTrunc.lean
Description: Stupid truncation of chain complexes: `stupidTrunc` — the truncation that kills all terms outside a range.

Name: IsSupported.lean
Description: Support condition for embedded complexes: `IsSupported e C` — a complex is supported on the image of the embedding `e`.

Name: Restriction.lean
Description: Restriction along a complex embedding: truncating a complex to its supported part via an embedding.

Name: TruncLE.lean
Description: Truncation from above (≤n): `HomologicalComplex.truncLE n C` — setting all terms above degree `n` to zero.

Name: TruncLEHomology.lean
Description: Homology of the LE-truncation: `H(truncLE n C) ≅ H(C)` in degrees `≤ n`.

Name: TruncGE.lean
Description: Truncation from below (≥n): `HomologicalComplex.truncGE n C` — setting all terms below degree `n` to zero.

Name: TruncGEHomology.lean
Description: Homology of the GE-truncation: `H(truncGE n C) ≅ H(C)` in degrees `≥ n`.

Name: HomEquiv.lean
Description: Hom equivalence for embedded complexes: `Hom(extend e C, D) ≅ Hom(C, restrict e D)`.

Name: Boundary.lean
Description: Boundary maps in embedded complexes: the boundary differential in a chain complex embedding.

Name: CochainComplex.lean
Description: Cochain complex embeddings: the special case of embeddings for cochain complexes indexed by ℤ.

Name: AreComplementary.lean
Description: Complementary homology embeddings: two embeddings of chain complexes are complementary iff they cover all degrees.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Embeddings of chain complexes: `HomologicalComplex.Embedding c c'` — an order-embedding of index types compatible with the complex shapes.

Name: ExtendHomology.lean
