Description: Homology of extended complexes: `H(extend e C) ≅ H(C)` where the extension does not change homology.

Name: ExtendHomotopy.lean
Description: Homotopies of extended complexes: chain homotopies extend compatibly with the embedding extension.

Name: Connect.lean
Description: Connecting morphisms: the connecting homomorphism `δ : Hⁿ → Hⁿ⁺¹` arising from a short exact sequence of embedded complexes.

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extension along a complex embedding: extending a chain complex along an embedding by adding zero objects.

Name: DerivedCategory
Description: The derived category D(A) of an abelian category A: the localization of the homotopy category K(A) at quasi-isomorphisms, with localization functors Q and Qh. Contains the triangulated structure, the single-object embedding singleFunctor (sending X to the complex concentrated in degree 0), Ext groups via extClass, and the distinguished triangle triangleOfSES_distinguished arising from a short exact sequence. An actively developed area with ongoing API additions (TODO markers throughout). Distinct from HomotopyCategory — quasi-isomorphisms are fully inverted here, not just homotopies.

Name: Ext
Description: Ext groups via the derived category: Extⁿ(X, Y) is defined as SmallShiftedHom ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X) ((singleFunctor C n).obj Y), i.e., morphisms X → Y[n] in D(A). Contains Ext, Ext.hom, Ext.homEquiv, and Ext.mk₀. The equivalence with classical Ext-via-injective-resolutions is established via hasExt_of_enoughInjectives, showing the two definitions agree whenever A has enough injectives. Distinct from Homology/Ext (the classical resolution-based definition) and DerivedCategory (the ambient triangulated category).

Name: Map.lean
Description: Maps on Ext groups.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Linear structure on Ext groups.

Name: TStructure.lean
Description: Ext and t-structures.

Name: MapBijective.lean
Description: Bijectivity conditions for maps on Ext.

Name: ExtClass.lean
Description: Ext class of a short exact sequence.

Name: EnoughInjectives.lean
Description: Ext via injective resolutions in the derived category.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ext groups in the derived category: `Ext^n(X, Y) = Hom_{D(C)}(X, Y[n])`.

Name: ExactSequences.lean
Description: Long exact Ext sequences.

Name: EnoughProjectives.lean
Description: Ext via projective resolutions.

Name: ShortExact.lean
Description: Short exact sequences in the derived category: `ShortExact` sequences and their distinguished triangle counterparts.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Linear structure on the derived category: the `k`-linear enrichment of `D(C)` and its compatibility with triangulated structure.

Name: KProjective.lean
Description: K-projective complexes: `IsKProjective C` — complexes for which `Hom(C, acyclic) = 0`; the existence of K-projective resolutions.

Name: TStructure.lean
Description: t-structure on the derived category: the standard t-structure `(D^{≤0}, D^{≥0})` and its heart (abelian category).

Name: SmallShiftedHom.lean
Description: Small shifted hom sets: `SmallShiftedHom X Y n = Hom_{D(C)}(X, Y[n])` and their computation.

Name: SingleTriangle.lean
Description: Triangles from single-complex maps: distinguished triangles arising from the cone construction on a single complex.

Name: Fractions.lean
Description: Calculus of fractions for quasi-isomorphisms: the Ore localisation conditions ensuring `D(C)` has a nice description in terms of roofs.

Name: ExactFunctor.lean
Description: Exact functors in the derived category: functors that descend to the derived category and preserve distinguished triangles.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Derived category: `DerivedCategory C` — the localisation of the homotopy category `K(C)` at quasi-isomorphisms. The canonical functor `Q : K(C) ⥤ D(C)` and its universal property.

Name: KInjective.lean
Description: K-injective complexes: `IsKInjective C` — complexes for which `Hom(acyclic, C) = 0`; the existence of K-injective resolutions.

Name: HomologySequence.lean
Description: Long exact homology sequence: the long exact sequence `⋯ → Hⁿ(X) → Hⁿ(Y) → Hⁿ(Z) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X) → ⋯` in the derived category.

Name: FullyFaithful.lean
Description: Full faithfulness in the derived category: the fully faithful embedding of the heart into `D(C)` via the `t`-structure.

Name: HomotopyCategory
Description: The homotopy category K(A): chain complexes modulo chain homotopy equivalence, the intermediate step between chain complexes and the derived category. Fully formalized as a triangulated category (IsTriangulated instance proved), with the mapping cone (mappingCone), the subcategory of acyclic complexes (subcategoryAcyclic), and distinguished triangles (Pretriangulated.distinguishedTriangles). Contains the localization functor K(A) → D(A). Distinct from DerivedCategory — here only homotopies are quotiented, not all quasi-isomorphisms.

Name: Pretriangulated.lean
Description: Pretriangulated structure: `HomotopyCategory C` is a pretriangulated category with distinguished triangles from mapping cones.

Name: Shift.lean
Description: Shift functor on the homotopy category: `[n] : K(A) ⥤ K(A)` shifting complexes by `n` degrees.

Name: MappingCocone.lean
Description: Mapping cocone: the cocone `C(f)` dual to the mapping cone — `C(f)` in degree `n` is `Xn ⊕ Yn₊₁`.

Name: MappingCone.lean
Description: Mapping cone: `mappingCone f` — the complex `C(f)_n = X_n ⊕ Y_{n-1}` fitting in a distinguished triangle `X → Y → C(f) → X[1]`.

Name: ShiftSequence.lean
Description: Shift sequence structure: the family of shift functors `[n]` forms a shift sequence compatible with the triangulated structure.

Name: SpectralObject.lean
Description: Spectral objects in the homotopy category: objects giving rise to spectral sequences.

Name: Triangulated.lean
Description: Triangulated structure on the homotopy category: `HomotopyCategory C` is a triangulated category satisfying TR1–TR4.

Name: ShortExact.lean
Description: Short exact sequences in the homotopy category: `ShortExact X Y Z` and its distinguished triangle.

Name: SingleFunctors.lean
Description: Single-complex functors: `single n : A ⥤ K(A)` embedding an object as a complex concentrated in degree `n`.

Name: KProjective.lean
Description: K-projective complexes in the homotopy category: K-projective objects for computing left-derived functors.

Name: HomComplex.lean
Description: The internal Hom complex: `HomComplex C D` — the cochain complex of maps from `C` to `D` shifted by degree.

Name: HomComplexCohomology.lean
Description: Cohomology of the Hom complex: `Hⁿ(HomComplex C D) ≅ Hom_{K(C)}(C, D[n])`.

Name: Acyclic.lean
Description: Acyclic complexes in the homotopy category: `Acyclic C` — a complex with zero homology; the subcategory of acyclics.

Name: DegreewiseSplit.lean
Description: Degreewise split complexes: complexes whose component sequences split in each degree.

Name: HomComplexInduction.lean
Description: Induction on the Hom complex: recursive construction of chain maps using the Hom complex structure.

Name: HomologicalFunctor.lean
Description: Homological functors on the homotopy category: functors `K(A) ⥤ Ab` taking distinguished triangles to long exact sequences.

Name: KInjective.lean
Description: K-injective complexes in the homotopy category: K-injective objects and their role in computing derived functors.

Name: HomComplexShift.lean
Description: Shifted Hom complexes: `HomComplex (C[m]) D ≅ (HomComplex C D)[-m]`.

Name: HomComplexSingle.lean
Description: Hom complex with single complexes: `HomComplex (single n A) D ≅ D[-n]` as a complex.

Name: Factorizations
Description: Factorization systems on categories of chain complexes: the (epi, mono) factorization and its interaction with the homology functor. Contains the image complex and the canonical factorization of a chain map through its image.

Name: CM5b.lean
Description: Model category axiom CM5b for chain complexes: every chain map factors as a cofibration followed by a trivial fibration.

Name: CM5a.lean
Description: Model category axiom CM5a for chain complexes: every chain map factors as a trivial cofibration followed by a fibration.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Factorisation systems for chain complexes: the `(cofib, fib)` factorisation on chain maps used in model category structures.

Name: HomotopyCategory.lean
Description: The homotopy category K(A): the category obtained from homological complexes by quotienting by chain homotopies, and the localisation functor from HomologicalComplex A c. Distinct from Homology/Homotopy and Homology/Localization.

Name: Homotopy.lean
Description: Chain homotopies: a chain homotopy between chain maps f, g : A → B is a collection h : A i → B (next i) with f - g = d_B ∘ h + h ∘ d_A. Contains the homotopy equivalence relation and the null-homotopic class. Distinct from Homology/HomotopyCategory and Homology/HomotopyCofiber.

Name: HomotopyCofiber.lean
Description: The homotopy cofiber (mapping cone) of a chain map: the complex C(f) for a chain map f : A → B, fitting in the triangle A → B → C(f) → A[1]. Distinct from Homology/Homotopy and Triangulated/Basic.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: R-linear structure on homological complex categories: the R-linear structure on HomologicalComplex A c and the homotopy category when A is R-linear. Distinct from Homology/Additive and Linear/Basic.

Name: ImageToKernel.lean
Description: The image-to-kernel map: the canonical map im(dₙ₊₁) → ker(dₙ) in a chain complex, whose cokernel is the n-th homology group. Contains the imageToKernel map and basic properties. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Abelian/Images.

Name: HomologySequenceLemmas.lean
Description: Technical lemmas for the long exact sequence in homology: naturality, the connecting homomorphism, and the Snake Lemma in the complex setting. Distinct from Homology/HomologySequence and Abelian/Exact.

Name: HomologicalComplexBiprod.lean
Description: Biproducts in categories of homological complexes: the direct sum of two complexes as a biproduct, computed degreewise. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplexAbelian and Preadditive/Biproducts.

Name: HomologicalComplexAbelian.lean
Description: Abelian structure on categories of homological complexes: HomologicalComplex A c is abelian when A is, with kernels and cokernels computed degreewise. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Abelian/Basic.

Name: HomologicalComplexLimits.lean
Description: Limits and colimits in categories of homological complexes: degreewise computation of limits in HomologicalComplex C c when C has limits. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Limits/HasLimits.

Name: HomologySequence.lean
Description: The long exact sequence in homology: a short exact sequence 0 → A → B → C → 0 of chain complexes gives a long exact sequence … → Hₙ(A) → Hₙ(B) → Hₙ(C) → Hₙ₋₁(A) → …. Distinct from Homology/ExactSequence and Homology/HomologySequenceLemmas.

Name: HomologicalComplexLimitsEventuallyConstant.lean
Description: Limits of eventually-constant sequences of complexes: a sequence of complexes that stabilises degreewise has its limit equal to the stable value. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplexLimits.

Name: LocalCohomology.lean
Description: Local cohomology of a module over a ring: RΓ_I(M) = R lim_n Ext^*(R/I^n, M), with the basic definition and functoriality. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Abelian/RightDerived.

Name: Square.lean
Description: Commutative squares and pullback/pushout squares in homological complexes: squares of chain maps that are cartesian or cocartesian. Distinct from Homology/CommSq and Abelian/CommSq.

Name: SingleHomology.lean
Description: The homology of a single-object complex: Hₙ(Single m A) = A if n = m and 0 otherwise, with the isomorphism. Distinct from Homology/Single and Homology/HomologicalComplex.

Name: TotalComplex.lean
Description: The total complex of a bicomplex: for a bicomplex C_{p,q} with horizontal and vertical differentials, the total complex Tot(C)_n = ⊕_{p+q=n} C_{p,q} with the combined differential. Distinct from Homology/Double and Homology/HomologicalBicomplex.

Name: TotalComplexSymmetry.lean
Description: Symmetry of the total complex: the natural isomorphism Tot(C) ≅ Tot(C^T) where C^T is the transposed bicomplex (swapping rows and columns, with sign adjustment). Distinct from Homology/TotalComplex and Homology/BifunctorFlip.

Name: TotalComplexShift.lean
Description: The interaction of the shift functor with the total complex: Tot(C[m][n]) ≅ Tot(C)[m+n]. Distinct from Homology/TotalComplex and Homology/BifunctorShift.

Name: Single.lean
Description: The single-object chain complex: the chain complex concentrated in degree n with object A and all other terms 0, and the functor Single n : A ⥤ HomologicalComplex A c. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/AlternatingConst.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on chain complexes: the tensor product of two chain complexes (with the Koszul sign convention on differentials), making HomologicalComplex A c a monoidal category. Distinct from Homology/Bifunctor and Monoidal/Category.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: The derived category as a localisation: the derived category D(A) as the localisation of K(A) at quasi-isomorphisms, and the derived functors as localised functors. Distinct from Homology/HomotopyCategory and Localization/Construction.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite of a chain complex: for each complex C the opposite complex C^op in A^op, and the dualisation of exactness. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Abelian/Opposite.

Name: Refinements.lean
Description: Refinement lemmas specific to `HomologicalComplex`: technical results for subdividing morphisms in exact sequences. Complements the general refinement theory in `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Refinements`.

Name: QuasiIso.lean
Description: Quasi-isomorphisms of chain complexes: a chain map f : A → B is a quasi-isomorphism if Hₙ(f) is an isomorphism for all n. Contains the QuasiIso predicate and the 2-of-3 property. Distinct from Homology/Homotopy (homotopy equivalence implies quasi-iso) and Homology/Localization.

Name: HomologicalComplex.lean
Description: Homological complexes: the HomologicalComplex C c type for a category C and a complex shape c, with differential d : X i → X (next c i), the chain map type, and the category structure. Foundation for all Homology files.

Name: BifunctorShift.lean
Description: The interaction of the shift functor with bifunctors on chain complexes: the sign rule for the shift in each argument. Distinct from Homology/Bifunctor and Homology/TotalComplexShift.

Name: BifunctorHomotopy.lean
Description: Homotopies for bifunctors on chain complexes: homotopy equivalences between bifunctor evaluations, used in the homotopy invariance of the tensor product. Distinct from Homology/Bifunctor and Homology/Homotopy.

Name: CochainComplexOpposite.lean
Description: `CochainComplex.opEquivalence C`: equivalence of categories between `(CochainComplex C ℤ)ᵒᵖ` and `CochainComplex Cᵒᵖ ℤ`. Two morphisms in `CochainComplex C ℤ` are homotopic iff they are homotopic as morphisms in `CochainComplex Cᵒᵖ ℤ`.

Name: CommSq.lean
Description: Commutative squares in homological complexes: a commutative square of chain maps and its interaction with the mapping cone and homotopy. Distinct from Homology/Homotopy and Abelian/CommSq.

Name: CochainComplexPlus.lean
Description: The bounded-below cochain complex category: chain complexes C with Cⁿ = 0 for n ≪ 0, and the full subcategory C⁺ of the derived category. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/HomotopyCategory.

Name: BifunctorFlip.lean
Description: Flipping the arguments of a bifunctor on chain complexes: the symmetry isomorphism for the tensor product (exchanging the two factors). Distinct from Homology/Bifunctor and Homology/TotalComplexSymmetry.

Name: AlternatingConst.lean
Description: The alternating constant complex: for endomorphisms `φ, ψ : X → X` with `φ ∘ ψ = ψ ∘ φ = 0`, defines periodic chain and cochain complexes `... → X --φ--> X --ψ--> X --φ--> ...` and computes their homology. The complex with `φ = 𝟙` and `ψ = 0` is homotopy equivalent to the single complex at degree 0.

Name: Additive.lean
Description: Additive structure on homological complex categories: the preadditive and additive structure on HomologicalComplex C c and the homotopy category, with the interaction between additive functors and chain complexes. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/Linear.

Name: Augment.lean
Description: Augmented chain complexes: extending a chain complex by an augmentation map ε : C₀ → A (the augmented complex C → A → 0), used in reduced homology. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/Single.

Name: BifunctorAssociator.lean
Description: Associativity for bifunctors on chain complexes: the associator natural isomorphism for the tensor product of three complexes. Distinct from Homology/Bifunctor and Homology/TotalComplex.

Name: Bifunctor.lean
Description: Bifunctors on homological complexes: a functor of two complex arguments compatible with the grading, used in the tensor product of complexes and the bifunctor total complex. Distinct from Homology/TotalComplex and Homology/BifunctorShift.

Name: ComplexShape.lean
Description: Complex shapes: the ComplexShape ι type parameterising the index set and the prev/next functions for chain and cochain complexes (ChainComplex uses ℕ with n → n-1, CochainComplex uses ℤ with n → n+1). Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/ComplexShapeSigns.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functors on homological complexes: the functor C ⥤ HomologicalComplex D c induced from a functor C ⥤ D, and its interaction with the homology functor. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/Additive.

Name: ExactSequenceFour.lean
Description: `ComposableArrows.Exact.cokerIsoKer`: for an exact sequence of at least four terms, the isomorphism from the cokernel of the `k → k+1` map to the kernel of the `k+2 → k+3` map. Works for preadditive balanced categories. Intended for exact sequences in abelian categories.

Name: GrothendieckAbelian.lean
Description: For a complex shape `c` with no loop and `Small.{w} ι`, if `C` is a Grothendieck abelian category (`IsGrothendieckAbelian.{w} C`) then `HomologicalComplex C c` is also Grothendieck abelian. Relies on the generator results in `Generator.HomologicalComplex`.

Name: HomologicalBicomplex.lean
Description: Homological bicomplexes: doubly-graded chain complexes with commuting horizontal and vertical differentials (the anticommutative version). Distinct from Homology/Double and Homology/TotalComplex.

Name: HasNoLoop.lean
Description: The HasNoLoop predicate on complex shapes: a complex shape has no loops if there is no index i with next i = i, ensuring the complex is non-degenerate. Distinct from Homology/ComplexShape and Homology/HomologicalComplex.

Name: ExactSequence.lean
Description: Exact sequences of chain complexes: a sequence of chain maps is exact if the image of each map equals the kernel of the next. Contains short and long exact sequences of complexes. Distinct from Homology/HomologySequence and Abelian/Exact.

Name: ConcreteCategory.lean
Description: Homological complexes in concrete categories: computing homology groups in categories of modules, abelian groups, etc., via the underlying set-level maps. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Abelian/Basic.

Name: ComplexShapeSigns.lean
Description: Sign conventions for complex shapes: the signs (−1)^n on differentials in the tensor product complex, the Koszul sign rule for graded commutativity. Distinct from Homology/ComplexShape and Homology/BifunctorShift.

Name: DifferentialObject.lean
Description: Differential objects: the DifferentialObject C typeclass for objects equipped with a differential d : X → X satisfying d² = 0, as an alternative foundation for chain complexes. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Homology/ComplexShape.

Name: EulerCharacteristic.lean
Description: The Euler characteristic of a complex: χ(C) = ∑ (-1)^n rank(Hₙ(C)) (or alternating sum of Betti numbers), with additivity on short exact sequences. Distinct from Homology/HomologicalComplex and Abelian/Basic.

Name: Double.lean
Description: `double f hi₀₁`: the two-term homological complex consisting of `X₀` in degree `i₀` and `X₁` in degree `i₁` with differential `f : X₀ → X₁` (and zero elsewhere), given a proof `hi₀₁ : c.Rel i₀ i₁`.

Name: Central
Description: Central elements and the center of a ring or algebra: the subring Z(R) = {z | for all r, zr = rz}.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix algebras as central simple algebras: Mₙ(k) is a central simple k-algebra for any field k, with the verification of centrality and simplicity. Distinct from Central/Basic and Azumaya/Matrix.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of central simple algebras: A ⊗_k B is central simple when A and B are, establishing the group law in the Brauer group. Distinct from Central/Basic and Algebra/Bilinear.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphism algebras as central simple algebras: End_D(V) for a finite-dimensional D-vector space V is a central simple k-algebra (where k = center of D). Distinct from Central/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of central simple algebras: IsCentral together with IsSimpleRing gives central simple algebras, with the tensor product of CSAs being a CSA. Distinct from Central/Defs and Azumaya/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for central algebras: the IsCentral R A predicate asserting that the center of A equals the image of algebraMap R → A, making A a central R-algebra. Distinct from Central/Basic (derived properties) and Ring/Center.

Name: Category
Description: Algebraic categories: the categories of groups, rings, modules, and their morphisms in categorical language.

Name: ModuleCat
Description: The category ModuleCat R of left R-modules: the primary category for homological algebra in Mathlib. Contains the abelian category structure, tensor product, Hom functor, limits, colimits, and the free module adjunction. Distinct from FGModuleCat (no finiteness restriction).

Name: Presheaf
Description: Presheaves of modules over a presheaf of rings: if O is a presheaf of rings on a category C, a presheaf of O-modules is a presheaf M of abelian groups with a compatible O-action. The category PresheafMod(O) and the forgetful functor to presheaves of abelian groups. The algebraic setup for quasi-coherent sheaves before imposing the sheaf condition.

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pullback for presheaves of modules.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal presheaves of modules.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits of presheaves of modules.

Name: Pushforward.lean
Description: Pushforward for presheaves of modules.

Name: Sheafify.lean
Description: Sheafify functor for module presheaves.

Name: Sheafification.lean
Description: Sheafification of presheaves of modules.

Name: PushforwardZeroMonoidal.lean
Description: Zero monoidal pushforward.

Name: ColimitFunctor.lean
Description: Colimit functor for presheaves of modules.

Name: ChangeOfRings.lean
Description: Change of rings for presheaves of modules.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Abelian structure on presheaves of modules.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits of presheaves of modules.

Name: Generator.lean
Description: Generator for presheaves of modules.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free presheaves of modules.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epi/mono in presheaves of modules.

Name: Sheaf
Description: Sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings on a site: the full subcategory of PresheafMod satisfying the sheaf condition. The abelian category structure on sheaves of modules, direct and inverse image functors. The categorical formalization of quasi-coherent sheaves on a scheme. Distinct from Presheaf (which does not impose the gluing axiom).

Name: PullbackContinuous.lean
Description: Continuous pullback for sheaves of modules.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Localization for sheaves of modules.

Name: PullbackFree.lean
Description: Free pullback for sheaves of modules.

Name: Quasicoherent.lean
Description: Quasi-coherent sheaves in ModuleCat.

Name: PushforwardContinuous.lean
Description: Continuous pushforward for sheaves of modules.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in sheaves of modules: products and equalisers.

Name: ChangeOfRings.lean
Description: Change of rings for sheaves of modules.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: The category of sheaves of modules is abelian.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits for sheaves of modules.

Name: Generators.lean
Description: Generators for sheaves of modules.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free sheaves of modules: the free `𝒪`-module on a sheaf of sets.

Name: Topology
Description: Topological modules in the categorical context: the category of topological R-modules (R a topological ring) as a full subcategory of ModuleCat with continuous structure maps. Completions and the interaction between the module category and the topology. Used to bridge the algebraic ModuleCat and the analytic theory of Banach and Fréchet modules.

Name: Homology.lean
Description: Topological homology in ModuleCat.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Topology on ModuleCat objects.

Name: Differentials
Description: The module of Kähler differentials Ω_{A/R} as an object in ModuleCat: the universal derivation d : A → Ω_{A/R} in the categorical language of R-modules. The first fundamental exact sequence (for A → B → C) and the second fundamental sequence (for an ideal I ⊆ A). Distinct from RingTheory/Kaehler (same construction, ring-theoretic language) — this folder works in the categorical ModuleCat framework.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Presheaf of differentials.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Module of differentials in a category: `Ω_{A/R}` as an object of ModuleCat.

Name: Ext
Description: The Ext functor in ModuleCat: Extⁿ(M, N) as the n-th derived functor of Hom(M, -), computed via injective resolutions. The long exact sequence of Ext from a short exact sequence. Ext¹(M, N) classifies extensions 0 → N → E → M → 0. Distinct from the algebraic Ext in RingTheory — this folder uses the categorical derived-functor machinery of Algebra/Homology.

Name: HasExt.lean
Description: HasExt typeclass for modules.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Ext groups for finite modules.

Name: DimensionShifting.lean
Description: Dimension shifting for Ext.

Name: Monoidal
Description: The monoidal structure on ModuleCat: the tensor product M ⊗_R N as the categorical tensor product, the unit R, and the symmetric monoidal structure. The internal hom [M, N] = Hom_R(M, N) making ModuleCat a closed symmetric monoidal category. Used in the categorical treatment of algebras (monoids in ModuleCat) and coalgebras (comonoids).

Name: Closed.lean
Description: Closed monoidal structure on ModuleCat.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric monoidal structure on ModuleCat.

Name: Adjunction.lean
Description: Tensor-Hom adjunction in ModuleCat.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on ModuleCat: `⊗_R`.

Name: Products.lean
Description: Products in ModuleCat: the product of modules as the categorical product.

Name: Presheaf.lean
Description: Presheaves of modules: `PresheafOfModules X` as a `ModuleCat`-valued presheaf on a category `X`.

Name: ProjectiveDimension.lean
Description: Projective dimension of modules: the minimum length of a projective resolution.

Name: Projective.lean
Description: Projective modules: characterisation in ModuleCat — a module `P` is projective iff every epimorphism to `P` splits.

Name: LeftResolution.lean
Description: Left resolutions in ModuleCat: projective resolutions and their existence for all modules.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels in ModuleCat: kernel of a linear map as an equaliser with the zero map.

Name: Localization.lean
Description: Localisation in ModuleCat: the localisation functor `S⁻¹ : ModuleCat R ⥤ ModuleCat (S⁻¹R)`.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in ModuleCat: products, equalisers, and pullbacks computed as submodules of products.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subobjects in ModuleCat: subobjects correspond to submodules.

Name: Stalk.lean
Description: Stalk functors for module presheaves: the stalk of a presheaf of modules at a point.

Name: Ulift.lean
Description: Universe lifting for ModuleCat: `ULift` equivalences for module categories across universes.

Name: Tannaka.lean
Description: Tannaka duality for modules: reconstruction of a group scheme from its category of representations.

Name: Semi.lean
Description: Semi-simplicity in ModuleCat: conditions for all modules to be semisimple (Maschke's theorem context).

Name: Pseudofunctor.lean
Description: Pseudofunctor structure on ModuleCat: how `ModuleCat` varies pseudofunctorially over the category of rings.

Name: Simple.lean
Description: Simple modules in ModuleCat: characterisation as modules with no proper submodules.

Name: Sheaf.lean
Description: Sheaves of modules on a site: the category of sheaves of `𝒪`-modules on a Grothendieck site.

Name: Biproducts.lean
Description: Biproducts in ModuleCat: direct sums are biproducts; `ModuleCat R` is a preadditive category with biproducts.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of modules: `ModuleCat R` — `R`-modules as objects, linear maps as morphisms; basic instances and limits.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in ModuleCat: direct sums, coequalisers, and filtered colimits.

Name: ChangeOfRings.lean
Description: Change of rings: restriction and extension of scalars as adjoint functors between `ModuleCat R` and `ModuleCat S`.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: ModuleCat is abelian: `ModuleCat R` is an abelian category with kernels, cokernels, and exact sequences.

Name: AB.lean
Description: AB conditions for ModuleCat: the abelian category axioms AB3–AB5 for the category of modules over a ring.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebra structure on ModuleCat: the monoidal structure making modules into algebras in ModuleCat.

Name: Adjunctions.lean
Description: Adjunctions in ModuleCat: the tensor-hom adjunction `(M ⊗ -) ⊣ Hom(M, -)` and free-forgetful adjunctions.

Name: Free.lean
Description: Free module functor: the free module `R[ι]` as a left adjoint to the forgetful functor.

Name: FilteredColimits.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in ModuleCat: commutation with finite limits; accessibility.

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Injective modules in ModuleCat: characterisation and Baer's injective criterion.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Images in ModuleCat: image factorisation as range of a linear map.

Name: EnoughInjectives.lean
Description: Enough injectives in ModuleCat: every module embeds into an injective module (Baer's criterion).

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Descent for modules: faithfully flat descent for module categories.

Name: ExteriorPower.lean
Description: Exterior power functor on ModuleCat: `⋀ⁿ : ModuleCat R ⥤ ModuleCat R` as a categorical functor.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms in ModuleCat: characterisation as surjective/injective linear maps.

Name: HopfAlgCat
Description: The category of Hopf algebras: bialgebras equipped with an antipode S satisfying m ∘ (S ⊗ id) ∘ Δ = η ∘ ε = m ∘ (id ⊗ S) ∘ Δ. Defines HopfAlgCat, its forgetful functor to BialgCat, and the monoidal structure inherited from the tensor product of bialgebras. Distinct from BialgCat (no antipode required) and AlgCat (no coalgebra structure).

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on HopfAlgCat: tensor product of Hopf algebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of Hopf algebras: `HopfAlgCat R` — Hopf algebras with antipode; Hopf algebra homomorphisms as morphisms.

Name: Grp
Description: The categories Grp (groups) and Ab (abelian groups) as Lean categories: objects, morphisms (GroupHom), limits and colimits (free products, direct sums), and the forgetful functor to Monoid. The abelian category structure on Ab is in CategoryTheory/Abelian, not here.

Name: LargeColimits.lean
Description: Large colimits in Grp: colimits over large diagrams and set-theoretic issues.

Name: LeftExactFunctor.lean
Description: Left-exact functors from Ab: the Hom functor `Hom(-, M)` is left-exact; characterisation.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in Grp/Ab: products, equalisers, and pullbacks; the limit is the subgroup of the product.

Name: Injective.lean
Description: Injective objects in Ab: characterisation of injective abelian groups as divisible groups; Baer's criterion.

Name: IsFinite.lean
Description: Finite group objects: conditions for a group object in a category to be finite.

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels in Grp/Ab: kernel of a group homomorphism as an equaliser with the zero map.

Name: Preadditive.lean
Description: Preadditive structure on Ab: Ab is a preadditive category (morphisms form abelian groups under composition).

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: Yoneda lemma for Grp: the Yoneda embedding and natural transformations from representable functors on groups.

Name: Zero.lean
Description: Zero object in Ab: the trivial group is both initial and terminal in Ab.

Name: ZModuleEquivalence.lean
Description: ℤ-module equivalence: the equivalence `Ab ≃ ℤ-Mod` — abelian groups are the same as ℤ-modules.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-shrinking for Grp: `Shrink.{u}` equivalences for the category of groups.

Name: Subobject.lean
Description: Subobjects in Grp/Ab: subobjects correspond to subgroups; the subobject classifier.

Name: Ulift.lean
Description: Universe lifting for Grp: `ULift` equivalences allowing groups in different universe levels.

Name: Images.lean
Description: Images in Grp/Ab: the image factorisation of a group homomorphism as epi followed by mono.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of groups: `Grp` and `Ab` — objects are groups/abelian groups, morphisms are group homomorphisms; basic instances.

Name: Biproducts.lean
Description: Biproducts in Ab: direct sums are both products and coproducts in the category of abelian groups.

Name: CartesianMonoidal.lean
Description: Cartesian monoidal structure on Grp: the Cartesian product making `Grp` a symmetric monoidal category.

Name: AB.lean
Description: AB conditions for Grp/Ab: the Grothendieck axioms AB3–AB5 for the category of abelian groups.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Grp is abelian: `Ab` (the category of abelian groups) is an abelian category with kernels, cokernels, exact sequences.

Name: Adjunctions.lean
Description: Adjunctions in Grp: the abelianisation adjunction `Grp ⇌ Ab` and free group adjunction.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in Grp/Ab: coproducts (free products / direct sums), coequalisers, and pushouts.

Name: FilteredColimits.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in Grp: Grp is accessible and filtered colimits commute with finite limits.

Name: FiniteGrp.lean
Description: Category of finite groups: `FinGrp` — finite groups as objects, the full subcategory of `Grp`.

Name: ForgetCorepresentable.lean
Description: Forgetful functor is corepresentable: `forget Grp` is corepresented by ℤ (the free group on one generator).

Name: EnoughInjectives.lean
Description: Enough injectives in Ab: the category of abelian groups has enough injectives (divisible groups are injective).

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epimorphisms and monomorphisms in Grp/Ab: characterisation of epis and monos in group categories.

Name: EquivalenceGroupAddGroup.lean
Description: Equivalence between Grp and AddGrp: the additive/multiplicative notation equivalence as a categorical equivalence.

Name: Semigrp
Description: The category Semigrp of semigroups: the simplest algebraic category, with no identity or inverse axiom. Contains the free semigroup and forgetful functors to MonCat. Rarely the primary object of study but foundational for the algebraic category hierarchy.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of semigroups: `SemigroupCat` — semigroups as objects, semigroup homomorphisms as morphisms.

Name: Ring
Description: The categories RingCat (rings) and CommRingCat (commutative rings): objects, morphisms (RingHom), limits (via products and equalizers), colimits (via pushouts and tensor products), and the free ring functor. CommRingCat is equivalent to the opposite of AffineSchemes.

Name: Under
Description: The under-category of rings under a fixed ring R: objects are ring maps R → S, morphisms are commutative triangles. This is the categorical model for R-algebras. The forgetful functor to CommRingCat and the free R-algebra functor. Used to state universal properties of tensor products and polynomial algebras as initial objects in the under-category.

Name: Property.lean
Description: Properties of morphisms in `Ring/R`.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in `Ring/R`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Under category of rings: `R-Alg` as `Ring/R`.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in RingCat: products and equalisers of rings computed as subrings of products.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard ring category instances: ℤ, ℚ, ℝ, ℂ, polynomial rings as objects of RingCat.

Name: FinitePresentation.lean
Description: Finitely presented rings in RingCat: the full subcategory of finitely-presented rings.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topological rings in RingCat: the forgetful functor from topological rings to RingCat.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Small ring categories: universe conditions for RingCat.

Name: LinearAlgebra.lean
Description: Linear algebra in RingCat: the interaction between RingCat morphisms and ModuleCat structures.

Name: FilteredColimits.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in RingCat: accessibility and commutation with finite limits.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in RingCat: coproducts (tensor products), pushouts, and coequalisers.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of rings: `RingCat`, `CommRingCat` — rings/comm. rings as objects; ring homomorphisms as morphisms.

Name: Adjunctions.lean
Description: Adjunctions in RingCat: the free ring adjunction, forgetful functor adjunctions.

Name: EqualizerPushout.lean
Description: Equalisers and pushouts in RingCat: explicit constructions of equalisers and pushouts of ring homomorphisms.

Name: Epi.lean
Description: Epimorphisms in RingCat: characterisation of epimorphisms (not just surjections, e.g. ℤ → ℚ is epi).

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard constructions in RingCat: products, localisations, and specific ring objects.

Name: MonCat
Description: The categories MonCat (monoids) and CommMonCat (commutative monoids): objects, morphisms (MonoidHom), free monoids, and forgetful functors to Grp and Type. The Eckmann-Hilton argument and delooping constructions live adjacent to this folder.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in MonCat: products and equalisers of monoids.

Name: ForgetCorepresentable.lean
Description: Forgetful functor for MonCat is corepresentable: `forget MonCat` is corepresented by the free monoid on one generator.

Name: Yoneda.lean
Description: Yoneda lemma for MonCat: Yoneda natural transformations from representable functors on monoids.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe shrinking for MonCat: `Shrink` equivalences for the category of monoids.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of monoids: `MonCat` — monoids as objects, monoid homomorphisms as morphisms.

Name: Adjunctions.lean
Description: Adjunctions in MonCat: the free monoid adjunction and abelianisation.

Name: FilteredColimits.lean
Description: Filtered colimits in MonCat: accessibility and filtered colimits commuting with finite limits.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in MonCat: coproducts (free products of monoids) and coequalisers.

Name: CoalgCat
Description: The category of coassociative counital coalgebras: R-modules equipped with a coproduct Δ : C → C ⊗ C and counit ε : C → R satisfying coassociativity (Δ ⊗ id) ∘ Δ = (id ⊗ Δ) ∘ Δ and counit axioms. Defines CoalgCat, its morphisms, the forgetful functor to ModuleCat, a monoidal structure, and the equivalence with comonoid objects in ModuleCat (comonEquivalence). Distinct from BialgCat (coalgebras without a compatible algebra structure) and AlgCat (the categorical dual).

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on CoalgCat: the tensor product of coalgebras.

Name: ComonEquivalence.lean
Description: Equivalence between CoalgCat and comonoid objects: coalgebras are equivalent to comonoid objects in the monoidal category of modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of coalgebras: `CoalgCat R` — `R`-coalgebras with comultiplication and counit; limits, colimits.

Name: BialgCat
Description: The category of bialgebras: R-modules carrying compatible algebra and coalgebra structures (Δ and ε are algebra homomorphisms, equivalently μ and η are coalgebra homomorphisms). Defines BialgCat, its morphisms, forgetful functors to AlgCat and CoalgCat, and the monoidal structure given by tensor product of bialgebras. Distinct from HopfAlgCat (additionally requires an antipode) and CoalgCat (no algebra structure).

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on BialgCat: the tensor product of bialgebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of bialgebras: `BialgCat R` — `R`-bialgebras (algebra + coalgebra compatible structures) as objects, bialgebra homomorphisms as morphisms.

Name: AlgCat
Description: The category AlgCat R of (associative, unital) R-algebras: objects are R-algebras, morphisms are AlgHoms. Contains limits, colimits, the free algebra adjunction, and forgetful functors to RingCat and ModuleCat. Distinct from CommAlgCat which restricts to commutative algebras.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on AlgCat: the tensor product `A ⊗_R B` making `AlgCat R` a symmetric monoidal category.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric monoidal structure on AlgCat: the braiding and coherence maps for the tensor product of algebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of algebras: `AlgCat R` — `R`-algebras as objects and algebra homomorphisms as morphisms; limits, colimits, and forgetful functors.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in AlgCat: products, equalisers, and pullbacks in the category of `R`-algebras.

Name: FGModuleCat
Description: The category FGModuleCat R of finitely generated R-modules: the full subcategory of ModuleCat on finitely generated objects. Relevant when finiteness is essential — e.g., for duality, determinants, or Noetherian arguments. Distinct from ModuleCat, which allows infinite-dimensional objects.

Name: EssentiallySmall.lean
Description: Essentially small FGModuleCat: the category of f.g. modules is essentially small over Noetherian rings.

Name: Limits.lean
Description: Limits in FGModuleCat: finite limits in the category of finitely-generated modules.

Name: Colimits.lean
Description: Colimits in FGModuleCat: finite colimits and the conditions for their existence.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: FGModuleCat is abelian: finitely-generated modules over a Noetherian ring form an abelian category.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of finitely-generated modules: `FGModuleCat R` — finitely-generated `R`-modules as objects; the full subcategory of `ModuleCat R`.

Name: ContinuousCohomology
Description: Continuous cohomology of a topological group G with coefficients in a continuous R-linear representation M: defined via the standard complex of continuous cochains Cⁿ(G, M) = {continuous maps Gⁿ → M} with the homogeneous differential. Defines continuousCohomology and homogeneousCochains. A foundational file covering the basic complex construction; further derived-functor properties are in development. Distinct from GroupCohomology (the algebraic version with no continuity requirement).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous cohomology in the categorical framework: the Ext functor for continuous group representations via derived categories.

Name: CommAlgCat
Description: The category CommAlgCat R of commutative R-algebras: the full subcategory of AlgCat on commutative objects. Contains the adjunction with affine schemes (Spec ⊣ Γ) and colimits via tensor products. Distinct from AlgCat: commutativity enables the tensor product to be the categorical coproduct.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on CommAlgCat: the tensor product making commutative algebras a symmetric monoidal category.

Name: FiniteType.lean
Description: Finite-type commutative algebras: the full subcategory of `CommAlgCat R` of finitely-generated algebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Category of commutative algebras: `CommAlgCat R` — commutative `R`-algebras; limits, colimits, and the forgetful functor to AlgCat.

Name: GrpWithZero.lean
Description: The category of groups-with-zero: objects are GroupWithZero instances, morphisms are maps preserving multiplication and zero. Contains the category structure and examples. Distinct from Category/BoolRing and GroupWithZero/Defs.

Name: CommBialgCat.lean
Description: The category of commutative bialgebras CommBialgCat: objects are commutative R-algebras with a compatible coalgebra structure (comultiplication and counit), morphisms are bialgebra homomorphisms. Distinct from Category/BoolRing and Monoidal/Bimon_.

Name: BoolRing.lean
Description: The category of Boolean rings BoolRing: objects are Boolean rings (every element is idempotent: x² = x), morphisms are ring homomorphisms. Contains the categorical structure and the equivalence with Boolean algebras via Stone duality. Distinct from Category/GrpWithZero and Ring/BooleanRing.

Name: BrauerGroup
Description: The Brauer group of a field: equivalence classes of central simple algebras under tensor product, classified by Galois cohomology.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: The Brauer group of a field: the group Br(k) of Morita equivalence classes of central simple k-algebras, with tensor product as group operation. Contains the BrauerGroup type, the neutral element (k itself), and the inverse (opposite algebra). Distinct from Azumaya/Basic and Central/Basic.

Name: Colimit
Description: Colimits of algebraic structures: direct limits of rings, modules, and groups along a directed system.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Filtered colimits of rings: the direct limit of a directed system of rings with the universal property and the interaction with ring homomorphisms. Distinct from Colimit/DirectLimit and Ring/Basic.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products commute with filtered colimits: the canonical map colim(A ⊗ Bᵢ) → A ⊗ colim(Bᵢ) is an isomorphism for filtered systems. Distinct from Colimit/Module and Algebra/Bilinear.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Filtered colimits of modules: the direct limit of a directed system of R-modules with the exactness of filtered colimits (filtered colimits are exact). Distinct from Colimit/DirectLimit (general structures) and Module/Basic.

Name: DirectLimit.lean
Description: Direct limits (filtered colimits) of algebraic structures: the direct limit of a directed system of rings, modules, or groups as the algebraic colimit, with the universal property. Distinct from Colimit/Ring and Colimit/Module.

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: Finiteness results for colimits: an element of a filtered colimit comes from a finite stage, and a relation between elements holds in a finite stage. Distinct from Colimit/DirectLimit and Presentable/Finite.

Name: CharZero
Description: The CharZero typeclass: rings where n * 1 is nonzero for all n > 0, including Q, R, and C.

Name: Infinite.lean
Description: Characteristic-zero rings are infinite: from CharZero R the canonical map ℕ → R is injective, making R infinite. Distinct from CharZero/Defs and Group/Torsion.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Characteristic zero for quotient rings: conditions for R/I to have characteristic zero. Distinct from CharZero/Defs and CharP/Quotient.

Name: AddMonoidHom.lean
Description: AddMonoidHom results in characteristic zero: injectivity of the canonical maps ℕ → R and ℤ → R in a characteristic-0 ring, and cancellation in ℕ. Distinct from CharZero/Defs and Ring/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for characteristic zero: the CharZero R typeclass asserting that the canonical map ℕ → R (n ↦ n • 1) is injective. Contains the definition and the equivalence with CharP R 0. Distinct from CharZero/Infinite (cardinality consequence) and CharP/Defs.

Name: CharP
Description: The CharP typeclass: rings of characteristic p where p * 1 = 0, with the Frobenius endomorphism in characteristic p.

Name: MixedCharZero.lean
Description: Mixed characteristic: rings of characteristic 0 whose residue field has characteristic p > 0, used in p-adic and perfectoid theory. Distinct from CharP/LocalRing and CharP/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Characteristic of product rings: the characteristic of ∏ i, R i is lcm of the characteristics of the factors (or 0 if unbounded). Distinct from CharP/Basic and Ring/Pi.

Name: LinearMaps.lean
Description: Linear maps in characteristic p: results about linear maps over characteristic-p fields, including the Frobenius as an ℱ-linear map and its effect on rank. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: LocalRing.lean
Description: Characteristic of local rings: the mixed-characteristic local ring (residue field of characteristic p, fraction field of characteristic 0) setup, and the p-adic ramification structure. Distinct from CharP/Basic and CharP/MixedCharZero.

Name: Subring.lean
Description: Characteristic of subrings: a subring of R inherits the characteristic of R. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Ring/Centralizer.

Name: Two.lean
Description: Characteristic-2 specialisations: the identities x + x = 0, -x = x, and (a + b)^2 = a^2 + b^2 that hold in characteristic 2. Distinct from CharP/Basic and CharP/Lemmas.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Characteristic of quotient rings: if R has characteristic p and I is a proper ideal, R/I has characteristic dividing p; conditions for equality. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Ring/Parity.

Name: Reduced.lean
Description: Reduced rings in characteristic p: rings with no nonzero nilpotents in characteristic p, and the perfectoid/perfect hull constructions. Distinct from CharP/Frobenius and CharP/Basic.

Name: CharAndCard.lean
Description: Relationship between characteristic and cardinality: in a finite field of cardinality q the characteristic is prime and q = p^n, with the formula relating char and |F|. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Field/ZMod.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for characteristic: the CharP R p typeclass asserting that p • (1 : R) = 0 and p is the smallest such positive integer. Contains CharP.cast_eq_zero and the uniqueness of the characteristic. Distinct from CharP/Basic (derived results).

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Characteristic of algebras: if R has characteristic p and A is an R-algebra then A has characteristic p (or a divisor), and the interaction of CharP with algebraMap. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results for characteristic-p rings: the characterisation of CharP via the kernel of ℤ → R, the prime characteristic theorem (characteristic is 0 or prime in a domain), and basic instances. Distinct from CharP/Defs (the typeclass) and CharP/Lemmas.

Name: Invertible.lean
Description: Invertibility in characteristic-p rings: elements of the form (p : R) become invertible after localising at p, and the p-invertible (CharP.IsInvertible) typeclass. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Ring/Units.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Computational lemmas in characteristic-p rings: the Freshman's dream (a + b)^p = a^p + b^p, binomial coefficient divisibility by p, and related identities. Distinct from CharP/Basic and CharP/Frobenius.

Name: Frobenius.lean
Description: The Frobenius endomorphism in characteristic p: the ring homomorphism φ_p : R → R given by x ↦ x^p, its properties in perfect fields, and the connection to the Frobenius lift. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Field/Basic.

Name: IntermediateField.lean
Description: Characteristic of intermediate fields: a field extension L/K has the same characteristic as K, applied to intermediate fields between K and L. Distinct from CharP/Basic and Field/Basic.

Name: AffineMonoid
Description: Affine monoids: finitely generated cancellative commutative monoids embedded in Z^n, used in toric geometry.

Name: Irreducible.lean
Description: Irreducible elements in affine monoids: elements that cannot be expressed as a sum of two non-unit elements, the building blocks for Hilbert basis computations. Distinct from AffineMonoid/Basic and Prime/Defs.

Name: UniqueSums.lean
Description: Unique sum decompositions in affine monoids: the unique-sums property asserting that the representation n = a + b is unique (up to trivial variations). Distinct from AffineMonoid/Basic and FreeMonoid/UniqueProds.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Affine monoids: finitely generated, cancellative, commutative, torsion-free monoids embeddable in ℤⁿ, the foundation for toric geometry. Contains the AffineMonoid typeclass and the Gorenstein property. Foundation for all AffineMonoid files.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embeddings of affine monoids: injective monoid homomorphisms between affine monoids and the associated rank and saturation conditions. Distinct from AffineMonoid/Basic and AffineMonoid/Irreducible.

Name: AddTorsor
Description: Additive torsors: sets on which an abelian group acts freely and transitively, the algebraic model for affine spaces.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for add torsors: the AddTorsor G P typeclass asserting that an additive group G acts freely and transitively on a set P via +ᵥ, with the vsub subtraction p -ᵥ q : G. Distinct from AddTorsor/Basic (derived lemmas).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic lemmas for add torsors: cancellation, uniqueness of translations, and the interaction between +ᵥ and -ᵥ. Contains instances for G acting on itself and for affine spaces. Distinct from AddTorsor/Defs (the typeclass definition).

Name: AddConstMap
Description: Maps that commute with adding a constant: a structure for studying quasi-periodic functions.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences that add a constant: AddConstMapEquiv for bijections f : G ≃ G of the form f(x) = x + c, with the group structure under composition. Distinct from AddConstMap/Basic (non-invertible maps).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Maps that add a fixed constant: the AddConstMap G H c typeclass for functions f : G → H satisfying f(x) = f(0) + c • x or f(x + y) = f(x) + c for a fixed c. Contains the basic instances, the group structure, and the interaction with AddMonoidHom. Distinct from AddConstMap/Equiv (invertible maps).

Name: BigOperators
Description: Big operators: sum and product notation for iterated sums and products over Finset, with reindexing and splitting lemmas.

Name: GroupWithZero
Description: Big-operator lemmas specific to GroupWithZero: products that are zero iff a factor is zero (Finset.prod_eq_zero_iff), and division of products. Handles the zero-divisor subtlety absent from the Group subfolder.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Finset big operators in a group with zero: `∏ x in s, f x` and zero-divisor behaviour.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Big operators and group-with-zero actions: `Finset.prod` interacting with scalar multiplication by a group-with-zero element.

Name: Ring
Description: Ring-specific big-operator identities: the geometric sum formula ∑ xⁱ = (xⁿ-1)/(x-1), Finset.prod_add_adjacent, multinomial coefficients, and distributivity of ∏ over ∑. Requires both addition and multiplication, unlike the Group or GroupWithZero subfolders.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Ring big operators for Multiset: `Multiset.sum`, `Multiset.prod` in rings, and their properties.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Ring big operators specialised to ℕ: specific Nat identities for `∑` and `∏`.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Ring big operators for Finset: `Finset.sum_mul`, `Finset.mul_sum`, distributivity lemmas for `∑` and `∏` over Finsets.

Name: List.lean
Description: Ring big operators for List: `List.sum_mul`, `List.prod_eq_zero_iff`, and related results.

Name: Finsupp
Description: Big-operator lemmas for Finsupp (finitely supported functions α →₀ β): expressing Finsupp.sum and Finsupp.prod in terms of Finset.sum/prod, and interchange of summation order. Distinct from BigOperators/Group which works with arbitrary Finsets rather than the Finsupp support.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Finsupp big operators for Fin-indexed types: specific lemmas about `∑ i : Fin n, f i` in the Finsupp context.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Big operators for Finsupp: `Finsupp.sum`, `Finsupp.prod`, and their interaction with `Finset.sum`/`Finset.prod`. Core API.

Name: Group
Description: The core ∑ / ∏ API over Finset for groups and monoids: Finset.sum, Finset.prod, and their congruence, reindexing, splitting, and telescoping lemmas. The primary file mathematicians use for any finite sum or product in an additive or multiplicative commutative monoid.

Name: Multiset
Description: Big operators over Multiset: Multiset.sum and Multiset.prod for commutative monoids, where the order of terms does not matter but repetition is tracked. The bridge between List (ordered, with repetition) and Finset (unordered, no repetition). Used in polynomial coefficient formulas and in counting arguments. Distinct from Finset (no multiplicities).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Multiset big operator definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multiset group big operators.

Name: List
Description: Big operators over List: List.sum and List.prod, which preserve the order of terms. Relates to Finset summation via List.sum_toFinset and the fact that List.sum is order-dependent (for noncommutative monoids). Used when the indexing order matters or when building Finset results from List primitives. Distinct from Finset (unordered) and Multiset (unordered but allows repetition).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: List big operator lemmas.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: List big operator definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: List group big operators: `List.prod`.

Name: Finset
Description: Big-operator API over Finset: the primary ∑ and ∏ notation for monoid-valued functions indexed by Finset, with congruence, reindexing (Finset.sum_congr, Finset.sum_bij), splitting (Finset.sum_union for disjoint sets), and commutativity of double sums. The most-used subfolder of BigOperators — the majority of Mathlib's finite summation lemmas live here. Distinct from List and Multiset (different index structures with ordering or repetition).

Name: Piecewise.lean
Description: Piecewise Finset big operators.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi Finset big operators.

Name: Powerset.lean
Description: Powerset Finset big operators.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma Finset big operators.

Name: Preimage.lean
Description: Preimage-based big operators.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Finset big operator lemmas.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Finset big operator definitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Finset group big operators: `∏ x in s, f x`.

Name: Gaps.lean
Description: Gaps in Finset big operators.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Big operators over intervals.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator-based Finset big operators.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Big operators over Option types: ∑ x : Option α, f x = f none + ∑ a : α, f (some a), and the corresponding product version. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: NatAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Big operators over natural number antidiagonals: ∑ (i, j) in Finset.antidiagonal n, f i j summed over pairs (i, j) with i + j = n. Used in convolution and binomial-type identities. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and BigOperators/Intervals.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Big operators for modules: ∑ i, c i • x i and the interaction of linear combinations with module operations. Distinct from BigOperators and Module/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Big operators for Pi types: ∑ and ∏ in ∏ i, f i with componentwise operations, and the interaction with Pi module and ring structures. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and Module/Pi.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Big operators with values in WithTop: sums ∑ in WithTop α (adjoining +∞) with the convention that +∞ is absorbing in products. Distinct from BigOperators and Order/WithTop.

Name: Sym.lean
Description: Big operators over symmetric powers: sums and products indexed by Sym n α (the n-element multisets of α), used in symmetric function theory. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and combinatorics files.

Name: RingEquiv.lean
Description: Big operators and ring equivalences: naturality of ∑ and ∏ under ring isomorphisms — f (∑ x ∈ s, g x) = ∑ x ∈ s, f (g x) for a ring equiv f. Distinct from BigOperators and Ring/Equiv.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Big operators and modular arithmetic: ∑ x ∈ s, f x ≡ ∑ x ∈ s, g x [MOD n] when f ≡ g pointwise. Distinct from BigOperators and Ring/Parity.

Name: Expect.lean
Description: The expectation operator 𝔼[f] = (∑_{x ∈ S} f x) / |S|: the average of a function over a finite set, with basic linearity, bound, and convexity results. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Balance.lean
Description: Balanced big operators: sums ∑ where each value appears with balanced multiplicity, used in Fourier analysis and combinatorics (e.g., balanced incomplete block designs). Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and BigOperators/Module.

Name: Associated.lean
Description: Big operator results for associated elements: products of associated elements are associated, and the interaction of ∏ with the divisibility and associate relations. Distinct from BigOperators files and Divisibility/Basic.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Big operator results for fields: ∑ and ∏ in a field, geometric sums, division in sums, and the inclusion-exclusion principle over fields. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and Field/Basic.

Name: Intervals.lean
Description: Big operators over ordered intervals: ∑ i ∈ Finset.Ico a b, f i and ∏ i ∈ Finset.Icc a b, f i, with the telescoping identity and boundary terms. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin and BigOperators/NatAntidiagonal.

Name: Finprod.lean
Description: Finitely supported products (finprod) over arbitrary types: ∏ᶠ x, f x using finite support, with the interaction between finprod and Finset.prod. Distinct from BigOperators/Fin (Finset-indexed) and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Big operators indexed by Fin n: ∑ i : Fin n, f i and ∏ i : Fin n, f i, including the telescoping identity ∑ i : Fin (n+1), f i.succ = ∑ i : Fin n, f i + f n. Distinct from BigOperators and BigOperators/Intervals.

Name: Azumaya
Description: Azumaya algebras: locally free algebras of finite rank that are locally isomorphic to matrix algebras, generalizing central simple algebras.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix algebras as Azumaya algebras: Mₙ(R) is an Azumaya R-algebra for any commutative ring R, with the explicit isomorphism Mₙ(R) ⊗_R Mₙ(R)^op ≅ End_R(Rⁿ). Distinct from Azumaya/Basic and BrauerGroup/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Azumaya algebras: the IsAzumaya R A predicate asserting that A is a faithful, finitely generated, projective R-module and A ⊗_R A^op ≅ End_R(A). Distinct from Azumaya/Basic (derived properties) and Azumaya/Matrix.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of Azumaya algebras: tensor products of Azumaya algebras are Azumaya, the Brauer equivalence relation, and the connection to central simple algebras over fields. Distinct from Azumaya/Defs and Azumaya/Matrix.

Name: Algebra
Description: Algebras over a commutative ring: the Algebra typeclass, algebra homomorphisms, subalgebras, and tensor product of algebras.

Name: Subalgebra
Description: Subalgebras of an R-algebra: sub-rings that are also closed under the scalar action of R. The Subalgebra type, the lattice of subalgebras, the subalgebra generated by a set, and the inclusion as an AlgHom. Distinct from Subring (which ignores the scalar action) and Submodule (which ignores multiplication).

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on subalgebras: `s * t` as a subalgebra and the pointwise product. Distinct from Operations.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Subalgebras of pi algebras: componentwise subalgebras of `Π i, A i`. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order structure on subalgebras: inclusion order and the interaction with the lattice operations. Distinct from Lattice.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Subalgebras of product algebras: `s.prod t ≤ A × B` and the product of two subalgebras. Distinct from Pi.

Name: Unitization.lean
Description: Unitization of a subalgebra: the subalgebra of the unitization generated by a non-unital subalgebra. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Tower law for subalgebras: if `R ≤ S ≤ A`, then `A` as an `R`-module via `S` satisfies the tower property. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank of a subalgebra: `Module.rank K S` for a subalgebra `S` of a `K`-algebra, and its relationship to the ambient algebra's rank.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on subalgebras: images and preimages under algebra maps, range and kernel subalgebras. Distinct from Map.

Name: Directed.lean
Description: Directed unions of subalgebras: a directed union of subalgebras is a subalgebra. Distinct from Lattice.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: Centralizer subalgebra: `Subalgebra.centralizer s` — elements commuting with all of `s`, as a subalgebra. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for subalgebras: `Subalgebra.toSubring`, `Subalgebra.map`, `Subalgebra.adjoin`, span, and the lattice structure. Main workhorse for subalgebra manipulation.

Name: IsSimpleOrder.lean
Description: Simple order on subalgebras: conditions under which the lattice of subalgebras is simple (only `⊥` and `⊤`). Distinct from Lattice.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Subalgebra of the opposite algebra: `Subalgebra.op s` — transporting a subalgebra to the opposite ring. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Subalgebras of matrix algebras: conditions for a set of matrices to form a subalgebra and the adjoin of a matrix. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice of subalgebras: `⊓` is intersection, `⊔` is the subalgebra generated by the union; completeness. Distinct from Basic.

Name: Spectrum
Description: The spectrum of an element in a unital algebra: σ(a) = {λ ∈ k | (a − λ·1) is not invertible}. Foundational for Banach and C*-algebra spectral theory. Distinct from the prime spectrum of a ring — this spectrum lives in the scalar field, not in the ring itself.

Name: Quasispectrum.lean
Description: Quasispectrum: `quasispectrum R a` — the spectrum for non-unital algebras, defined without requiring invertibility. Relates to the spectrum in the unitization.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Spectrum in product algebras: `spectrum R (f : Π i, A i) = ⋃ i, spectrum R (f i)`. Componentwise characterisation. Distinct from Spectrum/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Spectrum of an algebra element: `spectrum R a = {x : R | ¬IsUnit (algebraMap R A x - a)}`, its basic properties (nonempty over algebraically closed fields, closed under polynomial maps). Distinct from Pi and Quasispectrum variants.

Name: Hom
Description: Algebra homomorphisms (AlgHom): ring homomorphisms R → A → B that also commute with the R-algebra structure. Distinct from RingHom because they carry the additional datum that the scalar action is preserved. Contains composition, the identity AlgHom, kernel/range, and the AlgHom typeclass hierarchy.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: Algebra homomorphisms from ℚ: any `ℚ`-algebra has a unique `ℚ`-algebra map from ℚ, making `RatAlgHom` a singleton. Provides the `Rat.instAlgebraHom` instance.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: Algebras over ℚ: every characteristic-0 ring is a ℚ-algebra via the canonical map ℚ → A, and uniqueness of the ℚ-algebra structure. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and CharZero/Defs.

Name: RestrictScalars.lean
Description: Restriction of scalars: given a ring map R → S and an S-algebra A, the RestrictScalars R S A structure makes A into an R-algebra via composition with the ring map. Distinct from Algebra/Tower and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Binary product algebras: the R-algebra structure on A × B with componentwise operations and the product algebraMap. Distinct from Algebra/Pi (arbitrary products) and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite algebra: if A is an R-algebra then A^op is an R-algebra with reversed multiplication, and the anti-isomorphism A →ₐ[R] A^op. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Product algebras (infinite): the R-algebra structure on ∏ i, A i when each A i is an R-algebra, with componentwise algebraMap. Distinct from Algebra/Prod (binary product) and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-level shrinking for algebras: the Shrink construction transporting an R-algebra to a smaller universe. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Module/Shrink.

Name: Unitization.lean
Description: Unitization of a non-unital algebra: the Unitization R A construction freely adjoining a unit to a non-unital R-algebra, with the universal property that any unital algebra map from A extends. Distinct from Algebra/NonUnitalSubalgebra and Ring/Units.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: Algebras over ZMod n: the unique R-algebra structure when R has characteristic n, and the ZMod n-algebra instances for rings of characteristic n. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and CharP/Defs.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring algebra instances along ring equivalences: if A ≅ B (as rings/modules) and A is an R-algebra, the R-algebra structure transfers to B. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Ring/TransferInstance.

Name: StrictPositivity.lean
Description: Strictly positive elements in ordered algebras: elements satisfying 0 < a in an ordered R-algebra, with the multiplicative closure and positivity-preserving algebra maps. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Order/Algebra.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Towers of algebras: the IsScalarTower R S A typeclass asserting that the R-action on A factors through S, with the tower axiom (r • (s • a) = (r • s) • a). Contains basic instances and the interaction with algebraMap. Distinct from Algebra/RestrictScalars and Algebra/Basic.

Name: Epi.lean
Description: Epimorphisms in the category of R-algebras: characterisation showing that algebra epimorphisms are not just surjections — localisations and separable extensions are also epi. Distinct from Algebra/Hom and Ring/InjSurj.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Algebra equivalences (R-algebra isomorphisms): the AlgEquiv R A B type, its group structure, interaction with algebraMap, and the associated API. Distinct from Algebra/Hom (non-invertible maps) and Ring/Equiv.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for the Algebra typeclass: the algebraMap ring homomorphism R → A, the smul-algebraMap compatibility axiom, and basic instances (R is an R-algebra via id, any ring is a ℤ-algebra). Distinct from Algebra/Basic (derived lemmas) and Algebra/Hom.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of R-algebras: consequences of the Algebra typeclass — the algebraMap is central, scalar multiplication agrees with ring multiplication by scalars, and basic instances for subalgebras and quotients. Distinct from Algebra/Defs (the typeclass definition) and Algebra/Hom.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Bilinear maps in the algebra context: the R-bilinear multiplication map A ⊗_R A → A, the universal property of the tensor product as R-algebras, and the associated linear maps. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Module/Bimodule.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Algebras over fields: when R is a field every R-algebra is a vector space, and the algebraMap is injective. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Field/Basic.

Name: NonUnitalSubalgebra.lean
Description: Non-unital subalgebras: subsets of an R-algebra closed under the module structure and multiplication, not required to contain the unit element. Distinct from Algebra/NonUnitalHom and Ring/Centralizer.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on sub-R-modules viewed as algebras: multiplication A • B, powers A ^ n, and the lattice structure on submodules under these operations. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Module/Lattice.

Name: NonUnitalHom.lean
Description: Non-unital algebra homomorphisms: NonUnitalAlgHom for maps between non-unital R-algebras preserving the module structure and multiplication but not necessarily the unit. Distinct from Algebra/Hom (unital version) and Algebra/NonUnitalSubalgebra.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Algebra homomorphisms (R-algebra maps): the AlgHom R A B type, composition, the category of R-algebras, and the relationship with ring homomorphisms. Distinct from Algebra/Equiv (isomorphisms) and Algebra/Defs.

Name: IsSimpleRing.lean
Description: Simple rings and simple algebras: the IsSimpleRing typeclass (no proper two-sided ideals), with instances for matrix algebras and division rings. Distinct from Algebra/Basic and Ring/Idempotent.

Name: FreeMonoid
Description: The free monoid on a type: lists with concatenation, the universal property, and maps from free monoids to monoids.

Name: Symbols.lean
Description: Singleton words in the free monoid: the map FreeMonoid.of : α → FreeMonoid α sending a to the single-letter word [a] and its universal role in the free monoid. Distinct from FreeMonoid/Basic.

Name: UniqueProds.lean
Description: Unique products in the free monoid: the unique-products property (no two distinct pairs (u, v) have uv = w for a given word w), and the connection to ordered group embeddability. Distinct from FreeMonoid/Basic and AffineMonoid/UniqueSums.

Name: FreeSemigroup.lean
Description: The free semigroup as a sub-structure: the free semigroup on α consists of nonempty words (FreeMonoid α \ {1}), with the inherited semigroup structure. Distinct from FreeMonoid/Basic and FreeMonoid/UniqueProds.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The free monoid on a type: FreeMonoid α = List α with concatenation as multiplication, the universal property (any function α → M extends uniquely to a monoid hom), and the length homomorphism. Foundation for all FreeMonoid files.

Name: Count.lean
Description: The letter-count map: the monoid homomorphism FreeMonoid α →* ℕ counting occurrences of a fixed letter a, and its basic properties. Distinct from FreeMonoid/Basic and FreeMonoid/Symbols.

Name: FreeAlgebra
Description: The free (noncommutative) algebra on a set of generators over a ring: the universal associative algebra without relations.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of the free R-algebra: |FreeAlgebra R X| = max(|R|, |X|, ℵ₀) for an infinite set X or ring R. Distinct from the main FreeAlgebra definitions and Algebra/Basic.

Name: FreeAbelianGroup
Description: The free abelian group on a type: formal integer linear combinations, the universal property, and connection to Finsupp.

Name: UniqueSums.lean
Description: Unique sums in the free abelian group: FreeAbelianGroup α has the unique-sums property (no two distinct finitely supported signed combinations of the same elements yield the same element). Distinct from FreeAbelianGroup/Finsupp and AffineMonoid/UniqueSums.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: The free abelian group as finitely supported integer-valued functions: the isomorphism FreeAbelianGroup α ≅ α →₀ ℤ and the induced group and module structure. Distinct from FreeAbelianGroup/UniqueSums and DirectSum/Finsupp.

Name: GroupWithZero
Description: Groups with zero: monoids with a zero element where nonzero elements form a group, the algebraic abstraction of a division ring.

Name: Submonoid
Description: Submonoids of a GroupWithZero: multiplicative subsets containing 1 but not necessarily 0. Used to define localizations at multiplicative sets in ring theory, where the localized ring inverts the nonzero elements. Distinct from Group/Submonoid which lacks a zero element.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise submonoid operations in a group-with-zero: `s * t` as a submonoid-set.

Name: Primal.lean
Description: Primal elements in a group-with-zero: conditions for primality in submonoids of `G₀`.

Name: CancelMulZero.lean
Description: Cancellative submonoids in a group-with-zero: conditions for cancellation in submonoids of `G₀`.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard submonoid instances in a group-with-zero: `G₀ˣ` as a submonoid and related instances.

Name: Units
Description: Units of a GroupWithZero: elements a where there exists b with a * b = 1 = b * a. Because 0 is not a unit, this gives the nonzero elements with their group structure. Contains the embedding into the ambient GroupWithZero and the relationship to the field structure.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for units of a group-with-zero: specific identities for `G₀ˣ`.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences involving units of a group-with-zero: isomorphisms between `G₀ˣ` and related structures.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for units of a group-with-zero: `G₀ˣ`, invertibility, and `IsUnit` conditions.

Name: Action
Description: Actions of GroupWithZero structures: SMul instances where the scalar monoid has a zero that annihilates. Needed for modules over division rings and for scalar multiplication by real numbers where zero scaling collapses everything. Distinct from Group/Action which excludes zero scalars.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise GroupWithZero actions on sets: lifting a GroupWithZero scalar action to Set and Finset, with the convention 0 • S = ∅ and s • ∅ = ∅. Used in convex geometry (scaling a convex set by a nonneg scalar) and in module theory (the zero scalar collapses the set). Distinct from Group/Action/Pointwise (no zero, no annihilation convention).

Name: Set.lean
Description: Pointwise set action by group-with-zero.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Pointwise Finset action by group-with-zero.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi group-with-zero actions: pointwise action on `Π i, α i`.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Opposite group-with-zero action: the action via `MulOpposite` of a group-with-zero.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of group-with-zero actions: zero-preserving equivariant maps.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units acting in a group-with-zero: the action of `G₀ˣ` on `G₀`.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transfer of group-with-zero action instances along equivalences.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product group-with-zero actions: diagonal action on `α × β`.

Name: ConjAct.lean
Description: Conjugation action for group-with-zero: the conjugation action of `G₀` on itself.

Name: Center.lean
Description: Central elements acting: the center of a group-with-zero action and its commutativity properties.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic group-with-zero action API: interaction of scalar multiplication with zero, `smul_zero`, `zero_smul`.

Name: Faithful.lean
Description: Faithful group-with-zero actions: `FaithfulSMul` for group-with-zero scalars.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphisms for group-with-zero actions: the endomorphism monoid with zero.

Name: Defs.lean
