Description: Definitions for group-with-zero actions: `SMulZeroClass`, `MulZeroClass.SMul`, and the zero-preservation axioms.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise set operations for types with a GroupWithZero structure: set multiplication, scalar multiplication of sets by a GroupWithZero element, and the interaction with zero. Analogous to Group/Pointwise but with zero-divisor considerations.

Name: Set
Description: Pointwise GroupWithZero operations on sets: set-valued scalar multiplication {r • s | s ∈ S} where r is a GroupWithZero element, with the zero-annihilation convention. Used in describing the dilation of a set by a scalar in a module. Distinct from Group/Pointwise/Set (no zero element) and from the Finset variant (requires finiteness).

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality for pointwise group-with-zero sets.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pointwise set operations for group-with-zero.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Pointwise Finset operations in a group-with-zero: `s * t` and `c • s` for Finsets in `G₀`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Binary product group-with-zero: the GroupWithZero structure on G × H. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Pi (indexed) and GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: ProdHom.lean
Description: Product homomorphisms for groups-with-zero: the diagonal and product constructions for MonoidWithZeroHom. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Prod and GroupWithZero/Hom.

Name: Range.lean
Description: The range of a group-with-zero homomorphism: the image is a sub-group-with-zero. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Hom and GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi-type group-with-zero: the GroupWithZero structure on ∏ i, G i with componentwise multiplication and zero. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and GroupWithZero/Prod.

Name: NeZero.lean
Description: The NeZero typeclass: NeZero a asserts a ≠ 0 as a typeclass, enabling automatic discharge of nonzero hypotheses in group-with-zero computations. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Ring/NonZeroDivisors.

Name: NonZeroDivisors.lean
Description: Non-zero-divisors in a group-with-zero: elements a ≠ 0 such that ab = 0 → b = 0, forming the submonoid nonZeroDivisors R. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Ring/NonZeroDivisors.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite group-with-zero G^op: reversed multiplication with the same zero and inverse, and the anti-isomorphism. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/Opposite.

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular elements in a group-with-zero: left-regular (ax = ay → x = y) and right-regular elements, which coincide with the non-zero-divisors in a CancelMonoidWithZero. Distinct from GroupWithZero/NonZeroDivisors and Regular/Basic.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring group-with-zero instances along equivalences. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/TransferInstance.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift for groups-with-zero: the GroupWithZero structure on ULift G. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: WithZero.lean
Description: The WithZero construction: freely adjoining an absorbing zero to a monoid or group G to form WithZero G = Option G, with 0 absorbing under multiplication. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Defs and GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: Torsion.lean
Description: Torsion in groups-with-zero: nonzero torsion elements (elements g with gⁿ = 1 for some n > 0) and the torsion subgroup of the units. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/Torsion.

Name: Semiconj.lean
Description: Semiconjugacy in groups-with-zero: the SemiconjBy a b c predicate (ab = ca) in the context of a zero element, and its basic properties. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Commute and Group/Conj.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-level shrinking for groups-with-zero. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: Subgroup.lean
Description: Subgroups of a group-with-zero: the group of units Gˣ as a subgroup of G, and its interaction with divisibility and inverses. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Ring/Subgroup.

Name: Conj.lean
Description: Conjugation in groups-with-zero: the conjugation action by units and fixed-point sets. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/Conj.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for groups-with-zero and related structures: the MulZeroClass, MonoidWithZero, GroupWithZero, and CancelMonoidWithZero typeclasses, with the absorbing zero axiom. Foundation for all GroupWithZero files.

Name: Divisibility.lean
Description: Divisibility in groups-with-zero: a ∣ b in the presence of an absorbing zero, with dvd_zero (everything divides 0) and zero_dvd (0 only divides 0). Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Divisibility/Basic.

Name: Commute.lean
Description: Commutativity in groups-with-zero: Commute a b (ab = ba) and the semiconjugate relation in the presence of zero, with commuting elements and their powers. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Ring/Commute.

Name: Associated.lean
Description: Associated elements in a group-with-zero: a and b are associates if a = u * b for a unit u, with the associated equivalence relation and interaction with divisibility. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Divisibility/Units.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic lemmas for groups-with-zero: zero-product property, cancellation by nonzero elements, units vs nonzero elements, and the interaction between inv and zero. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Defs and GroupWithZero/Commute.

Name: Center.lean
Description: The center of a group-with-zero: elements commuting with all others (including zero), and the center of the units subgroup. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/Center.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences (isomorphisms) of groups-with-zero: MulEquiv restricted to GroupWithZero and the associated API. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/InjSurj.

Name: Int.lean
Description: Integer powers in a group-with-zero: x ^ n for n : ℤ via zpow, with x ^ (-n) = (x ^ n)⁻¹ and the full zpow laws. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and GroupWithZero/Nat.

Name: Invertible.lean
Description: The Invertible typeclass for groups-with-zero: elements with an explicit two-sided inverse (distinct from the partial inv which sends 0 to 0). Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Ring/Invertible.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: Natural number powers in a group-with-zero: x ^ n for n : ℕ with x ^ 0 = 1 and x ^ (n+1) = x * x ^ n. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Int and GroupWithZero/Basic.

Name: InjSurj.lean
Description: Transfer of group-with-zero structure: Function.Injective.groupWithZero and Function.Surjective versions. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/InjSurj.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of groups-with-zero: the MonoidWithZeroHom type, composition, and the zero homomorphism. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Ring/Hom.

Name: Idempotent.lean
Description: Idempotents in a group-with-zero: the only idempotents in a CancelMonoidWithZero are 0 and 1. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/Idempotent.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator functions valued in a group-with-zero: the indicator 1_S : α → G₀ taking value 1 on S and 0 elsewhere, with products and sums. Distinct from GroupWithZero/Basic and Group/Indicator.

Name: Group
Description: Groups and monoids: the core typeclass hierarchy (Semigroup, Monoid, Group), subgroups, and basic group lemmas.

Name: Subgroup
Description: The Subgroup type: bundled subsets of a group closed under multiplication and inversion. The lattice of subgroups (inf = intersection, sup = generated subgroup), normal subgroups as a sublattice, and coset quotients. The primary home of subgroup theory at the algebraic level; GroupTheory/Subgroup builds more advanced results on top.

Name: ZPowers
Description: The subgroup generated by integer powers of a single element: zpowers g = {g^n | n : ℤ}, the cyclic subgroup ⟨g⟩. The universal property (every homomorphism from ⟨g⟩ is determined by the image of g), the order of g as |⟨g⟩|, and the isomorphism ⟨g⟩ ≅ ℤ/nℤ. Distinct from the parent Subgroup folder (arbitrary subgroups) — this is specifically the cyclic closure of one element.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas about zpowers subgroups.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Subgroup generated by integer powers: `zpowers g`.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Subgroups of the opposite group: `Subgroup.op H` and the `ᵐᵒᵖ`-conjugate.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Images and preimages of subgroups: `Subgroup.map f H` and `Subgroup.comap f H`, their basic properties.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice of subgroups: intersection as meet, subgroup generated by union as join; completeness.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on subgroups: `s * t` as a set, `smul_set_closure`, and coset arithmetic.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Ordered subgroups: `OrderedSubgroup` and subgroups of ordered groups.

Name: MulOppositeLemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for subgroups in the opposite group: additional results about `Subgroup.op` and `Subgroup.comap`.

Name: Ker.lean
Description: Kernel and range of group homomorphisms: `MonoidHom.ker`, `MonoidHom.range`, first isomorphism theorem.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Subgroup G`: a `Submonoid G` closed under inverses. Type definition and the `SubgroupClass` typeclass.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for subgroups: `Subgroup.closure`, `Subgroup.map`, `Subgroup.comap`, the lattice, and the correspondence theorem.

Name: Actions.lean
Description: Actions on subgroups: the conjugation action of a group on its subgroups and the orbit-stabiliser for this action.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finsupp structure of subgroups: the free abelian group structure on a subgroup via Finsupp.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite subgroups: `Subgroup.Finite`, the index formula, and finiteness of a subgroup given the group is finite.

Name: Even.lean
Description: Even-index subgroups: the subgroup of squares and even-exponent elements.

Name: Submonoid
Description: The Submonoid type: bundled subsets of a monoid closed under multiplication and containing 1. The lattice of submonoids, the submonoid generated by a set, and the closure operator. Distinct from Subgroup (no inversion required) and Subsemigroup (must also contain the identity).

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on submonoids: images, preimages, products, and derived submonoids.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Submonoids of the opposite monoid: `Submonoid.op s` in `Mᵒᵖ`.

Name: MulAction.lean
Description: MulAction of a submonoid: the action of `M` restricts to an action of any submonoid `S ≤ M`.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise submonoid operations: `s * t` as a submonoid-set, `smul_set`, and pointwise API.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Submonoid of units: `Submonoid.IsUnit` — the submonoid of units inside a monoid.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of a submonoid map: `Submonoid.support f` — elements of the submonoid on which `f` is nontrivial.

Name: Saturation.lean
Description: Saturated submonoids: `Submonoid.saturate s` — the smallest submonoid containing `s` and closed under divisibility.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Submonoid M`: a subset closed under `1` and `*`. Type definition and `SubmonoidClass`.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators and submonoids: `Finset.prod` taking values in a submonoid.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for submonoids: `Submonoid.closure`, `Submonoid.map`, the lattice, and `Submonoid.powers`.

Name: DistribMulAction.lean
Description: DistribMulAction on submonoids: the action of a submonoid as a `DistribMulAction`.

Name: Membership.lean
Description: Membership in submonoids: `mem_closure_iff`, `mem_powers_iff`, and characterisation lemmas.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finsupp and submonoids: the free monoid structure and Finsupp representations.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite submonoids: `Submonoid.Finite` and finiteness conditions.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise operations on sets and functions within a group: set multiplication A * B = {a * b | a ∈ A, b ∈ B}, set inversion A⁻¹, and the resulting algebraic structure. Used in additive combinatorics (sumsets) and geometric group theory. Distinct from Group/Action, which is about an external group acting on a set.

Name: Set
Description: Pointwise operations on Set: A * B = {a * b | a ∈ A, b ∈ B} and A⁻¹ = {a⁻¹ | a ∈ A} for a group or monoid. The resulting set-valued algebra (Set forms a near-ring under pointwise operations). Used in group theory (the product HK of two subgroups) and in analysis (neighborhoods form a filter under addition). Distinct from Finset (finiteness allows cardinality bounds).

Name: ListOfFn.lean
Description: Pointwise sets from lists.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice operations on pointwise sets.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Small pointwise sets.

Name: Scalar.lean
Description: Scalar operations on sets.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for pointwise sets.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pointwise set group operations.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite pointwise sets.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of pointwise sets.

Name: Finset
Description: Pointwise operations on Finset: Finset multiplication A * B = (A ×ˢ B).image (· * ·) and addition A + B, with the resulting Finset semiring structure. The doubling trick |A + A| ≤ |A|² and Plünnecke-Ruzsa estimates. Used in additive combinatorics and in bounding sumsets. Distinct from Pointwise/Set (same operations on Set, without finiteness constraints or cardinality bounds).

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Intervals in pointwise Finsets.

Name: Scalar.lean
Description: Scalar operations on Finsets.

Name: Density.lean
Description: Density of pointwise Finsets.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pointwise Finset group operations.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for pointwise Finsets.

Name: Semiconj
Description: Semiconjugacy: SemiconjBy a x y := a * x = y * a, a one-sided conjugation relation weaker than Commute. Contains closure under composition and powers, used in ring theory (for skew polynomial rings) and dynamical systems. Distinct from Commute, which requires a * b = b * a.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Semiconjugacy of units: `SemiconjBy aˣ x y` and the interaction between semiconjugacy and units.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `SemiconjBy a x y`: `a * x = y * a`. The definition and its immediate corollaries.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for semiconjugacy: `SemiconjBy a x y` (`a * x = y * a`) — basic lemmas and group/ring instances.

Name: Subsemigroup
Description: The Subsemigroup type: subsets closed only under multiplication, without requiring an identity or inverses. The coarsest substructure notion. Used in the initial segment of the typeclass hierarchy before identity and invertibility are imposed.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Subsemigroups of the opposite semigroup: `Subsemigroup.op s` in `Mᵒᵖ`.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on subsemigroups: images, preimages, and products.

Name: Membership.lean
Description: Membership in subsemigroups: `mem_closure_iff` and characterisation of elements in the generated subsemigroup.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for subsemigroups: `Subsemigroup.closure`, `Subsemigroup.map`, and the lattice.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Subsemigroup M`: a subset closed under multiplication. Type definition and `SubsemigroupClass`.

Name: Units
Description: The Units type: units of a monoid packaged as a quadruple (val, inv, val_inv, inv_val). The group of units Mˣ, the embedding Mˣ →* M, and the map from a group homomorphism G →* M to Gˣ →* Mˣ. Distinct from Invertible (which is a typeclass without packaging the inverse).

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of units: `Units.map f` — a ring hom `f : α →* β` induces a group hom `αˣ →* βˣ`.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Units of the opposite monoid: `(αᵐᵒᵖ)ˣ ≅ αˣ` and the `MulOpposite.instUnits` instance.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Isomorphisms involving units: `Units.equivIsUnit` and equivalences between units of different rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for units: `Units.mk`, `IsUnit.unit`, `Units.map`, and the group structure on `αˣ`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Units α`: a pair `(val, inv, val_inv, inv_val)` giving an explicit two-sided inverse in a monoid.

Name: WithOne
Description: Adjoining a formal identity to a semigroup: WithOne α = Option α with a * 1 = a = 1 * a. The universal way to make a semigroup into a monoid. Contains the embedding α →* WithOne α and the universal property. Distinct from WithZero, which adjoins a zero rather than a one.

Name: Map.lean
Description: Maps involving `WithOne`: `WithOne.lift` — extending a map from `α` to a monoid to one from `WithOne α`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `WithOne α = α ⊕ Unit`: the type with an adjoined identity element and its monoid instance.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for `WithOne α`: adjoining an identity element to a type, basic instances and `WithOne.coe_inj`.

Name: TypeTags
Description: The Additive and Multiplicative type tags: newtype wrappers that convert between additive and multiplicative typeclass instances. Additive α carries additive instances when α has multiplicative ones, and vice versa. Essential for @[to_additive] automation and for structures that are naturally stated in one convention but needed in another.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms for type tags: converting `MonoidHom` ↔ `AddMonoidHom` via the type tag equivalence.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations for type tags: `Additive.smul` and related pointwise type-tag instances.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Type tags `Additive` and `Multiplicative`: the core API converting between additive and multiplicative notation.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finiteness for type tags: `Fintype (Additive α)` and `Fintype (Multiplicative α)` instances.

Name: UniqueProds
Description: The UniqueProds and UniqueSums properties: conditions on a monoid M guaranteeing that in any finite product in the monoid ring R[M], the product of two elements has a unique maximal term. Used to prove that group rings over ordered groups are domains. Distinct from standard group axioms — this is a property about the monoid ring, not the monoid itself.

Name: VectorSpace.lean
Description: Unique products in vector spaces: `UniqueProds` instances for vector spaces over ordered fields.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unique products property: `UniqueProds G` — every pair of finite subsets `A, B` has a unique pair `(a, b) ∈ A × B` with `a * b` not achieved by any other pair. Implies the group ring has no zero divisors.

Name: Pi
Description: Pi-type instances for groups and monoids: the pointwise group structure on (i : ι) → f i, with component-wise multiplication and the universal property of the product. Distinct from BigOperators which computes over finite index sets; Pi covers all (possibly infinite) products.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of pi types: `(Π i, G i)ˣ ≅ Π i, Gᵢˣ` and unit-related pi lemmas.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for pi groups: pointwise operations, `Pi.funext_iff`, and interaction with products.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pi group instances: `Π i, G i` is a group/monoid pointwise; core instances and `Pi.mulLeft`.

Name: Equiv
Description: Multiplicative and additive equivalences: MulEquiv α β (bijective MonoidHoms with a two-sided inverse), and AddEquiv α β. Contains the group of automorphisms, composition, inversion, and the relationship to Equiv. Distinct from Hom which only requires one-directional maps.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Isomorphism with the opposite monoid: `MulEquiv.toOpposite` — a commutative monoid is isomorphic to its opposite.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Isomorphisms for type tags: `MulEquiv.toAdditive`, `Additive ≃+ α` instances.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite group isomorphisms: `MulEquiv` instances for `Fin n`, and automorphism group of a finite group.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for group/monoid isomorphisms: `MulEquiv.trans`, `.symm`, the group structure on `Aut M`, and standard isomorphisms.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `MulEquiv α β`: a bijective monoid homomorphism with a monoid-homomorphism inverse.

Name: Fin
Description: Group and monoid structure on Fin n: modular arithmetic, the cyclic group ℤ/nℤ realized as Fin n, and its universal property. Distinct from Data/Fin which focuses on the ordered and fintype structure without the algebraic group law.

Name: Tuple.lean
Description: Tuple operations on Fin-indexed families: `Fin.cons`, `Fin.snoc`, and their interaction with group operations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Group structure on Fin n: `Fin.addGroupWithOne`, the cyclic group structure `ℤ/nℤ ≅ Fin n`, and `Fin.val_natCast`.

Name: Action
Description: Group and monoid actions on types: the MulAction and SMul typeclasses, the orbit and stabilizer of a point, fixed points, and the orbit-stabilizer relation. The algebraic foundation used by Topology/Algebra and GroupTheory/GroupAction. Distinct from Ring/Action which additionally requires distributivity.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise lifting of group actions to power sets: if G acts on α, then G acts on Set α and Finset α pointwise via g • S = {g • s | s ∈ S}. The set-theoretic version of the group action on a set of elements, used in sumset theory and in the definition of G-invariant subsets. Distinct from Group/Pointwise (which defines the set product A * B rather than lifting an existing action).

Name: Set
Description: Pointwise group actions lifted to Set: the action of G on Set α by g • S = {g • s | s ∈ S}, with stabilisers of sets, G-invariant subsets (fixed points of the Set-action), and the orbit of a subset. Distinct from the parent Pointwise folder (which defines set-arithmetic) — this folder specifically lifts an existing G-action on α to sets of elements of α.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite pointwise action on sets.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pointwise action on sets.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Pointwise action on Finsets.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Group action on product types: the diagonal action on `α × β`.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Group action on sigma types: the action on `Σ i, α i`.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Group action on pi types: the pointwise action on `Π i, α i`.

Name: Pretransitive.lean
Description: `IsPretransitive M α`: there exists a group element mapping any element to any other (possibly with fixed point).

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Group action instances for type tags: `Additive`, `Multiplicative` action instances.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Group action of units: the action of `αˣ` on `α` by multiplication, and its properties.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Group action on sum types: the action on `α ⊕ β` acting on each component.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring group action instances along equivalences: `Equiv.smul` and related transfer lemmas.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphisms as a group action: the action of `End M` on `M` and its compatibility with the monoid structure.

Name: Equidecomp.lean
Description: Equidecomposability: `Equidecomposable G s t` — sets equidecomposable under a group action (Banach–Tarski context).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for group actions: `MulAction.toFun`, orbit maps, fixed-point lemmas, and the basic instances for Nat/Int scalars.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for group actions: `SMul`, `MulAction`, `AddAction`, `DistribMulAction` typeclasses and their basic axioms.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Action of the opposite monoid: `MulOpposite.instSMul` — right actions via the opposite monoid.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Group action on `Option α`: extending an action on `α` to `Option α` by fixing `none`.

Name: Faithful.lean
Description: `FaithfulSMul M α`: the action is faithful iff distinct elements of `M` act differently. Properties and instances.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms of group actions: `MulActionHom M X Y` — equivariant maps; composition and the identity.

Name: Commute
Description: Commuting pairs of elements: Commute a b := a * b = b * a and the weaker SemiconjBy a x y := a * x = y * a. Contains closure under powers, the centralizer, and the center as a submonoid. Distinct from Semiconj (which formalizes one-sided conjugation rather than commutativity).

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Commuting elements and homomorphisms: if `Commute a b` then `Commute (f a) (f b)` for a monoid hom `f`.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Commuting units: `Commute aˣ b` and the interaction between commutativity and units.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: API for commuting elements: `Commute a b` (`a * b = b * a`) — basic lemmas for rings and groups.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Commute a b = SemiConjBy a b b`: two elements commute iff one is a semiconjugacy of the other by itself.

Name: Hom
Description: Monoid and group homomorphisms: the MonoidHom and AddMonoidHom bundled types, the MonoidHom typeclass hierarchy, kernel (as a subgroup), range, composition, identity, and the hom-set structure. The primary morphism type for all algebraic structures built on Monoid.

Name: End.lean
Description: Endomorphism monoid: `MonoidHom.id` and `MonoidHom.comp` make `End M` a monoid.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard group homomorphism instances: `MonoidHom` for `ℤ`, `ℚ`, and other specific types.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `MonoidHom M N`: a function preserving `1` and `*`, with `FunLike` and `MonoidHomClass` instances.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for monoid/group homomorphisms: `MonoidHom.comp`, kernel, image, and the group structure on `Hom(G, H)`.

Name: CompTypeclasses.lean
Description: Typeclass composition for group homomorphisms: `MonoidHom.comp_typeclasses` — instances for composed homomorphisms.

Name: Irreducible
Description: Irreducible elements in a commutative monoid: elements p where p = a * b implies a or b is a unit. The monoid-level foundation for factorization theory, used by GCDMonoid and UniqueFactorizationDomain. Distinct from Group/Units which is about invertibility, not factorization.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for irreducibility: interactions with primes, UFDs, and specific ring types.

Name: Indecomposable.lean
Description: Indecomposable elements: a weaker notion than irreducible; `Indecomposable a` means `a` is not a product of two non-units.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of irreducibility: `Irreducible a` (in a monoid) — `a` is not a unit and `a = b * c` implies `b` or `c` is a unit.

Name: Nat
Description: The monoid structure on ℕ: ℕ as the free commutative monoid on one generator, the universal property of ℕ in the category of additive commutative monoids, and iterated-addition lemmas. Distinct from Data/Nat which focuses on divisibility, primality, and combinatorial properties.

Name: Range.lean
Description: Range of natural number functions: lemmas about `Finset.range n` and its interaction with group operations.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tag instances for ℕ: `Multiplicative ℕ` and `Additive ℕ` instances.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of ℕ: `ℕˣ = {1}` (only 1 is a unit in ℕ).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Group structure on ℕ: `Nat.instAddMonoidWithOne` and the basic arithmetic instances for ℕ.

Name: Even.lean
Description: Even natural numbers: `Even n ↔ 2 ∣ n` for ℕ and parity lemmas.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Monoid homomorphisms from ℕ: `Nat.castRingHom`, `Nat.cast`, and related natural number maps.

Name: Int
Description: The group structure on ℤ: ℤ as the free abelian group on one generator, the universal property of ℤ in the category of groups, and lemmas about integer powers and multiples. Distinct from Data/Int which covers divisibility, factorization, and number-theoretic properties.

Name: TypeTags.lean
Description: Type tag instances for ℤ: `Multiplicative ℤ` and `Additive ℤ` instances.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of ℤ: `ℤˣ = {1, -1}` and the group isomorphism `ℤˣ ≅ ℤ/2ℤ`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Group structure on ℤ: `Int.instAddGroupWithOne`, `Int.natCast`, and the basic arithmetic instances for ℤ.

Name: Even.lean
Description: Even and odd integers: `Even n ↔ 2 ∣ n`, `Odd n ↔ ¬ Even n`, and related parity lemmas for ℤ.

Name: Invertible
Description: The Invertible typeclass: a witness that a specific element a is invertible, producing ⅟a. Avoids hypothesis clutter in contexts where invertibility of a particular element is needed without assuming the whole group structure. Distinct from Units which packages invertibility into a new type.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Invertible a`: the typeclass carrying an explicit two-sided inverse `⊢ a * ⅟a = 1` and `⊢ ⅟a * a = 1`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Invertible elements: `Invertible a` — an element with explicit left and right inverses. API: `invOf`, interaction with `IsUnit`.

Name: NatPowAssoc.lean
Description: Power-associativity for natural number powers: the NatPowAssoc typeclass for magmas satisfying x ^ (m + n) = x ^ m * x ^ n, enabling pow for structures weaker than monoids. Distinct from Group/Basic and Group/PNatPowAssoc.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Modular equivalence in additive groups: a ≡ b [ZMOD n] (meaning n ∣ b - a in ℤ), with the ring of congruences. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Parity.

Name: PNatPowAssoc.lean
Description: Power-associativity for ℕ+ powers: the PNatPowAssoc typeclass for x ^ n with n : ℕ+. Distinct from Group/NatPowAssoc and Group/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite group: G^op with reversed multiplication and the anti-isomorphism G →ₐ G^op (g ↦ g⁻¹ or g ↦ gᵒᵖ). Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator functions in the group setting: the indicator function 1_S : α → G (equal to 1 on S and 0 off S, in a GroupWithZero or Module), with sum and product formulas. Distinct from Group/Basic and GroupWithZero/Indicator.

Name: Idempotent.lean
Description: Idempotent elements in a monoid: elements e with e * e = e, the submonoid of idempotents, and the connection to direct sum decompositions. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Idempotent.

Name: MinimalAxioms.lean
Description: Minimal axiom sets for groups and monoids: reduced axiom systems (e.g., only right inverses and right identity) from which the full axioms are derived. Distinct from Group/Defs.

Name: InjSurj.lean
Description: Injective and surjective group homomorphisms: transfer of group structure along injective/surjective maps (Function.Injective.group and Function.Surjective.group). Distinct from Group/Basic and Group/TransferInstance.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring group instances along equivalences: Function.Injective.group and Equiv.group for transporting group structure along injections or bijections. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/TransferInstance.

Name: Torsion.lean
Description: Torsion elements and torsion-free groups: an element g is torsion if g ^ n = 1 for some n > 0, with the torsion subgroup, torsion-free quotient, and the IsTorsionFree typeclass. Distinct from Group/Basic and GroupWithZero/Torsion.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift for groups: the Group structure on ULift G lifting to a higher universe, with the isomorphism ULift G ≅ G. Distinct from Group/Basic.

Name: Translate.lean
Description: Translations in a group: the left translation Lₐ : G → G (x ↦ a * x) and right translation Rₐ (x ↦ x * a) as group automorphisms, and their interaction with cosets. Distinct from Group/Basic and Group/Conj.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: Group structure on PUnit: the trivial group with a unique element, providing the terminal group. Distinct from Group/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product groups: the Group (and AddGroup) structure on G × H with componentwise operations, projection homomorphisms, and the universal property. Distinct from Group/Basic and Pi instances.

Name: Support.lean
Description: The support of a function G → M: mulSupport f = {x | f x ≠ 1} and addSupport, with operations (∩, ∪, image) and the interaction with group operations. Distinct from Group/Basic and Notation/Support.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-level shrinking for groups: the Shrink construction for group instances. Distinct from Group/Basic.

Name: ConjFinite.lean
Description: Conjugacy in finite groups: conjugacy classes, the class equation |G| = |Z(G)| + ∑ |cls(g)|, and the fixed-point theorem for p-groups. Distinct from Group/Conj (the abstract conjugation) and Group/Basic.

Name: Conj.lean
Description: Conjugation in groups: the conjugation action g • x = gxg⁻¹, conjugacy classes, centralizers C_G(x), and the inner automorphism Inn(G). Distinct from Group/Center and Group/ConjFinite.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embeddings of groups (injective monoid homomorphisms): the associated subgroup structure and the MonoidHom.injective criterion. Distinct from Group/InjSurj and Group/Prod.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for groups, monoids, and semigroups: the Semigroup, Monoid, Group, CommGroup, and AddGroup typeclasses with their axioms and operator notation. Foundation for all Group files.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic group theory lemmas: consequences of the group axioms — unique inverses, cancellation, (ab)⁻¹ = b⁻¹a⁻¹, inv_inv, and the interaction with pow. Distinct from Group/Defs (the typeclass) and Group/InjSurj.

Name: AddChar.lean
Description: Additive characters of finite abelian groups: additive group homomorphisms G →+ ℂˣ, the group structure on characters (Pontryagin dual), and the orthogonality of characters. Distinct from Group/Basic and DirectSum/AddChar.

Name: Commutator.lean
Description: Commutators in groups: the commutator [g, h] = g⁻¹h⁻¹gh, the derived (commutator) subgroup [G, G], and the abelianisation G/[G,G]. Distinct from Group/Conj and Group/Center.

Name: Center.lean
Description: The center of a group or monoid: Z(G) = {z | ∀ g, zg = gz} as a subgroup, the center of the endomorphism ring, and basic properties including Z(G) = G iff G is abelian. Distinct from Group/Conj and Ring/Center.

Name: ForwardDiff.lean
Description: The forward difference operator on groups: Δ_h f(x) = f(x + h) - f(x) and iterated differences Δ^n, used in discrete calculus and polynomial interpolation. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Identities.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finitely supported functions as groups: the AddCommGroup structure on α →₀ M and its interaction with AddMonoidHom. Distinct from Group/Basic and DirectSum/Basic.

Name: Ideal.lean
Description: Additive group structure of ideals and subrings: results treating ideals as additive subgroups and the group-theoretic properties inherited by ideal quotients. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: Graph.lean
Description: The Cayley graph of a group: the directed graph Cay(G, S) on G where g → gs for each s ∈ S, and basic properties. Distinct from Group/Basic and Group/Conj.

Name: Even.lean
Description: Even and odd elements: the IsOfFinOrder.even and Odd predicates in a group (x = a * a or not), with basic arithmetic of even/odd elements. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Parity.

Name: End.lean
Description: The endomorphism monoid of a group: End(G) with composition as multiplication and, when G is abelian, the endomorphism ring structure. Distinct from Group/Basic and Ring/Basic.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Extensionality for group and monoid homomorphisms: two homomorphisms are equal iff they agree on a generating set, with the funext lemma for AddMonoidHom and MonoidHom. Distinct from Group/Basic and Group/Defs.

Name: EvenFunction.lean
Description: Even and odd functions G → M: f is even if f(-x) = f(x), odd if f(-x) = -f(x). Contains the even/odd decomposition f = (f + f∘neg)/2 + (f - f∘neg)/2 in a module over a 2-invertible ring. Distinct from Group/Even and Group/Basic.

Name: GCDMonoid
Description: GCD monoids: monoids with a greatest common divisor operation, generalizing Z and polynomial rings.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: GCD and LCM of a multiset: Multiset.gcd and Multiset.lcm, the GCD/LCM of all elements of a multiset. Distinct from GCDMonoid/Finset and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: Nat.lean
Description: ℕ as a GCD monoid: the GCDMonoid and UniqueFactorizationMonoid instances for ℕ, Nat.Coprime, and the prime factorisation of natural numbers. Distinct from GCDMonoid/Basic and EuclideanDomain/Int.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: GCD monoid structure on PUnit: the trivial GCD monoid with gcd(*,*) = *. Distinct from GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: IntegrallyClosed.lean
Description: Integrally closed GCD domains: a GCD domain satisfies the condition that every element integral over R is already in R. Distinct from GCDMonoid/Basic and Ring/IsFormallyReal.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: GCD monoids and UFDs: the GCDMonoid typeclass with gcd and lcm satisfying divisibility axioms, the NormalizedGCDMonoid subclass, and the UniqueFactorizationMonoid (UFD) typeclass. Foundation for all GCDMonoid files.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: GCD and LCM over finite sets: Finset.gcd and Finset.lcm, with the identities gcd(a, Finset.gcd s f) = Finset.gcd s (gcd a ∘ f). Distinct from GCDMonoid/Basic and BigOperators/Fin.

Name: FinsetLemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary Finset lemmas for GCD computations: technical helper lemmas about products, GCDs, and their interaction with Finset operations. Distinct from GCDMonoid/Finset and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: Divisibility
Description: Divisibility in monoids and rings: the Dvd typeclass, divisibility lattice, and associates.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Divisibility in product monoids: componentwise divisibility (a, b) ∣ (c, d) iff a ∣ c and b ∣ d. Distinct from Divisibility/Basic and Ring/Prod.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Divisibility and units: a ∣ b and b ∣ a iff a and b are associates (a = u • b for a unit u), and units divide everything. Distinct from Divisibility/Basic and Ring/Units.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Divisibility and homomorphisms: a monoid homomorphism f preserves divisibility (a ∣ b → f a ∣ f b), with conditions for reflection. Distinct from Divisibility/Basic and Ring/InjSurj.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Divisibility in monoids: the dvd relation a ∣ b (∃ c, b = a * c), basic properties (dvd is a preorder, every element divides 0), and the interaction with multiplication and addition. Foundation for all Divisibility files.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Divisibility in finite monoids: every finite ascending chain of divisors stabilises (ACC for dvd), and divisibility in finite groups equals the unit relation. Distinct from Divisibility/Basic and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: DirectSum
Description: Direct sums of indexed families of monoids, groups, or modules: the algebraic coproduct with projections and inclusions.

Name: Internal.lean
Description: Internal direct sums of submodules: M is an internal direct sum of submodules Mᵢ if every element is uniquely expressible as a finite sum of elements from the Mᵢ. Distinct from DirectSum/Basic (external direct sum) and DirectSum/Decomposition.

Name: Idempotents.lean
Description: Idempotent decompositions via direct sums: a complete system of orthogonal idempotents {eᵢ} in a ring R gives R ≅ ⊕ᵢ Reᵢ. Distinct from DirectSum/Decomposition and Ring/Idempotent.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Linear maps between direct sums: decomposing a linear map ⊕ Mᵢ → ⊕ Nⱼ as a block matrix of component maps. Distinct from DirectSum/Basic and Module/Hom.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Ring structure on a graded direct sum: when the grading is multiplicative (AᵢAⱼ ⊆ Aᵢ₊ⱼ), the direct sum ⊕ Aᵢ is a ring via the GradedMonoid structure. Distinct from DirectSum/Algebra (R-algebra structure) and DirectSum/Module.

Name: Module.lean
Description: The R-module structure on a direct sum: ⊕ i, M i as an R-module when each M i is an R-module, with the interaction with DirectSum.of and the universal property. Distinct from DirectSum/Basic (abelian group) and DirectSum/Algebra.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebra structure on a graded direct sum: when each Aᵢ is an R-module and the graded multiplication AᵢAⱼ ⊆ Aᵢ₊ⱼ is given, the direct sum is an R-algebra. Distinct from DirectSum/Ring (ring structure) and DirectSum/Module.

Name: AddChar.lean
Description: Additive characters of direct sums: characters χ : (⊕ᵢ Gᵢ →+ ℂˣ) decompose as products of characters on each summand. Distinct from DirectSum/Basic and Group/AddChar.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The direct sum ⊕ i, M i of abelian groups: finitely supported functions i → M i with pointwise addition, the inclusion map DirectSum.of and the universal property. Foundation for all DirectSum files. Distinct from DirectSum/Module (R-module structure) and DirectSum/Ring.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: The isomorphism DirectSum.lof: (⊕ i, M) ≅ (ι →₀ M) between a direct sum with constant summands and finitely supported functions. Distinct from DirectSum/Basic and Finsupp files.

Name: Decomposition.lean
Description: Decompositions of an algebraic structure as a direct sum: the DirectSum.Decomposition typeclass asserting an isomorphism M ≅ ⊕ᵢ Mᵢ. Distinct from DirectSum/Basic and DirectSum/Internal.

Name: ContinuedFractions
Description: Continued fractions: finite and infinite, convergents, and the connection to the Euclidean algorithm and Diophantine approximation.

Name: Computation
Description: The algorithm that computes the continued fraction expansion of a real number: the generalized Euclidean algorithm producing partial quotients aₙ = ⌊xₙ⌋ and remainders xₙ₊₁ = 1/(xₙ - aₙ). Distinct from the parent folder's convergence and approximation theory — this subfolder is about constructing the expansion itself.

Name: CorrectnessTerminating.lean
Description: Correctness of the continued fraction algorithm for terminating inputs: rational numbers have finite continued fractions.

Name: TerminatesIffRat.lean
Description: The continued fraction algorithm terminates iff the input is rational.

Name: Translations.lean
Description: Translation lemmas between the continued fraction algorithm's intermediate values.

Name: ApproximationCorollaries.lean
Description: Corollaries of the continued fraction approximation theory: applications of convergent bounds to specific functions.

Name: Approximations.lean
Description: Approximation properties of continued fraction convergents: `|α - pₙ/qₙ| < 1/(qₙ qₙ₊₁)` and similar bounds.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Algorithm computing the continued fraction expansion of a real number: `GeneralizedContinuedFraction.of x` and its basic properties.

Name: Determinant.lean
Description: Determinant formula for continued fractions: the Cassini identity pₙqₙ₋₁ - pₙ₋₁qₙ = (-1)ⁿ for the convergents. Distinct from ContinuedFractions/ContinuantsRecurrence.

Name: TerminatedStable.lean
Description: Stability of terminated continued fractions: if a continued fraction terminates at step n (all subsequent partial quotients are zero), the convergents stabilise. Distinct from ContinuedFractions/Basic.

Name: Translations.lean
Description: Translation lemmas for continued fractions: shifting or reindexing partial quotients, and the effect on convergents. Distinct from ContinuedFractions/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continued fractions: the GeneralizedContinuedFraction type representing a₀ + 1/(a₁ + 1/(a₂ + …)), with partial quotients, the n-th convergent pₙ/qₙ, and the termination predicate. Foundation for all ContinuedFractions files.

Name: ContinuantsRecurrence.lean
Description: Continuant recurrence relations: the denominator continuants qₙ satisfy qₙ = aₙ • qₙ₋₁ + qₙ₋₂ and analogously for numerators. Distinct from ContinuedFractions/Basic and ContinuedFractions/Determinant.

Name: ConvergentsEquiv.lean
Description: Equivalence of convergent formulations: the two standard definitions of the n-th convergent (via the continuant recurrence and via iterated Möbius transformations) coincide. Distinct from ContinuedFractions/Basic and ContinuedFractions/Translations.

Name: FiniteSupport
Description: Algebraic structures defined using finite support: a unified framework for Finsupp and related constructions.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for finite support: the HasFiniteSupport predicate on functions f : α → M asserting that {x | f x ≠ 0} is finite, and its closure under pointwise operations. Distinct from FiniteSupport/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results for finite support: summing functions with finite support, the interaction with Finsupp, and basic operations (restriction, extension by zero) preserving finite support. Distinct from FiniteSupport/Defs.

Name: Field
Description: Fields and division rings: the Field typeclass, characteristic of a field, and the prime subfield.

Name: Subfield
Description: Subfields of a field: the Subfield typeclass, the lattice of subfields ordered by inclusion, the subfield generated by a set (the smallest subfield containing it), and prime subfields. Distinct from Subalgebra: subfields must also be closed under multiplicative inverse.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Subfield K`: a subring closed under inverses of nonzero elements. Type definition and basic instances.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for subfields: `Subfield.closure`, `Subfield.map`, the lattice of subfields, and the prime subfield.

Name: Action
Description: Actions of fields on vector spaces and algebras at the algebraic (non-analytic) level: the VectorSpace typeclass as a special case of Module over a field, and scalar action lemmas that exploit invertibility of nonzero scalars. Distinct from Module/LinearMap, which works over rings.

Name: ConjAct.lean
Description: Conjugation action on a field: the conjugation action of the multiplicative group of a field on itself.

Name: Power.lean
Description: Powers in fields: x ^ n for integer n, including negative powers via inv, with the full laws of exponents. Distinct from Field/Basic and GroupWithZero/Int.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ as a field: the canonical field structure on the rationals and the unique field embedding ℚ → k for any characteristic-0 field k. Distinct from Field/Basic and CharZero/Defs.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The opposite field: since fields are commutative, k^op ≅ k as fields. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/Opposite.

Name: Periodic.lean
Description: Periodic functions on fields: a function f : k → M is periodic with period T if f(x + T) = f(x) for all x. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/Periodic.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift for fields: the field structure on ULift k lifting to a higher universe. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/ULift.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: ZMod p as a field: ZMod p is a field when p is prime (the unique finite field of order p), with basic instances. Distinct from Field/Basic and CharP/CharAndCard.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-level shrinking for fields: the Shrink construction for field instances. Distinct from Field/Basic and Algebra/Shrink.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring field instances along ring equivalences. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/TransferInstance.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Field equivalences: the RingEquiv type restricted between fields and the associated API. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/Equiv.

Name: GeomSum.lean
Description: Geometric sum formula in fields: the identity (x^n - 1) / (x - 1) = ∑ i < n, x^i in a field where x ≠ 1. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/GeomSum.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic field theory: consequences of the Field typeclass — division, cancellation of nonzero elements, div_add_div, and the basic instances (ℚ, ℝ, ℂ, finite fields). Foundation for all Field files. Distinct from Field/Defs (the typeclass) and Field/Equiv.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for fields and division rings: the DivisionRing typeclass (a ring where every nonzero element is invertible) and Field (commutative division ring), with the inv operation and axioms. Distinct from Field/Basic (derived lemmas).

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Modular arithmetic in fields: congruence modulo a nonzero field element and the interaction with division. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/Parity.

Name: NegOnePow.lean
Description: Powers of −1 in a field: (−1)^n and its sign, used in alternating sums, determinant formulas, and the Koszul sign rule. Distinct from Field/Basic and Ring/NegOnePow.

Name: IsField.lean
Description: The IsField predicate: an alternative characterisation of fields not requiring the Field typeclass (a nontrivial commutative ring where every nonzero element is invertible), with the equivalence. Distinct from Field/Basic and Field/Defs.

Name: MinimalAxioms.lean
Description: Minimal axiom sets for fields: reduced sets of axioms from which the full Field typeclass can be derived, avoiding redundancy. Distinct from Field/Defs and Ring/MinimalAxioms.

Name: EuclideanDomain
Description: Euclidean domains: integral domains with a Euclidean function, supporting division with remainder and the Euclidean algorithm.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Fields are Euclidean domains: the trivial Euclidean structure on any field (every nonzero element has degree 0, division is exact). Distinct from EuclideanDomain/Defs and Field/Basic.

Name: Int.lean
Description: ℤ is a Euclidean domain: the Euclidean domain structure on ℤ with degree = Int.natAbs and the usual integer division algorithm. Distinct from EuclideanDomain/Defs and GCDMonoid/Nat.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The Euclidean algorithm and Bezout's identity in a Euclidean domain: for any a, b, there exist x, y with a*x + b*y = gcd(a, b), formalized as EuclideanDomain.gcd_eq_gcd_ab. The algorithm terminates because the Euclidean function strictly decreases on remainders. Bezout's identity is the key tool behind modular inverses, the RSA algorithm, and solving linear Diophantine equations. The GCD algorithm is one of the oldest non-trivial algorithms.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Euclidean domains: the EuclideanDomain typeclass with degree function and division algorithm producing quotient and remainder with remainder-degree strictly less than divisor-degree. Contains gcd_dvd_left and basic instances. Distinct from EuclideanDomain/Field and EuclideanDomain/Int.

Name: PEmptyInstances.lean
Description: Algebraic typeclass instances for PEmpty (the empty type): confirms it satisfies vacuous instances of rings, groups, and modules.

Name: QuadraticAlgebra.lean
Description: Quadratic algebras defined by a single generator x satisfying x² = bx + c, covering dual numbers, split-complex numbers, and complex numbers uniformly.

Name: QuadraticDiscriminant.lean
Description: The discriminant b² - 4ac of a quadratic polynomial and the quadratic formula: number of real roots based on the sign of the discriminant.

Name: Opposites.lean
Description: The opposite algebraic structure (MulOpposite / AddOpposite): reverses multiplication or addition order, used to relate left and right module structures.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Modular congruence a ≡ b [MOD n] for natural numbers and integers: basic properties, casting between types, and congruence arithmetic.

Name: NeZero.lean
Description: The NeZero typeclass asserting that a specific numeral or element is nonzero, used to avoid case splits in characteristic arguments.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Scoped notation for algebraic operations used throughout Mathlib, including interval notation and operator precedences.

Name: Quandle.lean
Description: Quandles and racks: self-distributive algebraic structures that axiomatize the Reidemeister moves, with applications to knot theory.

Name: RingQuot.lean
Description: Quotients of rings by a relation (not necessarily an ideal): the universal ring with that relation set to equality.

Name: Symmetrized.lean
Description: The symmetrization of a non-associative algebra: turning an associative algebra into a Jordan algebra via the symmetrized product a∘b = (ab+ba)/2.

Name: WithConv.lean
Description: The WithConv type wrapper: converts pointwise operations to convolution-style operations on finitely supported functions.

Name: README.md
Description: Overview document for the Algebra directory: describes the design principles and typeclass hierarchy for algebraic structures in Mathlib.

Name: Quaternion.lean
Description: Quaternions over a commutative ring: the four-dimensional algebra with generators i, j, k satisfying i²=j²=k²=ijk=-1.

Name: QuaternionBasis.lean
Description: Quaternion basis structures: a pair of elements in an algebra satisfying the quaternion relations, producing an embedding of the quaternions.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: General quotient constructions in algebra: the universal property of quotient types and the induced operations on equivalence classes.

Name: Exact.lean
Description: Exact sequences in algebra: the condition that the image of one map equals the kernel of the next, with short exact sequences.

Name: Expr.lean
Description: Utilities for reflecting algebraic expressions back into Lean syntax, used by tactics like ring and norm_num for proof generation.

Name: FiveLemma.lean
Description: The five lemma for commutative diagrams of modules: if four outer maps are isomorphisms, so is the middle one.

Name: DualQuaternion.lean
Description: Dual quaternions: an 8-dimensional algebra extending quaternions with a dual unit epsilon satisfying eps²=0, used in 3D rigid body mechanics.

Name: AlgebraicCard.lean
Description: Cardinality results establishing the existence of transcendental numbers: the set of algebraic numbers is countable (algebraic numbers are roots of integer polynomials, of which there are only countably many), so transcendental numbers must exist and in fact form a set of the same cardinality as ℝ. Liouville's explicit construction of a transcendental number (∑ 10^{-k!}) is the landmark example formalized in adjacent files.

Name: CubicDiscriminant.lean
Description: The discriminant of a cubic polynomial and the classification of real roots by its sign.

Name: DualNumber.lean
Description: Dual numbers R[eps]/(eps²): the ring extension used to formalize automatic differentiation and first-order Taylor approximations.

Name: Free.lean
Description: The free magma on a type: binary trees with leaves labeled by generators, the most general non-associative free structure.

Name: HierarchyDesign.lean
Description: Design notes for the algebraic typeclass hierarchy in Mathlib: documenting the rationale behind ordering and inheritance of algebraic structures.

Name: IsPrimePow.lean
Description: The IsPrimePow predicate: natural numbers of the form p^k for a prime p and k >= 1, with characterizations and basic lemmas.

Name: LinearRecurrence.lean
Description: Linear recurrences over a commutative ring: sequences satisfying a fixed linear recurrence relation, with the companion matrix and characteristic polynomial.

Name: GradedMulAction.lean
Description: Graded multiplication actions: scalar multiplication compatible with a grading, used in the theory of graded modules.

Name: FreeAlgebra.lean
Description: Lean-level lemmas for the free algebra complementing the FreeAlgebra directory, including specific universal property instances.

Name: FreeNonUnitalNonAssocAlgebra.lean
Description: The free non-unital non-associative algebra on a type: the most general free algebra without any imposed axioms.

Name: GradedMonoid.lean
Description: Graded monoids and graded semirings: monoids with a decomposition into graded pieces compatible with multiplication.

Name: Analysis
Description: Covers real and complex analysis, norms, limits, continuity, differentiation, integration, and functional analysis. Used to study convergence, estimate sizes, solve differential equations, and understand infinite-dimensional spaces. Relied on by differential equations, probability theory, mathematical physics, signal processing, and control theory.

Name: Normed
Description: Normed groups and normed additive commutative groups: the norm typeclass and its interaction with the group structure.

Name: Operator
Description: Bounded linear operators between normed spaces: the operator norm ‖T‖ = sup{‖Tx‖ | ‖x‖ ≤ 1}, the Banach space B(E, F), the open mapping theorem, and the closed graph theorem. The uniform boundedness principle (Banach-Steinhaus). Distinct from Normed/Algebra (the algebra B(E, E)) and LinearAlgebra/LinearMap (no continuity requirement).

Name: Compact
Description: Compact operators between Banach spaces: bounded linear maps T : E → F such that T maps bounded sets to precompact sets. The Arzelà-Ascoli criterion, the ideal property (compact composed with bounded is compact), and the spectral theory (nonzero spectrum is discrete with finite-dimensional eigenspaces — Riesz-Schauder theorem). Distinct from bounded operators (Normed/Operator) — compactness is a strict strengthening.

Name: FredholmAlternative.lean
Description: Fredholm alternative for compact operators.

Name: FiniteDimension.lean
Description: Compact operators in finite dimensions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Compact operators: `IsCompactOperator T` — maps bounded sets to relatively compact sets.

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extending continuous linear maps to the closure.

Name: LinearIsometry.lean
Description: Linear isometries: `LinearIsometry 𝕜 E F` — `‖f v‖ = ‖v‖`.

Name: ContinuousAlgEquiv.lean
Description: Continuous algebra equivalences.

Name: ContinuousLinearMap.lean
Description: Continuous linear map API: composition, scalar multiplication, operator norm.

Name: NormedSpace.lean
Description: Normed space of continuous linear maps.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product continuous linear maps.

Name: Mul.lean
Description: Multiplier algebra: `ContinuousLinearMap.mul`.

Name: NNNorm.lean
Description: NNNorm for continuous linear maps.

Name: Conformal.lean
Description: Conformal linear maps: `IsConformalMap f` — `f = c * isometry`.

Name: BanachSteinhaus.lean
Description: Banach–Steinhaus (uniform boundedness): a pointwise bounded family of continuous linear maps is uniformly bounded.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for continuous linear maps: `ContinuousLinearMap`, operator norm, composition.

Name: Asymptotics.lean
Description: Asymptotics for continuous linear maps.

Name: Banach.lean
Description: Open mapping theorem: a bijective continuous linear map between Banach spaces has a continuous inverse.

Name: CompleteCodomain.lean
Description: Continuous linear maps into complete spaces.

Name: Completeness.lean
Description: Completeness of spaces of continuous linear maps.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Continuous bilinear maps: `ContinuousLinearMap.comp` and `ContinuousBilinearMap`.

Name: BoundedLinearMaps.lean
Description: Bounded linear maps and their norms.

Name: Module
Description: Normed modules: modules M over a normed ring R where ‖r • m‖ ≤ ‖r‖ ‖m‖. Contains the NormedSpace typeclass (when R is a normed field) and continuity of scalar multiplication. Distinct from Normed/Group (no scalar multiplication) and NormedSpace (the field case).

Name: PiTensorProduct
Description: The projective tensor product of normed modules: the completion of the algebraic tensor product with the projective norm, in the generality of normed modules over a normed ring. Distinct from NormedSpace/PiTensorProduct (which requires a normed field scalar structure).

Name: ProjectiveSeminorm.lean
Description: Projective seminorm on pi tensor products.

Name: InjectiveSeminorm.lean
Description: Injective seminorm on pi tensor products.

Name: RCLike
Description: Normed module results specific to RCLike scalars (ℝ or ℂ): instances and lemmas that hold specifically when the scalar field is either ℝ or ℂ, leveraging the RCLike typeclass. Bridges the gap between real and complex normed spaces without duplicating proofs. Used in spectral theory and functional analysis over ℝ and ℂ.

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝ as an RCLike normed module.

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extending normed RCLike modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed modules over RCLike fields.

Name: Multilinear
Description: Continuous multilinear maps on normed modules: the Banach space of bounded k-linear maps E₁ × … × Eₖ → F, with the operator norm. The relationship to the iterated Fréchet derivative via iteratedFDeriv. Distinct from NormedSpace/Multilinear (requires a normed field; this folder works over a normed ring).

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying continuous multilinear maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous multilinear maps on normed modules.

Name: Alternating
Description: Continuous alternating multilinear maps on normed modules: the Banach space of bounded k-linear alternating maps E^k → F, with the operator norm. The connection to the exterior algebra and to differential k-forms. The inclusion into Multilinear. Distinct from NormedSpace/Alternating (same concept but with a full normed field scalar structure).

Name: Uncurry
Description: Uncurrying continuous alternating multilinear maps on normed modules: converting iterated continuous linear maps into a single continuous alternating multilinear map. Used to connect the iterated Fréchet derivative (a sequence of linear maps) with the single multilinear object needed for Taylor's theorem.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Given a continuous function linear in its first argument and alternating in the other `n` arguments, produces a continuous alternating form in `n + 1` arguments. Also: given a continuous alternating map of `n + 1` arguments, the uncurried form is alternating.

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying continuous alternating maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous alternating maps on normed modules.

Name: Ball
Description: The unit ball of a normed module: the set B(0, 1) = {x | ‖x‖ ≤ 1} (closed) or {x | ‖x‖ < 1} (open), its convexity, and its interaction with the norm topology. Bounding elements by showing they lie in a scaled ball. Used in functional analysis proofs (e.g., showing an operator is bounded by a ball condition).

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on the ball.

Name: RadialEquiv.lean
Description: Radial homeomorphism of the ball.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Group action on the unit ball.

Name: Homeomorph.lean
Description: Homeomorphism between ball and space.

Name: MultipliableUniformlyOn.lean
Description: Uniformly multipliable functions in normed modules.

Name: Normalize.lean
Description: Normalising vectors: `‖v‖⁻¹ • v`.

Name: MStructure.lean
Description: M-structure and L-projections in Banach spaces.

Name: FiniteDimension.lean
Description: Finite-dimensional normed modules: unique topology, local compactness.

Name: HahnBanach.lean
Description: Hahn–Banach theorem in normed modules.

Name: Ray.lean
Description: Rays in normed modules: `SameRay 𝕜 x y`.

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring normed module instances.

Name: WeakDual.lean
Description: Weak dual of a normed module.

Name: Span.lean
Description: Span in normed modules.

Name: RieszLemma.lean
Description: Riesz's lemma: the unit ball is not compact in infinite dimensions.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe-shrinking for normed modules.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion of normed modules.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connected components of normed modules.

Name: Complemented.lean
Description: Complemented subspaces of Banach spaces.

Name: Bases.lean
Description: Bases for normed modules.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed modules: `NormedSpace 𝕜 E`.

Name: ContinuousInverse.lean
Description: Continuous inverse of a continuous linear map.

Name: ENormedSpace.lean
Description: E-normed spaces: `ENormedSpace` with `ℝ≥0∞`-valued norm.

Name: Extr.lean
Description: Extremal points in normed modules.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual normed space `E* = E →L[𝕜] 𝕜`.

Name: Convex.lean
Description: Convex analysis in normed modules.

Name: DoubleDual.lean
Description: Double dual embedding `E → E**`.

Name: Order
Description: Normed lattices and Banach lattices: normed spaces with a lattice order satisfying |x| ≤ |y| ⟹ ‖x‖ ≤ ‖y‖. Contains the positive cone, the AL and AM properties, and Kakutani's representation theorem. Used in Lp theory. Distinct from Normed/Group (no order) and Algebra/Order/Module (no completeness or norm).

Name: Hom
Description: Order-preserving bounded linear operators between normed lattices: positive operators T : E → F satisfying T(x) ≥ 0 whenever x ≥ 0, with the operator norm. The Perron-Frobenius theorem (a positive operator on a Banach lattice with a spectral gap has a positive eigenvector). Used in Lp theory and ergodic theory.

Name: Ultra.lean
Description: Ultrametric order homs.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Order-preserving continuous maps.

Name: UpperLower.lean
Description: Upper and lower sets in normed ordered groups.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on normed ordered groups.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed lattices: `NormedLatticeAddCommGroup` — norm and lattice compatible.

Name: Unbundled
Description: Unbundled norm instances: the HasNorm, HasDist, and HasEdist typeclasses without requiring the full NormedAddCommGroup structure. Used for types where the norm exists but the full typeclass hierarchy is not yet established. Distinct from the bundled Normed/Group (complete typeclass) — this provides the raw norm without derived structure.

Name: SeminormFromConst.lean
Description: Seminorm from a constant bound.

Name: SeminormFromBounded.lean
Description: Constructing a seminorm from a bounded function.

Name: SpectralNorm.lean
Description: Spectral norm: `‖a‖_sp = lim ‖aⁿ‖^{1/n}`.

Name: SmoothingSeminorm.lean
Description: Smoothing/regularisation of a seminorm.

Name: RingSeminorm.lean
Description: Ring seminorms: unbundled submultiplicative seminorms.

Name: FiniteExtension.lean
Description: Finite extensions with norms.

Name: AlgebraNorm.lean
Description: Algebra norms: unbundled multiplicative norms on algebras.

Name: IsPowMulFaithful.lean
Description: Power-multiplicative faithful norms.

Name: InvariantExtension.lean
Description: Invariant norm extensions.

Name: Ring
Description: Normed rings: rings with a submultiplicative norm ‖ab‖ ≤ ‖a‖ ‖b‖, the algebraic layer below normed algebras. Contains SeminormedRing and NormedRing typeclasses and their metric space instances. Distinct from Normed/Algebra (which requires a scalar field) and Normed/Field (invertibility of nonzero elements).

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring normed ring instances.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary normed ring lemmas.

Name: Ultra.lean
Description: Non-archimedean normed rings.

Name: WithAbs.lean
Description: `WithAbs` type alias carrying an absolute value as a normed ring.

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units in normed rings: openness and continuity of inversion.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite normed rings.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed rings: `NormedRing R` — `‖a * b‖ ≤ ‖a‖ * ‖b‖`.

Name: InfiniteProd.lean
Description: Infinite products in normed rings.

Name: Int.lean
Description: ℤ as a normed ring.

Name: InfiniteSum.lean
Description: Infinite sums in normed rings.

Name: Algebra
Description: Normed algebras: associative algebras with a submultiplicative norm ‖ab‖ ≤ ‖a‖‖b‖. Banach algebras (normed algebras complete as metric spaces), the spectrum of an element, and the Banach algebra inverse function theorem (Neumann series). Distinct from CStarAlgebra (adds the C*-identity ‖a*a‖ = ‖a‖²) and Normed/Ring (no algebra structure over a scalar field).

Name: TrivSqZeroExt.lean
Description: Trivial square-zero extension as a normed algebra.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectrum in normed algebras: `spectrum 𝕜 a` and spectral radius.

Name: QuaternionExponential.lean
Description: Quaternion exponential: `exp q` for `q : ℍ`.

Name: UnitizationL1.lean
Description: L¹ unitization of a normed algebra.

Name: Unitization.lean
Description: Unitization of a normed algebra.

Name: Ultra.lean
Description: Ultrametric normed algebras.

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: Matrix exponential in a normed algebra: `NormedAlgebra.exp`.

Name: DualNumber.lean
Description: Dual number algebra `𝕜[ε]/(ε²)` with norm.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed algebras: `NormedAlgebra 𝕜 A` — `‖a * b‖ ≤ ‖a‖ * ‖b‖`.

Name: MatrixExponential.lean
Description: Matrix exponential `exp A` in `Mₙ(ℝ)` or `Mₙ(ℂ)`.

Name: GelfandMazur.lean
Description: Gelfand–Mazur theorem: a Banach division algebra over ℂ is isomorphic to ℂ.

Name: GelfandFormula.lean
Description: Gelfand's formula: `spectralRadius a = lim ‖aⁿ‖^{1/n}`.

Name: Affine
Description: Normed affine spaces: affine spaces modeled on a normed vector space, with the distance function d(x, y) = ‖y - x‖. Contains the metric structure on affine spaces and the interaction with convexity. Distinct from Analysis/Calculus/AddTorsor (differentiability on affine spaces) and LinearAlgebra/AffineSpace (no norm).

Name: Isometry.lean
Description: Isometries of normed affine spaces.

Name: Convex.lean
Description: Convexity in normed affine spaces.

Name: Simplex.lean
Description: Simplices in normed affine spaces.

Name: MazurUlam.lean
Description: Mazur–Ulam theorem: surjective isometries of normed spaces are affine.

Name: ContinuousAffineMap.lean
Description: Continuous affine maps between normed affine spaces.

Name: AddTorsorBases.lean
Description: Bases for normed affine spaces.

Name: AddTorsor.lean
Description: Normed affine spaces: `NormedAddTorsor V P`.

Name: Ceva.lean
Description: Ceva's theorem in normed affine spaces.

Name: AsymptoticCone.lean
Description: Asymptotic cone of a normed affine space.

Name: Field
Description: Normed fields: fields with a multiplicative norm |ab| = |a||b|. The primary examples are ℝ, ℂ, and p-adic fields. Contains the NormedField typeclass, the relationship to absolute values on fields, and instances for ℝ and ℂ. Distinct from Normed/Algebra (not necessarily a field) and Analysis/Complex (specific to ℂ).

Name: TransferInstance.lean
Description: Transferring normed field instances.

Name: ProperSpace.lean
Description: Proper normed fields: locally compact normed fields.

Name: Ultra.lean
Description: Non-archimedean (ultrametric) normed fields.

Name: WithAbs.lean
Description: `WithAbs` — a type carrying an absolute value as a normed field.

Name: UnitBall.lean
Description: Unit ball of a normed field.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for normed fields.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed fields: `NormedField 𝕜` — `‖a * b‖ = ‖a‖ * ‖b‖`.

Name: Approximation.lean
Description: Approximation in normed fields.

Name: Dense.lean
Description: Dense subfields of normed fields.

Name: Krasner.lean
Description: Krasner's lemma: in a complete non-archimedean field, roots of nearby polynomials are close.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard normed field instances: ℝ, ℂ, ℚ_p.

Name: Lp
Description: Lp spaces and their norms: the space ℓᵖ of sequences (xₙ) with ∑|xₙ|ᵖ < ∞, the space L^p(μ) of p-integrable functions, and the Hölder and Minkowski inequalities. The p = ∞ case (essential supremum). Distinct from MeasureTheory/Function/Lp (the measure-theoretic construction) — this folder focuses on the normed space structure.

Name: ProdLp.lean
Description: ProdLp: `E × F` with the Lp norm.

Name: PiLp.lean
Description: PiLp: `Π i, E i` with the Lp norm.

Name: WithLp.lean
Description: WithLp type synonym for imposing an Lp norm.

Name: SmoothApprox.lean
Description: Smooth approximation in Lp spaces.

Name: lpSpace.lean
Description: The `lp` space: sequences with finite Lp norm.

Name: LpEquiv.lean
Description: Equivalences between Lp space constructions.

Name: MeasurableSpace.lean
Description: Measurability in Lp spaces.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Lp norm on matrices.

Name: Group
Description: Normed groups and seminormed groups: the SeminormedAddCommGroup and NormedAddCommGroup typeclasses, the metric d(x, y) = ‖x - y‖, and the foundational lemmas. The base layer of the normed analysis hierarchy — all normed spaces, normed rings, and Banach spaces extend from here. Distinct from Normed/Algebra (adds multiplication) and Lp (specific spaces).

Name: SemiNormedGrp
Description: The category SemiNormedGrp of seminormed abelian groups: objects carry a seminorm (not necessarily separating), morphisms are bounded group homomorphisms. The quotient by the null-seminorm subgroup gives NormedGrp. Used in functional analysis to handle Lp-seminorms before quotienting by the a.e.-zero relation. Distinct from NormedGrp (the separated case).

Name: Kernels.lean
Description: Kernels in the category of seminormed groups.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion of seminormed groups.

Name: Rat.lean
Description: ℚ as a normed group.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient normed groups: `E / F` with quotient norm.

Name: Seminorm.lean
Description: Seminorms on groups.

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝ as a normed group.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary normed group lemmas.

Name: Int.lean
Description: ℤ as a normed group.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on sets in normed groups.

Name: NullSubmodule.lean
Description: Null submodule (elements with zero norm).

Name: Ultra.lean
Description: Ultrametric normed groups.

Name: Tannery.lean
Description: Tannery's theorem: sum of dominated convergent series.

Name: ZeroAtInfty.lean
Description: Functions vanishing at infinity in normed groups.

Name: Uniform.lean
Description: Uniform structure on normed groups.

Name: SeparationQuotient.lean
Description: Separation quotient of a seminormed group.

Name: SemiNormedGrp.lean
Description: Category of seminormed groups.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Submodules as normed groups.

Name: Subgroup.lean
Description: Subgroups of normed groups.

Name: InfiniteSum.lean
Description: Infinite sums in normed groups.

Name: CocompactMap.lean
Description: Cocompact maps on normed groups.

Name: Bounded.lean
Description: Bounded subsets of normed groups.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Completion of a normed group.

Name: Completeness.lean
Description: Completeness of normed groups.

Name: AddTorsor.lean
Description: Normed add torsors: normed affine spaces as torsors.

Name: AddCircle.lean
Description: Additive circle as a normed group.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normed (additive) commutative groups: `NormedAddCommGroup E` and basic API.

Name: BallSphere.lean
Description: Open ball and sphere in a normed group.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Normed group homomorphisms: continuous group maps with operator norm.

Name: FunctionSeries.lean
Description: Uniformly convergent function series in normed groups.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: Indicator functions in normed groups.

Name: HomCompletion.lean
Description: Completion of normed group homomorphisms.

Name: Continuity.lean
Description: Continuity in normed groups.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of normed groups: products, quotients, subgroups.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions of normed group typeclasses.

Name: ControlledClosure.lean
Description: Controlled closure lemma for normed groups.

Name: MulAction.lean
Description: Normed multiplicative actions: `NormedSpace.BoundedSMul 𝕜 E` requiring `‖c • x‖ ≤ ‖c‖ * ‖x‖`. Provides instances for ring actions and the compatibility between the norm and the multiplicative action structure. Distinct from the `NormedSpace` algebra files and NormedSpace/MStructure.

Name: NormedSpace
Description: Normed vector spaces over R or C: the norm typeclass, bounded linear operators, and the operator norm.

Name: Multilinear
Description: Continuous multilinear maps between normed spaces: the Banach space ContinuousMultilinearMap with the operator norm, and its role in the Taylor expansion (iteratedFDeriv). Contains the relationship between multilinear maps and tensors. Distinct from NormedSpace/Alternating (adds the alternating condition) and LinearAlgebra/Multilinear (no continuity or norm).

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying of multilinear maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous multilinear maps.

Name: PiTensorProduct
Description: The projective tensor product of normed spaces: the completion of the algebraic tensor product E ⊗ F with the projective norm ‖u‖ = inf{∑‖xᵢ‖‖yᵢ‖ | u = ∑xᵢ⊗yᵢ}. The projective tensor product represents bilinear maps. Distinct from the injective tensor product and from LinearAlgebra/PiTensorProduct (algebraic, no norm or completion).

Name: ProjectiveSeminorm.lean
Description: Projective norm on pi tensor products.

Name: InjectiveSeminorm.lean
Description: Injective norm on pi tensor products.

Name: Alternating
Description: Continuous alternating multilinear maps between normed spaces: the Banach space of continuous k-linear alternating maps E^k → F with the operator norm. Used in the definition of differential k-forms and exterior derivatives. Distinct from NormedSpace/Multilinear (no alternating condition) and LinearAlgebra/AlternatingMap (no continuity or norm).

Name: Uncurry
Description: Uncurrying continuous alternating multilinear maps in normed spaces: converting a curried alternating map E →L[𝕜] E →L[𝕜] … →L[𝕜] F (iterated continuous linear maps) into a single continuous alternating multilinear map. Used to relate the iterated Fréchet derivative to a single multilinear object.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Uncurrying alternating maps indexed by Fin.

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying alternating maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous alternating multilinear maps.

Name: HahnBanach
Description: The Hahn-Banach theorem and consequences: extending bounded linear functionals from a subspace while preserving the norm bound. Consequences include the bidual embedding, the separation of convex sets by hyperplanes, and the existence of supporting functionals. Distinct from Normed/Operator (about bounded operators, not functionals) — Hahn-Banach is specifically about the dual space.

Name: Separation.lean
Description: Hahn–Banach separation theorem: separating a point from a closed convex set.

Name: SeparatingDual.lean
Description: Separating dual: the dual separates points of `E`.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Hahn–Banach extension theorem: extending a bounded linear functional.

Name: MultipliableUniformlyOn.lean
Description: Infinite products `∏ f n` converging uniformly on compact sets of a normed space: `MultipliableUniformlyOn`, the Weierstrass M-test for products, and the uniform product being continuous/analytic. Distinct from NormedSpace/Normalize (single vectors) and Analytic/Constructions.

Name: Normalize.lean
Description: Normalising a nonzero vector: `‖x‖⁻¹ • x` has norm 1, and the `normalize` function. Proves `norm_normalize`, `normalize_smul`, and the retraction to the unit sphere. Distinct from NormedSpace/RieszLemma (nearly orthogonal vectors) and InnerProductSpace/Orthonormal.

Name: RieszLemma.lean
Description: Riesz lemma: for any proper closed subspace `F ⊊ E` of a normed space and `ε > 0`, there exists a unit vector `x` with `inf_{y ∈ F} ‖x - y‖ > 1 - ε`.
