Consequence: the closed unit ball of an infinite-dimensional normed space is not compact. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Orthogonal (exact orthogonality in Hilbert spaces).

Name: MStructure.lean
Description: M-structure on Banach spaces: `MIdeal` (a closed subspace `J ⊆ E` that is the range of an `L`-projection — an idempotent whose complementary projection also has norm ≤ 1), `MSummand`, and the M-ideal characterisation. Distinct from NormedSpace/ENormedSpace and NormedSpace/RieszLemma.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Path-connectedness and connectedness of normed spaces: any normed space over ℝ or ℂ is path-connected, and the complement of a finite set in a normed space of dimension ≥ 2 is path-connected. Distinct from Convex/PathConnected (convex sets) and Topology/Connected files.

Name: ENormedSpace.lean
Description: Extended normed spaces: `ENNNorm` — norms taking values in `ℝ≥0∞`, `ENormedAddGroupHom` (morphisms bounded in the extended norm sense). Used for Lᵖ spaces with `p = ∞` and integration theory. Distinct from NormedSpace/MStructure and the standard `NormedAddGroup` hierarchy.

Name: Extr.lean
Description: Extreme points of the unit ball in specific normed spaces: characterisation of extreme points of `{x | ‖x‖ ≤ 1}` in `C(K)`, `L¹`, and other concrete spaces. Distinct from Convex/Extreme (abstract theory) and CStarAlgebra/Extreme (C*-algebra ball).

Name: ODE
Description: Ordinary differential equations: the Picard-Lindelof existence and uniqueness theorem for ODEs in Banach spaces.

Name: PicardLindelof.lean
Description: Picard–Lindelöf theorem (Cauchy–Lipschitz): the IVP `x' = f(t, x)`, `x(t₀) = x₀` has a unique local solution whenever `f` is Lipschitz in `x`. Proved by showing the Picard iteration is a contraction on a Banach space of curves. Key: `IVP.exists_solution`. Distinct from ODE/Gronwall (uniqueness tool) and ODE/Basic.

Name: Transform.lean
Description: Transformations of ODEs: change of variables, variation of constants formula for linear inhomogeneous ODEs `x' = A(t)x + b(t)`, and reduction to autonomous form. Proves `variationOfConstants_solution`. Distinct from ODE/PicardLindelof (existence/uniqueness) and Calculus/VectorField.

Name: Gronwall.lean
Description: Gronwall's inequality: if `u(t) ≤ α(t) + ∫_a^t β(s) u(s) ds` then `u(t) ≤ α(t) + ∫_a^t α(s) β(s) exp(∫_s^t β(r) dr) ds`. Used to prove uniqueness and continuous dependence for ODEs. Key lemma: `gronwall_bound`. Distinct from ODE/DiscreteGronwall and ODE/PicardLindelof.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic ODE theory in Banach spaces: the initial value problem `x' = f(t, x)`, `x(t₀) = x₀`, existence and uniqueness under Lipschitz conditions. Provides the `IVP` framework and the local solution as a continuous function. Distinct from ODE/PicardLindelof (the main existence theorem) and ODE/Gronwall.

Name: DiscreteGronwall.lean
Description: Discrete Gronwall inequality: if `a_{n+1} ≤ b_n a_n + c_n` then `a_n ≤ (a_0 + ∑ cᵢ) * ∏ bᵢ`. Used in numerical analysis and discrete stability theory. Distinct from ODE/Gronwall (continuous version) and SpecificLimits/Normed.

Name: LocallyConvex
Description: Locally convex topological vector spaces: seminorm families, weak topologies, and the Hahn-Banach theorem.

Name: Separation.lean
Description: Geometric Hahn–Banach separation theorem in locally convex spaces: a closed convex set and a compact convex set with empty intersection can be separated by a continuous affine functional. Proves `geometric_hahn_banach_closed_compact`. Distinct from LocallyConvex/SeparatingDual (algebraic separation) and Convex/Side.

Name: StrongTopology.lean
Description: Strong topology on the continuous dual `E*`: convergence on bounded subsets of `E`, making `E*` a locally convex space. Defines `ContinuousLinearMap.strongTopology` and proves the strong dual of a Fréchet space is complete. Distinct from LocallyConvex/WeakDual (weak-* topology) and LocallyConvex/Bounded.

Name: Polar.lean
Description: Polar sets: `polar 𝕜 s = {f | ∀ x ∈ s, ‖f x‖ ≤ 1}` and the bipolar theorem (the bipolar of a set equals its closed absolutely convex hull). Provides the Mackey–Arens topology framework. Distinct from LocallyConvex/SeparatingDual and LocallyConvex/WeakDual.

Name: SeparatingDual.lean
Description: Locally convex spaces with separating dual: a locally convex space `E` where the continuous dual `E*` separates points (i.e., `⟪x, f⟫ = 0` for all `f ∈ E*` implies `x = 0`). Proves Hahn–Banach implies the dual is separating. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Separation (geometric separation) and LocallyConvex/WeakDual.

Name: WeakSpace.lean
Description: `WeakSpace 𝕜 E`: the space `E` equipped with the weak topology (topology generated by the dual `E*`). Proves the weak topology is locally convex and that weakly convergent sequences are bounded. Distinct from LocallyConvex/WeakDual (topology on the dual) and LocallyConvex/PointwiseConvergence.

Name: WithSeminorms.lean
Description: Locally convex topology generated by a family of seminorms `p : ι → Seminorm 𝕜 E`: `WithSeminorms p` and the neighbourhood filter at 0. Proves `WithSeminorms p ↔ LocallyConvexSpace` and that the seminorm topology is the initial topology. The main API for defining locally convex spaces via seminorms.

Name: WeakDual.lean
Description: Weak-* topology on the continuous dual: `WeakDual 𝕜 E` (the dual equipped with the topology of pointwise convergence), Banach–Alaoglu theorem (closed unit ball in `E*` is weak-* compact). Distinct from LocallyConvex/StrongTopology (strong dual) and LocallyConvex/SeparatingDual.

Name: WeakOperatorTopology.lean
Description: Weak operator topology in the locally convex setting: the topology on `E →L[𝕜] F` generated by the seminorms `T ↦ |⟪T x, f⟫|` for `x ∈ E`, `f ∈ F*`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/WeakOperatorTopology (Hilbert space specialisation) and LocallyConvex/WeakSpace.

Name: PointwiseConvergence.lean
Description: Topology of pointwise convergence on function spaces `Π i, E i` (product topology): its locally convex structure, and that convergence is characterised by `f_α i → f i` for each `i`. Connects to weak topologies. Distinct from LocallyConvex/WeakSpace (dual paring topology) and LocallyConvex/StrongTopology.

Name: BalancedCoreHull.lean
Description: Balanced core `balancedCore 𝕜 s` (largest balanced subset) and balanced hull `balancedHull 𝕜 s` (smallest balanced superset). Proves `balancedCore_eq_iInter` and the absorption property. Distinct from LocallyConvex/AbsConvex (balanced + convex) and Convex/Hull.

Name: Barrelled.lean
Description: Barrelled locally convex spaces: a TVS where every closed absorbing absolutely convex set (barrel) is a neighbourhood of 0. Proves the uniform boundedness principle holds in barrelled spaces. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Bounded (bounded sets) and LocallyConvex/Montel.

Name: AbsConvex.lean
Description: Absolutely convex sets (sets that are both convex and balanced): `AbsConvex`, closure under convex combinations with `|λ| + |μ| ≤ 1`. Proves the absolutely convex hull and its relationship to balanced hull and convex hull. Distinct from LocallyConvex/BalancedCoreHull (balanced hull only) and LocallyConvex/Basic.

Name: AbsConvexOpen.lean
Description: Open absolutely convex sets: every open neighbourhood of 0 in a locally convex space contains an open absolutely convex neighbourhood. Used as the basis for the topology. Distinct from LocallyConvex/AbsConvex (without openness) and LocallyConvex/Basic.

Name: ContinuousOfBounded.lean
Description: A linear map between locally convex spaces is continuous iff it sends bounded sets to bounded sets (Mackey criterion). Proves `LinearMap.continuous_of_continuousAt_zero` and the bounded ↔ continuous equivalence in Fréchet spaces. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Bounded (bounded sets) and LocallyConvex/Barrelled.

Name: Montel.lean
Description: Montel spaces: locally convex spaces in which every closed bounded set is compact (the Heine–Borel property). Proves that spaces of holomorphic functions and Schwartz spaces are Montel. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Barrelled (bounded absorbing sets) and LocallyConvex/Bounded.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `LocallyConvexSpace 𝕜 E`: a topological vector space whose topology has a neighbourhood basis at 0 consisting of convex sets. Proves the topology can equivalently be generated by a family of seminorms. The foundational file for the LocallyConvex folder.

Name: Bounded.lean
Description: Bounded sets in locally convex spaces: `Bornology.IsVonNBounded 𝕜 s` (absorbed by every neighbourhood of 0). Proves continuous linear maps send bounded sets to bounded sets, and characterises bounded sets via seminorms. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Barrelled and LocallyConvex/Basic.

Name: Matrix
Description: Analysis on matrices: operator norms, matrix exponentials, and spectral theory for finite-dimensional operators.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Löwner order on Hermitian matrices: `A ≤ B` iff `B - A` is positive semidefinite. Proves the order is compatible with matrix addition and scalar multiplication, and provides `PosSemidef.eigenvalues_nonneg`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/PosDef (characterisation) and Analysis/Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: PosDef.lean
Description: Positive definite matrices `Matrix.PosDef A` (Hermitian with all positive eigenvalues) and positive semidefinite matrices `Matrix.PosSemidef A`: characterisations via `⟪Ax, x⟫ > 0`, Cholesky-type existence, and square root `A^(1/2)`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/LDL (decomposition) and Analysis/Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectrum of a matrix as a linear operator on `𝕜^n`: `Matrix.spectrum A = {λ | ∃ v ≠ 0, A v = λ v}`, connection to characteristic polynomial roots, and the Cayley–Hamilton theorem consequence that eigenvalues are roots of `charpoly`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/Hermitian (real spectrum) and CStarAlgebra/Spectrum.

Name: Normed.lean
Description: Norms on matrices as operators: `Matrix.opNorm` (operator norm as a linear map `𝕜^n → 𝕜^m`), the Frobenius norm `‖A‖_F = √(∑ |Aᵢⱼ|²)`, and the submultiplicativity `‖A * B‖ ≤ ‖A‖ * ‖B‖`. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/CStarMatrix (C*-algebra structure) and Analysis/Matrix/PosDef.

Name: Hermitian.lean
Description: Hermitian matrices `Matrix.IsHermitian A` (satisfying `Aᴴ = A`): eigenvalue reality, the spectral theorem for Hermitian matrices (diagonalisation by a unitary), and `Matrix.IsHermitian.eigenvalues`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Spectrum (Hilbert space version) and Analysis/Matrix/PosDef.

Name: HermitianFunctionalCalculus.lean
Description: Functional calculus for Hermitian matrices: `Matrix.IsHermitian.funcCalc f A = U * diag (f ∘ eigenvalues A) * U*` for a Hermitian matrix `A` with unitary diagonalisation `U`. Proves the spectral mapping theorem `eigenvalues (f(A)) = f ∘ eigenvalues A`. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Spectrum (abstract functional calculus) and Analysis/Matrix/Hermitian.

Name: LDL.lean
Description: LDL* decomposition (LDLT factorisation) of a positive definite Hermitian matrix: `A = L * D * Lᴴ` with `L` lower triangular (unit diagonal) and `D` diagonal with positive entries. Provides `Matrix.LDL.lower` and `Matrix.LDL.diag`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/PosDef (positive definiteness) and LinearAlgebra/Matrix/Cholesky.

Name: Meromorphic
Description: Meromorphic functions on complex domains: poles, Laurent series expansions, and residues.

Name: NormalForm.lean
Description: Normal form of a meromorphic function near an isolated singularity: `f(z) = (z - z₀)^n * g(z)` with `g` analytic and nonvanishing at `z₀`, where `n = ord_{z₀}(f)`. Used to classify zeros (n > 0) and poles (n < 0). Distinct from Meromorphic/Order (the order number itself) and Meromorphic/TrailingCoefficient.

Name: IsolatedZeros.lean
Description: Isolated zeros and poles of meromorphic functions: a non-identically-zero meromorphic function has isolated zeros and poles. Proves `MeromorphicAt.isolated_zero_or_pole` and order classification. Distinct from Analytic/IsolatedZeros (analytic setting) and Meromorphic/Order.

Name: TrailingCoefficient.lean
Description: The leading (trailing) Laurent coefficient of a meromorphic function at an isolated singularity: `MeromorphicAt.leadingCoeff f z₀` — the coefficient of `(z-z₀)^{ord(f)}` in the Laurent expansion. Nonzero by definition. Distinct from Meromorphic/NormalForm (the full factorisation) and Meromorphic/Order.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order of a meromorphic function at a point: `MeromorphicAt.order f z₀ : ℤ` — positive for zeros, negative for poles, zero if nonvanishing. Proves `order (f * g) = order f + order g` and `order (1/f) = - order f`. Distinct from Meromorphic/NormalForm (the factored representation) and Analytic/Order.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Meromorphic functions on complex domains: instances proving that rational functions are meromorphic, and the field structure of `MeromorphicOn U`. Connects to the analytic/complex analysis infrastructure. Distinct from Meromorphic/Basic (abstract definition) and Complex/RemovableSingularity.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `MeromorphicAt f z₀`: a function is meromorphic at `z₀` if it is analytic on a punctured neighbourhood and the singularity is at worst a pole. Proves that meromorphic functions form a field (under pointwise operations), and `MeromorphicOn`. Distinct from Analytic/Basic (analytic, no poles allowed) and Meromorphic/Order.

Name: FactorizedRational.lean
Description: Meromorphic functions that are rational: every meromorphic function on the Riemann sphere is a rational function. Provides the factored form `c * ∏ (z - aᵢ) / ∏ (z - bⱼ)` and the connection to `Polynomial.roots`. Distinct from Meromorphic/Divisor (divisors) and Analysis/Polynomial.

Name: Divisor.lean
Description: The divisor `div f` of a meromorphic function: the formal sum `∑ ordₐ(f) * [a]` over all zeros and poles with their orders. Proves `div(f * g) = div(f) + div(g)` and the degree formula for rational functions. Distinct from Meromorphic/Order (order at a single point) and Analytic/Order.

Name: SpecialFunctions
Description: Special functions: exponential, logarithm, trigonometric and hyperbolic functions, the Gamma function, and the Riemann zeta function.

Name: Integrals
Description: Antiderivatives and definite integrals of special functions: ∫sin = -cos, ∫log = x log x - x, ∫1/(1+x²) = arctan, and other standard closed-form integrals. Used in analysis proofs and in number theory (integral representations). Distinct from Integrability (existence) — this folder gives explicit antiderivatives.

Name: PosLogEqCircleAverage.lean
Description: Positive log equals circle average formula.

Name: LogTrigonometric.lean
Description: Integrals involving log and trig functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Integrals of standard functions: `∫ x^n`, `∫ e^x`, `∫ sin`, `∫ cos`.

Name: Integrability
Description: Integrability of special functions: conditions under which exp, log, trigonometric functions, and their combinations are Lebesgue integrable. Contains improper integral comparisons and the Riemann-Lebesgue lemma (the Fourier transform of an L¹ function tends to 0). Distinct from Integrals (antiderivatives) — this folder is about existence, not computation.

Name: LogMeromorphic.lean
Description: Integrability of log of meromorphic functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Integrability of standard special functions.

Name: Log
Description: The real logarithm log : ℝ → ℝ: defined as the inverse of exp, with log(xy) = log x + log y for x, y > 0, differentiability (log'(x) = 1/x), convexity/concavity, and the asymptotic log x / x → 0. Used throughout number theory (prime counting) and analysis. Distinct from Complex/log (multi-valued complex logarithm) and Pow (general powers x^y = exp(y log x)).

Name: NegMulLog.lean
Description: The function `x ↦ -x log x` and its properties.

Name: Monotone.lean
Description: Monotonicity of the logarithm.

Name: InvLog.lean
Description: Inverse logarithm: `Real.exp ∘ Real.log = id`.

Name: Summable.lean
Description: Summability of log series.

Name: RpowTendsto.lean
Description: Tendsto results involving log and rpow.

Name: PosLog.lean
Description: The positive logarithm `max(0, log x)`.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivative of the real logarithm: `HasDerivAt Real.log (x⁻¹) x` for `x ≠ 0`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Real logarithm: `Real.log`, its derivative, and monotonicity.

Name: Base.lean
Description: Logarithm base b: `Real.logb b x = log x / log b`.

Name: ERealExp.lean
Description: Exponential on `EReal`.

Name: ENNRealLogExp.lean
Description: Log-exp adjunction for `ℝ≥0∞`.

Name: ENNRealLog.lean
Description: Logarithm on `ℝ≥0∞`.

Name: Trigonometric
Description: Trigonometric and inverse trigonometric functions: sin, cos, tan (as quotient), arcsin, arccos, arctan with their power series, Pythagorean identity, addition formulas, and derivative formulas. The period 2π of sin and cos. Distinct from Analysis/Real/Pi (the constant π) and Complex/SpecialFunctions (complex-valued trig). The analytic foundation for all angle-based results in Mathlib.

Name: Chebyshev
Description: Chebyshev polynomials Tₙ and Uₙ: defined by Tₙ(cos θ) = cos(nθ) and Uₙ(cos θ) = sin((n+1)θ)/sin(θ). The recurrence Tₙ₊₁ = 2x·Tₙ - Tₙ₋₁, orthogonality on [-1,1] with respect to the weight (1-x²)^{-1/2}, and extremal properties (Tₙ has the smallest sup-norm among monic polynomials of degree n). Used in approximation theory and numerical analysis.

Name: Orthogonality.lean
Description: Orthogonality of Chebyshev polynomials.

Name: RootsExtrema.lean
Description: Roots and extrema of Chebyshev polynomials.

Name: Extremal.lean
Description: Extremal properties of Chebyshev polynomials.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Chebyshev polynomials Tₙ(x) via cos.

Name: ChebyshevGauss.lean
Description: Gauss quadrature at Chebyshev nodes.

Name: EulerSineProd.lean
Description: Euler's product formula: `sin(πz) = πz ∏ (1 - z²/n²)`.

Name: Inverse.lean
Description: Inverse trigonometric functions: `Real.arcsin`, `Real.arccos`, `Real.arctan`.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of trig functions: `HasDerivAt Real.sin (Real.cos x) x`.

Name: DerivHyp.lean
Description: Derivatives of hyperbolic functions.

Name: Series.lean
Description: Series expansions of trig functions.

Name: Sinc.lean
Description: Sinc function `sinc x = sin x / x`.

Name: InverseDeriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of inverse trig functions.

Name: Meromorphic.lean
Description: Meromorphic properties of trig functions.

Name: Cotangent.lean
Description: Cotangent function and its properties.

Name: ArctanDeriv.lean
Description: Derivative of arctan: `arctan' x = 1/(1+x²)`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Real trigonometric functions: `Real.sin`, `Real.cos`, periodicity, and identities.

Name: Angle.lean
Description: Angles in ℝ/2πℤ: `Real.Angle`.

Name: Arctan.lean
Description: Real arctan: `Real.arctan` and its properties.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Complex extensions of real trig functions.

Name: ComplexDeriv.lean
Description: Complex derivatives of trig functions.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Trigonometric bounds: `sin x ≤ x`, `cos x ≥ 1 - x²/2`.

Name: Chebyshev.lean
Description: Chebyshev polynomials and trigonometry: `cos(nθ) = Tₙ(cosθ)`.

Name: Pow
Description: Power functions x^y for real and complex exponents: rpow x y = exp(y * log x) for x > 0, the extension to x ≥ 0 (with 0^0 = 1 by convention), and cpow for complex numbers. Contains continuity, differentiability (d/dx xʸ = y x^(y-1)), and monotonicity. Distinct from the algebraic npow (natural number exponents by repeated multiplication) — this is the analytic real/complex power.

Name: NNReal.lean
Description: Power function for NNReal: `NNReal.rpow` and its properties.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integrals of power functions.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Real power function: `Real.rpow x y = x^y` and its continuity, monotonicity.

Name: NthRootLemmas.lean
Description: N-th root lemmas for rpow.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Complex power function: `Complex.cpow z w` and its properties.

Name: Asymptotics.lean
Description: Asymptotics of power functions: `x^r = o(x^s)` for `r < s` as `x → ∞`.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of power functions: `HasDerivAt (·^r)` and `HasDerivAt Real.rpow`.

Name: Continuity.lean
Description: Continuity of `rpow`.

Name: ContinuousFunctionalCalculus
Description: Applying special functions to operators via the continuous functional calculus: exp(A), log(A), and √A for A a normal element of a C*-algebra, proved using the algebraic functional calculus from Analysis/CStarAlgebra. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/SpecialFunctions (the construction) — this folder applies specific functions and proves their properties.

Name: Rpow
Description: Real powers aˢ for s : ℝ applied to positive elements of a C*-algebra via the continuous functional calculus: defined by aˢ = exp(s · log a) for a > 0. The semigroup property (aˢ · aᵗ = aˢ⁺ᵗ) and the norm bound ‖aˢ‖ = ‖a‖ˢ for s ≥ 0. Used in interpolation theory and in fractional powers of positive operators.

Name: Isometric.lean
Description: Isometric properties of real powers via CFC.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order properties of real powers.

Name: RingInverseOrder.lean
Description: Ring inverse order for real powers.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Real power `a^r` for positive self-adjoint elements via CFC.

Name: ConjSqrt.lean
Description: Conjugate square root via CFC.

Name: IntegralRepresentation.lean
Description: Integral representation of `a^r` via CFC.

Name: PosPart
Description: Positive and negative parts of self-adjoint elements via the functional calculus: a⁺ = f₊(a) and a⁻ = f₋(a) where f₊(t) = max(t,0) and f₋(t) = max(-t,0), giving a = a⁺ - a⁻ with a⁺, a⁻ ≥ 0 and a⁺a⁻ = 0. Used in the Jordan decomposition of self-adjoint elements and in defining the absolute value |a| = a⁺ + a⁻.

Name: Isometric.lean
Description: Isometric properties of the positive part.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Positive part `a⁺ = (|a| + a)/2` via CFC.

Name: ExpLog
Description: Exponential and logarithm applied to C*-algebra elements via the continuous functional calculus: exp(a) for any element a, log(a) for invertible positive a. The Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff formula to first order, and the polar decomposition A = U|A| for invertible A. Used in the theory of one-parameter unitary groups e^{itH}.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order properties of exp/log via CFC.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic exp/log via CFC.

Name: ExpLog.lean
Description: Exponential and logarithm via CFC.

Name: Abs.lean
Description: Absolute value via CFC: `|a| = sqrt(a* a)` in a C*-algebra.

Name: Complex
Description: Complex-valued special functions: the complex exponential exp(z) = ∑zⁿ/n!, the complex logarithm log(z) (a branch on ℂ∖(-∞,0]), and complex trigonometric functions via sin(z) = (e^(iz) - e^(-iz))/(2i). Euler's formula e^(iπ) + 1 = 0. Distinct from Analysis/Real/Pi (the constant π) and Trigonometric (real-valued trig functions).

Name: Log.lean
Description: Complex logarithm: `Complex.log` (principal branch), its derivative, and discontinuity.

Name: CircleMap.lean
Description: Circle map `θ ↦ exp(iθ)`.

Name: LogDeriv.lean
Description: Derivative of complex logarithm.

Name: LogBounds.lean
Description: Bounds on the complex logarithm.

Name: CircleAddChar.lean
Description: Additive characters on the circle.

Name: Arctan.lean
Description: Complex arctan function.

Name: Analytic.lean
Description: Analyticity of complex special functions.

Name: Circle.lean
Description: Circle group in special functions: the map `θ ↦ e^{iθ}` from ℝ to `circle`.

Name: Arg.lean
Description: Argument of a complex number: `Complex.arg`.

Name: Elliptic
Description: The Weierstrass ℘-function ℘(z; Λ) and its derivative ℘'(z; Λ): doubly periodic meromorphic functions on ℂ satisfying the differential equation (℘')² = 4℘³ − g₂℘ − g₃, where g₂ and g₃ are the Eisenstein invariants of the lattice Λ. Contains weierstrassP, derivWeierstrassP, and derivWeierstrassP_sq (the differential equation). Proves periodicity and meromorphicity. Distinct from AlgebraicGeometry/EllipticCurve (the algebraic curve model connected to ℘ via the uniformization theorem).

Name: Weierstrass.lean
Description: Weierstrass elliptic functions ℘(z) and ζ(z).

Name: Gaussian
Description: Gaussian integrals: ∫_{-∞}^∞ e^(-x²) dx = √π (Gaussian integral), the Gaussian measure γ = (2π)^(-n/2) e^(-|x|²/2) dˣ on ℝⁿ, and the heat kernel. Used in probability (normal distribution), Fourier analysis (the Gaussian is its own Fourier transform), and number theory (Jacobi theta functions).

Name: PoissonSummation.lean
Description: Poisson summation formula applied to the Gaussian.

Name: GaussianIntegral.lean
Description: Gaussian integral: `∫_{-∞}^∞ e^{-x²} dx = √π`.

Name: FourierTransform.lean
Description: Fourier transform of the Gaussian: `ℱ[e^{-x²}](ξ) = √π e^{-πξ²}`.

Name: Gamma
Description: The Gamma function Γ(s) = ∫₀^∞ t^(s-1)e^(-t)dt: analytic continuation to ℂ∖{0,-1,-2,…}, the functional equation Γ(s+1) = sΓ(s), the reflection formula Γ(s)Γ(1-s) = π/sin(πs), the Weierstrass product, and Stirling's asymptotic Γ(s) ~ √(2π/s)(s/e)^s. Used in number theory (zeta function, L-functions) and probability (gamma distribution).

Name: Deligne.lean
Description: Deligne's formula for Gamma: the product formula and interpolation.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivative of the Gamma function: `Γ'(x) = ∫_0^∞ t^{x-1} e^{-t} log t dt`.

Name: Digamma.lean
Description: Digamma function ψ(x) = Γ'(x)/Γ(x).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Gamma function: `Real.Gamma s = ∫_0^∞ t^{s-1} e^{-t} dt`, functional equation, values at integers.

Name: Beta.lean
Description: Beta function: `Real.betaIntegral a b = ∫_0^1 x^{a-1}(1-x)^{b-1}dx = Γ(a)Γ(b)/Γ(a+b)`.

Name: BohrMollerup.lean
Description: Bohr–Mollerup theorem: Γ is the unique log-convex function with Γ(1)=1 and Γ(x+1)=xΓ(x).

Name: NonIntegrable.lean
Description: Examples of non-integrable functions: `1/x` is not integrable on `(0,1]`, `x^(-1)` near 0, and related functions. Proves `¬ Integrable (fun x => x⁻¹)` on appropriate intervals. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals (convergent improper integrals) and MeasureTheory/Function/LpSpace.

Name: OrdinaryHypergeometric.lean
Description: The ordinary hypergeometric function `₂F₁(a, b; c; z)` defined as a power series for `|z| < 1`, its analytic continuation, and special values. Active area in Mathlib; exact scope requires verification against current source.

Name: Pochhammer.lean
Description: Pochhammer symbol (rising factorial): `ascPochhammer ℝ n x = x * (x+1) * … * (x+n-1)` as an element of `ℝ[X]`, its evaluation, and the relation `Γ(x+n)/Γ(x) = (x)_n`. Distinct from Nat.factorial and Analysis/Polynomial files.

Name: JapaneseBracket.lean
Description: Japanese bracket `⟨x⟩ = (1 + ‖x‖²)^(1/2)`: integrability conditions `⟨·⟩^s ∈ L¹(ℝⁿ)` for `s < -n/2`, and norm estimates `‖x‖ ≤ ⟨x⟩ ≤ ‖x‖ + 1`. Used in Sobolev and Schwartz space theory. Distinct from Distribution/SchwartzSpace and SpecialFunctions/Sqrt.

Name: MulExpNegMulSq.lean
Description: Properties of `x ↦ x^n * exp(-c * x²)` for `c > 0`: boundedness, integrability, and the fact that it belongs to the Schwartz class. Key: `Real.hasDerivAt_mul_exp_neg_mul_sq`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/MulExpNegMulSqIntegral (integrals) and SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals.

Name: MulExpNegMulSqIntegral.lean
Description: Integrals of `x^n * exp(-c * x²)` over ℝ: the recursive formula via integration by parts, the Gaussian moments `∫ x^(2n) exp(-x²) = (2n-1)!! √π / 2^n`, and the normalization constants. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/MulExpNegMulSq (function properties) and SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals.

Name: SmoothTransition.lean
Description: Smooth bump/cutoff functions: `Real.smoothTransition` — a C^∞ function that is 0 for `x ≤ 0`, strictly increasing on `(0,1)`, and 1 for `x ≥ 1`. Built from `exp(-1/x)`. Used to construct partitions of unity and to mollify. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Sigmoid (analytic transition) and Convex/PartitionOfUnity.

Name: Sqrt.lean
Description: Real square root `Real.sqrt x = if x ≥ 0 then √x else 0`: continuity, `sqrt_sq`, `sq_sqrt`, the derivative `1/(2√x)` for `x > 0`, and `sqrt_mul`. The foundational file for real sqrt; other files derive complex sqrt and Hilbert space square roots. Distinct from Complex/SqrtDeriv and InnerProductSpace/Positive.

Name: Stirling.lean
Description: Stirling's approximation: `n! ~ √(2πn) * (n/e)^n` as `n → ∞`, proved via the Wallis product and log-convexity of the Gamma function. Key: `Real.stirling_tendsto`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Pochhammer (Pochhammer symbol) and SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals.

Name: PolarCoord.lean
Description: Polar coordinates in ℝ²: the diffeomorphism `polarCoord : ℝ² → (0,∞) × (-π, π)` given by `(r, θ) ↦ (r cos θ, r sin θ)`, its Jacobian `r`, and the change-of-variables formula `∫ f(x,y) dxdy = ∫ f(r cos θ, r sin θ) r dr dθ`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/ImproperIntegrals (Gaussian integral) and MeasureTheory/Measure/Hausdorff.

Name: PolynomialExp.lean
Description: Asymptotic behaviour of polynomial × exponential products: `p(x) * exp(-c*x) → 0` as `x → ∞` for any polynomial `p` and `c > 0`. Proves `Real.tendsto_polynomial_mul_exp_neg_atTop`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/CompareExp (exp vs power comparison) and SpecialFunctions/MulExpNegMulSq.

Name: Sigmoid.lean
Description: Sigmoid function `Real.sigmoid x = 1 / (1 + exp(-x))`: strict monotonicity, range `(0,1)`, derivative `sigmoid x * (1 - sigmoid x)`, and the relation `sigmoid x + sigmoid (-x) = 1`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Exp (exponential) and SpecialFunctions/SmoothTransition.

Name: Artanh.lean
Description: Inverse hyperbolic tangent `Real.artanh x = log((1+x)/(1-x))/2` for `|x| < 1`: domain, derivative `1/(1-x²)`, and the series expansion. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Arcosh and SpecialFunctions/Arsinh.

Name: Bernstein.lean
Description: Bernstein polynomials `B_{n,k}(x) = C(n,k) * x^k * (1-x)^(n-k)` and Bernstein's proof of the Weierstrass approximation theorem: `∑_k f(k/n) * B_{n,k} → f` uniformly on `[0,1]`. Proves `BernsteinPolynomial.tendsto_uniformly`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Exp and Convex/Approximation.

Name: BinaryEntropy.lean
Description: Binary entropy function `h(p) = -p * log p - (1-p) * log(1-p)` for `p ∈ [0,1]`: concavity, the maximum `h(1/2) = log 2`, derivative `log((1-p)/p)`, and bounds used in information theory. Distinct from InformationTheory files and SpecialFunctions/MulExpNegMulSq.

Name: Arcosh.lean
Description: Inverse hyperbolic cosine `Real.arcosh x = log(x + √(x²-1))` for `x ≥ 1`: strict monotonicity, continuity, derivative `1/√(x²-1)`, and the identity `arcosh (cosh x) = x`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Arsinh (sinh⁻¹) and SpecialFunctions/Artanh.

Name: ArithmeticGeometricMean.lean
Description: Arithmetic-geometric mean (AGM) iteration: the sequences `aₙ₊₁ = (aₙ + bₙ)/2` and `bₙ₊₁ = √(aₙbₙ)` converge to a common limit `M(a,b)`. Proves convergence, the AGM inequality `M(a,b) ≤ (a+b)/2`, and the relation to elliptic integrals. Distinct from SpecificLimits/ArithmeticGeometric and SpecialFunctions/Sqrt.

Name: Arsinh.lean
Description: Inverse hyperbolic sine `Real.arsinh x = log(x + √(x²+1))` for all `x ∈ ℝ`: bijectivity, derivative `1/√(1+x²)`, and `arsinh (sinh x) = x`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Arcosh (cosh⁻¹) and SpecialFunctions/Artanh.

Name: ExpDeriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of the real exponential and natural logarithm: `HasDerivAt Real.exp (exp x) x` and `HasDerivAt Real.log (1/x) x` for `x > 0`. Includes derivatives of `x^r` for real `r`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Exp (non-calculus properties) and SpecialFunctions/Exponential (matrix exponential).

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: The exponential map `NormedSpace.exp 𝕜 : A → A` in a Banach algebra `A` (matrix exponential, operator exponential): convergence of `∑ xⁿ/n!`, the functional equation `exp(x+y) = exp x * exp y` for commuting `x, y`, and the derivative `deriv (exp) x = exp x`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Exp (scalar ℝ-valued exponential).

Name: ImproperIntegrals.lean
Description: Improper integrals of specific functions: the Gaussian integral `∫ exp(-x²) dx = √π`, `∫_0^∞ x^(s-1) e^{-x} dx = Γ(s)`, and `∫_0^∞ sin(x)/x dx = π/2`. Distinct from MeasureTheory integration files and SpecialFunctions/MulExpNegMulSqIntegral.

Name: Choose.lean
Description: Analytic properties of the binomial coefficient as a function of real or complex arguments: the generalised binomial `(s choose k) = s*(s-1)*…*(s-k+1)/k!` and its analyticity. Connects to the Gamma function and Analytic/Binomial. Distinct from Nat.choose combinatorics files.

Name: CompareExp.lean
Description: Comparison of exponential growth with polynomial growth: `x^n = o(exp x)` as `x → ∞`, and the log dominates every negative power. Proves `Real.tendsto_pow_mul_exp_neg_atTop_nhds_zero`. Distinct from Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics and SpecialFunctions/Exp.

Name: Exp.lean
Description: The real exponential function `Real.exp`: positivity `exp x > 0`, strict monotonicity, `exp 0 = 1`, the functional equation `exp (x + y) = exp x * exp y`, and the series definition. The foundational file for `Real.exp`; other files derive logarithm, trig functions, etc.

Name: SpecificLimits
Description: Specific limit computations: convergence of series (geometric, power series), and limit values used across analysis.

Name: FloorPow.lean
Description: Limits involving floor and ceiling of powers: `⌊b^n⌋ / b^n → 1`, and related asymptotic results for sequences defined by `⌊x^n⌋`. Distinct from SpecificLimits/Normed (abstract series) and SpecificLimits/Fibonacci.

Name: Normed.lean
Description: Specific limit and summability results in normed spaces: geometric series `∑ r^n = 1/(1-r)` for `‖r‖ < 1`, ratio test, root test, and `tendsto_pow_atTop_nhds_zero_of_norm_lt_one`. The main library of named convergence results. Distinct from SpecificLimits/RCLike (RCLike-specific) and Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics.

Name: RCLike.lean
Description: Specific limit results specialised to `RCLike 𝕜` fields: limits involving `re`, `im`, `conj`, and `normSq` of convergent sequences, and RCLike-specialised summability criteria. Distinct from SpecificLimits/Normed (general normed) and RCLike/Lemmas.

Name: ArithmeticGeometric.lean
Description: Arithmetic-geometric mean inequality as a limit: convergence of the AGM iteration and specific limit results relating arithmetic and geometric means of sequences. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/ArithmeticGeometricMean (the AGM function itself) and SpecificLimits/Normed.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Convergence results for standard series and limits: the geometric series ∑ rⁿ = 1/(1-r) for |r| < 1 (tsum_geometric_of_lt_one), convergence of the exponential series to eˣ, the p-series test (∑ 1/nᵖ converges iff p > 1), and the limit (1 + 1/n)ⁿ → e. The primary reference file for established limit values used throughout analysis, probability, and number theory — including compound interest and fractal geometry.

Name: Fibonacci.lean
Description: The Fibonacci sequence `fib n` and the limit `fib(n+1)/fib(n) → φ` (golden ratio `φ = (1+√5)/2`). Proves `tendsto_fibonacci_div_fibonacci_succ`. Distinct from SpecificLimits/Normed (general normed space limits) and NumberTheory files on Fibonacci.

Name: VonNeumannAlgebra
Description: Von Neumann algebras: weakly closed *-subalgebras of B(H), the double commutant theorem, and the factor classification.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `VonNeumannAlgebra H`: a C*-subalgebra of `B(H)` (bounded operators on a Hilbert space `H`) that equals its double commutant, equivalently a C*-algebra that is closed in the weak operator topology. Provides `VonNeumannAlgebra.instStarAlgebra`, the bicommutant theorem statement, and basic instances. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Basic (abstract C*-algebras) and InnerProductSpace/StandardSubspace.

Name: Polynomial
Description: Analytic properties of polynomials: uniform convergence, polynomial approximation, and the Stone-Weierstrass theorem.

Name: MahlerMeasure.lean
Description: Mahler measure of a polynomial: `MahlerMeasure p = exp(∫_0^1 log |p(e^{2πit})| dt)` — the geometric mean of `|p|` on the unit circle. Proves `MahlerMeasure (p * q) = MahlerMeasure p * MahlerMeasure q` and Jensen's formula connection. Distinct from Analysis/Polynomial/CauchyBound and Complex/JensenFormula.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: Norms on polynomial rings: the sup norm on `[-1,1]`, the L² norm on `[a,b]`, and estimates like `‖p‖ ≤ (deg p + 1) * ‖p‖_∞`. Used in approximation theory. Distinct from Analysis/Polynomial/Basic (continuity) and Analysis/Polynomial/CauchyBound.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order of vanishing of a polynomial at a point: `Polynomial.order_of_root` and the factorisation `p = (X - a)^n * q` with `q(a) ≠ 0`. Distinct from Analytic/Order (order for analytic functions) and Analysis/Polynomial/Factorization.

Name: Fourier.lean
Description: Fourier analysis of polynomials: Fourier coefficients of polynomial functions on the torus, and the relationship between polynomial approximation and Fourier series truncation. Distinct from Analysis/Polynomial/Basic (real/complex analysis) and Fourier/FourierTransform.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Analytic and metric properties of polynomials as functions on normed fields: `Polynomial.continuous`, `Polynomial.differentiable`, and `Polynomial.norm_le_of_forall_le`. The bridge between the algebraic `Mathlib/RingTheory/Polynomial` files and analytic facts. Distinct from Analytic/Polynomial (analyticity) and Analysis/Polynomial/Norm.

Name: CauchyBound.lean
Description: Cauchy's bound on the roots of a polynomial: all roots of `p ∈ 𝕜[X]` lie in the disk of radius `1 + max_{i<n} |aᵢ/aₙ|`. Proves `Polynomial.roots_lt_cauchyBound`. Distinct from Analysis/Polynomial/Factorization (factorisation) and Analytic/IsolatedZeros.

Name: Factorization.lean
Description: Factorisation of polynomials over normed fields: `Polynomial.roots` as a multiset, the splitting field in a complete normed algebraically closed field, and `Polynomial.eq_prod_roots_of_splits`. Distinct from Analysis/Polynomial/CauchyBound (bounds on roots) and RingTheory/Polynomial factorisation files.

Name: RCLike
Description: The RCLike typeclass: a unified treatment of R and C that shares theorems about the absolute value and inner product structure.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Miscellaneous lemmas for `RCLike 𝕜`: norm properties of `re`, `im`, `conj` and `normSq`; `abs_of_nonneg` in the RCLike setting; and lemmas bridging the algebraic and analytic aspects not in Basic. Distinct from RCLike/Basic (core definitions) and RCLike/Sqrt.

Name: Inner.lean
Description: Inner product spaces over `RCLike` fields: the extension of a real inner product to a complex inner product via `inner 𝕜 x y = ⟪x, y⟫_ℝ - I * ⟪x, iy⟫_ℝ`, and the uniform treatment of real and complex Hilbert spaces. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs (concrete definitions) and RCLike/Basic.

Name: TangentCone.lean
Description: Tangent cones in the RCLike setting: `tangentConeAt 𝕜 s x` and its properties when `𝕜` is ℝ or ℂ. Used in the Lagrange multiplier theorem over complex or real fields. Distinct from Calculus/TangentCone (real setting) and RCLike/Basic.

Name: Sqrt.lean
Description: Square root in an `RCLike` field: `RCLike.sqrt x` defined as the real square root of `re x` or the complex square root, with `sqrt (normSq x) = ‖x‖`. Distinct from SpecialFunctions/Sqrt (real sqrt) and Complex/SqrtDeriv (complex sqrt derivative).

Name: BoundedContinuous.lean
Description: Bounded continuous functions with values in an `RCLike` field: `BoundedContinuousFunction.normSq`, `re`, `im` as continuous maps `BoundedContinuousFunction X 𝕜 → BoundedContinuousFunction X ℝ`. Distinct from RCLike/ContinuousMap (continuous but not necessarily bounded) and RCLike/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `RCLike 𝕜` (formerly `IsROrC`): a typeclass unifying ℝ and ℂ, with `re`, `im`, `conj`, `normSq`, `I` (with `I = 0` in ℝ), and `ofReal`. Provides instances for both ℝ and ℂ and the derived norm `‖x‖ = √(normSq x)`. The foundational file for the RCLike folder.

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extension of ℝ-linear maps to 𝕜-linear maps using the RCLike structure: `LinearMap.extendTo𝕜` (complexification of a real-linear map `f : E →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ` to a `𝕜`-linear map). Used in the complex Hahn–Banach theorem. Distinct from RCLike/Inner (inner product extension) and LocallyConvex/Separation.

Name: ContinuousMap.lean
Description: Continuous maps to RCLike fields: `ContinuousMap.re`, `ContinuousMap.im`, `ContinuousMap.conj` as continuous map operations, and the `StarModule` structure of `C(X, 𝕜)`. Distinct from RCLike/BoundedContinuous and CStarAlgebra/ContinuousMap.

Name: Real
Description: Real analysis specifics: the real number line as a complete ordered field, sup/inf, and properties of R as a normed space.

Name: Pi
Description: The number π: analytical definitions (π = 2∫₀¹ √(1-x²) dx, or as twice the smallest positive zero of cos), the irrationality and transcendence of π, and the formulas π = 4(1 - 1/3 + 1/5 - …) and π²/6 = ∑1/n². Distinct from Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Trigonometric (which defines sin and cos) — this folder specifically concerns the constant π.

Name: Leibniz.lean
Description: Leibniz formula: `π/4 = 1 - 1/3 + 1/5 - 1/7 + ⋯`.

Name: Wallis.lean
Description: Wallis product: `π/2 = ∏ (4n²)/(4n²-1)`.

Name: Irrational.lean
Description: Irrationality of π.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds for π: `3 < π < 4` and tighter estimates.

Name: Chudnovsky.lean
Description: Chudnovsky formula for 1/π.

Name: OfDigits.lean
Description: Real numbers defined by their digit expansions: `Nat.ofDigits` and the correspondence between sequences of digits in base `b` and real numbers in `[0,1]`. Proves the series `∑ dₙ/bⁿ` converges. Distinct from Real/Hyperreal and NumberTheory/DigitExpansion files.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectrum of elements in real Banach algebras and real C*-algebras: the complexification technique (embedding into the complex version), and the spectral radius formula over ℝ. Distinct from Complex/Spectrum (complex Banach algebras) and CStarAlgebra/Spectrum.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of the real numbers: the formal proof that ℝ is uncountable (¬ Countable ℝ) via Cantor's diagonal argument, and the strict inequality #ℕ < #ℝ. Contains the identification of the cardinality of the continuum #ℝ = 2^#ℕ (beth-one) and the non-denumerability of any interval (a, b). Establishes that there are strictly more real numbers than natural numbers — an infinite hierarchy of infinities.

Name: Hyperreal.lean
Description: Hyperreal numbers: `Hyperreal` as an ultrapower of ℝ with respect to a nonprincipal ultrafilter, providing infinitesimals and infinite elements. Proves transfer principle instances and `Hyperreal.infinitesimal`. Distinct from the ultrafilter constructions in Order files and Real/OfDigits.

Name: InnerProductSpace
Description: Inner product spaces and Hilbert spaces: orthogonality, Gram-Schmidt, the Riesz representation theorem, and spectral theory.

Name: Projection
Description: Orthogonal projections in inner product spaces: for a closed subspace K ⊆ H, the projection P_K : H → K satisfying ‖x - P_K x‖ = dist(x, K) and x - P_K x ⊥ K. Contains the Riesz representation theorem (bounded linear functionals on H are inner products with a fixed vector) and the direct sum H = K ⊕ K⊥. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Harmonic (Fourier analysis).

Name: Reflection.lean
Description: Reflections via orthogonal projections.

Name: Submodule.lean
Description: Projection onto a submodule.

Name: Minimal.lean
Description: Best approximation via orthogonal projection.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Orthogonal projection onto a closed subspace: `orthogonalProjection K`.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Orthogonal projections in finite dimensions.

Name: Harmonic
Description: Harmonic analysis in inner product spaces: spherical harmonics on the unit sphere Sⁿ⁻¹ ⊆ ℝⁿ, their orthogonality with respect to the spherical measure, and the decomposition of L²(Sⁿ) into harmonic polynomial spaces. Distinct from Complex/Harmonic (harmonic functions in ℂ) and InnerProductSpace/Projection (orthogonal projections).

Name: HarmonicContOnCl.lean
Description: Harmonic functions continuous on closure.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of harmonic functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Harmonic analysis on inner product spaces: spherical harmonics.

Name: Positive.lean
Description: Positive operators on Hilbert spaces: `ContinuousLinearMap.IsPositive T` meaning `0 ≤ T` (i.e., `⟪Tx, x⟫ ≥ 0` for all `x`). The square root of a positive operator, and the Löwner order on positive operators. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Adjoint (adjoints) and CStarAlgebra/Projection.

Name: PiL2.lean
Description: `EuclideanSpace 𝕜 ι = PiLp 2 (fun _ : ι => 𝕜)` as a Hilbert space with `⟪x, y⟫ = ∑ xᵢ * conj yᵢ`. Provides the canonical `OrthonormalBasis` of coordinate vectors and `EuclideanSpace.equiv`. The model for finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/ProdL2 and InnerProductSpace/l2Space.

Name: ProdL2.lean
Description: Product of two Hilbert spaces `E × F` with the L²-norm `‖(x, y)‖² = ‖x‖² + ‖y‖²` and inner product `⟪(x₁,y₁), (x₂,y₂)⟫ = ⟪x₁,x₂⟫ + ⟪y₁,y₂⟫`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/PiL2 (indexed product) and InnerProductSpace/TensorProduct.

Name: Reproducing.lean
Description: Reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces (RKHS): a Hilbert space `H` of functions on a set `X` where point evaluations are continuous linear functionals, so there exists a kernel `K(x, y) = ⟪K_y, K_x⟫`. Exact scope and API requires verification against current Mathlib source.

Name: Rayleigh.lean
Description: Rayleigh quotient and the spectral theorem for compact self-adjoint operators: the largest eigenvalue equals `sup {⟪Tx, x⟫ / ‖x‖² | x ≠ 0}` (Rayleigh quotient). Proves `IsSelfAdjoint.hasEigenvalue_iSup_of_compact` and the min-max principle. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Spectrum and InnerProductSpace/JointEigenspace.

Name: Orthonormal.lean
Description: Orthonormal sets and Hilbert bases: `Orthonormal 𝕜 v` (`⟪v i, v j⟫ = if i = j then 1 else 0`), existence of maximal orthonormal sets (Zorn), Parseval's identity `∑ |⟪x, eᵢ⟫|² = ‖x‖²`, and `HilbertBasis`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/GramSchmidtOrtho (construction) and InnerProductSpace/Orthogonal.

Name: NormPow.lean
Description: Powers of the norm in inner product spaces: `‖x‖^(2*n)` expressed as the `n`-fold inner product `⟪x, x⟫^n`, and continuity of `x ↦ ‖x‖^r` for real `r`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Calculus (derivatives of norm) and InnerProductSpace/Defs.

Name: MulOpposite.lean
Description: Inner product space structure on the opposite algebra `Eᵐᵒᵖ`: the inner product `⟪a.unop, b.unop⟫` transferred to `Eᵐᵒᵖ`. Used to make conjugate-linear structures available. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs and algebra opposite files.

Name: OfNorm.lean
Description: Constructing an inner product from a norm satisfying the parallelogram law `2‖x‖² + 2‖y‖² = ‖x+y‖² + ‖x-y‖²`: the Jordan–von Neumann theorem. Provides `InnerProductSpace.ofNorm` and the polarisation identity. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs (axiomatic definition).

Name: Orthogonal.lean
Description: Orthogonal complements: `Submodule.orthogonal K = Kᗮ = {v | ∀ u ∈ K, ⟪u, v⟫ = 0}`. Proves `(Kᗮ)ᗮ = closure K`, `K ⊕ Kᗮ = E` (projection theorem for closed subspaces), and the orthogonal projection `orthogonalProjection K`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Subspace and InnerProductSpace/Defs.

Name: Orientation.lean
Description: Orientation in finite-dimensional inner product spaces: `OrthonormalBasis.orientation`, the orientation class of an orthonormal basis, and the oriented volume form. Proves two orthonormal bases have the same orientation iff their transition matrix has positive determinant. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/TwoDim and LinearAlgebra/Orientation.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Hilbert tensor product of two Hilbert spaces: `E ⊗[ℂ] F` with inner product `⟪x₁ ⊗ y₁, x₂ ⊗ y₂⟫ = ⟪x₁, x₂⟫ * ⟪y₁, y₂⟫`, completion to a Hilbert space. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/ProdL2 (direct sum) and LinearAlgebra/TensorProduct.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetric (formally self-adjoint) densely defined operators on a Hilbert space: `LinearMap.IsSymmetric T` meaning `⟪Tx, y⟫ = ⟪x, Ty⟫` for all `x, y` in the domain. Proves eigenvalues of a symmetric operator are real. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Adjoint (bounded adjoints) and InnerProductSpace/LinearPMap (unbounded case).

Name: Trace.lean
Description: Trace-class operators on a Hilbert space: `trace T = ∑ ⟪T eᵢ, eᵢ⟫` (sum over any orthonormal basis), independence of basis, and the trace norm making trace-class operators a Banach *-ideal of `B(H)`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/SingularValues (singular values) and Matrix norms.

Name: WeakOperatorTopology.lean
Description: Weak operator topology (WOT) on bounded operators `E →L[𝕜] E`: the topology of pointwise weak convergence `⟪T_α x, y⟫ → ⟪Tx, y⟫`. Proves the WOT is weaker than the strong operator topology and coarser than the norm topology. Distinct from LocallyConvex/WeakOperatorTopology (general TVS version) and CStarAlgebra/Basic.

Name: TwoDim.lean
Description: Two-dimensional inner product spaces: `Orientation.areaForm` (the symplectic form / cross product), `Orientation.rotate` (rotation by π/2), and the formula `‖x ∧ y‖ = ‖x‖ * ‖y‖ * sin(angle x y)`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Orientation (general finite-dimensional) and Complex/Angle.

Name: Subspace.lean
Description: Hilbert subspaces: closed subspaces of a Hilbert space are themselves Hilbert spaces, orthogonal decomposition `E = K ⊕ Kᗮ`, and the orthogonal projection `orthogonalProjection K` as a bounded self-adjoint idempotent. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Orthogonal (orthogonal complement theory) and InnerProductSpace/Orthonormal.

Name: SingularValues.lean
Description: Singular values of a compact operator `T : E → F` between Hilbert spaces: `singularValues T` as the eigenvalues of `(T* T)^(1/2)`, the singular value decomposition (SVD), and the min-max characterisation. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Adjoint (adjoint) and InnerProductSpace/Spectrum (eigenvalues of normal operators).

Name: Semisimple.lean
Description: Semisimplicity results in the context of inner product spaces or operator algebras, possibly relating to the semisimple decomposition of a self-adjoint or normal operator. Exact content requires verification against current Mathlib source.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectral theorem for self-adjoint and normal compact operators: every compact self-adjoint operator on a Hilbert space has a complete orthonormal system of eigenvectors, and the eigenvalues are real. Proves `IsSymmetric.hasEigenvalue_of_isCompact`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Rayleigh (extremal characterisation) and CStarAlgebra/Spectrum.

Name: StarOrder.lean
Description: The Löwner (star) order on the self-adjoint operators in a C*-algebra or on `B(H)`: `a ≤ b` iff `b - a` is positive. Proves the order is compatible with the C*-algebra structure and connects to `ContinuousLinearMap.IsPositive`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Positive (positive operators) and CStarAlgebra/Spectrum.

Name: StandardSubspace.lean
Description: Standard subspaces in the sense of Tomita–Takesaki modular theory: closed real subspaces `H` of a complex Hilbert space satisfying `H ∩ iH = {0}` and `H + iH` dense. Active area; exact scope and API requires verification against current Mathlib source.

Name: ConformalLinearMap.lean
Description: Conformal linear maps in inner product spaces: a linear map `f : E → F` is conformal at `x` iff `f = c • isometry` for some scalar `c > 0` and linear isometry. Characterises conformality via `‖f x‖ = c * ‖x‖` for all `x`. Distinct from Complex/Conformal (holomorphic conformal maps) and InnerProductSpace/Adjoint.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Hilbert space completion of a pre-Hilbert space (inner product space that may not be complete): `InnerProductSpace.Completion E` as the uniform space completion, with the extended inner product making it a Hilbert space. Proves `Completion.innerProductSpace`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs and NormedSpace completions.

Name: Continuous.lean
Description: Continuity results for the inner product: `inner` is continuous in both arguments jointly, and `norm_sq` is continuous. Also proves `ContinuousLinearMap.inner_apply` and related norm-continuity facts. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Calculus (differentiability) and InnerProductSpace/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: The `InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E` typeclass: the inner product `inner 𝕜 E : E → E → 𝕜` with sesquilinearity, conjugate symmetry, and positive definiteness. Derives the norm `‖x‖ = √⟪x, x⟫` and proves the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality `|⟪x, y⟫| ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖`. The foundational file for the InnerProductSpace folder.

Name: Convex.lean
Description: Convexity in inner product spaces: the metric projection onto a closed convex set is well-defined (projection theorem), characterised by `inner_sub_projection_le`, and the nearest-point map is nonexpansive. Distinct from Convex/MetricSpace (same results for general metric spaces) and InnerProductSpace/Orthogonal.

Name: Coalgebra.lean
Description: A finite-dimensional inner product space has a coalgebra structure when it has an algebra structure: comultiplication is the adjoint of multiplication, and counit is the adjoint of the unit map. Conversely, a coalgebra structure on an inner product space induces an algebra structure. Useful for finite-dimensional C*-algebras with faithful traces.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: Affine maps preserving inner products: `AffineIsometry` and `AffineIsometryEquiv` in inner product spaces. Proves that affine isometries are characterised by preservation of distances, and the Mazur–Ulam theorem in the finite-dimensional case. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs (abstract inner product) and Convex/LinearIsometry.

Name: Adjoint.lean
Description: Adjoint operators on Hilbert spaces: `ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint T` characterised by `⟪Tx, y⟫ = ⟪x, T*y⟫`, self-adjoint (`T = T*`), unitary (`T* T = id`), and normal (`T T* = T* T`) operators. Proves the adjoint is a conjugate-linear isometric involution. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Symmetric (unbounded self-adjoint) and CStarAlgebra/ContinuousLinearMap.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The Cauchy-Schwarz inequality in inner product spaces: |⟨x, y⟩|² ≤ ‖x‖² · ‖y‖², formalized as inner_mul_le_norm_mul_norm. Contains the equality condition (x and y are proportional), the triangle inequality as a consequence, and the polarization identity. The universal inequality of Hilbert space theory — used in probability (correlation), statistics (covariance bounds), linear algebra, and quantum mechanics.

Name: CanonicalTensor.lean
Description: `InnerProductSpace.canonicalContravariantTensor E : E ⊗[ℝ] E →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ` — the inner product as a linear map on the tensor product. `InnerProductSpace.canonicalCovariantTensor E : E ⊗[ℝ] E` — the corresponding covariant tensor. `canonicalCovariantTensor_eq_sum`: equals `∑ i, v i ⊗ v i` for any orthonormal basis `v`.

Name: Calculus.lean
Description: Differentiability of the inner product and norm in an inner product space: `hasFDerivAt_inner`, the gradient of `x ↦ ⟪x, y⟫` is `y`, and the derivative of `x ↦ ‖x‖` is `x/‖x‖` at `x ≠ 0`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs (algebraic structure) and InnerProductSpace/NormPow.

Name: LaxMilgram.lean
Description: Lax–Milgram theorem: a bounded coercive sesquilinear form `B : E × E → 𝕜` on a Hilbert space `E` (satisfying `|B(u,v)| ≤ C‖u‖‖v‖` and `Re B(u,u) ≥ δ‖u‖²`) defines a bounded invertible linear map via the Riesz theorem. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Dual (Riesz representation) and Calculus/Implicit.

Name: Laplacian.lean
Description: The Laplacian operator in an inner product space or Euclidean setting: `innerSL_laplacian` as the trace of the Hessian, and properties of harmonic functions (kernel of the Laplacian). Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Calculus (general differentiability) and Distribution files.

Name: LinearMap.lean
Description: Linear maps between inner product spaces: the matrix of an operator with respect to an orthonormal basis, the relationship between `HasAdjoint` and the transpose, and `LinearMap.IsSymmetric`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Adjoint (continuous adjoint) and InnerProductSpace/Defs.

Name: MeanErgodic.lean
Description: Von Neumann mean ergodic theorem: for a unitary operator `U` on a Hilbert space, the Cesàro means `(1/n) ∑_{k=0}^{n-1} Uᵏ f` converge in norm to the orthogonal projection of `f` onto the fixed-point subspace `ker(U - 1)`. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Adjoint and MeasureTheory ergodic files.

Name: LinearPMap.lean
Description: Partially defined linear maps (linear `PMap`s) on Hilbert spaces: the adjoint of a densely defined operator, symmetry and self-adjointness for unbounded operators. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Symmetric (symmetric operators) and InnerProductSpace/Adjoint (bounded case).

Name: l2Space.lean
Description: `lp (fun _ : ι => 𝕜) 2` (square-summable sequences) as a Hilbert space with inner product `⟪x, y⟫ = ∑ xᵢ * conj yᵢ`. Provides `l2Space` instances and proves completeness. Distinguished from `EuclideanSpace` (finite index) and InnerProductSpace/PiL2.

Name: EuclideanDist.lean
Description: Euclidean distance in terms of the inner product: `EuclideanDist.dist_eq`, `‖x - y‖² = ‖x‖² - 2⟪x, y⟫ + ‖y‖²`, and the parallelogram law. Used to show that `EuclideanSpace 𝕜 ι` is an inner product space. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/PiL2 (product space) and InnerProductSpace/Defs.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Riesz representation theorem: the map `x ↦ ⟪x, ·⟫` is a conjugate-linear isometric isomorphism `E ≃ₗᵢ⋆[𝕜] NormedSpace.Dual 𝕜 E`. Proves `InnerProductSpace.toDualMap` is surjective (Hilbert case). Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Defs and LocallyConvex/SeparatingDual.

Name: GramMatrix.lean
Description: Gram matrix `M i j = ⟪v i, v j⟫` of a family of vectors `v : ι → E`: properties (Hermitian, positive semidefinite), the Gram determinant `det M = 0` iff the vectors are linearly dependent. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/GramSchmidtOrtho (orthogonalisation process).

Name: JointEigenspace.lean
Description: Joint eigenspaces for commuting families of self-adjoint operators on a Hilbert space: `EigenspaceDecomposition` for a commuting family, simultaneous diagonalisation of commuting self-adjoint operators. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Spectrum (single operator) and InnerProductSpace/Adjoint.

Name: GramSchmidtOrtho.lean
Description: Gram–Schmidt orthogonalisation: `gramSchmidt 𝕜 f` produces an orthonormal family from a linearly independent family by successive projection and normalisation. Proves `gramSchmidt_orthonormal` and span preservation. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Orthonormal (properties of orthonormal sets) and LinearAlgebra/Matrix/DotProduct.

Name: Asymptotics
Description: Asymptotic notation: big-O, little-o, and equivalence of functions, with their algebraic properties and composition rules.

Name: SpecificAsymptotics.lean
Description: Asymptotic estimates for specific functions: `log`, `Real.exp`, powers, and their combinations as `x → ∞` or at zero. Includes `isLittleO_log_id_atTop` and related named results. Distinct from Asymptotics/Lemmas (abstract rules) and SpecialFunctions/CompareExp.

Name: LinearGrowth.lean
Description: Linear growth conditions: functions satisfying `‖f x‖ ≤ C * ‖x‖ + D`, their closure under operations, and applications to Lipschitz-type estimates. Distinct from Asymptotics/ExpGrowth (exponential bounds) and Asymptotics/SuperpolynomialDecay.

Name: SuperpolynomialDecay.lean
Description: `SuperpolynomialDecay l k f`: the function `f` decays faster than any polynomial `x^(-n)` along filter `l`. Characterises Schwartz-like decay without full smoothness requirements. Distinct from Distribution/SchwartzSpace (which requires smooth Schwartz functions) and Asymptotics/ExpGrowth.

Name: TVS.lean
Description: Asymptotics in topological vector spaces: `IsBigO` and `IsLittleO` for functions taking values in a TVS rather than a normed space, using the neighbourhood filter at 0 in place of a norm bound. Distinct from Asymptotics/Defs (normed setting) and LocallyConvex/Bounded.

Name: Theta.lean
Description: Θ-notation: `IsTheta l f g` meaning `f = O(g)` and `g = O(f)` simultaneously. Provides algebraic rules (product, quotient) and comparison with `IsEquivalent`. Distinct from Asymptotics/AsymptoticEquivalent (which requires `f/g → 1`) and Asymptotics/Defs.

Name: Completion.lean
Description: Extension of `IsBigO` and `IsLittleO` relations to the uniform space completion of the codomain. Proves that o/O estimates are preserved when passing to completions, enabling transfer of asymptotic results. Distinct from Asymptotics/Defs (core definitions) and Asymptotics/Lemmas (algebraic manipulations).

Name: AsymptoticEquivalent.lean
Description: Asymptotic equivalence `f ~[l] g` (meaning `(f - g) = o(g)` along filter `l`): definition, basic algebra (products, quotients, powers), and comparison with `IsBigO`/`IsLittleO`. Key lemma: `IsEquivalent.tendsto_iff`. Distinct from Asymptotics/Defs (which defines O/o) and Asymptotics/Theta (two-sided bounds).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions: `IsBigOWith c l f g` (∃ bound with explicit constant c), `IsBigO l f g` (f = O(g)), and `IsLittleO l f g` (f = o(g)) along a filter `l`. Contains the unfolding lemmas and the relationship between the three predicates. The foundational file for the Asymptotics folder.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Algebraic and order lemmas for `IsBigO`/`IsLittleO`: transitivity, sum, product, scalar multiplication, composition, and norm-stripping rules. The main workhorse file for asymptotic manipulations. Distinct from Asymptotics/Defs (definitions) and Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics (concrete functions).

Name: ExpGrowth.lean
Description: Functions of at most exponential growth: `IsExpGrowth` or similar predicates bounding `‖f x‖` by `C * exp(c * ‖x‖)`. Used in distribution theory and PDE estimates. Distinct from Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics (named function asymptotics) and SpecialFunctions/CompareExp.

Name: BoxIntegral
Description: The box (Henstock-Kurzweil) integral: a generalization of the Riemann integral defined via tagged partitions of rectangles.

Name: Partition
Description: Tagged partitions of boxes: a subdivision of a box into sub-boxes, each equipped with a tag point. The Henstock condition (tag lies in its sub-box within a gauge-specified distance) is what distinguishes the Henstock-Kurzweil integral from the Riemann integral. Distinct from Box (the geometric object) — Partition is the analytical machinery for integration.

Name: Split.lean
Description: Splitting partitions: subdividing along a hyperplane.

Name: SubboxInduction.lean
Description: Subbox induction for partitions.

Name: Tagged.lean
Description: Tagged partitions: partitions with a distinguished point in each box.

Name: Measure.lean
Description: Measure of a partition: volume of each piece.

Name: Additive.lean
Description: Additive functions on box partitions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Partitions of boxes: `BoxIntegral.Partition I J` — subdivisions of box `I`.

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Filter of tagged partitions: the filter controlling refinements.

Name: Box
Description: Rectangular boxes in ℝⁿ: the Box type defined by lower and upper coordinate bounds, with volume (product of side lengths), membership, and the partial order by inclusion. The elementary domain over which the box (Henstock-Kurzweil) integral is defined. Distinct from BoxIntegral/Partition — this is the geometric building block, not the subdivision.

Name: SubboxInduction.lean
Description: Induction on subboxes: transfinite induction for box integrals.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Boxes in ℝⁿ: `BoxIntegral.Box` — rectangular boxes `[a, b] = {x | ∀ i, aᵢ ≤ xᵢ ≤ bᵢ}` and their volumes.

Name: Integrability.lean
Description: Conditions under which a function is box-integrable: every `BoxIntegral.IntegrationParams`-integrable function is McShane-integrable, and McShane-integrable functions coincide with Bochner-integrable functions. Connects the box integral framework to the Bochner/Lebesgue theory. Distinct from BoxIntegral/Basic (integral definition) and MeasureTheory integration.

Name: UnitPartition.lean
Description: Partitions of the unit box `[0,1]^n` used in constructing integrals: dyadic and uniform tagged partitions, their refinements, and the Henstock–Saks lemma infrastructure. Provides the combinatorial underpinning for the convergence proofs in BoxIntegral/Basic. Distinct from BoxIntegral/Basic (which uses these partitions).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The `BoxIntegral.integral` for a family of Riemann-type integrals parameterised by `BoxIntegral.IntegrationParams`, unifying the Riemann, Henstock–Kurzweil, and McShane integrals in one framework. Defines tagged partitions, the Riemann sum, and the integral as a limit over filtered tagged partitions. Distinct from BoxIntegral/Integrability (when the integral exists) and BoxIntegral/DivergenceTheorem.

Name: DivergenceTheorem.lean
Description: Divergence theorem for the box integral (Henstock–Kurzweil version): the integral of the divergence of a vector field over a box equals the flux through the boundary. The key result `BoxIntegral.hasIntegral_GP_divergence` connects the GP (Generalized Perron) integral to Stokes' theorem in the rectangular setting. Distinct from BoxIntegral/Basic and MeasureTheory-based versions of the divergence theorem.

Name: Calculus
Description: Differential calculus in normed spaces: Frechet derivative, the chain rule, higher derivatives, Taylor's theorem, and the inverse function theorem.

Name: InverseFunctionTheorem
Description: The inverse function theorem: a C^1 map with invertible Fréchet derivative at x₀ is a local C^1 diffeomorphism near x₀. Contains the open mapping theorem and the constant rank theorem. Distinct from ImplicitFunction (which handles the implicit case) and ContDiff (which studies higher regularity).

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Inverse function theorem for scalar derivatives.

Name: FDeriv.lean
Description: Inverse function theorem via Fréchet derivatives.

Name: FiniteDimensional.lean
Description: Inverse function theorem in finite dimensions.

Name: Analytic.lean
Description: Inverse function theorem for analytic functions.

Name: ApproximatesLinearOn.lean
Description: Functions approximating a linear map: the contraction argument.

Name: ContDiff.lean
Description: C^n inverse function theorem.

Name: ImplicitFunction
Description: The implicit function theorem: if F : E × F → G satisfies F(x₀, y₀) = 0 and D₂F(x₀, y₀) is invertible, then locally y = g(x) is the unique solution to F(x, y) = 0. Distinct from InverseFunctionTheorem (the special case F(x, y) = y - h(x)) — the implicit function theorem is the more general statement.

Name: ProdDomain.lean
Description: Implicit function theorem on product domains.

Name: Bivariate.lean
Description: Implicit function theorem for bivariate functions.

Name: Gradient
Description: The gradient ∇f of a function on an inner product space: the unique vector satisfying ⟨∇f(x), v⟩ = fderiv f x v for all v, obtained via the Riesz representation theorem. Contains gradient descent, the relationship between ∇f and level sets, and the divergence theorem setup. Distinct from FDeriv (the cotangent-valued derivative, not the gradient vector).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Gradient in inner product spaces: `gradient f x` such that `⟪gradient f x, v⟫ = df(x)(v)`.

Name: IteratedDeriv
Description: Iterated Fréchet derivatives iteratedFDeriv 𝕜 n f x : E [×n]→L[𝕜] F: the nth derivative as a continuous symmetric multilinear map. Contains the Taylor formula with remainder in Banach spaces and the symmetry of mixed partials. Distinct from ContDiff (which defines the C^n condition) — IteratedDeriv computes the actual derivative.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for iterated derivatives.

Name: WithinZpow.lean
Description: Iterated derivatives of integer power functions.

Name: FaaDiBruno.lean
Description: Faà di Bruno for iterated univariate derivatives.

Name: ConvergenceOnBall.lean
Description: Convergence of Taylor series on a ball.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Iterated derivatives: `iteratedDeriv n f x`.

Name: TangentCone
Description: Tangent cones to subsets of a normed space: the Bouligand tangent cone TangentConeAt s x = {v | ∃ tₙ → 0⁺, xₙ ∈ s, (xₙ − x)/tₙ → v}, measuring feasible directions at a point on the boundary. Contains tangentConeAt, UniqueDiffWithinAt (the condition for a unique supporting functional, used to extend the chain rule to restrictions), and UniqueDiffOn. Used in directional differentiability and set-valued analysis. Distinct from Manifold/Tangent (the tangent bundle on smooth manifolds).

Name: ProperSpace.lean
Description: Tangent cones in proper metric spaces.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Tangent cones of product sets.

Name: Seq.lean
Description: Sequential characterisation of tangent cones.

Name: Real.lean
Description: Tangent cones over ℝ: the real line case with explicit interval descriptions.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Tangent cone definitions: `tangentConeAt 𝕜 s x` — the set of directions along which sequences in `s` converge to `x`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Tangent cone basics: definition and basic properties.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Tangent cones of pi-type sets: `tangentConeAt 𝕜 (Π i, sᵢ) x = Π i, tangentConeAt 𝕜 sᵢ xᵢ`.

Name: DimOne.lean
Description: Tangent cones in dimension one: explicit computation for subsets of ℝ.

Name: LocalExtr
Description: Local extrema of real-valued functions: Fermat's theorem (local extremum ⟹ derivative zero), the second derivative test, and Lagrange multipliers for constrained optimization. Contains IsLocalMax, IsLocalMin, and their characterizations via the derivative. Distinct from Calculus/Gradient which gives the geometric picture, and Convex/SpecificFunctions which uses convexity.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Local extrema of polynomial functions.

Name: Rolle.lean
Description: Rolle's theorem via local extrema.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Local extrema: `IsLocalMin f x` and `IsLocalMax f x`.

Name: LineDeriv.lean
Description: Local extrema and line derivatives.

Name: LineDeriv
Description: Directional (line) derivatives: the derivative of f in direction v at x, lim_{t→0} (f(x + tv) - f(x)) / t. Weaker than the Fréchet derivative. Contains the relationship between directional derivatives and FDeriv (the Fréchet derivative implies all directional derivatives exist), and the Gâteaux derivative. Distinct from Deriv (one-variable functions) and FDeriv (full linear approximation).

Name: Measurable.lean
Description: Measurability of line derivatives.

Name: QuadraticMap.lean
Description: Line derivatives of quadratic maps.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Line derivatives: `lineDerivWithin 𝕜 f s x v = lim_{t→0} (f(x + tv) - f(x)) / t`.

Name: IntegrationByParts.lean
Description: Integration by parts for line derivatives.

Name: FDeriv
Description: The Fréchet derivative fderiv 𝕜 f x : E →L[𝕜] F: the bounded linear map best approximating f near x. The central object of infinite-dimensional calculus. Contains the chain rule, linearity in f, and the relationship to Gâteaux derivatives. Distinct from Deriv (one-variable scalar) and ContDiff (higher-order differentiability).

Name: Mul.lean
Description: FDeriv of products: the product rule for Fréchet derivatives.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: FDeriv and the norm.

Name: Partial.lean
Description: Partial Fréchet derivatives.

Name: Extend.lean
Description: Extending FDeriv to the boundary.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: FDeriv of linear maps.

Name: Measurable.lean
Description: Measurability of the Fréchet derivative.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: FDeriv of pi-type functions.

Name: Star.lean
Description: FDeriv and star: the Fréchet derivative of the star map in a C*-algebra.

Name: Symmetric.lean
Description: Symmetry of second derivatives: `fderiv²` is symmetric when the function is C².

Name: WithLp.lean
Description: FDeriv into WithLp spaces.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: FDeriv of power functions.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: FDeriv of products.

Name: RestrictScalars.lean
Description: Scalar restriction for FDeriv: viewing a ℂ-derivative as an ℝ-derivative.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core Fréchet derivative API: `HasFDerivAt`, `fderiv`, composition and basic rules.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: FDeriv of bilinear maps: the Fréchet derivative of a continuous bilinear map.

Name: Comp.lean
Description: Chain rule for FDeriv: `HasFDerivAt.comp`.

Name: Add.lean
Description: FDeriv of sums: `HasFDerivAt.add` — Fréchet derivative of a sum.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: FDeriv of affine maps.

Name: Analytic.lean
Description: FDeriv of analytic functions.

Name: CompCLM.lean
Description: FDeriv of composition with continuous linear maps.

Name: ContinuousMultilinearMap.lean
Description: FDeriv of continuous multilinear maps.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: FDeriv definitions: `HasFDerivAt`, `HasFDerivWithinAt`.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: FDeriv of continuous linear equivalences: an equivalence is its own derivative.

Name: Congr.lean
Description: Congruence lemmas for FDeriv.

Name: Const.lean
Description: FDeriv of constant functions is zero.

Name: ContinuousAlternatingMap.lean
Description: FDeriv of continuous alternating maps.

Name: Conformal
Description: Conformal maps between normed spaces: maps whose derivative at every point is a scalar multiple of an isometry, preserving angles. In ℝ² ≅ ℂ this characterizes holomorphic maps with nonzero derivative. Contains the Cauchy-Riemann equations as the conformality condition in two dimensions. Distinct from isometries (which preserve distances, not just angles).

Name: NormedSpace.lean
Description: Conformal maps in normed spaces.

Name: InnerProduct.lean
Description: Conformal maps in inner product spaces.

Name: BumpFunction
Description: Smooth bump functions: compactly supported C^∞ functions φ : ℝⁿ → ℝ with 0 ≤ φ ≤ 1, equal to 1 on a given compact set and 0 outside a slightly larger set. Used to patch local constructions together (partitions of unity, test functions). Distinct from the Schwartz space (which requires rapid decay but not compact support).

Name: InnerProduct.lean
Description: Bump functions in inner product spaces.

Name: Normed.lean
Description: Bump functions in normed spaces.

Name: SmoothApprox.lean
Description: Smooth approximation via bump functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Smooth bump functions: `SmoothBumpFunction` supported in a ball.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Convolution with bump functions: approximate identity.

Name: FiniteDimension.lean
Description: Bump functions in finite dimensions.

Name: AddTorsor
Description: Calculus in affine spaces: the Fréchet derivative of maps between affine spaces (modeled on normed vector spaces), where the derivative is a linear map between the underlying vector spaces. Accounts for the absence of a distinguished origin. Distinct from FDeriv (which requires a vector space) and Gradient (which requires an inner product).

Name: Coord.lean
Description: Coordinate systems on affine spaces.

Name: AffineMap.lean
Description: Derivatives of affine maps on affine spaces.

Name: ContDiff
Description: Continuously differentiable functions of class C^n: iterated Fréchet differentiability, the C^n typeclass ContDiff 𝕜 n f, and the C^∞ (smooth) case. Contains the chain rule for C^n maps, the C^n implicit and inverse function theorems, and the C^n structure on products and compositions. Distinct from Deriv (one-variable) and FDeriv (first derivative only).

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations preserving C^n: sum, product, composition.

Name: FTaylorSeries.lean
Description: Formal Taylor series for smooth functions: `fTaylorSeries 𝕜 f` and its convergence.

Name: FiniteDimension.lean
Description: C^n functions in finite dimensions.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomial functions are C^∞.

Name: WithLp.lean
Description: C^n functions into WithLp spaces.

Name: RestrictScalars.lean
Description: Scalar restriction for C^n functions.

Name: RCLike.lean
Description: C^n functions over RCLike fields.

Name: FaaDiBruno.lean
Description: Faà di Bruno's formula for iterated derivatives of compositions.

Name: Comp.lean
Description: Chain rule for C^n functions.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Norm bounds for derivatives: bounds on `‖iteratedFDeriv 𝕜 n f x‖` for smooth functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: C^n smooth functions: `ContDiff 𝕜 n f` — iterated Fréchet differentiability; core API.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Convolution of C^n functions.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of C^n functions: `ContDiff.deriv`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions of C^n smoothness: `ContDiff`, `ContDiffOn`, `ContDiffAt` typeclasses.

Name: CPolynomial.lean
Description: Continuously polynomial and C^n functions.

Name: DifferentialForm
Description: Differential forms on smooth manifolds: alternating multilinear forms on tangent spaces. Defines the exterior derivative extDeriv and extDerivWithin and proves basic properties including linearity and the pullback formula extDeriv_pullback. Lays the groundwork for the de Rham complex; closed and exact forms and the generalized Stokes theorem are not yet present in this file. Distinct from LinearAlgebra/ExteriorAlgebra (the algebraic exterior algebra with no manifold structure).

Name: VectorField.lean
Description: Interior product of a vector field with a differential form.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Differential forms on smooth manifolds: k-forms and their wedge product.

Name: Deriv
Description: The one-variable real derivative: deriv f x := the unique a such that (f(x+h) - f(x))/h → a, as a special case of the Fréchet derivative for functions ℝ → E. Contains the standard differentiation rules (product rule, chain rule, L'Hôpital), and mean value theorems. Distinct from FDeriv (multi-variable Fréchet derivative) — Deriv is the scalar specialization.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Derivative of powers: `hasDerivAt_pow` — `d/dx xⁿ = n xⁿ⁻¹`.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product rule for derivatives.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Derivative of polynomial functions.

Name: Mul.lean
Description: Product rule: `HasDerivAt.mul` — the derivative of a product.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Derivative of pi-type functions.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of derivatives.

Name: ZPow.lean
Description: Derivative of integer powers: `hasDerivAt_zpow` for `x^n` with `n : ℤ`.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Derivative of the star map.

Name: Shift.lean
Description: Derivative under a shift: `(f(x + c))' = f'(x + c)`.

Name: Slope.lean
Description: Slope and derivative: `dslope f c` and its relationship to `deriv f`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core derivative API: `HasDerivAt`, `HasDerivWithinAt`, `deriv`, `derivWithin`, and basic rules.

Name: Comp.lean
Description: Chain rule: `HasDerivAt.comp` — the derivative of a composition.

Name: AffineMap.lean
