Description: Derivatives of affine maps: the derivative of an affine map is its linear part.

Name: Abs.lean
Description: Derivative of the absolute value: `|x|' = sign(x)`.

Name: Add.lean
Description: Derivatives of sums: `HasDerivAt.add` and `HasDerivAt.neg`.

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Derivative of linear maps: a continuous linear map is its own derivative.

Name: MeanValue.lean
Description: The mean value theorem and its consequences: for f differentiable on (a, b) and continuous on [a, b], there exists c ∈ (a, b) with f'(c) = (f(b) - f(a)) / (b - a). Contains Rolle's theorem (the special case f(a) = f(b)), Cauchy's generalized MVT, and the corollaries: constant functions have zero derivative, positive derivative implies strict monotonicity, and the bound |f(b) - f(a)| ≤ sup|f'| · |b - a|.

Name: Inverse.lean
Description: Derivative of the inverse function: `HasDerivAt.hasDerivAt_inverse` at invertible points.

Name: CompMul.lean
Description: Derivative of composition with multiplication.

Name: Inv.lean
Description: Derivative of the inverse: `(x⁻¹)' = -x⁻²`.

Name: ContDiffHolder
Description: Functions with C^n regularity and a Hölder-continuous nth derivative: the intermediate regularity class between C^n and C^(n+1). Used in PDE theory where smoothness beyond a certain integer order is quantified by a Hölder exponent α ∈ (0,1]. Distinct from ContDiff (integer regularity only) and FDeriv (only first derivative).

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise C^{n,α} (Hölder-smooth) functions.

Name: ParametricIntervalIntegral.lean
Description: Differentiation under the integral sign for interval integrals: `intervalIntegral.hasFDerivAt_integral_of_dominated_loc_of_deriv`. Specialises the dominated convergence approach to `∫ t in a..b, f x t ∂μ`. Distinct from Calculus/ParametricIntegral (Bochner integral version).

Name: Rademacher.lean
Description: Rademacher's theorem: every locally Lipschitz function `f : ℝⁿ → ℝᵐ` is differentiable Lebesgue-almost everywhere. The proof uses the Lebesgue differentiation theorem and the structure of Lipschitz functions. Distinct from Calculus/MeanValue (pointwise MVT) and MeasureTheory differentiation results.

Name: ParametricIntegral.lean
Description: Differentiation under the integral sign for parameter-dependent Bochner integrals: `hasFDerivAt_integral_of_dominated_loc_of_deriv`. Proves that `x ↦ ∫ t, f x t` is differentiable when the derivative `∂f/∂x` is dominated. Distinct from Calculus/ParametricIntervalIntegral (interval integral version).

Name: MeanValue.lean
Description: Mean value theorem and its variants: the scalar MVT, the vector-valued norm inequality `‖f b - f a‖ ≤ (b - a) * sup ‖f'‖`, and `lipschitzOnWith_of_nnnorm_deriv_le`. Key identifiers: `norm_image_sub_le_of_norm_deriv_le_segment`, `Convex.inner_smul_le_inner_smul_iff`. Distinct from Calculus/Monotone and Complex/MeanValue.

Name: Monotone.lean
Description: Monotonicity criteria: a function is monotone iff its derivative is nonneg, and strictly monotone iff the derivative is positive on a dense set. Proves `StrictMonoOn.deriv_pos` and related results. Distinct from Calculus/MeanValue (MVT) and Calculus/Darboux (IVP for derivatives).

Name: UniformLimitsDeriv.lean
Description: If a sequence of differentiable functions `fₙ → f` pointwise and `fₙ'` converges uniformly, then `f` is differentiable and `f' = lim fₙ'`. Key lemma: `hasFDerivAt_of_tendsTo_uniformlyOnFilter`. Distinct from Calculus/SmoothSeries (series rather than sequences) and Analytic/Constructions.

Name: VectorField.lean
Description: Vector fields on normed spaces and their Lie brackets: `lieBracket 𝕜 V W` for smooth vector fields, the Jacobi identity, and flows. Also includes basic flow/trajectory concepts for autonomous ODEs. Distinct from ODE/PicardLindelof (existence of flows) and Calculus/MeanValue.

Name: Taylor.lean
Description: Taylor's theorem with remainder: for an n-times-differentiable function f, the Taylor polynomial of degree n approximates f with an error term controlled by the (n+1)-th derivative. Contains the Lagrange remainder taylorMeanValue, the Peano remainder, and the general Taylor formula in Banach spaces. Used in numerical analysis, ODE theory, and approximation theory — the magic behind calculators and series expansions.

Name: SmoothSeries.lean
Description: Smoothness of series: if `∑ fₙ` converges uniformly and `∑ fₙ'` also converges uniformly on compact sets, then the sum is differentiable and the derivative is the sum of the derivatives. Extends to C^n functions. Distinct from Calculus/UniformLimitsDeriv (single sequence version) and Analytic/Uniqueness.

Name: TangentCone.lean
Description: Tangent cone `tangentConeAt 𝕜 s x`: the set of limit directions of sequences in `s` approaching `x`. Used as a necessary condition for Lagrange multipliers and in convex analysis. Distinct from Calculus/LagrangeMultipliers (which uses the tangent cone) and Convex/Basic.

Name: LogDerivUniformlyOn.lean
Description: Uniform convergence of logarithmic derivatives of a uniformly convergent sequence of analytic functions: if `fₙ → f` uniformly on compact sets then `logDeriv fₙ → logDeriv f`. Application to Weierstrass products. Distinct from Calculus/LogDeriv (pointwise logarithmic derivative) and Analytic/Uniqueness.

Name: DSlope.lean
Description: Divided slope (divided difference at a point): `dslope f c x = (f x - f c) / (x - c)` for a function on a field, extended by `f'(c)` at `x = c` by continuity. Proves that `dslope f c` is continuous whenever `f` is differentiable at `c`. Distinct from Calculus/Deriv files and Calculus/Slope (slope on intervals).

Name: FormalMultilinearSeries.lean
Description: `FormalMultilinearSeries 𝕜 E F`: a sequence of continuous multilinear maps `E^n → F` forming a formal power series. Defines the partial sums, the `apply` (evaluation at a vector) operation, and norm bounds. Distinct from Analytic/Basic (when the series converges) and Analytic/Composition (composition of such series).

Name: DiffContOnCl.lean
Description: `DiffContOnCl 𝕜 f s`: a function differentiable on an open set `s` and continuous on its closure `closure s`. Develops the algebra of `DiffContOnCl` functions and the maximum modulus principle consequence. Distinct from Calculus/MeanValue (estimates on open sets) and Complex/AbsMax.

Name: Darboux.lean
Description: Darboux's theorem: the derivative of a real-valued differentiable function satisfies the intermediate value property, even if not continuous. Proves `intermediate_value_Icc` for derivatives. Distinct from Calculus/MeanValue (mean value inequality) and Calculus/Monotone (monotonicity criteria).

Name: DerivativeTest.lean
Description: First and second derivative tests for local extrema: `IsLocalMin`/`IsLocalMax` criteria via sign changes of `f'` or positivity/negativity of `f''`. Distinct from Calculus/Monotone (global monotonicity) and Calculus/Darboux (intermediate value for derivatives).

Name: LHopital.lean
Description: L'Hôpital's rule for indeterminate forms `0/0` and `∞/∞`: `deriv_tendsto_nhds` and its variants. Handles one-sided limits and the case where the limit of the ratio of derivatives is `±∞`. Distinct from Calculus/DSlope (divided differences) and Calculus/MeanValue.

Name: LogDeriv.lean
Description: Logarithmic derivative `logDeriv f = deriv f / f`: definition, chain rule `logDeriv (f * g) = logDeriv f + logDeriv g`, and applications to infinite products. Used to count zeros/poles via argument principle. Distinct from Calculus/LogDerivUniformlyOn (uniform convergence version) and SpecialFunctions/ExpDeriv.

Name: LagrangeMultipliers.lean
Description: Lagrange multiplier theorem: necessary conditions for a local extremum of `f` on a level set `{x | g x = c}` — at an extremum the gradients are proportional. Stated in terms of `HasFDerivAt` and linear independence of the constraint gradients. Distinct from Calculus/TangentCone (geometric approach) and Convex/Extrema.

Name: Implicit.lean
Description: Implicit function theorem in Banach spaces: if `F(a, b) = 0` and the partial derivative `∂F/∂y` at `(a,b)` is a linear isomorphism, then `F(x, g(x)) = 0` for a unique continuous `g` near `a`. Distinct from Calculus/ImplicitContDiff (the smooth version) and Analytic/Inverse (analytic version).

Name: ImplicitContDiff.lean
Description: C^n implicit function theorem: if `F` is `C^n` and the hypotheses of the implicit function theorem hold, then the implicitly defined function `g` is also `C^n`. Distinct from Calculus/Implicit (the continuous version) and Calculus/MeanValue.

Name: AbsoluteValue
Description: Absolute values on rings: the AbsoluteValue typeclass, archimedean and non-archimedean absolute values.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Equivalence of absolute values on a field: two absolute values are equivalent when they induce the same topology, or equivalently when one is a positive real power of the other. Provides `AbsoluteValue.Equiv` and the key characterisation theorem. Distinct from the core absolute value definitions in Algebra/Order/AbsoluteValue (which defines the typeclass) and from Ostrowski's theorem (which classifies absolute values on ℚ).

Name: Analytic
Description: Analytic functions: functions representable by convergent power series, with the identity theorem and analytic continuation.

Name: OfScalars.lean
Description: Lifting a scalar power series to a vector-valued one: if a formal power series `p : FormalMultilinearSeries 𝕜 𝕜 𝕜` converges, the corresponding series of scalar multiples of a fixed vector converges to the scalar sum times that vector. Provides `FormalMultilinearSeries.ofScalars`. Distinct from Analytic/Basic and Analytic/Linear.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order of vanishing of an analytic function at a point: `AnalyticAt.order`, the smallest `n` such that the `n`-th power series coefficient is nonzero. Proves that the order is additive under multiplication and gives the local factorisation `f = (· - a)^n * g` with `g` analytic and nonvanishing. Distinct from Analytic/IsolatedZeros and Meromorphic/Order.

Name: IteratedFDeriv.lean
Description: Relationship between the terms of a convergent power series and the iterated Fréchet derivatives of its sum: `HasFPowerSeriesAt.iteratedFDeriv`. Proves that the `n`-th coefficient of a power series equals `iteratedFDeriv 𝕜 n f x / n!`. Distinct from Analytic/Basic (convergence) and Calculus/FormalMultilinearSeries (series operations).

Name: Linear.lean
Description: Continuous linear maps and continuous multilinear maps are analytic: `ContinuousLinearMap.analyticAt`, `ContinuousMultilinearMap.analyticAt`. The power series is the single-term series given by the map itself. Distinct from Analytic/Constructions (operations on analytic functions) and Analytic/Polynomial (polynomial maps).

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomial functions (in the sense of `Polynomial.eval`) are analytic: their power series terminates at the degree. Provides instances `Polynomial.analyticAt` and `Polynomial.analyticOn`. Distinct from Analytic/CPolynomial (continuously polynomial maps on Banach spaces) and Analysis/Polynomial/Basic.

Name: Within.lean
Description: Analyticity within a set: `AnalyticWithinAt 𝕜 f s x` and `AnalyticWithinOn 𝕜 f s`. Develops the theory of power series representations restricted to a subset, including composition and basic operations in the within-set setting. Distinct from Analytic/Basic (global analyticity at a point) and Analytic/Constructions.

Name: WithLp.lean
Description: Analyticity of functions into or from `WithLp p E` spaces (products or `lp` spaces with a renormed topology). Transfers `AnalyticAt` across the `WithLp.equiv` equivalence. Distinct from Analytic/Constructions (general arithmetic) and the `WithLp` files in LinearAlgebra.

Name: RadiusLiminf.lean
Description: Characterisation of the radius of convergence as a liminf: `FormalMultilinearSeries.radius_liminf_eq`. Proves the Cauchy–Hadamard formula `r⁻¹ = limsup ‖pₙ‖^(1/n)` in the multilinear setting. Distinct from Analytic/ConvergenceRadius (general radius theory) and Asymptotics/ExpGrowth.

Name: Uniqueness.lean
Description: Uniqueness theorem for analytic functions: if two functions analytic on a connected open set agree on a set with an accumulation point then they are equal. Provides `AnalyticOn.eq_of_frequently_eq` and related results. Distinct from Analytic/IsolatedZeros (which proves zeros are isolated) and Complex/LocallyUniformLimit.

Name: IsolatedZeros.lean
Description: Isolated zeros of analytic functions: `AnalyticAt.eq_zero_or_eventually_ne_zero` (zeros are isolated unless the function is identically zero). Proves the identity theorem: two analytic functions agreeing on a set with an accumulation point agree everywhere on a connected domain. Distinct from Analytic/Uniqueness (which also treats the identity theorem from a different angle) and Meromorphic/IsolatedZeros.

Name: ChangeOrigin.lean
Description: Re-expansion of a formal power series around a new centre: `FormalMultilinearSeries.changeOrigin` and the proof that a function analytic on a ball remains analytic when the base point is moved within that ball. Key lemma: `HasFPowerSeriesOnBall.changeOrigin`. Distinct from Analytic/Basic (pointwise analyticity) and Analytic/Composition (substitution of series).

Name: Composition.lean
Description: Composition of formal multilinear series: `FormalMultilinearSeries.comp` and the proof that the composition of two analytic functions is analytic. Develops the combinatorial book-keeping for composing power series term-by-term. Distinct from Analytic/ChangeOrigin (re-centering a single series) and Analytic/Constructions (arithmetic operations).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions and results for analytic functions: `HasFPowerSeriesOnBall`, `HasFPowerSeriesAt`, `AnalyticAt`, and `AnalyticOn`. Proves that analytic functions are continuous, infinitely Fréchet-differentiable, and that the power series representation is unique. Distinct from Analytic/Constructions (specific operations) and Analytic/Composition (composition of power series).

Name: Binomial.lean
Description: The binomial series `(1 + x)^s` for a general exponent `s : ℂ`, proved to converge and represent the analytic continuation of the power function for `‖x‖ < 1`. Establishes `hasSum_one_add_pow` and related identities. Distinct from Analytic/Polynomial (polynomial special cases) and SpecialFunctions/Choose (combinatorial binomial coefficients).

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions preserving analyticity: sums, scalar multiples, products, and negation of analytic functions. Proves that continuous linear maps send analytic functions to analytic functions, and provides instances for `AnalyticAt` under ring operations. Distinct from Analytic/Linear (linear maps specifically) and Analytic/Composition (function composition).

Name: CPolynomialDef.lean
Description: Defines `CPolynomialAt 𝕜 f x`: the function `f` is given by a finite formal multilinear series at `x`, meaning it agrees with a polynomial map in a neighbourhood. Provides the bare type definition and immediate structural lemmas. Distinct from Analytic/CPolynomial which develops the full theory.

Name: Inverse.lean
Description: Analytic inverse function theorem: if `f` is analytic at `x` with invertible Fréchet derivative, then `f` has a local analytic inverse. Builds the inverse power series via a contraction mapping argument. Distinct from Calculus/Implicit (implicit function theorem) and Analytic/Composition (general series composition).

Name: ConvergenceRadius.lean
Description: The radius of convergence `FormalMultilinearSeries.radius` of a formal power series, proved to equal the liminf characterisation of the Cauchy–Hadamard formula. Establishes that the series converges absolutely inside the radius and diverges outside. Distinct from Analytic/RadiusLiminf (which gives the limsup/liminf formula more explicitly).

Name: CPolynomial.lean
Description: Properties and API for `CPolynomialAt` and `CPolynomialOn` — functions that are given by a polynomial (finite power series converging on all of the space). Proves that continuously polynomial maps are analytic and that the class is closed under composition and arithmetic operations. Distinct from Analytic/CPolynomialDef which contains only the definition.

Name: AperiodicOrder
Description: Aperiodic order and quasicrystals: the mathematics of ordered structures lacking translational periodicity.

Name: Delone
Description: Delone sets (also Delaunay sets): subsets Λ ⊆ ℝⁿ that are simultaneously uniformly discrete (any ball of radius r contains at most one point of Λ) and relatively dense (every ball of radius R contains at least one point of Λ). The mathematical model for the atomic positions in a quasicrystal. Distinct from lattices (which are periodic) — Delone sets allow aperiodic but still controlled arrangements.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Delone sets: uniformly discrete and relatively dense subsets of ℝⁿ; Delone sets as models for quasicrystals.

Name: Distribution
Description: Distributions (generalized functions): linear functionals on test function spaces, derivatives of distributions, and the Dirac delta.

Name: SchwartzSpace
Description: The Schwartz space 𝒮(ℝⁿ): smooth functions f satisfying ‖xᵅ ∂ᵝf‖_∞ < ∞ for all multi-indices α, β (rapidly decreasing with all derivatives). Equipped with the Fréchet topology from these seminorms. The natural domain for the Fourier transform (which maps 𝒮 to 𝒮). Tempered distributions are the dual 𝒮'. Distinct from BumpFunction (compact support) and Lp spaces (no smoothness).

Name: Fourier.lean
Description: Fourier transform on the Schwartz space.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Derivatives of Schwartz functions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Schwartz space API: `SchwartzMap 𝕜 E F` and its locally convex topology.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of a distribution: `Distribution.support T` as the complement of the largest open set on which `T = 0`. Proves `support (∂T/∂xᵢ) ⊆ support T` and the compactly supported distributions embed into the dual of smooth functions. Distinct from Distribution/Distribution (general theory).

Name: Sobolev.lean
Description: Sobolev spaces `H^s(ℝⁿ)` defined spectrally via the Fourier transform: `(1 + |ξ|²)^(s/2) * f̂ ∈ L²`. Proves `H^s ⊂ H^t` for `s > t` and the Sobolev embedding `H^s ⊂ C^k` for `s > n/2 + k`. Distinct from FunctionalSpaces/SobolevInequality (Gagliardo–Nirenberg-type) and Distribution/FourierSchwartz.

Name: SchwartzSpace.lean
Description: The Schwartz space `𝒮(E, F)` (`SchwartzMap E F`): smooth functions `f : E → F` with `‖x^α ∂^β f(x)‖` bounded for all multi-indices `α, β`. Defines the Fréchet topology via the family of seminorms `SchwartzMap.seminorm`, and proves the space is complete. Distinct from Distribution/TestFunction (compactly supported) and Distribution/TemperateGrowth.

Name: TestFunction.lean
Description: Test functions `𝒟(ℝⁿ)`: smooth compactly supported functions, their inductive limit topology (convergence means uniform convergence of all derivatives supported in a fixed compact set). Proves denseness of test functions in `L^p`. Distinct from Distribution/ContDiffMapSupportedIn (supported in a fixed open set) and Distribution/SchwartzSpace.

Name: TemperedDistribution.lean
Description: Tempered distributions `𝒮'(ℝⁿ)`: continuous linear functionals on the Schwartz space. Proves that `L^p` functions define tempered distributions, and that the Fourier transform extends to an automorphism of `𝒮'`. Distinct from Distribution/Distribution (functionals on test functions) and Distribution/SchwartzSpace.

Name: TemperateGrowth.lean
Description: Functions of temperate growth: `MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable` functions whose growth is bounded polynomially, used to define the action of polynomially growing functions as multipliers on tempered distributions. Distinct from Distribution/SchwartzSpace (decay conditions) and Asymptotics/ExpGrowth.

Name: DerivNotation.lean
Description: Notation file for distributional and weak derivatives: sets up the `∂` operator and related syntax for use in the distribution theory files. Distinct from Distribution/Distribution (the actual derivative definitions) and Distribution/SchwartzSpace.

Name: ContDiffMapSupportedIn.lean
Description: `ContDiffMapSupportedIn 𝕜 n s`: smooth functions supported in the open set `s`, forming a locally convex space. Defines the topology of uniform convergence of all partial derivatives on compact subsets of `s`. Distinct from Distribution/TestFunction (compactly supported smooth functions globally) and Distribution/SchwartzSpace.

Name: AEEqOfIntegralContDiff.lean
Description: If two locally integrable functions `f` and `g` satisfy `∫ φ * f = ∫ φ * g` for all smooth compactly supported test functions `φ`, then `f = g` almost everywhere. Provides the key lemma `ae_eq_of_integral_contDiff_smul_eq` used to identify distributional equality with pointwise a.e. equality. Distinct from Distribution/Distribution (general distributions) and MeasureTheory integration.

Name: FourierSchwartz.lean
Description: The Fourier transform as a continuous linear automorphism of the Schwartz space: `fourierTransformCLM : 𝒮(ℝⁿ) ≃L[ℂ] 𝒮(ℝⁿ)`. Proves that `ℱ` is an isometry on L² when restricted to Schwartz functions. Distinct from Fourier/FourierTransform (L¹ theory) and Distribution/FourierMultiplier.

Name: FourierMultiplier.lean
Description: Fourier multiplier operators on Schwartz space: `fourierMultiplierCLM m` where `m` is a tempered multiplier function, mapping `𝒮(ℝⁿ)` to itself continuously. Distinct from Fourier/FourierTransform (the Fourier transform itself) and Distribution/FourierSchwartz.

Name: Distribution.lean
Description: The type of distributions `Distribution E`: continuous linear functionals `𝒟(ℝⁿ) →L[ℝ] E` on the space of test functions, with the weak-* topology. Defines distributional derivatives `∂ T / ∂ xᵢ` and proves the Leibniz rule. Distinct from Distribution/TemperedDistribution (functionals on Schwartz space) and Distribution/TestFunction.

Name: Fourier
Description: Fourier analysis: the Fourier transform on R^n, the Fourier series on the circle, Parseval's theorem, and the Plancherel formula.

Name: FiniteAbelian
Description: Fourier analysis on finite abelian groups G: the discrete Fourier transform f̂(χ) = Σ_{g∈G} f(g)χ(g), the Plancherel formula ‖f‖² = |G|⁻¹‖f̂‖², and Pontryagin duality Ĝ ≅ G. Used in additive combinatorics (Fourier analytic proofs of Roth's theorem) and in number theory (Dirichlet characters as elements of ℤ/nℤ-hat). Distinct from the continuous Fourier transform on ℝⁿ (Analysis/Fourier).

Name: PontryaginDuality.lean
Description: Pontryagin duality for finite abelian groups.

Name: Orthogonality.lean
Description: Orthogonality relations for characters of finite abelian groups.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation file: defines the `𝓕` symbol for the Fourier transform and related scoped notation used throughout the Fourier folder. Contains no mathematical theorems.

Name: LpSpace.lean
Description: Fourier transform on `Lᵖ` spaces: extension of `ℱ` from `L¹ ∩ L²` to all of `L²` as a unitary operator (Plancherel), and the Hausdorff–Young inequality `‖f̂‖_{p'} ≤ ‖f‖_p` for `1 ≤ p ≤ 2`. Distinct from Fourier/Inversion (pointwise inversion) and Fourier/FourierTransform.

Name: Inversion.lean
Description: Fourier inversion theorem: `f(x) = ∫ f̂(ξ) e^{2πi ξ x} dξ` for `f ∈ L¹ ∩ C(ℝⁿ)` with `f̂ ∈ L¹`. Proves `Real.fourierInversion` and the Plancherel theorem `‖f̂‖₂ = ‖f‖₂`. Distinct from Fourier/PoissonSummation (discrete version) and Fourier/LpSpace.

Name: ZMod.lean
Description: Discrete Fourier transform on `ℤ/nℤ`: the DFT matrix, its unitarity, and the convolution theorem over finite cyclic groups. Distinct from Fourier/AddCircleMulti (continuous torus) and Fourier/FourierTransform (continuous groups).

Name: RiemannLebesgueLemma.lean
Description: Riemann–Lebesgue lemma: `f̂(ξ) → 0` as `|ξ| → ∞` for any `f ∈ L¹(ℝⁿ)`. Proved by density of smooth compactly supported functions in `L¹`. Distinct from Fourier/FourierTransform (definition) and Fourier/LpSpace (Lᵖ theory).

Name: PoissonSummation.lean
Description: Poisson summation formula: `∑_{n ∈ ℤ} f(n) = ∑_{n ∈ ℤ} f̂(n)` for `f` in the Schwartz class. Proved via periodisation and Fourier series. Distinct from Fourier/Inversion (continuous Fourier inversion) and Fourier/ZMod.

Name: BoundedContinuousFunctionChar.lean
Description: Characters of locally compact abelian groups as bounded continuous functions: the evaluation map `BoundedContinuousFunction.eval` for Pontryagin dual characters, and the `L∞` norm of a character is 1. Used in abstract Fourier analysis. Distinct from Fourier/FourierTransform (integral transform) and Complex/Circle.

Name: AddCircleMulti.lean
Description: Fourier series on the product torus `(ℝ/ℤ)^d`: multi-dimensional Fourier coefficients, the Fourier basis `e^{2πi k·x}` indexed by `k : ℤ^d`, and completeness of the trigonometric system in `L²((ℝ/ℤ)^d)`. Distinct from the one-dimensional Fourier series in AddCircle files and Fourier/FourierTransform (transform on ℝⁿ).

Name: AddCircle.lean
Description: Fourier series on the additive circle ℝ / ℤ: the decomposition of L²(ℝ/ℤ) into the orthonormal basis of characters {e^{2πinx}}_{n∈ℤ}. The Fourier coefficients f̂(n) = ∫₀¹ f(x)e^{-2πinx}dx, the Parseval identity ∑|f̂(n)|² = ‖f‖², and pointwise convergence results. The primary Mathlib file for Fourier analysis on compact abelian groups — the foundation of signal processing and music theory.

Name: FourierTransformDeriv.lean
Description: Differentiability of the Fourier transform: if `x^α f(x) ∈ L¹` for all multi-indices `α`, then `f̂ ∈ C^∞`. Concretely, `(∂^α f̂)(ξ) = ℱ[((-2πix)^α f)](ξ)`. Distinct from Fourier/FourierTransform (definition) and Distribution/FourierSchwartz.

Name: FourierTransform.lean
Description: The Fourier transform `fourierIntegral 𝕜 L μ e f x = ∫ y, e (L y x) • f y ∂μ` in a general additive LCA-group setting, and the specialisations `Real.fourierIntegral` and `VectorFourier.fourierIntegral`. Proves linearity, the L¹ → C₀ bound, and continuity. The foundational file for the Fourier folder.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Convolution `f * g = ∫ f(x-y) g(y) dy` and its Fourier transform: `(f * g)^hat = f^hat * g^hat` (convolution theorem). Proves that `L¹ * L¹ ⊆ L¹` and the convolution is associative and commutative. Distinct from Fourier/FourierTransform (the transform itself) and Fourier/RiemannLebesgueLemma.

Name: FunctionalSpaces
Description: Function spaces: Lp spaces, Sobolev spaces, Hardy spaces, and their norms, completeness, and duality.

Name: SobolevInequality.lean
Description: Sobolev inequality (Gagliardo–Nirenberg–Sobolev): functions in `W^{1,p}(ℝⁿ)` with `p < n` belong to `Lᵍ` for `q = np/(n-p)`, with the estimate `‖f‖_q ≤ C ‖∇f‖_p`. Proves `MeasureTheory.MemLp.sobolev_inequality` and the Morrey embedding for `p > n`. Distinct from Distribution/Sobolev (spectral Sobolev spaces) and MeasureTheory/Function/LpSpace.

Name: Complex
Description: Complex analysis: holomorphic functions, the Cauchy integral formula, the residue theorem, and conformal mappings.

Name: UpperHalfPlane
Description: The upper half-plane ℍ = {z ∈ ℂ | Im(z) > 0}: the SL₂(ℝ)-action by Möbius transformations, the hyperbolic metric ds² = (dx² + dy²)/y², and the modular group SL₂(ℤ). Foundation for modular forms (NumberTheory/ModularForms). Distinct from UnitDisc (a biholomorphic copy) — the half-plane model is more natural for modular forms.

Name: MoebiusAction.lean
Description: Möbius transformations on ℍ: the `SL(2,ℝ)` action `γ·z = (az+b)/(cz+d)`.

Name: Metric.lean
Description: Hyperbolic metric on ℍ: the Poincaré metric.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topology of the upper half-plane: open subsets and compactness.

Name: ProperAction.lean
Description: Properness of the `SL(2,ℤ)` action on ℍ.

Name: Measure.lean
Description: Hyperbolic measure on ℍ: `dμ = dx dy / y²`.

Name: Exp.lean
Description: Exponential function on the upper half-plane.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Upper half-plane `ℍ`: `{z : ℂ | 0 < z.im}` as a complex domain.

Name: Manifold.lean
Description: Upper half-plane as a complex manifold.

Name: FunctionsBoundedAtInfty.lean
Description: Functions bounded at infinity on ℍ: decay conditions at the cusp.

Name: ValueDistribution
Description: Nevanlinna theory: the first fundamental theorem of value distribution for meromorphic functions. Defines the Nevanlinna characteristic T(r, f) (characteristic), proximity function m(f, a, r) (proximity), and logarithmic counting function N(f, a, r) (logCounting), and proves T(r, f) = m(r, f) + N(r, f) + O(1) (FirstMainTheorem). An ongoing formalization; the Second Main Theorem, deficiency relations, and Picard's theorem are not yet present. Distinct from Complex/Meromorphic (basic definitions without value-distribution theory).

Name: Proximity
Description: The proximity function m(r, a, f): measures how close a meromorphic function f stays to the value a on the circle |z| = r, defined as m(r, a, f) = (1/2π) ∫ log⁺|1/(f(re^{iθ}) − a)| dθ. Contains proximity, monotonicity, non-negativity, proximity_add_top_le, and proximity_mul_top_le. Together with LogCounting, it forms the Nevanlinna characteristic T(r, f) = m + N via the First Main Theorem. Distinct from LogCounting (the pole-counting component) and ValueDistribution (the combined theory).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Proximity function `m(r, a, f)` in Nevanlinna theory.

Name: LogCounting
Description: The logarithmic counting function N(r, a, f): counts a-points (or poles when a = ∞) of a meromorphic function f in the disc |z| ≤ r, weighted logarithmically to reflect multiplicity. Contains logCounting, monotonicity, non-negativity, logCounting_add_top_le, and logCounting_mul_top_le. A core component of Nevanlinna's first fundamental theorem T(r, f) = m(r, f) + N(r, f) + O(1). Distinct from Proximity (the complementary term in the Nevanlinna characteristic) and ValueDistribution (the combined theory).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Log-counting function `N(r, a, f)` for Nevanlinna theory.

Name: Asymptotic.lean
Description: Asymptotic behaviour of the log-counting function.

Name: FirstMainTheorem.lean
Description: Nevanlinna's first main theorem: `T(r, f) = m(r, f) + N(r, f) + O(1)`.

Name: CharacteristicFunction.lean
Description: Nevanlinna characteristic function `T(r, f)` for meromorphic functions.

Name: UnitDisc
Description: The open unit disc 𝔻 = {z ∈ ℂ | |z| < 1}: Möbius transformations mapping 𝔻 to itself, the Schwarz lemma (holomorphic self-maps of 𝔻 fixing 0 are rotations or contractions), and the Poincaré hyperbolic metric. Distinct from UpperHalfPlane (a biholomorphic model of the same hyperbolic plane, via the Cayley transform).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unit disk `𝔻`: the open unit disk in ℂ, its topology and function spaces.

Name: Harmonic
Description: Harmonic functions in the complex plane: real-valued functions satisfying Δu = 0, arising as real and imaginary parts of holomorphic functions. Contains the mean value property, the maximum principle, and Poisson's formula for the disc. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Harmonic (spherical harmonics in higher dimensions) and from holomorphic functions (complex-valued).

Name: MeanValue.lean
Description: Mean value property for harmonic functions.

Name: Poisson.lean
Description: Poisson integral formula for harmonic functions on the disk.

Name: Analytic.lean
Description: Analytic functions are harmonic: real and imaginary parts of analytic functions satisfy Δf = 0.

Name: Liouville.lean
Description: Liouville's theorem for harmonic functions: bounded harmonic functions on ℝⁿ are constant.

Name: Polynomial
Description: Analytic results on complex polynomials: the fundamental theorem of algebra (every non-constant polynomial has a root in ℂ), Rouché's theorem (counting zeros via winding numbers), and factorization of complex polynomials into linear factors. Distinct from Algebra/Polynomial/Eval (algebraic evaluation) — this folder uses analytic methods.

Name: UnitTrinomial.lean
Description: Unit trinomials: trinomials `z^n + z^k + 1` and their root properties.

Name: GaussLucas.lean
Description: Gauss–Lucas theorem: roots of `f'` lie in the convex hull of roots of `f`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The fundamental theorem of algebra: every non-constant polynomial with complex coefficients has at least one complex root. The Mathlib proof proceeds analytically (the modulus |p(z)| attains its minimum on ℂ, and that minimum must be zero). Contains the corollary IsAlgClosed ℂ (the complex numbers are algebraically closed) and the factorization of complex polynomials into linear factors over ℂ.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order structure on ℂ: ℂ does not admit a total order compatible with its field structure, but this file treats the partial order on the self-adjoint elements (real parts) and positivity conditions. Distinct from Complex/Positivity (positivity tactic extension) and RCLike/Basic.

Name: OperatorNorm.lean
Description: Operator norms of ℂ-linear and ℝ-linear maps on ℂ: `‖smulRight 1 z‖ = ‖z‖`, the operator norm of complex-linear maps, and the relationship between `ℝ`-linear and `ℂ`-linear norm. Distinct from Complex/Basic (field norm) and InnerProductSpace/Adjoint.

Name: Periodic.lean
Description: Periodicity of complex-valued functions: functions periodic with respect to a lattice in ℂ, e.g., doubly periodic (elliptic) functions or the periodicity of `exp`. Distinct from Complex/Exponential (periodicity of `exp` specifically) and Complex/Circle.

Name: Poisson.lean
Description: Poisson integral formula: representation of a harmonic function on the disk by its boundary values via the Poisson kernel `P_r(θ) = (1 - r²)/(1 - 2r cos θ + r²)`. Proves `u(re^{iθ}) = ∫ P_r(θ-t) u(e^{it}) dt / 2π`. Distinct from Complex/MeanValue (mean value property) and Complex/CauchyIntegral.

Name: PhragmenLindelof.lean
Description: Phragmén–Lindelöf principle: maximum modulus theorem for unbounded regions (strips, sectors, angles). Generalises `AbsMax` to domains where the boundary is not compact, using polynomial growth bounds. Key lemma: `PhragmenLindelof.horizontal_strip`. Distinct from Complex/AbsMax and Complex/Hadamard.

Name: OpenMapping.lean
Description: Open mapping theorem: a nonconstant holomorphic function on a connected open set is an open map. Proves `Complex.isOpen_map_of_holomorphicOn`. Corollary: the maximum modulus principle. Distinct from Complex/AbsMax (consequence) and Complex/Liouville.

Name: Liouville.lean
Description: Liouville's theorem: a bounded entire function is constant. Proved via the Cauchy integral formula giving `|f'(z)| ≤ M/R` for all `R`. Application: the fundamental theorem of algebra (every non-constant complex polynomial has a root). Distinct from Complex/AbsMax and Complex/OpenMapping.

Name: JensenFormula.lean
Description: Jensen's formula: for `f` holomorphic on a disk `D(0, R)` with zeros `a₁, …, aₙ` counted with multiplicity, `log |f(0)| = ∑ log(|aᵢ|/R) + (1/2π) ∫₀²π log |f(Re^{iθ})| dθ`. Application to counting zeros. Distinct from Complex/CauchyIntegral and Complex/Liouville.

Name: LocallyUniformLimit.lean
Description: Locally uniform limits of holomorphic functions are holomorphic: `Complex.locallyUniformLimit_holomorphic`. Proves Weierstrass's theorem on sequences of holomorphic functions, and that the derivatives also converge locally uniformly. Distinct from Calculus/UniformLimitsDeriv (Banach-space version) and Analytic/Uniqueness.

Name: Norm.lean
Description: Properties of the norm on ℂ: `Complex.normSq_eq_abs`, estimates for `‖z + w‖`, `‖z * w‖ = ‖z‖ * ‖w‖`, and bounds used in analysis. Provides the `NormedField ℂ` structure details. Distinct from Complex/Basic (which imports this) and Complex/ExponentialBounds.

Name: MeanValue.lean
Description: Mean value property for holomorphic functions: `f(z₀) = (1/2π) ∫₀²π f(z₀ + re^{iθ}) dθ` (the value at the centre equals the average on a circle). Equivalently, the arithmetic mean of values on a circle. Distinct from Calculus/MeanValue (MVT for derivatives) and Complex/Poisson.

Name: SummableUniformlyOn.lean
Description: Summability of series of analytic functions uniformly on compact subsets of an open domain: `UniformlySummableOn` and Weierstrass M-test for holomorphic series. Connects to `LocallyUniformLimit` results. Distinct from Complex/LocallyUniformLimit (limits) and Calculus/SmoothSeries.

Name: SqrtDeriv.lean
Description: Derivative of the complex square root: `HasDerivAt Complex.sqrt z (1 / (2 * sqrt z))` for `z ≠ 0`. Analyticity of the principal square root on `ℂ \ (-∞, 0]`. Distinct from RCLike/Sqrt (square root in the RCLike setting) and SpecialFunctions/Sqrt (real sqrt).

Name: TaylorSeries.lean
Description: Taylor series of holomorphic functions: `Complex.taylorSeries f z₀`, convergence on the largest disk contained in the domain, and the coefficients `aₙ = f^(n)(z₀) / n!`. Connects the analytic power series representation to iterated complex derivatives. Distinct from Analytic/IteratedFDeriv and Complex/CauchyIntegral.

Name: Trigonometric.lean
Description: Complex trigonometric functions `sin`, `cos`, `tan` defined via `exp`: periodicity, addition formulas, zeros of `sin` and `cos`, and `sin z = 0 ↔ z ∈ πℤ`. Distinct from Complex/Exponential (exp and Euler's formula) and SpecialFunctions files for real trigonometry.

Name: Tietze.lean
Description: Tietze extension theorem for complex-valued functions: a continuous `ℂ`-valued function on a closed subset of a normal topological space extends to a continuous function on the whole space. Follows from the real version applied to real and imaginary parts. Distinct from Complex/Basic and the general topology Tietze theorem.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectrum of elements in a complex Banach algebra: the Gelfand–Mazur theorem (`spectrum.eq_singleton_of_isUnit_sub_iff`), the spectrum is nonempty and compact, and the spectral radius formula. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Spectrum (C*-algebra spectral theory) and Real/Spectrum (real Banach algebras).

Name: RealDeriv.lean
Description: ℝ-differentiability vs ℂ-differentiability: the Cauchy–Riemann equations, `HasDerivAt` for complex-valued functions as `ℝ`-linear maps, and the equivalence between `ℂ`-differentiability and `ℝ`-differentiability with the CR condition. Distinct from Complex/Conformal and InnerProductSpace/Calculus.

Name: Positivity.lean
Description: Extension of Lean's `positivity` tactic for complex-valued expressions: lemmas asserting `0 ≤ Complex.normSq z`, `0 < Complex.abs z` for `z ≠ 0`, etc. Distinct from Complex/Order (structural order on ℂ) and Complex/Norm.

Name: ReImTopology.lean
Description: Topological properties of `re` and `im` as maps `ℂ → ℝ`: the product topology on `ℝ × ℝ` coincides with the topology on ℂ, `re` and `im` are continuous open surjections. Proves that open sets in ℂ are determined by their real and imaginary projections. Distinct from Complex/Basic (norm/field structure).

Name: Schwarz.lean
Description: Schwarz lemma: a holomorphic map `f : D(0,1) → D(0,1)` with `f(0) = 0` satisfies `|f(z)| ≤ |z|` and `|f'(0)| ≤ 1`; equality forces `f` to be a rotation. Proves `Complex.schwarz_le` and the rigidity statement. Distinct from Complex/AbsMax (general max modulus) and Complex/HalfPlane.

Name: RemovableSingularity.lean
Description: Riemann's removable singularity theorem: if `f` is holomorphic on a punctured neighbourhood of `z₀` and bounded near `z₀`, then `z₀` is a removable singularity (f extends to a holomorphic function at `z₀`). Key: `Complex.analyticAt_of_boundedNhds`. Distinct from Meromorphic/Basic and Complex/Liouville.

Name: CanonicalDecomposition.lean
Description: Canonical decomposition (Blaschke product factors) for meromorphic functions on a disk: introduces canonical factors that replace `(· - u)` by factors taking values of norm one on the boundary circle. Follows Lang's *Introduction to Complex Hyperbolic Spaces*, p. 160. TODO: formulate the full canonical decomposition.

Name: BorelCaratheodory.lean
Description: Borel–Carathéodory theorem: if `f` is analytic on `D(0, R)` and `Re f ≤ M` on `D(0, R)`, then `|f z| ≤ (2r/(R-r))|f 0| + (2R/(R-r))M` on `D(0, r)`. Provides a quantitative bound on `|f|` from a bound on `Re f`. Distinct from Complex/AbsMax and Complex/PhragmenLindelof.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality results for ℂ: `Cardinal.mk_complex` showing `#ℂ = 𝔠` (the cardinality of the continuum), via the bijection `ℂ ≃ ℝ × ℝ`. Distinct from similar cardinality files in SetTheory and from Complex/Basic.

Name: Circle.lean
Description: The unit circle `circle = {z : ℂ | ‖z‖ = 1}` as a compact topological group, integration on `circle` via the arc-length measure, and the relationship between `circle` and `S¹`. Distinct from Complex/CoveringMap (exp as a covering) and Fourier/BoundedContinuousFunctionChar.

Name: CauchyIntegral.lean
Description: Cauchy's integral theorem and Cauchy's integral formula: for a holomorphic function `f` on a simply connected domain, `∮ f dz = 0`, and `f(w) = (1/2πi) ∮ f(z)/(z-w) dz`. Key identifiers: `Complex.integral_eq_zero_of_holomorphicOn`, `Complex.cauchyIntegralFormula`. Distinct from Complex/HasPrimitives and Complex/TaylorSeries.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic analytic structure of ℂ: `Complex.normedField`, `Complex.completeSpace`, and the relationship between `Complex.abs` (the norm) and `Complex.normSq`. Proves ℂ is a complete normed field and establishes the re/im projections as continuous linear maps. The foundational file for the Complex folder.

Name: AbsMax.lean
Description: Maximum modulus principle: a holomorphic function on a connected open set that attains its maximum modulus in the interior is constant. Proves `Complex.abs_le_abs_of_holomorphicOn` and the strict version. Distinct from Complex/PhragmenLindelof (unbounded domains) and Complex/Liouville.

Name: AbelLimit.lean
Description: Abel's limit theorem: if a power series `∑ aₙ xⁿ` converges at `x = 1`, then its sum is the limit of the partial sums, and the power series has the same limit as `x → 1⁻` within a Stolz angle. Distinct from Analytic/ConvergenceRadius (convergence inside the disk) and SpecificLimits/Normed.

Name: Angle.lean
Description: The angle between two complex numbers as vectors in ℝ²: `Complex.angle z w`, defined via the inner product. Proves `Complex.angle_add_arg_eq_pi_iff` and related geometric identities. Distinct from Complex/Arg (the principal argument of a single complex number) and InnerProductSpace/TwoDim.

Name: Asymptotics.lean
Description: Asymptotic estimates for complex analytic functions: O/o bounds as `|z| → ∞` or near singularities, and estimates derived from the Phragmén–Lindelöf principle. Connects `IsBigO` machinery to the complex analysis setting. Distinct from Asymptotics/SpecificAsymptotics (real setting) and Complex/PhragmenLindelof.

Name: Arg.lean
Description: The principal argument `Complex.arg : ℂ → ℝ` taking values in `(-π, π]`, its continuity properties (discontinuous on the negative real axis), and `Complex.abs_mul_exp_arg_mul_I`. Distinct from Complex/Angle (angle between two vectors) and Complex/Exponential (Euler's formula).

Name: HasPrimitives.lean
Description: A holomorphic function on a simply connected domain has a primitive (antiderivative): `Complex.HasPrimitives`. Proves existence via integration and the converse Cauchy theorem criterion. Distinct from Complex/CauchyIntegral (Cauchy theorem) and Complex/Convex (primitives on convex sets).

Name: HalfPlane.lean
Description: The upper half-plane `ℍ = {z : ℂ | 0 < Im z}`: its complex-analytic structure, the Möbius transformation between `ℍ` and the unit disk, and basic holomorphic maps on `ℍ`. Distinct from Complex/Schwarz (maps on the unit disk) and Complex/Conformal.

Name: IntegerCompl.lean
Description: `Complex.integerComplement`: the complement `ℂ \ ℤ` of the integers in the complex plane. Basic lemmas about this open set, including that the upper half-plane embeds into the integer complement.

Name: Isometry.lean
Description: Isometries of ℂ: characterisation of surjective isometries of ℂ as compositions of rotations, reflections, and translations. Proves `Complex.isometry_iff` and related results about complex-linear and antilinear isometries. Distinct from Complex/Conformal (angle-preserving) and InnerProductSpace for general Hilbert spaces.

Name: IsIntegral.lean
Description: Algebraic integrality in ℂ: complex numbers integral over a subring (satisfying a monic polynomial equation). Proves that every element of ℂ algebraic over ℚ is integral over ℤ, and develops `IsIntegral` instances for ℂ. Distinct from Complex/Spectrum (operator spectrum) and RingTheory integral closure files.

Name: Hadamard.lean
Description: Hadamard's three-lines theorem: if `f` is holomorphic on the strip `{0 < Re z < 1}`, continuous on its closure, and bounded by `M₀` on `Re z = 0` and `M₁` on `Re z = 1`, then `|f(x+iy)| ≤ M₀^(1-x) * M₁^x` on the strip. Key to interpolation theory. Distinct from Complex/PhragmenLindelof and Complex/AbsMax.

Name: Convex.lean
Description: Convexity results in ℂ: a convex open subset of ℂ is simply connected, and holomorphic functions on convex sets have primitives. Connects to `Complex/HasPrimitives`. Distinct from Convex/Basic (general convex analysis) and Complex/CauchyIntegral.

Name: Conformal.lean
Description: Conformal maps in ℂ: a ℂ-differentiable map with nonzero derivative is conformal (angle-preserving). Proves `conformalAt_iff_isHolomorphicAt_or_isAntiholomorphicAt` and the characterisation via the Cauchy–Riemann equations. Distinct from Complex/RealDeriv (ℝ vs ℂ differentiability) and InnerProductSpace/ConformalLinearMap.

Name: CoveringMap.lean
Description: The complex exponential `exp : ℂ → ℂˣ` as a covering map: `Complex.exp_isCoveringMap`, its fibres are the cosets of `2πiℤ`, and the deck transformations are translations by `2πin`. Distinct from Complex/Circle (unit circle) and Complex/Exponential (basic exp properties).

Name: ExponentialBounds.lean
Description: Norm bounds on the complex exponential: `‖exp z‖ = exp (re z)`, estimates for `‖exp z - 1‖`, and related quantitative bounds used in analytic number theory and PDE estimates. Distinct from Complex/Exponential (structural properties) and Asymptotics/ExpGrowth.

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: The complex exponential function: Euler's formula `exp (x + iy) = exp x * (cos y + i * sin y)`, periodicity `exp (z + 2πi) = exp z`, and the relationship between `Complex.exp` and trigonometric functions. Distinct from Complex/CoveringMap (topological properties) and SpecialFunctions/Exp (real exp).

Name: Convex
Description: Convex analysis: convex sets and functions in normed spaces, supporting hyperplanes, and the Hahn-Banach geometric form.

Name: SpecificFunctions
Description: Convexity of specific analytic functions: convexity of exp, concavity of log, convexity of x ↦ xᵖ for p ≥ 1, and the power mean inequality as a consequence. Used throughout Analysis for estimates. Distinct from Convex/Strict (structural convexity conditions) and Analysis/MeanInequalities (derived inequalities).

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Convexity of power functions `x^p` for `p ≥ 1`.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Convexity via derivatives: a function with nonneg second derivative is convex.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Convexity of specific functions: `log`, `exp`, power functions.

Name: Strict
Description: Strictly convex functions and sets: functions where equality in f((1-t)x + ty) ≤ (1-t)f(x) + tf(y) forces x = y, and sets where the open segment lies strictly inside. Contains the uniqueness of minimizers for strictly convex functions and strict convexity of norms. Distinct from Convex/SpecificFunctions (examples) and Convex/Cone (cone structure).

Name: Extreme.lean
Description: Extreme points of strictly convex sets.

Name: Cone
Description: Convex cones: subsets C of a vector space closed under positive scaling (λ > 0, x ∈ C ⟹ λx ∈ C) and addition. The dual cone C* = {y | ∀ x ∈ C, ⟨x,y⟩ ≥ 0}, and the Farkas lemma (a linear system has a solution in C iff no contradiction exists in C*). Distinct from Convex/Strict (strict convexity of functions, not cone structure).

Name: InnerDual.lean
Description: Inner dual cone in inner product spaces.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation for the Analysis/Convex/Cone folder.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor product of convex cones.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extending linear maps using cones: Hahn-Banach via cones.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Convex cones in a vector space: `ConvexCone 𝕜 E`, closedness, and basic operations.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Closure of convex cones.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual cone: `s* = {y | ∀ x ∈ s, ⟪y, x⟫ ≥ 0}`.

Name: SimplicialComplex
Description: Geometric simplicial complexes: finite collections of simplices in Euclidean space closed under taking faces, with the geometric realization topology. Distinct from AlgebraicTopology/SimplicialComplex (combinatorial simplicial complexes, not necessarily embedded in ℝⁿ) and from Analysis/Convex/Cone (cones, not simplices).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simplicial complexes as geometric realisations of abstract simplicial complexes.

Name: AffineIndependentUnion.lean
Description: Affinely independent unions for simplicial complexes.

Name: Quasiconvex.lean
Description: `QuasiconvexOn 𝕜 s f`: the sublevel sets `{x ∈ s | f x ≤ c}` are convex for every `c`. Proves that convex functions are quasiconvex, and studies basic operations (max of quasiconvex is quasiconvex). Distinct from Convex/Function (strict convexity) and Convex/Star.

Name: Piecewise.lean
Description: Piecewise-defined convex functions: conditions under which a function defined differently on two regions is globally convex. Includes the gluing lemma for convex functions along a common boundary. Distinct from Convex/Function (global convexity) and Convex/Slope.

Name: PathConnected.lean
Description: Convex sets are path-connected: the straight-line path `t ↦ (1-t) * x + t * y` lies in a convex set. Proves `Convex.pathConnectedSpace`. Distinct from Convex/Contractible (contractibility, a stronger statement) and Convex/Topology.

Name: Segment.lean
Description: Segments `segment 𝕜 x y = {z | ∃ t ∈ [0,1], z = (1-t) • x + t • y}` and `openSegment 𝕜 x y` (strict interior). Proves membership criteria, that segments are connected, and the interaction with convex sets. Distinct from Convex/Between (betweenness relation) and Convex/Combination.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation overview for the Analysis/Convex folder: describes the organisational structure of the folder, lists main files and their dependencies, and provides guidance for contributors. This is a markdown documentation file, not a Lean source file.

Name: Radon.lean
Description: Radon's theorem: any `d+2` points in `ℝᵈ` can be partitioned into two sets whose convex hulls intersect. Proved using affine dependence. Distinct from Convex/Caratheodory (another combinatorial convexity theorem) and Convex/Hull.

Name: PartitionOfUnity.lean
Description: Convex partitions of unity: a partition of unity `{φᵢ}` subordinate to an open cover, with each `φᵢ` taking values in `[0,1]`. Used to construct convex combinations of local objects. Distinct from the general Topology/UrysohnLemma and Convex/Combination.

Name: Measure.lean
Description: Measure-theoretic aspects of convex sets: convex sets are Lebesgue-measurable, a convex function is measurable, and the Lebesgue measure of convex bodies is positive when the interior is nonempty. Distinct from MeasureTheory/Measure and Convex/Topology (pure topology).

Name: LinearIsometry.lean
Description: Convexity is preserved by linear isometries and their inverses: `LinearIsometryEquiv.convexOn_preimage`. Used to transfer convex structure between isometric spaces. Distinct from Convex/ContinuousLinearEquiv (general continuous isomorphisms) and Convex/Basic.

Name: KreinMilman.lean
Description: Krein–Milman theorem: every nonempty compact convex subset of a locally convex space is the closed convex hull of its extreme points. Proves `IsCompact.convexHull_extremePoints`. Distinct from Convex/Extreme (definition) and LocallyConvex/Separation (used in proof).

Name: NNReal.lean
Description: Convexity in `ℝ≥0`: `NNReal.inner_le_nnorm_mul_nnorm` and convex function results specialised to the nonnegative reals. Distinct from Convex/Mul (general multiplication convexity) and NNReal algebra files.

Name: Mul.lean
Description: Convexity of multiplication-related functions: `x ↦ x * y` for fixed `y ≥ 0` is convex, log-convexity, and the convexity of `x ↦ x^p` for `p ≥ 1`. Distinct from Convex/Function (general convex function API) and Convex/NNReal.

Name: MetricSpace.lean
Description: Metric structure of convex sets: the metric projection `Metric.proj` onto a closed convex set is well-defined (unique nearest point), nonexpansive, and characterised by `inner_sub_proj_le`. Distinct from Convex/Topology (general topology) and InnerProductSpace/Convex.

Name: Side.lean
Description: Open and closed half-spaces defined by a continuous linear functional: `LinearMap.openHalfspace`, the separation of a point from a convex set by a hyperplane. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Separation (geometric Hahn–Banach) and Convex/StoneSeparation.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topological properties of convex sets: `Convex.interior_nonempty`, the closure of a convex set is convex, the interior is convex, and a convex set is connected. Also includes `Convex.is_path_connected` and the topological characterisation of the intrinsic interior. Distinct from Convex/Contractible and Convex/PathConnected.

Name: Strong.lean
Description: Strongly convex functions: `StrongConvexOn 𝕜 m s f` requiring `f(tx+(1-t)y) ≤ tf(x)+(1-t)f(y) - m*t*(1-t)*‖x-y‖²/2`. Proves strongly convex functions have a unique minimiser and characterises them via the gradient condition. Distinct from Convex/Strict (strict convexity) and Convex/Function.

Name: StrictConvexSpace.lean
Description: `StrictConvexSpace 𝕜 E`: a normed space where every point on the unit sphere is an extreme point of the unit ball, equivalently `‖t • x + (1-t) • y‖ < 1` whenever `x ≠ y` are unit vectors and `0 < t < 1`. Provides instances for `ℝ`, `ℂ`, inner product spaces, and `Lᵖ` for `1 < p < ∞`. Distinct from Convex/Uniform (uniform convexity) and Convex/StrictConvexBetween.

Name: Visible.lean
Description: Visible sets: `IsVisible s x` when `s` is star-shaped at `x` with respect to some interior point, i.e., the line segment from `x` to any point of `s` intersects `s` only as intended. Used in h-principle and ample set theory. Distinct from Convex/Star (star-shaped sets) and Convex/AmpleSet.

Name: Uniform.lean
Description: `UniformConvexSpace E`: a normed space where for each `ε > 0` there exists `δ > 0` such that `‖x - y‖ ≥ ε` forces `‖(x+y)/2‖ ≤ 1 - δ`. Proves uniform convexity implies strict convexity and reflexivity. Provides instances for `Lᵖ` spaces. Distinct from Convex/StrictConvexSpace.

Name: TotallyBounded.lean
Description: Total boundedness of convex sets: a convex subset of a normed space that is contained in a ball is totally bounded; the convex hull of a totally bounded set is totally bounded. Distinct from Convex/Body (compact convex bodies) and Convex/Topology.

Name: StrictConvexBetween.lean
Description: Betweenness in strictly convex spaces: in a strictly convex normed space, `‖x + y‖ = ‖x‖ + ‖y‖` iff `x` and `y` are positively proportional. Characterises collinearity via the norm. Distinct from Convex/StrictConvexSpace (the space axiom) and Convex/Between.

Name: StdSimplex.lean
Description: The standard simplex `stdSimplex 𝕜 ι = {f : ι → 𝕜 | ∑ f = 1 ∧ ∀ i, 0 ≤ f i}` as a compact convex set. Proves `stdSimplex ↔ Finset.centerMass` representation and the face structure. Distinct from Convex/Combination (general convex combinations) and Convex/Caratheodory.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star-shaped sets: `StarConvex 𝕜 x s` (the set is star-shaped at `x` — every segment from `x` to a point of `s` lies in `s`). Proves convex implies star-shaped, operations on star-shaped sets, and the star-shaped interior. Distinct from Convex/Basic (convexity) and Convex/Visible.

Name: Slope.lean
Description: Slope monotonicity for convex functions on ℝ: `ConvexOn.slope_mono` — the slope `(f(b)-f(a))/(b-a)` is increasing in both `a` and `b`. The characterisation of convexity on ℝ via slope monotonicity. Distinct from Calculus/DSlope (divided slope) and Convex/Deriv.

Name: StrictCombination.lean
Description: Strict convex combinations: a point in the interior of a convex hull lies in a strict convex combination of vertices. Used for the algebraic interior and strict separation results. Distinct from Convex/Combination (general combinations) and Convex/Intrinsic.

Name: Strict.lean
Description: `StrictConvexOn 𝕜 s f`: strict inequality in the convexity condition for interior points. Proves that strictly convex functions have a unique minimiser on a convex set, and that strict convexity is preserved under sums and positive multiples. Distinct from Convex/Function (non-strict convexity) and Convex/StrictConvexSpace.

Name: StoneSeparation.lean
Description: Stone separation theorem: if two disjoint convex sets can be separated by a convex set in every two-point subspace, they can be separated globally. A combinatorial precursor to the Hahn–Banach separation theorem. Distinct from LocallyConvex/Separation and Convex/Side.

Name: Join.lean
Description: Join of two convex sets: `ConvexJoin k s t` — the union of all segments connecting a point of `s` to a point of `t`. Proves `ConvexJoin = convexHull (s ∪ t)` under convexity assumptions. Distinct from Convex/Hull (convex hull of a single set) and Convex/Segment.

Name: Continuous.lean
Description: Continuity of convex functions on normed spaces: a convex function on a finite-dimensional normed space is locally Lipschitz, hence continuous, in the interior of its domain. Proves `ConvexOn.continuousOn_interior`. Distinct from Convex/Function (algebraic properties) and Convex/Deriv (differentiability).

Name: Combination.lean
Description: Finite convex combinations: `Finset.centerMass` as the convex combination `∑ wᵢ xᵢ` with `∑ wᵢ = 1` and `wᵢ ≥ 0`, membership of `centerMass` in the convex hull, and the centerMass ∈ convexHull lemma. Distinct from Convex/Hull (the set-theoretic hull) and Convex/StrictCombination.

Name: Caratheodory.lean
Description: Carathéodory's theorem: every point in the convex hull of `s ⊆ ℝⁿ` lies in the convex hull of at most `n+1` points of `s`. Proved by reducing the number of affinely dependent points. Distinct from Convex/Hull (general convex hull) and Convex/Independent.

Name: Deriv.lean
Description: Differentiability of convex functions: one-sided directional derivatives exist everywhere, the subdifferential, and if a convex function is differentiable then the derivative is monotone. Proves `ConvexOn.inner_smul_le_inner_smul`. Distinct from Convex/Continuous (continuity) and Convex/Slope (slope monotonicity on ℝ).

Name: Contractible.lean
Description: Convex subsets of a topological vector space are contractible: the straight-line homotopy `H(x, t) = (1-t) • x + t • x₀` contracts the set to any interior point `x₀`. Provides `Convex.contractibleSpace`. Distinct from Convex/PathConnected (path connectivity) and Convex/Topology.

Name: ContinuousLinearEquiv.lean
Description: Convexity is preserved under continuous linear equivalences: `ContinuousLinearEquiv.convexOn_preimage` and the image of a convex set under an invertible continuous linear map is convex. Distinct from Convex/LinearIsometry (isometries preserving convexity) and Convex/Basic.

Name: Body.lean
Description: Convex bodies: `ConvexBody E` — nonempty compact convex subsets of a real normed space, forming a metric space under the Hausdorff distance. Proves the Hausdorff metric and basic operations (Minkowski sum, dilation). Distinct from Convex/Basic (abstract convex sets) and Convex/Topology.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core definitions: `Convex 𝕜 s` (a set closed under convex combinations), `ConvexOn 𝕜 s f` (a convex function), and their immediate closure properties (intersection, image under affine maps). The foundational file for the Convex folder; most other files import this.

Name: Approximation.lean
Description: Approximation of convex functions: uniform approximation by smooth convex functions, and density of smooth convex functions in the space of convex functions on compact sets. Distinct from Convex/Function (properties of convex functions) and SpecialFunctions/Bernstein (polynomial approximation).

Name: AmpleSet.lean
Description: Ample sets: sets `s` in a real vector space such that for every `x ∈ s` and every half-space `H`, the convex hull of `s ∩ H` has `x` in its interior. Used in h-principle and flexibility results for differential relations. Distinct from Convex/Basic (standard convex sets) and Convex/Hull.

Name: Birkhoff.lean
Description: Birkhoff's theorem: every doubly stochastic matrix is a convex combination of permutation matrices (vertices of the Birkhoff polytope). Proved using Hall's marriage theorem. Distinct from Convex/DoublyStochasticMatrix (the convex structure) and Convex/KreinMilman (extreme point theory).

Name: BetweenList.lean
Description: Betweenness for ordered lists of points: `PointsWithinSegment`, the condition that a list of points lies in order along a line segment. Used for interval geometry and affine combinatorics. Distinct from Convex/Between (pairwise betweenness) and Convex/Combination.

Name: Between.lean
Description: Betweenness as an affine-geometric relation: `Wbtw R x y z` (`y` is weakly between `x` and `z` on the line), its characterisation via `∃ t ∈ [0,1], y = t • x + (1-t) • z`, and basic transitivity/collinearity lemmas. Distinct from Convex/Segment (the segment itself) and Convex/BetweenList.

Name: DoublyStochasticMatrix.lean
Description: Doubly stochastic matrices: `doublyStochastic n` as a convex polytope, its vertices are permutation matrices, and basic operations (Kronecker product, convex combination). Used in Birkhoff's theorem. Distinct from Convex/Birkhoff (the vertex theorem) and Analysis/Matrix.

Name: Independent.lean
Description: Affinely independent sets: `AffineIndependent k p`, no point is an affine combination of the others, and characterisation via the injectivity of the affine span map. Distinct from Convex/Combination (convex, not affine, combinations) and LinearAlgebra independence files.

Name: Hull.lean
Description: `convexHull 𝕜 s`: the smallest convex set containing `s`, its characterisation as finite convex combinations, and the closure/interior of the convex hull. Key: `convexHull_eq` (explicit description as centerMass). Distinct from Convex/Combination (finite combinations) and Convex/Caratheodory.

Name: GaugeRescale.lean
Description: Rescaling and renormalisation via the gauge functional: using `gauge K` to rescale a convex set `K` to the unit ball, and norm-equivalence results. Applied in locally convex space theory. Distinct from Convex/Gauge (the gauge itself) and LocallyConvex/WithSeminorms.

Name: Jensen.lean
Description: Jensen's inequality for finite convex combinations: `ConvexOn.smul_le_sum` and the general form `f(∑ wᵢ xᵢ) ≤ ∑ wᵢ f(xᵢ)` for probability weights. Distinct from Convex/Integral (measure-theoretic Jensen) and Convex/Function.

Name: Intrinsic.lean
Description: Intrinsic interior and relative interior: `intrinsicInterior k s` (the interior of `s` within its affine span), `intrinsicFrontier`, and `intrinsicClosure`. Proves that the intrinsic interior of a convex set is convex and nonempty if `s` has nonempty interior in its affine span. Distinct from Convex/Topology (standard topological interior).

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Jensen's inequality for integrals: if `f` is convex and `φ` is integrable, then `f(∫ φ dμ) ≤ ∫ f ∘ φ dμ` for a probability measure `μ`. Also the integral form of the centroid belonging to the convex hull. Distinct from Convex/Jensen (finite sum version) and MeasureTheory integration.

Name: Gauge.lean
Description: Minkowski gauge functional `gauge K x = sInf {r > 0 | x ∈ r • K}`: convexity for convex `K`, the gauge of a balanced convex absorbing set is a seminorm, and `gauge_le_one_iff`. Distinct from Convex/EGauge (extended real-valued version) and LocallyConvex/Basic.

Name: Extrema.lean
Description: Extrema of convex/concave functions: a convex function on a compact convex set attains its maximum at an extreme point, and a linear functional attains its maximum on a compact convex set at an extreme point. Distinct from Convex/KreinMilman (representation) and Convex/Extreme (definition of extreme points).

Name: Exposed.lean
Description: Exposed faces and exposed points of convex sets: `IsExposed s t` (t is an exposed face of s, lying in a supporting hyperplane where s is on one side), and `Set.exposedPoints`. Every exposed point is an extreme point but not conversely. Distinct from Convex/Extreme (extreme points) and Convex/KreinMilman.

Name: EGauge.lean
Description: Extended gauge (Minkowski functional with `ℝ≥0∞`-values): `egauge K x` for a set `K` that may not absorb every vector, taking value `∞` when the gauge is undefined. Properties: `egauge_neg`, `egauge_smul`. Distinct from Convex/Gauge (finite gauge) and Convex/Basic.

Name: FunctionTopology.lean
Description: Topological properties of convex functions: upper and lower semicontinuity, epigraph characterisations, and pointwise limits of convex functions. Proves `ConvexOn.isClosed_epigraph`. Distinct from Convex/Continuous (local Lipschitz) and Convex/Function (algebraic properties).

Name: Function.lean
Description: `ConvexOn 𝕜 s f`, `ConcaveOn 𝕜 s f`, `StrictConvexOn`, `StrictConcaveOn`: definitions and algebraic closure properties (sums, scalar multiples, supremum of convex functions is convex). The algebraic workhorse for convex function theory. Distinct from Convex/Basic (sets) and Convex/Continuous (regularity).

Name: Extreme.lean
Description: Extreme points of convex sets: `x ∈ Set.extremePoints s` iff `x ∈ s` and `x` is not in the relative interior of any segment in `s`. Proves basic properties: extreme points of a face are extreme in the whole set. Distinct from Convex/Exposed (exposed points) and Convex/KreinMilman.

Name: CStarAlgebra
Description: C*-algebras: Banach *-algebras satisfying the C*-identity, the Gelfand-Naimark theorem, and operator algebra fundamentals.

Name: SpecialFunctions
Description: Special functions applied to C*-algebra elements via the functional calculus: the exponential exp(a), the square root √a for positive a, the absolute value |a|, and logarithm log(a) for invertible positive a. Contains the polar decomposition a = u|a|. Distinct from ContinuousFunctionalCalculus (the general machinery) — this applies specific functions.

Name: PosPart.lean
Description: Positive and negative parts in C*-algebras: `a⁺ = f(a)` where `f(t) = max(t, 0)`.

Name: Unitary
Description: Unitary elements of a C*-algebra: elements u satisfying u*u = uu* = 1, forming the unitary group U(A). Contains the exponential map u = exp(ih) for self-adjoint h, the connected component of the identity, and the spectral theorem for normal unitaries (σ(u) ⊆ S¹). Distinct from the unitary group in LinearAlgebra (finite-dimensional matrix setting).

Name: Span.lean
Description: Span of unitaries: every element is a linear combination of unitaries.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Maps between unitary groups.

Name: Connected.lean
Description: Connectedness of the unitary group.

Name: ContinuousFunctionalCalculus
Description: The continuous functional calculus for normal elements of a C*-algebra: for a normal a ∈ A the unique *-homomorphism cfcHom : C(σ(a)) → A sending z ↦ a. Contains cfc (the functional calculus map), the spectral mapping theorem (cfcHom_map_spectrum, cfc_map_spectrum), positivity (f(a) ≥ 0 when f ≥ 0), and the absolute value |a| = √(a*a) construction. Covers unital, non-unital, real, and complex cases. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/SpecialFunctions (which applies specific functions via this calculus).

Name: Range.lean
Description: Range of the functional calculus map.

Name: RealImaginaryPart.lean
Description: Real and imaginary parts via functional calculus.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Functional calculus in pi C*-algebras.

Name: Projection.lean
Description: Projections via functional calculus: `1_{[t,∞)}(a)` is a projection.

Name: Unital.lean
Description: Unital continuous functional calculus.

Name: Unitary.lean
Description: Unitaries in the functional calculus: `exp(ia)` is unitary for self-adjoint `a`.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restriction of the functional calculus to subalgebras.

Name: Unique.lean
Description: Uniqueness of the functional calculus.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order and the functional calculus: `f ≤ g → f(a) ≤ g(a)` for self-adjoint `a`.

Name: Continuity.lean
Description: Continuity of the functional calculus map.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Functional calculus instances: for normal elements in C*-algebras.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Continuous functional calculus: the *-homomorphism `C(σ(a)) → A` sending `id ↦ a`.

Name: Commute.lean
Description: Commutativity in the functional calculus: `f(a)` commutes with anything commuting with `a`.

Name: NonUnital.lean
Description: Non-unital continuous functional calculus.

Name: Note.lean
Description: Documentation notes on the functional calculus.

Name: Integral.lean
Description: Integration and the functional calculus.

Name: Isometric.lean
Description: Isometric property of the functional calculus.

Name: Module
Description: Hilbert C*-modules: generalizations of Hilbert spaces where the inner product ⟨·,·⟩ takes values in a C*-algebra A rather than ℂ. Adjointable operators, the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality in this setting, and the connection to the external tensor product of Hilbert spaces. Distinct from inner product spaces (scalar-valued inner product) and from A-modules (no inner product).

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Type synonyms for C*-modules.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Hilbert C*-modules: `HilbertModule A E` — a Banach `A`-module with an `A`-valued inner product.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Hilbert C*-modules: constructions of C*-modules.

Name: lpSpace.lean
Description: ℓᵖ direct sums of C*-algebras: the C*-algebra structure on `lp (fun i => A i) p` when each `A i` is a C*-algebra and `p = ∞` (or `p = 2` for Hilbert modules). Provides the C*-identity for the direct sum norm. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Matrix (matrix algebras) and InnerProductSpace/l2Space.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix algebras `Mₙ(A)` where `A` is a C*-algebra: the C*-algebra structure on `Matrix n n A`, block-matrix operations, and the identification with `A ⊗ Mₙ(ℂ)`. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/CStarMatrix (the specific case `A = ℂ`) and Analysis/Matrix/Normed.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: *-homomorphisms between C*-algebras (`CStarAlgHom`): automatic isometry for injective maps, the kernel is a closed two-sided ideal, and the image is a C*-subalgebra. Provides the category of C*-algebras with these morphisms. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/GelfandNaimarkSegal (representations on Hilbert spaces) and CStarAlgebra/Classes.

Name: GelfandDuality.lean
Description: Gelfand duality: every commutative unital C*-algebra is isometrically *-isomorphic to `C(X, ℂ)` for `X` its character space (maximal ideal space). Proves `gelfandTransform` is an isometric *-isomorphism and that the character space is a compact Hausdorff space. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/ContinuousMap (the `C(X, A)` construction) and CStarAlgebra/Spectrum.

Name: GelfandNaimarkSegal.lean
Description: The Gelfand–Naimark–Segal (GNS) construction: given a state `φ` on a C*-algebra `A`, produces a Hilbert space `H_φ` and a *-representation `π_φ : A → B(H_φ)`. Proves the universal property: every C*-algebra embeds isometrically into `B(H)` for some Hilbert space `H`. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Hom (abstract *-homomorphisms) and InnerProductSpace/Completion.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectral theory in C*-algebras: the spectrum of a normal element via continuous functional calculus `continuousFunctionalCalculus`, the spectral mapping theorem, and the C*-identity for the functional calculus. Proves the spectrum is real for self-adjoint elements. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Fuglede and Complex/Spectrum (Banach algebra spectrum).

Name: Unitization.lean
Description: Unitization of a non-unital C*-algebra `A`: `Unitization 𝕜 A` with the C*-norm defined as the operator norm of the left-multiplication representation. Proves the C*-identity for the unitization and the universal property. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Multiplier (which embeds into a larger C*-algebra) and CStarAlgebra/Basic.

Name: Projection.lean
Description: Projections in C*-algebras: self-adjoint idempotents `p = p* = p²`. Proves the lattice structure of projections (meet and join), Murray–von Neumann equivalence, and the relationship between projections and closed left ideals. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Positive (general positive elements) and InnerProductSpace/Orthogonal.

Name: Multiplier.lean
Description: The multiplier algebra `M(A)` of a non-unital C*-algebra `A`: the largest unital C*-algebra in which `A` is an essential ideal. Defines `DoubleCentralizer` and proves `M(A)` is a C*-algebra with `A` as a closed two-sided ideal. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Unitization (a smaller unitization) and CStarAlgebra/Basic.

Name: PositiveLinearMap.lean
Description: Positive linear maps on C*-algebras: `IsPositive φ` (φ maps positive elements to positive elements). Proves that positive maps are bounded and self-adjoint, and develops their order-theoretic properties. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/CompletelyPositiveMap (stronger complete positivity condition).

Name: CompletelyPositiveMap.lean
Description: Completely positive maps between C*-algebras: `IsCompletelyPositive φ`, meaning every matrix amplification `id ⊗ φ` is positive. Proves that *-homomorphisms are completely positive and develops the basic algebra of CP maps. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/PositiveLinearMap (positive but not necessarily completely positive maps).

Name: ContinuousLinearMap.lean
Description: C*-algebra structure on bounded operators: `B(H) = H →L[ℂ] H` (continuous linear maps on a Hilbert space) as a C*-algebra with the operator norm and adjoint as involution. Provides the canonical instance `CStarAlgebra (H →L[ℂ] H)`. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Basic (abstract axioms) and InnerProductSpace/Adjoint (adjoint construction).

Name: Classes.lean
Description: Type-class hierarchy for C*-algebras and related structures: `NonUnitalCStarAlgebra`, `CStarAlgebra`, `WeakDual.CharacterSpace`, and their relationships. Collects the typeclass declarations and basic instance construction lemmas. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Basic (which proves structural theorems) and CStarAlgebra/Hom.

Name: ApproximateUnit.lean
Description: Approximate units (approximate identities) in C*-algebras: `CStarAlgebra.ApproximateUnit`, the existence theorem for σ-unital C*-algebras, and the Cohen–Hewitt factorisation consequence. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Basic (the C*-identity and basic algebra) and CStarAlgebra/Unitization (adding a unit).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The `CStarAlgebra` typeclass: a Banach *-algebra satisfying the C*-identity `‖a* a‖ = ‖a‖²`. Proves basic consequences: the norm is determined by the spectral radius, *-homomorphisms are automatically contractive, and the self-adjoint part is closed. The foundational file for the CStarAlgebra folder.

Name: Extreme.lean
Description: Extreme points of the unit ball in a C*-algebra: characterisation as the partial isometries `v` with `(1 - v*v)` and `(1 - vv*)` having operator-norm-zero complements (i.e., `v` is a unitary or a corner of a unitary). Distinct from Convex/Extreme (general extreme point theory) and CStarAlgebra/Projection.

Name: Fuglede.lean
Description: Fuglede's theorem: if `N` is a normal operator in a C*-algebra and `T` commutes with `N`, then `T` commutes with `N*`. Proved via the Borel functional calculus or a direct Banach-algebra argument. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Spectrum (spectral theory) and InnerProductSpace/Symmetric.

Name: Exponential.lean
Description: The exponential map in a unital C*-algebra: `NormedSpace.exp ℂ a` for `a` self-adjoint gives a unitary, and `exp` maps the self-adjoint elements to positive unitaries. Connects to the spectrum via `exp (spectrum a) = spectrum (exp a)`. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/Spectrum (spectral theory) and SpecialFunctions/Exponential.

Name: ContinuousMap.lean
Description: The C*-algebra `C(X, A)` of continuous functions from a compact Hausdorff space `X` to a C*-algebra `A`: instance, norm, and involution. Proves `C(X, ℂ)` is a commutative C*-algebra. Distinct from CStarAlgebra/GelfandDuality (which goes in the other direction) and RCLike/ContinuousMap.

Name: CStarMatrix.lean
Description: C*-algebra structure on matrices: `Matrix n n ℂ` as a C*-algebra with the operator norm. Proves the C*-identity for matrices and provides instances connecting `CStarAlgebra` to `Matrix`. Distinct from Analysis/Matrix/Normed (which covers matrix norms more generally) and CStarAlgebra/Matrix (which may treat matrix algebras over general C*-algebras).

Name: PSeriesComplex.lean
Description: Complex p-series: convergence of the sum of n^(-s) for complex s, and the relation to the Riemann zeta function.

Name: Quaternion.lean
Description: Quaternionic analysis: the norm, inner product, and metric space structure on quaternions as a normed division algebra.

Name: Oscillation.lean
Description: Oscillation of a function on a set: the supremum of |f(x)-f(y)|, used to characterize Riemann integrability and points of continuity.

Name: PSeries.lean
Description: Real p-series: the series sum 1/n^p converges iff p > 1, proved via the integral test.

Name: Seminorm.lean
Description: Seminorms on vector spaces: the seminorm typeclass, the topology they generate, and the connection to locally convex spaces.

Name: SumIntegralExpDecay.lean
Description: Summation-integration comparisons for exponentially decaying functions, used in analytic number theory and analysis.

Name: SumOverResidueClass.lean
Description: Sums over arithmetic progressions: estimates for partial sums over residue classes using Euler-Maclaurin or Fourier methods.

Name: Subadditive.lean
Description: Subadditive functions and Fekete's lemma: if f(m+n) <= f(m)+f(n) then lim f(n)/n exists and equals inf f(n)/n.

Name: SumIntegralComparisons.lean
Description: Comparison between sums and integrals for monotone functions: bounding the sum of f(n) via the integral of f and vice versa.

Name: MellinTransform.lean
Description: The Mellin transform: the integral transform connecting Dirichlet series and the zeta function via a multiplicative substitution.

Name: Convolution.lean
Description: Convolution of functions: the operation (f * g)(x) = integral f(y)g(x-y)dy on normed spaces, with Lp and approximate identity results.

Name: Hofer.lean
Description: Hofer's lemma: a metric space lemma producing perturbed points where a function is nearly maximized, used in symplectic geometry.

Name: BoundedVariation.lean
