Description: Functions of bounded variation on intervals: total variation, Jordan decomposition, and the Lebesgue-Vitali theorem.

Name: ConstantSpeed.lean
Description: Constant speed reparametrization of curves: a path can always be reparametrized to have constant speed equal to its total length.

Name: LConvolution.lean
Description: L-function convolution: Dirichlet convolution in the analytic setting, connecting multiplicative and additive structure.

Name: MeanInequalitiesPow.lean
Description: Power mean inequalities: comparison of power means M_p <= M_q for p <= q and the AM-GM inequality via power means.

Name: MellinInversion.lean
Description: The Mellin inversion theorem: recovering f from its Mellin transform via a Bromwich integral.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Matrix analysis: the operator norm on matrices, the exponential map exp(A), and estimates for matrix power series.

Name: MeanInequalities.lean
Description: Classical mean inequalities: the AM-GM inequality (the arithmetic mean of nonneg reals is always at least the geometric mean), Young's inequality (ab ≤ aᵖ/p + bᵖ'/p' for conjugate exponents), Hölder's inequality, and Minkowski's inequality for sums. The foundational analytic inequalities used in Lp theory, probability, and optimization — including the mathematics of diversification and growth rates.

Name: AlgebraicTopology
Description: Contains homotopy theory, homology, cohomology, and fundamental groupoids of spaces. Used to prove topological invariance, compute obstructions, and classify spaces up to homotopy equivalence. Relied on by topology, geometry, algebraic geometry, and mathematical physics.

Name: SimplicialComplex
Description: Simplicial complexes: combinatorial models for topological spaces built from simplices, with geometric realization.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Abstract simplicial complexes: `SimplicialComplex 𝕜 E` — a collection of finite subsets (faces) of a set `E` closed under taking subsets, with the geometric realisation. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Basic (combinatorial simplicial sets) and AlgebraicTopology/SingularHomology.

Name: SimplicialCategory
Description: The simplex category: the category of finite ordinals and order-preserving maps, generating all simplicial structure.

Name: SimplicialObject.lean
Description: Simplicial objects in a simplicial category: the interaction between the simplicial enrichment and simplicial object structure. Distinct from SimplicialCategory/Basic and SimplicialObject/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simplicial categories: `SimplicialCategory C` — a category enriched over simplicial sets. The `sHom` functor and enriched composition. Distinct from SimplicialCategory/SimplicialObject and SimplicialObject/Basic.

Name: SimplicialObject
Description: Simplicial objects in a category: functors from the opposite simplex category to C, with face and degeneracy maps satisfying the simplicial identities.

Name: II.lean
Description: The interval object `Δ[1]` as a simplicial object: `SimplicialObject.II` — the simplicial interval used for homotopies. Distinct from SimplicialSet/StdSimplexOne.

Name: Op.lean
Description: Opposite of a simplicial object: `SimplicialObject.op X` and the reversal `X.op : CosimplicialObject C` (switching from simplicial to cosimplicial). Distinct from SimplicialSet/Op.

Name: Split.lean
Description: Split simplicial objects: `Split.SplitEpi` — simplicial objects that split at each degree, admitting a basis of non-degenerate simplices. Used in the Dold–Kan correspondence. Distinct from SimplicialObject/Basic.

Name: Homotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy of maps between simplicial objects: `SimplicialObject.Homotopy f g` via extra degeneracies, and the homotopy category of simplicial objects. Distinct from SimplicialObject/ChainHomotopy.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simplicial objects in a category: `SimplicialObject C = Δᵒᵖ ⥤ C` — contravariant functors from the simplex category. Face and degeneracy maps, augmented simplicial objects, and the truncation/skeleton. The foundational file.

Name: ChainHomotopy.lean
Description: Chain homotopies for simplicial abelian groups: the extra degeneracy construction giving chain homotopies, used in the proof of the Dold–Kan equivalence. Distinct from SimplicialObject/Homotopy.

Name: Coskeletal.lean
Description: Coskeletal simplicial objects: `SimplicialObject.cosk n X` — the right adjoint to the truncation functor, extending a truncated simplicial object to a full one. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Coskeletal.

Name: SingularHomology
Description: Singular homology: homology groups H_n(X) defined via singular simplices, with the long exact sequence of a pair.

Name: HomotopyInvariance.lean
Description: Homotopy invariance of singular homology at the chain level: homotopic maps induce chain homotopic maps on the singular chain complex. Distinct from SingularHomology/HomotopyInvarianceTopCat.

Name: HomotopyInvarianceTopCat.lean
Description: Homotopy invariance of singular homology for topological spaces: homotopic maps `f ≃ g : X → Y` induce equal maps `H_n(f) = H_n(g)`. Distinct from SingularHomology/HomotopyInvariance.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Singular homology groups: `singularHomology n X` — the `n`-th homology of the singular chain complex `C_*(X) = ℤ[Sing X]`. Definition, functoriality, and the augmented complex. Distinct from SingularHomology/HomotopyInvariance.

Name: HomologyZero.lean
Description: H₀ of a topological space: `H₀(X) ≅ ⊕_{components} ℤ` — the free abelian group on connected components. Distinct from SingularHomology/Basic and FundamentalGroupoid/Basic.

Name: SimplicialSet
Description: Simplicial sets: simplicial objects in Set, modeling infinity-groupoids and providing a combinatorial foundation for homotopy theory.

Name: KanComplex
Description: Kan complexes: simplicial sets satisfying all horn-filling conditions — for every horn inclusion Λⁿₖ → Δⁿ and every map Λⁿₖ → X there exists a filler Δⁿ → X. Develops the algebraic structure of homotopy: MulStruct (composition of simplices) and RelStruct (relative maps), providing the framework for defining homotopy groups πₙ(X, x). Fibrant objects in the Kan-Quillen model structure, and the combinatorial models for homotopy types. Distinct from AnodyneExtensions (the generating acyclic cofibrations) and Quasicategory (inner horns only).

Name: MulStruct.lean
Description: Multiplicative structure on Kan complexes.

Name: Homology
Description: Homology of simplicial sets: applying the alternating-face-map chain complex to a simplicial abelian group to obtain homology groups. The singular homology of a topological space X is the simplicial set homology of Sing(X). Distinct from AlgebraicTopology/SingularHomology which works directly with topological spaces.

Name: HomotopyInvariance.lean
Description: Homotopy invariance of simplicial homology.

Name: HomologyZero.lean
Description: H₀ of a simplicial set: free abelian group on connected components.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simplicial homology: `simplicial_homology n X`.

Name: AnodyneExtensions
Description: Anodyne extensions: the class of monomorphisms of simplicial sets defined as the left lifting class of Kan fibrations — anodyneExtensions = (fibrations _).llp. Proved (via the small object argument) to be generated by the horn inclusions Λⁿₖ ↪ Δⁿ as retracts of transfinite compositions of their pushouts. Contains innerAnodyneExtensions (inner horns only, for quasicategories) and strongAnodyneExtensions (a combinatorial subclass). A map is a Kan fibration iff it has the RLP against all anodyne extensions. Distinct from KanComplex (which concerns the simplicial sets themselves, not maps between them).

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Rank of a pairing in anodyne extensions.

Name: PairingCore.lean
Description: Core pairing data for anodyne extensions.

Name: RelativeCellComplex.lean
Description: Anodyne extensions as relative cell complexes.

Name: RankNat.lean
Description: Natural number rank for anodyne extensions.

Name: IsUniquelyCodimOneFace.lean
Description: Uniquely codimension-1 face conditions for anodyne extensions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Anodyne extensions: maps with the left lifting property against Kan fibrations.

Name: Pairing.lean
Description: Pairing construction for anodyne extensions.

Name: Op.lean
Description: Anodyne extensions in the opposite category.

Name: ProdStdSimplexOne.lean
Description: Products with `Δ[1]`: the cylinder `X × Δ[1]` and its role in simplicial homotopies. Distinct from SimplicialSet/ProdStdSimplex and SimplicialSet/Homotopy.

Name: ProdStdSimplex.lean
Description: Products with the standard simplex: `X × Δ[n]` and the shuffle map `Δ[m] × Δ[n] → Δ[m+n]`. Used in the Eilenberg–Zilber theorem. Distinct from SimplicialSet/StdSimplex and SimplicialSet/ProdStdSimplexOne.

Name: PushoutProduct.lean
Description: Pushout products in simplicial sets: the pushout product `f □ g` of two maps, used to formulate the Leibniz rule for model category axioms. Distinct from SimplicialSet/FiniteColimits and ModelCategory/Basic.

Name: RelativeMorphism.lean
Description: Relative morphisms of simplicial sets: maps `X → Y` relative to a subcomplex `A ↪ X`, i.e., maps that restrict to a given map on `A`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Subcomplex.

Name: RegularEpi.lean
Description: Regular epimorphisms of simplicial sets: characterisation of regular epis (effective coequalisers), which in `sSet` are surjective on simplices. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Monomorphisms.

Name: Presentable.lean
Description: Presentability of the category of simplicial sets: `sSet` is a locally presentable category, generated by the standard simplices `Δ[n]`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Basic.

Name: Nonempty.lean
Description: Non-empty simplicial sets: `SimplicialSet.Nonempty X` — simplicial sets with at least one vertex (0-simplex). Distinct from SimplicialSet/Basic.

Name: NonDegenerateSimplicesSubcomplex.lean
Description: Non-degenerate simplices and subcomplexes: the set of non-degenerate simplices of a subcomplex and its relationship to the ambient simplicial set. Distinct from SimplicialSet/NonDegenerateSimplices and SimplicialSet/Subcomplex.

Name: Op.lean
Description: Opposite simplicial set: `X.op` — the simplicial set obtained by reversing the simplicial structure, `X.op_n = X_n` with face/degeneracy maps reversed. Distinct from SimplicialObject/Op.

Name: PiZero.lean
Description: π₀ of a simplicial set: `SimplicialSet.π₀ X` — the set of connected components, i.e., the coequaliser of `d₀, d₁ : X₁ ⇉ X₀`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/HomotopyCat.

Name: Path.lean
Description: Paths in a simplicial set: `X.Path x y` — the set of 1-simplices with boundary `(x, y)`. Used in defining the fundamental groupoid of a Kan complex. Distinct from FundamentalGroupoid/Basic.

Name: Simplices.lean
Description: The category of simplices of a simplicial set: `SimplicialSet.Simplices X` — the category whose objects are pairs `([n], x ∈ X_n)` and morphisms are face/degeneracy maps. Distinct from SimplicialSet/NonDegenerateSimplices.

Name: SubcomplexEvaluation.lean
Description: Evaluation at objects for subcomplexes: the functor `SSet.Subcomplex X ⥤ Type` evaluating a subcomplex at each `[n]`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Subcomplex.

Name: SubcomplexColimits.lean
Description: Colimits in the category of subcomplexes: `SubcomplexColimits` — unions of subcomplexes as colimits. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Subcomplex and SimplicialSet/FiniteColimits.

Name: SubcomplexOp.lean
Description: Opposite of a subcomplex: the opposite subcomplex `A.op` of `A ↪ X`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Subcomplex and SimplicialSet/Op.

Name: TopAdj.lean
Description: Adjunction between simplicial sets and topological spaces: the geometric realisation `|·| : sSet ⇌ Top : Sing` adjunction, where `Sing X` is the singular simplicial set. Distinct from SimplicialSet/NerveAdjunction.

Name: Subdivision.lean
Description: Barycentric subdivision of simplicial sets: `sd X` — the subdivision functor, and the natural transformation `sd X → X`. Used to prove subdivisions of Kan complexes are Kan. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Skeleton.

Name: Subcomplex.lean
Description: Subcomplexes of simplicial sets: `SSet.Subcomplex X` — a sub-presheaf that is a simplicial set. The lattice of subcomplexes and the subcomplex generated by a set of simplices. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Skeleton.

Name: Splitting.lean
Description: Splittings of simplicial sets: a splitting of `X` is a choice of non-degenerate simplices providing a direct-sum decomposition in the normalised chain complex. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Degenerate and SimplicialObject/Split.

Name: Skeleton.lean
Description: Skeleton and coskeleton adjunction: `sk n ⊣ cosk n` — the `n`-skeleton `sk n X` truncates `X` above dimension `n`. The skeleton filtration of a CW-complex. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Coskeletal.

Name: StdSimplex.lean
Description: Standard simplex `Δ[n]`: the representable simplicial set `Δ[n] = Hom_Δ(-, [n])`. The Yoneda lemma gives `Hom(Δ[n], X) ≅ X_n`. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic and SimplicialSet/Boundary.

Name: StrictSegal.lean
Description: Strict Segal simplicial sets: simplicial sets satisfying the Segal condition strictly (not just up to homotopy), characterised as nerves of categories. The nerve is strictly Segal. Distinct from Quasicategory/StrictSegal.

Name: StdSimplexOne.lean
Description: The standard 1-simplex `Δ[1]`: explicit description of `Δ[1]` and its role as the simplicial interval. The two vertices and the non-degenerate 1-simplex. Distinct from SimplicialSet/StdSimplex.

Name: Dimension.lean
Description: Dimension of a simplicial set: `SimplicialSet.dim X` — the maximum `n` for which `X` has non-degenerate `n`-simplices. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Finite and SimplicialSet/Skeleton.

Name: Degenerate.lean
Description: Degenerate simplices: `SimplicialSet.Degenerate X n` — the subcomplex generated by degenerate `n`-simplices. The Eilenberg–Zilber lemma: the quotient by degenerate simplices is the normalised simplicial set. Distinct from SimplicialSet/NonDegenerateSimplices.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite simplicial sets: `FinSimplicialSet` — simplicial sets with finitely many non-degenerate simplices. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Dimension and SimplicialSet/FiniteColimits.

Name: FiniteProd.lean
Description: Finite products of simplicial sets: `X × Y` computed as the levelwise product `(X × Y)_n = X_n × Y_n`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Monoidal and SimplicialSet/ProdStdSimplex.

Name: FiniteColimits.lean
Description: Finite colimits in the category of simplicial sets: `SSet` has all small colimits (computed levelwise), and finite colimits for finite simplicial sets. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Finite and SimplicialSet/HornColimits.

Name: Coskeletal.lean
Description: Coskeletal simplicial sets: `cosk n X` — the coskeleton functor, right adjoint to `sk n`. An `n`-coskeletal simplicial set is determined by its `n`-skeleton. Distinct from SimplicialObject/Coskeletal.

Name: Boundary.lean
Description: Boundary of the standard simplex: `∂Δ[n]` — the subcomplex of `Δ[n]` omitting the non-degenerate top simplex. The inclusion `∂Δ[n] ↪ Δ[n]` as the generating cofibration. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Horn and SimplicialSet/StdSimplex.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Simplicial sets: `SSet = SimplicialObject (Type)` — the category of simplicial sets. Basic operations: boundary, horn, standard simplex `Δ[n]`, and the geometric realisation adjunction. The main API file.

Name: CategoryWithFibrations.lean
Description: Category with fibrations structure on simplicial sets: the Kan fibrations as the fibrations, used in the Kan–Quillen model structure setup. Distinct from SimplicialSet/KanComplex and ModelCategory/CategoryWithCofibrations.

Name: CompStructTruncated.lean
Description: Composition structures for truncated simplicial sets: `CompStruct` for simplicial sets truncated at low dimensions. Distinct from SimplicialSet/CompStruct and SimplexCategory/Truncated.

Name: CompStruct.lean
Description: Composition structures on simplicial sets: `CompStruct X` — data witnessing that a simplicial set has composites for composable morphisms (inner horn fillings). Distinct from SimplicialSet/StrictSegal and Quasicategory/Basic.

Name: HoFunctorMonoidal.lean
Description: The homotopy category functor as a monoidal functor: `Ho : sSet → Cat` is lax monoidal with respect to the cartesian products. Distinct from SimplicialSet/HomotopyCat and SimplicialSet/Monoidal.

Name: Nerve.lean
Description: Nerve of a category: `nerve C` — the simplicial set with `(nerve C)_n = Functor([n], C)` (composable chains of morphisms). Used to embed categories into simplicial sets. Distinct from Quasicategory/Nerve.

Name: Monomorphisms.lean
Description: Monomorphisms of simplicial sets: `Mono f` iff each `f_n` is injective. Characterisation: monomorphisms are exactly the levelwise injective maps. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Boundary (which is monic).

Name: NerveAdjunction.lean
Description: The nerve-realisation adjunction: `|-| ⊣ nerve` — the geometric realisation `|X|` is left adjoint to the nerve functor. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Nerve and SimplicialSet/TopAdj.

Name: NonDegenerateSimplices.lean
Description: Non-degenerate simplices: a simplex `x ∈ X_n` is non-degenerate if it is not in the image of any degeneracy map. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Degenerate and SimplicialSet/Simplices.

Name: NerveNondegenerate.lean
Description: Non-degenerate simplices of the nerve: the non-degenerate `n`-simplices of `nerve C` are exactly the composable chains of non-identity morphisms of length `n`. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Nerve and SimplicialSet/NonDegenerateSimplices.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on simplicial sets: `SSet` is a symmetric monoidal category under the cartesian product (Cartesian monoidal structure). Distinct from SimplicialSet/FiniteProd.

Name: HomotopyCat.lean
Description: The homotopy category of simplicial sets: `Ho(sSet)` — the localisation of `sSet` at weak homotopy equivalences. The `Ho` functor. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Homotopy.

Name: Homotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy of simplicial set maps: `SSet.Homotopy f g` via the cylinder `X × Δ[1]`, and the homotopy relation as an equivalence on maps. Distinct from SimplicialSet/HomotopyCat.

Name: Horn.lean
Description: Horn inclusions: `Λ[n, k] ↪ Δ[n]` — the `k`-th horn (standard simplex minus the `k`-th face and interior). Inner horns `0 < k < n` and outer horns. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Boundary.

Name: KanComplex.lean
Description: Kan complexes: `KanComplex X` — simplicial sets satisfying all horn filling conditions (including outer horns). Kan complexes model ∞-groupoids (homotopy types). Distinct from Quasicategory/Basic (inner horns only).

Name: HornColimits.lean
Description: Colimits of horn diagrams: pushouts and colimits in `sSet` arising from horn inclusions, used in establishing the model structure. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Horn and SimplicialSet/FiniteColimits.

Name: SimplexCategory
Description: The simplex category as a Lean category: objects [n], morphisms as monotone maps, and the cosimplicial identities.

Name: GeneratorsRelations
Description: Presentation of the simplex category by generators and relations: the face maps δᵢ and degeneracy maps σᵢ as generators, subject to the cosimplicial identities. Used to verify the simplex category axioms and to give explicit computations with simplicial maps.

Name: NormalForms.lean
Description: Normal forms for simplex category morphisms.

Name: EpiMono.lean
Description: Epi/mono in the simplex category from generators.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Generators and relations for the simplex category.

Name: Augmented
Description: Augmented simplicial objects: simplicial objects X• equipped with an extra map X₀ → X₋₁ to a chosen object (the augmentation). The augmented simplex category Δ₊ with the extra object [-1], used in the construction of simplicial resolutions and the nerve of a category with a chosen object.

Name: Monoidal.lean
Description: Monoidal structure on the augmented simplex category.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Augmented simplex category: adding the empty object `[-1]`.

Name: SemiSimplexCategory.lean
Description: Semi-simplex category: the subcategory of Δ consisting only of injective (coface) maps, used for semi-simplicial objects. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic.

Name: Rev.lean
Description: Reversal autoequivalence of the simplex category: `SimplexCategory.rev : Δ ≌ Δᵒᵖ` — the involution sending `[n] ↦ [n]` and reversing order. Distinct from SimplicialObject/Op and SimplexCategory/Basic.

Name: Truncated.lean
Description: Truncated simplex category `Δ_≤n`: the full subcategory of `Δ` on objects `[0], …, [n]`. Truncated simplicial objects and their skeleton/coskeleton. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic and SimplicialSet/Skeleton.

Name: ToMkOne.lean
Description: Maps to `[1]` in the simplex category: the classification of all order-preserving maps `[n] → [1]`, which correspond to subsets `S ⊆ {0,…,n}`. Used in simplicial set theory. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `SimplexCategory`: the category with objects `[n] = {0,…,n}` and order-preserving maps, as `NonemptyFinLinOrd`. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic (API).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Main API for the simplex category Δ: `SimplexCategory`, the unique maps `δ i` (coface) and `σ i` (codegeneracy), and simplicial identities. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Defs (type definition) and SimplicialObject/Basic.

Name: MorphismProperty.lean
Description: Morphism properties in the simplex category: characterisation of monomorphisms (injective order-preserving maps), epimorphisms (surjective), and isomorphisms. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic.

Name: DeltaZeroIter.lean
Description: Iterated `δ₀` maps in the simplex category: the composition `δ₀^n : [0] → [n]` and its role in the augmented simplex category. Distinct from SimplexCategory/Basic.

Name: FundamentalGroupoid
Description: The fundamental groupoid of a space: a groupoid with objects as points and morphisms as homotopy classes of paths.

Name: Product.lean
Description: Fundamental groupoid of a product: `Π₁(X × Y) ≅ Π₁(X) × Π₁(Y)`. Distinct from FundamentalGroupoid/Basic and Topology/Homotopy/Product.

Name: PUnit.lean
Description: Fundamental groupoid of the one-point space: `Π₁(PUnit) ≅ PUnit` (trivial groupoid). Distinct from FundamentalGroupoid/Basic.

Name: SimplyConnected.lean
Description: Simply connected spaces via the fundamental groupoid: `SimplyConnected X` iff `Π₁(X)` is a connected groupoid with trivial automorphism groups (i.e., `π₁(X, x) = 1` for all `x`). Distinct from FundamentalGroupoid/FundamentalGroup and Topology/Homotopy/Contractible.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fundamental groupoid `FundamentalGroupoid X`: the groupoid whose objects are points of `X` and morphisms are homotopy classes of paths. Defines `FundamentalGroupoid.fundamentalGroupoidFunctor` and basic operations. Distinct from FundamentalGroupoid/FundamentalGroup.

Name: FundamentalGroup.lean
Description: Fundamental group `π₁(X, x)`: the vertex group of the fundamental groupoid at a base point, as a group. The isomorphism `π₁(X, x) ≅ π₁(X, y)` for path-connected `X`. Distinct from Topology/Homotopy/HomotopyGroup.

Name: InducedMaps.lean
Description: Maps induced on fundamental groupoids: a continuous map `f : X → Y` induces a functor `f_* : Π₁(X) → Π₁(Y)` and a group homomorphism on `π₁`. Distinct from FundamentalGroupoid/Basic.

Name: DoldKan
Description: The Dold-Kan correspondence: an equivalence between simplicial abelian groups and non-negatively graded chain complexes.

Name: NCompGamma.lean
Description: The natural isomorphism `N ∘ Γ ≅ id`: showing `N(Γ(C)) ≅ C` for chain complexes `C`. The other half of the Dold–Kan equivalence. Distinct from DoldKan/GammaCompN.

Name: Normalized.lean
Description: The normalised Moore complex: `NormalizedMooreComplex A` — the chain complex `N(A)` with the explicit boundary formula, and comparison with the `AlternatingFaceMapComplex`. Distinct from DoldKan/FunctorN.

Name: Homotopies.lean
Description: Chain homotopies in the Dold–Kan proof: the explicit chain homotopies constructed to prove `N ∘ Γ ≅ id` and `Γ ∘ N ≅ id`. Distinct from DoldKan/HomotopyEquivalence.

Name: HomotopyEquivalence.lean
Description: Homotopy equivalences in the Dold–Kan construction: the unit and counit of the Dold–Kan adjunction are homotopy equivalences before being shown to be isomorphisms. Distinct from DoldKan/Homotopies.

Name: Notations.lean
Description: Notation file for the Dold–Kan correspondence: defines `N`, `Γ`, and related notation used throughout the DoldKan folder. Contains no mathematical theorems.

Name: Projections.lean
Description: The projectors `P_q` and `Q_q`: endomorphisms of simplicial abelian groups iteratively projecting onto normalised components. Used in constructing `P_∞` and proving the Dold–Kan decomposition. Distinct from DoldKan/PInfty.

Name: SplitSimplicialObject.lean
Description: Split simplicial objects: simplicial objects admitting a splitting into non-degenerate parts, and their interaction with the Dold–Kan functors. Distinct from SimplicialObject/Split and DoldKan/Decomposition.

Name: NReflectsIso.lean
Description: The normalisation functor `N` reflects isomorphisms: a simplicial map `f` is an isomorphism iff `N(f)` is an isomorphism of chain complexes. Distinct from DoldKan/FunctorN and DoldKan/Equivalence.

Name: PInfty.lean
Description: The idempotent `P_∞`: the endomorphism of a simplicial abelian group that projects onto the normalised part, defined as the limit `P_∞ = lim_{q→∞} P_q`. Proves `P_∞ ∘ P_∞ = P_∞`. Distinct from DoldKan/Projections.

Name: GammaCompN.lean
Description: The natural isomorphism `Γ ∘ N ≅ id`: showing the composition `Γ(N(A)) ≅ A` for simplicial abelian groups `A`. One half of the Dold–Kan unit/counit. Distinct from DoldKan/NCompGamma.

Name: Degeneracies.lean
Description: Degeneracy operators in the Dold–Kan context: the behaviour of the normalisation functor `N` on degenerate simplices, showing degenerate simplices contribute 0 to the normalised complex. Distinct from DoldKan/Faces and DoldKan/Decomposition.

Name: Equivalence.lean
Description: Main Dold–Kan equivalence theorem: the normalisation functor `N : SimplicialAbelianGroup ≌ ChainComplex⁺(Ab)` is an equivalence of categories. Assembles the components from FunctorN, FunctorGamma, GammaCompN, NCompGamma. Distinct from DoldKan/EquivalenceAdditive.

Name: Compatibility.lean
Description: Compatibility of the Dold–Kan normalisation functor `N` with additive structures: the normalisation of a product splits as a product of normalisations. Distinct from DoldKan/Normalized and DoldKan/FunctorN.

Name: Decomposition.lean
Description: Decomposition of simplicial abelian groups: every simplex decomposes as a sum of degenerate and normalised parts via the projectors `P_infty` and `Q_infty`. Distinct from DoldKan/Projections and DoldKan/PInfty.

Name: EquivalenceAdditive.lean
Description: Dold–Kan equivalence for additive categories: generalisation of the Dold–Kan correspondence to simplicial objects in any additive category. Distinct from DoldKan/Equivalence (Ab case) and DoldKan/EquivalencePseudoabelian.

Name: FunctorGamma.lean
Description: The Gamma functor `Γ`: from non-negatively graded chain complexes to simplicial abelian groups. Definition of `Γ(C)_n = ⊕_{surj [n]→[k]} C_k`, its simplicial structure, and functoriality. Distinct from DoldKan/FunctorN (the other direction).

Name: FunctorN.lean
Description: The normalisation functor `N`: from simplicial abelian groups to non-negatively graded chain complexes. `N(A)_n = ⋂_i ker(d_i : A_n → A_{n-1})` with boundary `d_0`. Distinct from DoldKan/FunctorGamma.

Name: EquivalencePseudoabelian.lean
Description: Dold–Kan for pseudo-abelian (Karoubian) categories: extension of the Dold–Kan equivalence to simplicial objects in a Karoubian additive category via idempotent splitting. Distinct from DoldKan/EquivalenceAdditive.

Name: Faces.lean
Description: Face operators in the Dold–Kan normalisation: how the face maps `d_i` interact with the projectors `P_q` and the normalised Moore complex. Distinct from DoldKan/Degeneracies and DoldKan/Projections.

Name: ModelCategory
Description: Model categories: categories with weak equivalences, fibrations, and cofibrations satisfying Quillen's axioms, the framework for abstract homotopy theory.

Name: IsCofibrant.lean
Description: Cofibrant objects: `IsCofibrant X` — the unique map `∅ → X` is a cofibration. Basic properties and interactions with pushouts. Distinct from ModelCategory/CofibrantObjectHomotopy.

Name: JoyalTrick.lean
Description: Joyal's trick in model categories: a technique for establishing the model category axioms by verifying a smaller set of conditions (cofibrations + fibrant/cofibrant generation). Distinct from ModelCategory/Basic.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Basic properties of model categories deduced from the axioms: closure properties of fibrations, cofibrations, and weak equivalences (2-of-3, retract, lifting properties). Distinct from `CategoryWithCofibrations` (definitions only).

Name: FundamentalLemma.lean
Description: Fundamental lemma of model categories: a map that is both a cofibration and a weak equivalence (acyclic cofibration) has the left lifting property against all fibrations. Distinct from ModelCategory/BrownLemma.

Name: Homotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy in model categories: the general definition combining left and right homotopy, and the equivalence of the two for maps between bifibrant objects. Distinct from ModelCategory/LeftHomotopy and ModelCategory/RightHomotopy.

Name: PathObject.lean
Description: Path objects in a model category: `PathObj Y` — a factorisation of the diagonal `Y → Y × Y` as weak equivalence followed by fibration. Dual to cylinder objects. Distinct from ModelCategory/Cylinder.

Name: RightHomotopy.lean
Description: Right homotopy via path objects: `RightHomotopy f g` — two maps are right homotopic if they are homotopic via a path object `Path(Y)`. Distinct from ModelCategory/PathObject and ModelCategory/LeftHomotopy.

Name: Over.lean
Description: Model category structure on slice categories: `C/X` inherits a model category structure from `C` (the over-model structure). Distinct from ModelCategory/Basic and CategoryTheory/Limits/Over.

Name: LeftHomotopy.lean
Description: Left homotopy via cylinder objects: `LeftHomotopy f g` — two maps `f g : X → Y` are left homotopic if they are homotopic via a cylinder `Cyl(X)`. Distinct from ModelCategory/Cylinder and ModelCategory/RightHomotopy.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Opposite model category: if `C` is a model category then so is `Cᵒᵖ` with cofibrations and fibrations swapped. Distinct from ModelCategory/Basic.

Name: BrownLemma.lean
Description: Brown's lemma: a weak equivalence between fibrant objects that has a section which is also a weak equivalence is an acyclic fibration. Used to show the homotopy category is well-defined. Distinct from ModelCategory/FundamentalLemma.

Name: CategoryWithCofibrations.lean
Description: Categories with cofibrations (Waldhausen categories): a weaker structure than a model category, having only cofibrations and weak equivalences. Used in K-theory. Distinct from ModelCategory/Basic.

Name: BifibrantObjectHomotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy between maps between bifibrant objects: in a model category, homotopy between maps `f g : X → Y` with `X` cofibrant and `Y` fibrant is an equivalence relation. Distinct from ModelCategory/Bifibrant and ModelCategory/Homotopy.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Model categories: `ModelCategory C` — a category with three distinguished classes of morphisms (weak equivalences, cofibrations, fibrations) satisfying the model category axioms (MC1–MC5). The foundational file for the ModelCategory folder.

Name: Bifibrant.lean
Description: Bifibrant objects: objects that are both cofibrant and fibrant in a model category. Distinct from ModelCategory/IsCofibrant and ModelCategory/FibrantObjectHomotopy.

Name: DerivabilityStructureFibrant.lean
Description: Derivability structure for fibrant objects: the dual framework for fibrant objects, ensuring derived functors of right adjoints exist. Distinct from ModelCategory/DerivabilityStructureCofibrant.

Name: FibrantObjectHomotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy between maps into fibrant objects: right homotopy via a path object when the target is fibrant. Distinct from ModelCategory/RightHomotopy and ModelCategory/Bifibrant.

Name: DerivabilityStructureCofibrant.lean
Description: Derivability structure for cofibrant objects: the abstract framework ensuring that the localisation at cofibrant weak equivalences has a well-behaved derived functor. Distinct from ModelCategory/DerivabilityStructureFibrant.

Name: CofibrantObjectHomotopy.lean
Description: Homotopy between maps from cofibrant objects: left homotopy via a cylinder object when the source is cofibrant. Distinct from ModelCategory/LeftHomotopy and ModelCategory/Bifibrant.

Name: Cylinder.lean
Description: Cylinder objects in a model category: `CylinderObj X` — a factorisation of the fold map `X ⊔ X → X` as cofibration followed by weak equivalence. Dually, path objects. Distinct from ModelCategory/PathObject.

Name: RelativeCellComplex
Description: Relative cell complexes: spaces built by attaching cells relative to a subspace, used in model category cofibrant replacement.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Relative cell complexes: `IsRelativeCellComplex i` — a morphism built by transfinite composition of pushouts of generating cofibrations. The abstract CW-complex notion used in model category theory. Distinct from RelativeCellComplex/AttachCells.

Name: AttachCells.lean
Description: Attaching cells to a simplicial set or topological space: the pushout `X ∪_{∂Dⁿ} Dⁿ` and the resulting CW-complex step. Distinct from RelativeCellComplex/Basic and SimplicialSet/Horn.

Name: Quasicategory
Description: Quasicategories (infinity-categories): simplicial sets satisfying the inner horn filling condition, Joyal's model for (infinity,1)-categories.

Name: StrictSegal.lean
Description: Strict Segal conditions: simplicial sets satisfying the strict (not just weak) Segal condition are nerves of categories. Distinct from Quasicategory/Basic and SimplicialSet/StrictSegal.

Name: TwoTruncated.lean
Description: 2-truncated quasicategories: quasicategories whose homotopy groups above dimension 2 vanish, corresponding to (2,1)-categories. Distinct from Quasicategory/Basic and SimplicialSet/KanComplex.

Name: StrictBicategory.lean
Description: `QCat.strictBicategory`: the strict bicategory of quasicategories, built from the `Cat`-enriched ordinary category `QCat.catEnrichedOrdinaryCategory` whose hom-categories are homotopy categories of simplicial internal homs (`hoFunctor : SSet ⥤ Cat`). `QCat.forgetEnrichment.equiv`: equivalence between the underlying category and the full subcategory of quasicategories.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quasicategories (∞-categories): `Quasicategory S` — a simplicial set satisfying the inner horn filling condition (all inner horns `Λ[n, k]` for `0 < k < n` have fillers). The foundational file for the Quasicategory folder.

Name: Nerve.lean
Description: The nerve of a category as a quasicategory: `nerve C` is a quasicategory, and the nerve functor `N : Cat → sSet` lands in quasicategories. Distinct from SimplicialSet/Nerve and Quasicategory/Basic.

Name: SimplicialNerve.lean
Description: The simplicial nerve of a category: the simplicial set N(C) with n-simplices being composable chains of n morphisms.

Name: SingularSet.lean
Description: The singular set of a topological space: the simplicial set Sing(X) with n-simplices being maps from the standard simplex to X.

Name: TopologicalSimplex.lean
Description: The topological standard simplex in Euclidean space: its topology, face maps, and the geometric realization functor.

Name: MooreComplex.lean
Description: The Moore complex of a simplicial abelian group: the normalized chain complex obtained by intersecting kernels of face maps.

Name: AlternatingFaceMapComplex.lean
Description: The alternating face map complex of a simplicial abelian group: the chain complex with differential equal to the alternating sum of face maps.

Name: CechNerve.lean
Description: The Cech nerve of a covering: the simplicial object recording all iterated intersections of covering sets.

Name: ExtraDegeneracy.lean
Description: Extra degeneracy maps: an extra degeneracy making a simplicial set or object contractible (split augmented).

Name: Geometry
Description: Tackles Euclidean, affine, and projective geometry, along with differential geometry concepts like manifolds, tangent bundles, and metrics. Used to describe shapes, curvature, and spatial relationships, and to prove geometric theorems. Relied on by topology, physics (general relativity), robotics, and computer graphics.

Name: Manifold
Description: Smooth manifolds: the C^n manifold typeclass, smooth maps, the tangent bundle, and differential forms.

Name: Riemannian
Description: Riemannian manifolds: smooth manifolds with a Riemannian metric (a smoothly varying positive-definite inner product on each tangent space). Defines IsRiemannianManifold, the geodesic extended distance riemannianEDist (via infimum of path lengths), and pathELength, and proves foundational metric space properties including the triangle inequality and symmetry. Advanced results such as the Hopf-Rinow theorem, Levi-Civita connection, and completeness theory are not yet present. Distinct from VectorBundle/CovariantDerivative (connections on general vector bundles).

Name: PathELength.lean
Description: Path length in Riemannian manifolds.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Riemannian manifolds: `RiemannianManifold I M`.

Name: MFDeriv
Description: The manifold derivative mfderiv: for a smooth map f : M → N between manifolds, the derivative df_x : T_x M → T_{f(x)} N as a bounded linear map between tangent spaces, defined via charts. The chain rule mfderiv (g ∘ f) = mfderiv g ∘ mfderiv f. The manifold-level analogue of FDeriv. Distinct from ContMDiff (smoothness condition without computing the derivative).

Name: SpecificFunctions.lean
Description: Manifold derivatives of specific functions: identity, composition.

Name: NormedSpace.lean
Description: Manifold derivative to normed spaces.

Name: UniqueDifferential.lean
Description: Unique differentiability in manifolds.

Name: Tangent.lean
Description: Tangent map as manifold derivative.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Manifold derivative basics: `mfderiv`, `MDifferentiableAt`.

Name: Atlas.lean
Description: Manifold derivatives in chart coordinates.

Name: FDeriv.lean
Description: Relation between `mfderiv` and `fderiv`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Manifold derivative definitions.

Name: Sheaf
Description: Sheaves on smooth manifolds: the sheaf of smooth functions C^∞(M), the structure sheaf of a smooth manifold, and sheaf-theoretic constructions (e.g., the sheaf of smooth sections of a vector bundle). Used to connect the differential-geometric and algebraic-geometric perspectives on manifolds.

Name: Smooth.lean
Description: Smooth sections of a sheaf.

Name: LocallyRingedSpace.lean
Description: Smooth manifold as a locally ringed space.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Sheaf of smooth functions on a manifold.

Name: VectorField
Description: Smooth vector fields on manifolds: sections of the tangent bundle, the Lie bracket [X, Y] of vector fields, and the Lie algebra of vector fields on M. The relationship to derivations on C^∞(M). Used in IntegralCurve (flows) and Manifold/Algebra (Lie algebra of a Lie group). Distinct from IntegralCurve (which studies curves defined by vector fields).

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pullback of vector fields.

Name: LieBracket.lean
Description: Lie bracket of vector fields.

Name: VectorBundle
Description: Vector bundles over smooth manifolds: the SmoothVectorBundle typeclass, tangent and cotangent bundles, smooth sections, the tensor product bundle. The transition functions and the Christoffel symbol representation of connections. Distinct from Topology/VectorBundle (topological vector bundles without smoothness).

Name: CovariantDerivative
Description: Covariant derivatives and linear connections on smooth vector bundles: a connection ∇ satisfies the Leibniz rule ∇_X(fs) = (Xf)s + f∇_Xs and ℝ-linearity in X. Defines IsCovariantDerivativeOn (the Leibniz and additivity axioms), the bundled CovariantDerivative, and the torsion tensor for connections on the tangent bundle. Provides the foundational definition; the curvature tensor and the Levi-Civita connection are not yet formalized. Distinct from Riemannian (the metric structure on the manifold) and DifferentialForm (the dual perspective via differential forms).

Name: Torsion.lean
Description: Torsion of a connection.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Covariant derivative on vector bundles.

Name: Riemannian.lean
Description: Riemannian metric as a tensor bundle section.

Name: Pullback.lean
Description: Pullback of vector bundles.

Name: SmoothSection.lean
Description: Smooth sections of vector bundles.

Name: Tensoriality.lean
Description: Tensoriality of vector bundle operations.

Name: Tangent.lean
Description: Tangent bundle as a vector bundle.

Name: FiberwiseLinear.lean
Description: Fiberwise linear maps between vector bundles.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Vector bundles on manifolds: `SmoothVectorBundle F E I`.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Hom bundle of vector bundles.

Name: MDifferentiable.lean
Description: Manifold-differentiable sections.

Name: LocalFrame.lean
Description: Local frame for a vector bundle.

Name: ContMDiff
Description: C^n maps between smooth manifolds (ContMDiff): the analogue of ContDiff for maps between manifolds, defined via local coordinate charts. Contains the chain rule for ContMDiff maps and the C^∞ (smooth) case. The manifold-level analogue of Analysis/Calculus/ContDiff. Distinct from MFDeriv (the derivative of manifold maps).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions of smooth maps between manifolds.

Name: NormedSpace.lean
Description: Smooth maps to normed spaces.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Constructions of smooth maps: composition, products, restrictions.

Name: Atlas.lean
Description: Smooth maps involving charts.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: C^n smooth maps between manifolds: core API.

Name: Algebra
Description: Lie groups and topological groups as smooth manifolds: a Lie group is a smooth manifold with a compatible group structure (multiplication and inversion are smooth). Contains the Lie algebra of a Lie group (tangent space at the identity), and the exponential map. Distinct from Algebra/Lie (abstract Lie algebras) — this folder requires a smooth manifold structure.

Name: SmoothFunctions.lean
Description: Ring of smooth functions `C^∞(M, ℝ)`.

Name: Structures.lean
Description: Smooth algebraic structures on manifolds.

Name: Monoid.lean
Description: Smooth monoids.

Name: LeftInvariantDerivation.lean
Description: Left-invariant derivations on Lie groups.

Name: LieGroup.lean
Description: Lie groups: `LieGroup I G` — smooth group.

Name: Instances
Description: Canonical manifold instances: ℝⁿ, Sⁿ (the n-sphere), real projective space ℝPⁿ, and other standard examples with their smooth structures. Used as test cases and building blocks. Distinct from Manifold/Algebra (Lie groups) and Manifold/IsManifold (the abstract definition).

Name: Sphere.lean
Description: Sphere Sⁿ as a smooth manifold.

Name: UnitsOfNormedAlgebra.lean
Description: Units of a normed algebra as a Lie group.

Name: Real.lean
Description: ℝⁿ as a smooth manifold.

Name: Icc.lean
Description: The interval `[a,b]` as a smooth manifold with corners.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Quotient manifolds.

Name: IsManifold
Description: The IsManifold typeclass: a topological space M is a C^n manifold modeled on a space E if it has an atlas of charts homeomorphic to open sets of E with C^n transition maps. Contains the definition, basic instances, and the model space axioms. The foundational definition on which all manifold theory in Mathlib is built.

Name: InteriorBoundary.lean
Description: Interior and boundary of a manifold with corners.

Name: ExtChartAt.lean
Description: Extended chart at a point.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: IsManifold predicate: conditions for a charted space to be a manifold.

Name: IntegralCurve
Description: Integral curves of vector fields: a smooth curve γ : ℝ → M satisfying γ'(t) = X(γ(t)) for a vector field X on M. Existence and uniqueness (Picard-Lindelöf on manifolds). The flow of a vector field. Used in the theory of ODEs on manifolds and in Lie group theory. Distinct from VectorField (vector fields themselves) and Analysis/ODE (ODEs in Banach spaces).

Name: Transform.lean
Description: Transformations of integral curves.

Name: UniformTime.lean
Description: Uniform time bounds for integral curves.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Integral curves of vector fields.

Name: ExistUnique.lean
Description: Existence and uniqueness of integral curves.

Name: Metrizable.lean
Description: Metrizability of manifolds: a smooth manifold modelled on a metrizable space is itself metrizable. Distinct from Topology/Metrizable/Basic and Manifold/ChartedSpace.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for manifolds: `𝓘(ℝ, n)` for the model with corners, and related notation used throughout the Manifold folder. Contains no mathematical theorems.

Name: LocalSourceTargetProperty.lean
Description: Local source-target properties: properties of maps `f : M → N` that depend on local data near both a source point and its image. Distinct from Manifold/LocalInvariantProperties.

Name: LocalDiffeomorph.lean
Description: Local diffeomorphisms: maps that are diffeomorphisms onto their image locally. The local form of immersions (rank theorem). Distinct from Manifold/Diffeomorph.

Name: LocalInvariantProperties.lean
Description: Local invariant properties of smooth maps: properties that can be checked in charts and are preserved under the structure groupoid. The abstract framework for defining properties of manifold maps. Distinct from Manifold/HasGroupoid.

Name: PartitionOfUnity.lean
Description: Partitions of unity on smooth manifolds: existence of a smooth partition of unity subordinate to any open cover of a smooth manifold (uses paracompactness). Distinct from Manifold/BumpFunction.

Name: StructureGroupoid.lean
Description: Structure groupoids: `StructureGroupoid H` — a pseudo-group of homeomorphisms of `H` used to define the class of manifolds (smooth, complex, etc.). Distinct from Manifold/HasGroupoid.

Name: WhitneyEmbedding.lean
Description: Whitney embedding theorem: every smooth `n`-manifold embeds smoothly in `ℝ^{2n}`. Distinct from Manifold/SmoothEmbedding.

Name: SmoothEmbedding.lean
Description: Smooth embeddings: `SmoothEmbedding I J f` — an immersion that is also a topological embedding. Whitney embedding theorem context. Distinct from Manifold/Immersion.

Name: PoincareConjecture.lean
Description: Poincaré conjecture statement in Lean: the statement that every compact simply connected 3-manifold is homeomorphic to S³. Active foundational area.

Name: SmoothApprox.lean
Description: Smooth approximation on manifolds: every continuous function on a smooth manifold can be approximated by smooth functions. Distinct from Manifold/PartitionOfUnity.

Name: Immersion.lean
Description: Immersions and submersions of manifolds: `Immersion I J f` (injective differential) and `Submersion I J f` (surjective differential). Distinct from Manifold/SmoothEmbedding.

Name: Complex.lean
Description: Complex manifolds: the complex structure groupoid and holomorphic maps between complex manifolds. Distinct from Manifold/StructureGroupoid and Analysis/Complex/Basic.

Name: ConformalGroupoid.lean
Description: Conformal groupoid: the pseudo-group of conformal maps on `ℝⁿ`, used to define conformal manifolds. Distinct from Manifold/StructureGroupoid and Analysis/Complex/Conformal.

Name: ChartedSpace.lean
Description: Charted spaces: `ChartedSpace H M` — a topological space `M` with an atlas of homeomorphisms to the model space `H`. The core structure for manifold theory. Distinct from Manifold/HasGroupoid.

Name: Bordism.lean
Description: Bordism theory on smooth manifolds: a relation between manifolds defined by cobordisms. Active area; exact scope requires verification.

Name: BumpFunction.lean
Description: Smooth bump functions on manifolds: `SmoothBumpFunction I x` — a smooth function supported in a chart neighbourhood of `x`, equal to 1 near `x`. Used for partitions of unity. Distinct from Manifold/PartitionOfUnity.

Name: ContMDiffMap.lean
Description: Smooth maps between manifolds: `ContMDiff I J n f` — smooth maps of class `C^n` between manifolds modelled on `I` and `J`. Basic instances: composition, identity, constant. Distinct from Manifold/ContMDiffMFDeriv.

Name: GroupLieAlgebra.lean
Description: Lie algebra of a Lie group: the Lie algebra as the tangent space at the identity, with the Lie bracket from commutator of left-invariant vector fields. Distinct from Manifold/DerivationBundle.

Name: HasGroupoid.lean
Description: Manifolds with a structure groupoid: `HasGroupoid M G` — the atlas is compatible with the pseudo-group `G` (e.g., smooth or complex structure). Distinct from Manifold/ChartedSpace and Manifold/StructureGroupoid.

Name: Diffeomorph.lean
Description: Diffeomorphisms: `Diffeomorph I J M N` — smooth bijections with smooth inverse between manifolds. The group of diffeomorphisms. Distinct from Manifold/LocalDiffeomorph.

Name: ContMDiffMFDeriv.lean
Description: Fréchet derivative vs. manifold derivative: `mfderiv I J f x` — the tangent map of a smooth map between manifolds, and its relationship with `fderiv` in charts. Distinct from Manifold/ContMDiffMap.

Name: DerivationBundle.lean
Description: Derivation bundle (tangent sheaf): the sheaf of smooth derivations `Derivation ℝ (C^∞ M) (C^∞ M)` as a model for the tangent bundle. Distinct from Manifold/ContMDiffMFDeriv.

Name: Polygon
Description: Polygons in the Euclidean plane: area, Pick's theorem, and convex polygons.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Polygons in the Euclidean plane: `Polygon` — an ordered sequence of vertices with straight-line sides. The signed area formula (shoelace formula) and basic properties. Distinct from Geometry/Euclidean/Triangle.

Name: RingedSpace
Description: Ringed spaces: topological spaces equipped with a sheaf of rings, the common generalization of manifolds and schemes.

Name: PresheafedSpace
Description: Presheafed spaces: pairs (X, O_X) of a topological space X and a presheaf O_X of rings (or types) on X. Morphisms are continuous maps with a compatible presheaf map. The coarsest geometric structure — less structured than ringed spaces (which require sheaves) and locally ringed spaces (which require local rings). Used as the ambient category in the definition of schemes.

Name: HasColimits.lean
Description: Presheafed spaces have colimits.

Name: Gluing.lean
Description: Gluing presheafed spaces.

Name: LocallyRingedSpace
Description: Locally ringed spaces: ringed spaces (X, O_X) where every stalk O_{X,x} is a local ring. Morphisms must be local ring homomorphisms on stalks. Both smooth manifolds (stalks = local smooth functions) and schemes (stalks = local rings of schemes) are locally ringed spaces. The categorical foundation for both differential geometry and algebraic geometry. Distinct from PresheafedSpace (no local ring condition on stalks).

Name: ResidueField.lean
Description: Residue field at a point of a locally ringed space.

Name: HasColimits.lean
Description: Locally ringed spaces have colimits.

Name: PresheafedSpace.lean
Description: Presheafed spaces: `PresheafedSpace C` — a topological space with a presheaf of objects from a category `C`. The category of presheafed spaces and functoriality. Distinct from RingedSpace/Basic.

Name: SheafedSpace.lean
Description: Sheafed spaces: `SheafedSpace C` — a presheafed space where the presheaf is a sheaf. Morphisms and the forgetful functor to presheafed spaces. Distinct from RingedSpace/PresheafedSpace.

Name: Stalks.lean
Description: Stalks of ringed spaces: `RingedSpace.stalk X x` — the colimit of sections over open sets containing `x`. The stalk map of a morphism and its relationship to local ring maps. Distinct from RingedSpace/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ringed spaces: `RingedSpace` — a topological space equipped with a sheaf of rings `𝒪_X`. Morphisms of ringed spaces (continuous maps with ring maps on sections). Distinct from RingedSpace/LocallyRingedSpace.

Name: LocallyRingedSpace.lean
Description: Locally ringed spaces: `LocallyRingedSpace` — a ringed space where each stalk `𝒪_{X,x}` is a local ring. Morphisms respect the maximal ideals. The category of locally ringed spaces. Distinct from RingedSpace/Basic.

Name: OpenImmersion.lean
Description: Open immersions of ringed spaces: a morphism `f : X → Y` of ringed spaces that is a homeomorphism onto an open set with isomorphic structure sheaf. Distinct from RingedSpace/Basic and AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/OpenImmersion.

Name: Group
Description: Geometric group theory: groups acting on metric spaces, the word metric, Cayley graphs, and quasi-isometry invariants.

Name: Growth
Description: Growth of groups: the growth function γ_S(n) = |{g ∈ G | |g|_S ≤ n}| for a finite generating set S. Polynomial growth (Gromov's theorem: iff virtually nilpotent), exponential growth, and intermediate growth (Grigorchuk's group). Used in geometric group theory. Distinct from GroupTheory/Rank (generators without a metric).

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation for group growth theory.

Name: QuotientInter.lean
Description: Growth via quotients and intersection.

Name: LinearLowerBound.lean
Description: Linear lower bound on group growth.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation overview for the Geometry/Group folder: geometry of groups and group-theoretic geometry (Cayley graphs, Schreier graphs). Markdown documentation file.

Name: Convex
Description: Convex geometry: convex hulls, faces, supporting hyperplanes, polytopes, and the Krein-Milman theorem.

Name: Cone
Description: Convex cones in a real vector space: pointed cones, the dual cone C* = {y | ⟨x,y⟩ ≥ 0 ∀ x ∈ C}, and the bipolar theorem C** = cl(C). Farkas' lemma: b ∈ cone(A) iff ∀ y ≥ 0, Aᵀy ≥ 0 ⟹ bᵀy ≥ 0. Used in optimization and duality theory. Distinct from Analysis/Convex/Cone (same concept in an analytic normed-space setting with topology).

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation for Geometry/Convex/Cone.

Name: Simplicial.lean
Description: Simplicial cones: cones generated by linearly independent rays.

Name: TensorProduct.lean
Description: Tensor products of cones.

Name: Pointed.lean
Description: Pointed cones: cones containing 0.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Convex cones in affine geometry.

Name: Dual.lean
Description: Dual cones in Euclidean geometry.

Name: DualFinite.lean
Description: Dual cones in finite-dimensional spaces.

Name: README.md
Description: Documentation overview for the Geometry/Convex folder: describes the organisation and scope of convex geometry results in the Geometry hierarchy (as distinct from Analysis/Convex). Markdown documentation file.

Name: Diffeology
Description: Diffeological spaces: a generalization of smooth manifolds that handles quotients and infinite-dimensional spaces gracefully.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Diffeological spaces: a generalisation of smooth manifolds where smoothness of maps is defined via plots (smooth maps from open subsets of ℝⁿ). Active area; exact scope requires verification.

Name: Euclidean
Description: Euclidean geometry: distance, angles, triangles, circles, and classical results (Pythagorean theorem, law of cosines) in Euclidean space.

Name: Sphere
Description: Spheres and circles in Euclidean space: the circumscribed circle of a triangle (Circumsphere), the inscribed circle (Insphere), and the nine-point circle. Ptolemy's theorem. The sphere Sⁿ⁻¹ ⊆ ℝⁿ as a metric space. Distinct from Angle (angle theorems) and Inversion (geometric inversions).

Name: Ptolemy.lean
Description: Ptolemy's theorem on cyclic quadrilaterals.

Name: SecondInter.lean
Description: Second intersection of a line with a sphere.

Name: Tangent.lean
Description: Tangent lines and circles.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Sphere in Euclidean space.

Name: OrthRadius.lean
Description: Orthogonal radii of a sphere.

Name: Power.lean
Description: Power of a point with respect to a sphere.

Name: Volume
Description: Volumes and areas in Euclidean space: the volume of a simplex, the Gram determinant formula for the volume of a parallelotope, and Pick's theorem (area of a lattice polygon). Used in the geometry of numbers. Distinct from Geometry/Polygon (basic polygon definitions) and MeasureTheory (Lebesgue measure).

Name: Measure.lean
Description: Volume measure in Euclidean space.

Name: Angle
Description: Angles in Euclidean space: the angle ∠BAC between vectors, the law of cosines, and the inscribed angle theorem. Oriented angles (using Complex numbers) and directed angles modulo π. Ptolemy's theorem and other circle angle theorems. Distinct from Sphere (circles and circumscribed circles) and Inversion (circle inversions).

Name: Unoriented
Description: Unoriented angles in the Euclidean plane: the angle ∠(u, v) = arccos(⟨u,v⟩/(‖u‖‖v‖)) taking values in [0, π]. The law of cosines in terms of unoriented angles. The triangle angle sum π. Distinct from Oriented (which has a sign and is better for inscribed angle theorems involving orientation of the plane).

Name: Projection.lean
Description: Projection formula for angles.

Name: RightAngle.lean
Description: Right angle conditions.

Name: TriangleInequality.lean
Description: Triangle inequality for angles.

Name: CrossProduct.lean
Description: Cross product and angle.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: Unoriented angles in affine spaces.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Unoriented angle: `EuclideanGeometry.angle`.

Name: Conformal.lean
Description: Conformal maps and unoriented angles.

Name: Oriented
Description: Oriented angles in the Euclidean plane: angles measured with a sign (+ counterclockwise, - clockwise) and taken modulo 2π (or modulo π for projective lines). The oriented angle ∡(u, v) using the complex number argument. Inscribed angle theorem for oriented angles. Distinct from Unoriented (which takes values in [0, π] with no sign information).

Name: RightAngle.lean
Description: Right angles in oriented geometry.

Name: Rotation.lean
Description: Rotations and oriented angles.

Name: Projection.lean
Description: Projections and oriented angles.

Name: Affine.lean
Description: Oriented angles in affine spaces.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Oriented angles: `EuclideanGeometry.oangle`.

Name: Sphere.lean
Description: Inscribed angle theorem.

Name: Incenter.lean
Description: Incenter via angle bisectors.

Name: Bisector.lean
Description: Angle bisector theorem.

Name: Inversion
Description: Circle inversion in the Euclidean plane: the map i(P) = r²/|OP|² · P/|OP| which swaps the interior and exterior of a circle. Inversions map circles and lines to circles and lines. The cross-ratio is preserved. Used in classical geometry proofs and in the Poincaré disc model (Complex/UnitDisc). Distinct from Angle (angles) and Sphere (spherical geometry).

Name: ImageHyperplane.lean
Description: Image of a hyperplane under inversion.

Name: Calculus.lean
Description: Differentiability of inversion.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Circle inversion in the Euclidean plane: the map x ↦ r²x/|x|² swapping the interior and exterior of a circle of radius r. Inversions preserve circles-and-lines as a class and preserve angles (conformal). Ptolemy's theorem as a consequence: for a cyclic quadrilateral ABCD, |AC|·|BD| = |AB|·|CD| + |AD|·|BC|. Used in classical geometry proofs and in the construction of the Poincaré disc model of hyperbolic geometry.

Name: SignedDist.lean
Description: Signed distance to a hyperplane in Euclidean space: `EuclideanGeometry.signedDist h p` — positive on one side, negative on the other. Distinct from Euclidean/Projection.

Name: Projection.lean
Description: Orthogonal projection in Euclidean affine spaces: the foot of the perpendicular from a point to a subspace, and its properties. Distinct from InnerProductSpace/Subspace and Euclidean/Basic.

Name: PerpBisector.lean
Description: Perpendicular bisector: the locus of points equidistant from two given points. The circumcenter lies on all three perpendicular bisectors. Distinct from Euclidean/Projection and Euclidean/Circumcenter.

Name: Triangle.lean
Description: Triangles in Euclidean geometry: basic triangle lemmas (angle sum = π, law of cosines, law of sines), the Pythagorean theorem. Distinct from Euclidean/Congruence and Euclidean/Similarity.

Name: Simplex.lean
Description: Simplices in Euclidean affine spaces: `Affine.Simplex` — an affinely independent set of points. Circumsphere, orthocentric tetrahedra. Distinct from Euclidean/Circumcenter.

Name: Similarity.lean
Description: Similar triangles in Euclidean geometry: `EuclideanGeometry.IsSimilar` — same angles, proportional sides. Distinct from Euclidean/Congruence.

Name: NinePointCircle.lean
Description: Nine-point circle: the circle passing through the midpoints of the sides, the feet of the altitudes, and the midpoints from vertices to orthocenter. Distinct from Euclidean/Circumcenter.

Name: Circumcenter.lean
Description: Circumcenter and circumradius: the circumcenter of a simplex (equidistant from all vertices), and the circumradius formula. Distinct from Euclidean/Simplex and Euclidean/NinePointCircle.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Euclidean affine spaces: `EuclideanGeometry.dist`, `EuclideanGeometry.angle`, and the basic metric geometry in an affine space with an inner product. Distinct from Euclidean/Projection.

Name: Altitude.lean
Description: Altitudes of a triangle in Euclidean space: the altitude from a vertex is the perpendicular from that vertex to the opposite side. The orthocenter (intersection of the three altitudes). Distinct from Euclidean/Triangle and Euclidean/MongePoint.

Name: MongePoint.lean
Description: Monge point of a tetrahedron: the generalisation of the orthocenter to 3D, where the six bimedians of a tetrahedron meet. Distinct from Euclidean/Altitude.

Name: Incenter.lean
Description: Incenter and inradius: the incenter of a triangle (equidistant from all sides) and the inradius formula `r = Area / s` where `s` is the semi-perimeter. Distinct from Euclidean/Circumcenter.

Name: Congruence.lean
Description: Congruent triangles (SSS, SAS, ASA): conditions for two triangles to be congruent (isometric) in Euclidean geometry. Distinct from Euclidean/Triangle and Euclidean/Similarity.

Name: FieldTheory
Description: Extends ring and group theory with fields, finite fields, algebraic extensions, Galois theory, and splitting fields. Used to solve polynomial equations by radicals, classify field extensions, and prove fundamental theorems of algebra. Relied on by number theory, algebraic geometry, and cryptology.

Name: Normal
Description: Normal field extensions: extensions where every irreducible polynomial with one root splits completely.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Normal K L`: a field extension is normal iff it is the splitting field of a (possibly infinite) set of polynomials. Distinct from Normal/Basic (API).

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Normal closure: `normalClosure K L` — the smallest normal extension of `K` containing `L`. Distinct from Normal/Basic and Galois/GaloisClosure.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Normal extensions: `Normal K L` — every irreducible polynomial over `K` with a root in `L` splits completely in `L`. Equivalently, `L` is the splitting field of some polynomial. Distinct from Normal/Closure.

Name: MvRatFunc
Description: Multivariate rational function fields k(X_1,...,X_n): the field of fractions of the multivariate polynomial ring.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: Transcendence degree via multivariate rational functions: `MvRatFunc.rank σ K` — the number of algebraically independent generators. Distinct from Dimension/DivisionRing and RatFunc/Basic.

Name: Minpoly
Description: Minimal polynomials of algebraic elements: the monic irreducible polynomial that an element satisfies over a base field.

Name: IsConjRoot.lean
Description: `IsConjRoot K x y` — `x` and `y` are conjugates over `K` (have the same minimal polynomial). Distinct from Minpoly/ConjRootClass.

Name: IsIntegrallyClosed.lean
Description: Minimal polynomial over integrally closed rings: `minpoly R x` for `R` integrally closed coincides with the characteristic polynomial when `x` is integral. Distinct from Minpoly/Basic.

Name: MinpolyDiv.lean
Description: Division by the minimal polynomial: the polynomial `minpolyDiv R x = (X - x) / minpoly R x` and its properties. Distinct from Minpoly/Basic.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Minimal polynomial for finite fields: the minimal polynomial of elements in finite field extensions, and Frobenius action. Distinct from Minpoly/Field and FieldTheory/Finite/Extension.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Minimal polynomial: `minpoly R x` — the monic polynomial of least degree in `R[X]` with `x` as a root. Basic API: `minpoly.dvd`, `minpoly.irreducible` over a field. Distinct from Minpoly/Field.

Name: ConjRootClass.lean
Description: Conjugate root class: the set of all roots of `minpoly K x` in a splitting field. Distinct from Minpoly/IsConjRoot.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Minimal polynomial over a field: `minpoly K x` is irreducible when `K` is a field and `x` is algebraic, and `[K(x) : K] = deg(minpoly K x)`. Distinct from Minpoly/Basic.

Name: SplittingField
Description: Splitting fields: the smallest extension in which a polynomial factors completely into linear factors.

Name: IsSplittingField.lean
Description: `IsSplittingField K L p` — `L` is a splitting field of `p` over `K`: `p` splits in `L[X]` and `L = K(roots p)`. Uniqueness up to isomorphism. Distinct from SplittingField/Construction.

Name: Construction.lean
Description: Construction of splitting fields: `SplittingField p` — the explicit construction of the splitting field of a polynomial `p` by iteratively adjoining roots. Distinct from SplittingField/IsSplittingField.

Name: RatFunc
Description: Rational function fields k(X): the field of fractions of k[X], with partial fractions and connections to algebraic curves.

Name: IntermediateField.lean
Description: Intermediate fields of `K(X)/K`: every intermediate field is either `K` or `K(f)` for some `f ∈ K(X)` (Lüroth's theorem). Distinct from RatFunc/Luroth.

Name: Luroth.lean
Description: Lüroth's theorem: every intermediate field of `K(X)/K` is of the form `K(f)` for some non-constant `f ∈ K(X)`. Distinct from RatFunc/IntermediateField.

Name: Valuation.lean
Description: Valuations on `RatFunc K`: the discrete valuation `v_p(f)` at an irreducible polynomial `p`, and the degree valuation `v_∞`. Distinct from RatFunc/Degree.

Name: Degree.lean
Description: Degree of a rational function: `RatFunc.degree f = deg(num f) - deg(denom f)` as an integer-valued degree function. Distinct from RatFunc/Basic.

Name: AsPolynomial.lean
Description: Rational functions as ratios of polynomials: `RatFunc.num` and `RatFunc.denom`, the reduced form, and `RatFunc.induction`. Distinct from RatFunc/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Rational function field `RatFunc K`: the field of fractions of `K[X]`, with explicit `num`/`denom` representation. Operations: addition, multiplication, evaluation. Distinct from RatFunc/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `RatFunc K`: as the field of fractions `FractionRing K[X]`. The `Field` instance and basic coercions. Distinct from RatFunc/Basic (API).

Name: PurelyInseparable
Description: Purely inseparable extensions: extensions in characteristic p where every element has p-power degree over the base field.

Name: PerfectClosure.lean
Description: Perfect closure: `PerfectClosure K` — the smallest perfect field containing `K`, obtained by adjoining all `p^n`-th roots. Distinct from PurelyInseparable/Basic.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Tower law for purely inseparable extensions: purely inseparable + separable = general separability condition. Distinct from PurelyInseparable/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Purely inseparable extensions: `IsPurelyInseparable K L` — every element `x ∈ L` satisfies `x^{p^n} ∈ K` for some `n`. Distinct from PurelyInseparable/Exponent.

Name: Exponent.lean
Description: Exponent of a purely inseparable extension: the minimum `n` with `L^{p^n} ⊆ K`. Distinct from PurelyInseparable/Basic.

Name: Galois
Description: Galois theory: the fundamental theorem relating intermediate fields of a Galois extension to subgroups of the Galois group.

Name: NormalBasis.lean
Description: Normal basis theorem: for a finite Galois extension `L/K`, there exists `α ∈ L` such that `{σ(α) | σ ∈ Gal(L/K)}` is a `K`-basis of `L`. Distinct from Galois/Basic.

Name: IsGaloisGroup.lean
Description: IsGaloisGroup predicate: `IsGaloisGroup K L G` — `G` acts as the Galois group of `L/K`. Distinct from Galois/Basic.

Name: Profinite.lean
Description: Profinite Galois groups: the absolute Galois group `Gal(K̄/K)` as a profinite group, with the Krull topology. Distinct from Galois/Infinite and Topology/Separation/Profinite.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for Galois groups: `Gal(L/K)` notation and related scoped notation used throughout the Galois folder.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Galois theory: `IsGalois K L` — a field extension that is normal and separable. The main Galois correspondence: `IntermediateField K L ↔ Subgroup (Gal L K)`. Distinct from Galois/GaloisClosure.

Name: Abelian.lean
Description: Abelian Galois extensions: `IsAbelianGalois E F` — Galois extensions with abelian Galois group. Kronecker–Weber theorem context (every abelian extension of ℚ is cyclotomic). Distinct from Galois/Basic.

Name: Infinite.lean
Description: Infinite Galois theory: `InfiniteGaloisTheory K L` — the profinite topology on `Gal(L/K)` for infinite Galois extensions and the Krull topology. Distinct from Galois/Profinite.

Name: GaloisClosure.lean
Description: Galois closure: `normalClosure K L` — the smallest Galois extension of `K` containing `L`. Distinct from Galois/Basic and Normal/Closure.

Name: Finite
Description: Finite fields: the classification theorem (GF(p^n) for prime p and n >= 1), their automorphism groups, and the Frobenius.

Name: Polynomial.lean
Description: Polynomials over finite fields: every function `𝔽_q → 𝔽_q` is a polynomial (since `|𝔽_q[X]/(X^q - X)| = q^q`). Chevalley–Warning theorem. Distinct from Finite/GaloisField.

Name: Trace.lean
Description: Trace of a finite field extension: `Finset.sum (traceForm 𝔽_{qⁿ}/𝔽_q)` — the absolute trace and its non-degeneracy. Distinct from Finite/Extension and NumberField/Norm.

Name: Valuation.lean
Description: Discrete valuation on a finite field extension: the unique valuation on `𝔽_q((t))` and its residue field. Distinct from Finite/GaloisField and RatFunc/Valuation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of finite fields: the structure theorem that every finite field has order pⁿ for a prime p, and the existence and uniqueness of GF(pⁿ). The multiplicative group of a finite field is cyclic. Contains ZMod.units_pow_card_sub_one_eq_one (Fermat's little theorem aᵖ⁻¹ ≡ 1 mod p) and Euler's generalization a^φ(n) ≡ 1 (mod n) for gcd(a,n) = 1, the basis of RSA encryption.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Extensions of finite fields: all extensions of `𝔽_q` are cyclic Galois, and there is a unique extension of degree `n` for each `n`. Distinct from Finite/GaloisField and Galois/Basic.

Name: GaloisField.lean
Description: `GaloisField p n` — the finite field with `pⁿ` elements as a specific construction. `GaloisField p 1 ≅ ZMod p` and the splitting field of `X^{pⁿ} - X`. Distinct from Finite/Extension.

Name: Differential
Description: Differential fields and Picard-Vessiot theory: fields with a derivation and the Galois theory of linear differential equations.

Name: Liouville.lean
Description: Liouville's theorem on integration in finite terms: an elementary function has an elementary antiderivative only in restricted circumstances. The Risch algorithm context. Distinct from Differential/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Differential algebra: `Derivation R A M` — an `R`-linear map `d : A → M` satisfying the Leibniz rule `d(ab) = a·d(b) + d(a)·b`. The module of derivations and the universal derivation `Ω_{A/R}`. Distinct from Differential/Liouville.

Name: IsRealClosed
Description: Real closed fields: ordered fields satisfying the intermediate value theorem for polynomials, the algebraic abstraction of R.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `IsRealClosed R` — every positive element is a square and every odd-degree polynomial has a root. Artin–Schreier theorem: a field is real closed iff it is ordered and its algebraic closure is a degree-2 extension. Distinct from IsAlgClosed/Basic.

Name: IsAlgClosed
Description: Algebraically closed fields: fields where every nonconstant polynomial has a root, the algebraic abstraction of C.

Name: Classification.lean
Description: Classification of algebraically closed fields: two algebraically closed fields of the same uncountable cardinality and characteristic are isomorphic (Steinitz). Distinct from IsAlgClosed/Basic.

Name: Spectrum.lean
Description: Spectrum over algebraically closed fields: every endomorphism of a finite-dimensional vector space over an algebraically closed field has an eigenvalue. Distinct from IsAlgClosed/Basic and LinearAlgebra/Eigenspace/Basic.

Name: AlgebraicClosure.lean
Description: Algebraic closure: `AlgebraicClosure K` — the algebraic closure of a field `K`, constructed as the quotient of a polynomial ring by a maximal ideal. Distinct from IsAlgClosed/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: `IsAlgClosed K` — every non-constant polynomial over `K` has a root. Proves `K` is algebraically closed iff every polynomial splits, iff `K` has no proper algebraic extensions. Distinct from IsAlgClosed/AlgebraicClosure.

Name: IntermediateField
Description: Intermediate fields of a field extension: the lattice of subfields between the base field and the top field.

Name: Adjoin
Description: Adjoining elements to a field: K(α) as the smallest intermediate field containing α, the isomorphism K(α) ≅ K[X]/(minpoly α) for algebraic α, and K(S) for a set S. The tower K ⊆ K(α) ⊆ K(α,β) ⊆ … and the degree [K(α):K] = deg(minpoly α). The primary tool for constructing field extensions step by step.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for field adjunction.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Adjoining elements to a field: `K(α)`.

Name: Algebra.lean
Description: Algebra structure of adjoined elements.

Name: ExtendRight.lean
Description: Extending elements to larger intermediate fields: `IntermediateField.extendRight` — restricting the codomain of an embedding. Distinct from IntermediateField/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Intermediate fields: `IntermediateField K L` — subfields of `L` containing `K`. The lattice of intermediate fields and `IntermediateField.adjoin K s`. Distinct from IntermediateField/Algebraic.

Name: Algebraic.lean
Description: Algebraic intermediate fields: if `E/F` is algebraic then every intermediate field is algebraic over `F`. Distinct from IntermediateField/Basic.

Name: Perfect.lean
Description: Perfect fields: fields where every irreducible polynomial is separable, including all characteristic-0 fields and finite fields.

Name: PerfectClosure.lean
Description: The perfect closure of a field: the smallest perfect extension, constructed by adjoining p-th roots of all elements.

Name: PolynomialGaloisGroup.lean
Description: The Galois group of a polynomial: the automorphism group of its splitting field, and the action on roots.

Name: NormalizedTrace.lean
Description: The normalized trace in a field extension: the trace divided by the degree, used in explicit computations.

Name: KummerPolynomial.lean
Description: Kummer polynomials: polynomials of the form X^n - a, whose splitting fields are Kummer extensions.

Name: Laurent.lean
Description: Laurent series fields k((X)): formal Laurent series with finitely many negative terms, the completion of k(X) at X = 0.

Name: LinearDisjoint.lean
Description: Linearly disjoint field extensions: two subextensions L and M are linearly disjoint if their tensor product over K is a domain.

Name: PrimeField.lean
Description: Prime fields: the unique minimal subfield of a field (Q or F_p), with the prime subfield inclusion map.

Name: SeparableDegree.lean
Description: The separable degree of a field extension: the number of K-algebra maps to an algebraic closure, and its multiplicativity in towers.

Name: SeparablyGenerated.lean
Description: Separably generated extensions: finitely generated extensions that have a separating transcendence basis.

Name: Tower.lean
Description: Tower law for field extensions: [L:K] = [L:M][M:K] and the analogous formula for separable and inseparable degrees.

Name: SeparableClosure.lean
Description: The separable closure: the largest separable extension inside an algebraic closure, with its Galois group.

Name: PrimitiveElement.lean
Description: The primitive element theorem: a finite separable extension K contained in L is simple, i.e., L = K(alpha) for some alpha.

Name: Relrank.lean
Description: The relative rank of field extensions: the cardinality of a transcendence basis and its independence from the choice.

Name: Separable.lean
Description: Separable polynomials and separable extensions: polynomials with no repeated roots and extensions generated by them.

Name: KummerExtension.lean
Description: Kummer extensions: cyclic Galois extensions L/K of degree n obtained by adjoining an n-th root of an element of K.

Name: CardinalEmb.lean
Description: Cardinality of embeddings of field extensions: counting K-algebra homomorphisms from L to an algebraic closure.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of field extensions: the size of L equals the max of the size of K and the degree, with consequences.

Name: ChevalleyWarning.lean
Description: The Chevalley-Warning theorem: a system of polynomials over F_p with total degree less than n variables has p-divisibly many solutions.

Name: AxGrothendieck.lean
Description: The Ax-Grothendieck theorem: an injective polynomial map from an algebraically closed field to itself is surjective.

Name: AbelRuffini.lean
Description: The Abel-Ruffini theorem: polynomials of degree 5 or more are not generally solvable by radicals, proved via solvable groups.

Name: AbsoluteGaloisGroup.lean
Description: The absolute Galois group Gal(K-bar/K): the profinite group of automorphisms of the algebraic closure, its topology.

Name: AlgebraicClosure.lean
Description: The algebraic closure: existence (every nonconstant polynomial has a root) and uniqueness up to isomorphism.

Name: Extension.lean
Description: Field extensions and their basic properties: degree, algebraic and transcendental elements, and the degree formula.

Name: IsSepClosed.lean
Description: Separably closed fields: fields with no proper separable algebraic extensions, and the separable closure.
