Name: JacobsonNoether.lean
Description: The Jacobson-Noether theorem: a purely inseparable algebraic extension of degree > 1 cannot be generated by a single element.

Name: KrullTopology.lean
Description: The Krull topology on Galois groups: the profinite topology making Gal(L/K) compact, totally disconnected, and Hausdorff.

Name: IsPerfectClosure.lean
Description: Characterizations of the perfect closure: universal properties and existence proofs.

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: Finiteness results in field theory: finite extensions are algebraic, and algebraic extensions of finitely generated fields.

Name: Fixed.lean
Description: Fixed fields of group actions: the subfield fixed by a group G of automorphisms, with the Artin theorem.

Name: Isaacs.lean
Description: Isaacs' theorem on polynomials over finite fields whose roots lie in a unique extension.

Name: InformationTheory
Description: Studies entropy, mutual information, coding theory, and channel capacity. Used to quantify uncertainty, compress data optimally, and guarantee reliable communication over noisy channels. Relied on by computer science, electrical engineering, statistics, and machine learning.

Name: KullbackLeibler
Description: The Kullback-Leibler divergence (relative entropy) between probability measures: definition, non-negativity (Gibbs inequality), and applications.

Name: KLFun.lean
Description: The KL function `klFun`: the function `(p, q) ↦ p * log(p/q)` appearing in the KL integrand, its convexity, continuity, and the variational formula `kl μ ν = sup_f (∫ f dμ - log ∫ exp f dν)`. Distinct from KullbackLeibler/Basic.

Name: ChainRule.lean
Description: Chain rule for KL divergence: `kl (μ ⊗ κ) (ν ⊗ η) = kl μ ν + ∫ kl (κ x) (η x) dμ`. Decomposition of the KL divergence of a joint distribution. Distinct from KullbackLeibler/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Kullback–Leibler divergence: `MeasureTheory.kl μ ν = ∫ log (dμ/dν) dμ` (matching the MeasureTheory/Measure/LogLikelihoodRatio definition). Non-negativity (Gibbs inequality), finiteness conditions, and `kl μ ν = 0 ↔ μ = ν`. Distinct from KullbackLeibler/ChainRule.

Name: Coding
Description: Coding theory: Shannon entropy, channel capacity, the noisy channel coding theorem, and error-correcting codes.

Name: UniquelyDecodable.lean
Description: Uniquely decodable codes: a code `C ⊆ Alphabet^*` is uniquely decodable if every string has at most one decomposition as a concatenation of codewords. The Sardinas–Patterson algorithm. Distinct from Coding/KraftMcMillan.

Name: KraftMcMillan.lean
Description: Kraft–McMillan inequality: for a uniquely decodable code with codeword lengths `lᵢ` over an alphabet of size `D`, `∑ D^{-lᵢ} ≤ 1`. Conversely, if `∑ D^{-lᵢ} ≤ 1` then a prefix-free code with those lengths exists. Distinct from Coding/UniquelyDecodable.

Name: Hamming.lean
Description: Hamming distance between finite sequences: the number of positions where they differ, and the Hamming ball of radius r.

Name: GroupTheory
Description: Formalizes groups, subgroups, cosets, normal subgroups, group actions, and Sylow theorems. Used to analyze symmetries, classify finite simple groups, and understand permutation structures. Relied on by algebra, number theory, geometry, combinatorics, and crystallography.

Name: OreLocalization
Description: Ore localization for groups and monoids: inverting a multiplicative set in a noncommutative setting.

Name: OreSet.lean
Description: Ore sets: `OreSet S R` — conditions on `S ⊆ R` (left Ore: for any `r ∈ R`, `s ∈ S`, ∃ `s' ∈ S`, `r' ∈ R`, `s'r = r's`) ensuring localisation exists. Distinct from OreLocalization/Basic.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of Ore localisations. Distinct from OreLocalization/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ore localisation: `OreLocalization S R` — inverting a left Ore set `S` in a (non-commutative) ring `R`, giving fractions `s⁻¹r`. Distinct from OreLocalization/OreSet.

Name: Perm
Description: Permutation groups: the symmetric group Sym(alpha), cycle type, the sign homomorphism, and the alternating group.

Name: Cycle
Description: Cycle decomposition of permutations: writing a permutation σ as a product of disjoint cycles, the cycle type (partition of n), the order of σ (lcm of cycle lengths), and support. The sign of a permutation as (-1)^(number of even-length cycles). Used in combinatorics (cycle index), group theory (conjugacy classes in Sₙ), and in Linear/Algebra/Determinant.

Name: PossibleTypes.lean
Description: Possible cycle types for permutations.

Name: Type.lean
Description: Cycle type as a multiset.

Name: Factors.lean
Description: Disjoint cycle factorisation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cycle permutations: `Equiv.Perm.IsCycle`.

Name: Concrete.lean
Description: Concrete cycle computations.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Permutations of `Option α`: the extension of `Equiv.Perm α` to `Equiv.Perm (Option α)` by fixing or moving `none`. Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: MaximalSubgroups.lean
Description: Maximal subgroups of symmetric groups: characterisation of maximal subgroups of `Sₙ` (intransitive, imprimitive, or primitive). Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: List.lean
Description: Permutations from lists: `List.formPerm l` — the cyclic permutation sending `l[i] ↦ l[i+1]`. Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: Sign.lean
Description: Sign of a permutation: `Equiv.Perm.sign σ = (-1)^(number of transpositions)` as a group homomorphism `Sₙ → {±1}`. The kernel is the alternating group `Aₙ`. Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: ViaEmbedding.lean
Description: Extending permutations via embeddings: given an embedding `f : α ↪ β`, extend a permutation of `α` to one of `β` fixing the complement of `range f`. Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of a permutation: `σ.support = {x | σ x ≠ x}`. Disjoint cycle decomposition and support of a product of disjoint permutations. Distinct from GroupAction/Support.

Name: Subgroup.lean
Description: Subgroups of the symmetric group: `Equiv.Perm.stabilizer s` and the intransitive subgroup `Sₙ₁ × Sₙ₂`. Distinct from GroupAction/Stabilizer.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finiteness results for permutation groups: `Equiv.Perm.finite`, the order formula `|Sₙ| = n!`. Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Generating sets for permutation groups: `Equiv.Perm.closure S` — the subgroup generated by transpositions in `S`. Distinct from Perm/ClosureSwap.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: Centraliser of a permutation: `Equiv.Perm.centralizer σ` — permutations commuting with `σ`, equal to the group of permutations preserving each cycle. Distinct from Perm/ConjAct.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic permutation group theory: `Equiv.Perm α` as a group, cycle notation, and disjoint cycle decomposition. Core API for the Perm folder.

Name: ClosureSwap.lean
Description: Transpositions generate the symmetric group: `Equiv.Perm.closure {swaps} = ⊤`. Distinct from Perm/Closure and Perm/Sign.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Permutations of `Fin n`: `Equiv.Perm (Fin n)` and the symmetric group `Sₙ`. The explicit cycle structure for small `n`. Distinct from Perm/Basic.

Name: DomMulAct.lean
Description: Domain multiplication action: the action of `Sₙ × Sₙ` on itself by `(σ, τ) • π = σ π τ⁻¹`. Distinct from Perm/ConjAct.

Name: ConjAct.lean
Description: Conjugation in the symmetric group: `σ τ σ⁻¹` maps the cycle type of `τ` by relabelling via `σ`. Two permutations are conjugate iff they have the same cycle type. Distinct from Perm/Centralizer.

Name: MonoidLocalization
Description: Localization of commutative monoids: the universal construction that inverts a submonoid, generalizing Z to Q.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for monoid localisation: technical results about the universal property and fraction manipulations. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: GrothendieckGroup.lean
Description: Grothendieck group: `GrothendieckGroup M` — the group completion of a commutative monoid, as a localisation by the whole monoid. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: Maps.lean
Description: Maps between monoid localisations: `MonoidLocalization.map f h` — a monoid homomorphism `f : M → N` extending to `S⁻¹M → T⁻¹N` when `f(S) ⊆ T`. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order structure on monoid localisations: when `M` is an ordered monoid, `S⁻¹M` inherits an order. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: MonoidWithZero.lean
Description: Monoid localisation for monoids-with-zero: inverting elements in a `MonoidWithZero`, and the field of fractions construction. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monoid localisation: `MonoidLocalization S M` — the universal monoid inverting a submonoid `S ⊆ M`. The universal property and the fraction representation. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Maps.

Name: Away.lean
Description: Localisation away from an element: `MonoidLocalization.Away a` — inverting a single element `a` to get `R[a⁻¹]`. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: Cardinality.lean
Description: Cardinality of monoid localisations: when the localisation is finite and bounds on its size. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite monoid localisations: conditions under which `MonoidLocalization S M` is finite. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: DivPairs.lean
Description: Division pairs in monoid localisations: representing fractions as pairs `(m, s)` with `s ∈ S`, and the equivalence relation on such pairs. Distinct from MonoidLocalization/Basic.

Name: Order
Description: The order of a group element: the smallest positive n with x^n = 1, and Lagrange's theorem for element orders.

Name: Min.lean
Description: Minimal order of an element: the smallest `n > 0` with `g^n = 1`. Lagrange's theorem: the order of `g` divides `|G|`. Distinct from GroupAction/Period.

Name: QuotientGroup
Description: Quotient groups G/N for normal subgroups N: the first isomorphism theorem and the correspondence theorem.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Finite quotient groups: `G ⧸ N` is finite when `G` is finite, with `|G ⧸ N| = |G| / |N|`. Distinct from QuotientGroup/Basic.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Modular congruence via quotient groups: `g ≡ h [GMODEQ N]` (i.e., `g⁻¹ h ∈ N`) and its equivalence with equality in `G ⧸ N`. Distinct from QuotientGroup/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Quotient group API: `G ⧸ N` for a normal subgroup `N`, the projection `QuotientGroup.mk`, the first isomorphism theorem, and the correspondence theorem. Distinct from QuotientGroup/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `G ⧸ N`: the quotient of a group `G` by a normal subgroup `N` as a `Group`. Contains the type definition and group operations. Distinct from QuotientGroup/Basic.

Name: Submonoid
Description: Submonoids: sub-structures closed under multiplication and containing the identity, with the generated submonoid.

Name: Inverses.lean
Description: Invertible elements of a submonoid: the subgroup of units of a submonoid, and conditions for a submonoid to be a group. Distinct from Submonoid/Center.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: Centraliser for submonoids: `Submonoid.centralizer s` in a monoid. Distinct from Subgroup/Centralizer.

Name: Center.lean
Description: Center of a monoid: `Submonoid.center M` — elements commuting with all others. Distinct from Subgroup/Center and Subsemigroup/Center.

Name: Subsemigroup
Description: Subsemigroups: subsets closed under the semigroup operation, with the generated subsemigroup.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: Centraliser in a semigroup: `Subsemigroup.centralizer s` — elements commuting with all of `s`. Distinct from Subgroup/Centralizer and Submonoid/Centralizer.

Name: Center.lean
Description: Center of a semigroup: `Subsemigroup.center S` — elements commuting with all others in a semigroup. Distinct from Submonoid/Center.

Name: SpecificGroups
Description: Specific named groups: the dihedral group, quaternion group, cyclic groups, free groups, and their properties.

Name: Cyclic
Description: Cyclic groups: groups generated by a single element. The characterization of cyclic groups as ℤ/nℤ (finite) or ℤ (infinite), their subgroup lattice (one subgroup of each order dividing n), and the automorphism group Aut(ℤ/nℤ) ≅ (ℤ/nℤ)×. Used throughout group theory as the simplest nonzero groups. Distinct from Alternating (specific finite simple groups).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cyclic group basics.

Name: Alternating
Description: The alternating group Aₙ: the kernel of the sign homomorphism Sₙ → {±1}, consisting of even permutations. Aₙ is simple for n ≥ 5 (a key step in the Abel-Ruffini theorem). Contains the index [Sₙ : Aₙ] = 2 and the generation of Aₙ by 3-cycles. Distinct from Cyclic (the cyclic groups ℤ/nℤ) and GroupTheory/Perm/Cycle (cycle structure).

Name: MaximalSubgroups.lean
Description: Maximal subgroups of alternating groups.

Name: Simple.lean
Description: Simplicity of Aₙ for n ≥ 5.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: Centraliser in alternating groups.

Name: KleinFour.lean
Description: Klein four subgroup of A₄.

Name: KleinFour.lean
Description: Klein four-group: `KleinFourGroup = ℤ/2ℤ × ℤ/2ℤ` — the unique non-cyclic group of order 4. Distinct from SpecificGroups/Cyclic.

Name: Quaternion.lean
Description: Quaternion group: `QuaternionGroup n` — the generalised quaternion group of order `4n` with generators `a`, `xa` satisfying `a^{2n} = 1`, `xa^2 = a^n`, `xa⁻¹xa = a`. Distinct from SpecificGroups/Dihedral.

Name: ZGroup.lean
Description: Z-groups: groups in which all Sylow subgroups are cyclic. Characterisation and solubility. Distinct from SpecificGroups/Cyclic.

Name: Alternating.lean
Description: Alternating group: `alternatingGroup n = ker(sign : Sₙ → {±1})`. Simplicity of `Aₙ` for `n ≥ 5` (proved via Iwasawa's criterion). Distinct from Perm/Sign.

Name: Cyclic.lean
Description: Cyclic groups: `IsCyclic G` — `G` is generated by a single element. Classification: cyclic groups are isomorphic to `ℤ/nℤ` or `ℤ`. Distinct from FiniteAbelian/Basic.

Name: Dihedral.lean
Description: Dihedral groups: `DihedralGroup n` — the symmetry group of a regular `n`-gon, with `2n` elements generated by a rotation `r` and reflection `s`. Distinct from SpecificGroups/Cyclic.

Name: Subgroup
Description: Subgroups: the Subgroup typeclass, normal subgroups, the lattice of subgroups, and Lagrange's theorem.

Name: Saturated.lean
Description: Saturated subgroups: a subgroup `H` is saturated if `xⁿ ∈ H` implies `x ∈ H` for some `n > 0` coprime to the prime. Used in p-local group theory. Distinct from Subgroup/Center.

Name: Simple.lean
Description: Simple groups: `IsSimpleGroup G` — no normal subgroups besides `{1}` and `G`. Simplicity criterion and the connection to the alternating group. Distinct from SpecificGroups/Alternating.

Name: Center.lean
Description: Center of a group: `Subgroup.center G = {g | ∀ h, g * h = h * g}`. Properties: `G` is abelian iff `center G = ⊤`. Distinct from Subgroup/Centralizer.

Name: Centralizer.lean
Description: Centraliser of a subset: `Subgroup.centralizer s = {g | ∀ x ∈ s, g * x = x * g}`. The centraliser is a subgroup, and `center G = centralizer univ`. Distinct from Subgroup/Center.

Name: GroupExtension
Description: Group extensions: the classification of extensions 0 to N to E to G to 0 by second cohomology H^2(G, N).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for group extensions: `GroupExtension.SemidirectProduct`, the section map, and the associated 2-cocycle. Distinct from GroupExtension/Basic (API).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Group extensions: `GroupExtension N G E` — a short exact sequence `1 → N → E → G → 1`. The extension class in `H²(G, N)` and split extensions. Distinct from GroupExtension/Defs.

Name: Congruence
Description: Group congruences: equivalence relations compatible with the group operation, used as an alternative to normal subgroups.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Congruence generated by a homomorphism: `Con.ker f` — the kernel congruence of a monoid homomorphism. First isomorphism theorem: `M / ker f ≅ image f`. Distinct from Congruence/Basic.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Congruence on the opposite monoid: `Con.opposite c` — a congruence on `M` gives a congruence on `Mᵒᵖ`. Distinct from Congruence/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `Con M`: the type of congruence relations on a monoid. Contains the typeclass and basic instances. Distinct from Congruence/Basic (API).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Congruence relations on monoids: `Con M` — equivalence relations on a monoid compatible with multiplication. The quotient monoid `M / c` and the universal property. Distinct from Congruence/Defs.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Congruence and big operators: `Finset.prod` and `Finset.sum` respect congruence relations. Distinct from Congruence/Basic.

Name: Coprod
Description: Coproducts (free products) of groups: the group G * H with the universal property for group morphisms from G and H.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Coproduct (free product) of groups: `G ∗ H` — the group generated by copies of `G` and `H` with no additional relations. Universal property: `Hom(G ∗ H, K) ≅ Hom(G, K) × Hom(H, K)`. Distinct from LinearAlgebra/FreeProduct/Basic (algebras).

Name: Abelianization
Description: The abelianization G^ab = G/[G,G]: the largest abelian quotient of G, with the universal property for maps to abelian groups.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Abelianization of finite groups: the abelianization of a finite group is a finite abelian group, and `|G/[G,G]|` divides `|G|`. Distinct from Abelianization/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Abelianization of a group: `Abelianization G = G / [G, G]` — the largest abelian quotient. The universal property: `Hom(Abelianization G, A) ≅ Hom_Ab(G, A)`. Distinct from Abelianization/Finite.

Name: Commutator
Description: Commutators and the commutator subgroup [G,G]: the derived series, solvability, and nilpotency via lower central series.

Name: Finite.lean
Description: Commutators in finite groups: bounds on the commutator subgroup size, the Schur–Zassenhaus theorem context, and finiteness results. Distinct from Commutator/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Commutator subgroup: `Subgroup.closure {⁅g, h⁆ | g h : G} = [G, G]` (derived subgroup). Properties: `G / [G, G]` is the abelianization, and `[G, G] = {1}` iff `G` is abelian. Distinct from Commutator/Finite.

Name: Coset
Description: Cosets of subgroups: left and right cosets, the index [G:H], and the double coset decomposition.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of left and right cosets: `leftCoset a H = {a * h | h ∈ H}`, the `HasLeftCoset` typeclass, and the quotient `G ⧸ H`. Distinct from Coset/Basic (API) and QuotientGroup/Defs.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Lagrange's theorem: for a finite group G and subgroup H, the order |H| divides |G| and [G : H] = |G| / |H|. Formalized as Subgroup.card_subgroup_dvd_card and Subgroup.index_eq_card. The corollary that the order of any element divides |G| is derived here, and Fermat's little theorem follows as a special case. The foundational divisibility result underlying all of finite group theory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Cosets and Lagrange's theorem: `Subgroup.index H` (index `[G : H]`), `|G| = |H| * [G : H]`, and the orbit-counting theorem. Distinct from Coset/Defs.

Name: FreeGroup
Description: Free groups: the universal group on a generating set, with the reduced word representation and the Nielsen-Schreier theorem.

Name: NielsenSchreier.lean
Description: Nielsen–Schreier theorem: every subgroup of a free group is free. Proved using the Schreier index formula. Distinct from FreeGroup/IsFreeGroup.

Name: Orbit.lean
Description: Orbits in free group actions: the orbit of a coset under the free group action, used in the Nielsen–Schreier proof. Distinct from FreeGroup/NielsenSchreier.

Name: Reduce.lean
Description: Reduction of free group words: `FreeGroup.reduce w` — the reduced form of a word by cancelling `a⁻¹a` and `aa⁻¹` pairs. Distinct from FreeGroup/Basic.

Name: IsFreeGroup.lean
Description: `IsFreeGroup G` typeclass: a group is free iff it is isomorphic to some `FreeGroup α`. The characterisation via the universal property and the Nielsen–Schreier theorem. Distinct from FreeGroup/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Free groups: `FreeGroup α` — the group freely generated by a set `α`, constructed as reduced words. Universal property: `Hom(FreeGroup α, G) ≅ (α → G)`. Distinct from FreeGroup/IsFreeGroup.

Name: CyclicallyReduced.lean
Description: Cyclically reduced words: a word is cyclically reduced if all cyclic rotations are reduced. Used in conjugacy in free groups. Distinct from FreeGroup/Reduce.

Name: GeneratorEquiv.lean
Description: Generator equivalences for free groups: `FreeGroup.equivOfBijective` — a bijection between generating sets induces an isomorphism of free groups. Distinct from FreeGroup/Basic.

Name: GroupAction
Description: Group actions: the typeclass, orbits, stabilizers, the orbit-stabilizer theorem, Burnside's lemma, and transitive actions.

Name: SubMulAction
Description: Sub-multiplication-actions: a subset S ⊆ M that is closed under the action of G (G • S ⊆ S), the SubMulAction typeclass. The restricted action G ↷ S. Used to define invariant subspaces of a representation (a subrepresentation is a SubMulAction of the vector space). Distinct from Subgroup (closed under the group operation, not an external action).

Name: OfStabilizer.lean
Description: Sub-mul-action from the stabilizer.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise sub-mul-actions.

Name: OfFixingSubgroup.lean
Description: Sub-mul-action from the fixing subgroup.

Name: Closure.lean
Description: Closure of sub-mul-actions.

Name: Combination.lean
Description: Combinations of sub-mul-actions.

Name: DomAct
Description: Domain actions: an action of G on the domain of a function, producing an action on the function space. If G acts on α, then G acts on (α → β) by (g • f)(a) = f(g⁻¹ • a). Used in representation theory (induced representations use domain actions) and in the definition of invariant functions. Distinct from the standard pointwise action on the range.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Domain actions: acting on the domain of a function.

Name: ActionHom.lean
Description: Domain action homomorphisms.

Name: Period.lean
Description: Period of an element in a group action: the smallest `n > 0` with `g^n • x = x`. Distinct from GroupAction/IterateAct and GroupTheory/Order/Min.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise action on sets: `g • s = {g • x | x ∈ s}` for a group action on sets of a type. Distinct from GroupAction/Basic.

Name: OfQuotient.lean
Description: Actions on quotient spaces: the action of `G` on `G / H` (left coset space), and the induced action on `H \ G / K` (double cosets). Distinct from QuotientGroup/Basic.

Name: MultiplePrimitivity.lean
Description: Multiple transitivity and primitivity: `n`-transitive actions and their relationship to `k`-point stabilisers. Distinct from GroupAction/MultipleTransitivity.

Name: MultipleTransitivity.lean
Description: `k`-transitive group actions: `MulAction.IsMultiplyTransitive k G X` — for any two `k`-tuples of distinct points, some `g ∈ G` maps one to the other. Distinct from GroupAction/MultiplePrimitivity.

Name: Primitive.lean
Description: Primitive group actions: `MulAction.IsPrimitive G X` — the action is transitive with no non-trivial block system. Equivalent to the stabiliser being a maximal subgroup. Distinct from GroupAction/Blocks.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of a group action element: `MulAction.support g = {x | g • x ≠ x}` — the non-fixed points of `g`. Distinct from GroupAction/FixedPoints.

Name: Transitive.lean
Description: Transitive group actions: `MulAction.IsTransitive G X` — for any `x, y ∈ X`, some `g ∈ G` maps `x` to `y`. Characterisation: single orbit, and `X ≅ G / Gₓ`. Distinct from GroupAction/MultipleTransitivity.

Name: SubMulAction.lean
Description: Sub-`G`-sets: `SubMulAction G X` — a subset closed under the `G`-action. The lattice of sub-`G`-sets. Distinct from GroupAction/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: Actions on quotients: the action of `G` on `G / H` and the orbit-stabiliser isomorphism `G / Gₓ ≅ orbit x`. Distinct from GroupAction/OfQuotient and QuotientGroup/Basic.

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Group actions on rings: `MulSemiringAction G R` — a group acting by ring homomorphisms. Fixed subrings and the Galois correspondence setup. Distinct from GroupAction/Basic.

Name: Jordan.lean
Description: Jordan's theorem on primitive permutation groups: a primitive group containing a `p`-cycle for a prime `p ≤ n-2` contains `Aₙ`. Distinct from GroupAction/Primitive.

Name: ConjAct.lean
Description: Conjugation action: `ConjAct G` — the group `G` acting on itself by conjugation `g • x = g * x * g⁻¹`. The class equation `|G| = |Z(G)| + ∑ |G|/|C_G(x)|`. Distinct from Subgroup/Center.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definition of `MulAction M α`: the action `HSMul.hSMul : M → α → α` satisfying `1 • x = x` and `(g * h) • x = g • (h • x)`. The foundational typeclass for group actions.

Name: CardCommute.lean
Description: Burnside's lemma (Cauchy–Frobenius): `|G\X| = (1/|G|) ∑_{g ∈ G} |X^g|` — the number of orbits equals the average number of fixed points. Distinct from GroupAction/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Core API for group actions: `MulAction.orbit`, `MulAction.stabilizer`, the orbit-stabilizer theorem `|G| = |orbit| * |stab|`, and `MulAction.orbitEquivQuotientStabilizer`. Distinct from GroupAction/Defs.

Name: Blocks.lean
Description: Blocks of imprimitivity: `MulAction.IsBlock G B` — a block is a subset `B` such that every `g • B` is either equal to `B` or disjoint. Used in the classification of primitive actions. Distinct from GroupAction/Primitive.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embedding of a group via its action: the Cayley embedding `G ↪ Sym G` sending `g` to left-multiplication by `g`. Distinct from GroupAction/Basic.

Name: IterateAct.lean
Description: Iterated group actions: `g ^ n • x` and the period of a group action. Distinct from GroupAction/Period.

Name: Iwasawa.lean
Description: Iwasawa's theorem: a primitive group action on a set with a specific normal subgroup structure gives simplicity criteria. Used in proving simplicity of alternating groups. Distinct from GroupAction/Primitive.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Equivariant maps: `MulActionHom G X Y` — maps commuting with the action `f (g • x) = g • f x`. The category of `G`-sets. Distinct from GroupAction/Basic.

Name: FixedPoints.lean
Description: Fixed points of group actions: `MulAction.fixedPoints G X = {x | ∀ g, g • x = x}` and the fixed-point subgroup/submodule. Distinct from GroupAction/Stabilizer.

Name: FixingSubgroup.lean
Description: Fixing subgroup: `MulAction.fixingSubgroup G s = {g | ∀ x ∈ s, g • x = x}` — the subgroup fixing a subset pointwise. Distinct from GroupAction/Stabilizer (which fixes a single point).

Name: Coxeter
Description: Coxeter groups: groups generated by reflections with braid relations, the Coxeter matrix, and connections to root systems.

Name: Length.lean
Description: Length function in Coxeter groups: `CoxeterGroup.length w` — the minimum number of generators in a word for `w`. Exchange and deletion conditions. Distinct from Coxeter/Basic.

Name: Matrix.lean
Description: Coxeter matrix: `Matrix.IsCoxeter M` — a symmetric integer matrix with `1` on diagonal and entries `≥ 2` off-diagonal (or `∞`). The Coxeter group `CoxeterGroup M` built from it. Distinct from Coxeter/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Coxeter groups: `CoxeterGroup M` — a group with generators `sᵢ` and relations `(sᵢ sⱼ)^{m_{ij}} = 1`. The word problem and the deletion condition. Distinct from Coxeter/Length.

Name: Inversion.lean
Description: Inversions in Coxeter groups: `CoxeterGroup.inversions w` — the set of positive roots sent to negative roots by `w`. `|inversions w| = ℓ(w)` (length). Distinct from Coxeter/Length.

Name: FiniteAbelian
Description: Finite abelian groups: the classification theorem (product of cyclic groups), invariant factors, and elementary divisors.

Name: Duality.lean
Description: Pontryagin duality for finite abelian groups: a finite abelian group `G` is non-canonically isomorphic to its character group `Hom(G, S¹)`. Distinct from FiniteAbelian/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Classification of finite abelian groups: every finite abelian group is isomorphic to `⊕ᵢ ℤ/nᵢℤ` with `n₁ | n₂ | ⋯ | nₖ` (invariant factor decomposition). Distinct from FiniteAbelian/Duality.

Name: NoncommCoprod.lean
Description: Non-commutative coproducts: the pushout of two group maps in the category of groups, avoiding commutativity.

Name: Nilpotent.lean
Description: Nilpotent groups: groups with a central series of finite length, the upper and lower central series, and the nilpotency class.

Name: NoncommPiCoprod.lean
Description: Non-commutative Pi coproducts: the free product of a family of groups indexed by a type.

Name: PGroup.lean
Description: P-groups: groups whose order is a power of a prime p, with Sylow subgroups and the p-group fixed point theorem.

Name: OrderOfElement.lean
Description: The order of a group element: orderOf x, the minimal period, and its divisibility properties.

Name: IsSubnormal.lean
Description: Subnormal subgroups: subgroups reachable by a finite chain of normal subgroup inclusions, and subnormal series.

Name: Index.lean
Description: The index of a subgroup [G:H]: the number of cosets, its multiplicativity in towers, and finite-index consequences.

Name: HNNExtension.lean
Description: HNN extensions: a construction adjoining a new element t satisfying conjugation relations between isomorphic subgroups.

Name: IndexNormal.lean
Description: Normal subgroups of finite index: if [G:H] = n then the normal core of H has index dividing n factorial.

Name: IsPerfect.lean
Description: Perfect groups: groups equal to their own commutator subgroup [G,G] = G.

Name: IndexNSmul.lean
Description: Scalar multiplication by the index: the lemma that n times x is in H whenever [G:H] = n and H is a normal subgroup.

Name: PresentedGroup.lean
Description: Presented groups: groups defined by generators and relations, with the universal property.

Name: Solvable.lean
Description: Solvable groups: groups with a derived series ending at 1, and the characterization via composition factors.

Name: SemidirectProduct.lean
Description: Semidirect products G rtimes H: group extensions that split, with the multiplication law and the split sequence.

Name: Sylow.lean
Description: The three Sylow theorems for a finite group G of order pⁿm (with p prime, p ∤ m): (1) existence of a Sylow p-subgroup of order pⁿ, (2) all Sylow p-subgroups are conjugate, (3) the number of Sylow p-subgroups divides m and is congruent to 1 mod p. Formalized as Sylow.exists_subgroup_card_pow_prime, Sylow.conj_eq, and Sylow.card_dvd_index. The primary tool for classifying groups of small order.

Name: Transfer.lean
Description: The transfer homomorphism: a map from G to the abelianization of H for H a subgroup of G, used in Burnside's transfer theorem.

Name: Torsion.lean
Description: Torsion elements and torsion subgroups: elements of finite order, and the torsion-free quotient.

Name: SchurZassenhaus.lean
Description: The Schur-Zassenhaus theorem: a normal Hall subgroup of coprime order has a complement, and all complements are conjugate.

Name: Rank.lean
Description: The rank of a group: the minimum number of generators, with results for finite groups and p-groups.

Name: PushoutI.lean
Description: Amalgamated free products (pushouts of groups): the pushout in the category of groups along a common subgroup.

Name: RegularWreathProduct.lean
Description: Regular wreath products G wreath H: the semidirect product of a direct power of G with H acting by permutation.

Name: Schreier.lean
Description: The Schreier index formula: the rank of a finite-index subgroup of a free group in terms of the index and rank of the ambient group.

Name: ResiduallyFinite.lean
Description: Residually finite groups: groups where every nontrivial element is detected by a finite quotient.

Name: CosetCover.lean
Description: Coset covers: when can a group be covered by a finite union of cosets, and the minimum number needed.

Name: CoprodI.lean
Description: Indexed coproducts (free products) of groups: the categorical coproduct in Grp, with the van Kampen theorem.

Name: DedekindFinite.lean
Description: Dedekind-finite groups: groups where every injective endomorphism is surjective, with connections to finite groups.

Name: Divisible.lean
Description: Divisible groups: abelian groups where every element has an nth root for all n, equivalent to being injective as a Z-module.

Name: Descent.lean
Description: Galois descent for groups: recovering a group over a base field from its base-change to an extension.

Name: Complement.lean
Description: Complements of subgroups: a subgroup K is a complement to H if G = HK and H intersect K = 1.

Name: ArchimedeanDensely.lean
Description: Archimedean densely ordered groups: ordered groups satisfying the Archimedean property with density of the order.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: Archimedean groups: linearly ordered groups where no element is infinitely large, with the embedding theorem.

Name: ClassEquation.lean
Description: The class equation |G| = |Z(G)| + sum [G:Ci]: partitions G by conjugacy classes, used to prove Cauchy's theorem.

Name: CommutingProbability.lean
Description: The commuting probability in a finite group: the fraction of pairs of elements that commute.

Name: Commensurable.lean
Description: Commensurable subgroups: subgroups with finite mutual index, forming an equivalence relation in the group.

Name: DivisibleHull.lean
Description: The divisible hull of an abelian group: the smallest divisible group containing it, analogous to the fraction field.

Name: Focal.lean
Description: The focal subgroup: the intersection of a Sylow subgroup P with the derived subgroup [G,G], used in transfer theory.

Name: FixedPointFree.lean
Description: Fixed-point-free actions: group actions where no nontrivial element fixes any point, with consequences for structure.

Name: Frattini.lean
Description: The Frattini subgroup Phi(G): the intersection of all maximal subgroups, equal to the set of non-generators.

Name: Goursat.lean
Description: Goursat's lemma: classifying subgroups of a direct product G x H via pairs of subgroups and an isomorphism.

Name: FreeAbelianGroup.lean
Description: Free abelian groups: their universal property, the presentation, and the isomorphism with finitely supported functions.

Name: Finiteness.lean
Description: Finiteness conditions for groups: finitely generated, finitely presented, and locally finite groups.

Name: EckmannHilton.lean
Description: The Eckmann-Hilton argument: two compatible unital binary operations on a set are equal and commutative.

Name: DoubleCoset.lean
Description: Double cosets HgK: the partition of G into orbits of the H x K-action, with the Mackey formula.

Name: Exponent.lean
Description: The exponent of a group: the least common multiple of all element orders, and its relation to the group's structure.

Name: FinitelyPresentedGroup.lean
Description: Finitely presented groups: groups with a finite generating set and finitely many relations.

Name: FiniteIndexNormalSubgroup.lean
Description: Normal subgroups of finite index and their properties, including characteristic subgroups of finite index.

Name: Data
Description: Provides fundamental data structures like Option, List, Finset, and Stream, along with basic operations on them, that underpin all formalized mathematics. Used to represent finite collections, choices, and sequences, and to manipulate them efficiently in proofs and calculations. Every math field in Mathlib relies on Data.

Name: Real
Description: The real numbers R: the Cauchy completion of Q, sup/inf, archimedean property, and density of Q in R.

Name: Sign.lean
Description: The sign function on ℝ: SignType.sign : ℝ → SignType (returning pos, neg, or zero), with the interaction with abs and the basic sign lemmas. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic and Data/Sign/Basic.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on sets of reals: the interaction between Set ℝ (or Finset ℝ) operations and the real number arithmetic, including the Minkowski sum. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: Hom.lean
Description: Homomorphisms from ℝ: ring maps ℝ → ℝ must be the identity (since ℝ has no non-trivial automorphisms over ℚ), and the interaction with star and order maps. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: StarOrdered.lean
Description: Star-ordered structure on ℝ: ℝ as a star-ordered ring (a ≤ b iff b - a = star c * c for some c), and the interaction with the real ordering. Distinct from Data/Real/Star.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star structure on ℝ: the trivial star (star r = r) making ℝ a commutative C*-algebra, and the interaction with the absolute value. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic and Algebra/Star/Basic.

Name: Sqrt.lean
Description: The real square root: Real.sqrt : ℝ → ℝ≥0 (with sqrt x = 0 for x < 0), satisfying sqrt x ^ 2 = x for x ≥ 0, and the basic interaction with the real order and abs. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: CompleteField.lean
Description: ℝ as a complete ordered field: the unique (up to isomorphism) Archimedean complete ordered field, with the universal property and the embedding of any Archimedean ordered field into ℝ. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The real numbers ℝ: defined as Cauchy sequences of rationals modulo equivalence (or equivalently as Dedekind cuts), with the field, order, and completeness structures. Foundation for all Real files in Mathlib.

Name: Archimedean.lean
Description: The Archimedean property of ℝ: for every r ∈ ℝ there exists n : ℕ with n > r, and the interaction with floor/ceiling, density of ℚ in ℝ, and Cauchy sequences. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: ENatENNReal.lean
Description: The interaction between ENat and ENNReal: the coercion ℕ∞ → ℝ≥0∞ and the interaction with arithmetic operations (+, ·, ≤). Distinct from Data/ENat/Basic and Data/ENNReal/Basic.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embeddings from ℚ and ℤ into ℝ: the canonical ring embeddings ℚ ↪ ℝ and ℤ ↪ ℝ, with the interaction with the order. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: ConjExponents.lean
Description: Conjugate exponents: the pair (p, q) with 1/p + 1/q = 1 (p > 1 and q > 1), with the basic lemmas used in Hölder's inequality. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: Rat
Description: The rational numbers Q: as a field of fractions of Z, with order, Archimedean property, and floor/ceiling.

Name: NatSqrt
Description: Natural number square roots of rationals: ℚ.sqrt and the criterion for a rational to be a perfect square. Used in basic irrationality proofs (√2 ∉ ℚ) and in quadratic field computations. Distinct from Data/Nat/NthRoot (integer roots) and Analysis/SpecialFunctions (real-valued roots).

Name: Real.lean
Description: Real square root from rational sqrt.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Nat square root of rationals.

Name: Cast
Description: Casting rationals to fields: the unique field homomorphism ℚ → F for any field (or characteristic-0 division ring) F, and the casting lemmas ratCast_add, ratCast_mul. The prime subfield of a characteristic-0 field is ℚ. Distinct from Data/Rat (arithmetic on ℚ) and Data/Int/Cast (casting ℤ).

Name: OfScientific.lean
Description: Scientific notation for rat casts.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order properties of rat casts.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Rat cast lemmas.

Name: CharZero.lean
Description: Rat cast in characteristic zero rings.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Rat cast definitions.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initial rational number setup: the basic definitions and instances for ℚ needed early in the import hierarchy. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: Floor.lean
Description: Floor and ceiling for ℚ: the FloorRing instance for ℚ (⌊p/q⌋ = Int.ediv p q), with the basic interaction between rational floor and Int.ediv. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for ℚ: a collection of computational lemmas about rational arithmetic not naturally placed elsewhere. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star structure on ℚ: the trivial star (star q = q) making ℚ a commutative star ring. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs and Algebra/Star/Basic.

Name: Sqrt.lean
Description: Rational square roots: the (non-existence of) square roots of non-squares in ℚ, with the irrationality of √2 and related results. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: The cardinality of ℚ: ℚ is countably infinite, with the explicit enumeration and the interaction with Countable. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for ℚ: ∑ and ∏ of rational families, and the casting between ℚ sums and ℝ sums. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for ℚ: the Rat type as a fraction p/q in lowest terms, the field operations, and the interaction with ℤ and ℕ. Foundation for all Rat files.

Name: Encodable.lean
Description: The encodability of ℚ: encoding a rational number as a natural number via Nat.pair applied to numerator and denominator. Distinct from Data/Rat/Defs.

Name: Denumerable.lean
Description: The denumerability of ℚ: an explicit bijection ℚ ≃ ℕ, and the Denumerable instance. Distinct from Data/Rat/Cardinal.

Name: QPF
Description: Quotient polynomial functors (QPFs): the categorical framework for defining inductive and coinductive types with good properties.

Name: Univariate
Description: Univariate quotient polynomial functors: quotients F/R of a PFunctor F by a relation R on shapes. Every container type (List, Tree, Stream) is a QPF. The initial algebra (inductive type) and final coalgebra (coinductive type) of a QPF. Bridges the gap between concrete data types and general categorical type theory.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Univariate quotient polynomial functors (QPFs).

Name: Multivariate
Description: Multivariate quotient polynomial functors: quotients of multivariate PFunctors by a natural equivalence relation. Used to define inductive types with additional identification (e.g., unordered pairs as a quotient of ordered pairs). The QPF construction produces valid initial algebras and final coalgebras. Distinct from Univariate (one-variable case).

Name: Constructions
Description: Standard constructions of multivariate QPFs: products (F × G), coproducts (F + G), composition (F ∘ G), constant functors, and the identity functor — all as QPFs. Used to build complex inductive datatypes (like rose trees or heterogeneous lists) from primitive QPF building blocks. Distinct from the top-level QPF/Multivariate (abstract definitions) — this folder provides the concrete constructors.

Name: Prj.lean
Description: Projection QPF.

Name: Quot.lean
Description: Quotient multivariate QPF.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma multivariate QPF.

Name: Fix.lean
Description: Fixed points of multivariate QPFs.

Name: Cofix.lean
Description: Cofixed points of multivariate QPFs.

Name: Comp.lean
Description: Composition of multivariate QPFs.

Name: Const.lean
Description: Constant multivariate QPF.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multivariate quotient polynomial functors.

Name: Set
Description: Sets as predicates (alpha to Prop): union, intersection, complement, image, preimage, and the algebra of sets.

Name: Pairwise
Description: Pairwise conditions on sets: Set.PairwiseDisjoint (no two sets in the family overlap), Set.Pairwise (a predicate holding for all distinct pairs). Used to characterize partitions and to state the pigeonhole principle. Distinct from Data/Set/Lattice (operations on families) — this folder is about properties relating elements to each other.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions in lattices.

Name: List.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions on lists.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions on sets.

Name: Chain.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions on chains.

Name: Pointwise
Description: Pointwise algebraic operations on sets: {a} * S = {a * s | s ∈ S}, S + T = {s + t | s ∈ S, t ∈ T}. Used in additive combinatorics (sumsets, Plünnecke-Ruzsa inequality) and in group theory (double cosets HgK). Distinct from Data/Set/Lattice (set-theoretic operations) — this folder lifts algebraic structure to sets.

Name: Support.lean
Description: Support of pointwise functions on sets.

Name: Lattice
Description: Lattice operations on sets indexed by an arbitrary type: Set.iUnion (⋃ i, s i), Set.iInter (⋂ i, s i), and their interaction with images and preimages. The modular law and distributivity. Used throughout measure theory and topology. Distinct from Data/Set (binary union/intersection) — this folder handles arbitrary (possibly infinite) indexed families.

Name: Image.lean
Description: Images in set lattices.

Name: Card
Description: Cardinality of sets via Set.ncard (ℕ-valued) and Set.encard (ℕ∞-valued): the number of elements of a finite set, with additivity for disjoint unions and the inclusion-exclusion principle. Distinct from Data/Set/Finite (the finiteness predicate without a cardinality count) and Data/Fintype (Fintype instances).

Name: Arithmetic.lean
Description: Cardinal arithmetic for set cardinalities.

Name: Finite
Description: Finite sets as predicates: Set.Finite s (there exists a Fintype instance on s) without fixing a canonical enumeration. Contains Set.Finite.toFinset (converting to Finset when needed), finiteness under union, image, and preimage. Distinct from Set/Card (which counts elements) and Data/Finset (which carries an explicit enumeration).

Name: Monad.lean
Description: Monad for finite sets.

Name: Powerset.lean
Description: Power set of finite sets.

Name: Range.lean
Description: Finite range of functions.

Name: List.lean
Description: Finite sets from lists.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic finite set API.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Finite set lattice operations.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Finite set lemmas.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: Sets and the opposite type: the interaction between Set α and Set αᵒᵖ (the MulOpposite or OrderDual), and the induced bijections on sets under the op/unop maps. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Set operation lemmas: a comprehensive collection of identities for ∪, ∩, \, ∅, univ, complementation — the Boolean algebra identities, absorption laws, and the interaction with ⊆. Distinct from Set/Basic (which contains foundational lemmas) and Set/BooleanAlgebra.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order-theoretic properties of sets: Set.OrdConnected (s is order-connected: a ∈ s, c ∈ s, a ≤ b ≤ c → b ∈ s), monotone/antitone image/preimage, and the interaction between Set operations and the order. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: PowersetCard.lean
Description: Sets of a given finite cardinality: the collection of all k-element subsets of a set s (the set of subsets of size exactly k), with the cardinality formula C(|s|, k). Distinct from Set/Card and Set/Basic.

Name: Piecewise.lean
Description: Piecewise functions on sets: Set.piecewise s f g (equals f on s and g off s), with the basic API and the interaction with Set.indicator, Set.image, and continuity. Distinct from Set/Function and Set/Basic.

Name: Monotone.lean
Description: Monotone and antitone sequences of sets: a sequence {sₙ} is monotone (sₙ ⊆ sₙ₊₁) or antitone (sₙ₊₁ ⊆ sₙ), with the limit as the union (resp. intersection) and the interaction with iUnion/iInter. Distinct from Set/Accumulate.

Name: MemPartition.lean
Description: Membership partitions of sets: a family of disjoint sets {sᵢ} whose union covers s, with the membership-based characterisation and the interaction with quotient types. Distinct from Set/Basic and Setoid/Partition.

Name: MulAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Multiplicative antidiagonals as sets: Set.mulAntidiagonal s t n = {(a, b) ∈ s × t | a * b = n} for a monoid, the set-theoretic version. Distinct from Data/Finset/MulAntidiagonal (Finset version).

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Set notation in Mathlib: the set-builder notation {x | p x}, {x ∈ s | p x}, the coercion notation (↑s for a set coerced to a type), and the localised set notation. Distinct from Set/Defs.

Name: NAry.lean
Description: N-ary operations on sets: Set.image2 f s t = {f a b | a ∈ s, b ∈ t} for a binary operation f, with the basic API (image2_union_left, image2_inter_subset_left) and the interaction with Set.prod. Distinct from Set/Image (unary) and Set/Basic.

Name: Subsingleton.lean
Description: Subsingleton and nontrivial sets: Set.Subsingleton s (s has at most one element) and Set.Nontrivial s (s has at least two elements), with the basic API and the interaction with Set.card. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Subset.lean
Description: Subset relations for sets: the basic API for ⊆ (antisymmetry, transitivity, interaction with ∪ ∩ \), and the subset-based simp lemmas. Distinct from Set/Basic and Set/Operations.

Name: Sups.lean
Description: Pairwise suprema and infima of sets: Set.sups s t = {a ⊔ b | a ∈ s, b ∈ t} (the set of all pairwise suprema) and Set.infs s t = {a ⊓ b | a ∈ s, b ∈ t}, used in the Helly-type theorems and lattice combinatorics. Distinct from Set/Basic and Set/Lattice.

Name: UnionLift.lean
Description: Lifting functions defined on a union: Set.unionLift (lifting a compatible family of functions fᵢ : sᵢ → β to a function ⋃ sᵢ → β), with the gluing conditions and the interaction with Set.iUnion. Distinct from Set/Piecewise and Set/Basic.

Name: SymmDiff.lean
Description: Symmetric difference of sets: s △ t = (s \ t) ∪ (t \ s) with the Boolean ring structure (△ as addition, ∩ as multiplication) and the interaction with other set operations. Distinct from Set/Operations and Set/BooleanAlgebra.

Name: Restrict.lean
Description: Restriction of functions to sets: Function.restrict f s : s → β (the function f : α → β restricted to the subtype of s), with the interaction between restricted functions and Set.image. Distinct from Set/Function and Set/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product sets: Set.prod s t = s ×ˢ t = {(a, b) | a ∈ s, b ∈ t} with the basic API (prod_union_left, prod_inter_prod, image_prod), and the interaction between Set.prod and Set.image. Distinct from Set/NAry (binary image) and Set/Basic.

Name: Semiring.lean
Description: The semiring structure on Set: Set α forms a semiring under disjoint union as addition and cartesian product as multiplication (or alternatively: ∪ and ×ˢ), used in formal language theory. Distinct from Set/BooleanAlgebra and Set/Basic.

Name: SMulAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Scalar multiplication antidiagonals as sets: Set.smulAntidiagonal s t n = {(a, b) ∈ s × t | a • b = n}, the set-theoretic version of the smul antidiagonal. Distinct from Data/Finset/SMulAntidiagonal.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma-type sets: Set.sigma s t = {⟨a, b⟩ | a ∈ s, b ∈ t a} (the dependent product of a family of sets), with the interaction between Set.sigma and Set.iUnion. Distinct from Set/Prod (non-dependent) and Set/Basic.

Name: List.lean
Description: Sets from lists: Set.ofList l (the set of elements of a list) and the interaction between List.Mem and Set membership, used to convert between list-based and set-based statements. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard set constructions: Set.range f (image of the whole domain), Set.sep p s ({a ∈ s | p a}), Set.pi s t (the cartesian product of a family of sets), and the interaction between these operations. Distinct from Set/Image and Set/Basic.

Name: CoeSort.lean
Description: Coercion of sets to types: the coercion of s : Set α to the subtype {a // a ∈ s}, with the interaction between set membership and subtype membership, and the interaction with SetLike. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Countable.lean
Description: Countable sets: Set.Countable s asserting s is at most countably infinite (∃ f : ℕ → α, s ⊆ Set.range f), with closure under image, preimage, union, and the interaction with the Countable typeclass. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Disjoint.lean
Description: Disjoint sets: Disjoint s t (s ∩ t = ∅) and Set.PairwiseDisjoint f (the values of f are pairwise disjoint), with the basic API and the partition interaction. Distinct from Set/Basic and Set/Operations.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core Set definitions for Mathlib: Set α = α → Prop with the basic membership, ⊆, ∪, ∩, \, ∅, and Set.univ definitions, and the API used throughout Mathlib before the full Set library is imported. Foundation for all Set files.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic set theory: additional lemmas for sets — the De Morgan laws, distributive laws, interaction between ∪ ∩ \ and ⊆, the ext lemma (s = t ↔ ∀ a, a ∈ s ↔ a ∈ t), and the subset-antisymmetry principle. Distinct from Set/Defs.

Name: Accumulate.lean
Description: Accumulating sets: Set.accumulate s n = ⋃_{k ≤ n} s k (the union of the first n+1 sets in a sequence), with the monotonicity and the limit. Used in measure theory and ergodic theory. Distinct from Set/Basic and Set/Lattice.

Name: BooleanAlgebra.lean
Description: Boolean algebra structure on Set: the complete Boolean algebra on Set α under ⊆, with complement (sᶜ), the De Morgan laws, and the distributive lattice axioms. Distinct from Set/Lattice (infinite unions/intersections) and Set/Operations.

Name: Card.lean
Description: The cardinality of sets: Set.ncard s (the cardinality as a natural number, returning 0 for infinite sets), with basic properties (ncard_union, ncard_image_of_injective) and the interaction with Finset.card and Set.Finite. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: BoolIndicator.lean
Description: Boolean indicator functions for sets: Set.boolIndicator s : α → Bool (true if a ∈ s, false otherwise), the Bool-valued version of Set.indicator, with the basic API and the interaction with Set.filter. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Image.lean
Description: Set images and preimages: Set.image f s = {f a | a ∈ s} and Set.preimage f t = {a | f a ∈ t}, with the fundamental lemmas (image_union, preimage_compl, image_preimage_subset) and monotonicity. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functor and monad structure on Set: Set as a functor (via Set.image) and monad (bind = ⋃ f '' s), and the interaction with the do-notation and the powerset monad. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Inclusion.lean
Description: Set inclusions: Set.inclusion (h : s ⊆ t) : s → t (the inclusion map from a subtype to a larger subtype), with the interaction with injections and the commutative triangle. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: The complete lattice of sets: Set.iUnion f = ⋃ i, f i and Set.iInter f = ⋂ i, f i, with the distributive laws (iUnion_inter, inter_iUnion), the monotone convergence, and Zorn's lemma formulations. Distinct from Set/BooleanAlgebra (finite ∪ ∩) and Set/Basic.

Name: Insert.lean
Description: Inserting elements into sets: Set.insert a s = {a} ∪ s and its basic API (mem_insert, insert_comm, insert_eq_of_mem), with the induction principle for finite sets via repeated insert. Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Enumerate.lean
Description: Enumerating sets: Set.enumerate (choice-based enumeration of elements of a countable set indexed by ℕ), with conditions for the enumeration to be a bijection. Distinct from Set/Countable and Set/Basic.

Name: Dissipate.lean
Description: Dissipating sequences of sets: a sequence {sₙ} of sets dissipates if every element belongs to only finitely many sₙ (lim sup sₙ = ∅), related to the Borel-Cantelli lemma. Distinct from Set/Accumulate and Set/Basic.

Name: Equitable.lean
Description: Equitable distributions: partitioning a finite set into parts whose sizes differ by at most 1, and the associated combinatorial bounds. Used in graph theory (equitable colourings). Distinct from Set/Basic.

Name: Function.lean
Description: Functions between sets: injective, surjective, and bijective as properties of functions f restricted to a domain and codomain set, with Set.InjOn, Set.SurjOn, and Set.BijOn. Distinct from Set/Basic and Set/Image.

Name: FiniteExhaustion.lean
Description: Finite exhaustion of sets: conditions for a set s to be expressible as a monotone union ⋃ sₙ of finite sets sₙ (the set is σ-finite in this sense), with the construction of the exhaustion. Distinct from Set/Countable and Set/Accumulate.

Name: Seq
Description: Sequences and lazy sequences: potentially infinite lists with head, tail, and corecursive definitions.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for lazy sequences: Seq α as a partial function ℕ → Option α whose defined region is a prefix of ℕ (a possibly finite stream), with Seq.cons, Seq.head, Seq.tail, and the terminates predicate. Foundation for all Seq files.

Name: Parallel.lean
Description: Parallel composition of computations: Computation.parallel l (run a list of computations in parallel and return the first to succeed), used in the coinductive theory of concurrency. Distinct from Seq/Computation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic lazy sequence theory: Seq.map, Seq.zip, Seq.filter, Seq.take, Seq.drop, Seq.toList (for finite Seqs), and the interaction between Seq and List and Stream. Distinct from Seq/Defs.

Name: Computation.lean
Description: Computations as lazy sequences: the Computation α monad (a Seq (Option α) that eventually produces Some a) representing potentially diverging computations, with Computation.pure, Computation.bind, and Computation.get. Distinct from Seq/Basic.

Name: Rel
Description: Binary relations as functions alpha to beta to Prop: composition, inverse, image, preimage, and relational algebra.

Name: Separated.lean
Description: Separated relations: two elements are separated by a relation if they lie in different connected components, used in the theory of covers and lattices. Distinct from Data/Rel/Cover.

Name: Cover.lean
Description: Cover relations: a relation r is a cover of s if s is the reflexive-transitive closure of r, used in the theory of ordered sets and lattices. Distinct from Data/Rel/Separated.

Name: PSigma
Description: Dependent pairs (PSigma): the type of pairs (a : alpha, b : beta a), with projections and basic operations.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on PSigma: the lexicographic order on (i : α) ×' f i (the non-dependent sigma type) and its interaction with the product order. Distinct from Data/Sigma/Basic and Data/Prod/Lex.

Name: PFunctor
Description: Polynomial functors (PFunctor): the categorical framework for defining inductive datatypes as W-types.

Name: Univariate
Description: Univariate polynomial functors: functors of the form F(X) = Σ_{a : A} X^(B a), encoding a 'shape' type A and a 'position' family B. The W-type (least fixed point) and M-type (greatest fixed point) built from a univariate PFunctor. Foundation for defining inductive types as initial algebras. Distinct from Multivariate (multiple type arguments).

Name: M.lean
Description: M-types: terminal coalgebras for PFunctors.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Univariate polynomial functors.

Name: Multivariate
Description: Multivariate polynomial functors: functors of the form F(X₁, …, Xₙ) = Σ_{a : A} X₁^(B₁ a) × … × Xₙ^(Bₙ a). Used to define simultaneous inductive/coinductive type families. The multivariate W-type and M-type constructions. Distinct from Univariate (one-variable case) and QPF (quotient polynomial functors).

Name: W.lean
Description: Initial algebra for multivariate PFunctors.

Name: M.lean
Description: Terminal coalgebra for multivariate PFunctors.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Multivariate polynomial functors.

Name: Ordmap
Description: Ordered maps: balanced binary search trees with order-theoretic operations, the computational model for Finset and Finmap.

Name: Ordset.lean
Description: Ordered sets via balanced BSTs: the Ordset α type (an ordered set backed by a weight-balanced BST), with insert, delete, find, and union operations satisfying the BST invariant. Distinct from Data/Ordmap/Ordnode.

Name: Ordnode.lean
Description: The Ordnode internal data structure: the weight-balanced binary search tree node, with the rotation and balance operations. Foundation for Data/Ordmap/Ordset.

Name: Invariants.lean
Description: Invariants for ordmap data structures: the weight-balance and BST invariants for the Ordmap (ordered map backed by a binary search tree), used in the correctness proofs. Distinct from Data/Ordmap/Ordnode and Data/Ordmap/Ordset.

Name: Ordering
Description: The Ordering type (lt, eq, gt): comparison results and their interaction with decidable linear orders.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for Ordering: technical lemmas about the Ordering type used in automation and tactic proofs. Distinct from Data/Ordering/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Ordering theory: the Ordering type (lt, eq, gt), its interaction with compare, and the CompareOrder instances for common types. Foundation for all Ordering files.

Name: Prod
Description: Cartesian products: the Prod type (alpha x beta), its operations, instances, and interaction with functions and orders.

Name: PProd.lean
Description: Proposition-level products: PProd α β (the product type living in Prop) and its interaction with the dependent pair And (·∧·) and the proofs-irrelevant product. Distinct from Data/Prod/Basic.

Name: TProd.lean
Description: Typeclassed iterated products: List.TProd (a type-level heterogeneous tuple type built from a list of types), with the head and tail projections. Distinct from Data/Prod/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Prod theory: additional lemmas for α × β — the projections, the interaction with equality, the functor/bifunctor structure, and the basic algebraic instances. Foundation for all Prod files.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: The lexicographic order on Prod: the lexicographic total order on α ×ₗ β (compare first by fst, then by snd), with the interaction with the natural product order. Distinct from Data/Prod/Basic.

Name: PNat
Description: Positive natural numbers N+: the subtype of n : N with 0 < n, with arithmetic operations and casts.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for PNat: the ℕ+ notation and numeral literals for positive naturals. Distinct from Data/PNat/Defs.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals in PNat: the Finsets Finset.Icc a b for PNat elements, with the interaction with the ℕ intervals. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on PNat: the linear order on ℕ+, the interaction with ℕ ordering, and the orderIso ℕ+ ≃o {n : ℕ | 0 < n}. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic.

Name: Xgcd.lean
Description: Extended GCD for PNat: the extended Euclidean algorithm for positive naturals, producing Bézout coefficients. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic and Data/Int/GCD.

Name: Prime.lean
Description: Prime PNat elements: the interaction between Nat.Prime and primality in ℕ+, with the coprimeness criterion. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for PNat: the ℕ+ type as a subtype, the coercion to ℕ, and the basic API. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic PNat theory: ℕ+ = {n : ℕ | 0 < n} with the ordered monoid structure, arithmetic (addition, multiplication, gcd), and the interaction with ℕ. Foundation for all PNat files.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences involving PNat: isomorphisms ℕ+ ≃ ℕ (via n ↦ n-1), and the interaction with SuccOrder. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic.

Name: Find.lean
Description: Finding PNat elements: Nat.find and its PNat version, the smallest positive n satisfying a decidable predicate. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic.

Name: Factors.lean
Description: Prime factorisation for PNat: the factorisation of a PNat into a multiset of prime PNats, and the interaction with UFD theory for ℕ+. Distinct from Data/PNat/Basic and GCDMonoid/Nat.

Name: Pi
Description: Pi types (Pi i, f i): function types with dependent codomain, pointwise operations, and instances.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals in Pi types: the Finset Finset.pi s f (all functions i → f(i) from a Finset s with i ↦ f(i)) as the product of intervals, and the cardinality = ∏ card (f i). Distinct from Data/Finset/Pi.

Name: Vector
Description: Fixed-length vectors (Vector alpha n): lists of length exactly n, with typed head, tail, and indexing.

Name: Mem.lean
Description: Membership in Vectors: Vector.Mem a v (a appears in the vector v), with the interaction between Vector.Mem and List.Mem and the index-based characterisation. Distinct from Vector/Basic.

Name: Snoc.lean
Description: Appending to the back of a Vector: Vector.snoc v a (add element a at the end), with the interaction between snoc, head, tail, and the reverse operation. Distinct from Vector/Basic.

Name: Zip.lean
Description: Zipping Vectors: Vector.zip and Vector.zipWith (element-wise combination of two vectors of the same length), with the basic API and the interaction with Vector.map. Distinct from Vector/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Vector theory: Vector.map, Vector.zip, Vector.replicate, Vector.append, Vector.ofFn, and the interaction between Vectors and Lists (Vector.toList, List.toVector). Distinct from Vector/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core Vector definitions: Vector α n = {l : List α // l.length = n}, with Vector.cons, Vector.head, Vector.tail, and the basic API. Foundation for all Vector files.

Name: MapLemmas.lean
Description: Map lemmas for Vectors: additional lemmas about Vector.map (naturality, interaction with Vector.zip and Vector.cons) and the associated simp lemmas. Distinct from Vector/Basic.

Name: Tree
Description: Binary trees: the Tree type, tree traversals, and size lemmas.

Name: RBMap.lean
Description: Red-black trees as ordered maps: an efficient balanced BST implementation using the red-black invariant, with O(log n) insert, delete, and lookup operations and a correctness proof. Distinct from Tree/Basic and Data/Ordmap/Ordnode.

Name: Traversable.lean
Description: The Traversable instance for Tree: Tree as a Functor and Traversable (allowing effectful traversal, e.g., collecting all nodes in a list), with the interaction between Tree.map and List.map. Distinct from Tree/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic binary tree theory: Tree α = nil | node (left : Tree α) (val : α) (right : Tree α), with Tree.map, Tree.depth, Tree.numNodes, Tree.toList (in-order), and the interaction with List. Foundation for all Tree files.

Name: Get.lean
Description: Getting elements from a tree by path: Tree.get (accessing a node by a list of left/right choices from the root), used in the formalisation of binary heaps and tree-indexed data structures. Distinct from Tree/Basic.

Name: Sym
Description: Symmetric products Sym n alpha: the type of unordered n-tuples, with multiset representation.

Name: Sym2
Description: The second symmetric product Sym2 α = {unordered pairs {a, b} | a b : α}: pairs quotiented by the swap equivalence. The diagonal {a, a} and the off-diagonal. Used to define edges of simple graphs (an edge is a Sym2 of vertices) and in combinatorics. Distinct from Sym n (arbitrary symmetric products) — Sym2 is the most-used special case.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order on Sym2.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Sym2 initialisation and basic definitions.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finsupp representation of Sym2.

Name: Sym2.lean
Description: The symmetric square Sym2: the type of unordered pairs {a, b} = Sym α 2 / (a,b)~(b,a), with Sym2.mk, the diagonal Sym2.diag a = {a,a}, the membership predicate, and the graph-theoretic interpretation (edges as Sym2 of vertices). Distinct from Sym/Basic.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of symmetric powers: |Sym α n| = C(|α| + n - 1, n) for a Fintype α (the stars-and-bars formula), with the interaction with Finset.sym and the generating function. Distinct from Sym/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic symmetric power theory: Sym α n = the type of n-element multisets of α (elements of Multiset α with card = n), with Sym.mk, Sym.cons, Sym.map, Sym.lift, and the interaction with Multiset. Foundation for all Sym files.

Name: ZMod
Description: The integers modulo n (ZMod n): ring structure, units, Chinese remainder theorem instances.

Name: QuotientGroup.lean
Description: ZMod as a quotient group: the additive group isomorphism ZMod n ≅ ℤ / nℤ (using the quotient group construction), with the interaction between the group and ring quotients. Distinct from ZMod/Basic and ZMod/QuotientRing.

Name: IntUnitsPower.lean
Description: Integer units in ZMod: the image of ℤˣ = {1, -1} in ZMod n (the signs ±1 modulo n), with the interaction between the sign of an integer and its reduction modulo n. Distinct from ZMod/Basic.

Name: QuotientRing.lean
Description: ZMod as a quotient ring: the ring isomorphism ZMod n ≅ ℤ / (n) (using Ideal.quotient), confirming that the direct definition agrees with the ring-theoretic quotient. Distinct from ZMod/Basic and ZMod/QuotientGroup.

Name: ValMinAbs.lean
Description: The minimum absolute value representative in ZMod n: ZMod.valMinAbs a (the representative of a in the range (-n/2, n/2] ⊆ ℤ), used in computing integer lifts and half-period reduction for modular arithmetic. Distinct from ZMod/Basic.

Name: Units.lean
Description: The unit group of ZMod n: the structure of (ZMod n)ˣ (isomorphic to the group of reduced residues mod n, of order φ(n)), with the primitive root theorem and the interaction with the Euler totient. Distinct from ZMod/Basic and ZMod/Coprime.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic ZMod theory: the CharP instance (ZMod n has characteristic n), the Chinese Remainder Theorem (ZMod (m*n) ≅ ZMod m × ZMod n for gcd m n = 1), and the canonical maps between ZMod n for divisibility. Distinct from ZMod/Defs.

Name: Aut.lean
Description: Automorphisms of ZMod n: the ring automorphism group of ZMod n is isomorphic to (ZMod n)ˣ via the map u ↦ (x ↦ u * x), with the interaction between ring automorphisms and unit multiplication. Distinct from ZMod/Basic.

Name: Coprime.lean
Description: Coprimeness in ZMod n: an element a : ZMod n is a unit iff gcd (a.val) n = 1, with the explicit inverse formula and the interaction between ZMod units and Nat.Coprime. Distinct from ZMod/Basic and ZMod/Units.

Name: Factorial.lean
Description: Factorial and ZMod: the vanishing of n! in ZMod n (Nat.factorial n ≡ 0 [MOD n] for n ≥ 2), and Wilson's theorem (p prime ↔ (p-1)! ≡ -1 [MOD p]). Distinct from ZMod/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for ZMod n: the type ZMod n = Fin n for n > 0 and ZMod 0 = ℤ, with the ring structure, the canonical cast ℤ → ZMod n, and the basic API. Foundation for all ZMod files.

Name: WSeq
Description: Weak sequences: computationally defined potentially-infinite sequences with an explicit termination marker.

Name: Productive.lean
Description: Productive weak sequences: WSeq.Productive s asserting that s always eventually produces the next element (does not diverge between elements), with the interaction between productivity and WSeq.toSeq. Distinct from WSeq/Basic.

Name: Relation.lean
Description: Relational properties of weak sequences: WSeq.Equiv (bisimilarity: two weak sequences are equivalent if they produce the same elements modulo think-steps), with the coinductive proof of equivalence. Distinct from WSeq/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic weak sequence theory: WSeq.map, WSeq.filter, WSeq.head, WSeq.tail, WSeq.toList (converges to a List when the sequence is finite), WSeq.append, and the basic API. Distinct from WSeq/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for weak sequences: WSeq α = Computation (Option (α × WSeq α)) (a coinductive type of potentially diverging sequences), with WSeq.nil, WSeq.cons, WSeq.think (a pause step), and the corecursor. Foundation for all WSeq files.

Name: W
Description: W-types (well-founded tree types): the general inductive type former and its recursion principle.

Name: Constructions.lean
Description: Standard W-type constructions: encoding of ℕ (unary, W Bool (Bool.rec Empty Unit)), List α (W (Option α) ...), and binary trees as W-types, demonstrating the universality of W-types for inductive types. Distinct from W/Basic.

Name: Cardinal.lean
Description: The cardinality of W-types: |W α β| for various arities β, with the cardinality of finitely-branching trees (countable if α is countable and each β a is finite), and the interaction with the cardinals arithmetic. Distinct from W/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: W-types (well-founded tree types): the W-type W α β for a type α of node labels and β : α → Type of arities, the free-algebra structure, and the well-founded recursion principle (W-recursion is definitionally well-founded). Foundation for all W files.

Name: Sum
Description: Sum types (alpha plus beta): disjoint unions with left/right injections and elimination.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice operations on sum types: the disjoint union as a lattice (when the components are lattice-ordered) and the interaction with the sum order. Distinct from Sum/Basic and Sum/Order.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on sum types: the disjoint union order (all inl values precede all inr values) and the lexicographic order on α ⊕ β, with the interaction with the component orders and WellFounded. Distinct from Sum/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic sum type theory: additional lemmas for α ⊕ β — the injections Sum.inl and Sum.inr, Sum.elim (the eliminator), the functor structure Sum.map, and the interaction with equality and decidability. Foundation for all Sum files.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals in sum types: the Finsets of elements in α ⊕ β lying in a given order interval (for linearly ordered α and β), with cardinality formulas. Distinct from Sum/Basic.

Name: Sigma
Description: Sigma types (Sigma a, beta a): dependent pairs with first and second projections.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: The lexicographic order on sigma types: Sigma.Lex r s on (i : α) × f i (compare first by i using r, then by second component using s i), with the interaction with the product order and WellFounded. Distinct from Sigma/Basic and Sigma/Order.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on sigma types: the pointwise and lexicographic partial orders on sigma types and their interaction with the component orders, including the WellFounded instance. Distinct from Sigma/Basic and Sigma/Lex.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic sigma type theory: the dependent pair type (i : α) × f i with projections Sigma.fst and Sigma.snd, the equality criterion Sigma.ext, and the interaction with dependent functions and Pi types. Foundation for all Sigma files.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals in sigma types: the Finset Finset.sigma s f of dependent pairs ⟨i, j⟩ with i ∈ s and j ∈ f i, and the cardinality formula card = ∑ card (f i). Distinct from Sigma/Basic.

Name: Setoid
Description: Setoids: types equipped with an equivalence relation, used to build quotient types.

Name: Partition
Description: Partitions of a type as setoids: a partition is a setoid where the equivalence classes are disjoint and cover the type. The bijection between setoids and partitions. Used to connect the combinatorial notion of partition (Data/Nat/Factorization) with the algebraic quotient construction.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of set partitions.

Name: Partition.lean
Description: Setoid partitions: the equivalence between setoids on α and partitions of α into disjoint nonempty parts, with Setoid.partition, the partition-to-setoid and setoid-to-partition maps, and the interaction with quotient types. Distinct from Setoid/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic setoid theory: the Setoid α typeclass (an equivalence relation r with the notation a ≈ b), the quotient Quotient s, the lift and map operations, and the interaction with the universal property. Foundation for all Setoid files.

Name: SetLike
Description: The SetLike typeclass: bundled subobjects (Subgroup, Submodule, etc.) that embed into a parent type.

Name: Fintype.lean
Description: Fintype instances for SetLike objects: conditions for a subgroup, subalgebra, or other SetLike structure to be a Fintype (when the ambient type has a decision procedure and the substructure is finite), with the cardinality. Distinct from SetLike/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The SetLike typeclass: SetLike A B asserting that A coerces to Set B (e.g., Subgroup G coerces to Set G), enabling generic membership notation a ∈ s for algebraic substructures. Foundation for all algebraic subobject types in Mathlib. Distinct from SetLike/Fintype.

Name: String
Description: Strings in Lean 4: character lists with basic string operations.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary String lemmas: technical results about String operations (length, append, reverse) and their interaction with List Char, used in parser and formal language formalisations. Distinct from String/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core String definitions for Mathlib: String.Pos (a valid position in a UTF-8 encoded string), String.startsWith, String.endsWith, String.isPrefixOf, and other derived operations beyond core Lean. Distinct from String/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic String theory in Mathlib: additional lemmas for Lean's String type — the isomorphism String ≅ List Char via String.toList and List.asString, String.length, and basic decidability instances. Foundation for String files.

Name: Stream
Description: Infinite streams (Stream alpha = N to alpha): head, tail, map, zip, and corecursive definitions.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initial Stream setup: the basic Stream definitions and lemmas needed early in the import hierarchy before the full Stream library is available. Distinct from Stream/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Stream: Stream α = ℕ → α (an infinite sequence) with Stream.cons, Stream.head (= s 0), Stream.tail (= s ∘ Nat.succ), Stream.map, Stream.zip, and Stream.corec (the coinductive corecursor). Foundation for Stream files.

Name: Sign
Description: The Sign type {-1, 0, 1}: the sign function on linearly ordered types and its multiplicative properties.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for SignType: the three-element type SignType = {pos, neg, zero} with its ordering (neg < zero < pos), multiplication (sign algebra), and the map sign : α → SignType for a LinearOrderedAddCommGroup α. Foundation for all Sign files.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic sign theory: the interaction between SignType and the real numbers (sign r = pos/neg/zero depending on r > 0, r < 0, r = 0), sign arithmetic (sign (a * b) = sign a * sign b), and the interaction with abs. Distinct from Sign/Defs.

Name: Option
Description: The Option type (some a or none): monadic structure, default values, and option-valued functions.

Name: NAry.lean
Description: N-ary operations on Option: Option.map₂ f (apply a binary function to optional arguments, returning Some iff both are Some). Distinct from Data/Option/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
