Description: Core Option definitions for Mathlib: Option.toList, Option.elim, and other derived operations beyond core Lean's Option. Distinct from Data/Option/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Option theory: additional lemmas for Option α — Option.get, Option.getD, Option.map, Option.bind, and the interaction with decidability and equality. Distinct from Data/Option/Defs and Data/Option/NAry.

Name: EReal
Description: Extended real numbers R union {-inf, +inf}: arithmetic with infinities used in measure theory and optimization.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on EReal: addition (∞ + (-∞) = ∞ by convention or undefined), multiplication (including sign rules for infinities), and truncated subtraction in ℝ̄. Distinct from EReal/Basic and EReal/Inv.

Name: Inv.lean
Description: Division and inversion in EReal: the inverse x⁻¹ in ℝ̄ (with conventions ±∞⁻¹ = 0, 0⁻¹ = ±∞ depending on sign) and the division x/y. Distinct from EReal/Basic and EReal/Operations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The extended real numbers ℝ̄ = [-∞, +∞]: the type EReal = WithBot (WithTop ℝ) with the linear order, the coercion ℝ → ℝ̄, and the interaction with +∞ and -∞. Foundation for all EReal files.

Name: ENNReal
Description: Extended nonnegative reals R>=0 union {+inf}: the primary codomain for measures, with the topology and arithmetic.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for ENNReal: a collection of computational lemmas about ENNReal arithmetic beyond the basic operations, used throughout Mathlib's measure theory and analysis. Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and ENNReal/Operations.

Name: Inv.lean
Description: Division and inversion in ENNReal: the inverse x⁻¹ in ℝ≥0∞ (with 0⁻¹ = 0 and ∞⁻¹ = 0 and ∞/∞ = 0), the division x/y, and basic lemmas. Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and ENNReal/Operations.

Name: Real.lean
Description: The coercion ENNReal ↔ ℝ: the map toReal : ℝ≥0∞ → ℝ (with (∞).toReal = 0) and its interaction with the arithmetic operations and order. Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and NNReal/Basic.

Name: Operations.lean
Description: Operations on ENNReal: addition, multiplication, subtraction (truncated), and power in ℝ≥0∞, with the key lemmas (a + b < ∞ ↔ a < ∞ and b < ∞, etc.). Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and ENNReal/Inv.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The extended nonnegative reals ℝ≥0∞: the type ENNReal = ℝ≥0 ∪ {∞} with the complete linear order, the addition and multiplication (including ∞ + x = ∞ and 0 · ∞ = 0), and the coercion from ℝ≥0. Foundation for all ENNReal files.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Actions of ENNReal on other types: the scalar multiplication of ENNReal on NNReal-modules and the interaction with the multiplication in ℝ≥0∞. Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Holder.lean
Description: Hölder's inequality for ENNReal: the ENNReal version of ∑ aᵢbᵢ ≤ (∑ aᵢᵖ)^(1/p) · (∑ bᵢᵍ)^(1/q) for conjugate exponents 1/p + 1/q = 1. Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and Real/ConjExponents.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for ENNReal: ∑ and ∏ of ENNReal families, including the monotone convergence for sums (∑ aᵢ = sup partial sums), and the interaction with the ∞ convention. Distinct from ENNReal/Basic and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: ENat
Description: Extended natural numbers N union {+inf}: used for cardinalities, lengths, and dimensions that may be infinite.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on ENat: the complete lattice ℕ∞ with sup = max (including ⊤ = ∞) and inf = min, and the interaction with the arithmetic. Distinct from ENat/Basic.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Power in ENat: n ^ m for n, m : ℕ∞ and the interaction with 0^0 = 1 and ∞^m for m ≥ 1. Distinct from ENat/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for ENat: the extended natural number type ℕ∞ = WithTop ℕ and the basic API. Distinct from ENat/Basic (derived results).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The extended natural numbers ℕ∞ = ℕ ⊔ {∞}: the complete linear order on WithTop ℕ with the coercion ℕ → ℕ∞, and basic arithmetic (∞ + n = ∞, 0 · ∞ = 0). Foundation for all ENat files.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for ENat: ∑ and ∏ of ENat families, with the interaction with the ∞ convention and the monotone convergence. Distinct from ENat/Basic and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Finite
Description: The Finite typeclass: types provably having a finite number of elements, with instances and decidability.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma types of finite types: (i : α) × f i is finite when α is finite and each f i is finite. Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Sigma/Basic.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Finite sets and the Finite typeclass: a Set s is finite iff s is a Finite subtype, with the interaction between Set.Finite and the Finite typeclass. Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Set/Finite.

Name: Vector.lean
Description: Finite types and vector types: Vector α n is finite when α is finite, with cardinality |Vector α n| = |α|^n. Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Vector/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Sum types of finite types: α ⊕ β is finite when α and β are finite. Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Sum/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for the Finite typeclass: Finite α asserting α is in bijection with Fin n for some n, the difference from Fintype (Finite is a Prop, Fintype is data), and basic instances. Foundation for all Finite files.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of finite types: the interaction between the Finite typeclass and Fintype.card, and the computation of cardinalities for standard constructions (products, sums, subtypes). Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Fintype/Card.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Products of finite types: α × β is finite when α and β are finite. Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Fintype/Prod.

Name: Perm.lean
Description: Finite types and permutations: the group Equiv.Perm α is finite when α is finite, with the cardinality |Perm α| = n!. Distinct from Data/Finite/Defs and Data/Fintype/Perm.

Name: FinEnum
Description: FinEnum: finitely enumerable types with a canonical bijection to Fin n.

Name: Option.lean
Description: The FinEnum instance for Option: Option α is a finite enumerable type when α is, with the enumeration (none :: some '' enumerate α). Distinct from Data/Fintype/Option and FinEnum/Basic.

Name: Fin
Description: The type Fin n = {i : N // i < n}: arithmetic, order, embeddings, and canonical isomorphisms.

Name: Tuple
Description: Operations on Fin-indexed tuples (n-tuples): insertAt (inserting at position i), removeAt (deleting position i), snoc (appending an element), init (all but last), tail (all but first). Used to define face and degeneracy maps on tuples, and to state multilinear and alternating map results. Distinct from Data/Fin (the ordered type Fin n itself).

Name: Reflection.lean
Description: Reflection of Fin tuples.

Name: NatAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Natural number antidiagonals for Fin tuples.

Name: Take.lean
Description: Taking initial segments of Fin tuples.

Name: Sort.lean
Description: Sorting Fin tuples: the sorted permutation of a Fin-indexed sequence.

Name: Finset.lean
Description: Finsets of Fin tuples.

Name: BubbleSortInduction.lean
Description: Bubble sort induction on Fin tuples.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fin-indexed tuples: `Fin.cons`, `Fin.tail`, and operations on tuples.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embeddings from Fin tuples.

Name: Curry.lean
Description: Currying of Fin-indexed tuple functions.

Name: Rev.lean
Description: Reversal of Fin n: the order-reversing bijection Fin.rev : Fin n → Fin n (i ↦ n - 1 - i), with its interaction with List.reverse and order operations. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Pigeonhole.lean
Description: The pigeonhole principle for Fin: if f : Fin (n+1) → Fin n then f is not injective, and related pigeonhole-type results for finite types. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic and Data/Fintype/Pigeonhole.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Successor and predecessor in Fin n: Fin.succ, Fin.castSucc, and Fin.pred (where defined), with the interaction between successor/predecessor and the linear order. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: VecNotation.lean
Description: Vector notation for Fin-indexed functions: the ![a, b, c] syntax for functions Fin 3 → α and the interaction with matrix row/column notation. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic and Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: SuccPredOrder.lean
Description: The SuccOrder and PredOrder instances for Fin n: Fin n as a linearly ordered type with well-behaved successor (for n ≥ 1) and predecessor maps. Distinct from Data/Fin/SuccPred.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: Embeddings from Fin n: order embeddings Fin n ↪o Fin m (for n ≤ m), the weakening and strengthening maps, and the interaction with Finset. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic theory of Fin n: Fin n = {i : ℕ | i < n} with the linear order, arithmetic (addition and multiplication mod n), and the interaction with ℕ and ZMod n. Foundation for all Fin files.

Name: Fin2.lean
Description: The two-element type Fin 2: explicit constructions and lemmas for the special case n = 2, used in boolean and parity arguments. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Parity.lean
Description: Parity of Fin n elements: even and odd elements of Fin n with the interaction with modular arithmetic and the parity of permutations. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic and Ring/Parity.

Name: FlagRange.lean
Description: The flag range of Fin: the sequence of Fin values obtained by iterating a flag (sequence of nested subsets), used in combinatorics. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: DList
Description: Difference lists: a representation of lists as function compositions enabling O(1) append.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Typeclass instances for DList (difference lists): the Monad and Traversable instances for DList, the representation of a DList as a function List α → List α, and the interaction with ordinary lists. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Bool
Description: The Bool type: logical operations, decidability, and the correspondence with Prop via decide.

Name: Count.lean
Description: Counting Bool values: the number of true values in a list of Booleans (List.countP id), with the interaction with List.filter and Finset operations. Distinct from Data/Bool/Basic.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Bool as a finite type and its set theory: the two-element type Bool viewed as a finite set, with the Bool.fintype instance and the interaction with Set operations. Distinct from Data/Bool/Basic.

Name: AllAny.lean
Description: The Bool.all and Bool.any predicates: checking whether a predicate holds for all or any elements of a list, with the interaction with Finset and List operations. Distinct from Data/Bool/Basic and Data/Bool/Count.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Bool theory in Mathlib: additional lemmas for the Bool type beyond core Lean — interactions with decidable propositions, the Bool ring structure (Bool ≅ ZMod 2 as a ring), and the xor operation. Foundation for all Bool files.

Name: Array
Description: Arrays in Lean 4: the built-in dynamic array type with push, get, set, and slice operations.

Name: Extract.lean
Description: Extraction and slicing of arrays: Array.extract and related operations for taking sub-arrays, with the interaction with List.take and List.drop. Distinct from Data/Array/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for arrays in Mathlib: additional definitions for Lean's built-in Array type, bridging the Array API with Mathlib's List and Finset machinery. Distinct from Data/Array/Extract.

Name: Analysis
Description: Analytic data structures: completions and computability aspects of analytic objects.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Computational representations of topological spaces: the Topology α type encoding a topology in a computable way (via a basis or subbasis), used in constructive analysis. Distinct from Data/Analysis/Filter and the main Mathlib Topology module.

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Computational representations of filters: the Filter α type in the computable sense as a set of sets satisfying the filter axioms, used in the constructive formalisation of convergence and topology. Distinct from Data/Analysis/Topology and the main Mathlib Filter module.

Name: DFinsupp
Description: Dependently typed finitely supported functions (DFinsupp): the dependent version of Finsupp used for direct sums over families.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for DFinsupp: the Π₀ notation for dependent finitely supported functions, and the single element notation. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on DFinsupp: the pointwise partial order, the lattice structure (when values are lattice-ordered), and the interaction with the support. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and DFinsupp/Lex.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: DFinsupp representation of multisets: the isomorphism between Multiset α and Π₀ a : α, ℕ (a DFinsupp with ℕ values). Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: NeLocus.lean
Description: The ne-locus of two DFinsupps: the finite set of indices where two DFinsupps differ, analogous to the symmetric difference of supports. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and DFinsupp/Ext.

Name: Submonoid.lean
Description: DFinsupp and submonoids: the restriction of a DFinsupp to a submonoid of the index type, and the interaction with the support. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and Algebra/Group/Basic.

Name: WellFounded.lean
Description: Well-foundedness of the DFinsupp order: the lex order on DFinsupp is well-founded when the value orders are well-founded. Distinct from DFinsupp/Lex and DFinsupp/Order.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: DFinsupp over a sigma type: the isomorphism Π₀ (i : Σ α, β α), f i ≅ Π₀ a, Π₀ b, f ⟨a, b⟩ (Fubini for DFinsupp). Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and Data/Sigma/Basic.

Name: Small.lean
Description: DFinsupp types in small universes: when Π₀ i, f i is Small (lives in a universe accessible from the index and value types). Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and Data/Countable/Small.

Name: Encodable.lean
Description: Encodable instances for DFinsupp: encoding a DFinsupp as a natural number via the encoding of its support and coefficient values. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Extensionality for DFinsupp: two DFinsupps are equal iff they agree on every index, with the ext lemma and its automation. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for DFinsupp: ∑ and ∏ over the support of a DFinsupp, with the interaction between DFinsupp.sum and Finset.sum. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for dependent finitely supported functions: Π₀ i, f i (DFinsupp f), a function i → f i with finite support, with the basic API including support, single, and zipWith. Foundation for all DFinsupp files.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: The lexicographic order on DFinsupp: the lex order comparing DFinsupps first at the smallest index where they differ. Distinct from DFinsupp/Order and DFinsupp/Defs.

Name: Module.lean
Description: Module structure on DFinsupp: Π₀ i, f i as an R-module when each f i is an R-module, with the pointwise action. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and Module/Basic.

Name: FiniteInfinite.lean
Description: Finiteness and infiniteness of DFinsupp types: when the type Π₀ i, f i is finite or infinite depending on the index type and value types. Distinct from DFinsupp/Defs and Data/Finite/Defs.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Intervals in DFinsupp: the order interval [f, g] = {h | f ≤ h ≤ g} as a Finset when the value types have finite intervals. Distinct from DFinsupp/Order and DFinsupp/Defs.

Name: Countable
Description: The Countable typeclass: types with an injection into N, closed under products, sums, and subtypes.

Name: Small.lean
Description: Countable types are small: a countable type is Small (lives in the universe of ℕ), with the interaction between Countable and the universe hierarchy. Distinct from Data/Countable/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definition of countable types: the Countable α typeclass asserting an injection α ↪ ℕ, and its basic API. Distinct from Data/Countable/Basic (derived closure lemmas).

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic results for countable types: closure of countability under products, sums, option, subtype, and quotient; the countability of ℕ, ℤ, ℚ; and the interaction with encodable types. Distinct from Data/Countable/Defs and Data/Countable/Small.

Name: Complex
Description: The complex numbers C: the field R[i], absolute value, conjugation, and the complex exponential.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for complex numbers: ∑ and ∏ of complex-number sequences, with the interaction between real/imaginary parts and sums. Distinct from Data/Complex/Basic and BigOperators/Pi.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The complex numbers ℂ: the field structure on ℝ × ℝ with the imaginary unit I satisfying I² = -1, the norm (absolute value) |z|, conjugation, and the real and imaginary part maps. Foundation for all complex number theory in Mathlib.

Name: Nat
Description: Natural number theory in Data: divisibility, factorization, primality, gcd/lcm, and concrete Nat computations.

Name: GCD
Description: GCD and LCM of natural numbers: Nat.gcd via the Euclidean algorithm, Bezout's identity (∃ a b : ℤ, a*m + b*n = gcd m n), and the extended Euclidean algorithm. Coprimality (gcd = 1) and the Chinese remainder theorem for ℕ. Distinct from Factorization (prime factorization) and from RingTheory/GCDMonoid (abstract GCD monoids).

Name: Prime.lean
Description: GCD and primality.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: GCD of big operators.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: GCD of naturals: `Nat.gcd`.

Name: Fib
Description: The Fibonacci sequence on ℕ: Nat.fib n with Fib 0 = 0, Fib 1 = 1, Fib (n+2) = Fib n + Fib (n+1). Properties: Cassini's identity Fib(n+1)² - Fib(n)Fib(n+2) = (-1)ⁿ, the fast doubling formula, and divisibility gcd(Fib m, Fib n) = Fib(gcd m n). Distinct from Data/Int/Fib (negative index extension).

Name: Zeckendorf.lean
Description: Zeckendorf's theorem: every nat is a sum of distinct non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fibonacci sequence: `Nat.fib n`.

Name: NthRoot
Description: Integer nth roots of natural numbers: Nat.sqrt (integer square root), Nat.iSqrt, and their characterization as ⌊n^(1/k)⌋. The non-existence of rational nth roots of non-perfect powers. Used in irrationality proofs and in norm computations. Distinct from Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Pow (real-valued roots).

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Nat nth root: `Nat.nthRoot k n`.

Name: Prime
Description: Prime natural numbers: Nat.Prime p (p ≥ 2 and not divisible by any smaller positive integer), primality testing, the infinitude of primes, the prime number theorem statement, and basic prime divisibility (p ∣ ab implies p ∣ a or p ∣ b). Distinct from Factorization (the full factorization) and NumberTheory/PrimeCounting (the function π(x)).

Name: Int.lean
Description: Primes in ℤ.

Name: Nth.lean
Description: The n-th prime `Nat.nthPrime n`.

Name: Pow.lean
Description: Prime powers.

Name: Infinite.lean
Description: The infinitude of primes: Nat.infinite_setOf_prime, the formal proof that the set of prime numbers is infinite. The Mathlib proof follows Euclid's construction — any finite set of primes misses the prime factors of their product plus one. Contains density corollaries and the fact that no finite set of primes suffices to generate all of ℕ by multiplication.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Prime number API.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Prime number definitions.

Name: Factorial.lean
Description: Primes and factorials.

Name: Order
Description: The order structure on ℕ: ℕ as a well-ordered type, strong induction and complete induction, the interaction between order and arithmetic (monotonicity of +, ×). The least-element principle. Distinct from Data/Nat/GCD (divisibility order) and Data/Nat/Cast (ring homomorphisms).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: ℕ order lemmas.

Name: Choose
Description: Binomial coefficients C(n, k) = n! / (k! (n-k)!): the recursive definition choose n k = choose (n-1) k + choose (n-1) (k-1), Pascal's triangle, symmetry, Vandermonde's identity, and the Kummer theorem (p-adic valuation of C(m+n, m)). Distinct from Factorial (just n!) and Combinatorics/Enumerative (higher-level counting).

Name: Mul.lean
Description: Multiplicative identities for binomial coefficients.

Name: Lucas.lean
Description: Lucas' theorem: `C(m,n) mod p` via base-p digits.

Name: Multinomial.lean
Description: Multinomial coefficients.

Name: Vandermonde.lean
Description: Vandermonde's convolution: `∑_k C(m,k)C(n,r-k) = C(m+n,r)`.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Vandermonde's identity and other choose sums.

Name: Factorization.lean
Description: Prime factorisation of binomial coefficients.

Name: Bounds.lean
Description: Bounds on binomial coefficients.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Binomial coefficient API: `Nat.choose n k`.

Name: Cast.lean
Description: Casting binomial coefficients to rings.

Name: Dvd.lean
Description: Divisibility properties of binomial coefficients.

Name: Central.lean
Description: Central binomial coefficients `C(2n, n)`.

Name: Cast
Description: Casting natural numbers to semirings: the unique semiring homomorphism ℕ → R, the natCast map, and casting lemmas (Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_mul). The foundation for numeric literals in Lean. Distinct from Data/Int/Cast (casting ℤ) and Data/Nat/Order (order on ℕ).

Name: Order
Description: The interaction between Nat.cast and order: monotonicity of the cast (n ≤ m → (n : R) ≤ (m : R) in an ordered semiring), the strict monotonicity for CharZero rings, and Nat.cast_le / Nat.cast_lt lemmas. Used whenever numerics reasoning crosses from ℕ into an ordered ring. Distinct from Data/Nat/Cast (the algebraic casting API without order).

Name: Ring.lean
Description: Nat cast order in rings.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Nat cast order in fields.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Nat cast order basics.

Name: SetInterval.lean
Description: Nat cast and set intervals.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Nat cast in products.

Name: WithTop.lean
Description: Nat cast with top.

Name: Synonym.lean
Description: Type synonyms for nat cast.

Name: NeZero.lean
Description: Nat cast and NeZero.

Name: Commute.lean
Description: Commutativity of nat casts.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Nat cast API.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Nat cast in fields.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Nat cast definitions.

Name: Digits
Description: Base-b digit representations of natural numbers: Nat.digits b n as a list of digits (least significant first), the sum-of-digits function, Nat.digitChar. Used in divisibility rules (a number is divisible by 9 iff its digit sum is) and in computability. Distinct from Data/Num (binary representation) — this handles arbitrary base b.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Supplementary lemmas for Nat.digits (base-b digit representations): digit-sum formulas, properties of digit extraction, and auxiliary results used in number theory. The sum of an arithmetic series 1 + 2 + … + n = n(n+1)/2 (Gauss's formula) is formalized as Finset.sum_range_id_eq and Gauss.sum_range in Data/Finset. This file contains the digit-theoretic context supporting related number-theoretic computations.

Name: Div.lean
Description: Division via digits.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Nat digit representations: `Nat.digits b n`.

Name: Factorization
Description: The prime factorization of natural numbers: Nat.factorization n : ℕ →₀ ℕ as a Finsupp (the exponent of each prime), multiplicativity (factorization(mn) = factorization(m) + factorization(n)), and the coprimality criterion. Used in number theory and in UniqueFactorizationDomain results. Distinct from Data/Nat/Prime (primality testing).

Name: LCM.lean
Description: LCM via factorisation.

Name: PrimePow.lean
Description: Prime powers via factorisation.

Name: Root.lean
Description: Roots via factorisation.

Name: Induction.lean
Description: Induction on factorisation.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Prime factorisation: `Nat.factorization n`.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Factorisation definitions.

Name: Divisors.lean
Description: Divisors via factorisation.

Name: Factorial
Description: The factorial function n! = 1 × 2 × … × n and the double factorial. Explicit values, monotonicity, divisibility (n! is divisible by every m ≤ n), and Stirling bounds. Used in binomial coefficients (Choose) and in the Gamma function (Γ(n+1) = n!). Distinct from Data/Nat/Choose (binomial coefficients, built on Factorial).

Name: DoubleFactorial.lean
Description: Double factorial `n!! = n(n-2)(n-4)⋯`.

Name: NatCast.lean
Description: Factorial and nat cast.

Name: SuperFactorial.lean
Description: Superfactorial `sf(n) = ∏_{k=1}^n k!`.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Factorial: `n! = ∏_{k=1}^n k`.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Factorial as a big operator.

Name: Cast.lean
Description: Factorial casts to rings.

Name: Periodic.lean
Description: Periodic functions on ℕ: a function f : ℕ → M is periodic with period n if f (a + n) = f a, with the basic instances and the interaction with Nat.ModEq. Distinct from Data/Nat/ModEq.

Name: Pairing.lean
Description: The Cantor pairing function: Nat.pair m n (a bijection ℕ × ℕ → ℕ) and its inverse Nat.unpair, used in encodability. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: PSub.lean
Description: Protected subtraction for ℕ: Nat.psub m n (returns Some (m - n) if n ≤ m and None otherwise), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: PrimeFin.lean
Description: Primes in Fin n: the interaction between Nat.Prime and the finite set of primes below n, with the finiteness of the set of primes ≤ n. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic and GCDMonoid/Nat.

Name: Multiplicity.lean
Description: Multiplicity of a prime in ℕ: the p-adic valuation vₚ(n) = the exponent of p in the factorisation of n, with the interaction with Nat.factors and the product formula. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic and UniqueFactorizationDomain/Multiplicity.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Modular arithmetic for ℕ: Nat.ModEq n a b (a ≡ b [MOD n]), the ring structure, and the Chinese Remainder Theorem. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic and Data/Int/ModEq.

Name: Nth.lean
Description: The n-th natural number satisfying a predicate: Nat.nth p n (the n-th smallest k satisfying p), the inverse of Nat.count. Distinct from Data/Nat/Find and Data/Nat/Count.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for ℕ in Mathlib: the setup of ℕ notation and numeral literals beyond core Lean. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Totient.lean
Description: Euler's totient function: Nat.totient n = card {k < n | gcd k n = 1} (the number of integers below n coprime to n), with the multiplicativity φ(mn) = φ(m)φ(n) for gcd m n = 1. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Successor and predecessor for ℕ: the SuccOrder and PredOrder instances for ℕ, and the interaction with the well-founded order. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: WithBot.lean
Description: Natural numbers with a bottom element: ℕ ∪ {⊥} = WithBot ℕ and the interaction with ENat, and the floor function from WithBot. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic and ENat/Defs.

Name: Upto.lean
Description: The type of naturals up to n: Nat.Upto n = {k : ℕ | k ≤ n} as a subtype, used in finite induction. Distinct from Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Size.lean
Description: The binary size of a natural number: Nat.size n (the number of bits needed to represent n, i.e., ⌈log₂(n+1)⌉), with the basic properties. Distinct from Data/Nat/Log.

Name: Set.lean
Description: Set-theoretic properties of ℕ: the interaction between ℕ and Set operations (Set.range, Set.image of Nat functions), and the Archimedean property expressed in terms of sets. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Squarefree.lean
Description: Squarefree natural numbers: a natural number n is squarefree if no prime appears to the power ≥ 2 in its factorisation, with the interaction with Nat.factors. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic and Squarefree/Basic.

Name: Sqrt.lean
Description: The integer square root of ℕ: Nat.sqrt n (the floor of √n), with the basic properties (Nat.sqrt n ^ 2 ≤ n < (Nat.sqrt n + 1) ^ 2). Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: ChineseRemainder.lean
Description: The Chinese Remainder Theorem for ℕ: if gcd m n = 1 then the system x ≡ a [MOD m], x ≡ b [MOD n] has a unique solution modulo mn. Distinct from Data/Nat/ModEq and RingTheory/Coprime/Ideal.

Name: Bitwise.lean
Description: Bitwise operations on ℕ: Nat.land, Nat.lor, Nat.xor, Nat.shiftLeft, Nat.shiftRight, with the basic lemmas. Distinct from Data/Nat/Bits and Data/Int/Bitwise.

Name: Dist.lean
Description: The distance between natural numbers: Nat.dist m n = max m n - min m n = |m - n| (truncated subtraction-based absolute difference), with the triangle inequality. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Count.lean
Description: Counting naturals with a property: Nat.count p n (the number of k < n satisfying decidable predicate p), with the basic API and the interaction with Finset.filter. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: BinaryRec.lean
Description: Binary recursion on natural numbers: Nat.binaryRec (induction on the binary representation), with the interaction between Nat.div2 and Nat.bit. Distinct from Data/Nat/Bitwise.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic natural number theory: additional lemmas for ℕ beyond core Lean — the ordering, arithmetic identities, and the interaction with ℤ and ℚ. Foundation for all Nat files.

Name: Bits.lean
Description: The bit representation of natural numbers: Nat.bits n (the list of bits from least to most significant), with the interaction between bits and arithmetic. Distinct from Data/Nat/Bitwise.

Name: BitIndices.lean
Description: Bit indices of natural numbers: the set of positions where the binary representation has a 1-bit, used in subset-sum and combinatorial arguments. Distinct from Data/Nat/Bitwise.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on ℕ: the lattice of ℕ with max = ⊔ and min = ⊓, and the interaction with the arithmetic. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initial natural number setup for Mathlib: the basic definitions and instances for ℕ needed early in the import hierarchy. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: MaxPowDiv.lean
Description: Maximum power divisor: Nat.maxPowDiv p n (the largest k with p^k ∣ n), used in the p-adic valuation. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic and UniqueFactorizationDomain/Multiplicity.

Name: Log.lean
Description: Natural number logarithm: Nat.log b n (the floor of log_b(n) for b ≥ 2 and n ≥ 1), with the basic properties (b^(log b n) ≤ n < b^(log b n + 1)). Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Factors.lean
Description: The fundamental theorem of arithmetic: every natural number n ≥ 2 factors uniquely into a product of primes. Nat.factors gives the sorted list of prime factors with multiplicity; Nat.prod_factors proves n = ∏ Nat.factors n for n > 0; and List.Perm-based uniqueness establishes the prime factorization is unique up to reordering. The foundation for divisibility theory in ℕ and the basis for modern cryptography.

Name: EvenOddRec.lean
Description: Even-odd recursion on ℕ: Nat.evenOddRec (induction on even/odd, equivalent to binary induction), used in parity arguments. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Hyperoperation.lean
Description: Hyperoperations (Ackermann hierarchy): the family of operations H n a b (H 0 = succ, H 1 = add, H 2 = mul, H 3 = pow, H 4 = tetration), with the basic recursion and growth properties. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Find.lean
Description: Finding naturals with a property: Nat.find (the smallest n satisfying a decidable predicate), with the basic API and the interaction with Nat.count. Distinct from Data/Nat/Basic.

Name: Multiset
Description: Multisets (finite bags): the quotient of List by permutation, with union, intersection, and sum.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi-type constructions for Multisets: the multiset of all functions from the support of a Multiset, used in symmetric function theory. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Powerset.lean
Description: Powersets of Multisets: Multiset.powerset s (all sub-multisets of s), with cardinality = ∏ (count a + 1) over the distinct elements. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Range.lean
Description: The range Multiset: Multiset.range n = {0, 1, …, n-1} as a Multiset, with the interaction with Finset.range and List.range. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Pairwise.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions on Multisets: Multiset.Pairwise r s asserting r holds between any two distinct elements (with distinct positions), the multiset analogue of List.Pairwise. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: MapFold.lean
Description: Map-fold compositions on Multisets: map followed by fold, with the interaction between Multiset.map, Multiset.sum, and Multiset.prod. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Fold.

Name: NatAntidiagonal.lean
Description: ℕ-valued antidiagonals for Multisets: the multiset analogue of the antidiagonal (pairs summing to n) with ℕ coefficients. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Antidiagonal.

Name: OrderedMonoid.lean
Description: Ordered monoid structure on Multisets: the interaction between the Multiset ordering (≤) and the additive monoid structure (addition as concatenation). Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Sym.lean
Description: Symmetric products of Multisets: Multiset.sym n s (all n-element sub-multisets of s, with repetition) and the interaction with Multiset.powerset. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: UnionInter.lean
Description: Union and intersection of Multisets: Multiset.union s t = s + (t - s) and Multiset.inter s t = s - (s - t), the lattice operations on multisets. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Lattice.

Name: ZeroCons.lean
Description: The zero-cons structure of Multisets: induction principle on Multiset (either 0 or a :: s), and the interaction with the nonempty condition. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Sums over Multisets: Multiset.sum (for an AddCommMonoid) and Multiset.prod (for a CommMonoid), as the main big-operator operations on Multisets. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Fold.

Name: Replicate.lean
Description: Replicated elements in Multisets: Multiset.replicate n a = n copies of a, with count_replicate and the interaction with Multiset.sum. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Sections.lean
Description: Sections of a Multiset of Multisets: the Multiset of all choices of one element from each component Multiset, with cardinality = product of component counts. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Pi.

Name: Sort.lean
Description: Sorting Multisets: converting a Multiset to a sorted list (Multiset.sort r s : List α), with the uniqueness of the sorted form for a linear order. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic and Data/List/Sort.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice structure on Multisets: the meet (Multiset.inter) and join (Multiset.union) operations making (Multiset α, ≤) a lattice. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Count.lean
Description: Counting in Multisets: Multiset.count a s (the multiplicity of a in s), with count_add, count_sub, and the interaction with Finset. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Dedup.lean
Description: Deduplication of Multisets: Multiset.dedup s (keep each element once), with the interaction with Finset.val and Multiset.Nodup. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Multiset: the Multiset α type as a quotient type, the basic operations, and the API. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic (derived results).

Name: Bind.lean
Description: The Multiset monad: Multiset as a monad with bind = flatMap (mapping and flattening), and the interaction with Multiset.map and Multiset.join. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: AddSub.lean
Description: Addition and subtraction in Multiset: Multiset.add (concatenation, the monoid operation) and Multiset.sub (truncated subtraction, removing elements), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Antidiagonal.lean
Description: Antidiagonals of Multisets: Multiset.antidiagonal s = the multiset of pairs (t₁, t₂) with t₁ + t₂ = s, used in the Cauchy product. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Multiset theory: Multiset α = Quotient of List α by permutation, with membership, count, sub-multiset (≤), and the basic operations. Foundation for all Multiset files.

Name: Fold.lean
Description: Folding over Multisets: Multiset.fold op s m (fold a commutative associative operation over a Multiset), with the big-operator interaction. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functor and traversable instances for Multiset: Multiset as a functor (via Multiset.map) and the traversable instance. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Intervals of Multisets: the sub-multisets between two Multisets, and the interaction with the partially ordered set structure. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Fintype.lean
Description: Fintype instances for Multisets: when the type of Multisets of α is finite, and the cardinality formula. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: DershowitzManna.lean
Description: The Dershowitz-Manna ordering on Multisets: the well-founded order on Multiset α (for a well-founded order on α) where s < t if s is obtained from t by replacing an element with finitely many smaller elements. Used in termination proofs. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Filtering Multisets: Multiset.filter p s (elements of s satisfying predicate p), with the partition into filter and complement. Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: FinsetOps.lean
Description: Finset-like operations on Multisets: the interaction between Multiset operations and Finset operations (union, intersection, etc. for ordered multisets). Distinct from Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Matrix
Description: Matrices as doubly-indexed functions (Matrix m n alpha): ring structure, block matrices, and det/trace.

Name: DMatrix.lean
Description: Dependent matrices (DMatrix): matrices where the type of each entry depends on the row and column index, generalising ordinary matrices. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: DualNumber.lean
Description: Dual numbers as 2×2 matrices: the embedding of the dual numbers R[ε]/(ε²) into Mat₂(R) via the matrix [[a, b], [0, a]] for a + bε. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Composition.lean
Description: Composition of matrix maps: the interaction between matrix multiplication and function composition, and the associated ring map Ring → Matrix n n Ring. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Diagonal.lean
Description: Diagonal matrices: the diagonal matrix diag f (equal to f i on the diagonal and 0 elsewhere), the trace (sum of diagonal entries), and the interaction with the determinant. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: PEquiv.lean
Description: Partial equivalences as matrices: the embedding of PEquiv α β (partial bijections) into Matrix α β ℕ (or Matrix α β R) as 0-1 matrices, used in permutation matrix theory. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Reflection.lean
Description: Reflection matrices: the explicit matrix form of reflection hyperplanes, used in the theory of Coxeter groups and root systems. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Invertible.lean
Description: Invertible matrices: the Invertible typeclass for matrices with an explicit inverse, and the interaction with the general linear group GL n R. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic and Ring/Invertible.

Name: Mul.lean
Description: Matrix multiplication: the main API for Matrix.mul, including the interaction with transpose (AB)ᵀ = BᵀAᵀ, the identity matrix, and the associated ring axioms. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic matrix theory: Matrix m n R = m → n → R with the ring structure (addition, multiplication, transpose), the identity matrix (1 : Matrix n n R), and the interaction with the module structure. Foundation for all Matrix files.

Name: Basis.lean
Description: The basis of the matrix space: the standard basis matrices {Eᵢⱼ}ᵢⱼ (= 1 at (i,j) and 0 elsewhere), their interaction with matrix multiplication (EᵢⱼEₖₗ = δⱼₖEᵢₗ), and the matrix form of a linear map. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Action.lean
Description: Group actions on matrices: the action of the general linear group GL n R on matrices, and the interaction with matrix multiplication. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Auto.lean
Description: Automation for matrix computations: norm_num and decide extensions for matrix arithmetic, enabling automated verification of matrix identities. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Cartan.lean
Description: Cartan matrices in matrix form: the explicit matrix form of Cartan matrices for the classical Lie algebras, used in the classification of semisimple Lie algebras. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: ColumnRowPartitioned.lean
Description: Column and row partitioned matrices: decomposing a matrix as a horizontal stack of column blocks or a vertical stack of row blocks. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Block.

Name: Bilinear.lean
Description: Bilinear forms and matrices: representing a bilinear form as a matrix and the interaction with the transpose and trace. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Block.lean
Description: Block matrices: the decomposition of a matrix into blocks, block multiplication, the block diagonal matrix, and the Schur complement. Distinct from Data/Matrix/Basic.

Name: Num
Description: Positional numeral representations (Num, PosNum): binary numbers for efficient kernel computation.

Name: Prime.lean
Description: Primality testing for Num: a decision procedure for Nat.Prime using the binary Num representation, used in norm_num extensions. Distinct from Data/Num/Basic.

Name: ZNum.lean
Description: Binary integers ZNum: an efficient binary representation of ℤ (using Num for positive and negative parts), with arithmetic and comparison. Distinct from Data/Num/Basic and Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for Num: the interaction between Num and ℕ via the coercion, and the correctness of the Num arithmetic. Distinct from Data/Num/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Binary numeric types: Num (an efficient binary representation of ℕ) and PosNum (positive binary naturals), with the basic arithmetic and comparison. Foundation for all Num files.

Name: Bitwise.lean
Description: Bitwise operations on Num: land, lor, xor, shiftLeft, shiftRight for the binary Num type, with the interaction with Nat.land etc. Distinct from Data/Num/Basic.

Name: NNReal
Description: Nonnegative reals R>=0: the subtype {x : R // 0 <= x} with its order, topology, and arithmetic.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star structure on NNReal: the trivial star (star x = x) making ℝ≥0 a commutative star semiring. Distinct from Data/NNReal/Defs and Algebra/Star/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for NNReal: ℝ≥0 as a subsemiring of ℝ with the coercion and the toNNReal truncation map, and the basic API. Foundation for all NNReal files.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic NNReal theory: additional results for ℝ≥0 = {r : ℝ | 0 ≤ r} beyond the definitions — the interaction between ℝ≥0 and ENNReal/EReal, and basic algebraic instances. Distinct from Data/NNReal/Defs.

Name: NNRat
Description: Nonnegative rationals Q>=0: the subtype {q : Q // 0 <= q} with arithmetic and order.

Name: Floor.lean
Description: Floor and ceiling for NNRat: the interaction of the floor function with ℚ≥0 and the basic floor/ceiling instances. Distinct from Data/NNRat/Defs.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary lemmas for NNRat: a collection of computational lemmas about ℚ≥0 arithmetic and its interaction with ℚ and ℝ≥0. Distinct from Data/NNRat/Defs.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order structure on NNRat: the linear order on ℚ≥0 and its interaction with the arithmetic operations. Distinct from Data/NNRat/Defs.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for NNRat: ∑ and ∏ of NNRat families, and the casting to ℚ and ℝ. Distinct from Data/NNRat/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for NNRat: ℚ≥0 = {q : ℚ | 0 ≤ q} as a subsemiring of ℚ, with the coercion and the basic API. Foundation for all NNRat files.

Name: Encodable.lean
Description: Encodability of NNRat: encoding NNRat as a natural number via the encoding of the numerator and denominator. Distinct from Data/NNRat/Defs.

Name: List
Description: Lists in Lean: the List type with append, map, filter, fold, length, and a large library of lemmas.

Name: Perm
Description: Permutations of lists: List.Perm, the reflexive-transitive-symmetric closure of the swap relation. Perm is equivalent to being a permutation of elements (same multiset). Used to define Multiset (List quotiented by Perm) and to state commutativity of summation. Distinct from GroupTheory/Perm (permutations as bijections of a type).

Name: Subperm.lean
Description: Sub-permutations of lists.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice of list permutations.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: List permutation basics.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi-type constructions for lists: the list of all functions from a List to a type (the cartesian product of lists), used in enumeration. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Permutation.lean
Description: Permutations of lists: List.Perm l₁ l₂ (l₁ is a permutation of l₂), closure under list operations, and the interaction with Multiset and Finset. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: PeriodicityLemma.lean
Description: Periodicity lemma for lists: if a list l satisfies l.take n = l.drop n (up to some bound), it is periodic, used in formal language and combinatorics. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Prime.lean
Description: Primality and lists: List.factors n (the prime factorisation of n as a sorted list), with the interaction between prime lists and UFD factorisation in ℕ. Distinct from Data/List/Basic and GCDMonoid/Nat.

Name: ReduceOption.lean
Description: Reducing Option lists: List.reduceOption l (keeping only the Some values from a List (Option α)), with the interaction with List.filterMap. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Range.lean
Description: The list range: List.range n = [0, 1, …, n-1] : List ℕ, List.Ico a b = [a, …, b-1], and the interaction with Finset.range. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: ProdSigma.lean
Description: Product and sigma list constructions: List.product l₁ l₂ = [(a, b) | a ∈ l₁, b ∈ l₂] and the sigma version, with the interaction with Finset.product. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Palindrome.lean
Description: Palindromes in lists: List.Palindrome l asserting l = l.reverse, with the basic instances and the interaction with List.reverse. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Nodup.lean
Description: Nodup lists (lists without duplicates): List.Nodup l (all elements distinct), closure under map, append, filter, and the interaction with Finset and Multiset. Distinct from Data/List/Dedup.

Name: NatAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Natural number antidiagonals as Lists: List.antidiagonal n = [(0,n), (1,n-1), …, (n,0)] : List (ℕ × ℕ), with the interaction with Finset.antidiagonal. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Monad.lean
Description: The List monad: List as a monad (pure = [·], bind = flatMap), and the interaction with do-notation and List.forM. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: NodupEquivFin.lean
Description: Equivalence between nodup lists and Fin: a list l of length n with Nodup is equivalent to a function Fin n → α, giving the interaction with Finset.card and Finset.equivFin. Distinct from Data/List/Nodup.

Name: Pairwise.lean
Description: Pairwise relations on lists: List.Pairwise r l asserting r holds between every pair of distinct positions, with closure under map, filter, and append. Distinct from Data/List/Nodup (the special case r = (≠)) and Data/List/Chain.

Name: OfFn.lean
Description: Lists from functions: List.ofFn f : List β for f : Fin n → β (the list [f 0, f 1, …, f (n-1)]), with the basic API and the interaction with Vector. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: OffDiag.lean
Description: The off-diagonal of a list: List.offDiag l = [(a, b) | a ∈ l, b ∈ l, a ≠ b] (all unordered pairs of distinct elements), with cardinality = length * (length - 1). Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Rotate.lean
Description: Rotating lists: List.rotate l k (rotate l by k positions), with the interaction with List.cycle and the periodicity. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: TakeWhile.lean
Description: takeWhile and dropWhile: List.takeWhile p l (take elements while p holds) and List.dropWhile p l (drop elements while p holds), with the partition property. Distinct from Data/List/TakeDrop.

Name: TakeDrop.lean
Description: Take and drop for lists: List.take n l and List.drop n l, with the interaction (take n l ++ drop n l = l), and lemmas about length and membership. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Sym.lean
Description: Symmetric products of lists: List.sym n l (the list of all n-element multisets from l), as the list version of Sym n α. Distinct from Data/List/Basic and Data/Sym/Basic.

Name: TFAE.lean
Description: The TFAE (The Following Are Equivalent) tactic infrastructure: List.TFAE l (all propositions in l are pairwise equivalent) and the tfae_have / tfae_finish tactic. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Zip.lean
Description: Zipping lists: List.zip l₁ l₂ = [(a, b) | (a, b) ∈ zip l₁ l₂] (truncates to the shorter list), List.zipWith, and the interaction with List.unzip. Distinct from Data/List/Map2 and Data/List/Basic.

Name: Triplewise.lean
Description: Triplewise relations on lists: List.Triplewise r l asserting r holds for every triple of distinct elements, generalising Pairwise. Distinct from Data/List/Pairwise.

Name: ToFinsupp.lean
Description: Converting a list to a finitely supported function: List.toFinsupp l = the function ℕ →₀ M encoding l as a finitely supported sequence. Distinct from Data/List/Basic and Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: Sublists.lean
Description: Sublists of a list: List.sublist l₁ l₂ (l₁ is obtained from l₂ by removing some elements preserving order) and List.sublists l (all subsequences), with cardinality 2^length l. Distinct from Data/List/Infix (contiguous sublists).

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma-type constructions for lists: the list of dependent pairs from a list of keys and a function assigning lists of values, used in association list theory. Distinct from Data/List/AList.

Name: Shortlex.lean
Description: The shortlex (length-lexicographic) order on lists: compare first by length and then lexicographically, making the set of finite lists well-ordered. Distinct from Data/List/Lex.

Name: Sections.lean
Description: Sections of a list of lists: List.sections l (all choices of one element from each list in l), with cardinality = product of lengths. Distinct from Data/List/Pi.

Name: Sort.lean
Description: Sorting lists: List.insertionSort and List.mergeSort (merge sort), with the correctness theorem (sorted and a permutation of the original), and the interaction with List.Perm. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: SplitOn.lean
Description: Splitting a list on a separator: List.splitOn a l (split at every occurrence of the separator element a, producing a List (List α)). Distinct from Data/List/SplitBy.

Name: SplitLengths.lean
Description: Splitting lists by length: conditions ensuring a split of a list into two parts of given lengths. Distinct from Data/List/TakeDrop.

Name: SplitBy.lean
Description: Splitting a list by a predicate: List.splitBy p l (split into maximal runs satisfying p and runs not satisfying p), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/TakeWhile.

Name: Duplicate.lean
Description: Duplicate detection in lists: List.Duplicate a l asserting a appears at least twice in l, with the interaction with List.Nodup and List.count. Distinct from Data/List/Nodup.

Name: DropRight.lean
Description: Dropping elements from the right of a list: List.dropRight n l (equivalent to take (length l - n) l), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/TakeDrop.

Name: Destutter.lean
Description: Destuttering a list: List.destutter r l (remove consecutive duplicates under a relation r), with the interaction with List.dedup and List.Chain'. Distinct from Data/List/Dedup.

Name: Enum.lean
Description: Enumeration of list elements: List.enum l = [(0, a₀), (1, a₁), …] and List.enumFrom k l, pairing each element with its index. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Fold.lean
Description: Fold operations on lists: List.foldl, List.foldr, and the associated big-operator lemmas for lists, including the interaction with commutative operations. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Flatten.lean
Description: Flattening nested lists: List.flatten (previously List.join) for List (List α), with the interaction with List.bind and List.map. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: FinRange.lean
Description: The list of all elements of Fin n: List.finRange n = [0, 1, …, n-1] : List (Fin n), with the interaction with Finset.univ. Distinct from Data/List/Range and Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core List definitions for Mathlib: additional definitions (List.Sublist, List.Pairwise, List.Nodup, List.Forall₂) beyond core Lean, forming the API base for all List files.

Name: Chain.lean
Description: Chaining predicates on lists: List.Chain r (pairwise adjacent relation), List.Chain' r (each consecutive pair satisfies r), and List.Pairwise for non-consecutive pairs. Distinct from Data/List/Pairwise.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic list theory beyond core Lean: additional lemmas for List α — nth, take, drop, zip, reverse, append interactions, and the basic algebraic structure of lists. Foundation for all List files.

Name: AList.lean
Description: Association lists: AList α β = a list of key-value pairs (α × β) with no duplicate keys, with the lookup, insert, erase, and union operations. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: ChainOfFn.lean
Description: Chains built from functions: constructing a Chain' from a function f : Fin n → α with the relation r(f i, f (i+1)). Distinct from Data/List/Chain and Data/List/OfFn.

Name: Dedup.lean
Description: Deduplication of lists: List.dedup l (remove duplicates preserving order of first occurrence), with the interaction with List.Nodup and Finset. Distinct from Data/List/Basic and Data/List/Nodup.

Name: Cycle.lean
Description: Cyclic equivalence of lists: List.IsRotation (one list is a rotation of another) and List.Cycle α (equivalence classes of lists under rotation), the cyclic order, and the interaction with List.rotate. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Count.lean
Description: Counting elements in lists: List.countP p l (number of elements satisfying p) and List.count a l (number of occurrences of a), with the interaction with List.filter and membership. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Forall2.lean
Description: Pointwise relations on lists: List.Forall₂ r l₁ l₂ asserting r holds between corresponding elements, with the interaction with List.map and List.zip. Distinct from Data/List/Pairwise.

Name: Lex.lean
Description: The lexicographic order on lists: List.Lex r l₁ l₂ (lexicographic order with respect to a relation r), with the decidability and the interaction with List.lt for ordered types. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary list lemmas: a collection of technical lemmas about lists not naturally placed elsewhere, including simp lemmas and decidability instances. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice operations on lists: the interaction of List.union, List.inter, and List.diff with the lattice structure on List, and the sublattice properties. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Lookmap.lean
Description: Map with lookup: List.lookmap f l (applies f to each element but only modifies elements where f returns Some), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: ModifyLast.lean
Description: Modifying the last element: List.modifyLast f l (apply f to the last element), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: MinMax.lean
Description: Minimum and maximum of lists: List.minimum' and List.maximum' for nonempty lists over a linear order, with the interaction with List.foldl. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Map2.lean
Description: Mapping two lists: List.zipWith (map over pairs of corresponding elements) and List.map₂ (partial map for lists of different lengths). Distinct from Data/List/Zip.

Name: Iterate.lean
Description: Iterating a function on a list: List.iterate f n a = [a, f a, f² a, …, fⁿ⁻¹ a] (the orbit of a under n iterations of f), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Induction.lean
Description: Induction principles for lists: additional induction principles beyond the standard (e.g., strong induction on length, induction from the right, induction on two lists simultaneously). Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Indexes.lean
Description: Index-based list operations: findIdx, nthFindIdx, and other operations accessing elements by their index in the list. Distinct from Data/List/Enum.

Name: GetD.lean
Description: Default get for lists: List.getD l n d (get the n-th element or the default d if out of bounds), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: Infix.lean
Description: Sublists and infixes: List.IsPrefix, List.IsSuffix, List.IsInfix, and the associated decidability and interaction with length. Distinct from Data/List/Sublists.

Name: Intervals.lean
Description: Interval predicates on lists: conditions for a sorted list to cover an interval [a, b], and the interaction with List.Ico and List.range. Distinct from Data/List/Basic.

Name: InsertNth.lean
Description: Inserting the n-th element: List.insertNth n a l (insert a before position n), with the interaction with take and drop. Distinct from Data/List/InsertIdx.

Name: InsertIdx.lean
Description: Inserting at an index: List.insertIdx n a l (insert element a at position n), with the basic API. Distinct from Data/List/InsertNth.

Name: Fintype
Description: Fintype: types with a decidable enumeration as a Finset, with cardinality and decidable forall/exists.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for products: α × β is Fintype with card = card α * card β. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Quotient.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for quotient types: Quotient r is Fintype when α is Fintype and r is a decidable equivalence. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Sets.lean
Description: Fintype instances for sets: the powerset Finset (set of all Finsets of a Fintype) and the Fintype instance for subtypes of a Fintype. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Powerset.lean
Description: The powerset of a finite type: the proof that a type with n elements has exactly 2ⁿ subsets, formalized as Fintype.card_powerset. The bijection between subsets and indicator functions (α → Bool) underlies this count. Contains Fintype instances for Finset α and the Boolean algebra structure on the powerset. Explains why search spaces and truth tables grow exponentially with the number of variables.

Name: Perm.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for permutations: Equiv.Perm α is a Fintype when α is Fintype, with card = (card α)!. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Card.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for Pi types: ∏ i, f i is Fintype with card = ∏ card (f i), using Finset.pi. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Prod.

Name: Pigeonhole.lean
Description: Pigeonhole principle for Fintype: if card α > card β then any function f : α → β is not injective; dually if card α < card β then no surjection exists. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Card.

Name: Units.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for unit groups: Rˣ is Fintype when R is a finite ring, with card Rˣ = Euler's φ(card R). Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic and Ring/Units.

Name: Vector.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for vectors: Vector α n is Fintype with card = (card α)^n. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: WithTopBot.lean
Description: Fintype instances for WithTop and WithBot: WithTop α and WithBot α are Fintype with card = card α + 1. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for sum types: α ⊕ β is Fintype with card = card α + card β. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Shrink.lean
Description: Universe shrinking for Fintype: transporting Fintype instances between universes. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: The Fintype instance for sigma types: (i : α) × f i is Fintype with card = ∑ card (f i). Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Sort.lean
Description: Sorting a Fintype: the sorted list of all elements of a Fintype by a linear order, and the interaction with Finset.sort. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic and Data/List/Sort.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Fintype: the Fintype α typeclass with Finset.univ and the membership ∈ universal set, and basic instances for common types. Foundation for all Fintype files.

Name: EquivFin.lean
Description: Equivalences between Fintype and Fin n: Fintype.equivFin α : α ≃ Fin (Fintype.card α) (a bijection from any Fintype to Fin n), with the interaction with permutations. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Card.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Fintype instances for Fin n: Fin n is a Fintype with card = n and the universal set {0,1,…,n-1}. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Defs and Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: CardEmbedding.lean
Description: Cardinality and embeddings: Fintype.card α ≤ Fintype.card β iff there is an injection α ↪ β (for finite types), with the pigeonhole principle and the surjection-embedding duality. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Card.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Fintype theory: additional lemmas for Fintype α (a type with a finitely enumerable Finset.univ), the interaction with Finset.card, and basic instances. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Defs.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for Fintype: ∑ x : α, f x and ∏ x : α, f x over a Fintype α (using Finset.univ), with the basic interaction between Fintype sums and Finset sums. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of Fintype: Fintype.card α = card (Finset.univ), the main cardinality computation lemmas, and the interaction with equivalences (Fintype.card α = Fintype.card β ↔ Nonempty (α ≃ β)). Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Fintype instances for Option: Option α is Fintype with card = card α + 1. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic and Data/Option/Basic.

Name: Order.lean
Description: Order-theoretic Fintype results: a finite linear order has a maximum and minimum element, and the interaction between Fintype and OrderIso. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Parity.lean
Description: Parity in Fintype: the number of even/odd elements in a Fintype, the interaction with the parity of permutations, and the sign homomorphism Equiv.Perm α → {±1}. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: OfMap.lean
Description: Fintype instances from surjective maps: if f : α → β is surjective and α is Fintype, then β is Fintype (with card β ≤ card α). Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Inv.lean
Description: Inverses in Fintype groups: over a finite group, every injective function is surjective and every surjective function is injective, with the automatic inverse. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic and Group/Basic.

Name: Lattice.lean
Description: Lattice instances for Fintype: the interaction of Fintype with lattice operations (Fintype.sum over a lattice-valued function, sup over Fintype). Distinct from Data/Fintype/Basic and Lattice/Basic.

Name: List.lean
Description: Fintype instances via lists: constructing Fintype instances from explicit lists of all elements, and the interaction between Fintype and List.nodup. Distinct from Data/Fintype/Defs.

Name: Finsupp
Description: Finitely supported functions (alpha to_0 beta): the free module functor, with support, sum, and the algebraic operations.

Name: MonomialOrder
Description: Monomial orders on finitely supported functions: total orders on (α →₀ ℕ) compatible with addition (e.g., lexicographic, graded lexicographic). Used in Gröbner basis theory to define leading monomials of multivariate polynomials. Distinct from the algebraic structure of Finsupp — this folder specifically orders monomials.

Name: DegLex.lean
Description: DegLex monomial order on Finsupp.

Name: Pointwise.lean
Description: Pointwise operations on Finsupp: the pointwise product of finitely supported functions (f * g)(a) = f(a) * g(a), and the interaction with the support and the big-operator sum. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: PointwiseSMul.lean
Description: Pointwise scalar multiplication on Finsupp: the action of a scalar r on a finitely supported function via (r • f)(a) = r • f(a). Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Pointwise and Data/Finsupp/SMul.

Name: PWO.lean
Description: Partially well-ordered Finsupp: the type (α →₀ ℕ) of monomials with the pointwise ≤ order is a partially well-ordered set, used in the termination of Buchberger's algorithm. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Order and Data/Finsupp/WellFounded.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on Finsupp: the pointwise partial order f ≤ g ↔ ∀ a, f a ≤ g a, the lattice structure (for lattice-valued M), and the interaction with the support. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Lex.

Name: NeLocus.lean
Description: The ne-locus of two Finsupps: the Finset {a | f a ≠ g a} (where two finitely supported functions differ), with the interaction with the support and the symmetric difference. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for Finsupp: the →₀ notation for finitely supported functions and the single f n syntax. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Defs.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Finsupp indexed by Option: the isomorphism (Option α →₀ M) ≅ M × (α →₀ M) splitting the none and some components. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: ToDFinsupp.lean
Description: Converting between Finsupp and DFinsupp: the canonical isomorphism (α →₀ M) ≅ (Π₀ a : α, M) between finitely supported functions and dependent finitely supported functions with constant value type. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/DFinsupp/Defs.

Name: Weight.lean
Description: Weight functions on Finsupp: a weight function w : α →₀ ℕ (a finitely supported weight vector), with the associated weighted degree Finsupp.weight and the interaction with monomial orders. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: WellFounded.lean
Description: Well-foundedness of Finsupp orders: the lex order on (α →₀ ℕ) is well-founded (when α has a well-order), used in the termination proofs for Gröbner basis algorithms. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Lex and Data/Finsupp/PWO.

Name: SMulWithZero.lean
Description: Scalar multiplication by zero for Finsupp: the interaction of the SMulWithZero structure with finitely supported functions, ensuring 0 • f = 0. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/SMul.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Finsupp over a sigma type: the isomorphism (Σ i, α i) →₀ M ≅ Π₀ i, (α i →₀ M) (Fubini for Finsupp). Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/Sigma/Basic.

Name: Single.lean
Description: The single element of Finsupp: Finsupp.single a m (the function equal to m at a and 0 elsewhere), with the basic API (support of single, sum over single). Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Defs.

Name: SMul.lean
Description: Scalar multiplication on Finsupp: the R-module structure on (α →₀ M) when M is an R-module, via pointwise scalar multiplication (r • f)(a) = r • f(a). Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Module/Basic.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for finitely supported functions: Finsupp α M = (α →₀ M) with the support, coeff, single, and the zero element. Foundation for all Finsupp files.

Name: Encodable.lean
Description: Encodable instances for Finsupp: encoding a finitely supported function as a natural number via the encoding of its support and values. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: Ext.lean
Description: Extensionality for Finsupp: two finitely supported functions are equal iff they agree on every index, with the ext lemma. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: BigOperators.lean
Description: Big operators for Finsupp: Finsupp.sum f g = ∑_{a ∈ f.support} g a (f a), the main big-operator operation for finitely supported functions, with basic lemmas. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: AList.lean
Description: DFinsupp/Finsupp and association lists: representing a finitely supported function as an association list (list of key-value pairs), with the interaction between AList and Finsupp. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: Antidiagonal.lean
Description: Antidiagonals for Finsupp: Finsupp.antidiagonal f = {(g, h) | g + h = f} as a Finset for f : α →₀ ℕ, used in Cauchy products of power series. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/Finset/NatAntidiagonal.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Finsupp theory: additional lemmas for (α →₀ M) beyond the definitions — the single map, support interaction, sum, and basic algebraic instances. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Defs (core definitions).

Name: Lex.lean
Description: Lexicographic order on Finsupp: the Finsupp.Lex order comparing finitely supported functions lexicographically by the smallest element of the symmetric difference of supports. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Order and Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: MonomialOrder.lean
Description: Monomial orders on Finsupp: total orders on (σ →₀ ℕ) (the type of monomials in MvPolynomial σ R) compatible with addition — lex, grlex, grevlex — used in Gröbner basis theory. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Lex and RingTheory/MvPolynomial/MonomialOrder.

Name: Multiset.lean
Description: Finsupp and multisets: the isomorphism (α →₀ ℕ) ≅ Multiset α between ℕ-valued finitely supported functions and multisets. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/Multiset/Basic.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals for Finsupp: the Finset Finsupp.Icc f g = {h : α →₀ ℕ | f ≤ h ≤ g} for finitely supported ℕ-functions. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Order and Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Finsupp indexed by Fin n: the isomorphism between (Fin n →₀ M) and Vector M n, and the restriction/extension maps. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Fintype.lean
Description: Fintype instances for Finsupp: when (α →₀ M) is a finite type (finite α and M), with the cardinality formula. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/Fintype/Basic.

Name: Indicator.lean
Description: The indicator finsupp: Finsupp.indicator s f for a Finset s and function f, the finitely supported function equal to f on s and 0 elsewhere. Distinct from Data/Finsupp/Basic and Data/Finset/Filter.

Name: Finset
Description: Finite sets (Finset alpha): decidable subsets represented as multisets, with union, intersection, image, and sum/prod.

Name: Lattice
Description: Lattice operations on Finset: Finset.sup and Finset.inf (fold over a Finset using join/meet), Finset.iSup and Finset.iInf (indexed suprema/infima), and their interaction with Finset.image and Finset.biUnion. Used when taking the join of finitely many elements in a lattice. Distinct from Data/Finset (basic Finset operations without lattice structure).

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Pi Finset lattice.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product Finset lattice.

Name: Union.lean
Description: Union in Finset lattice.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Finset lattice basics.

Name: Fold.lean
Description: Finset lattice fold operations.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Finset lattice lemmas.

Name: PiInduction.lean
Description: Induction principles for Pi-type Finsets: induction on Finset.pi s f by adding one element at a time to each component. Distinct from Data/Finset/Pi.

Name: Piecewise.lean
Description: Piecewise functions defined on Finsets: a function that is f on s and g off s, with the interaction between Finset.piecewise and sum/product. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Set/Piecewise.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Products of Finsets indexed by a Fintype: Finset.pi s f = ∏ᵢ f i (the Finset of functions i → f(i)) and the cardinality |pi s f| = ∏ |f i|. Distinct from Data/Finset/Prod.

Name: Preimage.lean
Description: Preimage of a Finset: Finset.preimage f t = {a | f a ∈ t} as a Finset (when f is injective on the preimage), with the interaction with card. Distinct from Data/Finset/Image.

Name: Powerset.lean
Description: Finset.powersetCard and the combinatorial counting formula: the number of k-element subsets of an n-element set equals C(n, k) = n! / (k!(n-k)!), formalized as Finset.card_powersetCard. Contains Pascal's recurrence C(n+1,k+1) = C(n,k) + C(n,k+1), the Vandermonde identity, and the binomial theorem via big operators. The fundamental formula used in probability, genetics, and combinatorial enumeration.

Name: PImage.lean
Description: Partial image of a Finset: Finset.pimage f s = the Finset of f(a) for a ∈ s when f is a partial function. Distinct from Data/Finset/Image.

Name: NoncommProd.lean
Description: Non-commutative products over Finsets: Finset.noncommProd s f for a function f : α → M where the multiplication is not assumed commutative, using the Multiset.noncommProd. Distinct from Data/Finset/Fold.

Name: NatDivisors.lean
Description: Finsets of natural number divisors: Nat.divisors n as a Finset, the interaction with Nat.factorization, and the sum-over-divisors formula. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and GCDMonoid/Nat.

Name: NatAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Natural number antidiagonals as Finsets: Finset.antidiagonal n = {(i, j) | i + j = n} as a Finset, with card (antidiagonal n) = n + 1. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and BigOperators/NatAntidiagonal.

Name: Pairwise.lean
Description: Pairwise conditions on Finsets: Set.Pairwise and Finset.Pairwise (pairwise disjointness, pairwise inequality, etc.), with the interaction with card. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/List/Pairwise.

Name: Order.lean
Description: The order structure on Finset: the partial order by inclusion (s ≤ t ↔ s ⊆ t), the lattice of Finsets (s ⊔ t = s ∪ t, s ⊓ t = s ∩ t), and the powerset ordering. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Option.lean
Description: Finsets of Option types: the interaction between Finset α and Finset (Option α) (erasure and insertion of none), and the card formula. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Option/Basic.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Product Finsets: Finset.product s t = s ×ˢ t = {(a, b) | a ∈ s, b ∈ t}, with card (s ×ˢ t) = card s * card t. Distinct from Data/Finset/Pi (multi-ary product).

Name: Sym.lean
Description: Symmetric powers of Finsets: Finset.sym s n = the Finset of n-element multisets from s (Sym n α), with card = C(card s + n - 1, n). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Sym/Basic.

Name: Sups.lean
Description: Suprema and infima of Finsets: Finset.sup s f and Finset.inf s f for a Finset s and function f : α → β into a SemilatticeSup. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Lattice/Basic.

Name: Sum.lean
Description: Disjoint union of Finsets (coproduct): Finset.disjSum s t (a Finset of α ⊕ β), and the interaction with Finset.sum and Finset.card. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Sum/Basic.

Name: Update.lean
Description: Updating a function on a Finset: Function.update and its interaction with Finset.sum and Finset.prod (the update formula for Finset.prod). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Union.lean
Description: Union and intersection of Finsets: s ∪ t and s ∩ t, the basic API (mem_union, card_union_add_card_inter, etc.), and the distributive laws. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: SymmDiff.lean
Description: Symmetric difference of Finsets: s △ t = (s \ t) ∪ (t \ s), with the Boolean algebra interaction and card (s △ t) = card s + card t - 2 * card (s ∩ t). Distinct from Data/Finset/SDiff.

Name: Sigma.lean
Description: Sigma-type Finsets: Finset.sigma s t = {⟨a, b⟩ | a ∈ s, b ∈ t a} (the dependent product Finset), with card = ∑ card (t a). Distinct from Data/Finset/Prod and Data/Sigma/Basic.

Name: SDiff.lean
Description: Set difference for Finsets: s \ t = {a ∈ s | a ∉ t} and the basic API (card_sdiff, sdiff_union_of_subset, etc.). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Range.lean
Description: The range Finset: Finset.range n = {0, 1, …, n-1}, the basic API (mem_range, card_range = n), and the interaction with Nat and Fin. Distinct from Data/Finset/Interval and Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Sort.lean
Description: Sorting a Finset: Finset.sort r s gives the sorted list of elements of s by a linear order r, with the uniqueness and the interaction with List.merge. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/List/Sort.

Name: SMulAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Scalar-multiplication antidiagonals as Finsets: Finset.smulAntidiagonal s t n = {(a, b) ∈ s × t | a • b = n} for scalar multiplication. Distinct from Data/Finset/MulAntidiagonal.

Name: Slice.lean
Description: Slices of Finsets: Finset.slice s k = {t ∈ s | card t = k} for a Finset of Finsets, and the Kruskal-Katona setup. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Finset/Card.

Name: DenselyOrdered.lean
Description: Finsets in densely ordered types: properties of Finsets in (ℚ, ℝ, etc.) with dense order, and conditions for a Finset to be an interval or have gaps. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Order/DenselyOrdered.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Core definitions for Finsets: the Finset α type (a Multiset with Nodup), membership ∈, the empty set ∅, singleton {a}, and the basic membership API. Foundation for all Finset files.

Name: Dedup.lean
Description: Deduplication for Finsets: converting a List to a Finset via List.toFinset (deduplication), with the interaction between List.Nodup and Finset membership. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Empty.lean
Description: The empty Finset: ∅ : Finset α and its interaction with all Finset operations (∅ ∪ s = s, s ∩ ∅ = ∅, card ∅ = 0, etc.). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Disjoint.lean
Description: Disjoint Finsets: the Disjoint s t predicate (s ∩ t = ∅), basic properties, and the interaction with card (card (s ∪ t) = card s + card t when disjoint). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Density.lean
Description: The density of a Finset: the ratio card s / card α for a Finset s in a Fintype α, with the basic density lemmas. Distinct from Data/Finset/Card.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic Finset theory: Finset α as a list without duplicates with the membership predicate, union, intersection, difference, insert, and erase operations. Contains the basic API beyond Data/Finset/Defs. Distinct from Data/Finset/Defs.

Name: Attr.lean
Description: Attributes and automation for Finset: the @[simp] and other automation attributes for Finset lemmas, and the decide tactic infrastructure for finite set membership. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Attach.lean
Description: Finset.attach: the operation attaching membership proofs to finset elements, producing a Finset {x // x ∈ s} and the interaction with map and filter. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: CastCard.lean
