Description: Casting Finset.card to other types: Nat.cast (Finset.card s) : R for a semiring R, with the interaction between finset cardinality and algebraic operations. Distinct from Data/Finset/Card.

Name: Card.lean
Description: Cardinality of Finsets: Finset.card s (the number of elements), the interaction with union (card (s ∪ t) = card s + card t - card (s ∩ t)), image, filter, and the pigeonhole principle. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: BooleanAlgebra.lean
Description: Boolean algebra structure on Finset: the powerset 2^α is a Boolean algebra under ⊆, with complement and De Morgan laws (for a Fintype α). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Set/BooleanAlgebra.

Name: Erase.lean
Description: Finset.erase: the operation s.erase a = s \ {a}, with the basic API and the interaction with card (card (s.erase a) = card s - 1 when a ∈ s). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals as Finsets: for a LocallyFiniteOrder, the Finsets Finset.Icc, Finset.Ico, Finset.Ioc, Finset.Ioo, and their cardinalities. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Order/LocallyFiniteOrder.

Name: Insert.lean
Description: Finset.insert: the operation insert a s = {a} ∪ s, with the API (mem_insert, card_insert_of_not_mem) and the inductive principle on Finsets via insert. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Image.lean
Description: Finset.image and Finset.map: the image of a Finset under a function (with deduplication) and the injective map; interaction with card (card (image f s) ≤ card s). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Finset/Filter.

Name: NAry.lean
Description: N-ary operations on Finsets: Finset.image₂ f s t = {f a b | a ∈ s, b ∈ t} for binary operations on Finsets, with the interaction with products and filters. Distinct from Data/Finset/Image and Data/Finset/Prod.

Name: MulAntidiagonal.lean
Description: Multiplicative antidiagonals as Finsets: Finset.mulAntidiagonal s t n = {(a, b) ∈ s × t | a * b = n} for a Finset antidiagonal over a monoid. Distinct from Data/Finset/NatAntidiagonal.

Name: Max.lean
Description: Maximum and minimum of a Finset: Finset.max' s (with nonemptiness proof) and Finset.min', with the interaction with the order structure. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Order/Lattice.

Name: Finsupp.lean
Description: Finsets of finsupp functions: when a Finset of finitely supported functions is finite, and the interaction with Finsupp.support. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Finsupp/Basic.

Name: Fin.lean
Description: Finsets of Fin n: the range Finset.fin n = {0,1,…,n-1} and its interaction with Finset.map and Finset.range. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic and Data/Fin/Basic.

Name: Filter.lean
Description: Finset.filter: {x ∈ s | p x} for a decidable predicate p, with the basic lemmas (filter card, filter map, filter inter, and the partition s = filter p s ∪ filter (¬p) s). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Grade.lean
Description: The grade of a Finset: card as a grading function, with the interaction with the rank function in graded posets. Distinct from Data/Finset/Card and Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: Functor and monad instances for Finset: Finset as a functor (via Finset.image) and the monad instance (bind = image ∘ union). Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Fold.lean
Description: Finset.fold: folding a commutative associative operation over a Finset (the multiset fold), with the interaction with Finset.sum and Finset.prod. Distinct from Data/Finset/Basic.

Name: Int
Description: The integers Z: ring operations, divisibility, gcd/lcm, prime factorization, and floor/ceiling/modulo.

Name: Order
Description: The order structure on ℤ: linear order, well-ordering of ℤ≥0, the interaction between order and ring operations (OrderedRing instance), floor division and its order properties. Distinct from Data/Int/Cast (ring homomorphisms from ℤ) and Data/Int (basic arithmetic).

Name: Units.lean
Description: Units of ℤ in ordered context.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: ℤ order lemmas.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: ℤ ordering basics.

Name: Fib
Description: The Fibonacci sequence as a function ℤ → ℤ: extending the standard Fibonacci sequence to negative integers, with Fib(-n) = (-1)ⁿ⁺¹ Fib(n). Cassini's identity, the Binet formula, and divisibility properties. Distinct from Data/Nat/Fib (Fibonacci on ℕ only) — this folder gives the full integer extension.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Fibonacci lemmas for ℤ.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fibonacci numbers in ℤ.

Name: Cast
Description: Casting integers to other rings: the unique ring homomorphism ℤ → R (the characteristic map), casting lemmas (Int.cast_add, Int.cast_mul, Int.cast_neg), and the interaction with natCast. Used pervasively to convert integer arithmetic into the target ring. Distinct from Data/Int (integer arithmetic) and Data/Int/Order (order on ℤ).

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Int cast lemmas.

Name: Pi.lean
Description: Int cast in pi types.

Name: Prod.lean
Description: Int cast in product types.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Int cast API.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Int cast definitions.

Name: Field.lean
Description: Int cast in fields.

Name: NatAbs.lean
Description: The natural absolute value Int.natAbs: Int.natAbs : ℤ → ℕ and its properties (natAbs (a * b) = natAbs a * natAbs b, natAbs (a + b) ≤ natAbs a + natAbs b). Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: NatPrime.lean
Description: Primes in ℤ: the interaction between Nat.Prime p and the primality of (p : ℤ) in the ring ℤ, with the factorisation theory. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: ModEq.lean
Description: Modular arithmetic for ℤ: Int.ModEq n a b (a ≡ b [ZMOD n], meaning n ∣ b - a), the ring of congruences, and the Chinese Remainder Theorem. Distinct from Data/Int/DivMod.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Auxiliary integer lemmas: a collection of computational lemmas about integer arithmetic not naturally fitting elsewhere (mod_cast, Int.toNat, casts). Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Log.lean
Description: Integer logarithm: Int.log b n (the integer part of log_b(n)) for integers, with the basic properties and the interaction with Nat.log. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and Data/Nat/Log.

Name: Notation.lean
Description: Notation for integers: the setup of integer notation (−n for negative integers, the ℤ type alias) and the interaction with the Lean numeral system. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: SuccPred.lean
Description: Successor and predecessor for ℤ: the SuccOrder and PredOrder instances for ℤ (succ n = n + 1, pred n = n - 1), with the interaction with the linear order. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: WithZero.lean
Description: The interaction of ℤ with the WithZero construction: embedding ℤ into WithZero ℤ and the associated monoid structure. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Star.lean
Description: Star structure on ℤ: the trivial star (star n = n) making ℤ a commutative star ring, since ℤ ⊆ ℝ and ℝ has star = identity. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and Algebra/Star/Basic.

Name: Range.lean
Description: Integer ranges as Lists: Int.range a b = [a, a+1, …, b-1] as a List ℤ, with the basic API. Distinct from Data/Int/Interval (Finset version) and Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Sqrt.lean
Description: Integer square root: Int.sqrt n (the integer floor of √n for n ≥ 0), with the basic properties. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and Data/Nat/Sqrt.

Name: LeastGreatest.lean
Description: Least and greatest elements of integer sets: the interaction between ℤ's ordering and the existence of minimum/maximum in bounded integer sets (the well-ordering principle for ℤ). Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Bitwise.lean
Description: Bitwise operations on ℤ: Int.land, Int.lor, Int.xor, Int.shiftLeft, Int.shiftRight in terms of the binary representation of integers. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and Data/Nat/Bitwise.

Name: CardIntervalMod.lean
Description: Cardinality of interval-mod sets: the number of integers in [a, b) congruent to r mod n, with the formula (b - a) / n (approximately). Distinct from Data/Int/ModEq.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic integer theory: additional lemmas for ℤ beyond core Lean — the ordering, the interaction with ℕ, the additive and multiplicative group structures, and the basic arithmetic. Distinct from Data/Int/Defs (if it exists).

Name: AbsoluteValue.lean
Description: The absolute value on ℤ: Int.natAbs (the ℕ-valued absolute value) and Int.toNat (truncated), with the interaction between |n| and the order and arithmetic. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Associated.lean
Description: Associates in ℤ: the associate relation in ℤ (a ~ᵤ b iff a = ±b), the interaction with divisibility, and the normalisation to non-negative values. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and Ring/Associated.

Name: CharZero.lean
Description: The CharZero instance for ℤ: ℤ has characteristic 0 (the map ℕ → ℤ is injective), with the implications for the ring theory of ℤ. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and CharZero/Defs.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initial integer setup for Mathlib: the basic definitions and instances for ℤ that are needed early in the import hierarchy, before the full Int library is available. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: Interval.lean
Description: Order intervals in ℤ: the Finsets Finset.Icc a b, Finset.Ico a b, etc. for integers, with the cardinality formulas (card (Icc a b) = b - a + 1 when a ≤ b). Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and Data/Finset/Interval.

Name: GCD.lean
Description: GCD and LCM for ℤ: Int.gcd and Int.lcm (defined via NatAbs), Bézout's theorem (Int.gcd_eq_gcd_ab: ∃ u v, gcd a b = u*a + v*b), and the interaction with divisibility. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic and GCDMonoid/Basic.

Name: ConditionallyCompleteOrder.lean
Description: The conditionally complete linear order on ℤ: the sup and inf of bounded sets of integers, and the interaction with the Archimedean property. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: DivMod.lean
Description: Division and modulo for ℤ: Int.ediv and Int.emod (Euclidean division), Int.div and Int.mod (truncated), with the basic identities and the interaction with NatAbs. Distinct from Data/Int/Basic.

Name: FunLike
Description: The FunLike typeclass: bundled functions (DFunLike) enabling coercion from structured morphism types to plain functions.

Name: Fintype.lean
Description: Fintype instances for FunLike types: when the coercion from F to (α → β) is injective and α and β are Fintype, the type of morphisms F is Fintype. Distinct from Data/FunLike/Basic.

Name: Graded.lean
Description: Graded FunLike morphisms: morphisms between graded types that preserve the grading, using the FunLike infrastructure. Distinct from Data/FunLike/Basic.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: The EquivLike typeclass: EquivLike E α β asserts that E coerces to bijective functions with an explicit inverse. Used for isomorphisms (Equiv, RingEquiv, etc.). Distinct from Data/FunLike/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: The FunLike typeclass: FunLike F α β asserts that F has a coercion to (α → β), enabling the use of function-like notation for morphisms. Foundation for all morphism typeclasses (MonoidHom, RingHom, etc.). Distinct from all other FunLike files.

Name: Embedding.lean
Description: The EmbeddingLike typeclass: EmbeddingLike F α β asserts that F coerces to injective functions α → β. Used for injective morphisms (OrderEmbedding, RingEmbedding). Distinct from Data/FunLike/Basic.

Name: FP
Description: Floating point: formalization of IEEE-754 floating point arithmetic and its rounding behavior.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Floating-point arithmetic formalisation: basic definitions for IEEE-754-like floating-point numbers, rounding modes, and the interaction with real numbers, used in verified numerical computation. Distinct from Data/Real/Basic.

Name: Semiquot.lean
Description: Semiquotients: a type whose elements are nonempty sets (choice-free representatives of equivalence classes), used in constructive mathematics.

Name: SProd.lean
Description: Heterogeneous products (SProd): a typeclass for structures that have a product operation between different types.

Name: Rel.lean
Description: Binary relations as a type (Rel alpha beta = alpha to beta to Prop): composition, inverse, image, and relational algebra operations.

Name: PFun.lean
Description: Partial functions (PFun alpha beta): domain, restriction, graph, and the monad structure on partial functions.

Name: Quot.lean
Description: Quotient types and setoids: Quot, Quotient, and Setoid, with the universal property and the choice principle.

Name: Subtype.lean
Description: Subtypes (the subtype of a predicate p): projections, membership, decidability, and the category of subtypes.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: Universe lifting for data types: the ULift typeclass instances for arithmetic and algebraic structures.

Name: Vector3.lean
Description: An alternative Vector implementation using a size-indexed type, with matrix-like operations.

Name: UInt.lean
Description: Unsigned integer types (UInt8, UInt16, UInt32, UInt64): their ring structure and interaction with Nat.

Name: TwoPointing.lean
Description: Two-pointings of a type: pairs of distinct elements used in the construction of the suspension and join of types.

Name: TypeVec.lean
Description: Type vectors (TypeVec n = Fin n to Type): used in the construction of polynomial functors and M-types.

Name: PEquiv.lean
Description: Partial equivalences (PEquiv alpha beta): partial bijections, composable and invertible partial functions.

Name: Char.lean
Description: Characters as Unicode code points: the Char type, conversion to/from Nat, and basic character predicates.

Name: Erased.lean
Description: Erased types: propositions that carry computational content erased at runtime, for proof-relevant/irrelevant distinctions.

Name: Bundle.lean
Description: Bundles: total spaces of dependent type families, used in the formalization of fiber bundles and vector bundles.

Name: BitVec.lean
Description: Bit vectors (BitVec n): n-bit unsigned integers with bitwise operations and their connection to UInt and Nat.

Name: Bracket.lean
Description: Bracket notation for Lie brackets and commutators: the bracket notation and its typeclass.

Name: FinEnum.lean
Description: FinEnum instances: concrete finite enumerations for Bool, Fin, Option, Sum, and other standard types.

Name: Opposite.lean
Description: The Opposite typeclass for data structures: the op and unop functions reversing the orientation of a type.

Name: Part.lean
Description: Partial values (Part alpha): an element may or may not exist, with assertion, get, and monadic operations.

Name: Ineq.lean
Description: Inequality lemmas for specific numeric types: collected arithmetic facts about N, Z, Q, and R.

Name: Finmap.lean
Description: Finite maps (Finmap): association lists quotiented by key permutation, with lookup, insert, and erase.

Name: Holor.lean
Description: Holors (higher-order tensors): n-dimensional arrays generalizing matrices, with index arithmetic and reshaping.

Name: Control
Description: Provides typeclasses and instances for functors, applicatives, monads, traversable structures, and related categorical abstractions in the computational sense. Used to write generic, effectful programs and proofs inside the Lean prover, enabling structures like Traversable collections or Monad transformations. Relied on by all mathematical developments in Mathlib that require generic iteration, accumulation, or effect management.

Name: Monad
Description: Monads and monad transformers: the Monad typeclass, StateT, ReaderT, ExceptT, and monad laws.

Name: Writer.lean
Description: Writer monad: `WriterT ω m α = m (α × ω)` — a monad transformer accumulating a monoid-valued log `ω` alongside a computation. Operations: `tell`, `listen`, `pass`. Distinct from Control/Monad/Basic.

Name: Cont.lean
Description: Continuation monad: `ContT r m α = (α → m r) → m r` — the continuation passing style monad transformer. The `callCC` operation and Delimited continuations. Distinct from Control/Monad/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Monad utilities: `Monad.map`, `Monad.seq`, `Monad.mapM`, `Monad.forM`, and the `MonadLift` / `MonadFunctor` typeclasses for lifting monadic computations. Distinct from Control/Monad/Cont and Control/Monad/Writer.

Name: Traversable
Description: Traversable functors: structures supporting effectful traversal with an Applicative, with the traverse operation and laws.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: `Traversable` instances for standard types: `List`, `Option`, `Array`, `Finset`, and others. Distinct from Traversable/Basic.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas about `Traversable`: the composition law (`traverse (f <=< g) = traverse f <=< traverse g`), identity law, and interactions with `Functor.map`. Distinct from Traversable/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Traversable functors: `Traversable t` — a `Functor` `t` with a `traverse` operation `traverse f xs : m (t β)` for any applicative `m`. Core definitions, naturality, and the `Traversable` laws. Distinct from Traversable/Equiv and Traversable/Instances.

Name: Equiv.lean
Description: Equivalences and traversable: transporting a `Traversable` instance along a type equivalence `t ≃ t'`. Distinct from Traversable/Basic and Traversable/Instances.

Name: Functor
Description: Functors in the computational sense: the Functor typeclass, laws, and functorial lifting of functions.

Name: Multivariate.lean
Description: Multivariate (polynomial) functors: `MvFunctor F` — functors `F : TypeVec n → Type` taking `n`-tuples of types. Used as the semantic model for inductive and coinductive types. Distinct from Control/Bitraversable/Basic.

Name: Bitraversable
Description: Bitraversable bifunctors: structures with two traversable positions, generalizing Traversable to pairs.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas for `Bitraversable`: coherence conditions, interaction with `Functor.map` and `Traversable.traverse`. Distinct from Bitraversable/Basic and Bitraversable/Instances.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: Standard `Bitraversable` instances: `Bitraversable` for `Prod`, `Sum`, `Either`, and other standard two-parameter types. Distinct from Bitraversable/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Bitraversable functors: `Bitraversable t` — functors `t : Type → Type → Type` with a `bitraverse` operation generalising `Traversable` to two type parameters. Core definitions and the naturality laws. Distinct from Bitraversable/Instances and Control/Traversable/Basic.

Name: EquivFunctor
Description: Functors that lift equivalences: relating computational functors to the mathematical notion of equivalence-preserving maps.

Name: Instances.lean
Description: `EquivFunctor` instances: `EquivFunctor` for standard type constructors (`List`, `Option`, `Finset`, etc.) — the ability to transport along type equivalences. Distinct from Logic/Equiv/Functor.

Name: LawfulFix.lean
Description: The LawfulFix typeclass: fixed points of functors satisfying the fixed-point equation and Scott-continuity laws.

Name: Lawful.lean
Description: Proofs of the Functor, Applicative, and Monad laws for standard instances in Mathlib.

Name: Functor.lean
Description: The Functor typeclass for computational functors: fmap and the identity/composition laws.

Name: ULiftable.lean
Description: ULiftable functors: functors that can be lifted across universe levels, used for universe polymorphism.

Name: ULift.lean
Description: ULift instances for monads and functors: lifting monadic computations to higher universe levels.

Name: Random.lean
Description: A random number generation monad: the Random typeclass for generating random values of arbitrary types.

Name: Fold.lean
Description: Foldable structures: the Foldable typeclass with toList and fold operations, and its relationship to Traversable.

Name: Bifunctor.lean
Description: Bifunctors: functors of two arguments with bimap, and instances for Prod and Sum.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Basic control flow utilities: functor, applicative, and monad combinators not covered by the standard library.

Name: Applicative.lean
Description: Applicative functors: the Applicative typeclass with pure and seq, and applicative laws.

Name: Fix.lean
Description: Fixed point combinators for computation: the Fix monad for computing least fixed points of monotone functors.

Name: EquivFunctor.lean
Description: EquivFunctor: functors that lift equivalences (bijections), a weaker condition than being a lawful functor.

Name: Combinators.lean
Description: Combinator functions for monads and functors: sequence, mapM, forM, and other higher-order combinators.

Name: Dynamics
Description: Studies iterated maps, flows, ergodic theory, and chaotic behavior of dynamical systems. Used to understand long-term behavior, invariant measures, and sensitive dependence on initial conditions. Relied on by ergodic theory, statistical mechanics, population biology, and differential equations.

Name: FixedPoints
Description: Fixed points of maps: the fixed point set, Banach fixed point theorem, and applications to ODEs and operator equations.

Name: Prufer.lean
Description: Prüfer sequences and fixed points: the Prüfer encoding of labelled trees via a fixed-point construction. Distinct from FixedPoints/Basic and Combinatorics/Enumerative.

Name: Topology.lean
Description: Topological fixed-point theorems: the Brouwer fixed-point theorem (every continuous map `D^n → D^n` has a fixed point) and the Lefschetz fixed-point theorem context. Distinct from FixedPoints/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Fixed points of functions: `Function.IsFixedPoint f x` (`f x = x`), the fixed-point set `fixedPoints f`, and Banach's fixed-point theorem (for contractions on complete metric spaces). Distinct from FixedPoints/Defs and Topology/MetricSpace/Contracting.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Definitions for fixed points: `Function.IsFixedPoint`, `Function.fixedPoints`, and `Function.PeriodicPts`. The core type definitions. Distinct from FixedPoints/Basic (API).

Name: PeriodicPts
Description: Periodic points and orbits of dynamical systems: the set of period-n points and its interaction with minimality.

Name: Lemmas.lean
Description: Lemmas about periodic points: `minimalPeriod` divides any period, the set of periodic points is invariant, and the relationship between minimal period and orbit size. Distinct from PeriodicPts/Defs.

Name: Defs.lean
Description: Periodic points: `Function.IsPeriodicPt f n x` (`f^[n] x = x`), the minimal period `Function.minimalPeriod`, and the period set. Core definitions. Distinct from PeriodicPts/Lemmas.

Name: TopologicalEntropy
Description: Topological entropy of a continuous map: the measure of complexity of the orbit structure using open covers.

Name: Semiconj.lean
Description: Topological entropy under semiconjugacy: if `h ∘ T = S ∘ h` (semiconjugacy), then `htop(S) ≤ htop(T)`. Distinct from TopologicalEntropy/Subset.

Name: Subset.lean
Description: Topological entropy on invariant subsets: `htop(T|_Y) ≤ htop(T)` for a `T`-invariant closed subset `Y`. Distinct from TopologicalEntropy/Semiconj.

Name: NetEntropy.lean
Description: Net entropy: `netEntropy T U n = log (max (n, U, T)-separated set)` and the topological entropy `h(T) = sup_U lim_{n→∞} netEntropy T U n / n`. Equivalent to cover entropy. Distinct from TopologicalEntropy/CoverEntropy.

Name: CoverEntropy.lean
Description: Cover entropy: `coverEntropy T U n = log (min cover of X by (n, U, T)-separated sets)` and its limit as `n → ∞`. One approach to defining topological entropy. Distinct from TopologicalEntropy/NetEntropy and TopologicalEntropy/DynamicalEntourage.

Name: DynamicalEntourage.lean
Description: Dynamical entourages: `dynamicalEntourage T U n = ⋂_{k=0}^{n-1} (T^k)⁻¹ U` — the entourage encoding the idea of being `U`-close for `n` steps. Used in the uniform-space definition of topological entropy. Distinct from TopologicalEntropy/CoverEntropy.

Name: BirkhoffSum
Description: Birkhoff sums and averages: the ergodic averages and the Birkhoff ergodic theorem.

Name: NormedSpace.lean
Description: Birkhoff sums in normed spaces: norm estimates `‖BirkhoffSum T f n x‖ ≤ n * ‖f‖_∞` and convergence results when `f` takes values in a Banach space. Distinct from BirkhoffSum/Average.

Name: QuasiMeasurePreserving.lean
Description: Birkhoff sums for quasi-measure-preserving maps: the Birkhoff ergodic theorem for maps that are quasi-measure-preserving (not necessarily measure-preserving). Distinct from BirkhoffSum/Average and Ergodic/MeasurePreserving.

Name: Average.lean
Description: Birkhoff averages: `BirkhoffAverage T f n x = (1/n) * ∑_{k=0}^{n-1} f(Tᵏ x)` — the Cesàro mean of `f` along the orbit of `x`. The ergodic theorem: Birkhoff averages converge a.e. to the conditional expectation. Distinct from BirkhoffSum/Basic.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Birkhoff sums: `BirkhoffSum T f n x = ∑_{k=0}^{n-1} f(Tᵏ x)` — the partial sum of `f` along the orbit of `x` under the map `T`. Core definition and basic algebraic properties. Distinct from BirkhoffSum/Average and BirkhoffSum/NormedSpace.

Name: Circle
Description: Dynamics on the circle S^1: circle rotations, irrational rotations, and topological degree.

Name: RotationNumber
Description: The rotation number of an orientation-preserving homeomorphism of S¹: the average rotation per iterate, ρ(f) = lim_{n→∞} (f̃ⁿ(x) - x)/n where f̃ is a lift to ℝ. Poincaré's theorem: ρ(f) is rational iff f has a periodic orbit. Used in the study of circle maps and quasi-periodic dynamics. Distinct from Dynamics/PeriodicPts (periodic points for general maps).

Name: TranslationNumber.lean
Description: Translation number of circle homeomorphisms: the rotation number as a real number.

Name: Ergodic
Description: Ergodic theory: measure-preserving transformations, ergodicity, the von Neumann and Birkhoff mean ergodic theorems.

Name: Action
Description: Ergodic group actions on measure spaces: a measure-preserving action G ↷ (X, μ) is ergodic if every G-invariant measurable set has measure 0 or 1. Contains the mean ergodic theorem for unitary representations and the pointwise ergodic theorem for ℤ-actions. Distinct from the top-level Dynamics/Ergodic (which has the ergodic theorems for a single map T).

Name: Regular.lean
Description: Regular ergodic actions.

Name: OfMinimal.lean
Description: Ergodic actions from minimal actions.

Name: Basic.lean
Description: Ergodic group actions: `ErgodicAction G X μ`.

Name: Function.lean
Description: Measurable functions in ergodic theory: `T`-invariant functions (`f ∘ T = f` a.e.) and their characterisation as a.e.-constant for ergodic `T`. Distinct from Ergodic/Ergodic.

Name: Extreme.lean
Description: Extreme invariant measures: the ergodic measures are exactly the extreme points of the convex set of invariant probability measures. Distinct from Ergodic/Ergodic and Analysis/Convex/KreinMilman.

Name: RadonNikodym.lean
Description: Radon–Nikodym in ergodic theory: the Radon–Nikodym derivative of a quasi-invariant measure under a dynamical system, and the cocycle equation. Distinct from Ergodic/MeasurePreserving and MeasureTheory/Measure/AbsolutelyContinuous.

Name: MeasurePreserving.lean
Description: Measure-preserving maps: `MeasurePreserving T μ ν` — `μ ∘ T⁻¹ = ν`, i.e., `T` preserves the measure. Core API: composition of measure-preserving maps, the push-forward. Distinct from Ergodic/Ergodic and MeasureTheory/Measure/QuasiMeasurePreserving.

Name: AddCircleAdd.lean
Description: Addition on the additive circle and ergodic theory: measurability and measure-preservation properties of addition maps on `ℝ/ℤ`. Distinct from Ergodic/AddCircle.

Name: AddCircle.lean
Description: Ergodicity of irrational rotations: the rotation `x ↦ x + α` on `ℝ/ℤ` is ergodic (for Lebesgue measure) iff `α` is irrational. The unique ergodicity of irrational rotations. Distinct from Ergodic/Ergodic and MeasureTheory/Constructions/AddCircle.

Name: Ergodic.lean
Description: Ergodic maps: `Ergodic T μ` — measure-preserving and every `T`-invariant measurable set has measure 0 or 1. The ergodic theorem and ergodic decomposition. Distinct from Ergodic/Conservative and BirkhoffSum/Average.

Name: Conservative.lean
Description: Conservative dynamical systems: `Conservative T μ` — every measurable set of positive measure returns to itself under `T`. Poincaré recurrence theorem: a measure-preserving map on a finite measure space is conservative. Distinct from Ergodic/MeasurePreserving.

Name: OmegaLimit.lean
Description: Omega-limit sets: the accumulation points of a trajectory as time goes to infinity, with topological properties.

Name: Transitive.lean
Description: Topological transitivity: a dynamical system is transitive if every pair of open sets has an orbit connecting them.

Name: Newton.lean
Description: Newton's method for finding fixed points: the iteration and its quadratic convergence proof.

Name: Flow.lean
Description: Flows: one-parameter group actions on a space satisfying the flow equation, the continuous-time model of dynamics.

Name: Minimal.lean
Description: Minimal dynamical systems: systems where every orbit is dense, and the characterization of minimality.

Name: Deprecated
Description: Contains old code kept temporarily for compatibility but no longer recommended. Not used for new mathematical proofs; it exists solely to help transition formalizations to newer, maintained versions. No active math fields rely on Deprecated.

Name: Aliases.lean
Description: Deprecated alias declarations: old names mapped to new ones to maintain backwards compatibility during library refactoring.

Name: Tactic.lean
Description: The main tactic aggregation file: re-exports the entire Mathlib.Tactic namespace, making all Mathlib proof tactics available via a single import statement. Used by downstream projects that need the full suite of Mathlib automation (linarith, ring, norm_num, positivity, simp extensions, and hundreds more) without importing all mathematical content separately.

Name: Init.lean
Description: Initialization file for Mathlib4: establishes fundamental notations, universe polymorphism conventions, and core infrastructure that every Mathlib file depends on. Sets up the Lean 4 environment with the notational conventions and basic definitions used consistently throughout the library, and is imported before any mathematical content.
